The image is the Royal Coat of Arms of the United Kingdom, featuring a shield supported by two lions, topped with a crown. The shield is divided into four quarters, each containing a different heraldic symbol. The entire emblem is surrounded by a decorative border.

LONDON : HIS MAJESTY'S STATIONERY OFFICE
1945

Price £1 5s. 0d. net

An oval-shaped stamp with the text "INDIA OFFICE" arched across the top and "LIBRARY" arched across the bottom. There is a small dot on the left side of the oval.

NOTE.

As a temporary war-time expedient to economise in paper and labour, the contents of this volume (the first since January, 1940) have been much reduced and are designed to serve essential official requirements. The usual lists of cadres of the All India Services etc., particulars of many other posts and holders, and records of service of a number of officers otherwise eligible for inclusion have thus been held over.

Unless they appear elsewhere in the volume, names in the Alphabetical Honours List (pages 57-112), and in the Record of Services (pages 113-383) are omitted from the Index of Names (pages 384-000), Indian and Burman names are indexed as in the Civil Lists of the Central and Provincial Governments of India and of the Government of Burma.

The principle of selection followed in the Alphabetical Honours List and in the Record of Services (subject to the remarks in paragraph 1) is explained on pages 57 and 113, respectively.

It is requested that intimations of errors in the lists may be addressed to the Editor of the India Office and Burma Office List, India Office, Whitehall, London, S.W.1.

OFFICE OF THE SECRETARY OF STATE FOR INDIA

WHITEHALL, S.W.1

SECRETARY OF STATE.

THE RT. HON. L. S. AMERY, M.P.

PERMANENT UNDER-SECRETARY OF STATE.

Sir Findlater Stewart, G.C.B., G.C.I.E., C.S.I.†
SIR DAVID T. MONTEATH, K.C.B., K.C.M.G., C.V.O., O.B.E.

PARLIAMENTARY UNDER-SECRETARY OF STATE.

THE RT. HON. THE EARL OF LISTOWEL.

DEPUTY UNDER-SECRETARIES OF STATE.

Sir William D. Croft, K.B.E., C.I.E., C.V.O.‡
SIR CECIL H. KISCH, K.C.I.E., C.B.

GOVERNMENT OF INDIA

Headquarters of Government—New Delhi

VICEROY AND GOVERNOR-GENERAL OF INDIA.

His Excellency Field Marshal The Right Honourable Viscount WAVELL OF CYRENAICA AND WINCHESTER, P.C., G.C.B., G.M.S.I., G.M.I.E., C.M.G., M.C.

Assumed charge of office 20 October, 1943.

PERSONAL STAFF OF H.E. THE VICEROY.

Private Secretary—Sir Evan Jenkins, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., I.C.S.

Deputy Private Secretary—G. E. B. Abell, Esq., O.B.E., I.C.S.

Assistant Private Secretary—Major C. W. B. Rankin.

Military Secretary—Col. D. H. Currie, C.B.E., M.C., D.C.M., I.A.

Comptroller—Major P. D. Coats, Royal Signals.

Surgeon—Lieut.-Col. H. Williamson, O.B.E., I.M.S. (retd.).

Aides-de-Camp—Capt. W. B. Henderson, Royal Marines; Capt. the Earl of Euston, Grenadier Guards; Capt. G. H. U. Crookshank, 3rd Hussars; Capt. J. B. Fortune, M.C., The Black Watch.

Indian Aides-de-Camp—Risaldar-Major and Hony. Lieut. Raghbir Singh, Sardar Bahadur, M.B.E., O.B.I., I.D.S.M., I.A.C.; Risaldar-Major and Hony. Lieut. Muzaffar Khan, Sardar Bahadur, O.B.I., I.A.C.

Comdt. of the Body Guard—Major J. R. L. Tweed, Ind. Armoured Corps.

Adjutant of the Body Guard—Lieut. P. A. C. Marriott, Ind. Armoured Corps.

GOVERNMENT OF MADRAS

Headquarters of Government—Fort St. George (Madras) (cold weather); Ootacamund (hot weather).

Governor—HIS EXCELLENCY CAPTAIN SIR ARTHUR OSWALD JAMES HOPE, G.C.I.E., M.C.

Took his seat, 12th March, 1940.

Secretary to Governor—A. J. Platt, I.C.S.

In consequence of the resignation of the Congress Ministry in October, 1939, the Province is temporarily administered by the Governor under s.93 of the Government of India Act, 1935.

GOVERNMENT OF THE UNITED PROVINCES OF
AGRA AND OUDH

Headquarters—Lucknow (cold weather), Naini Tal (hot weather).

Governor—HIS EXCELLENCY SIR MAURICE GARNIER HALLETT, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I.
I.C.S.

Took his seat 7th December, 1939.

Secretary to the Governor—I. W. Lewys Lloyd, O.B.E., I.C.S.

In consequence of the resignation of the Congress Ministry in November, 1939, the Province is temporarily administered by the Governor under s.93 of the Government of India Act, 1935.

Born 19 Aug., 1908. Succeeded 13 Aug., 1923.

Udaipur (Mewar). His Highness Maharajadhir-
aja Maharana Sir Bhupal Singh, Baha-
dur, G.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., Maharana of.
Born 22 Feb., 1884. Succeeded 24 May, 1930.

Wadhwan. Thakor Saheb Shri, Surendrashinhji
Minor Thakor, Saheb of.
Born 4 Jan., 1922. Succeeded 27 July, 1934.

Wankaner. Capt. His Highness Maharana Shri
Sir Amarsinhji, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., Maha-
rana Raj Saheb of.
Born 4 Jan., 1879. Succeeded 8 March, 1899.

TABLE OF ARTILLERY SALUTES FIRED IN INDIA
Persons. No. of Guns. Occasions on which Salute is fired.
Imperial Salute .. .. . 101 When the Sovereign is present in person.
On the anniversaries of the Birth, Accession, and Coronation of the reigning Sovereign; the Birthday of the Consort of the Sovereign and the Birthday of Queen Mary; Proclamation Day.
Royal Salute|| .. .. . 31
Viceroy and Governor-General .. .. . 31 On arrival at, or departure from, a military station within Indian territories, or when attending a State ceremony.
Members of the Royal Family .. .. . 31
Foreign Sovereigns and members of their families¶ .. .. . 21 } On arrival at, or departure from, a military station, or when attending a State ceremony.
Sultan of Zanzibar .. .. . 21
Ambassadors .. .. . 19
Governor-General of Portuguese India .. .. . 19
Prime Minister of Nepal¶ .. .. . 19
Governor of the French Settlements in India.. .. 17
Governors of His Majesty's Colonies .. .. . 17
Envoy Extraordinary and Ministers Plenipotentiary. 17
Lieutenant-Governors of His Majesty's Colonies 15
Plenipotentiaries and Envoys .. .. . 15
Maharajah of Bhutan .. .. . 15 } On assuming or relinquishing office, whether temporarily or permanently.
On occasions of a public arrival at or departure from a military station, and on formal ceremonial occasions, such as arriving at or leaving a Durbar, or when paying a formal visit to a Ruling Chief. Also on occasions of private arrival at or departure from a military station, if desired.
Governor of Damaun .. .. . 9
Governor of Diu .. .. . 9
Governors of Presidencies and Provinces in India. 17
Residents, 1st class .. .. . 13
Chief Commissioner, Andaman and Nicobar Islands. 13 } Same as Governors.
On assuming or relinquishing office; or, if desired, on arrival at or departure from Port Blair when making a journey outside his jurisdiction.
Residents, 2nd class .. .. . 13
Political Agents* .. .. . 11
Resident Adviser at Mukalla (within Qu'aiti and Kathiri territory). 11 } On assuming or relinquishing office, and on occasions of a public arrival at or departure from a military station.

* Within the territories of the State to which they are attached.

|| A Royal Salute will also be fired on the occasion of the official celebration of the reigning Sovereign's birthday.

The date of the official celebration will be notified annually in India Army Orders.

¶ The Maharajadhiraj of Nepal and the Sultan of Maskat are included in the entry "Foreign Sovereigns."

§ On the occasion of his visits to India as the representative of the Nepal Government.

Persons. No. of Guns. Occasions on which Salute is fired.
Commander-in-Chief in India (if a Field-Marshall). 19 On assuming or relinquishing office. On public arrival at or departure from a military station, and on formal ceremonial occasions. Also on occasions of private arrival or departure, if desired.
Commander-in-Chief in India (if a General) .. 17
Naval Commander-in-Chief, East Indies Squadron. † Same as for military officer of corresponding rank (see King's Regulations).
On assuming or relinquishing command.
Air Officer Commanding-in-Chief, Air Forces in India (if an Air Marshal). 15
Air Officer Commanding-in-Chief, Air Forces in India (if an Air Vice-Marshall). 13 On public arrival at or departure from a military station, provided that he is senior to any naval, military or air force officer in the cantonment area containing the air force station, and subject to this latter condition, also on occasions of private arrival or departure, if desired.
Flag Officer Commanding Royal Indian Navy (if a Vice-Admiral)** 15
Flag Officer Commanding, Royal Indian Navy (if a Rear-Admiral)** 13 To be fired from the shore battery when visiting an Indian port for the first time and his arrival is public.
General Officers Commanding-in-Chief, Commands† 15
Major-Generals Commanding Districts† .. 13 On assuming or relinquishing command, and on occasions of public arrival at or departure from, a military station within their command. Also on occasions of private arrival or departure, if desired.
Major-Generals and Brigadiers Commanding Brigades† 11

** If promoted during tenure of his appointment, or on the occasion of his first official visit to a newly appointed Governor-General, Governor, etc., the Flag Officer Commanding, Royal Indian Navy, is entitled to the ceremonial honours applicable to his rank when he lands officially.

† According to naval rank, with two guns added.

‡ No military officer shall receive an artillery salute unless he is in actual military command and is the senior military officer on the spot. Attention is invited to the extra guns allowed for individuals.

WARRANT OF PRECEDENCE IN INDIA.

GEORGE R.I.

GEORGE THE SIXTH, by the Grace of God, of Great Britain, Ireland, and the British Dominions beyond the Seas, King, Defender of the Faith, Emperor of India.

To all to whom these presents shall come:—

Whereas it hath been represented unto Us that it is advisable that the rank and precedence of persons holding appointments in India as regulated by Royal Warrant of His late Majesty King George the Fifth dated the 9th day of April 1930 should be altered, We do therefore hereby declare that it is Our will and pleasure that in lieu of the table laid down in the said recited Warrant the following table be henceforth observed with respect to the rank and precedence of persons herein-after named, namely:—

  1. 1. Governor-General and Viceroy of India.
  2. 2. Governors of Provinces within their respective charges.
  3. 3. Governors of Madras, Bombay and Bengal.
  4. 4. Commander-in-Chief in India.
  5. 5. Governors of the United Provinces and Punjab.
  6. 6. Governors of Bihar and the Central Provinces and Berar.
  7. 7. Governors of Assam, the North-West Frontier Province, Sind, and Orissa.
  8. 8. Chief Justice of India.
  9. 9. Members of the Governor-General's Executive Council.
  10. 10. Commander-in-Chief of His Majesty's Naval Forces in the East Indies.
  11. 11. President of the Council of State.
  12. 12. President of the Indian Legislative Assembly.
  13. 13. Judges of the Federal Court.
  1. 14. Chief Justices of High Courts.
  2. 15. Agent to the Governor-General, Baluchistan; Ministers of Governors*; and Residents of the First Class. (Within their respective charges.)
  3. 16. Chief Commissioner of Railways; General Officers Commanding, Northern, Southern, Eastern and Western Commands; and Officers of the rank of General.
  4. 17. Chief of the General Staff; and Ministers of the Governors of Madras, Bombay, and Bengal.*
  5. 18. Air Officer Commanding, Royal Air Force in India; and Ministers of the Governors of the United Provinces and Punjab.*
  6. 19. Ministers of the Governors of Bihar and the Central Provinces and Berar.*
  7. 20. Agent to the Governor-General, Baluchistan; Ministers of the Governors of Assam, North-West Frontier Province, Sind and Orissa;* and Residents of the First Class.
  8. 21. Presidents of Legislative Councils.
  9. 22. Speakers of Legislative Assemblies.
  10. 23. Chief Judges of Chief Courts; and Puisne Judges of High Courts.
  11. 24. Lieutenant-Generals.
  12. 25. Auditor-General in India; Chairman of the Federal Public Service Commission; and Chief Commissioner of Delhi within his charge.
  13. 26. Flag Officer Commanding, Royal Indian Navy; Members of the Railway Board; Officers Commanding Military Districts within their respective charges; Railway Financial Commissioner; Secretaries to the Governor-General; and Secretaries to the Government of India and in the Political Department.
  14. 27. Additional Secretaries to the Government of India and in the Political Department; Judges of Chief Courts; and Vice-Chairman, Imperial Council of Agricultural Research.
  15. 28. Chairman, Public Service Commissions, Madras, Bombay and Sind and Bengal; Chief Commissioner of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands within the charge; and Chief Secretaries to the Governments of Madras, Bombay and Bengal.
  16. 29. Chief Commissioner of Delhi; Commissioners of Revenue and Commissioner of Excise, Bombay; Director-General, Indian Medical Service; Director-General of Posts and Telegraphs; Director of Intelligence; Financial Adviser, Military Finance; Financial Commissioners; Joint Secretaries to the Government of India and in the Political Department; Judicial Commissioner of the North-West Frontier Province; Judicial Commissioner of Sind; Major-Generals; Members of a Board of Revenue; Members of the Central Board of Revenue; Members of the Federal Public Service Commission; Political Resident on the North-West Frontier; Secretary to the Governor-General's Executive Council; Secretaries to the Governors of Madras, Bombay and Bengal; and Surgeons-General.
  17. 30. The Advocate-General of India; and Vice-Chancellors of the Indian Universities.
  18. 31. Agents of State Railways; Chief Commissioner of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands; Controller of the Currency; Controller of Railway Accounts; Deputy Auditor-General. Assistant Judicial Commissioners; Chief Revenue Authority in Assam; Commissioners of Divisions; Judicial Commissioner, Western India States Agency; Residents of the Second Class; Revenue and Divisional Commissioners, North-West Frontier Province; and Revenue Commissioner, Sind and Orissa (within their respective charges).
  19. 32. Members of the Indian Civil Service and Members of the Indian Political Service serving in the Crown and External Affairs Departments of 30 years' standing, whose position but for this Article would not be lower than Article 36; and Officers Commanding Cavalry and Infantry Brigades and Brigade Areas, within their respective charges.
  20. 33. Advocates-General, Madras, Bombay and Bengal.
  21. 34. Chairman, Public Service Commissions, other than those of Madras, Bombay and Sind, and Bengal; and Chief Secretaries to the Governments of the United Provinces, Punjab, Bihar, and Central Provinces and Berar.
  22. 35. Brigadiers; Census Commissioner for India; Chief Controller of Standardisation, Railway Department; Chief Controller of Stores, Indian Stores Department; Director of Geological Survey; Director of Ordnance Factories; Educational Commissioner with the Government of India; His Majesty's Senior Trade Commissioner, Calcutta; Inspector-General of Forests and President, Forest Research Institute; Inspectors-General of Police in Provinces other than Assam, North-West Frontier Province, Sind and Orissa; Public Health Commissioner with the Government of India; and Surveyor-General of India.
  23. 36. Assistant Judicial Commissioners; Chief Revenue Authority in Assam; Chief Secretaries to the Governments of Assam, the North-West Frontier Province, Sind and Orissa; Commissioners of Divisions; Judicial Commissioner, Western India States Agency; Residents of the Second Class; Revenue and Divisional Commissioner, North-West Frontier Province; and Revenue Commissioners, Sind and Orissa.

* NOTE.—The Chief Minister ranks in the same Article as, but senior to, other Ministers.

37. Inspectors-General of Police, Assam, North-West Frontier Province, Sind and Orissa; Non-Official Mayors or Presidents of Municipal Corporations of Madras, Bombay and Calcutta, within their respective Municipal jurisdictions; Private Secretary to the Viceroy; Secretaries to Local Governments; and Secretaries to the Governors of Provinces other than Madras, Bombay and Bengal.

38. Accountants-General and Directors of Audit; Additional and Joint Secretaries to Local Governments; Chief Accounts Officers, State Railways; Chief Auditors, State Railways, of the rank of Accountant-General; Chief Commercial Managers, State Railways; Chief Conservators of Forests; Chief Engineers; Chief Engineer, Posts and Telegraphs; Chief Mechanical Engineers of State Railways; Chief Mining Engineer, Railway Board; Chief Operating Superintendents, State Railways; Chief Traffic Managers, State Railways; Chief Transportation Superintendent, G.I.P. Railway; Colonels; Commissioners of Police, Bombay and Calcutta; Deputy Director of Intellectual Research, Government of India; Directors of Agriculture; Director, Imperial Institute of Agricultural Research; Director of Civil Aviation; Director-General of Archaeology; Director-General of Observatories; Directors of Public Health under Provincial Governments; Directors of Public Instruction under Provincial Governments; Directors of Health and Prison Services, Sind and Orissa; Director, Military Lands and Cantonments; Directors, Railway Board; Expert Advisers, Imperial Council of Agricultural Research; His Majesty's Trade Commissioners, Bombay and Calcutta; Inspectors-General, Civil Hospitals; Inspectors-General of Prisons; Master, Security Printing, India, and Controller of Stamps; Members of the Indian Civil Service and Members of the Indian Political Service serving in the Crown and External Affairs Departments of 23 years' standing, whose position but for this Article would not be lower than Article 55; Military Accountant-General; Mint Masters, Calcutta and Bombay; President, Court of Wards, United Provinces; Settlement Commissioners; Sheriffs of Madras, Bombay and Calcutta; Solicitor to the Government of India; and Traffic Managers and Locomotive Superintendents of State Railways.

39. Advocates-General other than those of Madras, Bombay and Bengal; Chief Surveyor with the Government of India; Command Controllers of Military Accounts (except Western Command); Controller, Military Accounts and Pensions; Director, Botanical Survey; Director, Railway Clearing Accounts Office; Directors of the Survey of India; Director, Zoological Survey; Financial Adviser, Posts and Telegraphs; Legal Remembrancers to Provincial Governments; and Nautical Adviser to the Government of India.

40. Military Secretary to the Viceroy.

41. Standing Counsel for Bengal.

42. Presidency Senior Chaplains of the Church of Scotland.

43. Chairmen of the Port Trusts and of Improvement Trusts of Madras, Bombay, Calcutta and Karachi; Chief Executive Officers of the Municipalities of Madras, Bombay and Calcutta, within their charges; Chief Inspector of Mines; Collector of Customs, Calcutta and Bombay; Commissioners of Income Tax, Bengal and Bombay; Commissioner of Police, Madras; Controller of Emigrant Labour, Assam; Postmasters-General, Bengal and Assam, and Bombay; Senior Deputy Director-General, Posts and Telegraphs. Collectors and Magistrates of Districts; Commissioner of Ajmer-Merwara; Deputy Commissioners of Districts; Political Agents; and Residents (other than those of First and Second Class). (Within their respective charges.)

44. Collectors of Customs, other than those of Calcutta and Bombay; Collectors of Salt Revenue, Madras and Bombay; Collector of Stamp Revenue and Deputy Collector of Land Revenue, Calcutta, within their respective charges; Commissioners of Income-tax, other than those in Bengal and Bombay; Commissioner, Northern India Salt Revenue; Deputy Commissioner, Port Blair, within his charge; and Divisional and District and Sessions Judges (including the Judicial Commissioner of Chota Nagpur), within their respective charges.

45. Commissioner of Wakfs, Bengal; Commandant, Frontier Constabulary; Deputy Director of Intelligence, Peshawar; Deputy Financial Advisers, Military Finance; Deputy Secretaries to the Government of India and in the Political Department; Deputy Inspectors-General of Police; Director-General of Commercial Intelligence; Director of Inspection, Indian Stores Department; Director of Public Information, Government of India; Inspector-General of Railway Police and Police Assistant to the Agent to the Governor-General, Rajputana; Members of Provincial Public Service Commissions; Metallurgical Inspector, Indian Stores Department; Secretary to the Imperial Council of Agricultural Research; Secretary, Federal Public Service Commission; Secretary to the Railway Board; and Secretaries to the Agent to the Governor-General in Baluchistan and to Residents of the First Class (within the charges respectively of the Agent or the Resident).

46. Chief Medical Officers under the Crown and External Affairs Departments and in Delhi (within their respective charges); Chief Medical Officers and Chief Medical and Health Officers, State Railways; Deputy Directors-General, Posts and Telegraphs, other than the Senior Deputy Director-General; Director, All-India Institute of Hygiene and Public Health, Calcutta; Director, Central Research Institute, Kasauli; Director of the Imperial Institute of Veterinary Research, Muktesar; Director of the Indian Institute of Science; Postmasters-General other than those of Bengal and Assam and Bombay; and Principal of the Thomasan Engineering College, Roorkee.

47. Assistant Director of Ordnance Factories (if a Civilian); Budget Officer, Finance Department, Government of India; Chief Auditors of Railways, Class I; Chief Education Officer, Royal Air Force; Civilian Superintendents of Ordnance Factories; Comptrollers, Assam, North-West

Frontier Province, Sind and Orissa ; Conservators of Forests ; Controller of Army Factory Accounts ; Controller of Military Accounts, Western Command ; Deputy Agents, Deputy Traffic Managers, and Officers* of similar status of State Railways ; Deputy Chief Controller of Standardisation, Railway Board ; Deputy Director-General, Indian Medical Service ; Deputy Military Accountant-General ; Director, Medical Research ; District Controllers of Military Accounts ; Engineer-in-Chief, Lighthouse Department, and Chief Inspector of Lighthouses in British India ; Lieutenant-Colonels ; Members of the Indian Civil Service and Members of the Indian Political Service serving in the Crown and External Affairs Departments of 18 years' standing whose position but for this Article would not be lower than Article 56 ; Senior Chaplains of and above 20 years' service in India ; and Superintending† and Deputy Chief Engineers.

48. Actuary to the Government of India ; Chief Inspector of Explosives ; Chief Judges of Small Cause Courts, Madras, Bombay and Calcutta ; Controller of Printing and Stationery ; and Directors of Major Laboratories.

49. Administrators-General ; Central Intelligence Officers ; Chief Presidency Magistrates in Madras, Bombay and Calcutta ; Commissioners of Labour, Madras and Bombay ; Controller of Patents and Designs ; Directors of Industries ; Directors of Land Records ; Directors of Veterinary Services ; Excise Commissioners ; Inspectors-General of Registration ; Inspector of Municipal Committees and Local Boards, Madras ; Principal Research Institute, Cawnpore ; and Registrars of Co-operative Societies.

50. Audit Officer, Indian Stores Department ; Deputy Chief Accounts Officers, State Railways ; Deputy Controller of Railway Accounts ; Deputy Director, Railway Clearing Accounts ; Deputy Controllers of Stores, State Railways ; Deputy Directors, Railway Board ; Director, Regulations and Forms in the Defence Department ; Directors of Telegraphs ; Electrical Engineer-in-Chief, Posts and Telegraphs ; Junior Controllers of Military Accounts ; Officers in Class I of the General or the Public Works List of the Indian Audit and Accounts Service ; Opium Agent, Ghazipur ; Supervisor of Railway Labour ; and Superintendent of Manufacture, Clothing Factory, Shahjanpur.

51. District Judges not being Sessions Judges within their own districts.

52. First Assistants or Secretaries to Second Class Residents (within the charges of their respective Residents).

53. Military Secretaries and Private Secretaries to Governors ; and Central Publicity Officer, State Railways.

54. Senior Chaplains other than those already specified.

55. Assistant Directors of Intelligence. Collectors of Salt Revenue, Madras and Bombay ; Collectors and Magistrates of Districts ; Collector of Stamp Revenue and Deputy Collector of Land Revenue, Calcutta ; Commissioner of Ajmer-Merwara ; Deputy Commissioners of Districts ; Deputy Commissioner, Port Blair ; Deputy and Additional Deputy Secretaries to Provincial Governments ; Directors of Publicity or Public Information under Provincial Governments ; Divisional and District and Sessions Judges (including the Judicial Commissioner of Chota Nagpur) ; Political Agents ; Residents (other than those of the First and Second Class) ; Secretaries to the Agent to the Governor-General, Baluchistan, and to First Class Residents ; Settlement Officers ; and Superintendents of Police within their own charges.

56. Administrative Officer, Central Public Works Department ; Captain Superintendent, I.M.M.T.S. Dufferin ; Chief Aerodrome Officer ; Chief Forest Officer, Andaman and Nicobar Islands ; Chief Education Officer, Delhi, Ajmer-Merwara and Central India ; Chief Inspector of Aircraft ; Controllers of Inspection and Purchase Indian Stores Department (Senior Scale) ; Deputy Directors, Indian Stores Department ; Deputy Directors of Commercial Intelligence ; Deputy Director-General of Archæology ; Deputy Director of Industries, United Provinces ; Deputy Directors of Hospitals, Sind and Orissa ; Deputy Directors of Public Instruction ; Deputy Inspectors-General of Prisons ; Deputy Master, Security Printing, India ; Deputy Secretary, Railway Board ; First Assistants or Secretaries to Second Class Residents ; Government Solicitors other than the Solicitor and Second Solicitor to the Government of India ; Principals of major Government Colleges ; Principal, Prince of Wales Royal Indian Military College, Dehra Dun ; Principal of the Hyderabad Medical School, Sind ; Principal, Indian School of Mines ; Principal, Sir J. J. School of Art, Bombay ; Registrars to the High Courts ; Secretaries to Legislative Councils and Provincial Legislative Assemblies ; Senior Inspectors and Electric Inspector of Mines ; Superintendent of the Government Test House ; Superintendents of the Survey of India ; Superintendent, Mathematical Instrument Office. Officers of the All-India, Class I Central, Class I Railway, Class I Provincial and Indian Ordnance Services and of the Superior List of the Military Accounts Department (of 20 years' standing in the service or graded above officers of that standing).

* Officers of similar status are : Deputy Superintendents, Locomotives Department ; Superintendents, Carriage and Wagon Department ; Controllers of Stores ; Divisional Superintendents, State Railways ; Divisional Transportation Superintendent, G.I.P. Railway ; Signal Engineers ; State Railways Coal Superintendent ; Deputy Transportation Superintendents ; Deputy Chief Commercial Managers ; Deputy Chief Mechanical Engineers ; Deputy Chief Engineers ; Chief Electrical Engineers and Colliery Superintendent, E.I. Railway.

† Architectural, Electrical and Sanitary Specialist officers will take precedence in accordance with the rank in the Public Works Department fixed for their appointments but junior to all Public Works Department Officers of the corresponding rank.

57. Assistant Private Secretary to the Viceroy. Deputy Directors of Public Information, Government of India; Second Solicitor to the Government of India; Section and Assistant Masters of the Prince of Wales Royal Indian Military College, Dehra Dun; and Under Secretaries to the Government of India and in the Political Department.

58. Consulting Surveyor to the Government of Bombay; Directors of Survey, Bengal; Keeper of the Records of the Government of India; Librarian, Imperial Library; and Government Analyst, Madras.

59. Chemical Inspector, Indian Ordnance Department; Civil Engineer, Adviser to the Director of Ordnance Factories and Manufacture; District Judges not being Sessions Judges; Deputy Chief Inspector of Stores and Clothing; Education Officers, Grade II, and Education Officers, Grade III, on completion of 15 years' service. (Royal Air Force); Majors; Master of High Court, Madras; Members of the Indian Civil Service and Members of the Indian Political Service serving in the Crown and External Affairs Departments of 12 years' standing; Superintendents and Deputy Commissioners of Police of more than 15 but less than 20 years' standing; and Works Managers of Ordnance Factories.

60. Assistant Commissioners of Income-tax; Assistant Military Accountant-General; Assistant Superintendents of the Survey of India; Chief Mining Engineer, Northern India Salt Revenue; Chief Works Chemist, United Provinces; Controller of Naval Accounts; Controller of Royal Air Force Accounts; Deputy Controller of Stamps; Deputy Controller of Salt Revenue, Bombay; Deputy Commissioner of Salt Revenue, Madras; Examiner of Local Funds Accounts, Madras; General Manager, Rajputana Salt Resources; Mathematical Adviser, Survey of India; Superintendent, Bombay City Survey and Land Records; Superintendents and Deputy Commissioners of Police of less than 15 years' standing. Officers of the All-India, Class I Central, Class I Railway, and Class I Provincial and Indian Ordnance Services and of the Superior List of the Military Accounts Department, and Section and Assistant Masters of the Prince of Wales Royal Indian Military College, Dehra Dun (of 10 years' standing in the service or grade above officers of that standing).

61. Assistant Collectors, Salt Revenue, Bombay, and Assistant Commissioners, Salt Revenue, Madras, on maximum of their time-scale; Assistant Commissioner, Selection Grade, Northern India Salt Revenue; Assistant Commissioners, Northern India Salt Revenue, on maximum of the ordinary time-scale; Assistant Controllers of Inspection, Indian Stores Department; Assistant Directors, Indian Stores Department; Assistant Metallurgical Inspectors, Indian Stores Department; Assistant Directors of Public Health; Assistant Directors, Railway Board; Assistant Financial Advisers, Military Finance; Assistant Secretaries to the Government of India and in the Political Department; Assistant Secretary to the Railway Board; Chemical Examiners at Customs Houses; Chemical Examiner, United Provinces; Chemist at the Government Test House, Indian Stores Department; Chief Inspectors of Factories and Boilers; Controllers of Inspection and Purchase, Indian Stores Department (Junior scale); Controller of Telegraph Stores; Deputy Administrator-General, Bengal; Deputy Assistant Director, Pay and Pensions Directorate, Adjutant-General's Branch; Deputy Registrars of Co-operative Societies; Deputy Commissioners of Salt and Excise; Deputy Controller, Central Printing Office; Deputy Controller, Stationery; Director, Vaccine Institute, Belgaum; District Opium Officers; Divisional Engineers, Telegraph, and Divisional Engineers, Wireless; Divisional Forest Officers; Emigration Commissioners; Engineer, Lighthouse Department, and Inspector of Lighthouses in British India; Examiner of Questioned Documents; Executive Engineers; Factory Chemist and Factory Superintendent (Opium Factory, Ghazipur); First Assistant Commissioner, Port Blair; Honorary Presidency Magistrates; Income-tax Officers drawing the maximum pay of the time-scale; Judge of the City Civil Court, Madras; Judges of Courts of Small Causes in the towns of Madras, Bombay and Calcutta; Lady Assistants to the Inspectors-General, Civil Hospitals; Legal Assistant in the Legislative Department of the Government of India; Managers, Government of India Presses at Calcutta, Delhi and Simla; Mine Manager, Khewra; Officers of the Provincial Civil Services drawing the maximum pay of the time-scale or upwards; Officers of the First Division of the Superior Traffic Branch, Posts and Telegraphs; Physicist at the Government Test House, Indian Stores Department; Presidency Magistrates; Principal, Indian Medical School, Madras; Principal, Lawrence Royal Military School, Sanawar; Protectors of Emigrants; Public Prosecutors in Bengal and in Sind; Registrars to Chief Courts; Registrars of Joint Stock Companies; Secretary to the Court of Wards, United Provinces; Superintendents of Excise, Bombay; Superintendents of Central Jails and Civil Surgeons who are not included in any other article; and Superintendent of Telegraph Workshops.

1. The entries in the above table, which are in alphabetical order in each article, apply exclusively to the persons entered therein, and while regulating their relative precedence with each other, do not give them any precedence over members of the non-official community resident in India, who shall take their place according to usage.

2. Officers in the above table will take precedence in order of the numbers of the entries. Those included in one number will take precedence inter se according to the date of entry into that number with the exception of officers of the Defence Services, who rank inter se in accordance with their seniority, and of the Chief Justice of Bengal, who will rank before all other persons included in Article 14 irrespective of the date of their entry into that Article.

3. When an officer holds more than one position in the table, he will be entitled to the highest position accorded to him.

4. Officers who are temporarily officiating in any number in the table, will rank in that number below permanent incumbents.

5. All officers not mentioned in the above table, whose rank is regulated by comparison with rank in the army, to have the same rank with reference to civil servants as is enjoyed by military officers of equal grades.

6. All other persons who may not be mentioned in this table to take rank according to general usage, which is to be explained and determined by the Governor-General in his discretion in case any question shall arise.† When the position of any such person is so determined and notified, it shall be entered in the table in italics, provided he holds an appointment in India.

7. The following will take courtesy rank as shown:—

Consuls-General.—Immediately after Article 35, which includes Brigadiers.

Consuls.—Immediately after Article 38, which includes Colonels.

Vice-Consuls.—Immediately after Article 59, which includes Majors.

Consular officers de carrière will in their respective grades take precedence of consular officers who are not de carrière.

Among themselves Consular Officers will take precedence in their respective grades according to the dates of the Government of India notifications announcing the recognition of their appointments. An officiating incumbent of a grade will rank as an officer of that grade immediately below its permanent incumbents except that when an officer below the substantive grade of Consul officiates as a Consul-General, he will be ranked with Consuls and assigned a place immediately after permanent Consuls.

8. The following may be given, by courtesy, precedence as shown below, provided that they do not hold appointments in India:—

Peers according to their precedence in England.

Knights of the Garter, the Thistle and St. Patrick

Privy Councillors.

Advisers to the Secretary of State for India.

Baronets of England, Scotland, Ireland and the United

Kingdom according to date of Patents.

Knights Grand Cross of the Bath.

Knights Grand Commander of the Star of India.

Knights Grand Cross of St. Michael and St. George.

Knights Grand Commander of the Indian Empire.

Knights Grand Cross of the Royal Victorian Order.

Knights Grand Cross of the Order of the British Empire.

Knights Commander of the Bath.

Knights Commander of the Star of India.

Knights Commander of St. Michael and St. George.

Knights Commander of the Indian Empire.

Knights Commander of the Royal Victorian Order.

Knights Commander of the Order of the British Empire.

Knights Bachelor.

Immediately after Members of the Governor-General's Executive Council, Article 9.

Immediately after Puisne Judges of High Courts, Article 23.

Immediately after the Residents of the Second Class, Article 31.

9. All ladies, unless by virtue of holding an appointment themselves they are entitled to a higher position in the table, to take place according to the rank herein assigned to their respective husbands, with the exception of wives of Peers and of ladies having precedence in England independently of their husbands, and who are not in rank below the daughters of Barons; such ladies to take place according to their several ranks, with reference to such precedence in England immediately after the wives of Members of the Governor-General's Executive Council.

GIVEN at Our Court at St. James's this Ninth day of May in the year of Our Lord One thousand nine hundred and thirty-seven and in the First year of Our Reign.

BY HIS MAJESTY'S COMMAND,

ZETLAND.

† In virtue of the provisions of section 9 (ii) of the Indian Church Act, 1927, a Bishop or Archdeacon who held a bishopric or archdeaconry on the 1st March, 1930, takes rank as follows:—

Bishop of Calcutta, Metropolitan of India, immediately after Article 8.

Bishops of Madras and Bombay, immediately after Article 14.

Bishops of Lucknow and Nagpur, immediately after Article 25.

Bishops (not territorial) under licence from the Crown, immediately after Article 39.

Archdeacon of Lucknow, in Article 42.

OFFICE OF THE SECRETARY OF STATE FOR
BURMA

WHITEHALL, S.W.1

SECRETARY OF STATE,

THE RT. HON. L. S. AMERY, M.P.

PERMANENT UNDER-SECRETARY OF STATE,

* Sir Findlater Stewart, G.C.B., G.C.I.E., C.S.I.

SIR DAVID T. MONTEATH, K.C.B., K.C.M.G., C.V.O., O.B.E.

PARLIAMENTARY UNDER-SECRETARY OF STATE,

THE RT. HON. THE EARL OF LISTOWEL.

DEPUTY UNDER-SECRETARY OF STATE,

SIR JOHN C. WALTON, K.C.I.E., C.B., M.C.

ASSISTANT UNDER-SECRETARY OF STATE,

F. W. H. SMITH, C.I.E.

ADVISERS TO THE SECRETARY OF STATE,

SIR HENRY H. CRAW, K.B.E., C.I.E.

B. W. SWITHINBANK, C.B.E.

Parliamentary Private Secretary to the Secretary of State—Major C. E. Mott-Radclyffe, M.P.

Private Secretary to the Secretary of State—F. F. Turnbull.

Assistant Private Secretary—B. R. Curson.

Political Aide-de-Camp to the Secretary of State—Lieut.-Col. D. de M. S. Fraser, C.S.I., C.I.E.

Assistant to Ditto—A. E. Bamberger.

Private Secretary to Permanent Under-Secretary of State—Miss G. C. M. Lewis.†

Private Secretary to Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State—A. E. Bamberger.

Secretary—W. Johnston, M.C.

Principals—W. T. Annan, S. G. Pennells, H. F. Searle,† P. G. E. Nash, O.B.E.†

Assistant Principal—Miss M. S. Blundell.†

Higher Clerical Officer—Miss W. E. Gant.

HIGH COMMISSIONER.

Section 150 of the Government of Burma Act, 1935, provides that there may be a High Commissioner for Burma in the United Kingdom if the Governor of Burma in his discretion thinks fit so to direct. At present, however, by agreement with the Government of India, the Government of Burma continue to make use of the services of the High Commissioner for India as their agent in London except as regards trade matters.

As regards trade matters, their agent is the Director of the Imperial Institute, S.W.7, Sir H. A. F. Lindsay, K.C.I.E., C.B.E. An exception to this arrangement is that the Timber Adviser to the High Commissioner for India, Sir H. W. A. Watson, acts for the Government of Burma in collaboration with the Imperial Institute in matters affecting timber.

GOVERNMENT OF BURMA

Headquarters of Government—Simla.

Governor—HIS EXCELLENCY THE RT. HON. SIR REGINALD HUGH DORMAN-SMITH,
P.C., G.B.E.

WARRANT OF PRECEDENCE IN BURMA

GEORGE R.I.

GEORGE THE SIXTH, by the Grace of God, of Great Britain, Ireland and the British Dominion beyond the Seas, King, Defender of the Faith, Emperor of India.

To all to whom these presents shall come :—

Whereas it hath been represented unto Us that it is advisable that the rank and precedence of persons holding appointments in Burma should be regulated by Royal Warrant, We do therefore hereby declare that it is Our will and pleasure that the following table be henceforth observed with respect to the rank and precedence of persons hereinafter named, namely :—

  1. 1. Governor of Burma.
  2. 2. Chief Justice.
  3. * 3. Counsellors ; Ministers.
  4. 4. Commander-in-Chief of His Majesty's Naval Forces in the East Indies.
  5. 5. General Officer Commanding.
  6. 6. President of the Senate.
  7. 7. Speaker of the House of Representatives.
  8. 8. Financial Adviser ; Puisne Judges.
  9. 9. Auditor-General ; Chairman, Public Service Commission.
  10. 10. Chief Commissioner of Railways ; Financial Commissioners.
  11. 11. Advocate-General ; Vice-Chancellor, University of Rangoon.
  12. 12. Commissioners of Divisions within their respective charges.

13. Members of the Burma Civil Service (Class I) of 30 years' standing, whose position, but for this article, would not be lower than Article 15 ; Officer Commanding, Rangoon Brigade, within his area.

  1. 14. Brigadiers ; Inspector-General, Frontier Force ; Inspector-General of Police.
  2. 15. Chief Secretary ; Commissioners of Divisions ; Secretary to the Governor.

† 16. Chairman of the Port Commissioners, Rangoon ; Chairman of the Rangoon Development Trust ; Mayor of Rangoon (within Rangoon) ; Secretaries to Government.

17. Accountant-General ; Additional and Joint Secretaries to Government ; Chief Commercial Superintendent, Burma Railways ; Chief Conservator of Forests ; Chief Engineers, including the Chief Engineer, Burma Railways ; Chief Medical Officer, Burma Railways ; Chief Transportation Officer, Burma Railways ; Colonels ; Controller of Railway Accounts ; Director of Agriculture ; Director-General of Posts and Telegraphs ; Director of Public Health ; Director of Public Instruction ; Inspector-General of Civil Hospitals ; Inspector-General of Prisons ; Locomotive and Carriage Superintendent, Burma Railways ; Nautical Adviser and Principal Officer, Mercantile Marine Department.

18. Commissioner of Police, Rangoon ; Municipal Commissioner, Rangoon (within Rangoon).

19. Collector, Rangoon ; Deputy Commissioners of Districts ; District Magistrate, Rangoon Superintendents in the Shan States (within their respective charges).

20. Air Adviser to the Government of Burma ; Chief Inspector of Factories, Explosives and Mines ; Collector of Customs ; Commissioner of Excise ; Commissioner of Income-Tax ; Commissioner of Settlements and Land Records ; Deputy Inspectors-General of Police ; Deputy Secretaries to Government ; Director, Defence Bureau ; Director, Meteorological Department ; Director, Veterinary Services ; District and Sessions Judges—within their respective charges ; members of the Public Service Commission ; Registrar, Co-operative Societies ; Registrar, High Court ; Superintendent, Archaeological Survey ; Superintending Geologist, Geological Department.

21. Chief Auditor, Railway Audit Department ; Chief Education Officer, Federated Shan States ; Chief Public Works Officer, Federated Shan States (within the Federated Shan States) ; Chief-Tele-communication Engineer ; Comptroller of Posts and Telegraphs Accounts ; Conservators of Forests ; Controller of Military Accounts ; Deputy Railway Commissioner and officers of similar

* NOTE.—The Premier ranks in this article senior to the Counsellors and other Ministers, and the Counsellors rank senior to Ministers other than the Premier.

† NOTE.—The Mayor of Rangoon ranks in this article senior to the Chairman of the Port Commissioners, Rangoon, and the Chairman of the Rangoon Development Trust.

status.* Deputy Director-General, Posts and Telegraphs; Examiner, Local Fund Accounts; Lieutenant-Colonels; Members of the Burma Civil Service (Class I) of 18 years' standing, whose position, but for this article, would not be lower than Article 27; Principal Forest Officer, Federated Shan States, within the Federated Shan States; Senior Chaplains of and above 20 years' Government service; Superintending Engineers.†

22. Chief Judge of the Small Cause Court, Rangoon.

23. Administrator-General; Government Inspector of Railways.

24. Deputy Controller of Railway Accounts; Military Secretary to the Governor; Secretaries to the Financial Commissioners.

25. Senior Chaplains other than those already specified.

26. Collector, Rangoon; Deputy Commissioners of Districts; District and Sessions Judges; District Magistrate, Rangoon; Principal Engineer and Ship Surveyor; Settlement Officers; Superintendents in the Shan States; Superintendents of Police, within their own charges.

27. Government Advocates; Officers of Class I Services, including the Class I Railway Services and the Burma Audit and Accounts Service, Class I, and Senior Accounts Officers of the Burma Railways (of 20 years' standing or graded above officers of that standing); Port Officers, within their respective charges, and officers of similar status; Principal, University College, Rangoon; Secretaries to the Legislature; Senior Inspectors of Mines; Superintendents of the Survey of India.

28. Majors; Members of the Burma Civil Service (Class I) of 12 years' standing; Superintendents of Police and Deputy Commissioner of Police, Rangoon, of more than 15, but less than 20 years' standing; Under Secretaries to Government.

29. Assistant Commissioners of Income-Tax; Assistant Superintendents of the Survey of India; Officers of Class I Services including the Class I Railway Services and the Burma Audit and Accounts Service Class I, and Senior Accounts Officers of the Burma Railways (of 10 years' standing in the service or graded above officers of that standing); Superintendents of Police and the Deputy Commissioner of Police, Rangoon, of less than 15 years' standing; Supervisor of Jail Industries.

30. Assistant Directors of Public Health; Assistant Directors of Public Instruction; Assistant Secretaries to Government; Chemical Examiner; Chief Inspector of Boilers; Civil Surgeons not belonging to the Indian Medical Service; Deputy Registrars of Co-operative Societies; Divisional Engineers, Telegraphs; Divisional Forest Officers; Executive Engineers; Inspectors of Factories Senior Grade; Inspectors of Mines; Judges of the Small Cause Court, Rangoon; Officers of Class II Services and Assistant Accounts Officers drawing the maximum pay of the time-scale or upwards; Officers of District rank, Burma Railways; Postmaster, Rangoon—within Rangoon; Public Prosecutors; Superintendent, Government Printing and Stationery; Superintendents of Excise; Superintendents of Central Jails not belonging to the Indian Medical Service.

1. The entries in the above table, which are in alphabetical order in each article, apply exclusively to the persons entered therein, and while regulating their relative precedence with each other do not give them any precedence over members of the non-official community resident in Burma, who shall take their place according to usage.

2. Officers in the above table will take precedence in order of the numbers of the entries. Those included in one number will take precedence inter se according to the date of entry into that number with the exception of officers of the Defence Services, who rank inter se in accordance with their seniority.

3. When an officer holds more than one position in the table he will be entitled to the highest position accorded to him.

4. Officers who are temporarily officiating in any number in the table will rank in that number below permanent incumbents.

5. All officers not mentioned in the above table, whose rank is regulated by comparison with rank in the army, will have the same rank with reference to civil servants as is enjoyed by military officers of equal grades.

6. An officer serving under the Government of India whose services are placed by that Government at the disposal of the Government of Burma, will, if his rank in the Indian Warrant of Precedence is relatively higher than the rank assigned in the above table to the appointment held by him in Burma, take rank in whatever article in the above table most nearly corresponds to the article in which he is ranked in India.

7. All other persons who may not be mentioned in this table will take rank according to general usage, which is to be explained and determined by the Governor in his discretion in case any question shall arise. When the position of any such person is so determined and notified, it shall be entered in the table in italics, provided he holds an appointment in Burma.

* NOTE.—Officers of similar status are:—Deputy Locomotive Superintendent, Deputy Carriage and Wagon Superintendent, Superintendent of Stores, Deputy Chief Engineer, Deputy Traffic Managers, Commercial Research Officer, and Statistical and Organisation Officer.

† NOTE.—Architectural, Electrical and Sanitary Specialist Officers will take precedence in accordance with the rank in the Public Works Department fixed for their appointments but junior to all Public Works Department officers of the corresponding rank.

‡ NOTE.—The Nautical Surveyor and Assistant Sea Transport Officer is an officer of similar status.

8. The following will take courtesy rank, as shown:—

Bishop of Rangoon.—Immediately after Article 9.

Consuls-General.—Immediately after Article 14, which includes Brigadiers.

Consuls.—Immediately after Article 17, which includes Colonels.

Vice-Consuls.—Immediately after Article 28, which includes Majors.

Consular officers de carrière will in their respective grades take precedence of consular officers who are not de carrière.

Among themselves Consular Officers will take precedence in their respective grades according to the dates of the Government of Burma notifications announcing the recognition of their appointments. An officiating incumbent of a grade will rank as an officer of that grade immediately below its permanent incumbents except that when an officer below the substantive grade of Consul officiates as a Consul-General he will be ranked with Consuls and assigned a place immediately after permanent Consuls.

9. The following may be given, by courtesy, precedence as shown below, provided that they do not hold appointments in Burma:—

Peers according to their precedence in England.

Knights of the Garter, the Thistle and St. Patrick.

Privy Councillors.

Advisers to the Secretary of State for Burma.

Baronets of England, Scotland, Ireland and the United

Kingdom, according to date of Patents.

Knights Grand Cross of the Bath.

Knights Grand Commander of the Star of India.

Knights Grand Cross of St. Michael and St. George.

Knights Grand Commander of the Indian Empire.

Knights Grand Cross of the Royal Victorian Order.

Knights Grand Cross of the Order of the British Empire

Knights Commander of the Bath.

Knights Commander of the Star of India.

Knights Commander of St. Michael and St. George.

Knights Commander of the Indian Empire.

Knights Commander of the Royal Victorian Order.

Knights Commander of the Order of the British Empire.

Knights Bachelor.

Immediately after Ministers and
Councillors, Article 3.

Immediately after Puisne
Judges, Article 8.

Immediately after Commis-
sioners within their charges,
Article 12.

10. All ladies, unless by virtue of holding an appointment themselves they are entitled to a higher position in the table, will take place according to the rank herein assigned to their respective husbands, with the exception of wives of Peers and of ladies having precedence in England independently of their husbands, and who are not in rank below the daughters of Barons; such ladies to take place according to their several ranks, with reference to such precedence in England, immediately after the wives of Ministers and Councillors.

GIVEN at Our Court of St. James's this First day of December in the year of Our Lord One Thousand nine hundred and thirty-eight and in the Second year of our Reign.

BY HIS MAJESTY'S COMMAND,

ZETLAND.

ALPHABETICAL HONOURS LIST

(a) This list includes the name of:

(1) all persons appointed to the Orders of the Star of India and Indian Empire and to the Imperial Order of the Crown of India ;
(2) Knights Bachelor created on account of services in India or Burma ;
(3) Civil officials and non-officials of India and Burma appointed to other than Indian Orders ; and
(4) Military Officers of the Indian and Burma Services awarded a Knighthood in other than Indian Orders.

RECORD OF SERVICES

The following List includes Officers—

(1) of the Establishments of :—

  • (a) The Secretary of State for India,
  • (b) the Secretary of State for Burma,
  • (c) the High Commissioner for India,
  • (d) the Auditor of Indian Home Accounts ;

(2) of the

  • (a) All-India Services,
  • (b) Indian Political Service,
  • (c) Indian Ecclesiastical Establishment,
  • (d) Burma Class I Services,
  • (e) Burma Ecclesiastical Establishment ;

(3) in other Civil employment in India or in Burma whose substantive pay (exclusive of overseas pay) is at least Rs. 1000 a month.

Owing to the need for economy in labour and materials, it has not been found practicable to include in full particulars of the above classes of officers who retired between January, 1935, and January, 1940, or to retain the names of more than a limited number of officers who retired before January, 1935. The records of a number of officers eligible for inclusion have, for the same reason, been held over.

NOTE.—As a general rule, postings to officiating appointments are not shown.

EXPLANATION OF ABBREVIATIONS

Acad.—Academy. Expedn.—Expedition. N.W. Rly.—North Western Railway.

Acctnt.—Accountant.
Accts.—Accounts. F.C.H.—Fellow of Cooper's Hill College. Offd.—Officiated.
Addl.—Additional. Fin.—Finance. Offg.—Officiating.
Addn.—Addition. Finl.—Financial. Offr.—Officer.
A.G.G.—Agent to Governor-General. For.—Foreign. Optg.—Operating.
Agric.—Agriculture. F.S.S. { Federated Shan States
Fellow of the Royal Statistical Society.		 Parly.—Parliamentary.
Agricl.—Agricultural. Photo.—Photographic.
Appr.—Apprentice. P.M.O.—Principal Medical Officer.
Apptd.—Appointed. Pol.—Political.

A.T.C.L.—Associate of Trinity Coll., London. Gen.—General. Prest.—President.

Govt.—Government. Proby.—Probationary.

Prov.—Provinces.

Cantonmt.—Cantonment. Publn.—Publication.

Cmd.—Command. I.A.S.—Indian Agricultural Service. R.C.—Royal Commission.
Col.—Colonel. I.C.S.—Indian Civil Service. Registrn.—Registration.
Coll.—College, Collegiate. I.E.S.—Indian Educational Service. Regr.—Registrar.
Collr.—Collector. I.F.S.—Indian Forest Service. Regt.—Regiment.
Comm.—Commission. Inspn.—Inspection. Rest.—Resident.
Commr.—Commissioner. Inspr.—Inspector. Retd.—Retired.
Comptr.—Comptroller. Inst.—Institute. Rev.—Revenue.
Confd.—Confirmed. Instn.—Institution. R.I.E. Coll.—Royal Indian Engineering College, Cooper's Hill.
Contr.—Controller. Instn.—Instruction.
Consvr.—Conservator. I.P.—Indian Police. Sch.—School.
Cov.—Covenanted. I.P.S.—Indian Political Service. Sec.—Secretary.
Corpn.—Corporation. Irrign.—Irrigation. Sectt.—Secretariat.
D.A.G.—Deputy Accountant General. I.S.E.—Indian Service of Engineers. Senr.—Senior.

Depd.—Deputed. Subord.—Subordinate.
Depn.—Deputation. S.p.t.—Substantive pro tempore.

Deptl.—Departmental. Suptg.—Superintending.
Dir.—Director. Jud.—Judicial. Surgn.—Surgeon.
Divl.—Divisional. Junr.—Junior.
Divn.—Division.
D.M.O.—District Medical Officer. K.H.P.—King's Hon. Physician. T.C.E. Coll.—Thomason Civil Engineering College, Roorkee.
E.B. Rly.—Eastern Bengal Railway. K.H.S.—King's Hon. Surgeon. Topogrl.—Topographical.
Ecclesl.—Ecclesiastical. K-i-H. Medal.—Kaiser-i-Hind Medal for public service in India. Transfd.—Transferred.
Edn.—Edition. Transld.—Translated.
Educ.—Educated. Transln.—Translation.

RECORD OF SERVICES

* For further particulars see 1940 I.O. & B.O. List.

ABBAS ALI KHAN, SAHIB BAHADUR, M.A., LL.B. (b. 19th April, 1891).—Joined the service as offg. ch. presdy. magte., Madras, 26th May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1928; retd. March, 1943.

ABBASI, MUHAMMAD WAHAJUDDIN, Indian C.S. (ji. mag., United Provs.) (b. 4th July, 1905).—Educ. at Lucknow University and Jesus College, Cambridge; joined the service 11th Oct., 1930, arrived in India Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. sec. infm. dept., Dec., 1939; sec. ditto, Sept., 1942.

ABBOTT, STUART EVELYN, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Aug., 1910).—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. in 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offr., July, 1935; sec. to the Premier, Sept., 1939; O.B.E., June, 1944.

ABDUL ALI, ABUL FAIZ MUHAMMAD KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., late Bengal Civil Service (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

ABDUL ALI, HAMID MOIZUDIN, B.A., late Indian C.S. Sind (b. 5th Jan., 1880).—Retd., Feb., 1937.*

ABDUL ALI KHAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 7th Nov., 1904).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; dep. consvr., Nov., 1931.

ABDUL AZIZ, KHAN BAHADUR, MAIN, C.B.E., M.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 27th Jan., 1881).—Retd., May, 1936.*

ABDUL AZIZ, MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Service of Engineers, United Provs. (b. 13th Dec., 1884).—Retd., March, 1940.*

ABDUL GHAFOOR KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., M.Sc. (Delhi), M.Sc. (Tech.), Manch., M.I.Mech.E., M.I.E.E., M.I.E. (Ind.) (dep. dir. inspection) (b. 2nd Aug., 1893).—Joined service 14th Feb., 1921, as temp. engr., P.W.D., Delhi; March, 1923, elec. inspr. (in addn.), asst. dir. of insp. Indian Stores Dept., June, 1923; contr. of insp., Apl., 1930; dep. dir. of insp., Apl., 1930; dir. of insp., dept. of supply, June, 1941.

ABDUL GHAFFUR, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 3rd Nov., 1921; asst. engr. (prov.), Nov., 1922; confd. Jan., 1934; apptd. to the I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; exec. engr., Jan., 1937; on deptn. to Bahawalpur State as suptg. engr., Oct., 1941.

ABDUL HAFEEZ, MUHAMMAD, M.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 30th Sept., 1883).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

ABDUL HAMID, KHAN BAHADUR, B.Sc., M.B., B.S. (Alld.), D.P.H. (Eng.), Public Health Dept., United Provs. (asst. dir. of Public Health) (b. Apl., 1893).—Joined the service as health officer, 1 June, 1926.

ABDUL HAMID KHAN, Indian Forest Service (b. 28th Oct., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Nov., 1931; dep. consvr., Nov., 1936; systematic entomologist, forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Sept., 1943.

ABDUL KAIYUM KHAN, KHAN, Indian

Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 5th June, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 12th April, 1920; offg. supt., June, 1925; confd. Nov., 1926.

ABDUL KARIM, KHAN BAHADUR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., Bihar) (b. 26th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1917; asst. engr., Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr., June, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1927; Khan Bahadur, July, 1940; suptg. engr., Bihar.

ABDUL MAJID KHAN, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service, Judl. Branch (b. 12th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

ABDUL QADIR, SIR, KHAN BAHADUR, Barr. Apptd. addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, 20th Feb., 1930, Feb., 1932; and Feb., 1934; member of Council of India, July, 1934; adviser to the sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937, to July, 1939; temp. member of Govr. Gen.'s executive council, Oct., 1939, to Dec., 1939.

ABDUL QAIYUM AHMAD KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1912, as inspr. of police; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Apl., 1935; retd., 27th Sept., 1942.

ABDUL, QAIYUM KHAN, KHAN, BARA KHAN KHEL, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 5th July, 1898).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Apl., 1920; confd., Feb., 1922; supt., Nov., 1926.

ABDUL RASHID, MIAN, M.A., Judl. Dept., Punjab (judge, high ct., Lahore).—Apptd. asst. legal remembr., 5th March, 1925; offg. judge, high ct., Lahore, May, 1933; addl. ditto, Oct., 1933; judge, ditto, March, 1937.

ABDULLAH ABU SAIYID, KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. June, 1885).—Apptd. prinpl., govt. coll., Madrassah, Sylhet, and profr., Murarichand coll., Sylhet, 5th Feb., 1921; prinpl., Murarichand coll., July, 1933; retd., June, 1940.

ABDULLAH, MUHAMMAD ABOO (see Muhammad Aboo Abdullah).

ABDUR RAHIM, SIR, K.C.S.I., M.A., Barr.—Apptd. judge, Madras High Court, 24th July, 1908; member Royal commn. on public services in India, 1912; Kt., June, 1919; member of govrs.' exec. council, Bengal, Jan., 1921, to Dec., 1925; K.C.S.I., June, 1925; presdt., Indian legis. assembly, 1935; author of 'Principles of Muhammadan Jurisprudence.'

ABDUR RAHIM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 14th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at Punjab University and Peterhouse, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offr., Feb., 1936; office of rural reconstrn., Oct., 1938, to Mar., 1940; dir. of panchayats, Mar., 1940.

ABDUR RAHIM KHAN, MAJ., I.A. (Indian Political Service) (b. 9th Nov., 1898).—First commn. dated 17th July, 1920; in milty. employ prior to 31st May, 1925; asst. collr., Jhansi, June, 1925; asst. mag., Moradabad, Nov., 1925; asst. commr., Bannu, Apl. 1926; ditto, Thal, Nov., 1927; ditto, Kohat, Nov., 1928; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Feb., 1929; 1st asst. to resdt., Baroda, May, 1929;

asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Apl., 1930; city mag., Peshawar, Apl., 1931; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar and Hazara, Nov., 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Dera Ismail Khan, March, 1934; ditto, Peshawar, Oct., 1935; pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Nov., 1937; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Feb., 1938; pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1938; addl. sec. to N.W.F.P. govt. infm. dept., Sept., 1941; ditto devel., Dec., 1943; pol. agent, Loralai, Oct., 1944.

ABDUR RAHIM SAHIB, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service, Madras (b. 16th July, 1883).—Retd., July, 1938.*

ABDUR RAHMAN, KHAN BAHADUR KHWAJA, O.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., D.Ph., late Dept. of Public Health, Punjab (b. 21st Aug., 1881).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

ABDUR RAHMAN, KHAN BAHADUR, SHEIKH, late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 13th Feb., 1884).—Retired, Feb., 1939.*

ABDUR, RASHID KHAN, K.B., B.A. Indian Police (Supt.) (b. 2nd May, 1893); joined the service as depy. supt. of police on 2nd Jan., 1918; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Bengal, Dec., 1936.

ABDUR RAHMAN, SIR MUHAMMED, KHAN BAHADUR (b. 5th Oct., 1888).—(Judge, high ct., Lahore), Dean of faculty of law, Delhi Univ., 1927-34, vice-chancellor, Delhi Univ., 1930-34; apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, Aug., 1937; ditto, Lahore, Feb., 1943; vice-chancellor, Punjab Univ.

ABDUS SALAM SAIYID, KHAN BAHADUR, Income-tax Dept., Madras (asst. commr. of income-tax) (b. 15th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service, May, 1914; held non-gaz. appts.; inspector, 4th grade (Salt, etc., Dept.), Apl., 1920; income-tax offr., Apl., 1924; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, Dec., 1935; on for. serv., Hyderabad State, Feb., 1943.

ABDUSAMAD KHAN, M.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instn., Bihar) (b. 1st Apl., 1891).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1919; inspr. of schools, May, 1922; prof. of chemistry, science coll., Patna, Jan., 1928; offg. princpl., Greer Bhumihar Coll., Muzaffarpur, Jan., 1936; dep. dir. of public instn., Aug., 1936; dir., ditto, Jan., 1941.

ABELL, GEORGE EDMOND BRACKENBURY, O.B.E., Indian Civil Service (dep. pte. sec. to Viceroy) (b. 22nd June, 1904).—Educated at Marlborough and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928, arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. regr., co-op. soc., March, 1932; confd., July, 1932; settlement offr., Oct., 1935; sec. to govr., Punjab, May, 1941; dep. pte. sec. to the Viceroy, Apl., 1943; O.B.E., June, 1943.

ABERNETHY, JOHN, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 12th June, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 25th Nov., 1930; confd., Sept., 1932; off. dist. supt., June, 1933; confd., Feb., 1941.

ABIGAIL, REGINALD PHILIP, late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 11th Aug., 1892).—Educ. at Trent Coll., Derbyshire, and Bedford Modern Sch.; on mil. service from May, 1918, to March, 1923; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under secry. to govt., April, 1925; dep. commr., Jan., 1931; dep. commr., Dec., 1940; retd., 1945.

ABRAHAM, KILAKETHOLOGICAL MATHAI, B.A., late Indian Finance dept. (b. 23rd Nov., 1885).—Joined the service 11th Feb., 1910; asst. acct.-genl., Nov., 1915; ditto and currency officer,

Burma, Aug., 1924; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, March, 1927; dep. ditto, March, 1928; exr., local fund accts., Bombay, Oct., 1929; asst. dir., army audit, W.C., Nov., 1930; with finl. commr., rlwys., Apl., 1931; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, Apl., 1932; offg. acct.-genl., Bengal, June, 1933; offg. govt. exr. of accts., M. and S.M. and S.I. rlvs., Feb., 1935; dep. ch. accts. officer, E.I. rly, Aug., 1937; fin. adviser and ch. accts. offr., E.B. rly., Jan., 1939; retd., Nov., 1940.

ABU HAMID MUHAMMAD SHAMSOD-DOHA, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 1st Dec., 1901).—Joined the service 3rd Aug., 1924, apptd. supt., Bengal, March, 1937; awarded King's Police Medal, Feb., 1937.

ABU MUHAMMAD ARSHAD ALI, KHAN BAHADUR, MAULVI, B.A., Dept. of co-op. soc., Bengal (regr.) (b. 16th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service 24th Feb., 1909; asst. regr., May, 1924; regr., Oct., 1934.

ABU MUHAMMAD, S., K.B., M.A. (member, pub. serv. commn., United Provs.), apptd., April, 1937.

ABUL FAEZ MUJIBOR RAHMAN, M.Sc., (Cal.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 23rd Sept., 1897).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1923; arrived 10th Nov., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. & dep. collr., Nov., 1924; offg. dist. & sess. judge, Apl., 1929; addl. ditto, Oct., 1932; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1935.

ABUL FAZL ABDUL HAMID, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 17th Jan., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 15th Jan., 1922; supt., Feb., 1929; O.B.E., June, 1933.

ABUL MUZAFFAR AHMED, B.L., B.C.L., Barr.; late Bengal Civil Service (b. 21st June, 1883).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

ABUL QASIM, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian Police (supt., United Provinces) (b. 24th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service as dep. supt. on probn., 2nd Nov., 1908; confd., Nov., 1910; offg. supt., Sept., 1911; confd., July, 1931.

ACHARYA, BEJOY KRISHNA, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st May, 1912); educated at Hare School, Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., University College, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London.—Joined the service 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magtr. and dep. collr., March, 1938; dep. contr. distribn., civ. supplies, Calcutta, Nov., 1943.

ACHARYA, PRASANNA KUMAR (see Prasanna Kumar A.).

ACHARYA, S. V., B.A., B.L., Jud. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 26th June, 1886).—Joined service, 25th March, 1914; sub-judge, August, 1932; acting dist. and sess. judge, July, 1935; confd., August, 1938.

ACHARYA, SIR T. VIJAYARAGHAVA (see Vijayaraghava Acharya, Sir T.).

ACHARYA, V. T. KRISHNAMA (see Krishnama Acharya, V. T.).

ACHESON, JAMES GLASGOW, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (Indian Political Service) (b. 24th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived 22nd Nov., 1913 and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; temp. asst. pol. agent and asst. commr.; Quetta-Pishin, April, 1917;

cantonment mag., Quetta, Nov., 1917; asst. pol. agent in Kalat, and pol. agent in charge of Bolan Pass, Oct., 1918; on special duty in connection with Indo-Afghan Conference, March, 1920; member of British Mission to Kabul, Dec., 1920; dep. commr.; Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1923; pol. agent, Tochi, Oct., 1924; pol. agent, North Waziristan, Oct., 1925; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., May, 1927; confd., Dec., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1929; dep. commr., Peshawar, Apr., 1932; rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Apr., 1934; offg. for secty. to govt. of India for. dept., July, 1935; offg. resdt., Waziristan, Nov., 1935; pol. resdt., N.W.F.P., Apr., 1937; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1939; again, Apr., 1941; A.G.G., resdt. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, March, 1941; advr. to the govr., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1942; resdt. in Kashmir, Nov., 1943.

ACLAND, RT. REV. CANON RICHARD DYKE, M.A. (b. 3rd Oct., 1881).—Apptd. Bishop of Bombay, 13th Sept., 1929.

ACOITT, ARTHUR SYDNEY VERNON, M.A., late Indian C.S., Bombay (b. 31st July, 1888).—Retd., July, 1939.*

ACTON, HAMPDEN ANTONY GREER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 30th July, 1914).—Educ. at Eton and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

ACTON, THEODORE JOHN CHICHESTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 18th Aug., 1886).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, Keble Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911; and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; 2nd asst. to agent to the govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Jan., 1916; served as asst. pol. agent, and pol. agent, Sibi, from Sept., 1916; ditto, Quetta, June, 1920; jt. mag., Nov., 1920; actg. dep. commr., March, 1921; actg. mag. and collr., April, 1925; confd., Nov., 1928.

ADAIR, ARTHUR ROBIN, B.A., Indian C.S., (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 10th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Sch., Wimborne, and Emmanuel College, W Sch., Wimborne, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; spec. offr., pol. dept., Apr., 1941, to March, 1944.

ADAM, JOHN HUNTER, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Police (N.W.F.P.) (b. 1st June, 1882).—Retd., June, 1937.*

ADAM, RONALD WILLIAM LUMSDEN, Indian Police (b. 9th May, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1938.

ADAMI, SIR LEONARD CHRISTIAN, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 15th March, 1874).—Retd., March, 1931*

ADAMS, ARTHUR GEORGE, late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 16th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 29th Nov., 1913; dist. supt., May, 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1935; retd., 23rd Dec., 1942.

ADAMS, FREDERICK JAMES, Office of High Commr. for India (secty., general dept.) (b. 18th Feb., 1885).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 14th April, 1905; transfed. to Jud. and Public Dept., Feb., 1911; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to Jan., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1919; staff clerk, Apr., 1921; administrative officer, April, 1922; transfed. to Office of High

Commr. for India, asst. principal, Apr., 1922; principal exec. officer, Jan., 1924; suptg. ditto, April, 1927; secty., general dept., Oct., 1938.

ADAMS, HUGH BENEDICT, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (elec. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 27th Feb., 1893).—Apptd. asst. elec. engr., N.W. rly., 1st Dec., 1922; elec. engr., Nov., 1931; M.B.E., June, 1942.

ADAMS, JOHN EUSTACE, Indian Police (b. 9th Feb., 1915).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 16th Dec., 1934.

ADAMS, PHILIP FRANCIS, M.B.E., B.Com., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 18th July, 1914).—Educ. at Mitcham Secondary Sch. and London School of Economics; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; M.B.E., June, 1944.

ADAVI, VISHNUTIRTH NARAYANACHARYA, B.A. (Hons.); Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service, 7th March, 1925; currency officer, Bombay, Feb., 1935; serv. placed temp. at disp. of Reserve Bank from Apr., 1935; asst. dir. of audit, def. services, W. and Lahore dists., Nov., 1940; dy. C.A.O., American purchase, Sept., 1941.

ADDIS, FRANK LESLIE, Office of High Commissioner for India (higher clerical officer store dept.) (b. 25th Oct., 1889).—Educ. at Strand Sch., King's Coll.; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 14th Oct., 1909; and attd. to Store Dept.; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Dec., 1917; transfed. to office of High Commissioner for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1926.

ADDISON, SIR JAMES, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 13th Nov., 1879).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

ADDISON, LEWIS PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Bromsgrove and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; comdt., border mil. police, Dera Ghazi Khan, Nov., 1936; sub. divl. offr., Oct., 1939; asst. settlement offr., Jan., 1940; addl. dep. commr., June, 1940.

ADLARD, LIEUT.-COL. LEONARD STANLEY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 3rd Dec., 1893).—Joined the service 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; services placed at disposal of the Govt. of India, Nov., 1934; principi., Govt. sch. of engrg., Rasul, Aug., 1937; on mil. serv., Oct., 1939, to Nov., 1944; suptg. engr. (subst.), Apr., 1944; hon. a.d.c. to the Viceroy, June, 1944.

ADVANI, PRITAMDAS BHOJRAJ, O.B.E., M.Sc., Dept. of Industries, Bombay (dir.) (b. 21st May, 1891).—Appointed Director of Industries, 1st Aug., 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

ADVANI, RADHAKRISHNA TAHILRAM, M.B., B.S., Maj., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bombay (supt.) (b. 26th Jan., 1796).—Date of first commn., 1st March, 1922; in mil. employ until and Sept., 1928; sup., Jail Dept., Bombay, July, 1929; supt., Yeravda Cent. Prison, March, 1938.

AFZAL, LESLIE (named changed to Gregory, Leslie).

AGA HILALY, Indian C.S. (asst. magte. and collr., Bengal) (b. 20th May, 1911).—Educ. at Madras and King's Coll., Cambridge, arrived in India, 18th Feb., 1937, and served in Bengal as

asst. magte. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1938; under secty., fin. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1940; confd., Aug., 1940; under sec. educ. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1941; dep. contr., civi supplies Dec., 1943.

AGARWAL, KEDAR NATH (see Kedar Nath Agarwal).

AGARWAL, PREM PRAKASH, M.Sc., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 30th Nov., 1917).—Educ. at D.A.V. High Sch. and Govt. Jubilee Inter-Coll., Lucknow, Lucknow Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. after seln. in Aug., 1940, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

AGARWALA, SIR CLIFFORD MONMOHAN BARR (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, 11th July, 1933; kt., June, 1943.

AGGARWAL, KUNDAN LAL (see Kundan Lal, A.).

AGGARWALA, RAMRAKHA, M.A. (Punj.), M.A. (Delhi), Indian Audit Dept. (b. 29th Oct., 1899).—Joined the service 21st March, 1925; asst. acct. genl., Punjab, Apl., 1936; asst. acct. genl., Bombay, Jan., 1939; dep. acct. genl., Punjab, Nov., 1940.

AGHA HAIDER, SYED, Barr.-at-Law, late Judl. Dept., Punjab.—retd., July, 1936.*

AGHA, HASSANALLY GHULAMALLY KHAN (b. 1st Jan., 1895).—Cmfd. as diw. and sess. judge, Sind, Dec., 1938; adv. genl. for Sind, Nov., 1943.

AGHA, MUHAMMAD AKRAM (see Muhammad Akram, A.).

AGRAWAL, PITAM CHAND (see Pitam Chand, A.).

AHAD, ABDUL (see Abdul Ahad).

AHMAD, A. M. (see Abdul Muzaffar Ahmad).

AHMAD, ABU MUSA SHARFUDDIN, B.A., Indian Police, Bengal (b. 1st March, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn. after exam. of 1940.

AHMAD, ALI (see Ali Ahmad).

AHMAD, GHULAM (see Ghulam Ahmad).

AHMAD, IQBAL (see Iqbal Ahmad).

AHMAD, ISLAM (see Islam Ahmad).

AHMAD, KAMALUDDIN (see KAMALUDDIN Ahmad).

AHMAD, MOHAMMAD BASHIR, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 23rd Oct., 1899).—Joined the service 1st March, 1925; dep. comptr., Revs., Sept. 1936; ditto. Punjab, May, 1938; N.W.F.P., Nov., 1933; dep. acct. genl., Cent. exr. local fd. accts., Punjab Oct., 1940.

AHMAD, MIRZA MUZAFFAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 28th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept. 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offr., May, 1941.

AHMED, ABU SAYED MOHIUDDIN, Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 1st March, 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Feb., 1940.

AHMAD, MUHAMMAD BASHEER, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provinces) (b. 11th March, 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad, Aligarh Muslim Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in the United Provinces as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr., March, 1930; offg. sess. and sub-judge, May, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1934; confd., Jan., 1940; author of 'Problem of Rural Uplift in India.'

AHMAD, MUHI UDDIN, Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 1st Feb., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Feb., 1940.

AHMAD, MUJUDDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 1st Oct., 1909).—Educ. at Aminabad High Sch., Lucknow, Government High Sch., Sitapur, Government Jubilee Collegiate Sch., Lucknow, Univ. of Lucknow, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1936; under secty. to the resdt., Eastern States, Nov., 1938; addtl. asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, March, 1940; asst. commr., Bannu, April, 1941; asst. commr., Nowshera, Oct., 1941; dep. sec. to govt., N.W.F.P. (rev.), May, 1942; second sec. to agent genl. for India in Washington, Dec., 1944.

AHMAD, MUSHTAQ (see Mushtaq Ahmad).

AHMAD, NASIR, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 14th June, 1905).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1935; dep. secty, medl. etc. deptt., Punjab, Jan., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

AHMAD, NAZIR (see Nazir Ahmad).

AHMAD, NIAZ, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 1st May, 1914).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; joined the service 12th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; tfd. to Orissa, June, 1940; sugar cane inspr., Oct., 1939; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1940.

AHMAD, RAHIMUDDIN (see Rahimuddin Ahmad).

AHMAD, RIAZ (see Riaz Ahmad).

AHMAD SAID KHAN, MUHAMMAD, SIR, NAWAB OF CHHITARI, K.C.I.E., M.B.E.—M.B.E., June, 1918; C.I.E. June, 1921; apptd. member of govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., 3rd Jan., 1926; K.C.I.E., June, 1928, delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s council, Jan., 1932; govr., United Provs., Apl. to Nov., 1933; minister of home affairs, United Provs., Apl. to July, 1937.

AHMAD, SAIYED AMIN, B.A., late Indian Civil Service (Bihar), (b. 1st March, 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Patna and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. coll. and mag.; jt. mag., Dec., 1928; retd., 29th Aug., 1944.

AHMAD, SHAMSUDDIN (see Shamsuddin Ahmad).

AHMAD, SIRAJUDDIN (see Sirajuddin Ahmad).

AHMAD, SYED MUSHTAQ, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th July, 1901).—Joined the service 25th Feb., 1926; dep. acct. gen., United Provs., Sept., 1936; asst. acct. genl., posts and telegr., Delhi, Nov., 1937; deputy acct. gen., posts and telegr., Delhi, Apr., 1938; dep. comptr., N.W.F.P., Apr., 1939.
