BARLOW, HENRY ARTHUR NORTHEY, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 11th Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Marlborough and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived, 18th Nov., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. coll. and mag.; asst. commr., March, 1931; jt. mag., March, 1931; under secty. to A.G.G. in States of W.I., Dec., 1933; vice-consul, Kashgar, Oct., 1934; and again, Aug., 1936; city mag., Peshawar, Dec., 1936; secty. to Resident for Eastern States, Nov., 1937; under secty., home dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1940; H.M.'s vice-consul, Zahidan, Oct., 1941; ditto, Meshed, Feb., 1942; ditto, Kerman, Sept., 1942; sec. to resdt., Cent. India., Sept., 1944.

BARNARD, ALBERT ALFRED, O.B.E., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Assam) (b. 5th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1914; asst. engr., Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1923.

BARNARD, ALBERT WILLIAM STANDFIELD, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 1st Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Oct., 1920; offg. supt., March, 1923; confd., Apl., 1929; exec. offcr., regional transp. auth., March, 1940.

BARNE, REV. GEORGE DUNSFORD, M.A., C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 6th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1908; princpl. and chaplain, The Lawrence Royal Military Sch., Sanawar, Oct., 1912; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1923; Bishop of Lahore, Apl., 1932; vice-chancellor of Punjab univ., Jan., 1936.

BARNES, SIR GEORGE STAPYLTON, K.C.B., K.C.S.I. (late member, Viceroy's Exec. Council).—Retd., Apl., 1921.*

BARNES, REV. JOHN, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 7th Jan., 1898).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Bombay, 25th Feb., 1928; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1930.

BARNES, PAUL, Indian Police (b. 5th Jan., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 26th Nov., 1936; confd., Jan., 1939.

BARNS, C. J., All India Radio (News Editor, Central News Orgn.) (b. Oct., 1902).—Joined the service, 9th Sept., 1937.

BARON, CECIL GEORGE, late Indian State Railways (b. 12th Dec., 1881).—Retd., Dec., 1936.*

BARR, ARTHUR GUY, O.B.E., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 25th July, 1877).—Retd., July, 1932.*

BARR, DAVID COURTNEY, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 30th July, 1914).—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, and Imperial Coll. of Science and Technology, London; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937;

confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; cane inspr., Dec., 1940; jt. mag. and dep. collr., served under central govt., 1942, to March, 1944.

BARRETT, HAMILTON BERESFORD, late Indian Forest Service (b. 4th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1924; ret'd., Sept., 1943.

BARRETT, PAULIN FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (asst. magte. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 19th July, 1913).—Educ. at Ald. Newton's Boys' Sch., Leicester, Univ. Coll., Leicester, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Jan., 1937; arrived in India, March, 1937, and served in U.P. as asst. magte., and collr.

BARRETT, LEONARD MILLER FRANK, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 2nd March, 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; on depn. to Orissa, Apl. to Oct., 1936; suptg. engr., Apl., 1944.

BARRETTO, WILLIAM LOUIS, O.B.E., B.A., late Burma Commn. (b. 5th Aug., 1882).—Ret'd., Aug., 1937.*

BARRINGTON, JAMES, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 15th Aug., 1911).—Educ. at Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. settlement officer, Oct., 1939; dep. sec., fin. dept., 1944.

BARRON, DOUGLAS SHIELD, Indian C.S., C.I.E. (addtl. secty. (genl.), United Provs.) (b. 18th March, 1904).—Educ. at Archbishop Holgate's Sch., Barnsley, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 20th Dec., 1926, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; addl. dep. secty. (genl.), United Provs., Aug., 1939; addtl. secty., ditto, March, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

BARRON, HARRIE, B.A., B.A.I., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 5th Feb., 1890).—Ret'd., Apl., 1939.*

BARROW, JOHN ROTHNEY, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 25th Oct., 1891).—Ret'd., Sept., 1932.*

BARSON, ALFRED GEOFFREY, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag.) (b. 24th Aug., 1916).—Educ. at Dulwich and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 23rd Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BARTELS, REV. REGINALD CAIN, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 18th Nov., 1883).—Apptd. temporary chaplain, 6th June, 1921; junior chaplain, Apl., 1924; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1934.

BARTER, ELDER GAUL, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 9th Jan., 1892).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 6th Dec., 1916, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1928; confd., Sept., 1930; ret'd., 4th Aug., 1943.

BARTLETT, FREDERICK ROBERT, late India Office (b. 8th Aug., 1871).—Ret'd., Aug., 1936.*

BARTLEY, SIR CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Dec. 1882).—Educ. at St. Col-

umba's Coll., Londonderry, Methodist Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived 10th Dec., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., and dep. collr., Sept., 1914; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1921; transf. to Assam as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1930; supt. and remembr. of legal affairs and secty. to govt., legal dept., Bengal, Nov., 1931; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apl., 1932; Apl. and Nov., 1933; confd., March, 1934; kt., June, 1942; ret'd., Dec., 1942.

BARTLEY, FREDERICK DOUGLAS, C.B.E., late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 1st June, 1889).—Ret'd., Aug., 1936.*

BARTLEY, HERBERT GWYTHER, Indian Police (dep. insp. genl., Assam) (b. 18th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 6th Dec., 1912; supt., Dec., 1923; offg. dep., inspr. genl., Assam, Jan., 1936; conf., Dec., 1937; Ind. police medal, 1944.

BARTLEY, JOHN, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., LL.B., Barr., Indian C.S. (addtl. secty. to the govt. of India, legisl. dept.) (b. 2nd March, 1886).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; pol. agent, Hill Tippera (temp.), July, 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1924; offg. supt. and rembr. of legal affairs, Nov., 1923; offg. sec. govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., Apl., 1924; confd. and sec., legisl. council, Aug., 1925; offg. jt. sec. and draftsman to Govt. of India, legisl. dept., Apl., 1932; addl. ditto, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, legisl. dept., Apl., 1933; secty., council of state, Apl. 1933; jt. secty. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Oct., 1933; addtl. secty., ditto, Jan., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; C.S.I., Jan., 1941.

BARTON, JAMES CALVERT, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Apl., 1881).—Ret'd., March, 1938.*

BARTON, JOHN EVERARD, Indian State Rly. (dist. contr. of stores) (b. 30th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1921; resdt. engr., Feb., 1922; dist. contr. of stores, G.I.P. Rly., Dec., 1932.

BARTON, LESLIE ERIC, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., ret'd., late I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 3rd Feb., 1889).—First commn., 8th Sept., 1909; in mil. employ to May, 1919; asst. pol. agent, Khyber, May, 1919; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, July, 1921; asst. commr., Hangu, Oct., 1922; dep. commr., Kohat, March, 1925; asst. commr., Thal, Oct., 1925; dep. commr., Bannu, Apl., 1926; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Oct., 1928; secty. to A.G.G. Deccan states and resdt., Kolhapur, Oct., 1933; offg. adjutant, Kolhapur infy., Nov., 1933; resdt., Jaipur, Apl., 1934; offg. pol. agent, E. Rajputana states in addn., Aug., 1935; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Nov., 1935; resdt. in Mewar and pol. agt., S. Rajputana States, May, 1938; resdt., Eastern States, Apl., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1941; resident, Kashmir, Nov., 1941; confirmed as resident, Cl. II, Dec., 1941; ret'd., Aug., 1944.

BARTON, SIR WILLIAM PELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 29th May, 1871).—Ret'd., Apl., 1930.*

BARTY, DERMOT CAMPBELL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 27th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Fettes and St. Andrew's Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in

India, Nov., 1937, and served in Sind as asst. collr.

BARUA, GURU PRASAD, B.L., late Indian Police, Assam, (b. March, 1888).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1914; dep. supt., Oct., 1921; supt., Sept., 1931; retd., Mar., 1943.

BARUA, HARI PRASAD, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Assam) (b. 12th Apr., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1916; asst. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Sept., 1928; asst. ch. engr. and under secty. to Govt., P.W.D., Assam, March, 1934, to Nov., 1934; suptg. engr., July, 1944.

BARVE, SADASHIV GOVIND, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., Bombay) (b. 27th Apr., 1914).—Educ. at New English Sch., Budhwar Peth, Poona, Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Sind as asst. collr.; under secty., home dept., Bombay, Feb., 1940; dep. sec. ditto, Apr., 1940.

BASAK, KANTI CHANDRA, B.Sc. (Cal.), B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (magt. and collr., Bengal) (b. 17th Sept., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Calcutta and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-secty. to govt. fin., etc., depts., Bengal, Nov., 1930; confd., Feb., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1932, and again Feb., 1935; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1933; offg. asst. commr. of income tax, Oct., 1935; addtl. dist. magt., Dec., 1938; magt. and collr., confd., Dec., 1940; sec. educ., etc., dept., March, 1944.

BASANT LAL, LALA, late Income tax dept., Punjab (b. 15th Feb., 1880).—Retd., Feb., 1936.*

BASHEER, AHMAD MUHAMMAD (see Ahmad M. B.).

BASHI RAM, SAHGAL, M.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 10th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; under-secty. to govt., P.W.D., Punjab, March, 1931; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1935; suptg. engr. (roads), subst., Jan., 1938; retd., 10th July, 1944.

BASHIR-UD-DIN, SAIYID, late Judicial dept., Bihar (b. 11th Dec., 1885).—Apptd. dist. and sess. judge, 1st June, 1930; retd., Apr., 1941.

BASKIN, REV. JOHN EVETT D'ACRE, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 18th Dec., 1908).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 25th July, 1944.

BASSETT, THOMAS RALPH, late Indian Police (b. 25th July, 1894).—Joined the service 31st May, 1920; asst. supt., Apr., 1923; King's police medal, Jan., 1924; supt., June, 1925; asst. insp.-genl., rly. police, Oct., 1936; retd.; re-empld. from Sept., 1939; dep. dir. intel. bureau, govt. of India, July, 1944.

BASTIN, REGINALD WALTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd April, 1910).—Educ. at Trowbridge High Sch., University Coll. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1934; settlement offr., Nov., 1935; sec., bd. of rev., Bengal, Nov., 1942; addl. dist. mag., July, 1943; mag. and collr., July, 1944.

BASU, ADYA NATH, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 3rd Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 11th Jan., 1911; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1912; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1930; confd., Nov., 1934.

BASU, ASWANI KUMAR, M.A., B.L., late Excise and Salt dept., Bengal (b. March, 1882).—Retd., March, 1937.*

BASU, BIMALA PRASANA, B.Sc., B.A., Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Orissa) (b. 1st July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, on 9th Nov., 1923; divl. for. offr., Sasanda divn., Apr., 1944.

BASU, BIRENDRA KUMAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 8th Aug., 1888).—Educ. at schools in India, Calcutta univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived 20th Oct., 1913, and served as asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1919; confd., June, 1925.

BASU, MRIGANKA MAULI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st March, 1912).—Educ. at the Scottish Church Collegiate Sch., Calcutta, Univ. of Calcutta, the Sch. of Economics, London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1936; addl. dist. mag., Apr., 1941; mag. and collr., July, 1943; commr., commcl. taxes, Bengal, Apr., 1944.

BASU, NABA KUMAR, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Ry. (transpn. (power) supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 12th Feb., 1918, as asst. dist. loco. supt.; asst. supt. (junr.), E.I. rly, Oct., 1926; transpn. (power) supt., E.I. rly., May, 1934.

BASU, PRABODH CHANDRA, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th Jan., 1883).—Apptd. exec. engr., Bengal, 1st Jan., 1931.

BASU, SUBODH KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 7th April, 1900).—Joined the service on 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

BASU, SUKUMAR, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., imp. col. of agric. research) (b. 30th Oct., 1900).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1925; arrived, 21st Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1933; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Bengal, fin., comm. and marine dept., Oct., 1934; ditto, pol. and appt. depts., March, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; dep. secty., Home dept., Apr., 1937; secty., imp. cl. of agric. research, July, 1938; mag. and collr. (confd.), Feb., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1944; sec., agric. dept., Jan., 1944.

BASU, SURESH CHANDRA, RAI BAHADUR, late Bengal Civil Service (b. June, 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

BATALIA RAM, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 20th Nov., 1887).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

BATES, HARRY STUART, B.A., Indian Civil Service (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 16th March, 1893).—Educ. at St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; arrived 11th May, 1920, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., April, 1921, to Nov., 1923; city magistrate, July, 1925; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1926; confd., April, 1932; dept. sec. to govt., U.P. rev. dept. (temp.),

Oct., 1933; spec. manager, Court of Wards, Balrampur estate, Jan., 1935.

BATHEJA, HASSANAND RADHAKRISHNA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., G.B.B. coll., Muzaffarpur) (b. 11th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 6th Nov., 1918; prof. of economics, Ravenshaw coll., Cuttack, Jan., 1925; ditto, Patna coll., Oct., 1927; member, tariff bd., Jan., 1933; offg. dep. dir. of public instn., Bihar and Orissa, May, 1933; member, tariff bd., Oct., 1934; offg. princpl., Ravenshaw coll., Cuttack, Sept., 1935; offg. dep. dir. of public instn., Bihar, March, 1936; princpl., G.B.B. coll., Muzaffarpur, Dec., 1936; again, Jan., 1939.

BATHGATE, KEITH, Indian State Ry.s. (transpn. traffic commcl. supt.) (b. 1st March, 1900).—Joined the service, Jan., 1921; asst. supt. jnr. scale, May, 1923; senr. scale (provl.), March, 1938; confd., Jan., 1939.

BATRA, BODH RAJ, India Posts and Telegs. (div. engr.) (b. 10th May, 1902).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1925; div. engr., July, 1933.

BATRA, HARGOBIND LAL, M.C., Lieut.-Col., ret'd., late I.M.S. (Assam) (b. 14th June, 1885).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1910; in mil. employ to 13th Nov., 1921; civil surg., May, 1925; ret'd., June, 1940.

BATRA, RAM KANWAR, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 8th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1922; apptd. to Punjab Engrg. Service, 14th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Central Provs., June, 1924; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1929; and again, Jan., 1933; confd., June, 1933; suptg. engr., March, 1943.

BATTEN, HENRY HALLET, Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Burma Commn.—Retd., Oct., 1936.*

BATTEN, KENNETH CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (hr. exec. officer, genl. dept.) (b. 5th Aug., 1890).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 9th March, 1914; on mil. service from Jan., 1917, to Jan., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer (temp.), May, 1926; hr. exec. offcr., Oct., 1938.

BATTERSBY, ERIC WORSLEY, Indian Police (b. 18th Apl., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 6th Dec., 1935; a.d.c. to govr. of Burma, May, 1940.

BATTYE, RICHMOND KEITH MOLESWORTH, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Aug. 1905).—First commn., 3rd Sept., 1925; in military employ until 28th Jan., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, June, 1932; under sect. to A.G.G., Balu, Feb., 1933; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1933; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, May, 1934; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse, June, 1935; offg. pol. officer in Sikkim in addn., Nov., 1935; under secty. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Aug., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1937; pol. agent, Gilgit (in addition), June, 1939, to Oct., 1939; asst. comdt., Malwa Bhil corps and ex-officio asst. to pol. agent in Malwa, March, 1945; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr. in Quetta-Pishin, May, 1941; pol. agt., E. Kathiawar agency, Jan., 1943; on for. serv., Bastar agency, Sept., 1943.

BAUM, ERNEST FREDERICK, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Oct., 1881).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 9th Dec., 1905; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; on mil. duty, May, 1918, to July, 1918; offg. commr., May, 1924; confd., March, 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1940.

BAVDEKAR, RAJARAM SHRIPAD, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 16th Sept., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay and Cambridge (Sidney Sussex); joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1922; arrived, 9th Feb., 1923; and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1931; and again, Sept., 1932; confd., Oct. 1933; regr., high ct., Bombay, appellate side, June, 1934, to June, 1937; dist. and sess. judge from March, 1938.

BAVIN, LANCELOT NEVILLE, late Indian Police (b. 12th May, 1892).—Retd., Apl., 1937.*

BAW, MAUNG SHWE, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 28th Apl., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Rangoon and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Apl., 1939; secty., health and p.w., May, 1940.

BAVINGTON, REV. REGINALD, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 29th April, 1902).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 1st Feb., 1942.

BAWA KARTAR SINGH, M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th Apl., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th July, 1911; prof., Cuttack, Nov., 1921; offg. prof., science coll., Patna, Jan., 1936; confd., Aug., 1936; ret'd., 16th Apl., 1940.

BAWA RAMNICK SINGH, Diwan Bahadur, M.I.S.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 18th March, 1880).—Retd., March, 1935.*

BAXTER, GEORGE HERBERT, C.I.E., M.A., India Office (actg. asst. under sec. of state) (b. 30th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; war service: Roy. Fus., Sept., 1914, to Jan., 1915; R.G.A., Jan., 1915, to Armistice; apptd. jun. clerk, mil. dept., July, 1920; transfed. to fin. dept., Jan., 1921; asst. principal, Apl., 1921; deputed on special mission to Baghdad, Oct. to Dec., 1921; actg. pte. sec. to permit. under sec. of state, Jan., 1924; appt. as pte. sec. confd., Apl., 1924; principal, Nov., 1924; sec., Royal Commn. on Indian Currency and Finance, Aug., 1925, to July, 1926; adviser to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; actg. asst. secty., finl. dept., Jan.-Apl., 1932; and again, Dec., 1933; secty., cte. on Indian Reserve Bank legislation, July-Aug., 1933; secty. to Lord Privy Seal's confice. on air raid shelters (U.K.), Feb. to Apl., 1939; C.I.E., 1st Jan., 1942; actg. asst. under sec. of state, July, 1943.

BAXTER, GERALD DORRETT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 16th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 7th April, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1927; under sec. to govt., Aug., 1941; supt. of works, March, 1943.

BAXTER, JAMES, C.M.G., M.A., Fin. Advr. to the Govt. of Burma; apptd., Apl., 1937; C.M.G., 1st Jan., 1942.

BAXTER, NORMAN BERNARD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 12th June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1938.*

BAYLEY, VERNON THOMAS, M.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Punjab).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 20th Nov., 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; asst. dir., intell. bureau, Home Dept., March, 1937; liaison offr., ditto, March, 1942; M.B.E., June, 1941; supt., C.I.D., Punjab, Sept., 1942.

BAYLIS, ROBERT FRANCIS SARJEANT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 11th June, 1903).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., July, 1929; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Oct., 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1934; confd., Jan., 1940.

BAYNES, FREDERIC WILLIAM WILBERFORCE, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 4th Aug., 1889).—Educ. at Harrow and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after the exam. of 1912; arrd., 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1917; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1920; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1923; confd., Nov., 1929; dep. commr., March, 1930.

BAZALGETTE, JOHN EVELYN ARNOLD, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 24th Jan., 1907).—First commn., 30th Aug., 1926; in military employ prior to 11th Oct., 1932; under training in Punjab, Oct., 1932; P.A. to Resident at Hyderabad, Jan., 1933; under secty. to the A.G.G., Punjab States, Aug., 1934; under secty. to the Pol. Resdt., Persian Gulf, June, 1935; H.M.'s Consul, Kerman, Nov., 1935; vice-consul, Khorramshahr, Apr., 1936; confd. in pol. dept., Nov., 1936; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Dec., 1939; sec. in cmd. and adjt., Kolhapur infantry and ex-officio under secty. to resdt. for Kolhapur and Deccan States, Apr., 1945; secty. to the resident for Kolhapur and the Deccan States, Sept., 1941; asst. comdt., C.R.'s police, March, 1943; under sec. to resdt., Baroda and Gujwar States, Nov., 1943; asst. pol. agt., Punjab hill States, May, 1944; pol. agt. (in addn.), Sept., 1944.

BEADNELL, CHRISTOPHER BEADNELL, I.S.O., late Excise Dept., Madras (b. 20th Jan., 1880).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

BEADON, CORNWALLIS WILLIAM ROGER, Indian Police (b. 11th Oct., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 14th Nov., 1922; offg. dist. supt., March, 1932.

BEADON, ERIC HAMMET FAIRFAX, Indian Police (b. 19th March, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 10th Dec., 1926; confd. dist. supt., June, 1940.

BEADON, VERNON, Lieut.-Col., M.C., I.A., late Burma commn. (b. 14th Apr., 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.*

BEAMAN, GERALD WILLIAM DEVENISH, late Indian State Rlys., Burma (b. 18th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 24th March, 1909; dist. traffic supt., Sept., 1924; asst. traffic supt., March, 1928; dist. traffic supt. (temp.), Sept., 1928; offg. dep. traffic manager (transptn.), Burma rlys., Nov., 1933; retd., Apr., 1940.

BEAMAN, VALENTINE ARDEN HULME, M.B.E., late Burma Excise Service (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—Previous service from May, 1911; asst. supt., excise, June, 1912; supt., Sept., 1913; chief supt., May, 1933; retd., Feb., 1940.

BEAN, BASIL CLARENCE LAHEY, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 5th Nov., 1909; dist. ditto, Oct., 1918; divl. trans. traffic officer, Oct., 1924; offg. divl. supt., Apr., 1930; confd., Dec., 1932; O.B.E., Nov., 1935; dep. ch. optg. supt., Dec., 1937; divl. supt. (senior), May, 1938; retd., Sept., 1941.

BEAN, SALVADOR MICHAEL LAHEY, late Indian Audit and Accounts Service (b. 14th Oct., 1882).—Retd., July, 1935.*

BEASLEY, SIR HORACE OWEN COMPTON, Kt., B.A. (Cantab.), Barr., late Madras Judicial Dept. (b. 2nd July, 1877).—Retd., July, 1937.*

BEAUMONT, HERBERT CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 2nd June, 1912).—Educ. at Uppingham and Worcester Coll., Oxford; appointed after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Dec., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. liaison offr., Agartala, June, 1944.

BEAUMONT, RT. HON. SIR JOHN WILLIAM FISHER, P.C. (b. 4th Sept., 1877).—Chief justice, high ct., Bombay, June, 1930, to Oct., 1944; kt., Jan., 1931; apptd. member of judcl. cte. of Privy Council, Oct., 1944.

BECKETT, JAMES, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Madras) (b. 25th March, 1891).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1911; asst. supt., July, 1913; offg. supt., July, 1919; confd., Sept., 1926; asst. inspr.-genl., March, 1927; dep. commr., crime bch., Oct., 1932; awarded Indian Police medal, Jan., 1933; dep. inspr. genl. (confd.), Oct., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

BECKETT, RICHARD HENRY, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.Sc. (Lond.), late Indian Educl. Service, Central Provs. (b. 3rd Jan., 1882).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

BECKETT, RONALD BRYMER, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 17th Jan., 1891).—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 30th Nov., 1914, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; guardian to H.H. Nawab of Mamdot, Oct., 1915, to Jan., 1917; sec., municipal ctee., Lahore, March, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1926; registrar, high ct., Lahore, and addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1927; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, March, 1932; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, May, 1934; legal remembrancer and secty., legis. dept., Feb., 1937; again, Oct., 1939; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1938; spec. enq. offr., July, 1940; addtl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Sept., 1940; judge, ditto, July, 1941.

BEDEKAR, GOPAL VAMAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 10th Oct., 1908).—Educ. at the High Sch., Miraj, Bombay Univ., Battersea Polytechnic, London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

BEDFORD, JAMES DOUGLAS HARDY, C.S.I., late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 1st June, 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

BEDI, BABA DAYA SINGH, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Jan., 1899).—First commn., 14th July, 1921; in milty. employ to Sept., 1928; asst. pol. agent in southern states and Central India, Sept., 1931; under secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in the states of Western India, March, 1932; under-secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Oct., 1933; asst. commr., Thal, Sept., 1935; ditto, Kohat, Apr., 1937; under secty. to A.G.G., Resident and Chief Commr., Baluchistan, Dec., 1937; secty., ditto, June, 1938; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1938; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, March, 1939; pol. agent, Loralai, Apr., 1939; dep. commr., Hazara, Jan., 1942; pol. agt., Kurram, Sept., 1944.

BEDI, SHEO SINGH (see Sheo Singh Bedi).

BEDI, TARLOCHAN DAS, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 25th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1936; ditto, sub perm., Nov., 1939.

BEESON, CYRIL FREDERICK CHANINGTON, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., Indian Forest Service (forest entomologist, Dehra Dun) (b. 10th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1911; asst. consvr., Nov., 1912; dep. consvr., Nov., 1916; forest zoologist, July, 1920; forest entomologist, Dec., 1926; offg. consvr., variously from March, 1931; confd., Dec., 1936; offg. inspr.-genl. of forests and presdt., forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Aug., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1941.

BEEVOR, RALPH BRANTHWAYT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 29th Apl., 1895).—Educ. at Felsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1919; arrived, 1st Dec., 1919, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1927; confd., Nov., 1932; confd. in sel. grade, May, 1943; acting judge, high ct., Patna, Nov., 1943; offg. addtl. ditto, March, 1944.

BEHARI LAL, UPPAL, LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 19th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1910; asst. engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1933; ret'd., 31st May, 1943.

BELCHER, RONALD HARRY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 5th Jan., 1916).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital, Jesus Coll., Cambridge and Brasenose Coll., Oxford, appointed on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; a.r.p. offr., Sept., 1941; sub. divl. offr.

BELHAVEN and STENTON, LORD, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.).—Ret'd., March, 1923.*

BELL, SIR CHARLES ALFRED, K.C.I.E., C.M.G., late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Ret'd., March, 1919.*

BELL, CYRIL FRANCIS, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service, Central Provs. (b. 23rd Feb., 1883).—Ret'd., Feb., 1938.*

BELL, SIR ERNEST ALBERT SEYMOUR, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., late Railway Dept.—Ret'd., Oct., 1926.*

BELL, FRANK OWEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd June, 1907).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1931; settlemt. offr., from Oct., 1936.

BELL, HERBERT JASPER, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th May, 1886).—Ret'd., Jan., 1935.*

BELL, JOHN ALTON, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 27th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 29th Oct., 1910; in milty. employ from 5th Oct., 1915, to 18th Sept., 1919; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur Rly. Co. from 7th March, 1923; offg. exec. engr., E.I. Rly., Apl., 1927; offg. divl. supt., Feb., 1920; confd., June, 1933; offg. agent, E.I. Rly., Oct., 1935; confd., Jan., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Dec., 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1941.

BELL, JOHN MICHAEL GEOFFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and coll., Bengal) (b. 17th Apl., 1913).—Educ. at Winchester, Marlborough Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Jan., 1938; arrived in India, Feb., 1938; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1940; mag. and collr., Aug., 1944; dep. sec. civ. supplies, Dec., 1944.

BELL, SIR ROBERT DUNCAN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S., Bombay (b. 18th May, 1878).—Ret'd., Dec., 1937.*

BELL, ROBERT WALLACE, late Indian Police (b. 6th Nov., 1891).—Ret'd., Jan., 1935.*

BELL, ROGER CECIL SUTHERLAND, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 4th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Fettes, Worcester Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub.collr., March, 1931; offg. under secty. to govt., Madras, public dept., June, 1934; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Apl., 1936.

BELL, ROLAND STANLEY, late Indian Police, United Provs. (b. 8th Oct., 1882).—Ret'd., Oct., 1937.*

BELVALKAR, DR. SHRIPAD KRISHNA, M.A., Ph.D. (Harvard), late Indian Educl. Service, Bombay (b. 11th Dec., 1881).—Ret'd., Dec., 1936.*

BEMROSE, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 21st June, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 23rd Nov., 1925; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1932; confd., supt., Apl., 1938; asst. inspr. genl., March, 1941, to Jan., 1942.

BENCE-JONES, PHILIP REGINALD, M.A., M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I.M.E. (b. 12th Jan., 1897).—Appdt. princpl., MacLagan engrg. coll., 29th Mar., 1934; on milty. serv., March, 1941, to Oct., 1944.

BENEGAL, SANJIVA RAO (see Sanjiva Rao, B.).

BENEST, FRANCIS EDWARD, M.I.E.E., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 12th July, 1871).—Ret'd., July, 1936.*

BENJAMIN, HERBERT DANIEL, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 4th March, 1899).—Educ. at Monmouth Gr. Sch., and Oxford (Jesus); joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; and sub. divl. officer, June, 1924; under-secty. to govt. of Bengal, fnl., etc., dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. secty. to ditto, March, 1929; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1930; confd. March, 1935; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, legisl. dept., March, 1935; attd. legisl. dept., govt. of India, May, 1936; special offr., fin. dept., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1936; secty., legis. dept., govt. of Bengal, May, 1937; addl. secty. and draftsman, govt. of India, legis. dept., Dec., 1938; jt. secty. and addl. draftsman, ditto, Sept., 1939; secty. legis. dept., govt. of Bengal, Sept., 1940; addl. dep. secty., govt. of India legis. dept., Jan., 1941.

BENN, Rt. Hon. W. WEDGWOOD, D.S.O., D.F.C. (see Stansgate, Viscount).

BENNETT, SIR EDWARD, B.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (ret'd.) (b. 26th Oct., 1880).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, appdt. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., May, 1914; sess. and sub. judge, Jan., 1917; dist. and sessions judge, Oct., 1922; offg. judge,

high ct., Allahabad, Apl., 1928; and again, Apl., 1929; addl. judge, ditto, Oct., 1929; and again, March, 1931; judge, ditto, Dec., 1932; knighted, Jan., 1939; retd., Oct., 1940.

BENNET, WILLIAM RYLAND RAINSFORD MOORE, M.C., late Indian State Railways (b. 22nd May, 1881).—Retd., May, 1936.*

BENNETT, MAJOR CHRISTOPHER MACAULAY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 15th Feb., 1903).—Joined the service as exec. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1926; offg. exec. engr., March, 1933; confd., Oct., 1935.

BENNETT, ERIC, M.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 20th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at Berkhamsted and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 7th March, 1921; arrived, 29th Apl., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1923; spl. asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1928; ditto (subs.), Sept., 1935.

BENNETT, REV. FRANK, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estab. (United Provs.) (chaplain on probn.) (b. 6th Nov., 1901).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Feb., 1939.

BENNETT, HERBERT JOHN, Office of High Commr. for India (princpl. exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 30th June, 1881).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Store Depot, 6th Apl., 1901; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; supervisor, India Store Dept., Apl., 1921; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1922; princpl., ditto, May, 1938.

BENNETT, HUGH KENNETH, M.B.E. (b. 13th Apl., 1888).—Joined Mint Service as asst. engr., 1st March, 1910; head engr., 6th Dec., 1933; dy. mint master (offg. temp.), Jan., 1941; M.B.E., 12th June, 1941.

BENNETT, JOHN REGINALD WILLIAM, B.A., (Oxon), Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. 18th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at King's Sch., Warwick, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., July, 1917; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1919, to March, 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt., judl. dept., Oct., 1923; sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1927; and again, Nov., 1933; judl. secty. to govt., U.P., July, 1931; addtl. judge, ch. ct., Oudh, July, 1940; judge, ditto, Oct., 1940; judge, high ct., Allahabad, Oct. 1944.

BENNETT, JOHN THORNE MASEY, C.I.E., C.B.E., M.C., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 7th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1914; asst. supt., Jan., 1919; supt., July, 1924; C.B.E., June, 1933; offg. dep. inspr. genl. variously from Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1939; inspr. genl. and jt. sec. to govt., Punjab, Jan., 1940; dep. inspr. genl., C.I.D., Feb., 1940; again Jan., 1943; awarded Indian Police Medal, Sept., 1940.

BENNETT, JOSEPH WILLIAM, B.E., B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th July, 1890).—Retd., Sept., 1935.*

BENNISON, JOHN JENNINGS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th June, 1892).—Educ. at Middlesbrough High Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, Dec., 1916 and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from July, 1918, to July, 1919; offg. dep. commr., and jt. regr., co-op. societies, Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1925; confd., June,

1930; supt., census operations, Burma, Apl., 1930; retd., Apl., 1944.

BENSON, WILFRED JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 27th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935; under secty. p.w.d. (irrgn.), Apl., 1938; on mil. serv. from Oct., 1941.

BENTHALL, SIR EDWARD CHARLES (member, govr.-genl.'s exec. cl., war transp. and rlys.) (b. 26th Nov., 1893).—Govt., Imp. Bank of India, 1928-30; delegate, round table conf., London, 1931-32; served in Ind. Army retrenchment cttee., 1931; member, Council of State, 1932-33; member, Bengal legis. assembly, 1934-35; ditto, Council, 1937-38; dir., reserve bank of India, 1935-36; under home govt., 1940-42; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. cl. (war transp. and rlys.), July, 1942.

BENTON, GORDON WILLIAM, Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Central Provs.) (b. 25th March, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Nov., 1912; dist. supt., Sept., 1924; dep. dir. genl., police, Hyderabad State, Feb., 1929; and again, Nov., 1932; officer on spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., Aug., 1936; attd. for service with dir., intel. bureau, Oct., 1936; ditto, with rly. bd., Oct., 1937; on special duty, Kolhapur Residency, Apl. to June, 1939; confd. dep. inspr. genl., Sept., 1944.

BENTON, WILLIAM STEPHEN, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 21st Jan., 1893).—Asst. engr., 27th Dec., 1919; exec. engr. (prov.), Nov., 1935; confd., March, 1938; dir., civ. engrg., rly. bd., Apl., 1944.

BERG, CLEMENT LEONARD, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1927; confd., Oct., 1930; deputed to mil. engrg. serv., Karachi, Oct., 1937, to Apl., 1938; served under cent. govt., July, 1941, to June, 1943.

BERKELEY, GERALD EUSTACE, Indian State Rlys. (chief traffic manager, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 24th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as traffic probnr., 7th Nov., 1914; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1918; divl. traffic manager, Aug., 1924; goods supt., Oct., 1924; asst. traffic manager, May, 1926; offd. variously as dep. traffic manager from May, 1926; confd., Dec., 1929; ch. traffic manager, provl., May, 1939.

BERRINGTON, ARTHUR DAVIES, Lieut.-Commdr., R.N.R. (ret'd.) (Dep. Port Consvr., Madras) (b. 26th Nov., 1895).—Apptd. harbour-master, 2nd Aug., 1921; dep. port consvr., Sept., 1930.

BERRINGTON, PETER DAVIES, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 21st Nov., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

BERRY, WILLIAM, Public Works Dept., Punjab (b. 30th March, 1889).—Appt. profir. Maclagan Engrg. Coll., Lahore, 10th Oct., 1924; retd. and re-empld., 20th March, 1944.

BERTRAM, THOMAS, O.B.E., I.S.O. (b. 18th Sept., 1884).—Supt., viceregal estates, Feb., 1921; retd., Sept., 1939.

BERY, HARRANS LAL, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 9th Dec., 1912).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 30th Jan., 1936; confd., 1938.

BERY, KASH RAM, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th June, 1880).—Retd., June, 1935.*

BEST, KENNETH HENRY, Indian Police (b. 20th March, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Dec., 1937.

BESTALL, FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 22nd July, 1910).—Joined the

service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 3rd Dec., 1929; asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, Feb., 1940.

BETHAM, GEOFFREY LAWRENCE, C.I.E., M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Political Service (b. 8th Apl., 1889).—First commn., dated 20th Jan., 1909; asst. commr., May, 1919; asst. pol. agent, Mekran, March, 1924; pol. agent, Chagai, Jan., 1925; ditto, Sibi, July, 1927; ditto, Zhob, March, 1929; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apl., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; resdt., Mewar, March, 1935; H.B.M.'s minister, Nepal, Apl., 1938; ret'd., Apl., 1944.

BETT, JOHN RAYMOND, Indian C.S. (subcollr., Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1901).—Educ. at Wintringham Secondary Sch., Grimsby, Univ. Coll., London, and St. Edmunds Hall, Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., variously from Apl., 1936; secty. (home), Apl., 1944.

BETTERTON, FREDERICK ARCHIBALD, late Indian Service of Engrs., Bihar (b. 29th Dec., 1882).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

BEVAN, GILBERT JOHN BECKFORD, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent, Burma Rlys.) (b. 19th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 6th Jan., 1910; ditto (perm.), July, 1911; on milty. duty from 27th Sept., 1916, to 19th Sept., 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; dist. engr., Feb., 1921; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1933; confd., March, 1934.

BEVAN, WILLIAM JOHN, Indian State Rlys., Coal Dept. (elec. and mech. engr.) (b. 28th Aug., 1893).—Joined service, Apl., 1928; mech. engr., Apl., 1929; elec. and mech. engr., Jan., 1937.

BEVERIDGE, ERNEST JOHN, Indian Police (b. 14th Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; asst. supt., Deoli deten. jail, Jan., 1935.

BEWERS, DUDLEY FRANCIS, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, education dept.) (b. 28th Sept., 1893).—Apptd. clerk to local adviser to Indian Students, London, 25th Aug., 1919; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; clerical officer, Apl., 1922; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1925.

BEWOOR, SIR GURUNATH VENKATESH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dir. genl., posts and tels.) (b. 20th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at schools in India, Bombay Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 27th Nov., 1912, and served in the Central Prov., as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., March, 1919; offg. postmr.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, July, 1922; confd. as postmr.-gen., July, 1924; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1924; confd., June, 1925; dep. dir. genl., posts and tels., Nov., 1927; delegate to internal Univ. Postal Congress, London, March, 1929; P.M.G., Bombay, Oct., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1932 dir. genl., posts and tels., March, 1934; member, Council of State, March, 1936; kt., June, 1939; addl. sec., govt. of India (def.), Aug., 1941; sec., ditto (posts and air), July, 1942.

BEYNON, ALBERT CYRIL, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S., Burma (b. 28th Aug., 1896).—Retd., March, 1938,* and re-empld., serving as dep. sec., govt. of India (war), Nov., 1944.

BHADKAMKAR, BALAKRISHNA VASUDEO, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at Bombay and University Coll., London; arrived, 23rd March,

1919, and posted to Burma; transferred to United Provs as asst. mag. and collr., July, 1920; jt. mag., July, 1920; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1926; confd., May, 1932; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1932; excise commr., United Provs., May, 1938.

BHADRAN, CHAKAPANY AIYFNGAR RAMA, M.B.E., M.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 24th Sept., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 15th Nov., 1932; dep. consvr., Nov. 1941; M.B.E., June, 1943.

BHAGAT SINGH, M.A., late India Posts and Teles. (b. 30th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

BHAGAT, SURENDRA SINGH (see Surendra Singh, B.).

BHAGWATI PRASAD SAKSENA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 17th July, 1899).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1932; apprentice engr., Sept., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1925; offg. exec. engr., March, 1929; confd., Oct., 1934.

BHAGWATI PRASAD, VARMA, Rai Bahadur B.A., M.R.S.I., M.I.M.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th March, 1877).—Retd., Oct., 1935.*

BHALJA, GOVARDHAN SHANKERLAL, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (addtl. sec., govt. of India, war) (b. 8th Aug., 1895).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1920; arrived 8th Jan., 1921, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925, and Nov., 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; dir. of industries and regr. of co-op. societies, Aug., 1933; sec. to local self govt., etc., depts., Apl., 1937; finl. sec. to govt., and sec., p.w. dept., Nov., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1943; addtl. sec., govt. of India (war), Nov., 1944.

BHALLA, T. P. (see Tejasvi Prasad Bhalla).

BHANDARKAR, KASHINATH YESHWANT (asst. solr.) (b. Oct., 1902).—2nd cl. sub-judge, variously from Nov., 1927, to Oct., 1936; P.A. to rembr. legal affairs, Bombay, March, 1933; asst. secty., Bombay Govt. legal dept., Oct., 1933; asst. solr., Govt. of India, legis. dept., Apl., 1938.

BHANDARKAR, VAMAN PRABHAKAR, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 18th Apl., 1892).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1914; dist. ditto, Dec., 1936; cent. publicity offr., E.I.R., May, 1941.

BHANDARI, AMAR NATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge Punjab) (b. 21st Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Oxford Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 9th Dec., 1924, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1929; and again, Oct., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1933; confd., July, 1934; attd. govt. of India, legis. dept., Nov., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Delhi, Sept., 1936; dep. legal remembrancer and dep-sec., legis. dept., Punjab, June, 1939; addtl. judge, high ct., Lahore.

BHANDARI, JAIGOPAL, C.I.E., M.A., late India Finance Dept. (b. 14th Dec., 1882).—Retd., March, 1938,* and re-empld. on contract for 5 yrs. from Nov., 1939.

BHANDARI, MADAN GOPAL, C.I.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bombay (b. 3rd Jan., 1892).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 21st Oct., 1924; addl. supt., Oct., 1924; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Sept., 1927; supt., Borstal Institute, Lahore, Apl., 1928, to Dec., 1928; ditto, Central prison, Bombay, Apl., 1933; offg.

inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bombay, Feb., 1936 ; confd., July, 1939 ; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

BHANDARI, RAM NATH, M.D., Ch.B., Major, I.M.S., Jail Deptt., United Provs. (b. 15th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. supt., central prison, Bareilly, 5th Jan., 1929 ; ditto, Allahabad (Naini), March, 1937.

BHANDARKAR, GOPAL PRABHAKAR, M.B.E., B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Oct., 1882).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

BHANOT, HARNAM DAS, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge ; arrived, 9th Jan., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. ; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926 ; dep. commr. (provil.), May, 1928 ; ditto (subs. perm.), Dec., 1931 ; senr. secty. to finl. commrs., and dep. secty. to govt., Punjab, dev. dept., July, 1934 ; secty., ditto, fin. dept., Sept., 1937 ; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

BHANSALI, MANSEN DAMODAR, M.A., LL.B., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., govt. of India, communications dept.) (b. 7th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay and Dulwich Coll., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge ; apptd. after exam. in 1923 ; arrived in India and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte., Dec., 1923 ; offg. dep. sec. to govt., finance dept., Jan., 1931 ; confd., Nov., 1931 ; member, legisl. council, Bombay, 1932 ; offg. regr. of co-op. societies, Bombay, July, 1936 ; regr. ditto, Dec., 1938 ; apptd. ex-officio jt. secty. to Govt. of Bombay, revenue dept., and regr., co-ops. and dir. of rural development, Sept., 1939 ; dep. sec., govt. of India, communications dept., Oct., 1941.

BHARGAV, LACHHMAN PRASAD (see Lachhman, P.B.).

BHARGAVA, KALKAR PRASAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 15th July, 1908).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Oral, Allahabad Univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge ; joined the service 6th Oct., 1933 ; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. ; jt. mag., March, 1937.

BHARGAVA, MURARI LAL, B.Sc., LL.B., A.S.A.A., Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 1st Aug., 1895).—Apptd., Sept., 1926.

BHARGAVA, RAMESHWAR PRASAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 27th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931 ; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. ; jt. mag., Oct., 1934.

BHARGAVA, VASHISTHA, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 5th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies ; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930 ; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. ; jt. mag., Oct., 1934 ; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1940.

BHARUCHA, JAMASJI CURSETJI, Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (b. 9th July, 1887).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1912 ; in milty. employ until 3rd Apl., 1923 ; offg. dist. medl. officer, March, 1926 ; offg. surg., first dist., and supt., Rayapuram medl. sch., etc., Apl., 1928 ; offg. supt., jail dept., Burma, Aug., 1929 ; confd., July, 1931 ; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Burma, Oct., 1933 ; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, May, 1934 ; princpl., medl. sch., Agra, Feb., 1935 ; retd.,

BHARUCHA, JEHANGIR SORAB, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 13th Oct., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 6th Aug., 1923 ; offg. supt., Feb., 1932 ; confd., Nov., 1937 ; sec. and exec. offr., R.T.A., var. from Jan., 1942.

BHARUCHA, KAIKHUSRU BYRAMJI, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Deptt., Bihar (supt.) (b. 19th July, 1889).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920 ; in milty. employ up to 15th March, 1924 ; offg. supt., March, 1924 ; confd., Jan., 1928.

BHARUCHA, PHIROZSHAH BYRAMJI, D.S.O., O.B.E., M.D. (Lond.), F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (medl. supt., Mayo hosp., Lahore) (b. 21st Feb., 1886).—First commn., 30th July, 1910 ; O.B.E., Jan., 1920 ; on mil. duty till Apl., 1921, when he was apptd. prof'r. of anatomy, King Edward Medical Coll., Lahore ; offg. prof'r. of operative surgery, ditto, Oct., 1921 ; and again, March, 1926 ; medl. supt., Mayo hosp., Lahore, Nov., 1935 ; offg. princpl., King Edward Medl. Coll., Feb., 1936.

BHASKARA, RAO NAYUDU TAMARAPAKAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 10th May, 1896).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., Madras ; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921 ; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. ; sub.-coll. and jt. mag., Feb., 1925 ; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Feb., 1930 ; confd., May, 1942.

BHASKARAIYA, CHATTA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 27th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 6th March, 1920 ; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, July, 1921 ; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Calcutta, Jan., 1930 ; dep. acct.-genl., central revs., Calcutta, Feb., 1933 ; ditto, Bengal, Apl., 1936 ; examiner, outside audit dept., Bombay, Aug., 1938 ; ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Delhi, Sept., 1938 ; financial adviser to the ch. commr., Delhi, Apl., 1939 ; chief auditor, E.B. Rly., July, 1941 ; ditto, N.W. Rly., Aug., 1943.

BHASKARAN, PUDIYA VITTIL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Orissa) (b. 18th Nov., 1908).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 23rd Jan., 1933 ; confd., Jan., 1935.

BHAT, MADHUSUDAN DAMODAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (municipal commr. for the city of Bombay) (b. 4th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Elphinstone Coll., Bombay, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge ; joined the service, 9th Nov., 1921 ; arrived, 27th Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., offg. collr., dist. magte. and pol. agent, May, 1925, and again Aug., 1929 ; temp'y. under secty. to govt., fin. dept., Apl., 1929 ; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Jewhar ; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Jath, Feb., 1933 ; collr., March, 1936 ; secty. to govt., Sind, home, etc., depts. ; member, Sind. rev. trib., June, 1938 ; municipal commr. for the city of Bombay, Dec., 1938.

BHATIA, KISHINCHAND MULCHAND, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Dec., 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 12th Aug., 1931 ; confd. as exec. engr., Sind, Aug., 1940.

BHATIA, KRISHNA BEHARI, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 15th July, 1907).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad, Royal Coll. of Science, London, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London ; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929 ; arrived, 7th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. coll. and mag., jt. mag., Apl., 1933 ; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1936 ; sec., tariff bd., Apl., 1937, to Apl., 1938 ; confd. as mag. and collr., Sept., 1942 ; sec. to govt., United Provs. (agric.).

BHATIA, SOHAN LAL, M.A., M.D., B.Ch. (Cantab.), L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S., F.C.P.S. (Bom.), M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 5th Aug., 1891).—First commn. dated 15th May, 1920; served in milty. dept. prior to 22nd Aug., 1920; offg. prof. of physiology, etc., G.M. Coll., Sept., 1920; confd., Sept., 1923; princpl., G.M. Coll., and supt., J.J. group hosp., Oct., 1937; addl. dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Aug., 1941; dep. ditto, 4th Oct., 1943; member, academic coun. of Univ. of Delhi, Feb., 1944.

BHATTACHARJI, DAKSHINA RANJAN, M.Sc. Ph.D., D.Sc., late U.P. Edn. Serv., Cl. I (b. 18th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as Prof., Muir Cent. Coll., Alld., 22nd Aug., 1910; on for service with Alld. Univ., from July, 1922; ret'd., Aug., 1940.

BHATTACHARJI, DHARMADAS, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 1st March, 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 12th June, 1927; confd., June, 1929; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1934; offg. supt., Jan., 1936; confd., Aug., 1940; Indian police medal, 1941.

BHATTACHARJI, DURGAGATI, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 1st July, 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st Feb., 1936; confd., 1938.

BHATTACHARJI, MAHENDRA KUMAR, B.Sc. B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; confd., Jan., 1928.

BHATTACHARJI, TARAKESWAR, Raj Bahadur, B.A., Excise and Salt dept., Bengal (dep. commr.) (b. 25th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1906; sub-dep. collr., Dec., 1907; supt. of excise and salt, Apl., 1915; pers. asst. to commr. of excise, June, 1928; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1928; confd., March, 1930.

BHATTACHARYA, AMBARNATH (see Ambarnath Bhattacharya).

BHAUMIK, HARIPADA, O.B.E., B.A., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 10th June, 1882).—Ret'd., June, 1937.*

BHAVE, DIVAN BAHADUR MAHADEV ANANT, B.A., LL.M., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 14th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th Apl., 1914; asst. judge, Feb., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge variously from Apl., 1934; ret'd., June, 1939; apptd. member, public service commission, Bombay-Sind, June, 1939; Dwan Bahadur, June, 1939.

BHAWANISHANKAR RAO, KHAMBADKONE, O.B.E., B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 5th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as offer., cl. II, May, 1924; junr. dep. fin. advr. mil. fin., Apl., 1935; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1935; revtd. to mil. accts. dep., March, 1941; contr. field. dy. accts., Apl., 1941; O.B.E., June, 1941; jt. finl. advr., mil. fin., June, 1943, to Oct., 1943.

BHIDE, MAHADEVA VISHNU, B.A., late Indian C.S., Punjab (b. 13th Feb., 1883).—Educ. at Poona, Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 1st Jan., 1908, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. censor, Bombay, Jan., 1915, to April, 1919; postal censor, Lahore, May, 1919; offg. legal remembr. and sec. to govt., legislative dept., May, 1925; confd., Apl., 1926; actg. judge, Lahore High Ct., June-July, 1927, and again, March, 1933; addl. judge, ditto, variously from Nov., 1927; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1934; judge, high ct., Lahore, July, 1936; ret'd., 5th Feb., 1943.

BHIDE, MANOHAR RAMCHANDRA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 5th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Nagpur Univ., University Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; regr. co-op societies, June, 1938; under sec., Govt. of India, dept. of supply, Nov., 1941; dep. sec., branch secretariat, New Delhi, Dept. of Supply, Nov., 1942; regr. co-op. societies, and dir., rural reconstrn., March, 1944.

BHIDE, VITHAL SHIVARAM, B.A., LL.B., Barr.-at-Law, Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 23rd Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Mira High Sch., Bombay Univ., and Cambridge (non-collegiate); apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915 and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transf. to Bombay as asst. collr., Apl., 1919; asst. collr. and personal asst. to collr., Feb., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Apl., 1926; confd., Apl., 1935; offg. regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1927; secty. to genl. and educational depts., Bombay, Nov., 1938, to May, 1939; secty. rev. dept., Bombay, May, 1939, to Oct., 1941.

BHIM SEN, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 26th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1918; asst. engr., Sept., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1927; confd., Apl., 1929; on depn. to Orissa, Apl., 1936; suptg. engr. (perm.), Sept., 1943.

BHOLA, MATHURA PRASHAD, Rai Bahadur, O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 21st Sept., 1881).—Ret'd., Sept., 1936.*

BHOOTHALINGAM, SUBRAHMANYA, O.B.E. B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and sub-collr., Madras) (b. 22nd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., May 1933; spec. asst. settlement offr., Nov., 1934, and Nov., 1935; offr. in charge, agric. audit dept., Reserve Bank of India, Dec., 1938; under secty., govt. of India, dept. of supply, Jan., 1940; dep. ditto, July, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

BHORE, DAVID GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 28th Oct., 1915).—Educ. at Bromsgrove and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 12th Oct., 1937; confd., 15th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

BHORE, SIR JOSEPH WILLIAM, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., Madras (b. 6th Apl., 1879).—Ret'd., Apl., 1936.*

BHUTTO, SIR SHAH NAWAL KHAN, G.M.K., C.I.E., O.B.E., Member, Bombay-Sind public sev. commn., from Apl., 1937.

BICKERS, MAURICE HENRY TOWNSHEND, Col., India Posts and Telegraphs (Postmaster-General) (b. 9th June, 1891).—Joined the service as proby. supt., June, 1913; confd., June, 1915; Expedny. Force, Mesopotamia, Dec., 1917; depn. to Iraq Administration, March, 1921, to June, 1930; depy. Postmr.-General, Sept., 1937; deputation to field svce., Sept., 1941; dir., army postal services, Oct., 1943.

BICKFORD, WILLIAM REGINALD, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Sept., 1911).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty.,

home dept., Apl., 1939; instructor in A.R.P., May, 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

BIGGIE, HENRY JAMES LESLIE, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 12th March, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., on 20th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1927; confd., Nov., 1936; comdt. and dist. supt., Port Blair, Apl., 1944.

BIGGIE, LAWRENCE PATRICK, Indian Police (b. 24th Apl., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Nov., 1936; confd. Apl., 1938.

BIGGS-DAVISON, JOHN ALEC, Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 7th June, 1918).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn. after seln. in June, 1941, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; on depts. to Bengal, Aug., 1943.

BIGSBY, SYDNEY HERBERT, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 26th July, 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; on deputation to Jaipur State from 1st Aug., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1926; confd., March, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt., p.w.d., irrign. bch., May, 1936; confd., Nov., 1937; ret'd., July, 1940.

BIJAWAT, MUL CHAND (see Mul Chand Bijawat).

BIJAY, SIR CHAND MAHTAB (see Burdwan).

BILARAM, RUPCHAND (see Rupchand, B.).

BILL, CHARLES ESTWICK SYDNEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 15th March, 1914).—Educ. at Haileybury and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

BILL, VEN. SYDNEY ALFRED, M.A., Eccles. Dept., United Provs. (Bishop of Lucknow) (b. 21st Feb., 1884).—Apptd. a chaplain on probn., 15th Dec., 1911; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1914; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1924; archdeacon of Lucknow, Nov., 1925, to May, 1937; ret'd., Aug., 1938; Bishop of Lucknow, Feb., 1939.

BILLCLIFFE, LESLIE, India Office (asst. to advr. on publicity) (b. 25th Feb., 1893).—On mil. service, Aug., 1914, to May, 1919; apptd. clerical offr., June, 1922; resdt. clerk, Oct., 1937, to Feb., 1938; asst. to advr. on publicity, Feb., 1938.

BILLIMORIA, KAIKHOSRU DHANJISHAH, B.Sc. (b. 21st Sept., 1902).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1924, as dep. supt. of police, Bombay; prom. to Indian Police, as supt., 1st Apl., 1937.

BILMORIA, RUSTORNE SORABJI, M.A., L.L.B., Barr., Bombay Jud. Dept. (offl. assignee).—Apptd. offl. assignee, high ct., Bombay, 1st Dec., 1935.

BINDRA BAN, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 7th July, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st May, 1913; asst. auditor genl., Jan., 1933; dept. acct.-genl., posts and tels., May, 1936; asst. auditor genl., Nov., 1936; ditto, personnel, Apl., 1938; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1939; dep. acct. genl., P. & T., Delhi, June, 1940; D.A.G., United Provs., Apr., 1941.

BINGHAM, HON. HUGH TERENCE DE BURGH, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 31st Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. dep. dir. projects, rly. bd., May, 1924; offg. supt. of works, Aug., 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1929; and again, May, 1933; confd., June, 1935; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of

rlys., June, 1934; chief engr. (provl.), Aug., 1937; ret'd., Dec., 1940.

BINNEY, ANTHONY LOCKHART, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (advr. to the govr., Central Provs.) (b. 18th July, 1890).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1914, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on mil. service from March, 1915, to Sept., 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), Aug., 1925; confd., May, 1926; with resident, Hyderabad, from 3rd Sept., 1928; dir.-genl. and secty., rev. dept., Nizam's govt., Aug., 1931; offg. commr., June, 1934; finl. secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1935; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Central Provs., Feb., 1936; commr. (provl.), Nov., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; commr. confd., Feb., 1939; fin. commr., Jan., 1941; adviser to the govr., Jan., 1944.

BINNS, BERNARD OTTWELL, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of settlements, etc., Burma) (b. 16th July, 1898).—Educ. at Grammar Sch., Bury, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. commr., June, 1930; dep. secty., fin. dept., Dec., 1935; commr. of settlements and record, Burma, Dec., 1936; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; jt. sec., reconstrn. dept., 1944.

BION, ARNOLD EUSTACE, C.I.E., late Indian Police, Bihar and Orissa (b. 2nd Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 1st Dec., 1910; supt., June, 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1934; and again, Apl., 1936; confd., Dec., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1942; inspr.-genl., Bihar, July, 1940; ret'd., 23rd Oct., 1944.

BION, CLARENCE RAYMOND FIELD, late Indian Police, Assam (b. 31st July, 1884).—Ret'd., March, 1935.*

BIRCH, REV. CANON ORMONDE WINSTANLEY, M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estab. (b. 14th May, 1870).—Ret'd., Sept., 1936.*

BIRD, RICHARD ERNEST ROWLAND, Capt., I.A. (b. 5th March, 1912), apptd. to I.P.S. from mil. employ on probn., Feb., 1938; pol. offcr., Trucial Coast, Oct., 1944.

BIRDWOOD, BARON (SIR WILLIAM RIDDELL BIRDWOOD), G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., C.I.E., D.S.O., L.L.D., Field Marshal, I.A. (b. 13th Sept., 1865).—C.-in-C. in India and member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Aug., 1925, to Nov., 1930.*

BISCOE, DUDLEY HUGH, Capt., M.B.E., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 12th July, 1909).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1929; pers. asst. to A. to G.G., Gujarat States and redt. at Baroda, Dec., 1934; ditto, Cent. India, March, 1935; under trng. in U.P., June and Oct., 1935; asst. claims commr., Quetta, July, 1935; under secty. to A.G.G. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, June, 1937; confd., Feb., 1938; under-secty. and pers. asst. to A.G.G., resdt. and ch. commr., in Baluchistan, Apl., 1938; special duty in Mysore, Oct., 1939; asst. pol. agent, Nov., 1940, to Apl., 1943; M.B.E., June, 1943; pol. agt., Chattisgath States, Feb., 1944.

BISCOE, WALTER TREWEEK, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. comml. manager) (b. 8th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 11th July, 1910; on milty. duty from 6th Oct., 1914, to 28th Oct., 1918; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., June, 1928; resdt. manager, Indian rly. publicity bureau,

New York, Dec., 1930; divl. comml. manager, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1936; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, May, 1936; divl. supdt. (junior), July, 1938; ditto, senr., Oct., 1939; dep. chief comml. manager, provl., Apl., 1940; permanent Sept., 1941.

BISHUMBHAR DAYAL, M.A., Indian Police (b. 29th July, 1907).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., United Provs., 10th Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1932.

BISHEN CHAND, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Oct., 1884).—Retd. Oct., 1939.*

BISHOP, JOHN EADIE, Indian Police (b. 15th Dec., 1917).—Asst. supt., Bihar, apptd. on probn., Dec., 1937; confd., Apl., 1939.

BISHOP, LAURENCE ARTHUR, O.B.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 3rd Feb., 1895).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1914; asst. supt., Jan., 1920; on milty. duty from 4th Aug., 1916, to 2nd Jan., 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1924; confd., Feb., 1930; on foreign service as commr. of police, Travancore, Aug., 1928; reverted to Brit. service, Nov., 1932; confd. as D.I.G., July, 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; confd. as inspr.-genl., Aug., 1944.

BISHOP, THEODORE BENDYSH WATSON, B.A. Indian C.S. (advr. to the govr., United Provs.) (b. 13th Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Rugby, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; pte. sec. to the lieut.-govr., United Provs., May to July, 1916; jt. mag., Dec., 1916; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1924; mag. and collr. (prov. sub.), Jan., 1927; confd., July, 1927; dep. commr., Nov., 1927; magte. and collr. again, Nov., 1932; commr., Aug., 1939; confd., Nov., 1942; advr. to the govr., Dec., 1944.

BISHWA NATH LAHIRI, M.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 13th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 9th Aug., 1922; offg. supt., Dec., 1925; confd., Dec., 1932.

BISSETT, ALLAN, India Audit Office (asst. to auditor) (b. 4th March, 1895).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 5th Aug., 1914; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1926; suptg. exec. officer, Oct., 1934; asst. to auditor, Oct., 1943.

BISWAS, CHARU CHANDRA, C.I.E., Judl. Dept., Bengal, offg. judge, Calcutta high ct., from March, 1937, judge, Feb., 1940.

BISWAS, SARAT CHANDRA, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 1st June, 1897).—Joined service Jan., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1924; exec. engr. (provl.), Nov., 1938.

BISWAS, SATISH CHANDRA, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Indian State Rlys. (dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 12th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as dist. surg., 13th Apl., 1918; dist. medl. officer, Apl., 1929.

BISWAS, SATISH CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, M.B.E. (b. 23rd Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th July, 1904; supt., F. and P. Dept., Nov., 1927; asst. secty., ditto, May, 1935; asst. secty., political dept., Apl., 1937; M.B.E., June, 1939; ret'd., March, 1940.

BIVAR, HUGH GODFREY STUART, Indian C.S. A.I.R.O., (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 28th Nov., 1897).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford (Classical Exhibitioner); joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst.

mag. and collr.; sub-divl. officer, Aug., 1923; addl. presdy. mag., Apl., 1926; adju. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1928; offg. ditto, Nov., 1931; confd., Feb., 1934; on war service, May, 1940.

BLACK, D. G. LYALL, Indian Police (supt. United Provs.) (b. 4th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 9th July, 1920; offg. supt., Apl., 1923; confd., Aug., 1927.

BLACKALLER, REV. DAVID WILLIAM, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 31st July, 1913).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 27th Oct., 1942.

BLACKBURN, JOHN INGAMELLS, B.A. Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 24th Nov., 1910).—Educ. at County High Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

BLACKSTONE, THOMAS WEST, late Indian Police (b. 3rd July, 1881).—Retd., July, 1936.*

BLACKWOOD, JAMES FULTON, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 20th Nov., 1883).—Retd., June, 1935.*

BLAGDEN, JOHN BASIL (b. 8th Oct., 1901).—Judge, high ct., Rangoon, Feb., 1940; ditto, Bombay, July, 1944.

BLAIR, FRANCIS RITCHIE, M.A., late Indian Educ. Service, Bihar (b. 10th Apl., 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

BLAIR, JAMES RICHARD, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Bengal) (b. 20th Aug., 1890).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service from March, 1915, to Feb., 1919; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Feb., 1920; confd., March, 1921; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1922; mag. and collr., Nov., 1926; dep. secty., pol. and appt. dept., Apl., 1927; dep. secty. to govt. of India, army dept., June, 1930; offg. addl. secty., pol. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; secty., communications and works dept., Bengal, Oct., 1938; ch. sec. to Govt. (confd.), Apl., 1942.

BLAKE, ALEXANDER GRANVILLE, late Indian C.S., Madras (b. 28th Aug., 1895).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

BLAKE, EDGAR VALENTINE MYLES, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936; ret'd., Feb., 1940.

BLAKE, REGINALD GILBERT, India Office (executive officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 20th Feb., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 3rd Dec., 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; acting hr. exec. offr., July, 1939.

BLAKER, CHARLES EDWARD, O.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th June, 1881).—Retd., June, 1936.*

BLAKISTON, JOHN FRANCIS, C.I.E., late Archaeological Survey Dept. (b. 21st March, 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

BLANFORD, HARRY RICHARD, O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 15th June, 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

BLANK, ABRAHAM LEWIS, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 19th May, 1891).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Nov., 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; apptd. to the

I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Nov., 1919; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Oct., 1920; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1923; affg. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; commr Assam, Nov., 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; commr. for workmen's compn., March, 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Assam, legisl. dept., March, 1935; confd., Nov., 1935; secty., judicial dept. and legal remembrancer, Bengal, May, 1939; judge, Calcutta high ct., Feb., 1942.

BLEASE, REV. RUPERT GORDON, B.Sc., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 12th Oct., 1896).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 11th Dec., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929.

BLENCH, THOMAS, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Nov., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 15th Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

BLEWITT, ALBERT ERNEST, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 2nd Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 15th June, 1920; offg. supt., June, 1924; and again, Feb., 1930; confd., Aug., 1932.

BLOMFIELD, DOUGLAS JOHN, C.I.E., A.C.G.I., late Indian Service of Engrs., Bengal (b. 20th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908, as asst. engr.; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1927; confd., Feb., 1931; offg. ch. engr., March, 1934; retd., Dec., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

BLUMENTHAL, SIMON, late India Audit Office (b. 2nd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Audit Office, 25th Apl., 1901; minor staff clerk, Oct., 1919; staff clerk, Feb., 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1922; suptg. ditto, Aug., 1933; retd., Jan., 1944.

BLUNDEN, ALFRED CHARLES, Indian Police (cent. intell. offr., United Provs.) (b. 18th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1912; supt., Aug., 1924; addl. asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Jan., 1931; ditto, spec. bch., Apl., 1936; cent. intell. offr., U.P., Nov., 1937; confd. as dep. inspr. genl., March, 1942.

BLUNT, ALFRED DICKINSON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Nov., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

BO, U, Indian Police (b. 13th Aug., 1911).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 21st Jan., 1936.

BOAG, SIR GEORGE TOWNSEND, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1884).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. settlt. officer, Sept., 1912; spl. duty as asst. to controller of tan stuffs, Madras, Apl., 1918; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1919; offg. secy. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1923; supt. of census, Madras, Apl., 1920, to July, 1922; commr., corpn. of Madras, Nov., 1923, to Apl., 1925; on special duty at Bangalore under govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Apl. to June, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte. and sec. to govt. of Madras, fin. dept., June, 1925; confd., Aug., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; on spl. duty in ch. secretariat, June, 1930; curator, Madras record office, Nov., 1930; member, Indian tariff board, Apl., 1931; offg. addl. secty. to govt., Madras, public dept., Oct., 1933; secty. to govt., law and educn. dept., Madras, Apl., 1935; actg. second secty. to govt., Madras, Apl., 1935; ditto (provl. subs.), March, 1936; C.S.I., June, 1936; offg. ch. secty. to govt.,

Madras, July, 1936; again, July, 1938; secty. to the Governor, Apl., 1937; actg. govr. of Orissa, Aug.-Dec., 1938; adviser to the govr., Nov., 1939; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1941; retd., Nov., 1943.

BOCQUET, GUY SUTTON, C.I.E., V.D., late Indian State Railways (b. 14th May, 1882).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

BOEDDICKER, FRANCIS LEONARD FITZMAURICE, Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt., Burma Rlys.) (b. 18th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 7th July, 1910; dist. loco. supt., March, 1926; offg. dep. loco. supt., Apl., 1929.

BOKHARI, A. S., C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.), All-India Radio (contr. of broadcasting) (b. 1st Oct., 1898).—Apptd. station dir., All-India Radio, March, 1936; dep. contr. of broadcasting June, 1936; contr. of broadcasting, Apl., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1944.

BOLAM, DOUGLAS HAIG, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1916).—Educ. at Whitgift Sch., Croydon, and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 7th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; pers. asst. to commr., July, 1940; on famine duty, May, 1941; sub. divl. offr., Nov., 1941; pol. asst. and comdt., Border mil. police, May, 1942; asst. dir., food supplies, 1944.

BOLAM, JOHN WALLACE, Indian Police (b. 30th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 17th Nov., 1926; asst. to A.G.G. in C., India, in criminal bch., March, 1933; supt. of police, Benares State, Feb., 1934; supt., provl. sub., March, 1940; confd., June, 1940.

BOLDEY, TIGRANE DAVID, P.W. Dept., Burma (div. elec. engr.) (b. 16th June, 1896).—Joined the service as temp. engr., March, 1927 div. elec. engr., Apl., 1937.

BOLTON, SIR HORATIO NORMAN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1875).—Retd., Oct., 1931.*

BOLTON, REGINALD RAINSBURY, M.C., B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—(exec. engr., Burma).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1927; confd., Oct., 1929.

BOMFORD, GUY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 28th June, 1899).—First commn. dated 28th Feb., 1917; in milty. employ to 24th Aug., 1921; proby. asst. supt., Aug., 1921; confd., Oct., 1925; supt., Aug., 1929.

BONAR, ROBERT, A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. mech. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 10th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, 1st Apl., 1927; production engr., Sept., 1928; works manager, Oct., 1930; offg. divl. supt., mechl., E.I. Rly., Sept., 1935; dep. chief mechl. engr.; confd., Sept., 1937; chief ditto, provl., Nov., 1940.

BONARJEE, NEIL BRUNIAT, Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., United Provs.) (b. 10th March, 1901).—Educ. at Dulwich and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1925; arrived, 9th Dec., 1925; and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr.; jr. magte., March, 1927; offg. dep. commr., March, 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1934; officr. on spec. duty Imp. Council Agric. Research, Nov., 1936; ditto, dept. of e.h. and l., Nov., 1937; collr. and magte., Jan., 1939; sec. to govt., U.P., local self govt., etc., March, 1944.

BONTOFT, REV. REGINALD ARTHUR, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt., N.W.F.P.—Retd. Oct., 1936.*

BOON, GEORGE ALAN JAMES, Indian Police (b. 3rd Oct., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provs., Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

BOOTH, EDGAR HICKMAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.)—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1929; asst. exec. engr., 31st Oct., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1938; engr. offcr. (temp.), civil aviation directorate, June, 1940; tfd. to posts and air dept. of central govt., confd. as engr. offcr., Oct., 1944.

BOOTH, JOHN REGINALD TREVOR, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; appointed after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Jan., 1916; spl. duty in posts and telegraphs dept., July, 1918; postmr.-genl., Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1919; ditto, central circle, Feb., 1920; offg. dep. dir.-gen. of post office, May, 1924; postmr.-genl.
