Punjab and N.W.F. circle, Feb., 1925; dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., May, 1928; P.M.G., Bengal and Assam, and offg. senr., dep. dir.-genl., March, 1930; confd., Feb., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; P.M.G., Bombay circle, Oct., 1936; retd., 25th Nov., 1942.

BOOTH, JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Sind) (b. 14th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Coll., Co. Kildare, Balliol Coll., Oxford, University Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. minister, Khairpur state, May, 1935; collr. and dist. magte. (confd.), Jan., 1940; jt. sec. to govt., Bombay (fin.) and dir. of civ. supplies, July, 1943.

BOOTH-GRAVELY, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd May, 1882).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., and the Univ., Edinburgh, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Apl., 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr., asst. sec. to govt., March, 1909; offg. under-sec., May, 1910; asst. sec., rev. dept., Nov., 1910, to Nov., 1911; pte. sec. to lt.-govr., Aug., 1912; under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1913; dep. sec. to ditto, ditto (s.p.t.), Oct., 1916, to Apl., 1918; dep. commr., Apr., 1918; offg. sec. to govt. of Burma, Apl., 1920; member of Burma legisl. council, June, 1920; rev. sec. to govt. of Burma, Jan., 1921; sec. to govt., fin. and rev. dept., Feb., 1924; offg. commr., Jan., 1928; ditto (provl. subs.), Nov., 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ch. secty. to govt., Burma, Aug., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Burma, May, 1936; counsellor to the govr., Apl., 1937; K.C.M.G., Jan., 1939; acting govr. of Burma, July, 1939, to Sept., 1939; retd., June, 1940.

BOR, NORMAN LOFTUS, C.I.E., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (forest botanist) (b. 4th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 20th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Aug., 1924; pol. offcr., Balipura frontier tract, Dec., 1931; bot. for. offcr. and silvicul., Assam, Nov., 1936; for. botanist, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1937; admistr. (south), Assam refugee orgn., Oct., 1942; ch. refugee admstr., Burma refugee orgn., Assam, Dec., 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

BOSE (see also Basu).

BOSE, ARUN KUMAR, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., M.B.E., late Bihar and Orissa C.S. (b. 27th Nov., 1880).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

BOSE, BIRENDRA KUMAR, L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Sept., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

BOSE, CONSTANCE HOPE, Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, exec. grade, educn. dept.).—Apptd. educ. officer, 14th Apl., 1930.

BOSE, FANINDRA NATH, B.Sc., M.R.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 10th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1914, as junr. engr.; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; asst. exec. engr., E.I. Rly., Nov., 1927; exec. engr. (junr.), March, 1928; ditto (senr.), Oct., 1933.

BOSE, HEMENDRA KUMAR, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 10th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as probnr., 11th Feb., 1916; asst. acct.-genl., Feb., 1918; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, May, 1929; ditto, Bengal, Sept., 1929; examr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Aug., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., June, 1932; offg. dep. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Feb., 1933; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Apl., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., United Provs., Jan., 1935; offg. dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Jan., 1935; dep. ch. accounts offr., E.B. Rly., Jan., 1937; ditto, E.I. Rly., Jan., 1939; finl. advr. and ch. accts. offcr., E.B. Rly., June, 1940; acct.-genl., Bengal, Nov., 1940; confd. acct.-genl. (cl. I), Sept., 1944.

BOSE, KUMUDINI KANTA, B.S. (Univ. of Illinois), Assoc. A.S.M.E. (contr. of purchase) (b. 1st March, 1887).—Joined the service, 12th July, 1924, as asst. contr. of purchase, Indian Stores Dept.; contr. of purchase, Calcutta Circle, 9th July, 1937; deputy contr. of purchase (supply), dept. of supply.

BOSE, NABA KUMAR, Diwan Bahadur, B.Sc. Cal. (Glas.), A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (asst. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1892).—Asst. dist. loco. supt., Feb., 1918; asst. supt. junior scale, Oct., 1926; asst. supt. senior scale, May, 1934; dep. ch. mech. engr. provl., Oct., 1938; confd., Aug., 1939.

BOSE, SUDHANSU KUMAR, B.Sc., A.R.S.M., Indian Sch. of Mines (b. 7th Oct., 1900).—Apptd. prof. of mining and surveying, 7th Dec., 1927; offg. vice-princl., Indian sch. of mines, May, 1935.

BOSE, SUDHANSU MOHAN, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), Barr.-at-Law, apptd. Member, Public Service Commission, Bengal, 13th Apl., 1937.

BOSE, VIVIAN, B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), Barr. Judi. Dept., Central Provs. (judge, high ct., Nagpur).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

BOTTOMLEY, JOHN EDWARD, Indian State Rlys. (works manager, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 13th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1920; asst. works manager, G.I.P. Rly., Jan., 1925; works manager, Oct., 1932; dep. ch. mech. engineer, provl., Feb., 1941.

BOTTOMLEY, JOHN MELLOR, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 21st March, 1888).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's Sch., Crosby, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service as prof., Dacca Coll., 12th Feb., 1911; inspr. of schools, Nov., 1925; asst. dir. of public instrn., Aug., 1926; princpl., Chittagong Coll., Dec., 1926; asst. dir. of public instrn. for Muhammadan educn., Bengal, June, 1929; princpl., presy. coll., Calcutta, Aug., 1931; dir. of public instrn.,

Bengal, May, 1933; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, educn. dept., July, 1934; C.I.E., May, 1937; retd., March, 1943; re-empld., March, 1943.

BOTTOMS, ALVAN DOUGLAS, India Audit Office (higher executive officer) (b. 22nd Sept., 1899).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 8th Aug., 1919, after previous service from 25th Apr., 1917; second class clerk, 29th Apr., 1920; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; transf. to India Audit Office, March, 1922; exec. officer, Apr., 1922; higher exec. officer, Oct., 1934; lent to Min. of Wks., Oct., 1941; actg. suptg. exec. offr., Jan., 1944.

BOUCHIER, EDWARD WALLACE, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sub collr., Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1906).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar Sch., and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Sept., 1932; under secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, June, 1934; ditto, home dept., Apr., 1936; ditto, public dept., Sept., 1937, to July, 1938; secty., Central Road Traffic Board, Madras, Nov., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1944.

BOUCHIER, OWEN PLATT, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accs. dept.) (b. 2nd March, 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 14th Apr., 1920; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apr., 1925; actg. higher exec. offr., July, 1940.

BOULD, REGINALD PERRY, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 6th Jan., 1884).—Joined the service on N.W. Ry., on 16th Nov., 1905; services transferred to G.I.P. Ry. from 6th Apr., 1912; asst. tel. supt., Apr., 1912; dep. transptn. supt. (tels.), Oct., 1926; retd., Apr., 1940.

BOULTON, REV. WALTER, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 11th July, 1901).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Bengal, Jan., 1927; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1939.

BOURDILLON, SIR BERNARD HENRY, K.C.M.G., K.B.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1913; regr., high court, Allahabad, May, 1915; apptd. to I.A.R.O., Sept., 1917; jt. mag. (seconded), March, 1918; judl. asst., Persian Gulf, June, 1918; land acquisition offr., Baghdad, March, 1919; dep. sec., Iraq High commn., Dec., 1921; sec., ditto, Jan., 1922; mag. and collr., Apr., 1923, and sec. to high commr., Iraq; councillor to ditto, Nov., 1924; C.M.G., June, 1924; transferred to Colonial Service as Colonial Secty., Ceylon, 27th Nov., 1929; K.B.E., June, 1931; govr. of Uganda, Nov., 1932; K.C.M.G., Jan., 1934; retd. from Colonial Service, Dec., 1943.

BOURNE, FREDERICK CHALMERS, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., Home Dept., Punjab) (b. 12th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Rugby and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1926; ditto (perm.), Oct., 1929; senr. secty. to finl., commrs. and dy. secty. to govt., Punjab, development dept., Feb., 1932; secty. to govt., Punjab, elec. and industries dept., July, 1934, to March, 1937; dep. commr., March, 1937; secty. to govt., Punjab, Home Dept., Aug.,

1940; ch. sec. to govt., Oct., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; C.S.I., Jan., 1944.

BOURNE, GERALD COOKE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 23rd Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Gresham's Sch., Holt, Norfolk, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. registrar, high ct., Rangoon, Aug., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1934; regr., high ct., Rangoon, March, 1936; confd. dist. and sessions judge, March, 1938.

BOWDEN, JAMES WILLIAM, M.B., B.Ch. (Dub.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), Capt. I.M.S. (civil surgeon) (b. 16th Nov., 1902).—Date of first commn., 20th Oct., 1932, granted an antedate of one year; date of rank, 1st May, 1934; civil employ in Orissa, 15th Oct., 1938.

BOWEN, JOHN CHARLES EDWARDS, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 8th Oct., 1909).—First commn., 28th Aug., 1930; pers. asst. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Nov., 1934; under trng. in U.P., June, 1935; again, Oct., 1935; asst. to claims commr., Quetta, July, 1935; under secty. to resdt., Punjab States, Sept., 1937; confd., Sept., 1938; vice-consul, Zahidan, Apr., 1939; under sec. to resident for Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1941; on deputation, 1941; asst. commr., Nov., 1943.

BOWEN, ROWLAND, Indian Police (b. 27th Feb., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936; confd. Apr., 1938.

BOWER, GEORGE NOEL, B.A., late Imperial Customs Service (b. 25th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

BOWER, REV. JAMES ARNOLD HAROLD, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 19th Dec., 1897).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 21st Dec., 1944.

BOWERS, EDWIN GUTHRIE, Major, M.C., M.I.E.E. (mech. and elect. engr., Madras Port Trust) (b. 21st June, 1888).—Apptd. mech. and elect. engr., 1st Jan., 1928.

BOWLER, VERNON ROBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 8th Aug., 1889).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 23rd Jan., 1911, and attd. to record and registry dept.; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to March, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apr., 1922; higher clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1926; higher exec. offr., March, 1939; actg. prin. exec. offr., July, 1941.

BOWMAN, ARCHIBALD IAN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.), U.P. (b. 11th May, 1915).—Educ. at Glasgow High Sch. and Glasgow Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

BOWMAN, JOHN BLAKE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 26th May, 1917).—Educ. at George Watson's Boys' Coll., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

BOWN, ARTHUR MERVYN, B.A., M.C., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Gainsborough Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914; apptd., 3rd Feb., 1920; arrived, 25th March, 1920, and served in Burma; supt., Southern Shan States, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., July, 1924; offg. commr. of inc.-tax, Bengal, March, 1933; ditto, Burma, Nov., 1933;

ditto, N.W.F.P., March, 1934; ditto, N.W.F.P., Jan., 1935; confd., Jan., 1935; ditto, Burma, Apl., 1937; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1938; retd., 30th April, 1940.

BOWN, JOSEPH EDGAR, late Indian Police, Bihar (b. 16th Feb., 1883).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

BOWSTEAD, JOHN, C.I.E., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief sec. to govt., Orissa) (b. 16th Feb., 1897).—Educ. at King's Sch., Worcester, and Corpus Christi, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Nov., 1921; arrived, 13th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-sec., pol. and appt. dept., Apl., 1925; confd., July, 1926; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa Feudatory States, Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. agent, ditto, Feb., 1932; offg. secty. to A.G.G., Eastern States, and pol. agent, Sambalpur, Apl., 1933; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1935; confd., March, 1936; secty. to govr., Orissa, March, 1938; addtl. pol. agent, Orissa States, July, 1939; sec. to govr., Feb., 1940; addtl. sec., govt. of Orissa (home and fin.), July, 1940; offg. ch. sec. to govt. of Orissa, Aug., 1940; again, June, 1942; again, Feb., 1943; also dir. of civil def., and provl. press advr.; C.I.E., June, 1942.

BOYCE, PETER MCCONNELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 13th Aug., 1918).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., Wimbledon, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. after seln. in May, 1940, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; spec. offr., commn. dept., June, 1944.

BOYD, SIR DONALD JAMES, K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S., Punjab (b. 23rd Dec., 1877).—Retd., Apl., 1937.*

BOYD, THOMAS CRAWFORD, C.I.E., Col., late I.M.S. (b. 26th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1908; in mil. employ up to 7th Aug., 1921; chemical examr. and prof. of chemistry, Aug., 1921; prin. medl. coll., Calcutta, Apl., 1932; offg. surg.-genl., Bengal, Dec., 1934; and again, Oct., 1936; inspr. gen., civil hospitals, United Provs., June, 1939; retd., Aug., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

BOZMAN, GEOFFREY STEPHEN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (sec., govt. of India, infm. and broadcasting) (b. 26th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Whitgift Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. asst. settlt. offr., Feb., 1925; under secty. to govt., law dept., Madras, May, 1927; offg. secty., ditto, May, 1928; secty., tariff bd., June, 1930; secty. to agent of govt. of India in South Africa, Oct., 1932; commr. of Coorg, March, 1935; addl. dep.-secty. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, March, 1936; dep. secty., educn., health and lands dept., March, 1939; jt. secty., ditto, Apl., 1940; secty., dept. of Indians Overseas, govt. of India, Oct., 1941; secty., infm. and broadcasting, Dec., 1943; on depn. to U.K. and U.S.A., June-Oct., 1944; on spec. duty from Nov., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1938.

BRACKEN, SIR GEOFFREY THOMAS HIRST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S., Madras (b. 11th Apl., 1879).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

BRACKENBURY, SIR CECIL FABIAN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th March, 1881).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and spl. settlt. offr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1916; offg. collr.

and dist. mag., Dec., 1918; ditto, Dec., 1919; ditto, Nov., 1921; ditto, Nov., 1924; confd., Apl., 1925; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, July, 1933; 3rd member, bd. of rev., Madras (provl. subs.), Aug., 1933; offg. 2nd member, ditto, and commr. of excise, Nov., 1933; 3rd member, bd. of rev. and 2nd member, ditto (provl.), Jan., 1935; offg. ch. secty. to Govt. Madras, Jan., 1935; confd., March, 1935; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; K.C.I.E., June, 1938; retd., Sept., 1940.

BRADBURY, CECIL HOWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1914; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; retd., March, 1935.*

BRADBURY, REV. JOHN HENRY, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 8th Oct., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 23rd Oct., 1925; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; senr. ditto, Apl., 1937.

BRADFIELD, SIR ERNEST WILLIAM CHARLES, K.C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Gen., I.M.S., retd. (presdt., medical bd., India Office) (b. 28th May, 1880).—Joined the service, 31st Aug., 1903; prof. of surg., medl. coll., Jan., 1924; offg. supt., gen. hosp., Madras, May, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1928; reverted to milty. employ, Apl., 1931; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bombay, June, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; hon. surg. to the King, Dec., 1935; presdt. medl. council of India, Feb., 1937; dir.-genl., I.M.S., March, 1937; member, Council of State, Feb., 1937; presdt., medical bd., India Office, Nov., 1939; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

BRADFORD, WALTER GLYNNE, late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 15th Oct., 1890).—Retd., 14th July, 1939.*

BRADLEY, CHARLES ALFRED KEMBLE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 19th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 2nd Nov., 1913; dist. loco. supt., Nov., 1922; offg. dep. loco. supt., March, 1930; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. rly., May, 1934; confd., Nov., 1936; offg. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1934; and again, Apl., 1937.

BRADLEY, JOHN BERTRAM GRANVILLE, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (registrar, high ct., Burma) (b. 12th March, 1899).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; sub-divl. officer, June, 1923; offg. under-sec., to govt., fin. and rev. dept., June, 1926; under-secy. to govt., home and pol. dept., Apl., 1927; dep. registrar, high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1930; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1932; confd., Nov., 1933; registrar, highct., March, 1939.

BRADSHAW, JOHN RICHARD LANDON, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 8th Nov., 1896).—First commn. dated 16th Dec., 1914; joined the pol. dept., 23rd Oct., 1922; asst. commr., Bannu, March, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, May, 1926; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1928; asst. commr., Charsadda, Jan., 1929; 3rd asst. resdt., Aden, Feb., 1930; secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Dec., 1930; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Jan., 1933; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transf. depts., Jan., 1936; ditto, devel. depts., Apl., 1937; secty. to resdt. for Rajputana, Apl., 1938; resdt., Western Rajputana States, Apl., 1939; civil a.r.p. offr., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1940; dy.

commr., Mardan, Apr., 1941; resdt., Waziristan, Oct., 1943; pol. agt., Malwa, Sept., 1944.

BRADSHAW-SMITH, ROBERT ALFRED, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 5th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1913; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; prof., civil engrg. coll., Roorkee, Jan., 1925, to Oct., 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Apr., 1934, and again, Apr., 1936; ret'd., Apr., 1940.

BRADY, H. W., late Factory Inspection Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 3rd July, 1879).—Ret'd., July, 1935.*

BRAGANZA, LEOPOLD SCHOLASTICUS, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 10th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th Apr., 1913; temp. engr., G.I.P. Rly., Dec., 1921; resdt. engr., Apr., 1924; exec. engr., May, 1935; ret'd., Feb., 1943.

BRAHAWAR, RAJ NARAYAN (see Raj, N.B.).

BRAHMAWAR, RAJ NARAYAN (see Raj Narayan, B.).

BRAILSFORD, REV. CYRIL, A.K.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 1st Feb., 1925; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1927; senr. chaplain, July, 1935; ret'd. Feb., 1942.

BRAJ BHUSAN LAL, B.A., late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 3rd June, 1880).—Ret'd., June, 1935.*

BRAMWELL, ERNEST CHARLES, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. engr., mechanical transport) (b. 16th Jan., 1889).—Apptd. asst. inspr. of stores, March, 1926; asst. engr., mechanical transport, Aug., 1926.

BRANDER, GEORGE MACONACHIE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; colonization offcr., Dec., 1936; dep. commr., subst. provl., May, 1941; divl. sec. to fin. commrs. and dep. sec. to govt., Oct., 1942; sec. to govr., March, 1943.

BRANDER, JAMES PATERSON, M.A., late Indian C.S., Bombay (b. 30th July, 1876).—Ret'd., Nov., 1935.*

BRANDOM, HAROLD FRANK, Office of High Commissioner for India (hr. exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 28th July, 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., India Office, 13th Oct., 1919; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apr., 1922; actg. hr. exec. offcr. variously from Nov., 1936; confd., Feb., 1939.

BRANDON, EDWIN HAROLD, M.B.E., late Central P.W.D. (b. 6th Sept., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 13th May, 1903; supt., leg. dept., s.p.t., 2nd Nov., 1915; confd., June, 1916; offg. asst. sec., govt. of India, home dept., Apr., 1922; confd., June, 1923; M.B.E., June, 1928; transferred temp. to dept. of industries and labour, P.W. branch, from June, 1930; admin. officer, central p.w.d., March, 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1940.

BRAUND, HENRY BENEDICT LINTHWAITE, Barr.-at-Law, Judl. Dept., India (judge, high ct., Allahabad).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, June, 1934; ditto, Allahabad, Sept., 1939.

BRAY, SIR DENYS DE SAUMAREZ, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Nov., 1875).—Ret'd. from I.C.S., May, 1930; adviser to sec. of state for India, Apr., to July, 1937.*

BRAYDEN, DESMOND ANTHONY, O.B.E., Indian Police (cent. intell. offcr., Bengal and Assam) (b. 28th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., Apr., 1926; confd., March, 1932; on depn. under G. of I. Home Dept. Intelligence Bureau, as central intelligence officer, Bengal and Assam, March, 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

BRAYE, PHILLIP GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st Dec., 1894).—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 13th Sept., 1923; arrived, 13th Nov., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1926; confd., Nov., 1932; excise and opium commr. in C.I., Nov., 1934, to Nov., 1936; civil recruiting and liaison offcr., C.P., May, 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

BRAYNE, ALBERT FREDERIC LUCAS, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S., Bombay (b. 1st Apr., 1884).—Ret'd., May, 1935.*

BRAYNE, FRANK LUGARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.C., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 6th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at Monkton Combe Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, aptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sec. to Delhi munpl. cttee., June, 1910, to Sept., 1912; on military service from Dec., 1915 (M.C., March, 1919), to Nov., 1919; dep. commr. Jan., 1922; offg. commr., Apr., 1931; offg. commr., Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1935; commr. rural reconstruction, Oct., 1934; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; finl. commr. (dev.) and secty. to govr., Punjab, Sept., 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; ret'd., Nov., 1941.

BRAYSHAY, SIR MAURICE WILLIAM, Kt., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 7th March, 1883); ret'd., Oct., 1932; and re-empld.*

BREAKEY, ARTHUR WILLIAM HUMPHREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. supt., Federated Shan States) (b. 23rd March, 1909).—Educ. at the High Sch., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; appointed after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Dec., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. supt., Shan States, Dec., 1938; on mil. service, Nov., 1940.

BREBNER, SIR ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Aug., 1883).—Ret'd., Aug., 1938.*

BREE, PHILIP GEORGE, name changed to BRAYE (q.v.).

BREMNER, CLAUDE EDWARD URQUHART, M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service, (b. 30th Aug., 1891).—First commn. dated 4th March, 1911; in milty. employ. to July, 1919; asst. pol. agent, July, 1919; asst. commr. Dec., 1924; on foreign service in Baroda State from 17th May, 1927; pol. agent, South Waziristan, Apr., 1928; vice-consul, Ahwaz, May, 1932; pol. agent and consul, Muscat, Nov., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Apr., 1936; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Feb., 1937; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, June, 1937; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, March, 1938; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, June, 1938; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, March, 1939; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, July, 1939; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1939; H.B.M. consul in Portuguese possessions in India, Nova Goa, Nov., 1940.

BRENDON, PATRICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th March, 1913).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the

Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. col. offr., Nov., 1940.

BRERETON, CHARLES CECIL TRELAWNY, M.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Dec., 1889).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

BRETT, CYRIL TEMPLETON, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 23rd July, 1885).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1906; asst. supt., May, 1909; supt., Oct., 1915; prinl. police training coll., Hazaribagh, March, 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1930; confd., March, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1931; offg. inspr.-genl., March, 1936; confd. inspr.-genl., March, 1938; retd., 23rd, July, 1940.

BRETT, WILLIAM BAILLIE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Nov., 1889).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., pol., appt., and educ. depts., Oct., 1917; ditto and pte. sec. to govr. of Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1920; sec., Indian taxation enquiry cttee., Oct., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1926; mag. and collr., Nov., 1928; secty., fin. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Apr., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; relief commr., Feb., 1934; ch. secty. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1935; C.S.I., May, 1937; retd., July, 1940.

BREWITT, CHARLES PATRICK, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 17th March, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 2nd Jan., 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rly., March, 1931; offg. dep. agent, Burma rlys., Apr., 1935.

BRIDGE, MAURICE FREDERICK, M.B.E., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 14th March, 1893).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

BRIDGMAN, FRANCIS HARRY GEORGE, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 14th Nov., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; offg. supt., June, 1935; a.d.c. to the Viceroy, Dec., 1938 to Jan., 1941.

BRIDLE, HENRY LEWIS, India Office (higher clerical officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 1st May, 1892).—Apptd. clerical offr., 21st Oct., 1921; higher clerical officer, Apr., 1926; lent to min. of aircraft prodn. June, 1940.

BRIGGS, HERBERT SAMUEL, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 22nd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. clerk, second class, 25th June, 1900; minor staff clerk, May, 1920; transfid. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; actg. higher exec. officer, Dec., 1921; confd., Apr., 1922; retd., Jan., 1942.

BRIGGS, NORMAN, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail dept., Sind (b. 18th Nov., 1891).—First commn. dated 8th Aug., 1917; in milty. employ. to 24th March, 1925; supt., borstal institution, Lahore, Apr., 1925; offg. dep. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Punjab, March, 1932; dir. of health and prisons, Sind, Apr., 1936; inspr. genl., civil hospitals, United Provs., Aug., 1943.

BRIJ BHUSAN SARAN JETLY, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th July, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 21st July, 1924; offg. supt., Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

BRIJ BHUSHAN SARAN VARMA, B.A., LL.B., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 10th June, 1908).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Jan., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

BRIJ MOHAN LAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 13th Sept.,

1898).—Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. exec. engr. May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1931; suptg. engr. (subst.), July, 1944.

BRIJ NARAYAN, B.A., Rai Bahadur, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Orissa) (b. 10th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. exec. engr., Apr., 1925; confd., Oct., 1926; under secty. to govt., p.w.d. (b. and r.), Nov., 1933; suptg. engr. (confd.), March, 1940, Orissa.

BRIJ NARAYAN, O.B.E., M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 18th Aug., 1895).—Held non-gaz. appts., May, 1918, to Apr., 1923; apptd. offcr., cl. II, Apr., 1923; addl. dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1939; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Nov., 1941, to Nov., 1944; O.B.E., June, 1943.

BRISLEE, FRANK RICHARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 3rd Dec., 1895).—Educ. at King's Coll. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 28th Oct., 1921; arrived, 12th Dec., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1928; spl. rev. offr., Cauvery-Mettur project, May, 1930; offg. collr., Bangalore, Apr., 1935; secty., bd. of rev., Madras, March, 1936; collr. (subst.), Apr., 1940.

BRISTOW, SIR CHARLES HOLDITCH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (advr. to govr. of Bombay) (b. 28th Dec., 1887).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1911, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., and supt., land records, etc.; on mil. duty, May, 1915, to Apr., 1919; actg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agt., May, 1920; settlement officer, Dec., 1923; dep. sec. to govt., genl., edl. and marine depts. (temp.), Nov., 1925; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Dec., 1926, and again, Apr., 1928; on special duty, Deccan canals, June, 1927; collr., Sept., 1929; offg. secty. to govt., Bombay, home and eccl. depts., Jan., 1935; secty. to govr. of Bombay, Apr., 1935, to Aug., 1938; C.I.E., May, 1937; commr. (confd.), Dec., 1937; advr. to govr. of Bombay, July, 1940; kt., June, 1944.

BROACH, FRANK ERNEST, Indian Police (b. 2nd Feb., 1919) (asst. supt., Bihar).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., Apr., 1942, a.d.c. to govr., Nov., 1942.

BROADBENT, ROBERT EVELYN CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Simla, March, 1938, and dep. commr. in addn.

BROADFOOT, ROBERT CAMPBELL, N.D.A., Indian Agric. Service (princl., agricl. coll., Coimbatore) (b. 5th Feb., 1887).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., June, 1919; offg. princl., agricl. coll., Coimbatore, Jan., 1934; conu., June, 1935.

BROADWAY, SIR ALAN BRICE, Kt., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 2nd Nov., 1873).—Retd., Oct., 1933.*

BROCK, HENRY NORMAN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th March, 1914; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; offg. divl. ditto, variously from March, 1927; divl. supt., confd., Jan., 1938.

BROCK, LESLIE EVELYN, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 13th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1908; asst. loco. supt., Nov., 1908; dist. ditto, Apl., 1915; dist. loco. officer, Dec., 1926; divl. operating officer, Dec., 1926; offg. divl. supt., Apl., 1928; confd., Feb., 1931; ditto (senr.), Apl., 1934; dep. ch. operating supt., N.W. Ry. (prov.), May, 1934; dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936; ret'd., Jan., 1940.

BROCKLEHURST, WALTER HARRISON, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 7th Sept., 1880).—Ret'd., Apl., 1936.*

BRODIE, NEAL, M.Sc., F.C.S., A.I.C. (b. 11th July, 1893).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1913, as asst. chemist, Govt. Test House, Alipore; chemist, Dec., 1918; supt., Govt. Test House, Aug., 1925; dir., Industrial Research Bureau (temp.), Nov., 1934; confd., March, 1938; ret'd., Feb., 1942.

BROKENSHAW, ARTHUR, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 8th Nov., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1906; supt., Apl., 1920; contr., telegraph office, March, 1927; supt., Calcutta tel. office, May, 1929; ch. supt., Bombay tel. office, May, 1929; divl. engr., tels., and offg. dir. of engrg., March, 1931; confd., Sept., 1932; offg. p.m.g., Madras, Apl., 1934; dir., tel. engrg., Bombay, Sept., 1934; offg. p.m.g., Bombay, March, 1935; p.m.g., Madras, June, 1935; dep. dir.-gen., tels., Apl., 1936; chief. engr., posts and tels., May, 1938; ret'd., June, 1941.

BROMAGE, JOHN ALDHELM RAIKES, C.I.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (exec. sany. engr.) (b. 7th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. sany. engr., June, 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1933; suptg. engr., health (temp.), Aug., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

BROMLEY, RALPH EDWARD, late Indian Police (b. 12th Jan., 1893).—Ret'd., June, 1939.*

BROMMAGE, JOSEPH CHARLES, O.B.E., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 4th Feb., 1897).—Joined the service as offr., cl. II, March, 1923; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., May, 1933, to Feb., 1937; on depn. to Burma, Apl., 1937, to March, 1940; revtd. to mil. accts. dept., March, 1940; dep. fin. advr., sy. fin., Apl., 1941; jt. fin. advr., sy. fin., Nov., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; cl. I (prov. subs.), Jan., 1942; on depn. to mil. fin. dept., Feb., 1944; addtl. fin. advr., March to Sept., 1944.

BROOKE, REV. JOHN, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 26th June, 1903; apptd. junr. chaplain on probn., 19th Jan., 1939).

BROOKES, ALBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service, Burma (b. 22nd Aug., 1885).—Ret'd., Dec., 1939.*

BROOKS, JOHN BIRTWHISTLE TYRRILI, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (chief consvr., Bombay) (b. 13th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1912, as asst. consvr.; on milty. duty from 8th Nov., 1917, to 24th Nov., 1918; dep. consvr., Dec., 1917; offg. consvr., Feb., 1934; and again, Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; ch. consvr., June, 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

BROOME, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 18th March, 1910).—Educ. at Latymer Upper Sch., Hammersmith, and Cairns Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Nov., 1936.

BROOMFIELD, SIR ROBERT STONEHOUSE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 1st Dec.,

1882).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 25th Nov., 1905, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and chief inspr. of factories; asst. judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1909; jt. judge and addl. sess. judge, June, 1913; offg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1919; confd., March, 1925; judge, high ct., Bombay, Nov., 1929; kt., Jan., 1941; actg. ch. justice, var. from June, 1939; ret'd., Dec., 1942.

BROUGHTON, REV. ARTHUR HARDWICKE, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 18th Oct., 1881).—Ret'd., Oct., 1936.*

BROWN, ALAN SUNLEY, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 15th Feb., 1893).—Apptd. to store dept., Dec., 1921; clerical officer, Feb., 1924; exec. officer, Jan., 1926.

BROWN, ARTHUR FREDERICK ROBERT, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 9th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Aug., 1925.

BROWN, CAMPBELL OSBORNE, Indian State Ryis. (works mang., E.I. Ry.) (b. 11th Feb., 1895).—Production engr., Jan., 1928; works manager, senr. scale, Jan., 1937.

BROWN, CHARLES HILTON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st March, 1890). (Madras). ret'd., Jan., 1935.*

BROWN, GILBERT ALEXANDER MURRAY, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., N.W.F. Prov.) (b. 24th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service 1st Oct., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., May, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; on spl. duty in Waziristan survey circle, May, 1929; chief. engr. and secty. to govt., N.W.F. Prov., Oct., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1942.

BROWN, JAMES BIRCH, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief sec., Madras) (b. 18th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 25th Nov., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mily. duty, Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; sub-collr. and jt. mag. (seconded), Feb., 1918; temp. under-sec. to govt., June, 1919; offg. sec., board of rev. (separate rev.), July, 1922; dep. sec. to govt., finance dept., Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to ditto, Apl., 1925; dep. sec. to ditto, June, 1925; offg. collr. of salt rev., March, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. collr. and dist. mag., June, 1932; confd., July, 1933; offg. commr. of lab., Madras, May, 1934, to Aug., 1934; member, bd. of rev., June, 1941; chief sec. govt. of Madras, June, 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1943.

BROWN, LESLIE NANSCAWEN, B.A., late Indian C.S., Bombay (b. 19th Nov., 1886).—Ret'd., Nov., 1936.*

BROWN, OSCAR HENRY, O.B.E., B.A., Barr.-at-Law, Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 4th July, 1896).—Presdy. mag., Bombay, Apl., 1935, confd. as chief presdy. mag., May, 1941.

BROWN, RICHARD HAROLD, India Office (higher clerical officer, milty. dept.) (b. 27th Apl., 1882).—Appointed clerical officer, 10th March, 1922; higher clerical officer, Sept., 1932; actg. hr. exec. offr., Nov., 1941.

BROWN, ROBERT SIDNEY, O.B.E., India Office (principal, milty. dept.) (b. 10th Feb., 1889).—Apptd. second-class clerk, India Office, 1st March, 1911, and attd. to the registry and record dept.; transfed. to mil. dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to Jan., 1919;

minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; asst. principal, Apl., 1921; temp. reading clerk to council, June, 1926; to Aug., 1927; jt. editor, India Office List, Aug., 1927, to Oct., 1932; actg. princpl., Oct., 1932; confd., Dec., 1934; O.B.E. Jan., 1945.

BROWN, SIR STUART KELSON, K.C.I.E., C.B., C.V.O., late India Office (b. 24th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge (Porson Prize, 1906); apptd. junr. clerk, store dept., India Office, 11th Oct., 1909; transf. to judl. and public dept., March, 1910; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1911; pte. sec. to parl. under-sec. of state, July, 1913; senr. clerk, March, 1918; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919 (mentioned in despatches, July, 1917); asst. sec. to Lord Crewe's commee. on the India Office, March, 1919; actg. pte. sec. to sec. of state, June, 1919; pte. sec. to sec. of state, June, 1919, to Sept., 1924; principal, Apl., 1921; joint sec., mil. dept., Sept., 1924; C.V.O., Jan., 1925; personally attached to Prime Minister for Indian Round Table Conf., London, Nov., 1930, to Jan., 1931; sec.-genl. to Indian del. to Disarmament conf., Jan.-July, 1932; and again, Jan., 1933; C.B., June, 1932; actg. asst. under-secy. of state, Sept., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934; sec. to Expert Cttee. on the Defence of India, 1938-39; K.C.I.E., June, 1939; apptd. actg. under secy. of state, June, 1940, to Dec., 1941; retd., 6th Jan., 1942.

BROWN, WILLIAM SCOTT, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Madras) (b. 30th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and University, Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914; and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., home dept., Apl., 1920; offg. sec., board of rev., Madras, March, 1924; offg. collr. of Madras, etc., Aug., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Nov., 1928; collr. and magte. (provl. subs.), Sept., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; secty. to govt., p.w. and lab. depts., Madras, Jan., 1935, to Oct., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1936; sec. to govt., finance dept., Madras, March, 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1942; member, bd. of rev., Nov., 1942.

BROWNE, CECIL ARTHUR, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 17th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 1st Nov., 1927; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1934; confd., Oct., 1936; relief engr., and supply offr., Nov., 1936; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

BROWNE, ERNEST GARDINER, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 8th May, 1884).—Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 23rd Apl., 1906; confd., Oct., 1907; dist. opium officer, Apl., 1925; opium agent and manager, opium factory, Ghazipur, Jan., 1938; retd., 8th June, 1940; re-empld. in A.R.P. Dept., U.P., July, 1940.

BROWNE, ROBERT STEEL, O.B.E., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 31st July, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; pers. asst. to chief consvr., June, 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; on mil. duty, Jan., 1940.

BROWNE, WALTER FITZGERALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 3rd March, 1897).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1921; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1921; asst. mag. and collr., United Provs., 3rd Dec., 1921; asst. commr. from Oct., 1923, to May, 1924; jt. mag., Nov.,

1927; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1930; offg. magte. and collr., Feb., 1935; confd., Apl., 1935.

BROWNLOW, ANDREW L'ESTRANGE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 19th Feb., 1921; dist. supt., Jan., 1924.

BROWNSDON, THOMAS EDWARD, Capt., I.A., Indian Political service (b. 26th June, 1910); first commn., 30th Jan., 1932; pers. asst. to res. at Hyderabad, Jan., 1936; under trng. in U.P., Sept., 1936; under sec. to A.G.G., res. and C.C. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1938; confd. in Indian Political Service, June, 1939; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, July, 1939; asst. pol. agent in Baluchistan, under orders of pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1939; 2nd asst. pol. agent, Mekran, Dec., 1939; attaché, British Legation, Kabul, Dec., 1940; asst. commr., Kohat, 1941; under sec., ext. affairs dept., Sept., 1944.

BRUCE, HAROLD EASTON, M.C., Indian Police (centr. intell. offcr., Bihar and Orissa) (b. 18th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 20th March, 1921; offg. supt., Oct., 1923; confd., March, 1931; supt., police training coll., Feb., 1931; cent. intell. offcr., Bihar and Orissa, March, 1938.

BRUCE-JOY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 23rd March, 1891).—Joined the service as dist. loco. supt., 22nd May, 1920; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; supt., May, 1926; offg. divl. ditto, variously from March, 1927; confd., March, 1939.

BRUNYATE, SIR JAMES BENNETT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (retd.).—Retd. from Indian C.S., May, 1917; member of the Council of India, from May, 1917, to Sept., 1924.*

BRYAN, DENZIL ARNOLD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 15th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at the Lawrence Coll., Ghora Galli, Univ. of the Punjab, and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offr., Apl., 1939; addtl. dep. commr., March, 1939; regr., high ct., Apl., 1939; sec. to Premier, May, 1944.

BRYANT, STANLEY CLARE, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 13th March, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 11th May, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Apl., 1931.

BRYANT, WALTER TELHAM, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 12th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Apl., 1931; pte. secty. to govr., Madras, June, 1938; O.B.E., June, 1943.

BRYSON, CHRISTOPHER LENWOOD, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 9th Nov., 1908).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1935; under-secy., def. co-ord. dept., govt. of India, March, 1940; ditto, home dept., May, 1940; ditto, civil def., Oct., 1941, to 1943.

BU, SIR MYA, Barr., Judl. Dept., Burma (ch. justice, high ct., Rangoon) (b. 10th March, 1885).—Apptd. asst. gov. advocate, March, 1924; addl. judge, high ct., June, 1926; judge, ditto, Dec., 1926; ch. justice, Nov., 1935; kt., Feb., 1937.

BUCH, NILKANTHRAI MOHANLAL, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 9th July, 1908).—Educ. at Sch. of Economics and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1935; chairman, Lahore impr. trust, Nov., 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; dir. of civil supplies and addtl. dep. sec. to govt. (home), Apl., 1943.

BUCHANAN, REV. GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th July, 1906).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Church of Scotland, July, 1934; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1936.

BUCKLAND, SIR PHILIP LINDSAY, Kt., Barr. (b. 12th Sept., 1874).—Retd., Sept., 1934.*

BUCKLEY, HUGH CLIVE, C.S.I., M.D., F.R.C.S.E., Maj.-Gen., late I.M.S. (b. 31st Oct., 1880).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1905; in mil. employ till Nov., 1907, and from Apl., 1909, to Aug., 1911; plague medl. officer, Punjab, Sept., 1911; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to Apl., 1921; civil surgeon, United Provs., Apl., 1921; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., United Provs., March, 1933; confd., Dec., 1933; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1933; ditto, United Provs., Apl., 1935; surg.-genl. with the govt. of Bombay, March, 1937; retd., Dec., 1940; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; re-empld. as princpl. medl. coll., Agra, Aug., 1941.

BUCKNALL, MARK, B.A., India Office (b. 8th July, 1917).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., 2nd Oct., 1939; on milty. service from May, 1940.

BUCKNEY, FRANK, B.A., late Imperial Customs Service (b. 1st Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

BUDD, BERNARD WILFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 18th Dec., 1912).—Educ. at West Leeds High Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bombay as asst. commr.; served in Sind from Apl., 1941.

BUDH SINGH, SARDAR, Indian Police (b. 20th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 27th Jan., 1929; confd., Jan., 1931.

BULKLEY, ALFRED FRANCIS, late Indian Police, Madras (b. 9th March, 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1908; asst. supt., Jan., 1911; supt., Nov., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1935; confd., July, 1936; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1936; retd., Aug., 1941.

BULL, HERBERT GEORGE, O.B.E., India Office (actg. ch. exec. offr., mil. dept.) (b. 13th Feb., 1882).—Educ. at Owen's Gr. Sch., Islington; served as sec. divn. clerk, War Office, from Apl., 1900; apptd. 2nd class clerk, acct.-genl.'s dept., 17th Apl., 1902; transf. to registry and record dept., July, 1906; transf. to mil. dept., Jan., 1917; minor staff clerk, Apl., 1918; staff clerk (temp.), Oct., 1919; higher exec. offr., Apl., 1922; actg. suptg. exec. offir., March, 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; jt. editor, India Office List, May to Nov., 1934; actg. ch. exec. offr., June, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

BULL, WALTER, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Feb., 1882).—Retd., Feb., 1937.*

BUMSTEAD, W. R., Indian State Rlys. (dist. signal engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 28th Aug., 1893).—Joined service on M. & S.M. Rly., Jan., 1925; services transferred to N.W. Rly.

as asst. signal engr., Apl., 1929; dist. signal engr., Nov., 1938.

BUNN, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 29th Jan., 1911).—Educ. at Rutlish Sch., Merton, and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

BUNYAD HUSSAIN, KHAN BAHADUR SAYAD, B.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 2nd Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 13th May, 1912; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1913; dep. commr., confd., July, 1934.

BURBIDGE, HERBERT FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 25th July, 1904).—Joined the service, Dec., 1923; and deputed to police training coll., Sardah, Bengal; proby. asst. supt., Oct., 1925; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Dec., 1932; confd., Apl., 1935; asst. to the insptr.-genl. of police, 1938; supt., Cachar, May, 1940.

BURDON, SIR ERNEST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Jan., 1881).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. acctnt.-genl., Punjab, July, 1912; dep. acctnt.-genl., ditto, Oct., 1913; under-sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, July, 1914; addl. dep. finl. advr., mil. fin. branch, June, 1916; finl. adviser, Mesopotamia expedy. force, Dec., 1918, to May, 1919; temp. finl. adviser, mil. fin. branch, and member, Indian munitions bd., July, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1921; sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Jan., 1923; C.S.I., Jan., 1926; sec. to govt. of India fin. dept., Aug., 1927; auditor-genl. in India, Nov., 1920; kt., June, 1931; K.C.I.E., June, 1934; Kt. of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, June, 1936; retd., Aug., 1940.

BURDON, THOMAS WILKINSON, B.Sc., Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 4th March, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Dec., 1940; confd. Mar., 1943.

BURFORD, WALTER BECKWITH, Indian State Rlys. (dep. genl. mgr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 8th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 29th Dec., 1911; resdt. engr., G.I.P. Rly., Apl., 1920; offg. supt. of statistics, July, 1929; exec. engr., May, 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr., bridges, G.I.P. Rly., Apl., 1935; dep. genl. mgr., Feb., 1941.

BURGESS, FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 1st Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

BURGESS, GEOFFREY, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sec. to govr., Central Provs.) (b. 29th Apl., 1906).—Educ. at Hanley High Sch., Stoke-on-Trent, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, 12th Nov., 1928, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. sub-judge, Dec., 1931; regr., judl. commr.'s ct., Apl., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; confd. as sub-judge, Jan., 1935; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; sec. to govr., Jan., 1942; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1942.

BURKE, EDMUND PLUNKETT, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th June, 1885).—retd., July, 1938; chairman, Assam publ. serv. commn., March, 1940.*

BURKE, GERALD TYLER, C.I.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (member, medl. bd., India Office) (b. 31st May, 1882).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; in mil. employ to

15th Feb., 1924; prof. King George's medl. coll., Lucknow, Feb., 1924. civil surg., Nov., 1925; employed under Lucknow Univ., Apl., 1929; secty., India Medl. Council, May, 1935; retd., May, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; member, medl. board, India Office, May, 1940.

BURKE, SIR RICHARD JOHN CHARLES, Kt., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd. (b. 5th May, 1878).—Retd., Sept., 1933.*

BURKE, SAMUEL MARTIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 3rd July, 1906).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1936; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, July, 1941.

BURMAN, HAROLD PERCY, Indian State Rlys. (dist. elec. engr., E.B. rly.) (b. 25th June, 1896).—Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. elec. engr., E.B. rly., Jan., 1929; confd., March, 1933.

BURMAN, RAM PRASAD (see Ram Prasad B.).

BURN, REV. JOHN HUMPHREY, B.A., Indian Eccles. Establ. (senr. chaplain) (b. 6th May, 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 29th Sept., 1924; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1926; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1933.

BURN, SIR RICHARD, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.).—Retd., Jan., 1927.*

BURN, SIR SIDNEY, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 19th June, 1881).—Educ. at Wakefield Gr. Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1913; supt., Pudukkottai State, from Oct., 1915, to Oct., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1925; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1932; Oct., 1932; Jan., 1933; and again, July, 1933; confd., June, 1934; kt., June, 1939; retd. June, 1942.

BURNAND, WILLIAM HENRY, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Aug., 1880).—Retd., Apl., 1937.*

BURNE, LINDSAY ELIOTT LUMLEY, C.I.E., C.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Burma commn. (b. 1st July, 1877).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

BURNETT, ROBERT RICHARDSON, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Oct., 1897).—First commn. dated 3rd July, 1915; in milty. employ prior to 16th March, 1922; adjutant, Zhob levy. Corps, March, 1922; probnr. in pol. dept., Feb., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Mekran, May, 1926; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1927; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Sept., 1929; pol. agent, Zhob, Oct., 1929; secty. to agent to G.G. and C.C., Baluchistan, Oct., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1931; offg. dep. secty., for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1932; and again, May, 1933; offg. under secty., ditto, Feb., 1933; princpl. officer and adviser, Tonk state, Apl., 1934; pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Oct., 1936; pol. agent, Chhattisgarh States, Oct., 1939; addl. dy. secy., pol. dept., Sept., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1944; dep. secy. (pol.), Feb., 1943; jt. sec., ext. affairs dept., Apl., 1944; sec., ditto, Sept., 1944.

BURNS, FRANCIS GILBERT, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th Feb., 1886).—Retd., March, 1935.*

BURNS, HENRY GEORGE, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 29th Apl., 1903).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 6th Nov., 1924; asst. exec. engr.,

5th Sept., 1926; offg. exec. engr., March, 1931; confd., Oct., 1934; p.a. to ch. engr., March, 1938; M.B.E., June, 1943.

BURNS, WILLIAM, C.I.E., D.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 6th July, 1884).—Apptd. economic botanist, Poona, 11th Oct., 1908; deputed to mil. dept., Nov., 1917; princpl. Agricl. Coll., Poona, May, 1922; jt. dir. of agric. (temp.), Apl., 1926; offg. dir. of agric., Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1933; offg. agricl. expert, Aug., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; retd., July, 1939; agricl. commr. (on contract), July, 1939.

BURRELL, OSCAR LAWRENCE, Indian Police (district supt., Madras) (b. 13th March, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 14th Nov., 1927; confd., Aug., 1934; district supt. (confd.), July, 1941.

BURRELL, ROY HERBERT ADAMS, Indian Police (b. 14th June, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 16th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., March, 1928; supt. of police, jt. police force, Eastern States, July, 1940; confd., as dep. supt., Nov., 1941.

BURROWS, LIONEL BURTON, C.B.E., B.A., late Bengal Civil Service (b. 12th March, 1883).—Retd., March, 1938.*

BURTON, EDWARD STANLEY VINER, B.Sc., (Edin.), Indian Forest Service (b. 25th Nov., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1930; served with defence dept., air branch, Jan., 1942.

BURTON, FRANK, India Office (hr. exec. officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 4th Aug., 1889).—Apptd. second divn. clerk, Board of Trade, Sept., 1908; transf. to Board of Educn., Sept., 1909, and to Census Office, Apl., 1910; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 8th May, 1913, and attd. to registry and record dept., temporarily transf. to mil. dept., Sept., 1914; higher clerical officer, July, 1922; exec. officer, Sept., 1932; actg. hr. exec. officer, Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937; lent to min. of supply, June, 1940.

BURTON, SIR GEOFFREY POWNALL, K.C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (ret'd.) (advr. to govr., Central Provs.) (b. 19th Nov., 1884).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1909, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1913; spl. duty in sectt., Nov., 1916, to Jan., 1918; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1920; confd. as dep. commr., Feb., 1921; excise commr., March, 1924; rev. secty. to govt., C.P., Jan., 1929; offg. commr., Feb., 1932, and again, March, 1933; offg. commr. of settlements and dir., land records, etc., Sept., 1932; commr., May, 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; member, govr.'s exec. council, Central Provs., Apl., 1936; fin. commr., March, 1937; K.C.I.E., May, 1937; advr. to govr., Nov., 1939; and fin. commr. in addn.; retd., 22nd Nov., 1944; re-empld. as advr.

BURTON, LIONEL HAND, late Indian Police (b. 19th Aug., 1883).—Retd., March, 1937.*

BURTT, THEODORE, Indian Service of Engrs. (supg. engr., Punjab) (b. 2nd July, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. on 1st Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Apl., 1927; under-secty. (estab.), Punjab, March, 1932; offr. on spec. duty, Punjab irrgn. sectt., Apl., 1941; suptg. engr. (permitt.), Oct., 1943.

BURY, REV. PHINEAS, Indian Eccles. Establ. (chaplain on probation, Cent. Provs.) (b. 8th Oct 1902); apptd., 23rd Sept., 1939.

BURY, RONALD EDWARD, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 14th July, 1880).—Retd., July, 1935.*

BUSH, SYDNEY POYNTZ, A.R.I.B.A. (b. 6th Sept., 1889).—Appntd. consulting architect to govt. of Burma, 27th Apr., 1925.

BUSHBY, WALTER EDWIN, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chairman, Karachi Port Trust) (b. 28th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Nov., 1913; in milty. employ from 31st July, 1916, to 21st Jan., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; supt. engr., Oct., 1940; chairman, Karachi Port Trust, Apr., 1942.

BUSK, MICHAEL EDWARD, Burma Police (Cl. 1), (asst. supt.) (b. 25th Feb. 1920).—Educ. Uppingham Sch.; appntd. on probn. 19th Oct., 1939.

BUSS, JOHN ARTHUR GREGORY, Capt., I.A. (b. 10th Oct., 1912).—Appntd. on probn., to I.P.S. from mil. employ Nov., 1938; under-sec., res. Punjab States and asst. pol. agt. for Loharu and Dujana, March, 1944.

BUTCHER, WILLIAM HENRY, M.C., Indian State Ryls. (exec. engr.) (b. 15th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as temp. asst. engr., Burma ryls., 26th Jan., 1921; asst. engr., Jan., 1926; exec. engr., Burma ryls., March, 1935.

BUTLER, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, trade dept.) (b. 3rd Nov., 1876).—Appntd. to office of High Commr., 18th Oct., 1920; temp. admin. officer, Dec., 1922; high. exec. officer, Oct., 1929; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

BUTLER, FRANK GREGORY, Barr., late Indian C.S. Madras; (b. 11th July, 1887).—retd., Nov., 1937.*

BUTLER, SIR G. S., C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon.), F.I.C., Indian Ordnance Dept. (supt., cordite factory, Aruvankadu).—Appntd. chemist, cordite factory, Aruvankadu, 2nd Sept., 1910; manager and chemist, ditto, Dec., 1921; offg. supt., ditto, May, 1928; confd., Feb., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; dep. dir.-genl., munitions production, Dept. of Supply, Aug., 1940; kt., Jan., 1943.

BUTLER, HAROLD EDWIN, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 1st Apr., 1893).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1913, as asst. supt.; on milty. duty from Oct., 1917, to May, 1919; supt. of police, Sept., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; O.B.E., May, 1937; supt. (sel. grade), confd., Feb., 1942; addtl. dep. commr. (temp.), Feb., 1942; commr. of police (confd.), Nov., 1942; C.I.E., June, 1944.

BUTLER, JAMES MICHAEL BEECROFT, Indian Police (b. 9th Jan., 1919).—Appntd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Nov., 1938, confd. May, 1940.

BUTLER, SIR MONTAGU SHERARD DAWES, K.C.S.I., C.B., C.I.E., C.V.O., C.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th May, 1873).—Govt. of Central Provs., Jan., 1925, to Nov., 1929; and again, March, 1930, to Sept., 1933.*

BUTLER, RICHARD AUSTEN, M.P. (b. 9th Dec., 1902).—Member, Indian Franchise ctee., Jan. to June, 1932; parly. under-sec. of state for India, Sept., 1932, to May, 1937; parly. under-sec. of state for Burma, Apr.-May, 1937; delegate to Indian round-table conf., London, Nov.-Dec., 1932.

BUTLER, STANLEY GORDON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 15th Nov., 1885).—Retd. Aug., 1936.*

BUTTI, GHULAM HASAN, Judl. Dept., United

Provs.—Appntd. judge, chief ct., Oudh, Dec., 1940.

BUTTER, JOHN HENRY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 20th Apr., 1916).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; appntd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

BUTTERFIELD, EDWARD, D.S.O., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Burma Commission (b. 9th Apr., 1880).—Retd., June, 1935.*

BYERS, JOSEPH AUSTEN, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 1st Oct., 1895).—Educ. at King's Sch., Chester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 12th Dec., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr., and mag. sub. collr., and mag., Jan., 1924; addl. sessions judge, June, 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; confd., June, 1934; served temp. with govt. of Orissa, from Dec., 1936; addl. judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1942; puisne judge, June, 1942.

BYRNES, WILLIAM FRANCIS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th Oct., 1883); retd., Oct., 1935.*

BYRON, FRANCIS HARVEY, late Salt Eev. Dept., Burma (b. 21st Sept., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

BYU, U TUN, Barr., Burma Judl. Dept. (govt. advocate) (b. 22nd Aug., 1895).—Appntd. offg. asst. govt. advocate, 1st March, 1924; confd., Dec., 1926; offg. govt. advocate, Burma, May, 1936; confd., May, 1937.

CABLE, WILLIAM ARTHUR, Eng. Lieut.-Comdr., R.I.N., retd. (late Marine Survey dept., Bombay) (b. 15th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Sept., 1935.*

CADELL, SIR PATRICK ROBERT, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Retd., Dec., 1926*; kt., Jan., 1935.

CADMAN, SAMUEL PARKES HUBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 15th Nov., 1906).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

CAFFIN, ALBERT EDWARD, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 16th June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1927, and again, July, 1933; confd., Sept., 1933; asst. to inspr.-genl., police, C.I.D. intell., Oct., 1939, to 1944; Indian police medal, 1941; dep. commr., C.I.D., Oct., 1944.

CALDECOTT, CHARLES D'ARCY, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 18th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Feb., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1922; retd., June, 1940.

CALDER, CHARLES CUMMING, B.Sc., F.L.S. (b. 3rd Dec., 1884).—Supt., Royal Botanic Garden, Calcutta, and cinchona cultivation in Bengal, May, 1925; retd., Dec., 1939.*

CALDER, NORMAN DOUGLAS, V.D., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 13th Jan., 1882).—Retd., Jan., 1937.*

CALDER, WILLIAM BUCHANAN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 18th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1927; offg. exec. engr., Apr., 1931; confd., Oct., 1936; dep. sec., govt. Sind., Apr., 1941; addtl. ditto, Jan. to March, 1942.

CALDWELL, KENNETH SOMERVILLE, M.A., B.Sc., Ph.D., F.I.C., F.C.S., late Indian Educl. Service (Bihar) (b. 19th Feb., 1880).—Retd., Feb., 1935.*

CALVERT, WILLIAM HANDLEY, late Indian

Police (Assam) (b. 27th Jan., 1896).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

CAMBRIDGE, SYDNEY JOHN PELHAM, O.B.E., V.D., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 11th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service, 22nd Apr., 1912; asst. engr., Apr., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Apr., 1928; exec. engr., July, 1938; divl. supt., Dec., 1943; O.B.E., June, 1944.

CAME, WILLIAM GERALD, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (supgt. engr., Bihar) (b. 8th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; suptg. engr. (temp.), March, 1934; confd., under-secty. to govt., p.w.d., Nov., 1923; Apl., 1936; dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1938; tfd. to Orissa, July, 1940; ch. eng., and sec., govt. p.w.d., Nov., 1942; C.I.E., June, 1944.

CAMERON, DUNCAN CHARLES THOMAS, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 6th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. dist. supt., March, 1929, and again, June, 1933; confd., Aug., 1935.

CAMERON, GILBERT STUART, Dept. of Mines in India (inspr.) (b. 22nd Apr., 1891).—Joined the service as junr. inspr., 25th Apr., 1921; offg. inspr., June, 1934; confd., Sept., 1936.

CAMERON KER, EDWARD, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 31st March, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. auditor, E.I. Rly, 29th Feb., 1908; served in I.A.R.O., from Oct., 1914, to Sept., 1916; accts. offcr., gr. I, E.I.R., Dec., 1925; confd. as dep. chief accts. offcr., E.I.R., Dec., 1926; dep. ditto, E.B. Rly., Apr., 1930, to Oct., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1940.

CAMPBELL, ALEXANDER, M.C., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instn., Burma) (b. 29th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as prof. of English on 24th March, 1915; on mil. service from 31st Aug., 1916, to 14th Feb., 1919; registrar, Rangoon Univ., Dec., 1921; inspr. of schools, Burma, June, 1933; offg. dir. of public instn., Aug., 1933; and again, June, 1936; asst. dir. of public instn., Feb., 1935; dir. of public instn., Apr., 1939.

CAMPBELL, SIR ARCHIBALD YOUNG GIPPS, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 18th May, 1872).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

CAMPBELL, DOUGLAS COLIN, Indian Railway Accts. Service (chief accts. offcr.) (b. 28th May, 1891).—Joined the service as dep. chief accts. offcr., Burma Rlys., 20th March, 1932, on abolition of appt. as dir. of comml. audit; confd. as chief accts. offcr., July, 1933; dir., rly. clearing accts. office, Oct., 1934; chief accts. offcr., N.W. Rly., Oct., 1937; controller of rly. accts., Apr., 1939.

CAMPBELL, JAMES DONALD, D.S.O., Col., R.E., late Survey of India (b. 3rd May, 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.*

CAMPBELL, SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provs.).—Retd., Oct., 1922.*

CAMPBELL, JOHN HERBERT, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 23rd May, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 20th Oct., 1930; offg. exec. engr., May, 1936; confd., Oct., 1939.

CAMPBELL, WALTER FENDALL, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th May, 1894).—First commn., dated 14th Jan., 1914; joined the pol. dept., 21st Jan., 1921; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi,

June, 1921; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1926; asst. commr., Hangu, Nov., 1926; pol. agent, Kurram, Nov., 1927; dep. commr., D.I. Khan, Oct., 1928; ditto, Kohat, Oct., 1928; asst. commr., Thal, Nov., 1928; dep. ditto, Bannu, Nov., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., in the states of W.I., Oct., 1933; prime minister, Alwar, March, 1935, to Sept., 1936; pol. agent, Bundelkhan, Oct., 1937; resdt. in Waziristan, Feb., 1939; advr. to govr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; confd. as resdt., C.I., and C.C. of Panth-Piploda, May, 1942.

CANDY, RONALD HERBERT, C.I.E., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 13th June, 1886).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1912; offg. civil surg., Apr., 1923; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Bombay, July, 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; C.M.O. and civ. surg., Delhi, March, 1938.

CANNING, FREDERICK, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 31st Dec., 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

CANTLIE, SIR KEITH, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 6th Feb., 1886).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll. (Aberdeen), Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr., June, 1911; ditto, Assam, Apr., 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), May, 1915, to Apr., 1919; presdt., Manipur State Darbar, Sept., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Apr., 1923; confd., May, 1927; offg. commr., March, 1937, to Feb., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1939; member, rev. trib., Jan., 1942; kt., June, 1944.

CAPOOR, SHYAM BEHARI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 15th Jan., 1907).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1938; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1938; offg. ditto, March, 1939; confd. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1942.

CARBERY, MURLOUGH, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., D.S.O., M.C., Indian Agricultural Service (asst. dir. of agric., Bengal) (b. 24th July, 1890).—Joined the service as agric. chemist on 1st Dec., 1920; offg. asst. dir. of agric., Bengal, June, 1933; and again, Sept., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

CARDEN, VEN. HENRY CRAVEN, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estab. (Punjab) (b. 23rd Dec., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1936.*

CARDEW, DAVID, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

CARDEW, JOHN HORACE, late Indian State Rlys. (Burma Rlys.) (b. 31st July, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 3rd July, 1913; on milty. duty from 13th Dec., 1914, to 8th May, 1920; offg. variously as dist. loco. supt. from July, 1920; offg. dep. loco. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., May, 1931; ret'd., 5th May, 1944.

CAREY-EVANS, SIR THOMAS JOHN, Kt., M.C., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 26th June, 1884).—Entered I.M.S., 1907; served in Egypt and Mesopotamia during European War, 1914-18; civil medl. officer, Baghdad; resdt. medl. officer, Mysore State; surgeon to H.E. the Viceroy of India; ret'd., July, 1926; apptd. examr. of surgical instruments, India store dept., 13th June, 1925.

CARGILL, IAN PETER MAGILLIVRAY, M.B.E., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 29th Sept., 1915).—Educ. at Malvern and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; served in Sind from Oct., 1939; dep. admr., martial law, June, 1942; asst. rev. offr., Oct., 1943; under sec., fin. dept., Apl., 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1944.

CARLESS, HENRY ALFRED, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supl., United Provs.) (b. 26th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 20th Nov., 1914; offg. supt., Feb., 1921; confd., Jan., 1926; princl., police trng. school, Moradabad, Sept., 1930, to July, 1931; confd. in sel. grade, Oct., 1940; police advr. to resdt., Rajputana.

CARLESTON, HADDEN HAMILTON, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 25th July, 1904).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., and Cambridge (Trinity Hall); joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1929; collr. and dist. mag., C. & M. station, Bangalore, May, 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

CARMAN, FRANK, M.B.E., Indian Police (b. 15th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 26th Nov., 1936; confd., Jan., 1939; M.B.E., June, 1944.

CARNAGHAN, DOUGLAS WILLIAM BERESFORD, Indian Police (supl., Bombay) (b. 21st Dec., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, Nov., 1932; confd., Apl., 1935; exec. offr., regional transp. authy., March, 1940, to Jan., 1942; supt. (confd.), Dec., 1943.

CAROE, SIR OLAF KIRKPATRICK, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 15th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at Winchester and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; city mag., Peshawar, Nov., 1923; dep. commr., Peshawar, Apl., 1926; asst. dep. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1926; dep. commr., Hazara, Aug., 1927; ditto, Kohat, Nov., 1928; jt. dep. commr., Peshawar, Apl., 1930; dep. commr., ditto, July, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1933; dep. secty. to govt. of India, pol. dept., May, 1934; offg. resdt., Persian Gulf, Aug., 1937; offg. A.G.G., Resident and C.C. in Baluchistan, Nov., 1937; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, March, 1938; tempy. resdt. in Waziristan, July, 1938; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, March, 1939; secty., external affairs dept., Govt. of India, July, 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

CARPENTER, JOHN HOWARD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

CARR, SIR WILLIAM, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 9th Feb., 1872).—Retd., Feb., 1932.*

CARREY, JOHN, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., B.Sc., Maj., I.M.S., Medl. Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 23rd July, 1894).—First commn., 20th Jan., 1923; offg. civil surgeon, Central Provs., March, 1932; confd., March, 1934.

CARRIER, PHILIP LESLIE CARO, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Rlys. (C.M.O., Burma Rlys.) (b. 14th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, Burma Rlys., 14th Jan., 1927; offg. C.M.O., ditto, Nov., 1933; and again, March, 1936; confd., May, 1936.

CARRITT, MICHAEL JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 3rd Jan., 1906).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

CARROLL, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 21st July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 20th Dec., 1913; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; offg. consvr., May, 1934; confd., June, 1937; ret'd., March, 1940.

CARROTT, WILL JUDSON, Burma Civil Service (Cl. 1) (asst. commr.) (b. 18th Feb., 1916).—Educ. at Govt. English High Sch., Maymyo and Rangoon Univ.; apptd. on probn., 30th Sept., 1938; on milty. service, Nov., 1940.

CARSE, ROBERT, Indian Police (supl., Assam) (b. 4th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service, Nov., 1923, and deputed to police training coll., Sardah, Bengal; proby. asst. supt., Oct., 1925; confd., May, 1926; offg. supt., Feb., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935; pol. offcr., Balipara front, tract, July, 1938, to Nov., 1938; supr., Sibrazar, March, 1939; supt. river police, Sept., 1941; supt. rly. police, Nov. 1942.

CARSON, SIR CHARLES FRED, M.C., Col., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th Feb., 1886).—First commn. dated 28th June, 1908; asst. engr., July, 1913; on mil. duty from 23rd Oct., 1914, to 14th Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Dec., 1918; offg. divl. supt., June, 1930; offg. dep. agt., organisation, Apl., 1933; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), June, 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), N.W. rly, Sept., 1935; offg. agent, March, 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Apl., 1936; confd. gen. manager, N.W. rly., May, 1939; kt., Jan., 1941; ret'd., Feb., 1941.

CARSON, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM CHARTERIS, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (b. 21st July, 1874).—Retd., July, 1929.*

CARTER, ALFRED COLDRICK, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 6th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 28th Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1931; asst. commr., Nov., 1933; confd. as dist. supt., Nov., 1937; inspr.-genl. and police advr., East. States, Feb., 1941.

CARTER, ARTHUR CHARLES, O.B.E., Indian Police (supl., Bihar) (b. 27th May, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 24th March, 1922; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Feb., 1933; O.B.E., June, 1939.

CARTER, HORACE LEONARD, M.I.R.S.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. signal engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1898).—Asst. signal engr., 15th Dec., 1922; dist. ditto, 5th Feb., 1935.

CARTER, MALCOLM OGILVY, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd July, 1898).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 15th Dec., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1923; offg. mag. and collr., variously from Feb., 1929; offg. secty., bd. of rev., Bengal, Sept., 1934; mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; secty. land rev. commission, Bengal, Nov., 1938, to Sept., 1939; dir. of land record and surveys, Bengal, Dec., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; addl. sec. to govr., July, 1940; sec., ditto, Aug., 1940; confd. mag. and collr., Sept., 1942; civ. representative of govt. of Bengal with eastern army, Sept., 1942, to Nov., 1943;

CARTER, REGINALD ROBERT LANGHAM, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 16th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Bradfield and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt.,

home and pol. dept., March, 1930; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932; confd., May, 1937; secty. to finl. commr., Jan., 1940.

CARTER, THOMAS, M.B.E., Dept. of Printing and Stationery (manager G. of I. Press) (b. 22nd Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 27th March, 1920; asst. manager, G. of I. press, Feb., 1927; offg. manager, ditto, March, 1927; confd., Apl., 1931; M.B.E., June, 1934.

CARTLAND, WALTER CUTHBERT, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 14th Nov., 1879).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

CARTWRIGHT, ALBERT, Indian Police (b. 27th Dec., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 21st Nov., 1931; confd., Apl., 1934; sec., provl. transpt. authy., Sept., 1943; addtl. dep. supt., March, 1944.

CARVEY, EUSTACE MALTRAVERS, Indian State Ry's. (dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 22nd July, 1893).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1915; asst. contr. of stores, Aug., 1919; offg. dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., variously from Feb., 1921; confd., Dec., 1932.

CASE, ROBERT CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ry's. (ch. mech. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 19th Apl., 1893).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1919, as asst. loco. supt.; offg. dist. ditto, E.B. rly., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. dir., rly. bd., Nov., 1927; dep. ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. rly., Nov., 1931; offg. dir., rly. bd., Sept., 1936; dep. ch. contr., standardisation (mechl.), Oct., 1936. asst. dep. director-genl., munitions prod., dept. of supply, July, 1940; ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. rly., Aug., 1940.

CASEMENT, EDGAR REGINALD, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 13th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 29th Oct., 1910; on milty. duty from June, 1915, to Oct., 1918; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1930; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., March, 1934; confd., Nov., 1935; dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1935; retd., Oct., 1941.

CASEY, RICHARD GARDINER, P.C., D.S.O., M.C. (governor of Bengal).—Apptd., Jan., 1944.

CASSELLS, SIR ROBERT ARCHIBALD, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., D.S.O., p.s.c., General Indian Army (b. 15th March, 1876).—C-in-C, India, and member Govt.-Genl.'s exec. council, 29th Nov., 1935, to Dec., 1940; G.C.B., 1933; G.C.S.I., July, 1940.*

CASTENS, HERBERT ERNEST, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 10th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

CATER, SIR ALEXANDER NORMAN LEY, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. and Indian Political Service (b. 15th June, 1880).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

CAVE, LESLIE STORRER, late Indian State Ry's. (N.W. Ry.) (b. 3rd March, 1894).—Asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 28th Feb., 1922; divl. mech. engr., senr. scale, Feb., 1937; retd., May, 1940.

CHADHA, CHARANJIT LAL, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 21st Dec., 1899).—Retd., May, 1935.*

CHADWICK, SIR DAVID THOMAS, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 7th Apl., 1876).—Retd., March, 1929.*

CHADWICK, FREDERICK JOHN, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1894).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1921; asst. supt., Sept., 1925; offg. dist. supt., July, 1932; confd., July, 1933.

CHAGLÁ, MOHOMEDALI CURRIM B.A.,

Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 30th Sept., 1900).—Addl. judge (tempy.), high ct., Bombay, Aug., 1941, to Aug., 1942; judge, ditto, Aug., 1942.

CHAIN, U. BA MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 24th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

CHAINANI, HASHMATRAI KHUBCHAND, Indian C.S. (dep. sec. home dept., G.I.) (b. 29th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and mag.; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, Aug., 1933; offg. remembr. of legal affairs, June, 1935; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1935; offg. sec., legisl. council, Bombay, March, 1936 to 1938; commr. for workmen's compensation (in addn.), Apl., 1940; dep. sec., legal dept., and sec., legisl. cl., var. from Apl., 1938, to Jan., 1942; addtl. jt. sec. (home), June, 1942; served under central govt. from June, 1942, as dept. sec., home dept.
