CHAKKO, K. C. B.A., B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., govt. Victoria coll., Palghat) (b. 24th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. prof. of civil engrg., coll. of engrg., Madras, 18th Nov., 1921; princpl. coll. of engrg., Guindy, May, 1936.

CHAKRABATTI, JATINDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Indian Agricultural Service (b. 12th Jan., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

CHAKRAVARTHI, SUBRAHMANIYAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 30th Aug., 1910).—Educ. at the Hindu High Sch., Triplicane, University of Madras, The Sch. of Economics, London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., Dec., 1937; under sec., devel. dept., June, 1940; ditto, govt. of India (war transport), Sept., 1942.

CHAKRAVARTI, A., M.A., late Indian Educational Service, Madras (b. 1st July, 1883).—retd., July, 1938.*

CHAKRAVARTI, PHANI BHUSAN, O.B.E., Rai Bahadur, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 16th June, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1906; asst. auditor genl., Dec., 1934; O.B.E., May, 1937; offcr. on spec. duty in the fin. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1937.

CHAKRAVARTI, SURENDRA NATH, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; municipal engr., Delhi, Dec., 1933, to Apl., 1939; M.B.E., Feb., 1937; confd. as suptg. engr., July, 1942.

CHAKRAVARTY, ASHWINI KUMAR, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1890).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1914; pers. asst. to contr. of currency, Dec., 1925; supt., Govt. of India fin. dept., Dec., 1928; asst. acct. genl., Bengal, Oct., 1936; dep. ditto, Oct., 1939; L.A.D., Bengal, Sept., 1940.

CHAKRAVARTY, BIRENDRA NARAYAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 20th Dec., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Calcutta, University Coll., London, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served as asst. coll. and mag., Bengal; jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936; jt. sec., fin. dept., Apl., 1944.

CHAKRAVARTY, NARENDRA NARAIN, Rai Bahadur, M.A., late Income Tax Dept., Bengal

(b. Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1912; sub. dep. collr., Dec., 1913; inc. tax officer, Aug., 1923; asst. commr., July, 1929; retd., 16th Aug., 1943.

CHALAM, BALAJIPAT SESHA, late Indian State Ry. (b. 7th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. malariologist, E.B. rly., on probn., 23rd June, 1928; confd., Sept., 1932; retd., Nov., 1941.

CHALK, WALTER OLIVER, late Indian State Ry. (b. 29th Nov., 1881).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

CHAMBERS, DAVID GOSTLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 19th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; under-sec. to govt. of India, def. dept., Apl., 1941.

CHAMBERS, John, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 17th March, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1919; exec. engr., Apl., 1926; O.B.E., June, 1935; suptg. engr., Oct., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

CHAMBERS, LESLIE RAPER, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 30th Apl., 1910).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; asst. finl. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1940.

CHAMBERS, STANLEY PAUL, C.I.E., Bd. of Inland Revenue, on loan as income-tax adviser to the Central Bd. of Revenue, from Dec., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

CHAMIER, SIR EDWARD MAYNARD DESCHAMPS, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., Barr. (1887), late India Office (b. 4th June, 1866).—Retd., June, 1933.*

CHAMIER, MISS ETHEL, O.B.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 19th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 24th Sept., 1909; asst. inspectress, May, 1910; inspectress, Oct., 1915; O.B.E., June, 1939; retd., Jan., 1940.

CHAMPA LAL, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 1st Jan., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; offg. exec. engr., March, 1928; confd., Oct., 1932.

CHAMPHEKAR, MAHADEO LAXMAN, M.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Sind) (b. 6th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 7th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925, March, 1926, March, 1927, and Nov., 1927; confd., Oct., 1931; dep. secty, govt. of Sind, p.w.d., May, 1938; suptg. engr. (provl. perm.), Dec., 1943.

CHAMPION, FREDERICK WALTER, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., United Provs.) (b. 24th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service, as asst. consvr., on 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; consvr. (confd.), Aug. 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

CHAMPION, HARRY GEORGE, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 17th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 9th Dec., 1915; dep. consvr., July, 1919; asst. silviculturist, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1925; silviculturist, Jan., 1926 to Oct., 1936; consvr. (confd.), Feb., 1938; retd., 20th July, 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

CHAMPION, REGINALD STUART, O.B.E. (b. 21st March, 1895).—On milty. service, 1914-1917. Served in Occupied Enemy Territories Administration; appointed Deputy Governor,

Grade III, Hebron, Palestine, July, 1920; transferred to Beersheba, Apl., 1924; title changed to administrative officer, Jan., 1925; transferred to Jerusalem Division as asst. dist. commissioner, July, 1927; seconded for service as protectorate secretary, Aden, Oct., 1928; first asst. and protectorate secretary (now designated political sec.), Aden, March, 1932; offg. ch. commr., Aden, Apl., 1933; O.B.E., Jan., 1934.

CHANTOON, JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 4th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Nov., 1931.

CHANCE, PERCIVAL VINCENT, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Central Provs.) (b. 12th June, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1929; confd., Sept., 1930; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to Govt., C.P. and Berar; buildings and roads and irrig. bch., Apl., 1935; chief engr., Nov., 1937, C.I.E., Jan., 1943; retd., June, 1943.

CHANCE, WILLIAM HENRY, late Indian State Ry. (b. 28th Feb., 1888).—Joined the service, 15th Feb., 1912; senr. asst. auditor, Nov., 1926; dep. ch. accts. officer, Nov., 1930; dep. agent, Burma rly, March, 1931; ch. accts. officer, March, 1934; retd., March, 1944.

CHAND, AJIT (see Ajit Chand).

CHAND, BISHEN (see Bishen Chand).

CHAND, AMIR (see Amir Chand).

CHAND, HUKAM (see Hukam Chand).

CHAND, KARM (see Karm Chand).

CHAND, KHUB, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 16th Dec., 1911).—Educ. at Government High Schs., Delhi and Simla, Univ. of Delhi, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1937; under secty., govt. of India, def. dept., Apl., 1940, to Apl., 1941.

CHAND, KRISHAN, LL.M., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 30th Nov., 1917).—Educ. at Cantonment High Sch., Lahore, King's Coll., London, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd., on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 23rd Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

CHAND MAL, B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 7th Apl., 1911).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Delhi, Delhi Univ., Thomason C.E. Coll., Roorkee, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Jan., 1937; retd., 5th Sept., 1942.

CHAND, MUNISHWAR (see Munishwar Chand).

CHAND, PREM (see Prem Chand).

CHAND, SUMER (see Sumer Chand).

CHAND, TEK (see Tek Chand).

CHANDA, APURVA KUMAR, Indian Educl. Service (princl., presidency coll.) (b. 12th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1920; vice-prin., Dacca Coll., June, 1922; profr., presdy. coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1926; offg. prin., Krishnagore Coll., March, 1930; confd., June, 1930; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, May, 1934; confd., Oct., 1934; princpl., David Hare trng. coll., Nov., 1936; ditto, presdy. coll., Apl., 1943.

CHANDA, ASOKA KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 2nd Feb., 1902).—Joined the service, 3rd March, 1926; dept. acct.-genl., p. and t., Nagpur, June, 1936; examr., local fund

acts. with Govt. of Madras, Nov., 1936; examr., outside audit, Bengal, Feb., 1940.

CHANDAVARKAR, PROBHAKAR NARAYAN, C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., late Impl. Customs Service (b. 20th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as probr., 20th Jan., 1914; asst. collr., July, 1915; offg. collr., Sept., 1933; confd., Jan., 1940; C.I.F., June, 1942; ret'd., Aug., 1944.

CHANDIRAM KOTUMAL (see Kotumal, C.).
CHANDIRAMANI, SANTDAS BULCHAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 1st Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, King's Coll., London; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927; and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1930; temp. sess. and sub-judge, Oct., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; confd., Jan., 1940; sec. to govt., Punjab (fin.), Jan., 1942.

CHANDRA, CHAUDHURI NANAK, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 19th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Mooltan, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Nov., 1921; arrived, 9th March, 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Punjab as asst. commr., Apl., 1923; dep. commr. (provl.), Oct., 1929; confd., Jan., 1934; junr. secty. to finl. commrs., Dec., 1931; dep. secty. to govt., rev. dept., Feb., 1934, to Oct., 1935; addtl. ditto, Nov., 1938.

CHANDRA, GANESH CHANDRA, A.I.I.A., Archaeological Survey Dept. (b. 1st Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 29th Apl., 1920; supt., Archl. Survey, Nov., 1927; supt., Southern Circle, July, 1937.

CHANDRA HARISH (see Harish Chandra).
CHANDRA KISHOR, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1910; asst. engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1922; ret'd., 11th Nov., 1941.

CHANDRA, RAM (see Ram Chandra).

CHANDRA, ROMESH (see Romesh Chandra).

CHANDRA, SATISH, B.Sc., M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 16th Oct., 1916).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn., after exam. of 1940, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

CHANDRASEKHARA AYYAR, NAGAPUDI, Rao Bahadur, Judl. Dept., Madras (addl. judge, Madras high ct.) (b. 25th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as offg. city civil judge, Madras, 18th July, 1927; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; addtl. judge, Madras high ct. (temp.), Jul., 1941.

CHANDRASEKHARAN, VALIA VEETIL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 10th May, 1910).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 5th Feb., 1934; confd., March, 1936.

CHANDULAL, DONALD, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 28th June, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 29th Apl., 1927; confd., Apl., 1929; asst. I.G. rly. police, Apl., 1937; supt., spec. bch., C.I.D., Apl., 1939; confd. as supt., March, 1942; Indian police medal, Jan., 1943.

CHANDWANI, PESUMAL BHAGWANDAS, M.A., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.), (b. 1st Feb., 1888).—Joined the service, March, 1913; asst. audit offcr., July, 1927; transfed. to transpn. dept., March, 1930; divl. pers. offcr., May, 1935; ret'd., Feb., 1941.

CHANING PEARCE, WILLIAM ALAN, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1911).—Joined

the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 1st Nov., 1931; confd., Apl., 1934.

CHAPMAN, ARTHUR FREDERIC, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th Sept., 1886).—Ret'd., Apl., 1937.*

CHAPMAN, EDWARD FREDERIC GILBERT, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., July, 1930.

CHAPMAN, HAROLD VICTOR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 15th Apl., 1900).—Joined the service on 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

CHAPMAN, HENRY, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th Oct., 1883).—Asst. supt., junior scale, 1st Oct., 1928; supt. (power senr. scale, Jan., 1937; ret'd., Apl., 1940.

CHAPMAN, R. C. G., O.B.E., Maj. R.E. (deputy master, Security Printing) (b. 18th May, 1899).—Under mil. dept. up to 31st Aug., 1934; deputy master, Security Printing, Sept., 1934; O.B.E., June, 1942.

CHARAGH, MAHBUB ILAHI (see Mahbub Ilaahi Charagh).

CHARAN, ATMA, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, 7th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1934; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936; under secty., govt. of India, ind. and lab. dept., Nov., 1936; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1938; confd., Jan., 1940.

CHARI, PERUNGAVUR VARADA, M.B.E., A.C.G.I., M.I.E.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., A.A.I.E.E. (ch. elec. inspr. to govt., Madras) (b. 11th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. elec. engr., 1st Dec., 1918; elec. inspr., Feb., 1939; ch. elec. inspr. to govt., Oct., 1939.

CHARI, RAJAGOPALA THIRUVENKATA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. mag. and jt. collr., Madras) (b. 30th Nov., 1913).—Educ. at Madras Univ.; joined the service, 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938; and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Apl., 1940.

CHARLTON, JAMES, M.Sc., F.I.C., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 11th June, 1894).—Joined the service as agricl. chemist, agricl. coll., Mandalay, 26th Jan., 1921; princpl., ditto, in addn., Dec., 1922; offg. dir. of agricl., Burma, Sept., 1935; confd., March, 1936; ret'd., 10th June, 1943.

CHARSLEY, CLAUDE ROBERT, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 11th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service, 4th Nov., 1921, as asst. supt., on probn.; confd., July, 1924; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1928; confd., March, 1932; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; Indian ditto, July, 1940; confd. as dep. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

CHATARJI, ABANIBHUSHAN, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 13th Jan., 1907).—Educ. at Patna Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934; addl. dist. mag., May, 1939; provl. organiser, nat. war front., Apl., 1942; M.B.E., 1944; mag. and collr., May, 1944.

CHATARJI, ANIL CHANDRA, M.B., Lieut.-

Col., I.M.S. (dir. of pub. health, Bengal) (b. 21st Dec., 1891).—Date of commn., 17th Apl., 1919; in mil. employ up to 18th May, 1934; asst. dir. of public health, Delhi Prov., to Apl., 1936; dir., ditto, Bengal, June, 1936.

CHATARJI, GANESH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (profr., govt. coll., Lahore) (b. 4th March, 1894).—Joined the service as profr., C.T. coll., Lahore, on 1st Dec., 1919; profr., govt. coll., Lahore, Sept., 1921; princpl., cent. trng. coll., July, 1939.

CHATARJI, GOPENDRA BHUSHAN, BABU, M.A., LL.B., late United Provs. C.S. (b. 18th June, 1882).—Retd., June, 1937.*

CHATARJI, SUKUMAR, M.B.E., M.A., Bengal C.S. (inspr.-genl. of regn.) (b. 16th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1908; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1911; inspr.-genl. of regn., Bengal, May, 1936.

CHATARJI, SURRENDRA NATH, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 31st Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 29th June, 1925; offg. supt., March, 1932; confd., Nov., 1937; King's police medal, 1937.

CHATARJI, SURESH CHANDRA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 5th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1917; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1925; confd., Jan., 1928; suptg. engr., March, 1940.

CHATTARJI, HARI DAS, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., Bihar (b. 8th Aug., 1880).—Retd., Sept., 1936.*

CHATTARJI, RANGA LAL, M.A., B.L., Bihar C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 9th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 9th May, 1911; sub-judge, Aug., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1934; confd., Oct., 1934.

CHATTARJI, SHIBA PRIYA, Rai Bahadur, B.L., late Bihar C.S. (b. 1st Jan., 1882).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

CHATTERJEE, AKHIL CHANDAR, O.B.E., B.A., Indian State Ryis. (divl. transportation officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1895).—Joined service, Feb., 1917; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1919; junior scale, Aug., 1920; divl. transportation (traffic-commercial) officer, senior scale, Dec., 1937; O.B.E., June, 1939.

CHATTERJEE, SIR ATUL CHANDRA, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Nov., 1874); retd. Jan., 1925*; high commr. for India, 5th Jan., 1925, to 30th June, 1931; K.C.S.I., July, 1930; member of council of India, July, 1931, to July, 1936; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1933; advr. to sec. of state for India, Feb., 1942.

CHATTERJEE, BASANTA KUMAR, M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 14th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service, 11th June, 1913; asst. acct.-genl., Bengal, May, 1914; ditto, Punjab, Jan., 1918; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, May, 1923; ditto, Calcutta, Jan., 1924; ditto, Punjab, March, 1925; ditto, Calcutta, Apl., 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Jan., 1927; offg. dep. auditor-genl., June, 1930; dep. ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Feb., 1932; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, March, 1933; comptr., Assam., Nov., 1933; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. acct.-genl., Burma, March, 1935; ditto, Central Provs., March, 1936; acctnt.-genl. (confd.), July, 1939; ch. auditor, E.I. rly. and govt. exr. accts., B.N. rly., Jan., 1940.

CHATTERJEE, BISWESWAR, Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 15th Aug., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., on probn., Feb., 1940.

CHATTERJEE, HRISHI KESH, Dept. of Mines (jmr. inspr.) (b. 12th June, 1893).—

Joined the service as jmr. inspr. on probn., 29th Nov., 1925; confd., Aug., 1930.

CHATTERJEE, NRITYA GOPAL, Dept. of Mines (inspr. of mines) (b. 15th Jan., 1896).—Apptd. junior inspr. of mines on probn., 14th July, 1923; confd., July, 1925; inspr. of mines, Apl., 1938.

CHATTERJEE, SANTOSH KUMAR, B.A. (Cal. and Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 12th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Calcutta, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1929; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1935; dir. in directorate of food, Jan., 1945.

CHATTERJEE, SISIR CHANDRA, M.D., M.R.C.S., D.P.H., late Indian State Ryis. (b. 4th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as dist. surg., G.I.P. Rly., 28th March, 1918; offg. dep. C.M.O., May, 1929; ditto, G.I.P. Rly., May, 1931; offg. C.M.O., E.B. Rly., Apl., 1932; ditto, N.W. Rly., March, 1933; ditto, G.I.P. Rly., Sept., 1933; C.M.O., E.B. Rly., Nov., 1934; ditto, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1940; retd., Dec., 1943.

CHATTERJEE, SUBODH CHANDRA (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd., Sept., 1939.

CHATTERJEE, SURENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 10th March, 1910; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nagpur, Sept., 1930; asst. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Nov., 1931; dep. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Dec., 1932; ditto, Bengal, Jan., 1934; exr., local audit dept., Bengal, Dec., 1935; dep. acct. genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, Oct., 1939.

CHATTERJI (see entries under Chatarji).

CHATTERTON, SIR ALFRED, C.I.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras).—Retd., Aug., 1918.*

CHATURVEDI, MANOHAR DAS (see Manohar, D. C.).

CHAUDHARI, KALYAN CHANDRA, M.A. (Cal.), Indian Railway Accts. Service (accts. officer) (b. 20th July, 1898).—Joined the service, Jan., 1929; accts. officer, Jan., 1930.

CHAUDHARY, KEWAL SINGH, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1915).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Jaranwala, Lyall Pur., Punjab Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 8th Sept., 1939; arrived India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

CHAUDHRI AKBAR HUSAIN (see Akbar Husain, Chaudhri).

CHAUDHURI, CHANDRA MOHAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bihar) (b. 26th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, on 19th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., direction divn., May, 1944.

CHAUDHURI, HARI SADHAN GHOSH (see Ghosh Chaudhuri, H. S.).

CHAUDHURI, KUMUD RANJAN, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 2nd Oct., 1901).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1925; in training to Apl., 1927; asst. supt., May, 1927; offg. supt., March, 1932, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., 1937.

CHAUDHURI, KSHITISH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Feb., 1901).—Joined the service, 14th March, 1925; dep. acct.-genl., B. and O., Apl., 1935; dep. compr., Assam, Nov., 1936; exmr., local fund accts., Central Provs., Dec., 1940.

CHAUDHURI, MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR,

B.A., late Assam C.S. (b. 10th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1909; dep. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1910; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1912; dep. commr., Jan., 1933; commr. of excise, and regr. jt. stk. coys., etc., Assam, March, 1935; offg. secty., transf. depts., Assam, in addn., Sept., 1936; dir. of land records and surveys, etc., March, 1939; commr. of excise, regr. jt. stk. coys., etc., Feb., 1940; retd., Nov., 1941.

CHAUDHURI, NIRMALKANTI RAY (see Ray Chaudhuri, N.).

CHAUDHURI, NIRMALKANTI RAY (see Ray Chaudhur, N.).

CHAUDHURI, PRABHAT CHANDRA, O.B.E., Indian Agricul. Service (dep. dir. of sericulture, Bengal) (b. 9th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, 10th Oct., 1913; dep. dir. of sericulture, Oct., 1922; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

CHAUDHURI, PRAPHULLA CHANDRA, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1916; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Apl., 1918; ditto, Bengal, Feb., 1924; asst. auditor, E.B. rly., Dec., 1925; ditto, N.W. rly., June, 1927; dep. auditor (constn. bch.), N.W. rly., July, 1927; audit and accts. officer, Bombay dev. scheme, Apl., 1929; accts. officer, G.I.P. rly., March, 1930; ditto, E.B. rly., Apl., 1931; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts. office, Apl., 1933; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, E.B. rly., Jan., 1935; dep. ch. accts. offcr., E.I. rly., May, 1936; comptr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1937; ch. auditor, N.W. rly., Oct., 1939; acct. genl., Bihar, May, 1940; ch. accts. offcr., E.I. rly., Nov., 1941; confd. as acct.-genl. (cl. I), Aug., 1944.

CHAUDHURI, PRASANTA CHANDRA, M.A. (Punjab), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 21st Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab, Sch. of Oriental Studies, London, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1928; arrived, 9th Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1936; dep. contr. of broadcasting, govt. of India, Aug., 1940; addtl. dep. ch. contr., imports, central govt., June, 1942; dep. ditto, Jan., 1943; jt. ch. contr., Dec., 1944.

CHAUDHURI, RAMCHANDRA, Pandit, M.A., B.L., late Bihar C.S. (b. 21st Aug., 1879).—Retd., July, 1936.*

CHAUDHURI, SIKUMAR, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 14th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

CHAUDHURI, SURATH NATH (see Surath Nath, C.).

CHAUDHURI, TAJAL ISLAM MUHAMMAD NURANNABI (see Tajal I. M. N. Chaudhuri).

CHAUDHURI, TAPENDRA KUMAR GHOSH (see Ghosh Chaudhuri, T. K.).

CHAUNCY, FREDERICK CHARLES LESLIE, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Dec., 1904).—First commn., 27th Aug., 1924; in milty. employ until 12th Feb., 1930; under sec. to A.G.G. in C.I., Feb., 1931; under sec. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf; vice-consul, Mohammedah, Nov., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Zohb, May, 1937; sec. to resdt. for Baroda and the Gujarat States, Apl., 1939; ditto for Central India, Oct., 1940; asst. commandant, Malwa Bhil Corps and ex-officio asst. to pol. agent in Malwa, May, 1941; sec. to the resdt., Central India, July, 1941; H.B.M.'s

consul, Khorramshahr, March, 1942; sec. to resdt., Punjab States, June, 1943.

CHAVE, ELMER HARGREAVES, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Madras) (b. 10th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 14th Apl., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; ch. engr., Nov., 1940; served under cent. govt. for duty on Indus commn., Sept., 1941, to July, 1942; C.I.E., June, 1944.

CHAVES, HENRY JOSEPH, late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 8th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

CHEEMA, SARDAR GANDASING (see Gandasing, C. S.).

CHEESMAN, GEORGE MAURICE, M.B.E., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 16th May, 1876).—Apptd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept., India Office, 13th Nov., 1914; on mil. service, Sept., 1916, to Apl., 1918; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Apl., 1921; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1922; M.B.E., Jan., 1939; retd., May, 1941.

CHEIN, U. AUNG, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt. police, Burma, 21st Apl., 1928; confd., Apl., 1930; offg. dist. supt., Aug., 1934; confd., ditto, June, 1941.

CHENEVIX-TRENCH, SIR RICHARD HENRY KT., C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (retd.), (b. 4th Apl., 1876).—Retd., Apl., 1931.*

CHETPAT, PATTABHERAMA RAMASWAMI AYYAR (see Ramaswami Ayyar, Sir C. P.).

CHETTI, K.C.S. (see Sundaram Chetti, K.C.).

CHETTLE, GEORGE, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 18th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., on 19th Feb., 1921; offg. dist. supt., June, 1926; confd., July, 1927; awarded King's police medal, June, 1932; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

CHETTUR, KRISHNA KRISHNA, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 10th March, 1901).—Joined the service, 5th March, 1925; currency officer, Rangoon, Oct., 1930; ditto, Calcutta, Apl., 1933; under-secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1937; 1st secty. to cent. bd. rev., and ex-officio dep. secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1940; dep. sec. ditto, commerce dept., Aug., 1944.

CHETTUR MADHAVAN NAYAR (see Nayar, C. Madhavan).

CHETTUR, SANKARA KRISHNA, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 20th May, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Sept., 1931; spec. settlement offcr., May to Sept., 1937; sub-collr. and jt. mag., June, 1938.

CHETWODE, SIR PHILIP WALHOUSE, Bt., G.C.B., O.M., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., D.S.O., D.C.L., Field-Marshal (b. 21st Sept., 1869).—C.-in-C. in India and member govt.-genl.'s exec. council, Nov., 1930, to Nov., 1935.*

CHEYNE, GEORGE COLLIE, M.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Public Works Dept., Burma (b. 1st Jan., 1887).—Joined the Indian Forest Service in Burma as asst. consvr., 16th Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; M.B.E., June, 1924; transferred to public wks. dep. as river training expert, 24th Oct., 1927; retd., Feb., 1943.

CHHUTTAN LAL, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 11th Feb., 1882).—Retd., Feb., 1937.*

CHICK, ANDREW WILBERT, Legisl. Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 20th Aug., 1888),

—Joined legis. dept., 1908; supt., March, 1932; asst. secty., Oct., 1932.

CHIDELL, JOHN WALLACE PHILIP, M.A., Indian Office (princl., public and judl. dept.) (b. 26th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., 21st Dec., 1933; resdt. clk., Oct., 1935; pte. accty. to permt. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937; acting princpl., Oct., 1938; confd., Feb., 1939; lent to min. of food, June, 1940; ditto, min. of home security, July, 1941; sec. to Allied Contr. Commn., Feb., 1945.

CHINMULGUND, PANDURANG JAIRO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 20th Oct., 1913).—Educ. at New English Sch., Poona, Bombay Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 12th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

CHIRRAVOORIBALKRISHNA, RAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 15th July, 1914).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn., 2nd Oct., 1937; confd., 29th Oct., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., infm., and hony. secty., United Provs. soldiers' bd., Dec., 1939.

CHISHOLM, DONALD MALCOLM FRANCIS, O.B.E., V.D., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. commcl. manager, E.I. Rly.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 17th Sept., 1914; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., March, 1930; dep. ch., comml. manager (provl.), Apl., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

CHIT, U.BA, B.A., B.L., Burma Judl. Service (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 23rd of Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1912; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, March 1935.

CHITHAM, SIR CHARLES CARTER, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, Nov., 1906; asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1908; dist. supt., Jan., 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1926; confd., Aug., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1931; confd., June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; kt., June, 1936; offg. member, public service commn., Apl., 1937, to Sept., 1937; ret'd., Sept., 1941.

CHITHAM, JOHN ROWLAND, Indian Police (b. 21st Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 24th Nov., 1935; confd., 1938.

CHITRE, ATMARAM ANANT, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 17th May, 1877).—Ret'd., May, 1937.*

CHOPRA, AMAR NATH, M.B., B.S., D.T.M., D.P.H., Maj., I.M.S. (addl. asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 18th May, 1899).—First commn. dated 2nd March, 1930; in mil. employ to 7th Oct., 1935; P.A. to surg. genl. with govt., Bombay, 8th Oct., 1935; asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S., 1st Apl., 1938; offer. i.c. medl. store dept., Lahore cantt., Apl., 1939; att'd. C.P. jail dept., July, 1939; supt., central jail, Jubbulpore, Sept., 1939; addl. asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S., March, 1940.

CHOPRA, AMAR AATH, B.A., A.M.I.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Burma) (b. 15th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 4th Oct., 1915; asst. engr., Oct., 1916; on mil. duty with Indian D.F., 1st Aug. to 18th Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. ditto, confd., Oct., 1939.

CHOPRA, INDER SEN, M.B.E., Capt., I.A.

(b. 7th Sept., 1909).—Apptd. on probn., to I.P.S. from mil. employ, Jan., 1937; apptd. asst. pol. agt., Loralai Feb., 1937; dir., food supplies and dep. sec. revenue for Baluchistan, March, 1943.

CHOPRA, PRATAP CHANDRA, B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 24th Feb., 1895).—Asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1915; divl. transpn. officer, N.W. Rly., May, 1935; ret'd., May, 1940.

CHOPRA, SIR RAM NATH, C.I.E., M.B., Bt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 17th Aug., 1882).—Ret'd., Aug., 1939*; kt., Jan., 1941.

CHOWDHRY, KHEM CHANDRA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 25th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at D.A.V. High Sch., Lahore, Punjab Univ., and London Sch. of Economics; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CHRISTIE, WALTER HENRY JOHN, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab), Indian C.S. (dep. secty., govt. of India (food)) (b. 17th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and appts. depts., Aug., 1931; confd., Feb., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; under secty., govt. of India, dept. of communications, Dec., 1937; dep. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Sept., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; dep. secty., govt. of India (food), Feb., 1943.

CHRISTIE, WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (ch. sec. to govt., United Provs.) (b. 29th Feb., 1896).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1920; arrived, 29th Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1924; asst. commr., March, 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1926; offg. dep. commr., Delhi, Apl. to Nov., 1928; secty., bd. of rev., United Provs., Nov., 1929; offg. addl. dep. secty., govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Oct., 1932; dep. secty. to the govt. of India, finance dept., Apl., 1933; mag. and collr. (prov. subs.), Apl., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; offg. commr., N.I. salt rev., Apl., 1936, to Oct., 1937; finance secty., United Provs., May, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; confd. as ch. secty., govt. United Provs., July, 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1945.

CHRISTOPHERS, SIR SAMUEL RICHARD, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., Bt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 27th Nov., 1873).—Ret'd., Nov., 1930.*

CHUDASAMA, MANSINHJI MERUJI, LL.B., Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 25th Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 24th Dec., 1929; confd., Apl., 1931; confd. as supt., Sind, Dec., 1940; served under cent. govt. from Nov., 1943.

CHUNDER (see Chandra).

CHUNDER, KAMAL CHUNDER, B.A. (Calc.), B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Assam) (b. 4th Jan., 1895).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1919; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1919, and posted to Bengal; as asst. mag. and collr., services placed temp. under govt. of Assam, Dec., 1924; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Dec. 1924; offg. ditto, Bengal, Apl., 1927; confd., July, 1931.

CHUNI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S., Judicial Bch. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 22nd June, 1883).—Joined the service as proby. extra, asst. commr., 24th Jan., 1906; confd., Sept., 1910; sub-judge, Oct., 1912;

offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1921; confd., Oct., 1932; commr. for workmen's compensation (confd.), Nov., 1941.

CHUNI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, late Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 8th Apr., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th May, 1909; dep. supt., Dec., 1913; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Apr., 1933; ret'd., 7th Apr., 1943.

CHUNI LAL, HANDA, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 2nd Feb., 1907).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 21st Jan., 1929; proby. asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1933; under-secty. (constrn.) and dir. cent. designs, March, 1944.

CHURCHER, JOHN EDWARD GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 29th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa. Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 18th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Feb., 1933; confd., Aug., 1938.

CHURCHILL, EDWARD ARCHIBALD HICKLING, B.Sc., Indian Agricl. Service (princpl., agric. coll., Nagpur) (b. 15th Dec., 1889).—Apptd. asst. dir. of agric., 31st Jan., 1922; dep. dir. of agric., Jan., 1930; offg. princpl., agricl. coll., Nagpur, Nov., 1935; confd., Sept., 1938.

CINNA SAMUDRAM SESHAGIRI RAO HANUMANTHA RAO, RAO SAHIB, B.A. (inspr. of mun. cls., etc.) (b. 2nd May, 1884).—Apptd. dep. collr., 13th Aug., 1920; asst. secty., bd. of rev., Nov. to Dec., 1932; dep. inspr. of mun. councils and local bds., Sept., 1937, to March, 1938; inspr. ditto, Apr., 1938.

CLAGUE, SIR JOHN, C.M.G., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to sec. of state for Burma) (b. 29th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and as settl. offr.; offg. dep. dir., land records, Nov., 1916; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; on mil. duty, May, 1918, to Feb., 1919; dir. of agric., Burma, Nov., 1920; dep. commr., July, 1921; addl. sec. to govt., F.R. Dept. (in addn.), Nov., 1923; confd., July, 1924; offg. ch. secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Apr., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; offg. commr., May, 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; ret'd., March, 1937; adviser to sec. of state for Burma, Apr., 1937; to March, 1942; C.M.G., June, 1938; kt., June, 1938.

CLARE, REV. HEBER, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 16th Feb., 1933.

CLARENCE SMITH, JOHN ASHTON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 8th March, 1917).—Educ. at Rugby and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

CLARK, ALLAN McDONALD, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 16th Oct., 1895).—Educ. at Keith Gr. Sch. and Aberdeen, and Oxford (Non-Coll.) Universities; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the prov. of Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and sub-divl. officer, Nov., 1923; addl. mag. and collr., March, 1925; on spl. duty, rev. dept., Nov., 1925, to March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1927; confd., Nov., 1932; dep. sec., pol. and apppt. depts., Bengal, March, 1932.

CLARK, ARTHUR STIRLING, M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.)

(b. 3rd Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, and at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. junior clerk, store dept., 19th Feb., 1920; war service—European War, Oct., 1914, to Apr., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; services lent to Colonial Office, Apr. to Dec., 1921; principal exec. officer, Apr., 1922; contr. (tempy.), Apr., 1927; actg. dep. dir. inspection, Jan., 1940.

CLARK, FRANK KYNOC, M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educ. Service (b. 19th March, 1885).—Apptd. headmaster, Patwardhan high sch., Nagpur, June, 1917; offg. inspr. of schools, Jubbulpore Circle, Jan., 1927; confd., Nov., 1927; dep. dir., public instrn. and sec. to high school educn. board, Apr., 1931; educ. commr. with govt. of India (tempy.), March, 1932, to June, 1933; offg. princpl., Spencer training coll., Jubbulpore, June, 1933; ret'd., March, 1940.

CLARK, GEORGE EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 6th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Sandown (I. of W.) Secondary Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; addtl. under-secty., def. dept., July, 1940; under-secty., ditto, March, 1941.

CLARK, REGINALD (judge, high ct., Madras).—Apptd., July, 1944.

CLARK, THOMAS JOHN, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 26th Feb., 1895).—Ret'd., June, 1938.*

CLARK, THOMAS VINSON, Indian Police (b. 6th May, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, Nov., 1932.

CLARK, SIR WILLIAM HENRY, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I.—Member of council of govr.-genl. of India, commerce and industry dept., Nov., 1910, to Apr., 1916.*

CLARK, WILLIAM MORRISON, B.Sc., M.B.E., Indian Agricl. Service (b. 23rd May, 1895).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, on 3rd Apr., 1923; M.B.E., June, 1918; services transferred to Govt. of Bengal from Feb., 1938.

CLARKE, REV. ARTHUR, Indian Eccles. Establishment (junr. chaplain, United Provs.) (b. 13th March, 1901).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 9th Jan., 1937; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1939.

CLARKE, E. W., late Income Tax Dept., (b. 12th June, 1888).—Apptd. to income tax dept., central board of revenue, 7th March, 1919; asst. commr., Western range, Madras, March, 1924; offg. commr. variously from Dec., 1928; confd., March, 1937; ret'd., May, 1943.

CLARKE, GEOFFREY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Oct., 1882).—Ret'd., Oct., 1937.*

CLARKE, SIR GEOFFREY ROTHE, Kt., C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provs.).—Ret'd., Jan., 1926.*

CLARKE, MATTHEW JOHN, Maj., C.I.E., I.A. (commr., Burma) (b. 19th July, 1895).—First commn. dated 1st Dec., 1914; asst. commr., July, 1921; sec. to finl. commr., 11th Nov., 1926; offg. dep. commr., May, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1932; chairman, bd. of trustees for development of City of Rangoon, Aug., 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1934; offg. commr., Jan., 1941; commr., Feb., 1941.

CLARKE, VEN. MAURICE, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estab. (b. 21st July, 1893) (Archdeacon of Madras).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras, 15th Oct., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929;

senr. ditto, Oct., 1939; canon, 1939; archdeacon, 1940.

CLARKE, MAURICE NOBLE, Barr., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; on mil. service from Jan., 1915; joined the service, 13th Feb., 1920, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr., from 9th May, 1920; sub-judge, Dec., 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1926; confd., May, 1929; actg. judge, high ct., Nagpur, June-Oct., 1938; ret'd., Sept., 1942.

CLARKE, SIR REGINALD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Police Service (Bengal).—Ret'd., June, 1925.*

CLARKE, WILLIAM GEORGE, M.B.E., late Indian Police Service.—Ret'd.*

CLARKSON, WILLIAM M.B.E., India Office (hr. clerical officer, econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 10th May, 1899).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1920, after previous service from Apl., 1917; hr. clerical officer, Sept., 1936; M.B.E., June, 1942; actg. staff officer, July, 1942.

CLAUSON, MILES JOHN, B.A., India Office (actg. asst. secty., pol. (states) dept.) (b. 29th March, 1902).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; secty.'s office, G.P.O., Oct., 1925 to Dec., 1926; asst. princpl., India Office, Jan., 1927; resdt. clerk, 1st Oct., 1927; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state, Dec., 1928; actg. princpl., Jan., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; pte. secty. to secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937, to Nov., 1941; princpl. pol. dept., Nov., 1941; loaned to Treasury, Nov., 1943, to Nov., 1944; actg. asst. sec., pol. (states) dept., Dec., 1944.

CLAY, SIR JOSEPH MILES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to secty. of state for India) (b. 6th Sept., 1881).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., July, 1911; dep. commr., Garhwal, Apl., 1913; asst. mag. and collr., and forest settlt. offr. (in addn.), March, 1916; O.B.E., June, 1918; mag. and collr., Nov., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. commr., March, 1925; offg. sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Apl., 1927, and again, Oct., 1929; dep. commr., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Nov., 1931; confd., June, 1932; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Sept.-Dec., 1932; commr., Apl., 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Apl., 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1936; ret'd., Aug., 1937; adviser to sec. of state for India, Aug., 1937, to July, 1942.

CLAYDON, REV. EVAN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 15th May, 1898) (junr. chaplain, Punjab).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 27th March, 1928; junr. chaplain, March, 1929.

CLAYTON, SIR HUGH BYARD, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 16th Dec., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and railway mag.; asst. pol. agent, Nov., 1909; attached to mil. intelligence directorate, War Office, Sept., 1914, to Dec., 1918; offg. munpl. commr., city of Bombay, Dec., 1919; collr., Nov., 1920; munpl. commr., city of Bombay, Oct., 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; on spl. duty with govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., Nov., 1930, to March, 1931; commr., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1936; chairman, public services commn. for provs. of

Bombay and Sind, Apl., 1937; kt., June, 1938; K.-i-H. Gold Medal, Jan., 1944.

CLAYTON, RICHARD HENRY MICHAEL, B.A., I.C.S. (asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 11th Aug., 1907).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., Christ Church, Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Jan., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Apl., 1939; on depn., govt. of India, def. dept., Sept., 1939.

CLAYTON, ROBERT VICKERS, M.B., B.S., D.P.H., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 18th Dec., 1887).—Apptd. dist. medl. officer, N.W. Rly. (temp.), 26th Jan., 1928; ditto (perm.), B.B. & C.I. Rly., May, 1929; offg. dep. C.M.O., N.W. Rly., Nov., 1929; offg. C.M.O., N.W. Rly., Dec., 1929 P.M.O., G.I.P. Rly., July, 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1942.

CLEAR, THOMAS, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 17th June, 1886).—Ret'd., Nov., 1939.*

CLEARY, DENIS MACKROW, M.A., India Office (princl. ext. dept.) (b. 20th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at St. Ignatius Coll., Stamford Hill, and St. Olave's and St. Saviour's Gr. Sch.; St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., 19th Oct., 1931; resdt. clerk, Sept., 1934; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, Aug., 1936, to Oct., 1937; actg. princpl., Oct., 1937; confd., Jan., 1939; lent to min. of home security, June, 1940, to Sept., 1944.

CLEARY, MICHAEL FRANCIS, Indian Police (dep. dir., intell. bureau) (b. 4th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., on 4th Dec., 1914; confd., Nov., 1916; addl. supt., June, 1922; pers. assistant to dir. intell. bureau, govt. of India, Nov., 1923; supt., Dec., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; cent. intell. offr., Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1935; on depn. to Imp. Defence Coll., Jan., 1938; dep. dir., intel. bureau, March 1939 to Jan., 1941; asst. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1942, to Jan., 1943.

CLEE, CHARLES BEAUPRE BELL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty., rev. dept., Govt. of Sind) (b. 5th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Fitzwilliam Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1919; arrived, 28th Nov., 1919, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; actg. dir. of information, March, 1924; sec., tariff bd., 1st June, 1925; offg. dep. secty. to govt., finance dept., Bombay, Apl., 1928; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, home and eccl. depts., May, 1932; member, legisl. council, Oct., 1932; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, fin. dept., June, 1933, again Jan., 1936; collr. and mag., June, 1935; secty., fin. dept., Sind, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938; ch. sec. to govt., Sind, June, 1938 to Jan., 1939; offg. rev. commr. and sec. to govt. (rev.) Jan., 1943.

CLERICI, MARIO CLAUDE, Indian Police (b. 8th Nov., 1914).—Asst. supt., Bihar, apptd. on probn., 11th Nov., 1935; confd. Sept., 1937.

CLIFF, ARNOLD PEARSE, B.A., Indian Agric. Service (b. 3rd Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 5th Oct., 1920; secty., Indian Central Jute Ctee., Dec., 1936; addtl. dir. of agric., July, 1944.

CLIFFORD, REGINALD CHARLES, D.S.O., M.C., F.R.C.S.E., M.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., Medical Dept., United Provs. (b. 17th Sept., 1884); ret'd. Aug., 1939.*

CLIFFORD, WILLIAM WALTER, late Indian Police, Bombay (b. 26th Sept., 1887).—Ret'd., March, 1941.

CLIFT, FRANK CHARLES LECHMERE, A.C.H., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Jan., 1882).—Retd., Jan., 1937.*

CLIFT, GEORGE, B.Sc., Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 9th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 19th Aug., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1933; confd. Oct., 1938.

CLINTON-THOMAS, ROBERT ANTONY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 5th Apl., 1913).—Educ. at Haileybury and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; attaché, ext. affairs dept., Sept., 1941; vice-consul, Kerman, Dec., 1941; under sec. to resdt., E. States, Feb., 1944.

CLIVE, JOHN M'CLINTOCK, Lieut.-Col. M. C., Mil. accts. dept. (b. 20th Oct., 1891).—First commn., Apl., 1915; joined service as an offcr., cl. II, Aug., 1930.

CLOSE, ERNEST REGINALD WARD, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 4th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1921; asst. supt., July, 1923; offg. dist. supt., May, 1928; confd., July, 1930.

CLOUGH, ARTHUR ROBERT, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.), (b. 28th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; retd., 4th Apl., 1941.

CLOW, SIR ANDREW GOURLAY, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., F.S.S., Indian C.S. (gouv. of Assam) (b. 29th Apl., 1890).—Educ. at Merchiston Castle Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd., after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1914, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. record offr. and asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1917; dep. commr. (prov.), 1931; and settlt. offr., Sept., 1918, to June, 1919; sec. to Disorders Enquiry Ctee., Feb., 1920; controller, labour bureau, May, 1920; under-sec. to Govt. of India, dept. of industries, Apl., 1923; dep. sec., ditto, Aug., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; sec. ditto (temp.), Apl., 1928; adviser and substitute delegate, internl. labour conference, Geneva, May-June, 1929; member of R.C. on labour in India, July, 1929; on spl. duty in dept. of indus. and labour, Oct., 1931; jt. secty., ditto, Nov., 1931; offg. secty., ditto, June, 1935; and again, Jan., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1935; confd. as secty. to govt. of India, indus. and lab. dept., July, 1936; member, council of state, Feb., 1936; secty. dept. of lab., Nov., 1937; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s. exec. council, May-Sept., 1938; confd. Apl., 1939; Kt., June, 1939; pres. war transport bd., July, 1940; K.C.S.I., June, 1941; govr. of Assam, May, 1942.

CLUTTERBUCK, JACK ERNEST, Maj. R.E., Indian State Rlys. (ex. engr. G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 8th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1918; asst. exec. engr. Khyber Rly. Survey and Construction, Aug., 1919; asst. exec. engr. E.I. Rly., Apl., 1921; provl. perm. exec. engr., G.I.P. Rly., May, 1939; on mil. duty, Dec., 1939; confd. as exec. engr., March, 1940; dep. ch. engr., Dec., 1943.

CLUTTERBUCK, SIR PETER HENRY, Kt., C.I.E., C.B.E., F.Z.S., F.R.G.S., V.D., late Indian Forest Service.—Retd., Oct., 1926.*

CLYDE, DAVID, C.I.E., M.B., D.P.H., Col. I.M.S. (b. 30th March, 1894).—First commn., dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 12th Feb., 1924; asst. dir. of public health, Feb.,

1924; civil surg., Nov., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; inspr. gen. civil hospitals, May, 1944.

COATES, CHARLES LESLIE, Indian C.S. (under-sec. govt. of India supply dept.) (b. 1st Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. regr. co-op. societies, March-May, 1937; under-sec., govt. of India, communications dept., Feb., 1940; dep. sec. ditto, war transport, July, 1942; dep. sec. ditto supply, June, 1944.

COATES, SIR ERIC THOMAS, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (fin. adviser, milty. fin.) (b. 1st Oct., 1897).—Educ. at Heath Gr. Sch., Halifax, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1921, and posted to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and sub. divl. officer, April, 1923; on spl. duty in finl., commerce and marine depts., Nov., 1925, to March, 1926; offg. under-sec. in ditto, March, 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Nov., 1928; ditto, central revenues, Apl., 1929; offg. acct.-genl., Bombay, July, 1930; accts. offr. N.W.F.P., Oct., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, Nov., 1931; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Calcutta, Feb., 1932; offg. acct.-gen., Central Provs., Oct., 1933; ditto, Bombay, Apl., 1934; ditto, posts and tels., May, 1934; on spl. duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1935; jt. sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1938; fin. adviser, milty. fin., Apl., 1939; acct.-genl. (confd.), cl. II, Aug., 1940; C.S.I., June, 1941; kt., Jan., 1945.

COATES, WILLIAM JOSEPH, B.A., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 21st Aug., 1885).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., Jan., 1910; dep. mil. acct.-genl. (senr.), Apl., 1930; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., March, 1931; confd., as cl. I offcr., Oct., 1935; retd. 21st Aug., 1941.

COBB, EVELYN HEY, O.B.E., Maj., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 15th Sept., 1899).—First commn., dated 15th Apl., 1919; in milty. employ prior to 15th June, 1921; pers. asst. to ch. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1924; asst. collr., Agra, Dec., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Nov., 1927; supy. asst. commr., Peshawar, June, 1929; city mag., ditto, May, 1930; asst. commr., Charsadda, Dec., 1931; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1935; pol. agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral, March, 1937; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1939.

COBURN, MARMADUKE ROBERT, C.I.E., O.B.E., India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 9th March, 1885).—Joined the service as milty. acct., 11th March, 1910; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; dep. finl. adviser, May, 1920; offg. finl. adviser, July, 1925; offg. dir. of establishment, Nov., 1916; finl. advr., April, 1928; reverted to milty. accts. dept., April, 1933; dep. fin. advr. mil. fin., Oct., 1935; mil. acct. genl., April, 1937; C.I.E., June, 1939; addl. fin. adviser mil. fin., Oct., 1939, and again Sept., 1944; on depn. to U.K., Apl., 1944.

COCHRANE, HON. SIR ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., D.S.O., Commander, R.N., retd. (b. 8th Jan., 1885).—European War, 1914-1918 (desp. thrice, D.S.O. and bar); M.P. for East Fife, 1924-1929; M.P., Dumfries and Galloway, 1932-1936; K.C.S.I., March, 1936; govr. of Burma, May, 1936 to May, 1941; G.C.M.G., Apl., 1937.

COCHRANE, GEORGE ALFRED DAVIES, O.B.E., B.Sc., A.M.I. Struct.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr.,

Central Provs., 26th Sept., 1924; exec. engr. and under-secty. to govt. p.w.d., Sept., 1935; suptg. engr., Apl.-June, 1941; supt. of works, May, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

COCK, CHARLES MATTHEW, Indian State Rlys. (traction supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 27th Jan., 1895).—Apptd. traction engr., distribn., G.I.P. Rly., 21st Oct., 1929; traction supt., Apl., 1941.

COCKBURN, ARTHUR BALCOMBE, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 23rd Aug., 1885).—Joined the service, 6th Apl., 1905; asst. acct.-gen., May, 1914; examr. of local fund accts., Punjab, June, 1918; dep. acct.-gen., posts and tels., March, 1925; dep. acct.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1926; dep. dir. of audit, United Provs., Jan., 1927; offg. chief pay and accts. officer, April, 1927; dep. ch. accounting officer, United Provs., April, 1928, and again, March, 1930; ch. pay and accts. officer, U.P., March, 1929; dep. acct.-genl., U.P., Nov., 1931; audit Officer, Lloyd barrage and canals constn., March, 1932; offg. comptr., N.W.F.P., March, 1932; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1935; retd. July, 1940.

COCKBURN, GEORGE WILLIAM MACLOV, M.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) b. 27th May, 1913).—Educ. at Fettes and St. Andrew's Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.; asst. supt., Shan States, May, 1940; on military service, Nov., 1940.

COCKS, EDWARD JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 31st July, 1916).—Educ. at Leys School, Cambridge, and Christ's College, Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

COELHO, MANUEL ANTHONY TIMOTHY, B.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 24th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, Nov., 1936; apptd. dist. and sess. judge, on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937.

COEN, TERENCE BERNARD CREAGH, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 31st March, 1903).—Educ. at Uppingham and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty., local self-govt. dept., Dec., 1932; reg. high ct., Lahore, Nov., 1933; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Oct., 1935; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar Agency, March, 1936; secty. to the Resident at Hyderabad, Oct., 1937; confd. in the pol. service, Feb., 1938; secy. to resident for Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apr., 1941; on deputation, 1941; pol. agent, Gujarat, 5 Nov., 1944.

COFFEY, THOMAS MALO, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 4th May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922.

COGHILL, DUNCAN JOSEPH KNIGHT, Indian C.S. (mag. and colrr., United Provs.) (b. 25th Apr., 1903).—Educ. at Cheltenham and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr.; jt. mag., June, 1929; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Nov., 1930; asst. pol. agent, Zohb, Nov., 1932; offg. under-secty. to govt. of India, for, and pol. dept., March, 1934; offg. dep. secty., ditto, May, 1934 to Apl., 1935; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1935; confd. as mag. and colrr., Nov., 1941.

COGHLAN, JOHN ALOYSIUS, B.A., M.C., late Indian Police Service (Bombay) (b. 16th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt.,

on 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Nov., 1927; retd., Dec., 1940.

COHEN, WILLIAM CHARLES HENRY, late India Office (higher clerical officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 28th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital; apptd. second class clerk, 21st Jan., 1909, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; higher clerical officer, Apr., 1922; actg. hr. exec. offr., Jan., 1941; retd., July, 1943.

COHU, REV. CLIFFORD JOHN, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 30th Dec., 1883).—Retd., Feb., 1937.*

COKE-WALLIS, LEONARD GEORGE, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Ind. Political Service (b. 12th March, 1900).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1924, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and colrr.; under-secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and appts. depts., May, 1929; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Jan., 1930; under-secty. to C.C. and agent to G.G., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1930; secty. to A.G.G., Eastern States, April, 1933; vice-presdt., State Council, Cooch-Behar, Nov., 1936; sec. to resdt., Central India, Nov., 1939; pol. agent, Bhopal, Feb., 1940; sec. and excise asst. to res. C.I., Apl., 1940, and again Apl., 1941; pol. agt., Malwa, Apl., 1940; sec. to res. Hyderabad, July, 1941; pol. agt., Chhattisgarh States, July, 1943; offg. res. for Eastern states, June, 1943, and again, Feb., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

COLDMAN, REV. ALFRED THEODORE, B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt., Madras (b. 3rd July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

COLDSTREAM, SIR JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 23rd Dec., 1877).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

COLE, FRANCIS NORMAN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 29th July, 1906).—Educ. at Dulwich College, Trinity College, Oxford, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; pte. secty. to govr., Central Provs., Apr., 1934 to Nov., 1935.

COLE, GEORGE ASHMEAD, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 1st Feb., 1902).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; asst. commr., Kohat, 8th Apl., 1931; asst. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1932; asst. pol. agent. Bahrain, March, 1935; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1936; pol. agent in Chagai, Oct., 1937; asst. pol. agent in Zohb, Nov., 1939; pol. agent in Zohb (addtl. charge), Oct., 1940; asst. commr., Charsadda Jan., 1941; dy. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Sept., 1941; pol. agt., Khyber, Oct., 1943.

COLE, LESLIE PRICHARD, M.B.E., M.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 4th Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Madras, 20th Oct., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

COLEBROOK, EDWARD HILDER, M.C., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 16th Oct., 1898).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1921, as asst. supt. on probn.; confd. Apl., 1925; offg. spl. supt., Malabar spl. police, March, 1928; offg. dist. supt., Aug. 1929; confd., Nov., 1928; awarded King's police medal; Indian police medal, Jan., 1936 confd. as dep. inspr.-gen., Jan., 1943.

COLERIDGE, SIR JOHN FRANCIS STANHOPE DUKE, G.C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O., A.D.C., Lieut.-General, I.A. (b. 25th April, 1878).—Milty. secty., India Office, Nov., 1933 to May, 1936.*

COLLET, ARTHUR LOWE, O.B.E., Judl. Dept., Bengal (regr., etc., high ct., Calcutta) (b.

9th March, 1888).—Apptd. offg. regr. in insolvency, high ct., Calcutta, 23rd Nov., 1931; confd. (prov.), May, 1932; offg. regr., original side, Sept., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1941

COLLETT, HENRY ASHWOOD, V.D., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 1st Jan., 1880); retd., Aug., 1936.*

COLLINS, GODFREY FERDINANDO STRATFORD, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (advr. to govr., Sind) (b. 3rd Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from July, 1916 to Jan., 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; extra dep. dir. of civil supplies, Bombay, 1919; forest settlement officer (temp.), Sept., 1920; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1923; settlement officer, Oct., 1924; addl. dy. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Apl., 1926; confd., June, 1930; actg. regr., co-op. societies, June, 1926; secty. to gov. of Bombay, home and eccl. depts., Aug., 1929; member legisl. council, Bombay, 1929 and 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. pte. secty. to govr. of Bombay, Aug., 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. commr., Sind, Apl., 1935; confd., Nov., 1939; secty. to govt. rev. dept.; member, Sind rev. trib., Nov., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; advr. to govr., Jan., 1942.

COLLINS, JOHN LAVILLE, late Indian Police (b. 10th March, 1881).—Retd., March, 1936.*

COLLINS, TERENCE MOSTYN, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-gen., Central Provs.) (b. 21st March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1913; on mil. duty from 10th Nov., 1917 to 21st Oct., 1919; dist. supt., Jan., 1925; prinpl. training school, Saugor, March, 1925; central intel. officr., Central Provs., Nov., 1936; confd. as dep. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1944.

COLLIS, MAURICE STEWART, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 10th Jan., 1889).—Retd., Jan., 1936.*

COLLISTER, SIR HAROLD JAMES, late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Jan., 1885).—Educ. at South-Eastern Coll., Ramsgate, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; addl. sess. and sub-judge, Sept., 1917; jt. mag., Feb., 1918; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), July, 1918 to March, 1919; dist. and sessions judge, March, 1922; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1933, and July, 1934; judge, ditto, July, 1935; kt., Jan., 1944; retd., 23 Aug., 1944.

COLQUHOUN, SAMUEL, M.B.E., late Survey of India. (b. 23rd Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 18th Aug., 1905; manager, litho. bch., Nov., 1930; M.B.E., Jan., 1941; retd., Nov., 1939.

COLTHUP, SIDNEY ROBERT, India Office (hr. executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 11th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Rochester Math. Sch.; served as sec. div. clerk, Local Govt. Bd., from Feb. to Sept., 1912; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 23rd Sept., 1912, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from Aug., 1915 to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; hr. exec. officer, Sept., 1932; assessor of income tax, from Oct., 1935, to Dec., 1937, and from March, 1941.

COLVILLE, COL. SIR DAVID JOHN, P.C., G.C.I.E. (governor of Bombay).—Apptd., Feb., 1943.

COLVILLE, WILLIAM, Indian Police, O.B.E. (supt.) (b. 12th Apl., 1905).—Joined the service

as asst. supt., United Provs., 8th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., March, 1928; and again, Nov., 1933; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, Home dept., Apl., 1932; pers. asst. to dir., intelligence bureau, March, 1933; asst. to D.I.G. C.I.D., Apl., 1936 to Apl., 1937; supt., Oct., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

COLVIN, SIR CLEMENT PRESTON, Kt., O.B.E., V.D., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 12th July, 1879).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

COLVIN, ELLIOT JAMES DOWELL, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. retd.; late Indian Political Service (b. 27th July, 1885).—First commn., 9th Jan., 1904; in mil. employ to 11th July, 1908, when he was apptd. personal asst. to resdt., Hyderabad; pol. officer on probation, 12th Feb., 1912; appt. to pol. dept. confirmed, July, 1914; agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, June, 1915; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, India, 1921; under-sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Apl., 1922; on foreign service as adviser to H.H. the Maharaja of Rewa, Feb., 1924; offg. resdt. Gwalior, Apl., 1930; offg. pol. agent, E.R. States, Apl., 1930; confd., Sept., 1930; prime minister, Kashmir State, March, 1932, and again, March, 1935; C.I.E., June, 1933; resdt. for Baroda and Gujarat States, Jan., 1938; retd., July, 1940.

COLVIN, SIR GEORGE LETHBRIDGE, Kt., C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 1st Apl., 1878).—Agent, E.I. Ry., 30th Sept., 1921 to March, 1933; C.M.G., 1918; C.B., 1919; Kt., Jan., 1933.

COLYER, JOHN ALFRED, late Indian State Ryls., N.W. Ry. (b. 23rd Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

COMMISSARIAT, MANEKSHAH SORABSHAH, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Dec., 1881).—Retd., Dec., 1936.*

COMPTON, JOSEPH NEILD, A.M.I. Loco.E., Indian State Ryls. (dep. ch. mech. engineer, G.I.P., Ry.) (b. 25th Sept., 1900).—Asst. mechl. draftsman, junr. scale, Sept., 1922; confd. supt. senr. scale, Nov., 1938; dy. ch. mech. engr., Dec., 1940.

COMRIE, MARTIN CAMERON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 6 Nov., 1886).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

CONDER, STANLEY JOHN, India Office (higher executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 28th June, 1892).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 10th June, 1913, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from July, 1915 to Dec. 1918; exec. offr., Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, March, 1927; assessor of income tax, Oct., 1936; actg. suptg. exec. offr., Jan., 1941.

CONDON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B.E., late Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 22nd Feb., 1886).—Joined the service 8th Apl., 1910; King's Police Medal, Jan., 1925; promoted to I.P. as supt., Apl., 1936; retd., Feb., 1941.

CONLIN, JOHN FRANCIS, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Ajmer-Merwara) (b. 30th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 14th Oct., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1926, and again, Nov., 1932; asst. commr. in Andamans, Oct., 1926; supt., May, 1936; ditto, Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1936.

CONNOR, SIR FRANK POWELL, Kt., D.S.O., F.R.C.S., Major-Gen., I.M.S. retd. (b. 16th Sept., 1877).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

CONNOR, JAMES ESSON CLEAL, Burma Civil Service (Cl. 1) (asst. commr.) (b. 14th March., 1917).—Educ. at Govt. English High Sch., Maymyo, and Rangoon Univ., appt. on probn.,

30th Sept., 1939; joined the service, 24th Aug. 1940; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1940; and served as asst. commr.

CONNOR, WILLIAM CASEY, B.Sc., late Indian Police (b. 29th Nov., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

CONSTABLE, ERNEST HENRY, India Office (higher clerical officer, acclnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 8th July, 1883).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st March, 1922, after previous temporary service from Feb., 1920; higher clerical officer, 1st October, 1925.

CONSTANTINE, GEORGE BAXANDALL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 22nd June, 1902).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar School, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Feb., 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Feb., 1936; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1936; regr., high ct., Bombay, June, 1937 to Dec., 1938; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1938; chairman, aliens advisory cte., govt. of India, Oct., 1940 to Feb., 1941; offg. commr. of lab. and regr. trade unions, Bombay, var., from Apl., 1942.

CONTRACTOR, KHAN BAHADUR SHERLARIJ DADABHAI, M.B.E., B.A., late Bombay Educl. Service (b. 5th Sept., 1878).—Retd., Jan., 1936.* M.B.E., Jan., 1941; associate officer, Order of St. John, June, 1941.

CONTRACTOR, SORAB CURSHEDJI, M.B., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S., Jail dept., Madras (supt.) (b. 25th Jan., 1887).—First commn., dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 25th Sept., 1925; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., June, 1928.

COOK, BERNARD CHRISTOPHER ALLEN, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. fin. dept., govt. of India) (b. 20th July, 1906).—Educ. at Radley, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1929; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt., U.P., genl. bch. (tempy.), Apl., 1934; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935; under secty. fin. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1938; contr. of enemy firms and trading and custodian of enemy property, Sept., 1939; on spec. duty (commr.) coal, Jan., 1942; ditto in gov.-genl. reforms sectt., March, 1942; dep. sugar contr. for India, Apl., 1942; addl. dep. sec. fin., Aug., 1942; dep. ditto, Dec., 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

COOK, SIR EDWARD MITCHENER, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., United Provs.—Retd., June, 1926.*

COOK, JACK MERVYN GERARD, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 22nd March, 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 24th Nov., 1930; confd. Jan., 1933; confd. as supt., June, 1944.

COOK, JAMES HIGH ERNEST, late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 8th June, 1896).—Joined service as proby. supt., 26th Apl., 1907; confd., Jan., 1908; on dept. to I.E.F., Aug., 1914 to May, 1915; dep. P.M.G., July, 1932; P.M.G., Jan., 1938; ret. June, 1941.

COOK, WILLIAM LOUIS CHARLES GERARD, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 21st Feb., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 13th Nov., 1923; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1929; confd., Feb., 1936; asst. inspr. genl., March, 1938 to March, 1941.

COOKE, CHRISTOPHER HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (fin. secty. to govt., United Provs.) (b. 29th May, 1889).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922; and

served in United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; offg. dep. secty. to govt., United Provs., Jan., 1934; offg. secty., bd. of rev., United Provs., Oct., 1934; settlement officer, Oct., 1936; mag. and collr., Dec., 1937; rev. secty. to govt., U.P., Nov., 1939; fin. sec., ditto, March, 1944.

COOKE, CONRAD REGINALD, O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.E.E., India Posts and Telegraphs (divl. engr.) (b. 31st Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1925; divnl. engr., June, 1932; field service, Dec., 1940; confd. as dir. tels., Jan., 1944; O.B.E., June, 1944.

COOKE, GEORGE HUMPHREY, Major, I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Jan., 1903).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1923; in milty. employ to 19th Jan., 1930; asst. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1931; senr. sub-judge, Hazara, Nov., 1933; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1935; asst. commr. Tank, Oct., 1938; dep. commr., D.I. Khan, Jan., 1939; asst. commr. Tank, March, 1939; secty. to resdt. for E. States, July, 1940; dep. sec. pol. dept. (war), Sept., 1943.

COOKE, THOMAS FOTHERGILL, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 15th May, 1889).—Retd., June, 1937.*

COOKSEY, WILFRID, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Dec., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

COOPER, ALLAN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th Dec., 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

COOPER, CHARLES ROBERT PLANT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 26th Apl., 1882).—Retd., July, 1939.

COOPER, SIR DHANJISHAH BOMANJEE, Khan Bahadur.—Apptd. member, govr's exec. council Bombay, June, 1934, to March, 1937; knighted Feb., 1937.

COOPER, HAROLD HINTON, Indian State Rlys. (ch. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 20th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th Dec., 1914; offg. dist., loco. supt., Oct., 1919; offg. works manager, loco. and carr., Nov., 1926; pers. asst. to ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1927; works manager, loco., Sept., 1927; offg. supt., mechl. workshops, Aug., 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936; and again, March, 1937; provl. perm., Jan., 1939; confd., Jan., 1940; supt. mech. workshops, Nov., 1940; ch. engr. mechl. (provl.) Jan., 1941.

COOPER, JOHN WALTER, Office of High Commr. for India (principal exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 22nd Dec., 1879).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Store Dept, 9th June, 1898; supervisor, ditto, Aug., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; principal ditto (tempy.), April, 1927; confd., Apl., 1929.

COOPER, ROBERT FRANCIS, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th May, 1886).—Retd., Aug., 1937.*

COOREY, CLARENCE OSWOLD, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. sec., educ., etc., dept., Madras) (b. 28th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, Univ. of Ceylon and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., July, 1936; commr. Madura municip., Apl., 1940; jt. sec. bd. of rev., Aug., 1943; dep. sec., educ., etc., dept., Jan., 1944.

CORBAN-LUCAS, ARTHUR JOHN LIONEL, late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 23rd Aug., 1887).—Retd., Dec., 1936.*

CORFIELD, CONRAD LAURENCE, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.C., M.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (Indian Political service) (b. 15th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Feb., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. from 5th May, 1920; asst. pte. sec. to the Viceroy, May, 1921, to Dec., 1922; sec. to the agt. to the govr.-genl. in the states of Western India, Jan., 1925; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1926; secty. to agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana, Nov., 1928; pol. agent in Southern States of C.I. and in Malwa, June, 1931; secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1931; vice-presdt., Rewa State council, March, 1932; adviser to delegate for Rewa, Indian round-table conf., 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; jt. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1934; offg. addl. ditto, Apl., 1935; confid., Aug., 1936; jt. secty., F. and P. Dept., Apl., 1935; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; jt. secty., pol. dept., Apl., 1937; offg. secty. to H.E. the Crown Representative, May, 1937; jt. secty., pol. dept., Oct., 1937; resdt. at Jaipur, Nov., 1938; resdt. Rajputana and the ch. commr. Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1939; resdt. Jaipur, Oct., 1939; pol. agent and dep. commr. Quetta Pishin, Dec., 1940; resident, Punjab States, Sept., 1941; confid., Apl., 1942; C.S.I., June, 1942.

CORFIELD, WILLIAM FRANCIS, Office of the High Commissioner for India (hr. clerical officer, accts. dept.) (b. 24th Apl., 1882).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st June, 1923; actg. hr. ditto, Apl., 1927; confd., March, 1936.

CORIN, JOHN MADAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 9th May, 1907).—Educ. at St. Paul's, and Magdalene College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-secty., home, pol., etc., depts., Bombay, Aug., 1935; passport officer, Bombay, Nov., 1936; secty. to govr., Sind, Apl., 1937.

CORMACK, FREDERICK EUSON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. exec. engr., Assam) (b. 18th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service, Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Feb., 1914; I.A.R.O. from 27th June, 1916 to 7th June, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; temp. suptg. engr., March, 1938, ch. eng. and sec. to govt. p.w.d., Aug., 1943.

CORMACK, HENRY SLATER, M.C., M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S., retd., late Burma Medl. Dept., (b. 6th May, 1886).—First commn. dated 28th Jan., 1911; in mil. employ to 6th Nov., 1922; offg. ophthalmic surg., Rangoon, Nov., 1922; March, 1926, and again, May, 1929; civil surg., Nov., 1932; retd., May, 1941.

CORNELIUS, ALVIN ROBERT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 1st May, 1903).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., and Oct., 1932; and again, May, 1933; confid., Nov., 1936; attaché, govt. of India, legis. dept., May, 1941; dep. leg. remembrancer, Punjab, Dec., 1942; legal ditto and sec. govt. of Punjab, legis. dept., 1943.

CORNISH ARTHELSTAN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 22nd July, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 7th Oct., 1913; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1926; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly., March, 1935; and again, March, 1936; offg. dep. ch. opertg. supt., N.W.

Rly., Nov., 1935; dep. ch. mechl. engr. (prov. perm.), Feb., 1937; supt. mechl. workshops, Nov., 1937; retd., Nov., 1940.

CORNISH, HENRY DAUNCEY, Barr., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 7th Aug., 1877).—Retd., Aug., 1937.*

CORNWELL, RICHARD BARRY, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 20th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1909; dep. consvr., Oct., 1922; on deputation to Forest Research Institute, Sept., 1924; dist. forest officer, Oct., 1933; on mil. duty, July, 1940—Nov., 1944.

CORY, EDGAR MYRIE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 14th Sept., 1906; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; in milty. employ from 22nd May, 1916 to 12th Feb., 1920; dist. engr., Nov., 1925; offg. divl. transpn. supt., Nov., 1930; dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1933; offg. ch. engr., variously from May, 1936; chief engr. (prov.), Nov., 1937; retd., Jan., 1942.

COSGRAVE, SIR WILLIAM ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 6th Apl., 1879).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

COSTELLO, JOHN FRANCIS, M.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th March, 1879).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 28th May, 1909; resdt. engr., July, 1914; dist. engr., July, 1925; offg. principal, Bina Transportation School, Nov., 1926, and again, Dec., 1929; offg. dep. agent, May, 1929; Apl., 1930, and again, May, 1931; confid. (prov.), July, 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1932; retd., March, 1936.

COSTELLO, SIR LEONARD WILFRED JAMES, Kt., M.A., LL.B. (b. 25th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 30th Dec., 1926; knighted, June, 1935; retd., March, 1941.

COTTAM, WILLIAM JOHN, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 23rd March, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 5th Dec., 1924; addl. supt., June, 1930; spec. supt., intell. bch., July, 1944.

COTTER, EDWARD, C.I.E., M.B., B.Ch., D.P.H., Col. I.M.S. (public health commr. with the Govt. of India) (b. 6th Nov., 1892).—First commn., dated 8th Dec., 1915; in milty. employ prior to 1st July, 1930; asst. dir. pub. health, Burma, July, 1930; offg. dir., May, 1933; dep. public health commr. with the govt. of India, July, 1937; public health commr., March, 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

COTTON, REV. HENRY WILMOT STAPLETON, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (archdeacon of Lucknow) (b. 22nd Feb., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 22nd Oct., 1925; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; offg. archdeacon of Lucknow, Apl., 1936; and again, May, 1937; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1937; bishop's commry., Apl., 1938; archdeacon, Apl., 1938.

COTTON, JOHN RICHARD, M.B.E., Capt., I.A., Indian Pol. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1909).—First commn., 31st Jan., 1929; pers. asst. to ch. commr. and resdt., and C.-in-C. at Aden, Nov., 1934; passport offcr. (in addn.), Jan., 1935; under trng. in the Punjab, Jan., 1936; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, July, 1937; confid., March, 1938; attd. officer, Rajputana Agency, Feb., 1939; under sec. to resdt., Rajputana, Feb., 1939; sec. to resdt., Rajputana, in addn., March, 1939; under-sec. to resdt., Rajputana, Apl., 1939; ditto, Hyderabad, June, 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1944; sec. to resdt., W.I. States, May, 1944.

COUCHMAN, SIR HAROLD JOHN, D.S.O.,

M.C., Brig., R.E. (ret'd.), late Survey of India (b. 29th July, 1882).—Ret'd., Sept., 1937.*

COUESLANT, D. C., B.Sc. (Lond.), late Industries Dept., Bihar, (b. 23rd July, 1877).—Ret'd., July, 1937.*

COUPER, SIR THOMAS, Kt., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 15th March, 1878).—Ret'd., Dec., 1937.*

COUSENS, HENRY JAMES MORRISON, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 4th May, 1883).—Ret'd., May, 1938.*

COUSINS, EDMUND RICHARD JOHN RATCLIFFE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S.
