(commr., Bihar) (b. 22nd Apr., 1888).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, Apr., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1918; offg. dep. commr., March, 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1922; confd., July, 1925; dep. commr., Feb., 1926; offg. commr., Apr., 1934, C.I.E., June, 1938; commr., March, 1937; advr. to the govr., Bihar, Nov., 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1942.

COUTTS, DUNCAN, M.B., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd., late Civil Med. Dept., Bihar (b. 7th Nov. 1882).—Ret'd., Sept., 1936.*

COVENTRY, JOHN PHILIP CHETWYND, Indian Police (b. 14th Dec., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Dec., 1937; services placed at the disposal of the Govt. of Cent. Provs. and Berar from Oct., 1938; a.d.c. to the govr., Aug., 1941.

COWAN, ALAN CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 7th Sept., 1915).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd., on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

COWARD, DUNCAN CECIL, Indian State Ry's. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 16th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 25th June, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Ry's., Feb., 1932.

COWGILL, JOHN FELIX, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 11th June, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 25th Nov., 1923; pers. asst. to dir., intelligence bureau, home dept., March, 1930; confd. as supt., July, 1936; O.B.E., June, 1935; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, March, 1938; ret'd., Nov., 1940.

COWGILL, JOHN HARRY CHRISTOPHER, Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 14th Nov., 1927; offg. addl. supt., March, 1934; supt. confd., Nov., 1940.

COWHAM, Rev. ARTHUR GERARD, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estab. (junr. chaplain) (b. 27th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 25th Feb., 1929; junr., chaplain, Feb., 1931.

COWIE, JOHN GRANDISON, M.B.E., Dept. of Industries, Punjab (inspr. of industrial schools) (b. 12th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as vice-prinpl., Mayo sch. of arts, Sept., 1924; inspr. of industrial schools, May, 1928; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

COWLEY, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at Middlesbrough High Sch., Jesus Coll., Cambridge, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; a.r.p. offr., Apr., 1942; asst. orgnr. nat. war front, Sept., 1942.

COX, ALFRED INNES, O.B.E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. Medical Dept., Madras (dist. medl. officer) (b. 1st Nov., 1894).—First commn., 27th Sept., 1919; in milty. employ until 25th Jan., 1932; dist. medl. officer, Madras, May, 1934; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

COX, CUTHBERT EUSTACE CONNOR, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service, Central Provs. (b. 9th March., 1885).—Joined the service, 8th Nov., 1907, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Dec., 1912; offg. consvr., June, 1924, July, 1925, and again, May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1928; ch. commr., Nov., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1939; ret'd. March, 1940.

COX, EDMUND LEONARD, Indian Police (asst. comdt., Malabar Special Police) (b. 29th Jan., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; asst. comdt., Malabar spec. police, Oct., 1936.

COX, EDWARD ORAM, B.A.I., M.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 30th May, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; M.B.E., June, 1919; offg. suptg. engr., Apr., 1932; confd., Jan., 1935; ret'd., May, 1940.

COX, HUBERT EUSTACE, Indian State Railways (dep. ch. engr., signals, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 14th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service 2nd Jan., 1915; signal and interlocking engr., G.I.P. rly., Apr., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., signals, July, 1935; confd., Dec., 1935.

COX, VERNON MAURICE HERVALWIL, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Aug., 1898).—First commn., 12th Sept., 1917; in milty. employ to 1st Nov., 1927; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, Ja., 1930; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat and of Chagai, May, 1932; asst. commr., Peshawar, Feb., 1935; dep. commr., Peshawar, May, 1936; offcr. on spec. duty, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1936; pol. agent, ditto, March, 1937; rev. and divl. commr., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1938; home secty., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1939, and again, Nov., 1940; chief secty. ditto, Aug., 1940; dy. commr. Bannu, Feb., 1941; sec. to resdt. for Western India, Oct., 1941; pol. agt., E. Kathiawar and Sabar Kantha agencies, Apr., 1944.

COYAJEE, H. H. C., B.A., B.Sc., Barr.-at-Law, Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 24th Nov., 1897).—Addl. judge high ct., Bombay, March to July, 1943; judge, ditto, July, 1943.

COYAJEE, SIR JEHANGIR COOVERJEE, Kt., B.A. (Cantab.), LL.B. (Bom.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Sept., 1875); ret'd., Nov., 1931.*

CRACKNELL, ARTHUR JOHN, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 17th May, 1885).—Ret'd., Nov 1938.*

CRACKNELL, FREDERICK GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 12th Feb., 1910).—Educated at Leeds Grammar School and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1934; civil and sess. judge, Oct., 1941; dep. sec. govt. of India (home), Sept., 1944.

CRAIG, MALCOLM THOMAS, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Central Provs.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Apr., 1920; asst. exec. engr., May, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; ret'd., Apr., 1941.

CRAIG, THOMAS JOSEPH ALEXANDER, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 3rd Dec., 1881).—Ret'd., Dec., 1936.*

CRAIK, SIR HENRY DUFFIELD, Bart., G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.; (b. 2nd Jan.,

1876).—Educ. at Eton Coll., Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; regr. of the chief court, March, 1905; asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1908; settlt. Offr., Oct., 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1915; dep. commr., and rev. sec. to govt., Feb., 1916; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1919; offg. addl. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1919; offg. chief sec. to Punjab Govt., Apl., 1922; confd., July, 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1924; offg. finl. commr., Nov., 1927; commr. (prov.), Oct., 1929; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, Apl., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; K.C.S.I. Punjab, Apl., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1935; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, July, 1934; apptd. govr. of Punjab, Apl., 1938; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1941; retd. Apl., 1941; pol. advr. to H.E. Crown Representative, Oct., 1941, to May, 1943.

CRAKER, PHILLIP WILLIAM, O.B.E., late Indian State Railways Aug., 1887).—Asst. engr. 1st Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1920; exec. engr., Aug., 1937. On depn. to Burma Govt., Apl., 1938; retd. Apl., 1941.

CRANSTON, WILLIAM PATRICK, Capt. I.A. (b. 5th Oct., 1913).—Apptd. to I.P.S., on probn., from mil. employ., Oct., 1938; under-sec. res. Hyderabad, Feb., 1944.

CRAW, HENRY HEWAT, K.B.E., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (advr. to sec. of state for India and Burma) (b. 29th Oct., 1882).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., and Jena Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty, Oct., 1917, to Jan., 1919; offg. depy. commr., May, 1921; dep. commr., Jan., 1923; offg. commr., May, 1931; confd., June, 1935; ch. secty. to govt., Burma, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; finl. commr., Feb., 1938; counsellor to the govr., Burma, March, 1939 to Nov., 1941; K.B.E., Jan., 1942; advr. to sec. of state for Burma, Feb., 1942; ditto for India, in addn., June, 1943.

CRAWFORD, CHARLES ANTHONY, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (chief comml. mgr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 14th Apl., 1900).—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1916, and held non-gazetted appts.; asst. supt., Feb., 1923; asst. comml. manager, March, 1926; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, June, 1929; and again, Nov., 1935; confd., Dec., 1936; ch. comml. manager, Aug., 1940; reg. contr. rly. priorities, war transp., March, 1943; O.B.E., June, 1944.

CRAWFORD, WILLIAM GLASGOW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 28th Apl., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., May, 1931.

CRAWLEY, DESMOND JOHN CHETWODE, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and mgr., Madras) (b. 2nd June, 1917).—Educ. at King's School, Ely, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd. 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mgr.; sub. collr., 1940; under-sec., public dept., Jan., 1944; addtl. under-sec., devel. dept., March, 1944.

CRAWLEY, GEORGE RICHARD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 22nd

Apl., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1930; confd., Apl., 1933; A.D.C., to govr., Oct., 1936.

CREAGH-COEN (see Coen, T. B. C.).

CREANER, THOMAS AMBROSE, B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 24th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1930.

CREED, CLARENCE JAMES, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Police (inspr.-gen., Bihar) (b. 19th July, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 29th March, 1920; supt., Sept., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1936; confd. Jan., 1940; O.B.E., June, 1943; inspr.-gen., Oct., 1944.

CREEDY, HAROLD DOUGLAS, B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 15th June, 1885).—Retd., March, 1935.*

CREEK, ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 11th May, 1910).—Educated at the East Anglian School, Bury St. Edmunds, and St. John's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; mag. and collr., May, 1941.

CREIGHTON, THOMAS GORDON, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., M.I.L.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engr., N.W., Rly.) (b. 29th Dec., 1898); asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 11th Dec., 1922; divl. mech. engr., senr. scale, July, 1937; dep. ch. contrl. of standardisation, rly. board, Apl., 1940.

CRERRAR, SIR JAMES, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Sind) (b. 11th Dec., 1877).—Retd., Aug., 1934.*

CRICHTON, GERALD CHARLES LAWRENCE, Maj., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 30th July, 1900).—First commn. dated 14th July, 1921; prob. in pol. dept., May, 1926; settlement officer, Peshawar, Jan., 1927; asst. commr., Bannu, July, 1927; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana and C.C. Ajmer Merwara, June, 1928; pol. asst. to C.C. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1928; asst. commr., Tank, Nov., 1930; offg. asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Oct., 1931; under-secy. to govt., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transf. depts., Dec., 1933; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Oct., 1934; home secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1937; dep. secty., ext. dept., govt. of India, June, 1938; home sec., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1938; pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1939; ditto, Khyber, Sept., 1942; jt. sec. ext. affairs dept., Aug., 1943; counsellor, Brit. legn., Kabul, Dec., 1943.

CRICHTON, WALTER HUGH, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 24th July, 1896).—First commn., 8th Dec., 1924; agency surg., Kurram, Feb., 1933; health officer, Simla, Oct., 1934; asst. dir. pub. health, Delhi, Oct., 1936; chief health officer, Delhi, Apl., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; in temp. military employ from May, 1941.

CRICKMAY, JOSEPH GEORGE, India Office (actg. princpl. fin. dept.) (b. 23rd Apl., 1883).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 17th Jan., 1902; transf. to mil. dept., from Oct., 1914; minor staff clerk, March, 1917; staff clerk (actg.), Nov., 1919; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1922; representative of the India

Office on the Navy, Army and Air Force Insurance Fund Advisory Ctee., Apl., 1922; actg. suptg. exec. officer, March, 1935; and again, Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937; actg. princpl. fin. dept., Feb., 1943.

CRITCHLY, VERNON FAIRBANK (dep. chief engr. Punjab Electricity) (b. 22nd Sept., 1893).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1927; served as exec. and elec. engr. variously up to Dec., 1934; dep. chief engr., Jan., 1936; confd. as ch. engr., Oct., 1940.

CROAD, ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Dec., 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

CROCKETT, WILLIAM GORDON, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (b. 18th Dec., 1880).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 23rd Nov., 1900; junior clerk, Nov., 1914; M.B.E., Jan., 1918; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; personal asst. to high commr., Oct., 1920; senr. clerk, Apl., 1921; contr., store dept., Apl., 1922; retd., Dec., 1940; re-employed on temporary basis, as controller store dep., Dec., 1940.

CROFT, SIR WILLIAM DAWSON, K.B.E., C.I.E., C.V.O., B.A., India Office (b. 16th Aug., 1892).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, military dept., 28th May, 1919, after previous service as temporary junr. clerk since Nov., 1915; apptd. pte. sec. to the perm. under-sec. of state, Jan., 1920; principal, Apl., 1921; establishment officer, July, 1921 to Jan., 1922; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; pte. secty. to secty. of state, Apl., 1931 to Oct., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; asst. secty. Aug., 1936; posted to econ. and overseas dept., Oct., 1937; C.V.O., Jan., 1938; actg. asst. under-sec. of state for India, July, 1940; deputy under-sec. for India, 1st Jan., 1942; K.B.E., June, 1943; on loan to min. of state, Cairo.

CROFTON, DENIS HAYES, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 14th Dec., 1908).—Educated at Tonbridge School and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to govt., pol. dept., Bihar, June, 1936; attached to govt. of India, dept. of lab., Apl., 1939; under-secty. to govt. of India, dept. of lab., June, 1939; pte. sec. to Ind. rep., E. Group Supply Council, Feb., 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1943; pte. sec. to govr., Bihar, Feb., 1944.

CROFTON, RICHARD MARSH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. Central Provs.) (b. 6th Apl., 1891).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from Oct., 1917 to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., July, 1922; dep. commr. (provl.) and finl. sec. to govt., May, 1926; dep. commr., Dec., 1926; offg. rev. secty. to govt., Dec., 1927; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1931; excise and opium commr. in C. India, Nov., 1931; dir. genl., rev. dept., Hyderabad, Feb., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; commr. (confd.), March, 1941; prime minister, Bahawalpur State, Apl., 1942.

CROFTS, FRANK NORMAN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 23rd July, 1906).—Educ. at Rugby and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived 1st Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. collr. and mag.; temp. sess.

and sub-judge, May, 1935; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1936; confd., Jan., 1940.

CROMBIE, ALAN DOUGLAS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 20th Jan., 1894).—Educ. at Wolverhampton Grammar Sch.; joined the service, 25th Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1923; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1925; on foreign service as agent under Rajah of Pariakimedi, Nov., 1926; dep. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Madras, Dec., 1929; pte. secty. to govr., Madras, June 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; collr. (provl. subs.), Sept., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936; collr. and dist. mag., Sept., 1936.

CROMBIE, GEORGE EDMOND, M.A., late India Office (b. 14th June, 1908).—Educ. at Aberdeen Grammar Sch. and Fettes Coll., Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. asst. principal, 19th Oct., 1931; actg. resdt. clk., July to Aug., 1932; resdt. clk., July, 1934 to Oct., 1934; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, Oct., 1935; pte. secty. to parly. under-secty. of state, Aug., 1936; actg. princpl., Apl., 1937; confd., Oct., 1938; retd. May, 1942.

CROMBIE, PETER, M.A., India C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 2nd Nov., 1911).—Educ. at Fettes College, University of Aberdeen and Caius College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1938.

CROSSLEY, DONALD, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 22nd July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1929; confd., March, 1934; awarded Indian Police Medal, March, 1937.

CROSSLEY, THOMAS BIGHAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Feb., 1910).—Educated at Blackburn Grammar School and King's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

CROSTHWAIT, TIMOTHY LELAND, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 5th Aug., 1915).—Educ. at Wellington and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 19th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, 12th Feb., 1942; M.B.E., June, 1944.

CROSTHWAITE, SIR BERTRAM MAITLAND, Kt., V.D., late Indian State Riys. (b. 9th Oct., 1880).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

CROSTHWAITE, SIR HUGH STUART, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 20th Oct., 1879).—Educ. at Rugby Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1910-1912; chairman, Allahabad munpl. bd., Apl., 1913, to Oct., 1916; supt., Dehra Dun, from March, 1917; mag. and collr., Jan., 1921; sec. to govt. of United Prov., industries dept., Apl., 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; offg. finance secty. to govt., Apl., 1927; dep. commr., Nov., 1927; offg. commr., March, 1929; confd., May, 1932; offg. ch. commr., Delhi, Apl., 1934; member fed. public service commn., Feb., 1935 to Feb., 1940; retd., Feb., 1940; kt., Jan., 1941.

CROXFORD, ERNEST WILLIAM, India Audit Office (exec. officer) (b. 18th Jan., 1915).—Apptd.

exec. officer, 2nd Oct., 1933; actg. higher exec. offr., Sept., 1941.

CRUIKSHANK, MARTIN MELVIN, C.I.E., M.D., B.Sc., Ch.M., D.O.M.S., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S. (chief medl. officer, Delhi Province) (b. 22nd March, 1888).—First commn., 17th March, 1919; in milty. employ until 25th Feb., 1931; dist. medl. officer, Madras, May, 1934; prof. of surgery, Madras medl. coll., and surgeon, genl. hospital, Madras, Sept., 1934; supt. and surg., genl. hosp., Madras, Oct., 1939; ch. medl. offr., Delhi; March, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

CRUMP, EDWIN SAMUEL, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 6th July, 1882).—Retd., July, 1937.*

CRUMP, SIR LOUIS CHARLES, Kt., late Indian C.S.—Retd., Jan., 1929.*

CUMMING, SIR JOHN GHEST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 29th Dec., 1868).—Retd., Oct., 1921.*

CUMMING, ROUALEYN CHARLES ROSSITER, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl. police, and jl. secty., Assam) (b. 2nd Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 7th Dec., 1911; supt., Apl., 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1935; inspr.-genl., and jt. secty. (home dept.), Assam, Dec., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1942; King's police and fire serv. medals, 1944.

CUNLIFFE, SIR JOHN ROBERT ELLIS, Kt., Barr. (late judge, hgh ct., Calcutta) (b. 4th March, 1886).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

CUNNINGHAM, SIR CHARLES BANKS, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 8th May, 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

CUNNINGHAM, SIR GEORGE, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (govr., N.W.F.P.) Indian Political Service (b. 23rd March, 1888).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinborough, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam of 1910; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1911, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; arst. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1914; personal asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Oct., 1915; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1917; asst. commr., Tank, Dec., 1918; pol. asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Feb., 1919; sub-divl. offr., Tank, Apl., 1919; pte. sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., May, 1919; spl. duty on staff of chief pol. offr. with N.W. Frontier Field Force, May, 1919; O.B.E., Aug., 1920; asst. commr., Thal, Nov., 1920; pol. agent, Tochi, Apl., 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; counsellor, British Legation, Kabul, Nov., 1925; pte. sec. to the Viceroy, July, 1926; C.S.I., June, 1931; member, exec. council govr. N.W.F.P., June, 1932 to May, 1936; actg. govr. N.W.F.P., Nov., 1932; offg. agent to govr.-genl., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1932; K.C.I.E., June, 1935; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1937; govr., N.W.F.P., March, 1937.

CURGENVEN, SIR ARTHUR JOSEPH, B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 24th July, 1876).—Retd., July, 1936.*

CURRIE, DONALD McDONALD, B.Sc. (consvr., Madras) (b. 11th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922; confd. as consvr., May, 1944.

CURRIE, MARK MAINWARING LEE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 14th Apl., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

CURSETJI, MANEKJI JEHANGIR MANEKJI, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.K.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 18th Sept., 1890).—Joined the provl. service as asst. engr., June, 1915; asst. engr. (impl. service),

Jan., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., June, 1924.

CURTIS, GERALD COLVILLE SEYMOUR, B.A., O.B.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 4th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Westminster, Christchurch Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 8th October, 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served as asst. commr. in the Punjab; pol. asst. and commandant, Border Mily. police, May, 1932; colonisation officer and asst. pol. agent, Nasirabad, May, 1933; and again, Dec., 1934; pol. agent, Sibti, Nov., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Oct., 1936; asst. commr. and city mag., Peshawar, Aug., 1938; dep. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1938; dep. commr., Hazara, March, 1939; addl. dy. sec. ext. affairs dept., Oct., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; pol. agt., S. Waziristan, Dec., 1943.

CURTIS, SYDNEY JOSEPH, M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 1st Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922.

DABHOLKAR, ANANT YASHWANT, M.C., J.B., B.Sc. (Bom.), D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Public health dept., Bombay (dir.) (b. 30th June, 1888).—First commn., 17th March, 1919; in milty. employ prior to 11th Dec., 1929; asst. dir. of public health, Jan., 1930; dir., 1933.

DABOO, ERACH RUTTANJI, M.C., M.B., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S. (b. 9th May, 1888).—Civil surgeon, Assam, Feb., 1933; on mil. duty, June, 1941.

DADABHOY, SIR MANECKJI BYRAMJI, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E.—Apptd. pres., council of state, 21st Dec., 1932; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1936.

DADACHANJI, RATANJI SORABJI, B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 27th Nov., 1875).—Retd., Apl., 1935.*

DAFTARY, GAVRISHANKAR DULLABJI, B.E. (Civil), Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bombay) (b. 23rd Jan., 1889).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., March, 1913; asst. engr., March, 1914; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., March, 1923; suptg., ditto; confd., Feb., 1940.

DAGLISH, JULIAN, Burma Police (Class I.) (b. 19th Sept., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. Burma, on probn., Dec., 1937; transf. to milty. dept., Nov., 1940.

DAIN, SIR JOHN RUTHERFORD, C.I.E., B.A., late I.C.S. (Orissa) (b. 13th Jan., 1883).—Educ. at Tonbridge and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and transf. to Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1912; asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1914; jt. mag. and ditto, Nov., 1915; mag. and collr., Oct., 1922; sec. to govt. of Bihar and Orissa, rev. dept., Oct., 1923; dep. commr., Oct., 1926; C.I.E., March, 1929; offg. commr., Apl., 1929; confd., May, 1932; offg. member, bd. of rev., Bihar and Orissa, Aug., 1933; rev. commr., Orissa, Dec., 1936; knighted, June, 1938; member, fed. public service commn., May, 1939; ret. d., Oct., 1942.

DAINTITH, TERENCE JOSEPH, M.C., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 23rd Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1907; supt., July, 1908; offg. dep. p.m.g., Madras, Dec., 1933; dep. p.m.g., United Provs., March, 1934; p.m.g., August, 1938; senior dep. dir. gen., Apl., 1939; ret. d., May, 1940.

DALAL, Lalitchandra Ramanlal, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 17th Feb., 1918).—Educ. at Bombay Univ.; apptd. on probn.,

after examn. of 1942 and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

DALAL, SIR ARDESHIR RUSTAMJI, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 24th Apl., 1884).—Retd., July, 1931; planning and development member of Viceroy's exec. cl. since Aug., 1944.

DALAL, BHICAJI PHIROZESHAH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 1st Dec., 1913).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Coll., Coonoor, Bombay Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 17th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

DALAL, SIR DADIBA MERWANJEE, Kt., C.I.E.—Member of comtee. on Indian exchange and currency, 1919; chairman, govt. securities rehabilitation comtee., Bombay, 1921; apptd. member of council of sec. of state for India, 19th Nov., 1921; delegate for India at Genoa Economic Conference, and representative for India at The Indian retrenchment comtee., 1922; high commr. for India, 19th Apl., 1923, to 26th Sept., 1924; delegate for India at Impl. Economic Conference, 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; Kt., Jan., 1924.

DALIP SINGH, KANWAR, SIR, B.A., late Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 2nd June, 1885).—Apptd. asst. legal remembrancer, Nov., 1922; govt. advocate, Nov., 1924; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Oct., 1925; judge, ditto, Feb., 1930; Kt., Jan., 1943; retd., Oct., 1943.

DALIP SINGH, SIR M.A., Income-tax Dept., Punjab (asst. commr.) (b. 29th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, Aug., 1920; held non-gaz. appts., Aug., 1921-1921; collr. of Income-tax, Sept., 1921; on special duty, Punjab Secretariat, May, 1930; Income-tax officr., June, 1930; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, June, 1936; Kt., Jan., 1943.

DALJIT SINGH, RAJA, SIR, K.B.E., C.S.I.—Member of the legislative council, Punjab, 1913-1915; member govt.-gen.'s legislative council, 1913-1915; C.S.I., June, 1914; member of council of Sec. of State for India, March, 1915, to June, 1917; K.B.E., June, 1919; chief minister, Kashmir, to 1921.

D'ALMEIDA, WILLIAM, M.B.E. (b. 21st Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1909; supt., home dept., June, 1922; supt., Indian staty. commn., March, 1928; offg. asst. secty., home dept., Govt. of India, June, 1930; confd., March, 1933 offg. asst. secty., home dept., Gov. of India, June, 1930; confd., March, 1933; offg. under-secty., home dept., Govt. of India, Sept., 1934; asst. secty., ditto, Sept., 1934; retd., Aug., 1941.

DALY, CLIVE KIRKPATRICK, C.S.I., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Political Service (b. 3rd Apl., 1888).—First commn., 18th Dec., 1907; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; pol. agent, Bahrain, Nov., 1925; H.M. Consul, Seistan and Kain, March, 1929; offg. consul-genl., Khora-m, June, 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar agency, June, 1938; dep. resdt., Rajkot, March to June, 1939; dep. resdt., W.I. States Agency, March, 1939; resdt. for Baroda and the Gujarat States, July, 1939; confd. resdt., Cl. II, Dec., 1941; C.S.I., Jan., 1943; retd., 3 Apl., 1943.

DALYELL OF THE BINNS, Lieut.-Col. G., C.I.E., name changed from LOCH, P. G. (q.v.).

DALZIEL, WALTER WATSON, C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon.); Barr. Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Orissa) (b. 5th Oct., 1900).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and Corpus Christi, Oxford, joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived 7th Dec.,

1924, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settlement officr., Nov., 1927; offg. judl. commr., Apl., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1932; confd., Apl., 1934; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, legisl. dept., Apl. to Oct., 1935; sec. to govt., Orissa, law, comm. and lab. depts., Jan., 1940; again, Nov., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; sec. judl. dept., Feb., 1944.

DAM, SARAT CHANDRA, B.Sc., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 13th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service, 19th Apl., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Apl., 1923; offg. exec. engr., June, 1929; confd., Apl., 1932; p.a. to ch. engr., June, 1939.

DAMLE, KASHINATH RAGHUNATH, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 3rd March, 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University, King's College, London, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1940.

DANDEKER, NARAYAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., inc-tax) (b. 24th June, 1908).—Educ. at School of Oriental Studies, Lond.; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., and jt. mag. Apl., 1933; asst. collr. of customs, Madras, Jan., 1937; apptd. officer on special duty, income-tax department, Bengal, Nov., 1939; asst. commr. of income-tax, March, 1940; commr., May, 1941.

DANE, SIR LOUIS WILLIAM G.C.I.E., C.S.I., F.R.G.S., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Retd., May, 1913.*

DANIELS, MARK, O.B.E., B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 22nd Feb., 1892).—Educ. at Central Foundation School, London, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge (wrangler; scholar, 1910-14); apptd. temp. junr. clerk, finl. dept., 16th March, 1915; resdt. clerk, (temp.), Sept., 1915, to Dec. 1916; junr. clerk, store dept., 1st Nov., 1915; on mil. service, from Dec., 1916 to Feb., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920, as asst. controller, store dept.; senr. clerk, Dec., 1921; contr., Apl., 1922; O.B.E., June, 1934; officer for defence planning and co-ordn., Sept., 1939; actg. dir. of purchase, July, 1941.

DANTYAGI, VISHWESWAR DAYAL, M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service, 13th July, 1922; dep. acct. genl., central revenues, Apl., 1936; dep. acct. genl., P. & T., Delhi, Apl., 1937; asst. dir. of audit, defence services, W.C. and Lahore Dist., Oct., 1938; depy. dir. of audit, def. services, Nov., 1940.

DAR, JEEWAN NATH, RAI SAHIB, B.A., late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 31st Jan., 1884).—Retd. Jan., 1939.*

DAR, SHIAM KISHAN (judge, High Court, Allahabad), appt. actg. judge, Allahabad high ct., Nov., 1940; addl. judge, ditto, July, 1941; judge, Dec., 1941.

DAR, SHYAM SUNDAR LAL, M.A. (Cantab.), Barr., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Dec., 1895).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1920; arrived, 16th March, 1921, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and jt. mag.; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1925; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. excise commr., July, 1931; inspr.-genl., regstrn., United

Prov., Apl., 1932; mag. and collr., Nov., 1933.

DARBY, GEORGE SCOTT, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 16th April, 1885).—Retd., March, 1939.*

DARGAN, PATRICK ARTHUR, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgeon, Punjab) (b. 1st Aug., 1888).—First commn., 1st Nov., 1920; civil surgeon, Punjab, Apl., 1931.

DARLEY, SIR BERNARD D'OLIER, Kt., C.I.E. A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 24th Aug., 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1903, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; supt. engr., March, 1921; offg. chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., March, 1924; confd., Oct., 1925; Kt., Dec., 1928; on spl. duty in P.W.D., Nov., 1931; ret'd., March, 1934; apptd. commr. for Superior Services (India) Family Pensions Fund, Oct., 1937.

DARLING, GEORGE KENNETH, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 10th May, 1879).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

DARLING, HENRY JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 24th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. elec. engr., 13th Oct., 1926; dist. elec. engr., E.I. Rly., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. loco. supt., E.B. Rly., Aug., 1928; confd., Feb., 1930; ch. electrical engr., N.W. Rly., Nov., 1931; offg. ditto, E.I. Rly., Oct., 1935; confd., Nov., 1936; ret'd., Jan., 1945.

DARLING, SIR MALCOLM LYALL, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1880).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; tutor and guardian to H.H. the Raja of Devas, Jan., 1907; under sec. to govt. of Punjab, Feb., 1911, to July, 1913; dep. commr., and jt. registr., co-operative socs., Punjab, Jan., 1920; commr. of income tax, Oct., 1921; regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1927; commr., July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. finl. commr. (dev.) and secty. to govt., dev. dept., Jan., 1936; finl. commr., Punjab, Dec., 1936; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1939; ret'd., May, 1940.

DARRINGTON, VICTOR GEORGE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 25th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 12th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1924; senr. instructor, Madras Forest Coll., Dec., 1925; pers. asst. to ch. consvr., March, 1931 to March, 1934, and again, Jan., 1940.

DARSHAN SINGH, SARDAR, F.R.H.S., Barr. Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric. on 23rd Dec., 1913.

DART, REV. JOHN LOVERING CAMPBELL, M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Eccles. Estab. (Bombay) (b. 30th May, 1882).—Retd., March, 1936.*

DART, REV. THOMAS HENRY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 14th May, 1908).—apptd. chaplain on probn. Calcutta Diocese, 12th Aug. 1942

DARVELL, HAROLD F., late Marine deptt., Bengal (b. 8th Apl., 1881).—Retd., Apl., 1936.*

DARWIN, JOHN HENRY, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Retd. Nov., 1937.*

DAS, AMARENDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, O.B.E., M.A., late Bihar C.S. (b. 16th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1905; dep. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1907; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1928; confd. July, 1935; offg. secty., rev. bd., June, 1933; secty. bd. of rev.,

Dec., 1935; O.B.E., Jan., 1939; ret'd., July, 1940.

DAS, AMRITA LAL, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 1st Feb., 1906).—Joined the service as student, 9th May, 1928; asst. exec. engr., May, 1929; offg. exec. engr., May, 1933; confd., May, 1938.

DAS, ARJAN (see Arjan, Das).

DAS, CEDRIC PERCY MAYA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dir. of agric., United Provs.) (b. 8th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric. on 1st Sept., 1917; prof. agric. coll., Cawnpore, Nov., 1921; principl. ditto, Dec., 1925; dep. dir. of agric., United Provs., Nov., 1934; confd. in sel. grade, Apl., 1936; dir. of agric., Sept., 1941.

DAS, DEBES CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., home deptt., govt. of India) (b. 1st July, 1911).—Educ. at Mitra Institution, Calcutta, University of Calcutta and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the Service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; under-secty., govt. of Assam (home and pol. depts.), Oct., 1938; ditto, home deptt., govt. of India, June, 1940; dep. sec., ditto, Apl., 1943.

DAS, DHARM (see Dharm Das).

DAS, GURCHARN (see Gurcharn Das).

DAS, MATHRA (see Mathra Das).

DAS, NABAGOPAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 20th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Calcutta University, the School of Economics, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934; dy. agric. marketing adviser, July, 1940; sec., post-war reconstruct. ctte., May, 1944.

DAS, NIRANJAN (see Niranjan Das).

DAS, PRIA (see Prie Das).

DAS, SUDHANSHU KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S., Orissa (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 3rd Sept., 1898).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London Univ.; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 21st Dec., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1929; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; registrar, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1939; sec., legis. deptt., May, 1938; sec. bd. of conciliation, Nov., 1940; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1941; sec., judl. deptt., March, 1941 to March, 1944; acting high ct. judge, Patna, Nov., 1944.

DAS, SUNDER, K. (see Sunder Das, K.).

DAS, SUDHI RANJAN, apptd. judge high ct., Calcutta, Aug., 1944.

DAS GUPTA, KULADA CHARAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 3rd Jan., 1900).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and sub-divl. officer; jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1929, and again, Apl., 1933; addl. ditto, Oct., 1932; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1934; regr., high ct., March, 1942.

DAS GUPTA, PRITHWISH CHANDRA, M.A., A.C.A., Indian Audit Deptt. (b. 3rd Aug., 1897).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1925; offg. ch. auditor, Burma Rlys., May, 1935; exr., outside audit deptt., Bombay, Oct., 1935; again, Apl., 1937; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, March, 1937; examiner outside audit deptt. Bombay, April,

1937; dep. govt. examiner of accts., B.B. & C.I. Rly., Oct., 1938; ch. audtr., rly. clg. accts., Delhi, Nov., 1939; cost accounts offr.; offr. of finl. advr. (supply), Sept., 1940.

DAS GUPTA, SURENDRA NATH, C.I.E., M.A., Ph.D. (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. Oct., 1887).—Apptd. profrr., Presdy., Coll., Calcutta, 22nd March, 1924; prinpl. Sanskrit coll., Apl., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; retd., Oct., 1942.

DASH, ARTHUR JULES, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 24th Apl., 1887).—Educ. at Worcester Cathedral King's Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Bengal, Apl., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1916; I.A.R.O., from Aug., 1915, to Dec., 1918; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1921; dist. judge (temp.), Jan., 1923; mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; secty. to govt., dept. of educn., Dec., 1928; offg. commr., Apl., 1932; confd., March, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; retd., Sept., 1942.

DASS, AMARJIT KUMAR, Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 22nd Nov., 1916); apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Feb., 1940.

DASS, KANSHAL KUMAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 25th May, 1915).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

DASTUR, SIR HORMAZDIAR PRIROZ, Kt., B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay, Judicial Dept. (b. 20th March, 1877).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

DASTUR, JEHANGIR FARDUNJI, M.Sc., D.I.C. late Indian Agric. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 12th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, 14th May, 1910; mycologist, Dec., 1923; retd., Sept., 1941.

DATT, DEV (see Dev Datt).

DATTA (see also Dutt).

DATTA, ABINASH CHANDRA, late Income-tax dept., Assam (b. 30th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1915; income tax officer, Jan., 1927; asst. commr., June, 1934; retd., May, 1943.

DATTA, DINES CHANDRA, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 1st July, 1904).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 15th May, 1928; confd., July, 1930; asst. to pol. officer, Sadiya frontier tract, Apl., 1932; offg. supt., Jan., 1936.

DATTA, GURU SADAY (see Dutt, G.S.).

DATTA, NARENDRA NATH, Babu, B.A., Bihar C.S. (dep. mag. and dep. collr.) (b. 16th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service, 6th Aug., 1904; dep. collr., Oct., 1906; offg. dep. commr., May, 1933.

DATTA, PARES CHANDRA, M.B., Ch.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Railways (ch. medl. offr.) (b. 31st July, 1892).—Apptd. dist. medl. officer on probn., 1st May, 1925; confd., May, 1928; ch. med. offr., Apl., 1942.

DATTA, PROMODE CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, C.I.E.—Apptd. member, govt.'s exec. council, Assam., 31st March, 1934; vacated office, March 1937; advocate-general, Assam, Aug., 1939 to Oct., 1943.

DATTA, RABINDRA NATH, B.A. (Oxon.), M.Sc. (All.), Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Central Provs., 14th Oct., 1927; dep. consvr., Nov., 1931.

DATTA, SISIR KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S.

(asst. commr., Assam) (b. 27th Nov., 1911).—Educ. at Govt. High School Silchar, Assam; Calcutta Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service, 14th Jan., 1937; arrived in India, 19th Feb., 1937, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

DATTA, SUDHINDRA MOHAN, B.A., Indian Police (b. 18th March, 1910).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., 15th Feb., 1934; confd., Feb., 1936.

DATTATRAYA VAMAN REGE (see Rege, D. V.).

DAUBENY, RONALD GILES, O.B.E., Indian Police, Indian Political Service (b. 19th July, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 21st Nov., 1926; offg. A.D.C. to Govr., May, 1929; pte. secy. to ditto, Apl., 1932; A.D.C. to the Viceroy, Oct., 1933; under secty. to A.G.G., Eastern States, Apl., 1936; asst. mag. and collr., Moradabad, Nov., 1936; under-secty. to resdt. for Eastern States, Nov., 1937; asst. pol. agent, Orissa States, Nov., 1938; confd. in the Indian Political Service, Feb., 1939; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Dec., 1939; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Khorramshahr, Dec., 1940; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1941; sec. Brit. legation, Kabul, Feb., 1942; sec. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, June, 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

DAUKES, SIR CLENDON TUBERVILLE, Kt., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd. (b. 24th Dec., 1879).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

DAVE, ANANDBAI KARUNASHANKER, B.Sc., Indian Police, Central Provinces (b. 13th Jan., 1921).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn. after exam. of 1941.

DAVE, KARUNASHANKAR NANDLAL, B.A., LL.B., late Income Tax Dept., Central Provs. (b. Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1914; income tax officer, May, 1925; offg. asst. commr., Jan., 1926; confd., Feb., 1932; commr. of income tax, Assam., Feb., 1941; retd., 16th Sept., 1940.

DAVID, ALAN MICHAEL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 18th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 11th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924; transf. to def. dept., Sept., 1944.

DAVIDSON, DAVID ALEXANDER GIBB, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 15th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 19th May, 1922; asst. consvr., 1st Nov., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

DAVIES, ANDREW EVAN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bombay) (b. 24th March, 1914).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Bombay, Nov., 1933; confd., Apl., 1936; asst. comdt. Sind police rifles, Oct.-Dec., 1944.

DAVIES, ARTHUR CECIL, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 28th May, 1889).—Retd., July, 1939.*

DAVIES, HAROLD ESCOTT, B.A., Burma Office (asst. princpl.) (b. 5th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at Aldenham School and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., 11th Oct., 1937; resident clerk, May, 1939; on milty. service from June, 1940.

DAVIES, HENRY MERSON, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.).—Retd. Jan., 1939.*

DAVIES, JOHN HUGH RATHBONE, Indian Police (b. 16th June, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt. United Provs., on probn., Dec., 1937.

DAVIES, LAWRENCE STANLEY RICHARD GILBERT, late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 19th June, 1894).—Retd. June, 1939.*

DAVIES, P. W. WILTON (see Wilton Davies).

DAVIES, ROBERT WILLIAM HOME, B.A., Indian C.S. (judl. commr. for W.I. and for Ajmer-Merwara) (b. 10th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Jan., 1922; arrived, 18th March, 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr. and supt. of stamps, May, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1925; asst. mag. and collr., Apl., 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct. 1932; confd., Oct., 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Oct., 1934; ditto, Kathiawar, Sept., 1936; offg. judl. commr. for W.I., and for Ajmer-Merwara, June, 1938, to Oct., 1938; confd. May, 1939.

DAVIES, ROLAND GWYNNE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 11th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at the Grammar School, Bradford, and Queen's College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

DAVIES, VINCENT ELLIS, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Orissa) (b. 3rd June, 1903).—Educ. at St. John's School, Leatherhead, and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1926; arrived 20th Dec., 1926, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1933; sec. to govr. of Orissa, Jan., 1941; again July, 1942, confd. as mag. and collr., Apl., 1941.

DAVIES, WILLIAM FREDERICK MANT, Indian Police (b. 11th July, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Prov., 16th Nov., 1927; a.d.c. to govr., Mar., 1935, to Dec., 1936; served with cent. govt. civ. def. dept., Mar., 1942.

DAVIES-LEIGH (see under Leigh).

DAVIS, DAVID, Indian Forest Service (consvr. United Provs.) (b. 11th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1925; instructor, forest coll., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1930 to May, 1933; consvr. (confd.), Apl., 1940.

DAVIS, SIR GODFREY, Indian C.S. (chief judge, Sind) (b. 23rd Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., asst. rembr. of legal affairs, Jan., 1920; asst. judge and addnl. sess. judge, Dec., 1922; secy. to cttee. on Bombay Stock Exchange, Sept., 1923 to Jan., 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1924; ditto, and agent for sardars in the Deccan, Aug., 1925; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; secy. to govt., legal dept., Bombay, and rembr., legal affairs, Jan., 1932; member, legisl. council, Feb., 1932, judl. commr., Sind., Dec., 1935; chief judge, chief court of Sind, Apl., 1940; Kt., Jan., 1941.

DAVIS, HAROLD PERCY WHITTON, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Bombay) (b. 7th Dec., 1893).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1923; instr. forest research coll., June, 1926 to Apl., 1929; offg. consvr., June, 1935; served in Sind as extra consvr., Sept., 1934; consvr. from Nov., 1944.

DAVIS, PERCIVAL WILLIAM, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 19th July, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; offg. princl., Madras forest coll., June, 1933.

DAVIS, PERCY, M.B.E., Stationery and Printing Dept., Punjab (b. 22nd Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 3rd July, 1924, as works

manager, central jail press, on probn.; confd. July, 1925; supt., Govt. printing, May, 1935; M.B.E., Jan., 1935.

DAVIS, REGINALD TREBY, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Veterinary Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 10th June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1936.*

DAVIS, WILLIAM JOHN, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1907, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; on mil. duty from Aug., 1916 to Jan., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1933; confd., Feb., 1936; retd., Jan., 1940.

DAVISON, RICHARD GEORGE CHARLES, LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 5th June, 1916).—Educ. at Battersea Grammar School, King's College, London, and Trinity College, Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DAVY, ANTHONY EDWARD GAINS, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 10th Dec., 1908).—First commn., 30th Aug., 1928; pers. asst. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Oct., 1935; under sec. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Feb., 1936; under trng. in Punjab, Aug., 1936; city magistrate, Peshawar, March, 1938; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, Apr., 1939; confd. in Indian Political Service, May, 1939; asst. pol. agent, Orissa States, Dec., 1939; under sec. to resdt., Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1941; under-sec., ext. affairs dept., Nov., 1943; on depn. to Assam, Oct., 1944.

DAVY, HORACE FREDERICK, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Stores dept. (contr. of inspn.) (b. 19th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. laby. officer, 2nd Dec., 1913; dep. supt. of local manufactures, Dec., 1918; contr. of inspn. (temp.), May, 1923; confd., Jan., 1925; offg. dir. of inspn., June, 1928; dep. chief controller of purchase (munitions), dept. of Supply, June, 1940.

DAWKINS, CLINTON GEORGE EVELYN, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 16th June, 1882).—Retd. Nov., 1936.*

DAWSON, JAMES ALEXANDER, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 4th May, 1880).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

DAWSON, LIONEL EDWARD, B.A., M.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1910; in mil. employ. from 14th Feb., 1918 to 7th Feb., 1920; exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1918; pers. asst. to ch. engr. and under-sec. to govt., P.W. dept. (I.B.), Apl., 1927; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1931; confd., July, 1936; retd., 15th Jan., 1942.

DAWSON, SIR VERNON, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. and India Office (b. 22nd Feb., 1881).—Resigned from Indian C.S., Jan., 1921*; senr. clerk, India Office, March, 1921; principl. Apl., 1921; asst. secty., public and judl. dept., Apl., 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930; Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1934; retd., March, 1941; re-employed as temp. asst. secty., Apl., to Sept., 1941.

DAWSON, WILLIAM MAURICE GRANT, O.B.E., M.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., United Provs.) (b. 11th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; profr. civil engrg. Thomason Coll., Roorkee, Oct., 1935 to Oct., 1936; confd.

as suptg. engr., Jan., 1942; ch. engr., Oct., 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

DAY, BINGLEY WALDEMAR, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Stowe and Corpus Christi coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Nov., 1932; under-secty. public dept., Oct., 1937; tempy. dep. ditto, July, 1940; tempy. sec. educ. dept., Jan. to Feb., 1941.

DAY, REV. EDWARD RIDLEY, M.A., late Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 21st Aug., 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 16th Aug., 1920; junr. chaplain, Aug., 1922; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1932; retd. Aug., 1937.

DAY, FRANCIS EDWARD, India Audit Office (exec. offr.) (b. 22nd July, 1915).—Apptd. exec. officer, 23rd Jan., 1936; actg. hr. ditto, March, 1943; serving with H.M. forces.

DAY, JOHN THOMAS, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 18th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 27th May, 1910; asst. traffic supt., March, 1919; dist. ditto, Jan., 1922; asst. traffic manager, G.I.P. rly., Aug., 1923; goods supt., G.I.P. rly., May, 1926; divl. transport supt., Dec., 1933; retd., Dec., 1941.

DAYA KRISHNA, KHANNA, RAI SAHIB LALA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 19th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service, 24th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr., May, 1940.

DAYAL, BISHAMBHAR, (see Bishambhar Dayal).

DAYAL, DEVI (see Devi Dayal).

DAYAL, HARISHWAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 15th July, 1915).—Educ. at Govt. Intermediate Coll., Allahabad, and Allahabad Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1940; under-sec. to resdt. for W. India States, Apr., 1942; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar, Sept., 1942; under-sec. to resdt., Rajkot civ. station, Jan., 1943; under-sec. ext. affairs dept., govt. of India, March, 1944.

DAYAL, ISHWAR (see Ishwar, D.).

DAYAL, MADHU SUDAN (see Madhu Sudan, D.).

DAYAL, RAGHUBAR, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 26th Oct., 1900).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1925; arrived, 17th Nov., 1925, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag.; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Jan., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936.

DAYAL, RAJESHWAR (see Rajeshwar Dayal).

D'CUHNA, MARCEL DOMINIC, O.B.E., B.A., late Imp. Customs Service (b. 2nd June, 1881).—Retd., June, 1936.*

DE, GOSHTO BIHARI, B.A., B.L., late Central Provs. C.S. (Judl.) (b. March, 1881).—Retd., March, 1936.*

DE, INDRA NATH, Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 20th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 24th Apr., 1925; offg. supt., March, 1933; confd., Jan., 1938.

DE, INDU BHUSAN, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 18th Jan., 1902).

Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, Dec., 1926; exec. engr., Dec., 1935.

DE, JIBAN KRISHNA, B.Sc., B.E. (Cal.),

A.M.I.E., Indian P. and T. (dir. of tels.) (b. 31st Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. div. engr., 19th June, 1922; div. engr., Aug., 1930; dir. of tels., Dec., 1939.

DE, JNANANKUR, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 21st Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and University Coll., London; apptd. to I.C.S. by nomination; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1917; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Jan., 1922, and sub. divl. officer, May, 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; first land acquis. collr., Calcutta, Feb., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1939.

DE, KALI CHURUN, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (dep. contr. of stores, E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th Apr., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 8th Jan., 1920; asst. dir. (technical) rly. bd., Apr., 1924; dist. contr. of stores (tempy.), July, 1927; offg. dep. contr. of stores, variously from March, 1929; confd., July, 1940; contr. of stores, provl., July, 1940.

DE, MAKHANLAL, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 4th Dec., 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

DE, RAJENDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. July, 1891).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1914; proby. extra asst. consvr., July, 1916; confd., July, 1917; apptd. to I.F.S. as dep. consvr., 3rd Nov., 1928. junr. consvr., Mar., 1943.

DEAN, ARTHUR WILLIAM HENRY, C.I.E., B.Sc., M.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., central P.W.D.) (b. 7th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 3rd Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Apr., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; transfed. to Indore, Jan., 1933; tempy. suptg. engr., aviation circle, Feb., 1935; offg. suptg. engr., construction circle, Apr., 1936; confd., Dec., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1941.

DEAN, JOHN MARTIN, Indian Police (b. 9th July, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936.

DEANE, DONALD VICTOR, O.B.E., Major R.E. (b. 19th Oct., 1902).—(mint master Calcutta); mil. employ up to 21st Dec., 1932; dep. mint master on probn., 30th Dec., 1932; confd., Oct., 1934; offg. mint master variously from Feb., 1934; mint master, confd., Aug., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

DEANE, LANCELOT VICTOR, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 14th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 22nd Nov., 1925; asst. commr., Port Blair, Jan., 1930; asst. to D.I.G., June, 1939; confd. as supt., March, 1942.

DEANE, MAHOMED KHAIRUB, B.Sc., Factory Dept., Bombay (inspr.) (b. 16th Apr., 1891).—Apptd. inspr. of factories, Bombay, 20th Nov., 1923; offg. ch. inspr., Feb., 1931.

DEANS, HARRY SCOTT, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 29th March, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 15th Dec., 1922; on deputation to the Kashmir state from 4th March, 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

DEB, BIRENDRANATH, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. to Indian trade publicity officer) (b. 13th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. to Office of H.C. for I., Apr., 1921; clerical officer, Feb., 1924; exec. officer, Feb., 1927; asst. to Indian trade publicity officer, Nov., 1929.

DEBENDRA NATH SEN GUPTA (see Sen Gupta, D. N.).

DEBI DATTA MAI, RAI BAHADUR, B.A.,

late Indian Service of Engs. (United Provs.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

DEBI LAL SAH, B.A., M.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 7th March, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. working plan officer on 26th Dec., 1925; asst. consvr. July, 1926; dep. consvr., Feb., 1933.

DE CHAZEL, JOHN ROBERT, Indian Police (b. 19th Feb., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 26th Nov., 1936; confd., Jan., 1939; on depn., central govt., Oct., 1943.

DE GALE, HUGH OTWAY, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Punjab) (b. 9th Jan., 1891).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1911; asst. supt., May, 1913; on mil. duty from 15th Nov., 1917 to 28th Feb., 1919; King's police medal, Jan., 1921; supt., Jan., 1924; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1925; princpl. police training school, Phillour, Apl., 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1933; ditto, (provl. subs.), June, 1935; offg. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1938.

DEHEJIA, VENILAL TRIBHOVANDAS, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (under-secty., dept. of e. h. and l.) (b. 23rd July, 1908).—Educ. at Bombay University, Rl. College of Science, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and supt., salt and excise; dep. secty. rev. dept., Sind, Sept., 1939; attaché, e. h. and l. dept., govt. of India, March, 1940; under-secty., e. h. and l. dept., govt. of India, June, 1940.

DEHLAVI, SAMIULLA KHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Sept., 1913).—Educ. at Anglo-Urdu High Scho., Poonah, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 1st Oct., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1940.

DE KRETSEK, CLAUDE BERTRAM, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 23rd Oct., 1892).—Educ. at University Coll., London, and Wadhams Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, 17th Jan., 1918, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, offg. asst. collr. of customs, Jan., 1920, to July, 1921; services re-placed at disposal of govt. of Burma, July, 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1923; confd., Sept., 1927.

DE LA FOSSÉ, SIR CLAUDE FRASER, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Educl. Dept., United Provs.—Retd., 1923.*

DELAHAY, VEN. WILLIAM, late Indian Eccles. Estab., (Burma) (b. 25th Jan., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

DE LA HEY, MISS DOROTHY CISLEY, late Indian Educl. Service (Madras) (b. 6th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1936.*

DE LA NOUGERÉDE, FREDERICK ADAIR, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 15th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 16th July, 1915; asst. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., July, 1918; offg. dist. contr. of stores variously from Jan., 1919; confd., March, 1931; offg. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., Feb., 1935.

DELL, PERCY BERTRAM, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 15th Nov., 1888).—Second class clerk, bd. of trade, Sept., 1908 to Sept., 1909; ditto, bd. of edn., Sept., 1909 to June, 1911; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 9th June, 1911, and attd. to registry and record dept.; transfld. to store dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from

Aug., 1914 to July, 1919; transfld. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1926; higher exec. offcr., May, 1938; M.B.E., June, 1938; temp. prin. exec. offcr., Apl., 1941.

DE MONTMORENCY, SIR GEOFFREY FITZHERBEY, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., K.C.V.O., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 23rd Aug., 1876).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

DENCH, WILLIAM GEORGE, C.I.E., B.A., B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs., engr. (Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; under-secty. to govt., irrigation branch, Punjab, Apl., 1931; under-secty. (North), Punjab irrigation sectt., Jan., 1933; offg. supt. engr., May, 1933, and again, Feb., 1936; sec. p.w.d. irrig., June, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1944 retd., 1st Dec., 1943.

DENNEHY, HAROLD GEORGE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief secretary to the govt. of Assam) (b. 18th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Clifton and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 6th Dec., 1914, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1915 to March, 1919; under-sec. to govt. of Assam, Apl., 1923; offg. chief sec., ditto, Apl., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1925; confd., June, 1933; offg. secty. to govt. in transferred depts., Assam, June, 1929; confd., June, 1933; offg. ch. secty., Assam, Sept., 1936; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; sect. educ. and local self govt. depts., Feb., 1938; chief secty. to govt., Assam, Apl., 1939; confd., Oct., 1940; C.S.I., Jan., 1942.

DENNIS, REGINALD BURTON, B.Sc. (Engl.), A.C.G.I., M.I.E.E., F.R.A.I., F.R.G.S.—Indian p. and t. (dir. of tels.) (b. 25th Apl., 1900).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1923, as asst. divi. engr.; divi. engr., Jan., 1931; dy. ch. engr., Nov. 1940; dir. of tels., May, 1944.

DENT, THOMAS VALENTINE, Indian Forest service (b. 4th Apl., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 11th Oct., 1929.

DENT, EDWARD JOHN MACDONALD, Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 6th June, 1918).—Educ. at Wellington and Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in N.W.F.P. as asst. commr.

DÉO, SIR RAJENDRA NARAYAN BHANJA, Raja Kt., O.B.E.—Member of govt.'s exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1929 to Jan., 1934; vice-presdnt., ditto, Dec., 1931; O.B.E., June, 1918; knighted, June, 1933.

DERBYSHIRE, SIR HAROLD, Kt., K.C., M.C., Bengal Judl. Dept. (ch. justice, high ct., Calcutta).—Apptd. ch. justice, high ct., Calcutta, 12th Nov., 1934.

DE ROSARIO, MISS AGNES, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th Aug., 1881).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

DERRY, ROBERT LAWRIE, Indian Forest Service (b. 17th Dec., 1898) (dep. consvr., Bihar).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 17th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; divi. for. offr., forest research divn., Jan., 1940.

DESAI, CHANDULAL CHUNILAL, B.A., (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 27th Apl., 1900).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Cambridge Univ. (non-collegiate) joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived 26th Nov., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr., offg. dep. commr., June, 1927; and again, Feb., 1931; regr. co-op. society and dir. of indus., March, 1937; dep. commr., Nov.,

1937; sec. local self-govt. medical and public health dept., June, 1939; sec. local self-govt. dept., July, 1941.

DESAI, MANILAL JAGDISHBHAI, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 4th Jan., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dir. of land records, etc., Bombay, Oct., 1935; collr., June, 1937; dep. secty., rev. dept., Bombay, May, 1938, to March, 1939; settlement commr. and dir. land records, Jan., 1940 to March, 1942; sec. to govt. rev. dept., Sept., 1943.

DESAI, MANULAL CHUNILAL, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., United Provs.) (b. 25th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Cambridge (non-collegiate).—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., March, 1928; jt. mag., Nov., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1933; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1934; collr. and mag. (confd.), July, 1939.

DESAI, SHANKAR PANDURANG, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 17th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Bombay and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914 arrived, 7th Dec., 1915 and served in Assam as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt. of Assam, July, 1921, to July, 1923; asst. commr. and settl. officer, Oct., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1929, and again, Dec., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; offg. secty. fin. and rev. depts., June, 1931; and again, March, 1934; offg. secty., transf. depts., Assam, Apl., 1933; offg. dir. of land records, etc., Assam, Jan., 1934; and again, Nov., 1934; secty., fin. dept., Assam, Apl., 1936 to May, 1938; again secty. fin. and rev. depts., March, 1939; secty. educ. and local self-govt. depts., Jan., 1940; dir. of land records, etc.; dir. of surveys and commr. of inc. tax, Assam, Feb., 1941.

DESHMUKH, SIR CHINTAMAN DWARKANATH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (offr. on special duty, fin. dept., govt. of India) (b. 14th Jan., 1896).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1919; arrived, 20th Apl., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1925; confd., July, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; offg. rev. sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Feb., 1932; finl. secty. ditto, June, 1933; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; fin. secty. and secty. p.w.d., Cent. Provs., Nov., 1937; employed under the Cent. govt., Apl., 1939; offr. on special duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, July, 1939; Kt., Jan., 1944.

DE SMIDT, GERALD EWALD OVERBECK, D.S.O., late India Posts and Tels. (Burma) (b. 1st June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. sup., 1st Oct., 1906; on field service with Mesopotamia Expedy. Force from 15th Dec., 1915; dir. of tels. with M.E.F., Apl., 1919; on foreign service with Irak Admn., Nov., 1919; on deputation at India Office from 7th Aug., 1923; ditto, with N.W. Rly., from 23rd March, 1925; ditto, with Indo-European Tel. Dept., from 28th March, 1928; offg. dir., tel. engrg., Aug., 1933; confd., Sept., 1933; dep. dir.-genl., tels., May, 1934; p.m.g., Burma, March, 1936; dir.-genl. posts and tels., Burma, Apl., 1937; retd., June, 1940.

DE SOUZA, F. J., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Railways (govt. inspr. N.W. Rly.) (b. 29th

Sept., 1892).—Asst. exec. engr., 24th Oct., 1916; exec. engr., Oct., 1935; ditto senr. scale, Apl., 1939; confd. as govt. inspr. of rlys., Feb., 1944.

DEV DATT, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (govt. inspt. of rlys., Comm. Dept.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1915; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; ditto, senr. scale, July, 1935; sup., May, 1939; govt. inspr. (senr. scale), Feb., 1944.

DEVADOSS, SIR MUTHIAH DAVID, Kt., Barr., late Madras Judl. Dept.—Retd., Dec., 1928.*

DEVARAJA MUDALIYAR, TIRUPPANANKADU, RAO SAHIB, B.A., late Indian Police (b. 20th June, 1888).—Joined the service, 6th May, 1912; apptd. to the Indian Police as dist. sup., Madras, Jan., 1938; retd.

DEVASAHAYAM, JACOB, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Police (dist. sup.) (b. 9th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. sup., Madras, on 7th June, 1923; offg. dist. sup., June, 1929; RAO Sahib, Jan., 1932; confd., July, 1936.

DEVENISH, REV. ROBERT CECIL SYLVESTER, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 22nd Nov., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 27th Oct., 1919; junior chaplain, Oct., 1921, on mil. duty from 24th Nov., 1921, to 30th Nov., 1922; senr. chaplain March, 1928; offg. archdeacon, Apl., 1933; confd., May, 1934; retd. 28th Feb., 1943.

DEVI DAYAL, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Assam) (b. Oct., 1890).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1914; asst. engr. Feb., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; asst. ch. engr. and under-secty. to govt., p.w.d., Assam, July, 1932 to Apl., 1936.

DEVI DAYAL DHAWAN, RAI BAHADUR LALA, M.Sc., LL.B., late Punjab Civil Service, (b. 15th Oct., 1888).—Extra asst. commr., Jan., 1934; sub judge, Nov., 1920; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; judge, high court, Lahore, 29 Jan., 1943; retd., Oct., 1943.

DEVI DAYAL SAIGAL, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 10th Dec., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 8th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

DEVISINGH, M.A., Lala, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 27th Oct., 1908; asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1923; apptd. to the I.S.E., as asst. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1928; exec. engr., Punjab, Jan., 1933; retd.,

DE VINE, JAMES CHANCELLOR, late Indian Police (b. 9th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. sup., Burma, on 21st Oct., 1921; offg. dist. sup., March, 1927; confd., Aug., 1932; retd., June, 1940.

DEVLIN, REV. THOMAS STAFFORD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 27th May, 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Jan., 1923; junior chaplain, 5th Jan., 1925; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1933.

DEY, BIMAN BEHARI, M.Sc. (Cal.), D.Sc. (Lond.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 1st Nov., 1889).—Joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1916, and served in Bengal to 9th Apl., 1920; addl. prof. of chemistry, presdy. coll., Madras, Jan., 1924; prof. of chem., Feb., 1937; retd., Nov., 1944.

DEY, PROMODE KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (plant pathologist, United Provs.) (b. 25th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as plant pathologist on 1st March, 1920; confd. in sel. grade, May, 1941.

DEY, SUSHIL KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 16th Feb., 1907).—Educ. at Calcutta University, London School

of Economics and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal, as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. a dep. collr., Nov., 1931; controller, A.R.P., Bengal, Apl., 1944.

DHAND, MILKHI RAM, B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th March, 1892).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 1st Oct., 1914; asst. engr., Oct., 1915; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1925; exec. engr., Aug., 1927; retd., Dec., 1940.

DHANPAT RAI, RAI SAHIB, M.B.E., dept. of E. H. and L. (b. 25th July, 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

DHANVADA ANANDA RAO (see Ananda Rao, D.).

DHAR, DEBABRATA, B.A., Indian Police, Assam (b. 1st March, 1921).—Apptd. asst. supt on probn. after exam. of 1942.

DHAR, NIL RATAN (see Nil Ratzan Dhar).

DHAR, SATYENDRA MOHAN, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (secty. educ., dept., Bihar) (b. 25th March, 1897).—Educ. at Mayurbhanj Sch., Orissa, Univ. Coll. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1921; arrived, 18th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1928; offg. dist. mag., and collr., Dec., 1931; dir. of industries, Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1935; mag. and collr., Jan., 1936; secty., educ. dept., var., from Feb., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1942.

DHARM DAS, JAINI LALA, R.B., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 16th March, 1897).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930.

DHAVLE, SANKARA BALAJI, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Oct., 1882).—Educ. at Rajaram High School, Kolapur, Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 28th Dec., 1906, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1912; under-sec. to govt. fin. and munpl. depts., Nov., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1914; dist. and sess. judge; also supt. and rembr. of legal affairs, July, 1919; ditto and sec. to Bihar and Orissa legisl. council, Apl., 1920; offg. judge, high ct., Patna, Apl., 1929, and again, Nov., 1930; addl. judge, ditto, March, 1931, and again Jan., 1932; offg. judge, ditto, March, 1932, and March, 1933; confd., July, 1933; retd., 1942.

DHAWAN, PURUSHOTTAM LAL, RAI BAHADUR, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Oct., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 4th Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; profr., C.E. coll., Roorki, Oct., 1922; asst. dep. agent, Nov., 1923; offg. dep. chief engr., Dec., 1926; confd., Jan., 1934; offg. divl. supt., Oct., 1931; confd., May, 1934; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys, Bombay, July, 1934; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; member, fed. public services commn., May, 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; retd., Jan., 1941.

DHIR, RAM DAS, Ind. Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 29th Dec., 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 3rd Aug., 1932.

DHIRAJLAL, DAYABHAI NANAVATI (see Nanavati, D. D.).

DHURANDHAR, JAGANNATH RAVJI, O.B.E., B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 8th March, 1884).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 23rd Feb., 1918; sub-judge, June, 1919; offg. asst. secty. to govt., Bombay, legal dept., June,

1919; offg. dep. ditto, Apl., 1928; addl. dep. ditto, June, 1932; asst. judge, Oct., 1933; judge and sess. judge, May, 1935; dep. secty. to govt., legal dept., Bombay, May, 1935; secty. ditto, Apl., 1938; O.B.E., May, 1937; retd., March, 1941.

DIAS, ANTHONY LANCELOT, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 13th March, 1910).—Educ. at St. Vincent's High Sch., Poona, Univ. of Bombay, the Sch. of Economics, London, and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; supt. land records, N.C. Ahmedabad, from Nov., 1938 to Feb., 1939, and again May, 1939.

DIBAKAR MAHANTY, RAO BAHADUR, Madras C.S. (sub-collr.) (b. 10th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 19th March, 1917; dep. collr., Jan., 1924; offg. collr., Aug., 1931; sub-collr. (provl. subs.), Aug., 1932; confd., May, 1934; served in Orissa, Sept., 1935; Diwan of Jeypore, Jan., 1936.

DIBDIN, AUBREY, C.I.E., M.A., India Office (asst. secty., econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 4th March, 1892).—Educ. at Tonbridge and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, 26th Jan., 1920; war service—served in India with 1/4th Bn. The Queen's (R.W.S.R.), Oct., 1914 to Nov., 1919; asst. principal and pte. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, Apl., 1921; principal (actg.), Jan., 1924; confd., Apl., 1924; Internatl. labour conf., Geneva, 12th, 15th, 16th, 17th, 18th, 19th and 20th sessions; adviser to Indian Govt. delegation; 21st and 22nd sessions, delegate; jt. secty., R.C., on Indian labour, Apl., 1929 to May, 1931; dep. clerk of the council, Sept., 1934 to July, 1938; asst. secty., reforms dept. (temp.), July, 1938; asst. secty., public and judl. dept., Apl., ditto, econ. and overseas dept. June, 1941; C.I.E., June, 1943.

DIBLE, WILLIAM CUTHBERT, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1886).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; dep. commr., Nov., 1918; mag. and collr., Nov., 1923; offg. commr., Apl., 1934; confd., Feb., 1940; member bd. of rev.; C.I.E., June, 1938.

DI CADILHAC, ARTHUR MARIO AGRICOLA COLLIER GALLETTI, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 25th March, 1877).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

DI CADILHAC, ROBERT GALLETTI, Indian C.S. (jt. sec., bd. of rev., Madras) (b. 27th July, 1908).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and sub-collr., May, 1933; temp. addtl. jt. rev. co-ops., Sept., 1939; jt. sec., bd. of rev., July, 1942.

DICK, ALAN MACDONALD, C.B.E., M.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S. (prof. King Edward medical coll., Lahore) (b. 30th July, 1884).—First commission, 31st July, 1909; O.B.E., Feb., 1921; apptd. principal, medical sch., Amritsar, Sept., 1922; profr. of ophthalmology, King Edward medl. coll., Lahore, Oct., 1923; C.B.E., Jan., 1936.

DICKENS, DOUGLAS GLEN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th July, 1880).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

DICKESON, CHARLES FREDERICK, India Audit Office (higher exec. officer) (b. 23rd Feb.,

1893).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 24th Sept ; 1914, and attached to store dept. ; transf. to India audit office, Jan., 1915 ; exec. officer, Apl., 1922 ; higher clerical officer, Aug., 1926 ; higher exec. officer, Aug., 1930 ; actg. suptg. exec. officer, Jan., 1941.

DICKINSON, E. C., Punjab Educ. Service (prof. of English, govt. coll.) (b. 12th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 31st Dec., 1927 ; apptd. prof. of English, govt. coll., from 31st Dec., 1927.

DICKINSON, FRITZ GEORGE, M.C., B.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 11th June, 1891).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

DIKSHIT, KASHINATH NARAYAN, Rao Bahadur, M.A., Archaeological Survey Dept. (dir. genl.) (b. 21st Oct., 1889).—Joined the service, 29th March, 1917 ; supt., Sept., 1922 ; dep. dir.-genl., Feb., 1935 ; dir.-genl., Sept., 1937.

DIKSHIT, MADHAY JAGANNATH, M.A., B.Sc., late Bombay C.S. (b. 31st Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

DILAWAR HUSAIN KHAN, M.B.E., M.A., Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Orissa) (b. Apl., 1896).—Joined the service as proby. extra-asst. consvr., 5th Apl., 1922 ; confd., Apl., 1923 ; asst. consvr., Jan., 1929 ; for research offr., Orissa, Nov., 1938 ; M.B.E., June, 1943.

DILLON, HENRY VERE SHIRLEY, B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 9th Nov., 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

DILLON, WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 2 June, 1892).—Retd., June, 1937.

DIMOND, WILLIAM ELLIOT RANDAL, C.I.E., Col., I.M.S. (b. 29th Sept., 1893).—First commn., dated 27th Sept., 1919 ; in mil. employ to July, 1925 ; asst. dir. of public health, N.W.F.P. (civil), July, 1925 ; C.I.E., Jan., 1942 ; inspr. gen. civ. hosps. and prisons, Aug., 1944.

DIN MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, Shaikh, M.A., late Punjab C.S., Judl. Bch. (b. March, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1907 ; sub-judge, Oct., 1915 ; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1924 ; confd., June, 1933 ; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, March, 1934 ; and again, July, 1936 ; member, Ind. delimitn. ctce., Aug., 1935 ; judge, high ct., Lahore, March, 1937 ; retd., March, 1940.

DIPPIE, HERBERT, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1920 ; inspr. of schools, Aug., 1923 ; C.I.E., Jan., 1936 ; dir. of public instn., Orissa, Apl., 1936 ; retd., June, 1940.

DIVATIA, SIR HARSIDHBHAI VAJUBHAI, M.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Bombay (judge, high ct.).—Apptd., addl. judge, high ct., Bombay, 19th, June, 1933 ; judge ditto, June, 1934 ; chairman, textile enquiry comitee., Bombay, Oct., 1938 to July, 1939 ; Kt., Jan., 1945.

DIVATIA, SHRINIVAS SATYENDRA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind.) (b. 21st Dec., 1898).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 1st Nov., 1922 ; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1923 ; offg. exec. engr., June, 1927 ; confd., Nov., 1932.

DIVATIA, VIRMITRA BHIMRAV, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th Apl., 1882).—Retd., Apl., 1937.*

DIXON, ALGERNON DREW, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 17th Oct., 1907).—Educated at Bedford School, Corpus Christi College, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London ; apptd. after exam. of 1931 ; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the

United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. ; jt. mag., Feb., 1934 ; exec. offcr., Cawnpore improvement trust, Apl. to Oct., 1937.

DIXON, ARTHUR FREDERIC WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S.
