(collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 11th May, 1892).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge ; joined the service, 18th Aug., 1920 ; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. ; sub-collr. and jt. mag., and educn. dept., Sept., 1929 ; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1933 ; confd., March, 1935 ; officer in ch., reforms (Orissa) dept., March, 1936 ; secty., educ. dept., Orissa, Apl., 1936, and again, Dec., 1936 ; chief secty., Orissa, March to Nov., 1937 ; secty., local admin. dept., Feb., 1940 ; Diwan of Cochin, Oct., 1941 ; C.I.E. June, 1942 ; collr., Nov., 1943 ; member, bd. of rev., Sept., 1944.

DIXON, MICHAEL GREY, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 24th June, 1910).—First commn., 27th Aug., 1931 ; pers. asst. to A.G.G. in Central India, Nov., 1935 ; under trng. in for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1936 ; under-sec. to A.G.G., Gujarat States and resdt. at Baroda, March, 1936 ; under trng. in Punjab, Nov., 1936 ; asst. pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Apl., 1938 ; confd. in Indian political service, Feb., 1939 ; on special duty in Kashmir, Sept., 1939 ; under-secty. to resdt. for Punjab States, Apl., 1940 ; vice-consul, Kabul, Jan., 1944.

DOBSON, BERNARD HENRY, C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Jan., 1881).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge ; apptd. after exam. of 1903 ; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. offr. ; employed in directorate of mil. intell., War Office, from Dec., 1915 to July, 1919 ; dep. commr., Nov., 1919 ; C.B.E., Jan., 1920 ; offg. home sec. to govt., Dec., 1925 ; confd., Dec., 1926 ; offg. commr., Apl., 1929 ; confd., Dec., 1930 ; offg. finl. commr. (dev.), Punjab, Jan., 1935 ; offg. finl. commr. (rev.) and secty. rev. dept., Jan., 1936 ; confd., Oct., 1936 ; C.I.E., Jan., 1938 ; retd., May, 1940.

DODD, PATRICK HUBERT FORSTER, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1916).—Educ. at King's School, Canterbury, and Exeter Coll., Oxford ; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939 ; confd., 29th Aug., 1940 ; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.

DODD, SIR ROBERT JOHN SHERWOOD, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 26th July, 1878).—Retd., July, 1933.*

DODSWOTRH, OWEN ALARIC, I.S.O., late Indian Forest Service (b. 13th Sept., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

DODWELL, DAVID WILLIAM, M.A., Ph.D. (Columbia), Indian C.S. (sec., fin. dept.; Madras) (b. 13th Dec., 1898).—Educ. at Banbury County Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford ; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922 ; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. ; sub. collr., and mag., Jan., 1924 ; tutor to minor Maharaja of Travancore, June, 1925, to Apl., 1928 ; offg. collr. and mag., Sept., 1931 ; addl. dep. secty., fin. dept., Madras (temp.), Nov., 1935 ; and again, Aug., 1936 ; sec. fin. dept., Aug., 1942.

DODWELL, EDWARD ARTHUR, Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 9th Sept., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

DOGRA, HANS RAJ, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909 ; exec. engr., Oct., 1919 ; suptg. engr., Jan., 1937

chief engr., Madras, May, 1938; ret'd., Dec., 1940; re-appt'd. temp. ch. engr., March, 1944.

DOHERTY, REV. ROBERT BRANDAN, B.A., Indian Eccles., Estabt., Bombay (b. 14th July, 1906).—Appt'd. junr. chaplain on probn., 18th Dec., 1939.

DONALD, ARTHUR ALEXANDER, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 1st Apl., 1899).—Joined the service on 5th June, 1920; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1928; confd., Dec., 1931.

DONALD, SIR JAMES, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., LL.M., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 26th Dec., 1873).—Ret'd., Feb., 1929.*

DONALD, JOHN OMMANNEY STEWART, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 10th Oct., 1905).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1925; in milty. employ until 19th Jan., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Sept., 1932; ditto, 3th Waziristan, June, 1933; asst. commr., Manschra, March, 1936; asst. pol. agt. N. Waziristan, Apl., 1937; asst. commr., Hangu, March, 1938; ditto, Tank, Aug., 1940; city mag. and asst. commr., Peshawar, Aug., 1941; pol. agt., Kurram, Sept., 1942; dep. commr., May, 1944.

DONALD, SIR JOHN STUART, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian Pol. Dept.—Ret'd., Apl., 1920.*

DONALDSON, EION PELLY, C.M.G., Burma Office (principal) (b. 15th Apl., 1896).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford (classical scholar); appt'd. asst. principal secretary's office, Inland Revenue, 8th Sept., 1920; transf'd. to India Office, 1st Oct., 1924; pte. secty. to parly. under-secty. of state for India, Dec., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. ditto, Apl., 1930; principal, July, 1930; on depn. for the course of instn. at Impl. Defence Coll., Jan. to Dec., 1936; posted to Burma Office, Apl., 1937; war service—served in France with Machine Gun Corps, March, 1916 to March, 1919; Major, Reserve of Officers, June, 1919; on loan to the War Cabinet as actg. asst. secty., Feb., 1940; C.M.G., Jan., 1943.

DONALDSON, JAMES DUFF, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 9th Jan., 1884).—Held non-gaz. appts. from 5th Dec., 1905; confd. as asst. auditor, E.I. Rly., June, 1921; confd. as accts. officer, gr. I., Nov., 1929; dep. chief accts. officer., June, 1938; ret'd., Apl., 1940.

DONALDSON, JOHN COOTE, C.I.E., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 24th May, 1895).—Educ. at Felsted, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1920; arrived, 5th Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Feb., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1925; confd., July, 1932; offg. dep. secty. to govt., industries and revenue branches, Oct., 1927; dir. of ind., U.P., Oct., 1930; ditto Malerkota State, Sept., 1935; secty. to govt. U.P., Apl., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1939; jt. sec. to the govt.-genl. (public), March, 1940, to Sept., 1942.

DONNELLY, REV. ARTHUR FERGUSON, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 30th Sept., 1914).—Appt'd. chaplain on probn., Calcutta Diocese, 16th May, 1944.

DONNISON, FRANK SIEGFRIED VERNON, C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., judicial dept., Burma) (b. 3rd July, 1898).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 25th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; warden, oilfields, March, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1929; confd., Jan., 1936; secty. to fin. commrs., March, 1937; secty., judicial dept., March, 1939; C.B.E., Jan., 1943.

DOOG, JOHN MYLES GREGORY, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1884).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 5th Feb., 1910; asst. engr. (tempy.), Aug., 1910; ditto (perm.), Aug., 1911; on milty. duty from 18th July, 1917 to 3rd Apl., 1919; dist. engr., Jan., 1927; dep. ch. engr., July, 1937.

DORAB, N. D. KHANDALAVALA (see Khandalavala, D. N. D.).

DORAN, ALFRED JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd March, 1908; asst. supt., Jan., 1922; transpn. supt. (traffic comml.) E.I. Rly, May, 1934.

DORMAN-SMITH, SIR REGINALD HUGH, P.C., G.B.E.—Appt'd. Govt. of Burma, 6th May, 1941.

DOSSETOR, REV. FRANCIS ERNEST, M.A., late Indian Eccles Estabt. (b. 16th Dec., 1885).—Ret'd., Feb., 1939.*

DOUGLAS, REV. PERCY SHOLTO DOUGLAS, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 13th March, 1887).—Appt'd. chaplain on probn., 13th Feb., 1921; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1923; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1940.

DOUGLAS, WILLIAM CHALMERS, M.A. (Edin.), B.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 27th July, 1884).—Ret'd., Jan., 1935.*

DOW, SIR HUGH, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (govr. of Sind) (b. 8th May, 1888).—Educ. at Aske's Sch., Hatcham, S.E., and University Coll., London; appt'd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1910, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; munpl. commr., Surat, from Oct., 1916; asst. commr. in Sind for civil supplies and recruiting, July, 1918; dep. contr. of prices, Sind, Sept., 1919, to Apl., 1920; dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1921; in addn., passport officer from Jan. to July, 1923; actg. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1923; dep. sec., ditto, Nov., 1923 (tempy.); fin. advr. to P.W.D. in Sind (tempy.), Dec., 1925; offg. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1926; rev. offr., Lloyd barrage project (tempy.), Jan., 1927; C.I.E., June 1932; jt. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Oct., 1934; addl. secty., ditto (tempy.), June, 1936; offg. secty. ditto, Oct., 1936; confd., March, 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; dir. gen. of supply, govt. of India, Aug., 1939; vice-pres., war supply board, July, 1940; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1940; appt'd. govr. of Sind, 1 Apl., 1941.

DOW, THOMAS MILLER, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Kilmarnock Academy, George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; appt'd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916 to March, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Feb., 1923; mag. and collr., Sept., 1928; offg. secty. educ. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1937; confd., May, 1937; ret'd., March, 1941.

DOWLEY, FRANCIS MICHAEL, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 29th Sept., 1885).—joined the service, 1st Jan., 1907, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; on mil. duty from June, 1915 to July, 1920; offg. engr.-in-ch., Cauvery (Mettur) project, July, 1929; supt. engr., Nov., 1934; chief engr., Sept., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; ret'd., Jan., 1941.

DOWLING, LIONEL EDWARD, late Indian State Railways (b. 30th Aug., 1885).—Joined

the service, 4th June, 1915; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Sept., 1919; exec. engr., May, 1932; ret'd.

DOWNING, FRANCIS WILLIAM HALL, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 26th March, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., N.W.F.P., on 30th Sept., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1932; confd., Sept., 1933.

DOWNING, HENRY JULIAN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag.) (b. 22nd March, 1919).—Educ. at Trowbridge High Sch. and Hertford Coll., St. Andrews' Univ., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn. after seln., in May, 1940, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

DRACUP, ATHELSTANE HAMLEIGH, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab.), late Bombay C.S. (b. 26th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, Dec., 1913; dep. collr., Dec., 1915; offg. under-secty. to govt., Bombay, genl., educt., marine and eccl. depts., March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; provl. supt. census operations, Bombay, Feb., 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; ret'd., Oct., 1942.

DRAKE, ANTONY ELLIOT, Indian C.S. (b. 15th June, 1907).—Educ. at Aldenham School and New College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to resdt., Rajputana, March, 1937; asst. pol. agent and colonisation officer Nasirabad, Nov., 1939; asst. pol. offr., Nov., 1939; offr. in charge of civic guards and A.R.P. contrl. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1941; sec. to resdt., Mysore, Sept., 1943.

DRAKE, DONALD HENRY CHARLES, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 23rd Aug., 1887).—Ret'd., Aug., 1937.*

DRAKE, JOHN COLLARD BERNARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 7th March, 1884).—Ret'd., July, 1935.*

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, SIR DIGBY LIVINGSTONE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 21st Dec., 1877).—Ret'd., Dec., 1936.*

DRAVID, NILKANTH KRISHNA, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 24th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at London Sch. of Economics and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, Sept., 1938.

DRAYSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 11th Dec., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th Aug., 1904; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to Sept., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; pers. asst. to high commr., July, 1927; actg. higher exec. officer, Oct., 1928; confd., June, 1929; actg. princpl. exec. offr., Apl., 1941.

DREDGE, ALLAN LANCELOT AUSTIN, M.B.E., Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Nov., 1907).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1927; in mil. employ up to Oct., 1933; under-secty. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Oct., 1933; officer on special duty, Peshawar, Dec., 1935; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1936; officer on special duty, civil secretariat, Peshawar, Feb., 1936; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, March, 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; vice-consul, Zahidan, Nov., 1937; asst. commr., Bannu, Apl., 1939; ditto, Nowshera sub. divn., Aug., 1940; vice-consul, Kherramshahr, Nov., 1941;

M.B.E., June, 1944; dep. commr., Bannu July, 1944.

DREWE, GEOFFREY GRABHAM, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 3rd May, 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr., March, 1932; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1935; collr. and dist. mag., confd., Dec., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1942.

DRING, ARTHUR JOHN, Maj., C.I.E., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 4th Nov., 1902).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1923; in milty. employ to June, 1927; asst. pte. secty. to govr.-genl., 16th July, 1931; asst. commr., Tank, Feb., 1934; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Feb., 1935; dep. secty. to the govr.-genl.'s exec. council (temp.), May, 1936; pte. secty. to govr., N.W.F.P., March, 1937; secty., ditto, Apl., 1937; pol. agent in Chagai, March, 1940; home secty., N.W.F.P., July, 1940; pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; sec. to govt., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1943; ditto (food), Dec., 1943.

DRUMMOND, JOHN GRAHAM, late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 28th Oct., 1884).—Ret'd., Feb., 1936.*

DRUMMOND, ROBERT OLIVE, M.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., 11th Oct., 1929; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934; M.B.E., Jan., 1939.

D'SOUZA, FRANK, C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Dec., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 28th Nov., 1902, to 15th Nov., 1918; offg. asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1918; confd., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. dir. of traffic and statistics, rly. bd., March, 1929; confd., May, 1932; offg. dir., ditto, Apl., 1931; May, 1932; confd., June, 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; ret'd., July, 1940.

D'SOUZA, MICHAEL, M.B.E., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 8th Jan., 1887).—Held non-gazetted posts from 3rd Aug., 1903; supt., tel. traffic, July, 1925; ch. supt., Jan., 1936; ret'd., Nov., 1943; M.B.E., Jan., 1945.

DUBE, ANANT PARSHAO, Rai Bahadur, I.S.O. (b. 24th March, 1884).—Asst. secty., govt. of India, def. dept., March, 1937; I.S.O., June, 1938; ret'd., July, 1939; re-appointed asst. secty., govt. of India, defence co-ordn. dept., Sept., 1939; asst. secty., home dept., govt. of India, 1st to 23rd May, 1940; offcr. on spec. duty, office of dir.-genl. of supply, Aug., 1940.

DUBE, KAMLAKAR (see Kamlakar Dube).

DUBOIS, BASIL ALLEN, late Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 5th March, 1887).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1908; dep. supt., June, 1910; promoted to I.P.S. as supt., Apl., 1928; ret'd., Nov., 1940.

DU BOULAY, SIR JAMES HOUSSEMAINE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Ret'd., July, 1922.*

DUCKFIELD, WILLIAM EDMUND, late Indian Police (b. 28th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 2nd Oct., 1920; addl. supt., March, 1924; offg. supt., Apl., 1928; confd., Aug., 1928; dep. commr., Calcutta, Oct., 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1940.

DUGGAN, SIR JAMSHIROJI NASARVANJI, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., L.M. & S., D.O. (Oxon.), F.C.P.S., late Bombay Medical Dept. (b. 8th Apl., 1884).—Ret'd., Apl., 1939.*

DU HEAUME, FRANCIS HERBERT, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b.

27th May, 1897).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1920; asst. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Apl., 1922; asst. supt., Dec., 1925; officer in charge, intell. branch, N.W.F.P., May, 1926; supt., Feb., 1927; dist. officer, frontier constabulary, May, 1929; O.B.E., Jan., 1932; principl. trng. sch., Phillaur, Apl., 1934; dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1942; dep. dir., civil def., Oct., 1942, to Jan., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1943.

DUKE, CHARLES BERESFORD, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 19th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Feb., 1934; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Nov., 1934, to Jan., 1938; confd. in the Indian Pol. Service, Nov., 1937; asst. commr., Bannu, Oct., 1938; asst. commr., Charsadda, Apl., 1939; secty. to govr., N.W.F.P., March, 1940; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Nov., 1941; dep. secty., ext. affairs dept., Oct., 1943.

DUKE, JAMES ALEXANDER, late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 26th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service, Nov., 1907; asst. dist. supt., June, 1909; dist. supt., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1930; confd., June, 1933; retd., Aug., 1940.

DUKE, WILLIAM VICTOR, M.A., late Indian Eductl. Service (b. 14th Feb., 1886).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

DULAT, SHAMSHER SINGH, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 3rd Apl., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1934; addl. ditto, July, 1934; dist. and sess. judge, confd., Sept., 1940.

DUMBRECK, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 28th Nov., 1917).—Educ. at Linlithgow Academy, Edinburgh Univ., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 29th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

DUN, U. BA, Barr., Burma Judl. Dept. (b. 21st June, 1884).—Apptd. dep. govt. advocate and secty. to Burma legisl. council, 28th Jan., 1926.

DUNBAR, ARCHIBALD, M.A., late Indian Police (b. 3rd Oct., 1886).—Retd., Feb., 1937.*

DUNCAN, ARCHIBALD WALLACE, Lieut.-Col. (ret'd.), late I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (b. 11th July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

DUNCAN, JOHN ELDER, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 30th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930; retd., Aug., 1940.

DUNCAN, PATRICK THOMAS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 10th March, 1916).—Educ. at Bromsgrove and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in N.W.F.P. as asst. commr.

DUNCAN, PERCY RICHARD, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 15th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as forest engr., United Provs., on 1st Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Aug., 1930; retd., 15th Oct., 1944.

DUNCAN, ROBERT SYMON, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 27th Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, on 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1934;

on mil. serv. from Sept., 1939, to Sept., 1944; on depn., Bahawalpur State, Sept., 1944.

DUNDAS, AMBROSE DUNDAS FLUX, C.I.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Apl., 1899).—Educ. at Harrow and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., March, 1924; jt. mag., Oct., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1925; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Sept., 1925; pers. asst. to C.-C. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1925; asst. commr., Charsadda, Nov., 1927; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1928; ditto, Dir Swat and Chitral, Apl., 1932; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P. (transfd. depts.), Nov., 1932; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1933; dep. commr., Peshawar, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1936; employed on spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1936; secty. to resdt. for Punjab states, March, 1937; chief secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1937; resdt. in Waziristan, Aug., 1940; ch. secty., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1940; resdt. in Waziristan, Nov., 1941, to Oct., 1943; resdt. and divl. commr., Oct., 1943.

DUNDON, GEORGE HEWITT, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (supg. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-secty., public wks. dept. (irrign. branch), March, 1929; under-secty., Western admin., irrign. branch., June, 1937, to Oct., 1937; suptg. engr. (perm.), Feb., 1941.

DUNKLEY, SIR HERBERT FRANCIS, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Rangoon) (b. 2nd July, 1886).—Educ. at Wellington College Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; regr. chief court, Lower Burma, Jan., 1919, to Nov., 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; offg. regr., high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1926; acting judge, high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933; Kt., Jan., 1943.

DUNLOP, SIR R. W. L., Kt., C.I.E., D.S.O., late Indian Legisl. Dept.—Retd., July, 1927.*

DUNLOP, SIDNEY WILLIAM CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-sec., rev. dept.) (b. 9th May, 1914).—Educ. at Epsom Coll., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1940; under-sec., rev. dept., Dec., 1942.

DUNN, ARTHUR GEORGE, Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt., Burma rlys.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1922, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. ditto, Burma rlys., May, 1929; offg. transpn. officer (power), Jan., 1931; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Burma rlys., March, 1934.

DUNN, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 26th March, 1877).—Retd., Apl., 1935.*

DUNN, CUTHBERT LINDSAY, C.I.E., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 15th May, 1875).—Retd., May, 1930.*

DUNN, JOHN ALEXANDER, D.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., Geological Survey Dept. (supg. geologist) (b. 27th June, 1899).—Apptd. asst. supt., 17th Dec., 1921; curator, geol. museum, Calcutta, May, 1929; offg. supt., Apl., 1931; confd., July, 1939.

DUNN, SYDNEY ERNEST, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 26th Nov., 1899).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 22nd Oct.,

1919, after previous service from 25th Apl., 1917; second class clerk, 29th Apl., 1920; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; actg. higher exec. offr., July, 1941.

DUNNETT, SIR JAMES MACDONALD, K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 11th Apl., 1877).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

DUNNICLIFF, HORACE BARRATT, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., late Indian Educ. Service (b. 23rd Sept., 1885).—Joined the service as prof., Khalsa Coll., Amritsar, on 7th March, 1914; prof., govt. coll., Lahore, Aug., 1917; chemist, cordite factory, Aruvankadu, Nov., 1918; prof., govt. coll., Lahore, Apl., 1921; spl. chemical adviser for customs, Calcutta, Oct., 1928; princpl., govt. coll., Lahore, May, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; ch. chemist, cent. revs., Nov., 1939; ret'd., March, 1943; re-empld., war transport dept., Nov., 1943.

DUNSDON, ARTHUR CULHAM, Indian State Ry's. (dep. ch. engr.) (b. 28th June, 1887).—Joined the service, 9th Oct., 1913; resdt. engr. E.I. Ry., Oct., 1921; exec. engr., ditto, Aug., 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1934; dy. agent (styled dy. gl. mngr.), E.I. Ry., March, 1938; provl. perm. dep. ch. engr., May, 1939.

DURAI, KANDAPPA, B.Sc., Indian State Ry's. (divl. supt., E.I. Ry.) (b. 12th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 26th March, 1920; asst. engr., Jan., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1928; exec. engr., Dec., 1937; divl. supt., Aug., 1942.

DURAIRAJA AYYAR, NARAYANASWAMI AYYAR, M.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 17th March, 1891).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 13th Oct., 1915; asst. engr., Oct., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr., Aug., 1942.

DURGA PRASAD ROHATGI, Rai Bahadur, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Prov.) (b. 15th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service 12th Oct., 1922; apprentice engr., June, 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932; Rai Bahadur, June, 1939.

DURNO, LESLIE GEORGE, late Indian C.S. (b. 27th July, 1892).—Educ. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1921; arrived, 15th Nov., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. mag. and collr., Oct., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1927; confd., June, 1928; offg. dep. sec., finance, commerce and marine depts., Bengal, May, 1932; confd., July, 1933; dep. commr., March, 1937; ret'd., Dec., 1940.

DUROISBELLE, CHARLES, M.A., late Archaeological Survey dept. (b. 25th June, 1871).—Retd., June, 1931.*

DURRANT, GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 9th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1914; in mil. employ from 12th Oct., 1914, to 5th Oct., 1922; asst. supt., Oct., 1922; offg. supt., Nov., 1925; supt. (prov.) suba., Jan., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1937.

DURRELL, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian State Ry's. (elec. engr., Burma ry's.) (b. 6th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1914; offg. asst. elec. engr., Sept., 1919; confd., Jan., 1920; dist. elec. engr., Dec., 1927; offg. dep. loco. supt. (elec.), Oct., 1930.

DURST, THOMAS ORAM, India Office (higher exec. officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 25th Nov., 1889).—Educ. at Emanuel Sch., Wandsworth

Comm.; second div. clerk, bd. of edn., Apl., 1909, to Nov., 1911; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 27th Nov., 1911, and att'd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Jan., 1919; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, Sept., 1926; assessor of inc. tax, Jan., 1928; actg. suptg. exec. offr., July, 1940.

DUTCH, ROBERT AUSTEN, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 22nd Oct., 1905).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. coll. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1936, to Nov., 1937; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1937, to Oct., 1938; census supt., Bengal, Dec., 1939, to 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1942.

DUTT (see also Datta).

DUTT, BIRENDRA CHANDRA, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 21st Nov., 1895).—Joined the service as probnr., 28th Feb., 1920; asst. acct. genl., Bengal, Nov., 1922; ditto, Madras, Feb., 1924; ditto, central revs., March, 1926; currency officer, Lahore, Apl., 1927; ditto, Calcutta, Jan., 1931; exr., local fund acct., Bengal, Oct., 1933; exr., outside audit, Bengal, Apl., 1935; ch. audtr., E.B. rly., and govt. exr. accts., A.B. rly., Oct., 1938; comptr., Orissa, Sept., 1940; acct.-genl., Bihar, Nov., 1941.

DUTT, DEBPATI, Legis. Assembly Dept. (b. 1st Apl., 1884).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

DUTT, DIVAKAR, B.A., R.B., late Indian State Ry's. (b. 3rd Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st June, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1921; exec. engr., June, 1924; offg. dep. agent, N.W. rly., May, 1935; ret'd., Dec., 1941.

DUTT, NARESH CHANDRA, B.A., late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 7th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

DUTT, PAVITRA KUMAR, Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, suptg. exec. grade, educ. dept.) (b. 25th March, 1888).—Apptd. educ. officer, 8th Feb., 1929; jt. social secty. to Indian delegation to round-table conf., London, Oct., 1930; and again, Sept., 1931.

DUTT, RABINDRA CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 5th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Hare Sch., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Dec., 1939; addl. dist. mag., Apl., 1942; mag. and collr., Apl., 1943.

DUTT, SUBIMAL, B.Sc. (Cal.), Indian C.S. (addl. sec., agric. dept.) (b. 13th March, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Calcutta, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1929; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1936; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1936; addtl. under-secty., e.h. and l. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1938; under-secty., ditto, Feb., 1939; addtl. dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1940; agent of govt. of India in Malaya, Apl., 1941; addl. secty., agric. dept., Jan., 1945.

DUTTON, HUGH REGINALD, C.I.E., F.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Medical Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 21st Nov., 1875).—First

commn., 1st Sept., 1902; in mil. employ till March, 1920; supt., central jail, Bhagalpur, May, 1920; civil surgeon, Patna, March, 1922; princpl., Prince of Wales medical coll., Patna, March, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1930; retd., Nov., 1930; addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, Jan., 1931; examr. of surgical instruments, India store dept., Apl., 1932, to May, 1934; member, medl. bd., India Office, May, 1934 to May, 1940.

DYSON, WILLIAM GEOFFREY, M.C., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (chief consvr., Madras) (b. 7th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1915, as asst. consvr.; on mil. duty from 6th Dec., 1915, to 3rd Feb., 1919; dep. consvr., Dec., 1920; instr., Madras forest coll., May, 1923; princpl., ditto, Aug., 1923; dist. forest officer, July, 1924; actg. consvr., variously since Sept., 1929; provl. sylviculturist, July, 1931; consvr., Oct., 1938; chief ditto, Apl., 1940.

EADES, GERALD JOHN, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 20th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., 18th Dec., 1912; offg. dist. signal engr., Jan., 1925; confd., March, 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., signals, N.W. rly., Nov., 1933; confd., Aug., 1934; retd., March, 1941.

EADES, JOHN HENRY, P.W. Dept., Burma (elec. insp.) (b. 21st Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as elec. engr., Nov., 1926; elec. insp., Dec., 1930.

EADON, ALAN T. (Dir., Civil Aviation, Burma).—Apptd., Jan., 1939.

EAGEN, THOMAS GEORGE ROYAL, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ry's. (dep. ch. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th June, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1926; senr. sc., June, 1935; dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1934.

EARLE, ROBERT JOHN, Indian State Ry's. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 13th Feb., 1898).—Joined service, Oct., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; provl. perm. exec. engr., E.I. Rly., Feb., 1939.

EASTMAN NAGLE, JAMES NICHOLAS, Indian State Ry's. (dep. ch. trpt. mgr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 7th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 25th March, 1913; dist. traffic supt., July, 1926; publicity officer, Nov., 1928; dep. ch. trpt. mgr., Feb., 1942.

EASTMOND, ARCHIBALD, M.C., M.Inst.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 11th Oct., 1889).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

EASTON, THOMAS WILLIAM ROBERTSON, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 29th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 20th Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1926; confd., Oct., 1929; under-secty. to govt. (constrn.), Burma, Feb., 1936; suptg. engr. (perm.), March, 1944; on mil. serv., Apl., 1942, to June, 1944.

EASTWELL, WILFRED HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer) (b. 1st March, 1898).—Apptd. to India Office, store dept., as clerical officer, 1st Dec., 1919, after previous service in General Post Office from 22nd Oct., 1915; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Oct., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, June, 1922; actg. hr. exec. offcr., Sept.-Nov., 1936; temp. ditto, Dec., 1940.

EATES, KENNETH RAYE, Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 20th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1920, with prev. mil. service;

Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; King's ditto Jan., 1942; prom. to I.P. as supt., Apl., 1937. EAYRES, VERNON JOHN GEORGE, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 17th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Strand Sch., King's Coll.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 4th Sept., 1908; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to Apl., 1917; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; personal asst. to high commr., Oct., 1928, to Nov., 1936; hr. exec. officer, Nov., 1936; actg. princpl. ditto, May, 1940.

EBDEN, JAMES WYLDE, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 6th Feb., 1882).—Retd., Apl., 1937.*

ECCLESTON, REV. FREDERICK ERNEST, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 3rd March, 1905).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 11th May, 1944.

EDGAR, SAMUEL GARTHWAITE, C.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 8th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., United Provs., on 1st Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1919; on foreign service in Jodhpur, Nov., 1929; C.B.E., June, 1942; retd., Jan., 1942.

EDGUMBE, GEORGE AUBREY VALLETORT, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 9th Feb., 1931; mil. duty with B.N.V.R.F., Oct., 1940.

EDGECOMBE, ARTHUR RALPH BROOKS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 14th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 1st Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; confd., Oct., 1933.

EDGLEW, NORMAN GEORGE ARMSTRONG, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 19th June, 1888).—Educ. at schools in Switzerland, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bengal, Apl., 1912; under-sec., judl. pol. and appt. depts., Bengal, June, 1916; regr., high ct., Calcutta, appellate side, Apl., 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1924; services placed at disposal of govt. of Assam, Oct., 1927; Kaiser-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., judl. dept., Bengal, and supt. and remembrancer, legal affairs, March, 1933; confd., July, 1933; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, variously from June, 1934; confd., May, 1939.

EDMONDS, ALBERT CHARLES, B.A., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 2nd Nov., 1895).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, 5th Dec., 1920; retd., Apl., 1939.

EDMONDS, VEN. CANON HERBERT JAMES, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 20th March, 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., July, 1917; junr. chaplain, July, 1918; senr. chaplain, July, 1928; canon, 1932; archdeacon of Madras, 1937; retd., July, 1940.

EDMONDS, FRANCIS FREDERIC COLTON, M.A., B.Sc. (b. 16th Oct., 1901).—Apptd. insp. of schools, Coorg and Bangalore, Feb., 1930; retd., Nov., 1936; sec., fed. public serv. comm., June, 1940.

EDMUNDS, PERCY JAMES, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., India Posts and Tels. (ch. engr.) (b. 1st Feb., 1890).—Joined the service (wireless branch), 5th Dec., 1919; divl. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. dir. of wireless, Apl., 1916; confd., Apl., 1927; contr. of broadcasting in addn., March, 1935; dep. dir. genl., wireless, Apl., 1936; del. to internl. conf., Cairo, 1938; dep. chief engr., June, 1938; dir. of tels., Sept., 1939; P.M.G., Sept., 1940;

dy. dir.-genl. tels., Jan., 1941; ch. engr., June, 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

EDWARDS, ARTHUR JOHN CHARLES, C.I.E., F.I.A., Office of High Commr. for India (chief accounting officer) (b. 24th July, 1883).—Educ. at Aske's Hatcham Sch.; entered Natl. Debt. Office, May, 1903; pte. sec. to contr., Oct., 1911, to July, 1913; apptd. junior clerk, India Office, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 5th July, 1913; senr. clerk, Feb., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from Apl., 1921; clerk to commrs. of income tax, Apl., 1921, to Apl., 1922, and from Oct., 1922; asst. chief accounting officer, 1st Apl., 1922; deputy ditto, 21st Apl., 1922; apptd. Indian income tax officer, June, 1924; chief accountg. officer, and commnr. of inc. tax, Nov., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1943.

EDWARDS, CHARLES CYRIL ROGERS, D.S.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 15th July, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Jan., 1921; exec. engr., Burma rlys., March, 1934.

EDWARDS, COSMO GRANT NIVEN, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 13th Aug., 1896).—First commn. dated 19th June, 1915; joined the pol. dept., 8th March, 1921; asst. commt., Kohat, Apl., 1922; ditto, Mardan, Apl., 1926; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Peshawar, Dec., 1928; dep. commr., D.I. Khan, Apl., 1929; offg. under-secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1930; and again, March, 1931; offg. dep. sec. for. and pol. dept., June, 1930; and again, Sept., 1932; secty. to resdt. in Mysore, Feb., 1934; pol. agent, Kalat, Apl., 1938; ditto, in Bhopal, Apl., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1944; resdt., Kolhapur, Oct., 1942.

EDWARDS, EDWIN NORMAN, Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 10th Nov., 1935; confd., Mar., 1938.

EDWARDS, JOHN ASHWORTH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th Feb., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 23rd Dec., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934.

EDWARDS, OSBORNE GEORGE, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th June, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 24th Nov., 1905; resdt. engr., Dec., 1911; in milty. employ. from 6th Nov., 1916, to 24th Nov., 1920; dist. engr., Aug., 1924; offg. contr. of stores, May, 1932; confd., Aug., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1941.

EDWARDS, WILLIAM CECIL, late Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 19th Sept., 1888).—Ret'd., Oct., 1939.*

EGAN, ERIC MARTIN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. offr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th Oct., 1894).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1919; divl. transpn. offr., May, 1935.

EGAN, THOMAS JARLETTE, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (dir. of vety. services, United Provs.) (b. 5th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., March, 1922; supt., Apl., 1925; prof'r., Punjab vety. coll., Nov., 1933; dir. of vety. services, United Provs., July, 1934.

EGERTON, WILLIAM LE BELWARD, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 1st Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroys, March, 1927; asst. pol. agent, Kalat, Dec., 1929; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., Nov., 1930; secty., ditto, Feb., 1931; offg. pol. agent, Bhopal, in addn., Aug., 1935; offcr. on spec. duty, pol. dept., Apl., 1937; dep. secty., pol. dept., May, 1937; pol. agent, W.

Kathiawar Agency, Oct., 1937; sec. to resdt., W.I. States, March, 1938; pol. agent, E. Karhiawar agency, May, 1938; on spec. duty in pol. dept., Nov., 1938; and Jan. to Apl., 1939; officer on special duty under orders of resdt. for States of W. India, Jan., 1939; resdt. in Mewar and pol. agent, S. Rajputana States, Apl., 1939; addtl. dep. secty., pol. dept., Sept., 1940; secty. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Dec., 1940; pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Nov., 1941; sec. resdt., Mysore, Sept., 1942; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Sept., 1944.

EGGAR, SIR ARTHUR, M.A., Barr., Burma Judl. Dept. (advocate-genl., Burma) (b. 22nd May, 1877).—Apptd. acting asst. govt. advocate, 7th May, 1911; law officer to R.A.F., Middle-East, 1918-19; apptd. dep. govt. advocate and sec. to Burma legisl. council, Dec., 1922; offg. govt. advocate, March, 1924; confd., Dec., 1925; adv.-genl., Apl., 1937; Kt., Jan., 1938.

EKAMBARAM, KOLIPAKKAM VENKATISA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 2nd June, 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 13th Aug., 1932; exec. engr., Aug., 1941.

EKANATHA RAJARAM, RAO, B.A. (Hons.), Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 2nd March, 1901).—Joined the service, March, 1926; asst. collr. of customs, July, 1927.

ELKINGTON, J. R. S. W., Capt., R.E., (b. 14th June, 1904).—In milty. employ. to May, 1935; exec. engr., central p.w.d. (temp. aviation circle), to Oct., 1938; apptd. exec. engr., Nov., 1938; reverted to milty. duty, Dec., 1939.

ELLIOT, DAVID CLEPHAN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 17th Sept., 1917).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, St. Andrew's Univ., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., after seln., in May, 1940, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

ELLIOTT, REV. FRANK EDMUND, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior Chaplain) (b. 17th March, 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Nagpur Diocese, 12th Aug., 1942.

ELLIOTT, SIR IVO D'OVLY, Bart., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 7th March, 1882).—Ret'd., March, 1932.*

ELLIOTT, JACK, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 5th Jan., 1891).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., July, 1912; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1916; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1923; supt. (provl.), Apl., 1925; supt., govt. rly. police, Oct., 1926; pers. asst. to dep. inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Sept., 1927; asst. inspr.-genl., May, 1929; and again, Apl., 1933; dist. supt., Sept., 1930; offg. commr. of police, Madras, March, 1936; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1938; commr. for town of Madras, Oct., 1937; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1938; dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1939.

ELLIOTT, JAMES STURGESS, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 2nd March, 1914).—Educ. at Alderman Newton's Sch., Leicester, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; cane inspr., Dec., 1940; sub. divl. offr., June, 1941; jt. mag. and dep. collr., regional grain supply offr., Oct., 1943; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

ELLIOTT, REV. THOMAS REGINALD HATCHARD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 5th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bombay, 27th Oct., 1927; junr.

chaplain, Oct., 1927; senr. ditto, Oct., 1937; senr. presidency chaplain, Oct., 1938.

ELLIS, DICK, Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 28th March, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

ELLIS, EDWARD EARDLEY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th July, 1880).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

ELLIS, THOMAS HOBART, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 11th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Jan., 1919; arrived, 31st March, 1919, and posted to Bengal; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1924; offg. ditto, Feb., 1930; confd., July, 1930; chairman, conciliation board, Aug., 1938; regr., high ct., appl. side, Nov., 1938; spec. offr., A.R.P., Feb., 1942.

ELMES, ARCHIBALD JAMES, India Audit Office (higher exec. officer) (b. 8th Jan., 1893).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 6th Aug., 1914; on milty. service, Jan., 1917, to March, 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; transfed. to India Audit Office, Feb., 1922; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1933; on loan to min. of war transport, Sept., 1941.

ELMES, JULIUS PAUL JONATHAN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 1st Nov., 1887).—Retd., March, 1939.*

ELMORE, ALBERT EDWARD, late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 24th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

ELMSLIE, CYRIL STEWART, late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 10th Apl., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1913; supt., Aug., 1925; retd., June, 1940.

ELWIN, DONALD HAMILTON, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 15th Apl., 1904).—Educ. at Bradfield, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-secty., public dept., Madras, May, 1932; offg. under-secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1934; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Sept., 1943; pte. secty. to the gov. of Madras, Sept., 1935; comp. to nephew of Maharaja of Mysore, June, 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1939.

ELWIN, REGINALD BARNES, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th June, 1908).—Educ. at Bradfield, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Punjab, pol. and home dept., May, 1933; offg. judge, small cause ct., Simla, March, 1934; attd. home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1937; under-secty., def. co-ord. dept., etc., Sept., 1940; secty. to chairman, E.G. supply cl., up to Oct., 1942; pte. secty. to Indian member, Oct., 1943, to Apl., 1944; dep. secty., govt. of India (indus. and civ. supplies), Apl., 1943; dep. secty. ditto (war transport), June, 1944.

EMERSON, DONALD BOMFORD, B.A., B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th Feb., 1885).—Retd., July, 1936.*

EMERSON, GERALD HERBERT, Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1935; offg. asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1935; secty. to A.G.G., Eastern states, March, 1936; asst. commr., Nowshera sub-div., Nov., 1937; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1939; asst. commr.,

Mansebra, Apl., 1941; sec. to govt., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1941; pol. agt., Chagai, Sept., 1944.

EMERSON, SIR HERBERT WILLIAM, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st June, 1881).—Educ. at Calday Grange Gr. Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., manager, Bashahr State, June, 1911, to March, 1914; supt. and settlt. offr., Mandi State, March, 1915; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agri. dept., Oct., 1915; again supt. and settlt. offr., Mandi State, Jan., 1916; asst. commr. and settlt. offr., Punjab, Oct., 1917; dep. commr., Jan., 1922; C.B.E., Jan., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; sec. to govt., fin. dept., Apl., 1926; ch. sec., Nov., 1927; secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1930; C.S.I., June, 1931; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; govt. of Punjab, Apl., 1933, to Apl., 1938; G.C.I.E., May, 1937; retd., Aug., 1940.

EMERSON, RALF BILLING, Maj., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (genl. manager, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 3rd July, 1897).—Joined the service, 28th June, 1920; asst. exec. engr., G.I.P. Rly., Feb., 1927; provl. perm. exec. engr., Dec., 1938; dep. genl. manager, June, 1939; genl. manager, Dec., 1943.

EMINSON, BASIL FRANKLIN, M.B., B.S. (Lon.), Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (b. 24th Sept., 1887).—First commn dated 27th July, 1912; in milty. employ prior to 27th Nov., 1923; offg. civil surg., May, 1925; civil surg., Hyderabad, May, 1933; K.-in-H. Gold Medal, Jan., 1944; retd., Sept., 1942.

ENGLAND, FREDERICK SAMUEL EDWARD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 2nd July, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 24th Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

ENGLAND, WALTER BRASSINGTON, M.B.E., M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, public dept.) (b. 4th Apl., 1893).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to India Office, 4th Apl., 1921; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India, Apl., 1922; exec. officer, Jan., 1924; higher clerical officer, June, 1927; actg. higher exec. officer, Sept., 1929, to Aug., 1930; hr. exec. officer. (tempy.), Apl., 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

ENGLEFIELD, ARTHUR GUY, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 13th July, 1893).—Joined the service, asst. loco. supt., Dec., 1919; asst. rolling stock officer, July, 1926; asst. works manager, N.W. rly., Dec., 1932; divl. mechl. engr., N.W. rly. (provl.), Aug., 1934; confd., Sept., 1936.

ENSOR, ERIC LOVELL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. trans. supt. tels.) (b. 25th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. tel. supt., 4th Oct., 1921; asst. transpn. supt. (tels.), 1st Nov., 1922; offg. dep. ditto., Apl., 1932, and again, Sept., 1935; dep. trans. supt., tels., Jan., 1939.

ENSOR, PETER TEBBETT, B.Sc., Econ., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 7th June, 1915).—Educ. at Colfe's Sch., Lewisham, London Sch. of Economics, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 13th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; a.r.p. offr., Apl., 1942; sub. divl. offr., Apl., 1942; under-sec., govt. of Punjab, July, 1944.

ERACH MANEKSHAH NANAVATI (asst. Nanavati, E. M.)

ERSKINE, CLAUDE ERNEST TORIN, C.I.E.,

D.S.O., M.C., Col., I.A., retd. (b. 1st May, 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; in mil. employ to Apl., 1938; pers. asst. to secty., mil. dept., India Office, Sept., 1938, to Feb., 1945.

ERSKINE, LORD, JOHN FRANCIS ASHLEY, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (b. 26th Apl., 1895).—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; asst. pte. secty. to First Lord of Admy., 1920-1921; parly. pte. secty. to P.M.G., 1923; princpl. pte. secty. to Home Secretary, 1924; M.P. for Weston-super-Mare Divn. of Somerset, 1922-1923 and again 1924; asst. govt whip in Natl. Govt., 1932; G.C.I.E., Aug., 1934; Govt. of Madras, Nov., 1934, to Mar., 1940; G.C.S.I., May, 1940.

EUSTACE, EDWARD ARTHUR RAWLINS, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 18th Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and R.M. Coll., Sandhurst; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1923; arrived, 14th Nov., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., March, 1925; addl. under-sec. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., Dec., 1927; offg. regr., high ct., Lahore, Jan., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1931; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Nov., 1931; confd., July, 1935; home sec., govt. of Punjab, March, 1938; sec., (medl. etc. dept.), Oct., 1938, and again, Apl., 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

EUSTACE, JOHN CURTIS WEINHER, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 22nd Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Wellington; Brentwood Coll.; Victoria, British Columbia, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1933; dep. regr., co-op. societies, Punjab, Apl., 1935; offg. regr., ditto, March, 1936, and again Oct., 1936.

EVANS, AIDAN OSWALD, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 26th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 30th Oct., 1909; offg. dist. ditto, Feb., 1915; offg. dep. traffic manager, Aug., 1921; with Indian Rly. conference asscn., Apl., 1922; supt., July, 1925; ditto, operating dept., Oct., 1926; offg. secty. to agent, E.I. Rly., March, 1928; offg. divl. supt., March, 1929; offg. dep. agent, E.I. Rly., Dec., 1929; offg. divl. supt., Oct., 1930; and again, Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1936.

EVANS, ARTHUR GARBETT, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 6th May, 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 1st Nov., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933; inspr.-genl., Rampur State, Oct., 1938.

EVANS, ELWYN RICHARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 5th Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Brynmawr County Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; served in Sind, May, 1944.

EVANS, ERNEST SYDNEY, late India Audit Office (b. 16th Feb., 1876).—Retd., March, 1936.*

EVANS, SIR GEOFFREY, Kt., C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Agricl. Service.—Retd., Feb., 1925.*

EVANS, GEORGE EMLYN THOMAS HULSE, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 18th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd., Jan., 1932; dep. commr. police, Calcutta, March, 1934, to Feb., 1936; asst. inspr.-genl., March, 1940; King's police medal, 1942.

EVANS, HUBERT JOHN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag.,

United Provs.) (b. 21st Nov., 1904).—Educ. at City of London Sch., Jesus Coll., Cambridge, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, 27th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1932; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1936; dep. commr., Delhi, March, 1938; asst. settl. offr., sec. 1938; rev. sec., fin. commrs., Feb., 1939; dep. commr., subst. provl., May, 1940; dir. war. infm. and dep. sec., govt Punjab (home), March, 1942.

EVANS, LEONARD GLYDE LAVINGTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., and Indian Political Service (b. 9th Nov., 1888).—Retd., March, 1938.*

EVANS, SIR T. J. CAREY (see Carey-Evans, Sir T. J.).

EVANS, VICTOR DONALD CHAPPELL, Indian Police (b. 8th Jan., 1915).—Apptd., asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935; confd., July, 1937; served in Sind from Oct., 1939; a.r.p. contr., May, 1942; enq. and enforcement offr. and dep. sec., food dept., Nov., 1943.

EVANS, WILLIAM HUGH, B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 24th June, 1892).—Educ. at County Sch., Pwllheli, and Univ. Coll., Bangor; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1921; arrived, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; confd. Sept., 1927; retd., 31st Oct., 1943.

EVANS-GORDON, KENMURE ALICK GARTH, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 20th Aug., 1885).—First commn. dated 24th Jan., 1906; in mil. employ to 11th May, 1913, and again from 13th Oct., 1914; services lent to Rewa Durbar from 1st Jan., 1919; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1924; sec. to resdt. at Mysore and sec. to ch. commr., Coorg, Feb., 1925; pol. agent, Baghkhand, March, 1929; ditto, E. Kathiawar agency, Nov., 1930; vice-presdt., council of regency, Cooch Behar State, March, 1931; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar Agency, Nov., 1934; offg. resdt., Baroda, May, 1935; resdt., Jaipur, Nov., 1935; offg. A.G.G., Deccan states, June, 1936; resdt. for Kolhapur and Deccan States, Apl., 1937; ditto, June, 1938; confd. as resident, class II, Nov., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1939; retd., Aug., 1940.

EVERALL, WILLIAM TEAGUE, O.B.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Aug., 1880).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

EVERETT, CECIL HOWARD, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 9th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 6th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1930; supt. (subs. provl.), March, 1936; confd., Oct., 1936; central intell. offr., Lahore, July, 1944.

EVERS, REV. MERVYN SAXELBYE, M.A., M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 8th Sept., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 18th May, 1924; junr. chaplain, May, 1926; senr. ditto, May, 1932; retd.,

EVERSHED, WALLIS ALBURY, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd July, 1889).—Retd., July, 1937.*

EWBANK, ROBERT BENSON, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 22nd Oct., 1883).—Retd., July, 1936.*

EYERS, CYRIL, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 27th Sept., 1878).—Retd., July, 1935.*

FABRE-TONNERRE, JOSEPH, late Indian Police (Bihar) (b. 20th Apl., 1882).—Retd., Apl., 1937.*

FAIRHALL, CHRISTOPHER HAMLYN, Indian

Police (Punjab) (b. 1st Oct., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

FAIRLEY, FRANCIS NOEL, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 11th Dec., 1883).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store dept., 17th March, 1903; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1923; temp. suptg. exec. officer, Apl., 1938; ret'd., May, 1944.

FAIRLEY, JOHN, B.Sc., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 7th June, 1880).—Ret'd., June, 1935.*

FAIRWEATHER, SIR CHARLES EDWARD STUART, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 26th Nov., 1909; supt., Apl., 1923; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, June, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; confd., March, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offr., Order of St. John, June, 1941; confd. as commr., police, Calcutta, July, 1941; Kt., 1944; ret'd., 16th Jan., 1944.

FAIZMUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN, late Punjab C.S. (b. 16th Dec., 1899).—Extra asst. commr., Punjab, Jan., 1914; ret'd., Dec., 1944.

FAKHAR-UD-DIN, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad F.).

FALCONER, GEORGE ARTHUR, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 3rd June, 1894).—First commn., dated 28th Aug., 1917; joined pol. dept., 18th June, 1923; under-sec., to pol. resident in Persian Gulf, May, 1924; asst. resident, Aden, Aug., 1927; 2nd asst. to resdt., Kashmir, March, 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Deccan States, Apl., 1933; ditto, Gujerat States, Oct., 1933; offg. A.G.G., ditto, Apl., 1935; consul for Kerman, Nov., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1942; pol. agt., Bhopal, Nov., 1942; H.M. minister, Nepal, Apl., 1944.

FALSHAW, DONALD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 22nd Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Lancaster Royal Gr. Sch., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1932; offg. ditto, Nov., 1933; sub. perm., June, 1937; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1943.

FANN, JOHN MONTAGU GROSSE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 31st Dec., 1904).—Educ. at Latymer Upper Sch., Hammersmith, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, oilfields, Apl., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932; confd., Dec., 1937; sec., evacuee and welfare dept., 1944.

FANSHAW, JOCELYN HERBERT, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 21st Nov., 1880).—Ret'd., Nov., 1937.*

FAR BRO THER, ERNEST SAMUEL, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (dir., vety. services, Bombay) (b. 16th July, 1890).—Joined the service, Oct., 1913; on milty. duty from July, 1915, to Apl., 1919; offg. supt., Sind, July, 1919; ditto, Bombay Presdy., Feb., 1922; confd., Nov., 1923; dir., vety. services, June, 1932.

FARGUE, DUDLEY GORDON HERIOT DE LA, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Sept., 1897).—First commn. dated 21st Sept., 1916; joined pol. dept., 11th Apl., 1923; under-sec. to agent to G.G. in C.I., Apr., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1926; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Jan., 1928; offg. under-sec. for. and pol.

dept., July, 1930; on foreign service in Gwalior State, March, 1931; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1932; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1933; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1934; pol. agent, Zohb, Nov., 1934; home secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., July, 1938; dep. commr., Bannu, Oct., 1938; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar agency, Aug., 1940; pol. agent, Loralai, Sept., 1941; pol. agt., Kurram, May to June, 1944; rev. and divl. commr., Aug. to Oct., 1944; ch. sec. to govt., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1944.

FARMBROUGH, ALFRED, A.I.C., Office of High Commr. for India (chemist, store dept.) (b. 30th Sept., 1889).—Apptd. junior asst. chemist, India Store depot, 1909; asst. chemist, 1920; chemist, June, 1935.

FARMER, JOHN COTTON, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 18th May, 1886).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1906; asst. supt., Oct., 1908; supt., Nov., 1915; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1928, and Nov., 1929; confd., Jan., 1931; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; and again, Apl., 1935; confd., Dec., 1936; Indian police medal, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; ret'd., Aug., 1940.

FARMER, PATRICK SCOTT LETHBRIDGE, Indian Police (b. 18th July, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, Nov., 1932; asst. commr., Port Blair, Feb., 1938.

FARNON, JOSEPH ASHLEY ST. JOHN, M.A., late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 23rd July, 1882).—Ret'd., Jan., 1938.*

FARQUHAR, ARTHUR GRAHAM FRANCIS, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Glenalmond and Sch. of Economics, London; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1927; and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. under-secy. to govt., Oct., 1929; offg. dep. commr. variously from March, 1931; dep. commr. (provl.), Feb., 1939; confd., March, 1941; civil air recruiting offr., Feb., 1942; O.B.E., June, 1942; addl. sec. to govt., May, 1943; sec., local self govt. dept., Feb., 1944.

FARQUHARSON, FRANCIS ARCHIBALD, C.I.E., M.C., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 20th Jan., 1886).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Jan., 1907; on milty. duty from 11th March, 1915; to 26th May, 1919; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1928; under-secy., public wks. dept., irrig. branch (rev.), May, 1929; suptg.-engr., Apl., 1933; ch. engr. and secty. to govt., Punjab, I.B., May, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; ret'd., 20th July, 1941.

FARRAN, ARTHUR COURTNEY, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Karnatak Coll., Dharwar) (b. 15th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1915; prof. of history and pol. economy, Elph. Coll., Bombay, Jan., 1917; prof. of history and economy, Deccan Coll., Poona, July, 1926; offg. princpl. and prof., Elph. Coll., Bombay, June, 1929, and again June, 1932; princpl., Karnatak Coll., Dharwar, Nov., 1930.

FARRANT, GEORGE YORKE SILVERTHORNE, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 23rd June, 1894).—Joined the service, 28th Dec., 1919, as asst. supt., Bombay, offg. supt., Feb., 1924; confd., Sept., 1927; King's police medal, Jan., 1942; confd. supt. (sel. grade), Dec., 1943.

FARUQI, GHUFAN AHMAD, B.A., Indian

C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 1st Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Sitapur, Allahabad Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1939; officer on special duty, local self govt. dept., United Provs., June, 1939; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Aug., 1940.

FAULKNER, TERENCE BERNARD MICHAEL CUNLIFFE, Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 20th Dec., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939; confd., Dec., 1941; a.d.c. to govr., Sept., 1942, to Conf., 1943.

FAUQI, MUHAMMAD ZAHURUDDIN AHMAD (see Zahuruddin Ahmad, F. M.).

FAUQUI, NASIR AHMAD, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 15th Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-secty., rev. dept., Bombay, Aug., 1935; confd., Apl., 1936; under-secty., home and pol. depts., and passport offcr., Nov., 1937; attd. home dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1937; O.B.E., June, 1944.

FAWCETT, SIR CHARLES GORDON HILL, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 28th June, 1869).—Retd., June, 1929.*

FAWCUS, GEORGE ERNEST, C.I.E., O.B.E., V.D., M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educl. Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 12th March, 1885).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

FAWCUS, LOUIS REGINALD, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (member, bd. of rev., Bengal) (b. 7th Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Uppingham, Trinity Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settl. offr. transf. to Bengal, Apl., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1917; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to March, 1919; mag. and collr., Sept., 1925; offg. secty., bd. of rev., Bengal, Oct., 1927; offg. dir., land records, Bengal, March, 1929; offg. secy. to govt., Bengal agric. and ind. depts., Jan., 1931; confd., July, 1931; offg. secty., rev. dept., Bengal, June, 1935; offg. commr., Feb., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; member bd. of rev. (confd.), Feb., 1941.

FAY, ARCHIBALD THOMAS, late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 22nd Aug., 1883).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

FAZL, A. H. A. (see Abul Fazl, A. H.).

FAZL ALI, SIR SAYID, Barr., Judl. dept., Bihar (b. 19th Sept., 1886).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1929; performed duties of Chief Justice, Patna High ct., May to July, 1938; Kt., Jan., 1941.

FAZL MUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. Jan., 1888).—Apptd. asst. consvr., from 19th July, 1922; dep. consvr., July, 1926; retd., March, 1943.

FAZLUR RAHMAN KHONDKAR, B.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 1st Feb., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 22nd Apl., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; confd., supt., June, 1942; spec. supt., intell. branch., Aug., 1943.

FEARN, JOHN MARTIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 24th June, 1916).—Educ. at Dundee High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 24th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

FEARNLEY-WHITTINGSTALL, FRANCIS HERBERT, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 8th June, 1894).—Retd., June, 1936.*

FEARON, LEONARD KEARY, Salt Dept., Madras (dep. commr.) (b. 10th Nov., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 11th Dec., 1901; asst. inspr., Apl., 1905; inspr., Apl., 1910; asst. commr., Oct., 1920; dep. commr., March, 1932.

FELL, DOUGLAS YATES, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Sept., 1910).—Educ. at Charterhouse and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. pol. agent, Baluchistan Agency, Nov., 1938; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1938; offcr. spec. duty, C.I. Agency, May, 1940; under-secty. to the resdt. for Baroda and the Gujarat States and asst. pol. agent, Rewak-antha, Jan., 1941; sec. to resdt., Baroda and Gujarat States, July, 1944; sec. to resdt., Baroda and states of W.I. and Gujarat, Nov., 1944.

FELL, SIR GODFREY BUTLER HUNTER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma).—Retd., Sept., 1924.*

FENTON, RICHARD VINCENT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., C.P.) (b. 17th July, 1915).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 13th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in C.P. as asst. commr.

FENWICK, EDGAR NORMAN, late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 30th Aug., 1892).—Retd., June, 1938.*

FERGUSON, DONALD HARRY, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 8th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly., 16th June, 1924; supt., July, 1930.

FERGUSON, ROLAND EDWARD STUART, late Indian Police, Bihar (b. 22nd Aug., 1899).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

FERMIE, OTTO WILLIAM PATRICK, B.Sc., B.E., India Posts and Tels. (p.m.g.) (b. 16th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1918; asst. divl. engr., July, 1920; divl. engr., tels., July, 1928; supt. of tels., N.W. rly., Jan., 1932; offg. dir., tels., United Provs., March, 1936; confd., Aug., 1936; p.m.g., Jan., 1942.

FERMOR, SIR LEWIS LEIGH, Kt., O.B.E., D.Sc., A.R.S.M., F.R.S., F.G.S., F.A.S.B., M.Inst.M., late Geological Survey of India (b. 18th Sept., 1880).—Retd., Nov., 1935.*

FERRERS, VVYVYAN MACLEOD, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 20th Jan., 1877).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

FERRIE, ALLAN, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 12th Apl., 1892).—Retd., March, 1939.*

FFORDE, SIR CECIL ROBERT, Kt., K.C., late Judicial Dept., Punjab (b. 24th June, 1876).—Retd., March, 1931.*

FFORDE, THEOBALD PHILIP FEATHERSTONE-HAUGH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 25th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 27th Jan., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

FFOULKES, GEORGE ROBERT GWYNNE, Indian Police (b. 9th June, 1915).—Asst. supt., Bihar, apptd., on probn., 11th Nov., 1935; confd., Feb., 1940.

FIELD, SIR DONALD MOYLE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd. (b. 19th Nov., 1881).—Retd., July, 1935.*

FIELD, GEOFFREY GLENNIE, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 3rd June, 1895).—

Joined the service as asst. supt. on 22nd Nov., 1915; I.A.R.O. from 8th July, 1918, to 8th Aug., 1919; offg. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Dec., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

FIELD, JOHN BERTRAM FINCH, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 15th July, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. supt., Nov., 1933; confd., Dec., 1940.

FIELD, ROBERT EDWIN, O.B.E., India Office (actg. asst. sec., services and genl. dept.) (b. 27th Dec., 1888).—Apptd. second-class clerk, India Office, 22nd Apr., 1910, and attached to the pol. dept.; transf. to acct.-genl.'s dept., Dec., 1911; asst. sec. to cttee. on State technical scholarships, Apr. to Sept., 1912; transf. to dept. of sec. for Indian students, Sept., 1912; sub-warden, 21, Cromwell Road, S.W., Apr. to Oct., 1913; actg. warden, ditto, Nov., 1914, to Feb., 1919; sec. to London advisory cttee. for Indian students, Aug., 1913; minor staff clerk, Feb., 1916; asst. to eduel. adviser for Indian students, Feb., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; asst. to jt. sec. for Indian students, Nov., 1920; re-transf. to India Office as asst. princpl., Apr., 1921; jt. sec. to cttee. on Indian students, 1921-22; reading clerk to council, Nov., 1924; pte. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, June, 1926; actg. princpl., 1st Dec., 1928; confd., Apr., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1936; actg. asst. secty., services and gen. dept. and estabt. offr., July, 1940.

FIELDEN, LIONEL, C.I.E., late All-India Radio (b. 15th May, 1896).—Joined the service, as Controller of Broadcasting, on 30th Aug., 1935; retd., Apr., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

FIELDEN-CLARKE, VICTOR HERBERT TOM, late Indian Forest Service (b. 11th Feb., 1888).—Retd., Feb., 1935.*

FINDLAY, SIR CHARLES STEWART, Kt., M.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1874).—Retd., Jan., 1932.*

FINDLAY, JAMES, M.A., M.B., Ch.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd., late Jail Dept., Burma (b. 19th Aug., 1886).—Date of first commn., 26th July, 1913; supt., jail dept., Burma (temp), Mar., 1924; ditto (permn.) Feb., 1925; inspr.-genl. of prisons, March, 1934; retd., Aug., 1941.

FINLAY, WILLIAM WATERS, M.A. (Edin.), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 8th May, 1899).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1931, and again, Sept., 1933; offg. secty., bd. of rev., United Provs., Jan., 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1934; confd. mag. and collr., Nov., 1938.

FINLAYSON, ANDREW, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 11th Aug., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, on 17th Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; temp. supt. engr., Sept., 1943.

FINNEY, PHILIP EDMUND STANLEY, O.B.E., Indian Police Service (supt., Bengal) (b. 11th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., temp. employed as supt., Deoli jail, Dec., 1931; confd. as supt., July, 1937; O.B.E., June, 1934; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1938, to March, 1940; supt., centr. intell. bureau, July, 1943.

FISH, REV. FREDERIC JOHN, B.A., M.C., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 13th

Apr., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Punjab, 5th Feb., 1930; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1932.

FISHER, D., M.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd July, 1882).—Retd., July, 1937.*

FISHER, GERALD THOMAS, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 27th Aug., 1887).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1908; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, as asst. commr., Kohat, 13th May, 1919; under-sec. to agent to gov.-genl. in Central India, March, 1921; confd. as pol. officer, June, 1922; pol. agent, Chagai, March, 1924; H.M. Consul for Sistan and Kain, Jan., 1925; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Dec., 1927; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Nov., 1928; secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, March, 1932; ch. minister, Kapurthala state, Jan., 1935; resdt. at Gwalior and pol. agent for states of Rampur and Benares, Apr., 1937, again, Oct., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; resdt. for C. India, Feb., 1940; confd. as resdt., cl. I, July, 1941; C.S.I., June, 1942; retd., March, 1943.

FISHER, WILLIAM HUMPHREY WALTER, Indian Police (b. 30th March, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., May, 1943.

FISHWICK, DAVID, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 29th Apr., 1906).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch. and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., local govt. dept., Burma, March, 1933; asst. collr., Impl. customs service, Rangoon, March, 1934; asst. commr., Oct., 1934; joint registrar, co-operative societies, Oct., 1935; dep. commr. offg., Dec., 1935; confd., May, 1939.

FISK, SYDNEY PATRICK, Indian Police (b. 2nd June, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1938.

FITT, WATKIN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 22nd June, 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 10th Oct., 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; on mil. service from Feb., 1916, to Aug., 1919; second-class clerk, 29th Apr., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from Apr., 1921; exec. officer, Apr., 1922; higher exec. officer, Nov., 1934.

FITZE, SIR KENNETH SAMUEL, K.C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 6th Jan., 1887).—Educ. at Marlborough, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; apptd. templ. to pol. dept. as asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Apr., 1915; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, May, 1917; under-sec. to govt. of India for, and pol. dept., Nov., 1918; apptd., substantively to pol. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1919; census supt., Central India, Oct., 1921, to Jan., 1922; sec. to agent to gov.-genl. in Central India, Jan., 1922; dep. sec. to govt. of India, for, and pol. dept., Nov., 1924; pol. agent, Baghalkhand, Nov., 1926; jt. secty. to govt. of India, for, and pol. dept. (temp.), July, 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept., Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; jt. secty., India States enquiry comee., Jan., 1932; pol. agent, southern states of C.I., Nov., 1932; offg. and opium commr. in C.I., Apr., 1933; offg. resdt. A.G.G., in C.I., Jan., 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; resdt. for: Central India, Apr., 1937; ditto,

July, 1938; secty. to H.E. Crown Representative, Dec., 1940; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1941; confd. as sec. to Crown Repres., July, 1941; ret'd., June, 1944; advr. to sec. of state for India, June, 1944.

FITZHERBERT, RICHARD ANTHONY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 4th March, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; in milty. employ from 12th Nov., 1915, to 16th Jan., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; suptg. engr. (prov.), Feb., 1938; confd., Nov., 1938; ret'd., March, 1942.

FITZMAURICE, DESMOND FITZ-JOHN, C.I.E., B.A. Lieut.-Col., R.E., ret'd. (master, security printing) (b. 17th Aug., 1893).—Apptd. dep. mint master, on probn., Nov., 1929; ditto (temp.), Dec., 1932; offg. master, security printing, Sept., 1934; confd., Oct., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1941.

FLACK, ALAN WILLIAM, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Nov., 1913).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1937; sub-divl. offr., Dec., 1940; supt., cent. jail, Apr., 1943.

FLATT, LESLIE NEEVE, C.I.E., B.Sc., V.D., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (dir. mechl. engr., Rly. Bd.) (b. 26th March, 1889).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1913; asst. loco. supt., E.I. Rly., June, 1913; in milty. employ from 22nd Feb., 1916, to 24th Feb., 1919; works manager, G.I.P. Rly., Aug., 1920; dep. ch. mech. engr., March, 1923; dep. carr. and wagon supt., Oct., 1923; dep. ch. mech. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. dep. loco. supt., Apr., 1927; offg. loco. and carr. supt., Dec., 1930; offg. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. Rly., Feb., 1934; confd., July, 1935; ditto, N.W. Rly., Jan., 1938; dir. mechl. engr., Apr., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1943.

FLEETON, ERIC RALPH, Indian State Rlys. (supt.) (b. 24th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 1st Sept., 1920; transpn. (power) supt., E.I. Rly., Sept., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. Rly., March, 1936; provl. ch. mechl. engr., Apr., 1940.

FLETCHER, ALAN HENRY SAUL, M.B.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Assam) (b. 12th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1934; confd., Jan., 1942; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

FLETCHER, ANTONY LEOCADIA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at St. Joseph's High Sch., Trivandrum, the Universities of Madras and Nagpur, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offcr., Nov., 1936, to May, 1938.

FLETCHER, EDWARD WALTER, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd July, 1899).—First commn., 23rd Jan., 1919; in milty. employ to Jan., 1928; British trade agent, Gyantse, and asst. to pol. officer in Sikkim, Nov., 1929; secty., Brit. legn., Kabul, Dec., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, March, 1935; on depn. to Baltic States for 13 mths. from Dec., 1936; secty. to resdt. for W.I., May, 1938; addl. dep. sec. external affairs dept., Dec., 1939; first secty., Brit. legation, Kabul, Aug., 1940; commi. agt., Ahwaz, Apr., 1944.

FLETCHER, JOHN HINTON, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 23rd June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-secty. to govt., irrign. branch, Punjab, Jan., 1931; ditto (construction), Oct., 1934; offg. ditto (northern admin.), Feb., 1936; supt. engr. (permt.) and jt. sec., govt. of Punjab (home), July, 1941.

FLEURY, WALFRID RONALD, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Orissa) (b. 7th Apr., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 25th Aug., 1930.

FLEWETT, WILLIAM EDWARD, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 19th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. dep. consvr., Punjab, 16th Dec., 1921; div. forest officer, Andamans, Sept., 1931; offg. ch. forest officer, Andamans, Aug., 1931, to Aug., 1936; chief timber inspcn. offcr., dept. of supply, Sept., 1939; dep. dir., directorate-genl. of supply, July, 1940.

FLINT, HARRY EWART, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 22nd Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., May, 1925; O.B.E., June, 1932.

FLOWERDEW, RICHARD EDWARD, C.I.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 26th Sept., 1886).—Retd., Apr., 1939.*

FLUX, DUNDAS A. D. (see Dundas, A. D. F.).

FLUX, RONALD CECIL, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 2nd June, 1901).—Joined the service on 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

FORBES, GEORGE FREEMAN MURRAY, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 5th June, 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1922; asst. supt. (proby.), Sept., 1922; confd., Jan., 1927; offg. supt., govt. rly. police, May, 1927; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1927; confd., July, 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1932.

FORRESTER, CHARLES, A.H.W.C., F.I.C., Ph.D. (Edin.), F.R.S.E., A.M.I.Chem.E., Indian School of Mines (princpl., Indian Sch. of Mines) (b. 6th March, 1895).—Apptd. prof. of chemistry and assaying on probn., 3rd Nov., 1926; confd., 1929; offg. principal, Indian Sch. of Mines, May, 1927; Nov., 1929; and June, 1934; principal, ditto, Nov., 1936.

FORSYTH, JAMES PETERS, B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (actg. dir. of purchase, store dept.) (b. 22nd Apr., 1883).—Educ. at R. Gr. Sch., Lancaster, and Caius Coll., Cambridge (8th wrangler); apptd. junr. clerk, store dept., 19th Oct., 1906; senr. clerk, July, 1912; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; dir. of purchase (temp.), Apr., 1927; confd., Nov., 1932; actg. dir. genl., stores dept., May, 1940; on spl. duty, July, 1941.
