FORTESCUE, VEN. CECIL FRANCIS, L.Th., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Bombay) (b. 14th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 18th Jan., 1922; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1924; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1930; bishop's commissary (in addition), Apr., 1939; archdeacon of Bombay, Dec., 1939; ret'd., June, 1943.

FORTY, FREDERICK CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 17th Sept., 1894).—Apptd. to acctn. genl.'s dept., India Office, 15th Dec., 1919; transf. to office of High Commr. for India, 8th Apr., 1921; clerical officer, June, 1921; exec. officer, Apr., 1922; actg. hr. ditto, March, 1939; progress offr., Apr., 1941.

FOSTER, ESMOND LEWIS PEARCE, O.B.E.,

B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924; divl. forest officer, 5th. Andamans, Aug., 1935; ch. forest officer, ditto, Oct., 1935; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; utilization offr., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1942.

FOSTER, KENNETH WILLIAM, B.A., Burma C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 13th Aug., 1899).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Dec., 1921; dep. commr., Aug., 1937; secy. to financial commissioner, March, 1939; sec. to govt. of Burma, 1944.

FOSTER, SIR WILLIAM, Kt., C.I.E., B.A., late India Office.—Retd., Nov., 1923.*

FOWLE, FULWAR WILLIAM CRAVEN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th March, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. eng., 27th Nov., 1908; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; in milty. employ from 27th Sept., 1917, to 26th May, 1920; divl. engr., Feb., 1926; retd.

FOWLER, ROBERT WILLIAM DOUGHTY, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 6th March, 1914).—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Gr. Sch., Mansfield, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1937, and served as asst. commr.; on milty. service, Nov., 1940; addtl. under-secty., def. dept., March, 1941.

FOX, SIR CYRIL SANKEY, D.Sc., Geological Survey of India (dir.) (b. 24th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 2nd March, 1911; supt., Dec., 1930; offg. dir., July, 1936; confd., July, 1939; kt., June, 1943.

FOY, THOMAS ARTHUR WYNES, Indian Service of Engrs. (supitg. engr.) (b. 20th Feb., 1925).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1927; under-secty. to govt., irrign. branch, Punjab, May, 1931; ditto (constn.), Punjab irrign. sect., May, 1933; chief engr., irrign., Bikaner State, from Oct., 1935; suptg. engr. (permt.), Apl., 1943; served under govt. of India, external affairs, June, 1943.

FRAMJI, KAVSI KAIKHOSRU, M.B.E., B.A., B.Sc., Ind. Service of Engrs. (b. 21st March, 1908).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 30th July, 1932; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

FRAMPTON, HENRY JAMES, C.I.E., M.C., B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (joint secty., home dept., govt. of India) (b. 14th Aug., 1897).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; asst. mag. and collr., United Provs., 2nd Dec., 1921; jt. mag., Dec., 1923; offg. mag. and collr. Jan., 1926; dep. secty. to govt., United Provs., genl. bch., Oct., 1932; mag. and collr., Nov., 1936; dep. commr., March, 1937; dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India, May, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

FRANCE, REV. ALEXANDER, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 24th Oct., 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., United Provs., 16th March, 1925; junr. chaplain, March, 1927; senr. chaplain, Madras, 1937; retd., Oct., 1941.

FRANCE, ROBERT EDWARD, India Office (actg. princpl., E. & O. dept.) (b. 16th Aug., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 27th Oct., 1919, after previous service from 27th Apl., 1916; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Oct., 1919; clerical offr., 1st Jan., 1920; exec. offr., May, 1926; actg. hr. exec. offr., Oct., 1936; confd., Sept., 1938; temp. asst. admin. offr., March, 1940; lent to Miny. of Supply, Sept., 1940, to Dec., 1943; actg. princpl., econ. and overseas dept., Jan., 1944.

FRANKAU, GEORGE NEUBERG, Central

Provs. (b. 15th Feb., 1881).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

FRANKLIN, ERIC ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 3rd July, 1910).—Educ. at Government English High Sch., Maymyo, Rangoon Univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 7th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., Oct., 1939; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1941; dep. sec. ch. secs. dept., 1944.

FRANKLIN, GEORGE DENNE, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., M.B., B.C., Bt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 28th July, 1877).—Retd., July, 1934.*

FRANKLIN, MURIEL CURRAN, LL.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Burma) (b. 18th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as head mistress, govt. normal school, Rangoon, on 3rd Jan., 1924; inspectress of schools, Feb., 1929; retd., Nov., 1943.

FRANKLIN, ROGER WARWICK, late Indian Police Service (b. 2nd Sept., 1896).—Retd., June, 1932.*

FRASER, DENHOLME DE MONTALTE STUART, C.S.I., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (pol. a.d.c. to sec. of state) (b. 5th Oct., 1889).—First commn. dated 20th Jan., 1909; first asst. to pol. resident, Persian Gulf, Nov., 1919; under-sec. to agent to govr.-genl. in Central India, Nov., 1922; asst. mag. and collr., Moradabad, Dec., 1924; asst. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1925; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, May, 1926; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar States, Dec., 1926; pol. agent in Southern States of C.I. and Malwa, Oct., 1928; on foreign service in Tonk State, Oct., 1930; pol. agent, Malwa, Apl., 1935; offg. secty. to A.G.G. in C.I. in addn., May, 1936; pol. agent, Malwa, Apl., 1937; offg. resdt. for C.I., March to July, 1938; resdt. in Kashmir, Oct., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1938; confd. as 2nd class resdt., June, 1939; resdt. in Mysore, Nov., 1941; confd., June, 1942; C.S.I., Jan., 1944; pol. a.d.c. to sec. of State, Apl., 1945.

FRASER, DONALD MACDONALD, Maj., I.M.S., Civil Surgeon, United Provs.—Date of first commn., 18th Jan., 1925; civil surgeon, March, 1934.

FRASER, FREDERICK CHARLES, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 15th Feb., 1880).—Retd., Feb., 1935.*

FRASER, IAN MARTIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 13th Sept., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 7th March, 1921; arrived, 29th Apl., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Oct., 1927; and again, Sept., 1932; offg. registrar, high ct., Madras, July, 1929; confd., Oct., 1929; collr. (prov.) and commr. of Coorg, March, 1936; collr. (subst.), Feb., 1938.

FRASER, JAMES DUNCAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 11th Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Haileybury and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 18th Oct., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; a.d.c. to the govt., Nov., 1941; sub-divl. offr., Apl., 1943.

FRASER, SIR STUART MITFORD, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. and Political Dept.—Retd., June, 1920.*

FRASER, WILLIAM GORDON, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Burma) (b. 14th Nov., 1890).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

FRASER-TYTLER, SIR WILLIAM KERR, K.B.E., C.M.G., M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd.,

late Indian Political Service (b. 26th Dec., 1886).

—First commn. dated 24th Aug., 1910; asst. sec., for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1921; sec., Kabul legation, Apr., 1923; asst. to agent, govr.-genl., Madras States, Dec., 1925; asst. commr., Mansehra, March, 1927; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Dec., 1927; and again, Oct., 1930; dep. commr., D.I. Khan, June, 1928; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Oct., 1930; chargé d'affaires, ditto, Apr., 1932; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1933; confd., Sept., 1933; C.M.G., June, 1933; offg. foreign secty. to Govt. of India, May, 1933; offg. pte. secty. to Viceroy, May, 1934; H.B.M.'s minister, Kabul, March, 1935; K.B.E., Jan., 1939; ret'd., Dec., 1941.

FREAK, LESLIE ASHFIELD, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E. Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Punjab) (b. 28th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr. (subst.), March, 1942; chief engr. (subst.) and sec., govt. of Punjab, p.w.d., Apr., 1944.

FREDERICK, AMBULLUR EZEKIEL DAVID, B.A., Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 7th May, 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 6th Sept., 1928; confd., Sept., 1930; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; offg. dist. supt., Jan., 1936; confd., Aug., 1941.

FREDERICK, JESUBATHAM MOSES, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 19th Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 8th July, 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935.

FREELAND, SIR HENRY FRANCIS EDWARD, K.C.I.E., C.B., M.V.O., D.S.O., Maj.-Gen., R.E., late Railway Dept.—Ret'd., July, 1920.*

FREKE, CECIL GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 8th Oct., 1887).—Ret'd., Sept., 1939.*

FRENCH, LESLIE HAROLD VINCENT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 11th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Copthorne Sch., Sussex, Winchester Coll., and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1937; arrived in India, Jan., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Zabul, July, 1941; ret'd., May, 1944.

FRESTON, THOMAS ANTHONY, late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 20th May, 1898).—Ret'd., Sept., 1937.*

FROME, NORMAN FREDERIC, C.I.E., B.Sc. (Eng.), M.I.E.E., D.F.C., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (p.m.g.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1899).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1923, as asst. div. engr.; div. engr., Oct., 1931; dir. Sind and Baluchistan Circle, Nov., 1934; senr. elec. engr., March, 1935; dir. of tels., senr. elec. engr., March, 1935; elec. engr.-in-c., May, 1937; dir. of tels., Apr., 1938; p.m.g., June, 1941; addtl. ch. engr., July, 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

FRY, AUGUSTINE SARGOOD, C.I.E., M.B., Ch.M., F.R.C.S.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (Punjab) (b. 4th Nov., 1890).—First commn., 13th March, 1916; civil surg., Punjab, Apr., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; prof., operative surg., King Edward medl. coll., Lahore, Feb., 1942.

FRY, REV. ERNEST HENRY, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Madras, 16th Oct., 1931; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1943; on mil. duty, May, 1941, to Aug., 1943.

FRY, LESLIE ALFRED CHARLES, M.B.E., Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Apr., 1908).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1927; in mil. employ till Nov., 1933; asst. to A.G.G. in

Rajputana, Nov., 1933; asst. commr., Lucknow, June, 1934; under training in the Govt. of India secretariat, May, 1935; asst. commr., Lucknow, July, 1935; under-secty. to the resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1935; vice-consul Zabul, Dec., 1936; confd., Apr., 1937; under sec. to resdt., Punjab States, Feb., 1939; offcr. on spec. duty and 2nd asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Apr., 1940; asst. pol. agent, Chhatisgarh States, Oct., 1940; under-secty., ext. affairs dept., June, 1941; again, Nov., 1943; M.B.E., Jan., 1944; asst. to resdt., Kashmir, March, 1944.

FULLER, EDWIN GERALD, Burma Commn. (dep. commr.) (b. 16th Jan., 1893).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 20th Oct., 1914; extra asst. commr., March, 1921; addl. dist. magte., July, 1931; dep. commr., Aug., 1934.

FURLEY, HUGH DENYS, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 7th Jan., 1883).—Ret'd., Jan., 1938.*

FURNESS, CECIL ARUNDEL, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 5th March, 1885).—Apptd. to acctt.-genl.'s dept., India Office, 4th Sept., 1919; transfid. to office of high commr. for India, 1st Apr., 1921; clerical officer, Oct., 1921; exec. officer, Apr., 1922.

FURNESS, HORACE GEOFFREY, late Indian Police (b. 11th March, 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1909; asst. supt., July, 1912; I.A.R.O., from 6th Aug., 1916, to 28th Feb., 1919; offg. supt., Jan., 1920, and again, Jan., 1923; confd., Oct., 1926; princpl., police training sch., Vellore, June, 1936; ret'd., March, 1945.

FYFE, WILLIAM, late Dept. of Industries, Madras (b. 25th Jan., 1885).—Apptd. inspr. of industrial schools, 9th June, 1920; ret'd., Nov., 1941.

GABRIEL, SIR EDMUND VIVIAN, C.S.I., C.M.G., C.V.O., C.B.E., V.D., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Ret'd., March, 1920.*

GADRE, NARAYAN BALKRISHNA, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 3rd Dec., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 8th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933; under govt. of India def. dept., Nov., 1939.

GAINSFORD, DONALD, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 18th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 12th June, 1920; offg. supt., Apr., 1926; confd., Oct., 1931; King's police medal, March, 1940; asst. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1942.

GAISFORD, PHILIP, Lieut.-Col., C.I.E., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Nov., 1891).—First commn. dated 18th Jan., 1911; in milty. employ. to 24th June, 1919; asst. commr., June, 1919; sec. to agent to govr.-genl. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Apr., 1925; offg. under-sect. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., March, 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; on foreign service as guardian to H.H. the Maharajah of Gwalior, March, 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., March, 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; dep. secty., pol. dept., Apr., 1937, and again, Sept., 1937; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1937; resdt. at Gwalior and pol. agent for states of Rampur and Benares, Apr., 1938; pol. agent in Bhopal, Sept., 1939; resdt. for Kolhapur and the Deccan States, March, 1940; confd. as resdt., cl. I, Jan., 1944; C.I.E., June, 1942; resdt., Mysore, Oct., 1944.

GAIT, SIR EDWARD ALBERT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E.,

late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Retd., Dec., 1920; member of the Council of India, 20th June, 1922, to 19th June, 1927.*

GALBRAITH, HAROLD ALEXANDER, Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 4th Jan., 1895).—Non-gaz. appts. from Sept., 1914; prom. to I.P., Jan., 1942; Indian Police medal, Jan., 1944.

GALE, BERKELEY, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 5th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

GALE, LUCY, late India Office (b. 16th Jan., 1882).—Appt'd. clerical officer, 1st June, 1921; actg. hr. ditto, July, 1935; confd., Jan., 1936; ret'd., June, 1944.

GALE, U. MAUNG (C1), Indian Police (b. 5th May, 1889).—Appt'd. dist. supt., Burma, on 7th Apl., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1932.

GALE, U. MAUNG (A8), B.A., late Burma Commission (b. 15th June, 1880).—Retd., June, 1935.*

GALES, SIR ROBERT RICHARD, Kt., F.C.H., M.Inst.C.E., M.A.S.C.E., late Railway Dept.—Retd., Oct., 1919.*

GALLAGHER, WILLIAM NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 12th Jan., 1913).—Educ. at Royal Academical Institution, Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 18th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GALLANT, MAURICE NAHUM, O.B.E., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 24th March, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928; for. econ., Oct., 1934; O.B.E., June, 1944.

GALLETTI DI CADILHAC, R. (see Di Cadilhac, R.).

GALLOWAY ARNOLD CRAWSHAW, O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 5th Jan., 1901).—First commn., 24th Dec., 1920; in milty. employ to June, 1928; under-secty. to A.G.G. in the states of Western India, March, 1932; vice-consul, Zahidan, Jan., 1933; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Feb., 1924; secty., ditto, June, 1934; and again, Apl., 1937; secty., British legn., Kabul, Nov., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Bahrein, Nov., 1937; secty. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Feb., 1938; pol. agent, Kuwait, May, 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; consul-genl., Ahwaz, June, 1943; pol. agt., Muscat, Nov., 1944.

GALVIN, JOHN SMART, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Bombay (civil surg.) (b. 4th March, 1892).—First commn., 15th March, 1920; civil surg., March, 1931; offg. presdy. surg., Bombay, Feb., 1935.

GALVIN, PERCY HAROLD, Indian State Ry.s. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 6th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1913, as asst. supt., E.I. rly.; transpn. supt. (traffic comm.), E.I. rly., May, 1934.

GANDASING CHEEMA, SARDAR, D.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (horticulturist to govt. of Bombay) (b. 2nd Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as horticulturist, 13th June, 1921; for. nstr. (in addn.), May, 1944.

GANGA NATH BABU, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., United Provs.; (b. 11th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 19th May, 1908; sub-judge, June, 1923; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1933; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, variously from Oct., 1934; judge, ditto, Apl., 1937; ret'd., 6th Jan., 1943.

GANGARAM KAULA (see Kaula, G.).

GANGULI, ANIL BEHARI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st Dec.,

1907).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1933; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1938; addl. dep. sec., rev. dept., Bengal, May, 1942; dep. sec., rev., Sept., 1942.

GANGULI, JYOTI PRAKASH, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Stores Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1882).—Ret'd., June, 1937.*

GANGULI, SANAT KUMAR, B.L., late Bengal Judl. Service (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Appt'd. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1928; ret'd., Nov., 1941.

GANGULY, KESAV LAL, B.E., B.Sc., Indian State Ry.s. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 20th Feb., 1898).—Joined service, Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., E.B. Rly., Oct., 1923; on spec. duty, posts and air dept., Nov., 1944.

GARBETT, SIR COLIN CAMPBELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.M.G., B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 22nd May, 1881).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; appt'd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 28th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; settlt. offr., Mandi State, 1910; dist. judge, Simla, 1913; lent to mil. dept., 1915; asst. postal censor, Karachi, March, 1915; ditto, Bombay, Sept., 1916; on active service, Mesopotamia, 1916; revenue commr., Mesopotamia, Feb., 1917, and administrator, agric. development (mil.), June, 1917; twice mnt'd. in despatches; C.I.E., Aug., 1917; temp. empld. in pol. dept., India Office, July, 1919, to Aug., 1920; civil sec. to high commr., Mesopotamia, Sept., 1920, to May, 1922; dep. commr., Dec., 1921; C.M.G., June, 1922; ch. sec. to govt., Punjab, Apl., 1931; commr., Multan, March, 1935; C.S.I., June, 1935; finl. commr. (devel.), Dec., 1937; ditto (rev.), Sept., 1939; K.C.I.E., June, 1941; ret'd., Dec., 1941.

GARDINER, REGINALD THOMAS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 27th Apl., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1929; confd., Nov., 1930.

GARDINER, WILLIAM NORMAN, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 1st June, 1882).—Appt'd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 13th Nov., 1901; on mily. service, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; transf'd. to office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, March, 1926; ret'd., Dec., 1942.

GARDNER, JAMES CLARK MOLESWORTH, Indian Forest Service (b. 15th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 11th Dec., 1922; systematic entomologist, Aug., 1923; offg. forest entomologist, F.R.I., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1943.

GARDNER, JOHN WILLIAM BERTRAND, M.B.E., Defence Dept., Govt. of India (b. 13th Aug., 1886).—Ret'd., Apl., 1938.*

GAREWELL, SARDAR JAGDEO SINGH, M.R.C.V.S., I.V.S. (b. 18th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as supt., C.V.D. (Punjab and N.W.F.P.), 30th Jan., 1918; confd., Jan., 1921; princpl., Punjab vet. coll., Aug., 1940.

GARLAND, EDWARD ANDREW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1922, as asst. consvr., Bombay, dep. consvr., Nov., 1925; p.a. to I.G. off. crests, Dehra Dun, Aug., 1938.

GARNIER, CHARLES NEWDIGATE, O.B.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 5th Jan., 1883).—Ret'd., Apl., 1938.*

GARRAD, ERNEST WILLIAM, Indian Police

(b. 9th Apl., 1897) (supt.).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 12th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., July, 1929; addl. asst. inspr.-genl., Simla, June, 1935; supt., June, 1937.

GARRARD, HAROLD SEPTIMUS, India Office (asst. supt. of records) (b. 21st Aug., 1886).—Apptd. clerical officer, 17th Oct., 1921; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1927; asst. supt. of records, Apl., 1939; lent to Min. of Supply.

GARRETT, SIR JOSEPH HUGH, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 22nd June, 1880).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

GARROD, EDGAR SAMUEL, D.S.O., M.C., D.C.M., late Indian Police (N.W.F.P.) (b. 6th March, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 12th Oct., 1923; asst. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Jan., 1925; offg. supt., Punjab, May, 1933; retd., 1940.

GARROD, REV. WILLIAM FRANCIS, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 23rd March, 1893).—Apptd. junior chaplain, 10th Feb., 1930.

GARROD, WILLIAM HENRY EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Bombay) (b. 10th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; cons. pub. health engr. to govt., Nov., 1937; suptg. engr. (prov. subst.), March, 1942; permt., Oct., 1942; confd. as ch. engr., roads and bldgs., and sec. to govt., p.w.d., Apl., 1944.

GARSON, WILLIAM AUBREY, M.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd May, 1895).—Retd., June, 1938.*

GARSTIN, WILLIAM ARTHUR MACDONNELL, C.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 14th May, 1882).—Retd., May, 1937.*

GARVIN, STEPHEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 2nd Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GASH, REV. IVER JOHN, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senior chaplain) (b. 26th May, 1890).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probn., Bengal, 9th Oct., 1928; transferred to Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1929; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1930; transf. to Central Provs., Oct., 1932; senior chaplain, Jan., 1935.

GASKING, REV. CLEMENT AUGUSTINE, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 6th June, 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 12th Oct., 1926; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1928.

GASSON, SIR LIONEL BELL, late Indian Police (b. 9th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., July, 1912; supt., Jan., 1926; dep. commr., March, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; asst. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1930; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; supt., spl. branch, C.I.D., Apl., 1935; cent. intell. offcr., Madras Prov., May, 1938; commr. (confd.), July, 1939; Kt., Jan., 1944; retd., Aug., 1944.

GASTER, VIVIAN ISAAC, B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, principal exec. grade, education dept.) (b. 11th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; on mil. service, Nov., 1914, to Apl., 1917; asst. principal (temp.), Home Office, Oct., 1918, to Feb., 1920; asst. to local adviser to Indian students, London, Feb., 1920; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, as asst. to joint secs., Indian students' dept., Oct., 1920; principal exec. officer, educ. dept.,

Apl., 1925; on loan to Min. of Home Security, May, 1941.

GASTRELL, EVERARD HUDDLESTON, O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Apl., 1898).—First commn. dated 14th Nov., 1916; joined pol. dept., 8th March, 1922; Brit. Consul for Arabistan, July, 1922; vice-consul, Ahwaz, Nov., 1922; on settlement training in Punjab, Nov., 1926; services placed at disposal of ch. commr., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1927; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, March, 1927; vice-consul, Duzdap, Dec., 1930; secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Dec., 1931; and again, Nov., 1933; pol. agent, Bahrein, July, 1933; consul-genl. for Khorasan, March, 1934; claims commr., Baluchistan, July, 1935; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, March, 1936; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Nov., 1936; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Dec., 1936; pol. agent i/c. of the Bolan Pass and Chagai dist., Feb., 1937; O.B.E., May, 1937; sec. to resdt., Central India, March, 1939; census supt., Baluchistan, Dec., 1939; H.B.M.'s consul-genl. in the French Establishments in India, July, 1941; addtl. counsellor, Brit. legation, Tehran, Nov., 1944.

GATELEY, TERENCE PATRICK, M.B.E. (b. 16th Dec., 1898).—Exec. engr., Central Public Works Dept., Oct., 1933; M.B.E., June, 1941.

GATES, SIR FRANK CAMPBELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Barr. (1891), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Retd., Apl., 1914.*

GATFORD, HARRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 6th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1920; resdt. engr., E.I. rly., Apl., 1924; exec. engr. (junr.), E.I. rly., Apl., 1930; ditto (senr.), Nov., 1933.

GAUL, REV. ARTHUR CECIL, M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 20th Oct., 1880).—Retd., Oct., 1935.*

GAUNTLETT, SIR MAGER FREDERIC, K.C.I.E., K.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 12th Oct., 1873).—Retd., May, 1930.*

GAURI, NATH, P.M.S., late Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 20th Sept., 1884).—Retd., July, 1938.*

GAVAGHAN, EDWARD LAWRENCE, late Finance Dept., Govt. of India (b. 18th Dec., 1885).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

GAWAN-TAYLOR, EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magt., Sind) (b. 29th Oct., 1889).—Educ. at Durham Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. magt. and collr., supt. land records, Southern Div., Jan., 1921; on depn. as settlt. officer from Dec., 1921, to May, 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1925; ditto and Talukdari settlt. offr., Feb., 1926; confd., Apl., 1931.

GAWNE, EWAN MOORE, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (advr. to govr., Madras) (b. 26th March, 1889).—Educ. at Wellington, and Brase-nose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 8th Dec., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1919, to Feb., 1921; temp. dep. sec. to govt. of Madras, Apl., 1924, to June, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., July, 1925; confd., Feb., 1932; member, bd. of rev., Jan., 1940; again, Apl., 1944; ch. sec., Aug., 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; liaison offcr., Southern army, Feb., 1943; advr. to govr., Aug., 1944; C.S.I., Jan., 1945.

GAWTHORNE, ARTHUR RICHARDSON, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 12th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr.,

15th, Apr., 1920; exec. engr., Burma Ry., July, 1933.

GEARING, ALFRED HENRY, late Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 23rd March, 1890).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

GEILS, ERNEST DONALD, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 19th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, Oct., 1900; superintendent, Aug., 1932; chief superintendent, Nov., 1938; retd., Oct., 1940.

GELL, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM MONCREIFF, Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 8th Nov., 1917).—Educ. at Wellington and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

GELSON, WILLIAM EDWARD, B.Sc., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.Mech.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ry. (N.W. Ry.) (b. 5th Feb., 1899).—Asst. exec. engr., bridges, Nov., 1926; provl. perm. bridge engr., Oct., 1937; research officer, rly. board, Jan., 1941; retd., Dec., 1943.

GEMMELL, GEOFFREY WOODBURN, Lieut.-Col., I.A. Survey of India (supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1897).—First commn., 15th Nov., 1915; asst., supt., geodetic bch., May, 1927; supt., Apl. 1930.

GENNINGS, JOHN FREDERICK, C.I.E., C.B.E., Barr. (b. 21st Sept., 1885).—Joined the service as dir. of information (temp.), 26th Dec., 1920; confd., 10th Oct., 1924; member, legis. council, Bombay, 1932; commr. of labour, Bombay, Apl., 1933; delegate of Govt. of India to internatl. lab. conf., Geneva, 1933; presdnt., appellate ct., Dec., 1933; C.B.E., June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; retd., Sept., 1940.

GENTLE, FREDERICK WILLIAM (judge, high ct., Calcutta).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1936; judge, high ct., Calcutta, June, 1941.

GENTLE, JOSEPH ALFRED HECTOR ROBERTS, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 26th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 2nd Jan., 1925; dep. consvr., Jan., 1929.

GENTRY, EDGAR CHARLES (b. 10th Aug., 1893).—Architect, Central Public Works Dept., Oct., 1933.

GEORGE, FRANK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 9th Feb., 1907).—Educ. at Priory Sch., Shrewsbury, and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; secty., finl. commr., Sept., 1938; settlement offcr., Nov., 1938.

GEORGE, HUGH SHAW, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 29th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 6th Dec., 1915; on mil. duty from 9th Aug., 1917, to 8th March, 1919; dep. consvr., Nov., 1921; sylviculturist, C.P., Oct., 1932; consvr. (confd.), Jan., 1939; ch. consvr., Jan., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

GEORGE, JAMES, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 17th May, 1905).—Educ. at Priory County Sch., Shrewsbury, Queen's Coll., Cambridge, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Apl., 1931; offg. under-secty. to govt., Bengal, pol. and appt. depts., Sept., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936.

GEORGESON, WILLIAM WARES, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 13th March,

1906).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Jan., 1933; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), Aug., 1943.

GERAGHTY, MICHAEL JOSEPH, Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 19th Dec., 1929; mil. duty Nov., 1940.

GERRARD, FREDERICK WERNHAM, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 25th Nov., 1887).—Retd., Dec., 1933.*

GERRARD, MISS JESSIE MARGARET, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Lady Willingdon training coll., Madras) (b. 9th Jan., 1892).—Apptd. profr., Queen Mary's Coll., Madras, 8th Aug., 1921; princpl., Lady Willingdon trng. coll., Dec., 1925; confd., Jan., 1927.

GHAFFUR, ABDUL (see Abdul Ghaffur).

GHANSHYAM, DAS, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., late Punjab C.S. (b. 10th Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

GHARPUREY, H. G. (see Hari Ganpat Rao Gharpurey).

GHARPUREY, KHANDU GANPAT RAO, B.A. (Cal.), L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 20th July, 1880).—Retd., July, 1935.*

GHATAK, LABANYA MOHAN, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 2nd Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as an offcr., cl. II, May, 1923; dep. mil. acct. genl. (jnr.), March, 1935; ditto (senr.), Apl., 1936; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Apl., 1936; revtd. to mil. accts. dep., July, 1939; prom. cl. I (prov. subs.), June, 1942.

GHATAK, NARENDRANATH, M.B.E., Barr., Judi. Dept., Bengal (b. Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as master and official referee, high ct., original side, Calcutta, 22nd Nov., 1922.

GHATE, BAL GANGADHAR, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 27th Apl., 1928.

GHATE, NAMDEV SITARAMPANT, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 4th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

GHAZANFAR ALI NAQVI, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 17th Apl., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 27th May, 1926; offg. supt., March, 1933; confd., March, 1940.

GHOLAP, LAKSHMAN TRIMBAK, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 4th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay, as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. pol. agent, Jawhar, March, 1933; collr. and dist. magte., Dec., 1936; dep. sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1941; controller of Ind. shipping, Bombay, March, 1944.

GHOSAL, SIR JOSNA, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 13th June, 1871).—Retd., Jan., 1931.*

GHOSE, BIRENDRA NATH, Indian State Ry. (transpn. traffic-commcl. supt., E.I. Ry.) (b. 12th Aug., 1900).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1919; asst. supt., June, 1922; senr. scale (provl.), Feb., 1938; confd., Nov., 1938.

GHOSE, SIR SARAI KUMAR, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 3rd July, 1879).—Retd., July, 1939.*

GHOSH, AJIT KUMAR, Indian Police (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 3rd March, 1917).—Apptd.

asst. supt. on probn., Feb., 1940; confd. March, 1944.

GHOSH, AJOY KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Apl., 1908).—Educ. at King's Coll. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Nov., 1932; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1935; sec. bd. of rev., March, 1940; addl. dist. mag., Nov., 1940; addl. dep. sec., rev. dept., Sept., 1942; ditto and addl. dep. dir., civ. supplies in addn., Apl., 1943.

GHOSH CHAUDHURI, HARI SADHAN, B.Sc., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 11th March, 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; confd. supt., June, 1942; dep. comm. police, Calcutta, July, 1942.

GHOSH CHAUDHURI, TAPENDRA KUMAR, Rai Sahib, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as proby. dep. supt., Jan., 1911; confd., Jan., 1913; addl. supt., Dec., 1918; offg. supt., Apl., 1919; confd., May, 1927; retd., Jan., 1942.

GHOSH, JOTISH CHANDRA, Indian Police (asst., supt., Orissa) (b. 28th Nov., 1912).—Apptd. on probn., Jan., 1937; confd., 15th Dec., 1937; transfed. to Orissa, June, 1940.

GHOSH, J. M., Barr., Judl. Dept., Bengal (regr. in insolvency, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 16th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 14th May, 1921; asst. regr., high ct., Calcutta, original side, Nov., 1921; dep. regr., ditto, Dec., 1931; offg. regr., in insolvency, original side, Nov., 1932.

GHOSH, MAHIM CHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), B.A. (Calc.), late Indian C.S. (b. 20th Jan., 1880).—Educ. at Santosh Jahnawi Sch., Bengal, Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1911; retransf. to Bengal, Apl., 1912; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1916; offg. sec. to govt., judl. dept., Nov., 1927; confd., March, 1928; offg. judge, Calcutta high ct., May, 1930; and again, Nov., 1931; confd., Nov., 1932; retd., Jan., 1940.

GHOSH, NAGENDRA NATH, late Inc. Tax. Dept. (b. 8th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1902; inc. tax officer, Oct., 1927; asst. commr., Bihar, Nov., 1933; retd., 8th July, 1940.

GHOSH, NIBARAN CHANDRA, B.A., Indian State Rys. (ch. operating supt., E.I. Ry.) (b. 21st Dec., 1890).—Apptd. to the service, 20th March, 1913; asst. supt., E.I. rly., Jan., 1916; supt., Apl., 1928; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., variously, from Apl., 1934; confd., March, 1938; ch. operating supt., Aug., 1941.

GHOSH, SACHINDRA MOHAN, B.A., Indian Police (b. 1st Apl., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st March, 1935; confd., March, 1937.

GHOSH, SHOTTYENDRA KUMAR, Indian C.S. (contr. of emigrant labour, govt. of India) (b. 21st May, 1896).—Educ. at Scottish Churches Coll., Calcutta; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1927; addl. dist. mag., Apl., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1928; confd., Dec., 1933; contr. of emigrant labour, govt. of India, Nov., 1937.

GHOSH, SUKHENDRA NATH, B.A., B.Sc.

(Glas.), M.Inst.C.E., F.R.San.I., M.I.E. (Ind.), Public Health Dept., Bengal (b. 16th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. asst. sanitary engineer, Bengal, Nov., 1918; executive engineer, public health dept., 1921; chief engr., public health dept., 1939.

GHOSH, TARAK NATH, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 2nd Oct., 1914).—Apptd. on probn., Jan., 1937; confd., 15th Dec., 1937.

GHOSHAL, UMESH KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Hindu Sch., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1938; emptl. advr. to govt., June, 1941; asst. contr. A.R.P., Dec., 1942; dep. ditto, Apl., 1944.

GHULAM ABBAS, O.B.E., B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 17th May, 1902).—Joined the service, cl. II, March, 1925; asst. mil. acct.-genl., Apl., 1933; secty. Quetta Rec. Cttee., Nov., 1935; dep. mil. acct.-genl. (junr.), Oct., 1936; addl. dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1939; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Aug., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

GHULAM AHMAD, MIAN, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Police (asst. dir., intell. bureau) (b. 12th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 29th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., June, 1928; and again, Oct., 1932; confd., Apl., 1934; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., variously from Feb., 1934; asst. dir., intell. bureau, home dept., June, 1939 (confd., Oct., 1939), to Jan., 1942; dep. dir., ditto, Feb., 1942; O.B.E., June, 1943.

GHULAM AHMED, MADANI, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd March, 1913).—Educ. at Faiz-I-am High Sch., Meerut, and Aligarh Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

GHULAM MUHAMMAD, C.I.E., M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (finance officer, communications) (b. 29th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service, 7th March, 1920; asst. audit officer, O. & R. rly., Jan., 1921; govt. exr. of accts., B. & N.W. rlys., Jan., 1925; services placed at disposal of rly. board, Nov., 1925; on foreign service under Bhopal state, June, 1932, to Feb., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., March, 1934; offg. dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., finance, May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. finance officer, posts and tels., July, 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; fin. offcr., communications, Oct., 1937; ch. contr. of stores, March, 1940; contr.-genl. of purchase, Sept., 1940; addl. sec. to dept. of supply, March, 1941; C.I.E., June, 1941; on for. service, Hyderabad, May, 1942.

GIBBS, WALTER FELIX, Indian Police (b. 12th Apl., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., Dec., 1940.

GIBLING, MORLEY THEODORE, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Sind) (b. 12th March, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931; secty., cent. bd. of irrign., Simla, May, 1935, to Apl., 1939; suptg. engr. (confd.), Dec., 1943.

GIBSON, SIR EDMUND CURREY, K.C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 6th July, 1886).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London, St. John's Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.;

employed temp. under govt. of India; for. and pol. dept., from June, 1915; tutor and guardian to H.H. the Maharaja of Bharatpur; asst. pol. agent, E. Rajputana States (in addn.), Sept., 1917; addl. asst. master, Mayo Coll., March, 1918; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Aprl., 1918; dep. commr., Oct., 1923; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1924; pol. agent, Eastern States of Rajputana, March, 1925; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Aprl., 1927; offg. resdt. and agent to govr.-genl., Eastern States, Aprl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1933; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Nov., 1934; resdt., cl. II, Aug., 1935; resdt. for States of W.I., Oct., 1937; confd. as resdt., cl. I, May, 1939; K.C.I.E., June, 1941; empd. (temp.) as China relations offcr., Calcutta.

GIBSON, JOHN CARRINGTON, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 8th March, 1912; dist. loco. supt., Aprl., 1918; supt., E.I. rly., July, 1925; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), E.I. rly., Jan., 1936; divl. supt., Oct., 1938; ret'd., 5th Dec., 1943.

GIBSON, JOHN PERCIVAL, M.A., India Office (actg. asst. sec., pol. dept. (b. 2nd June, 1898).—Educ. at Royal Academ. Inst., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. asst. princpl., mil. dept., 19th Oct., 1921; transfed. to commerce and revenue dept., June, 1923, and to pol. dept., Aprl., 1924; resident clerk, Oct., 1924; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. under-sec. of state, Dec., 1928; princpl., Aprl., 1930; actg. asst. sec., pol. dept., July, 1941.

GIBSON, RAYMOND EVELYN, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Oct., 1878).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1902, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and registrn., Sind, Jan. to Aprl., 1906, and from Aprl., 1907; colonisation offr., Jamrao Canal, Dec., 1909, to Oct., 1910; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindhi translator to govt., Jan., 1911; pte. sec. to govr., Bombay, Oct., 1912, to March, 1914; collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1922; actg. commr. in Sind, Aprl., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; member of legisl. council, 1924; commr., Jan., 1932; rev. commr. for Sind, Aprl., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1936; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

GIDNEY, SIR CLAUDE HENRY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Haileybury, Hertford Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; held various temp. appts. under govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., from Oct., 1914; pol. offr. (on probn.), and asst. pol. agent, Quetta, Jan., 1918; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent, Kalat, Sept., 1918; pol. agent, Sibi, Nov., 1919; second asst. to agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Feb., 1920; sec. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Aprl., 1921; dep. commr., Bannu, Dec., 1923; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1926; secty. to ch. commr. and agent to govr.-genl., N.W.F.P., May, 1928; dep. commr., Bannu, May, 1930; secty. to ch. commr. and agent to govr.-genl., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1930; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., June, 1932; mem., exec. council of govr., N.W.F.P., Aprl. and Nov., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1932; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1934; member, govr.'s exec. council, N.W.F.P., May, 1936; resdt., Hyderabad Aprl., 1937; C.S.I.,

May, 1937; resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1938; confd. as resdt., cl. I, March, 1941; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1942; ret'd., 27th Sep., 1944.

GIDWANI, VISHINO LAKHIMAL, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at N.J. High Sch., Karachi, and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GILBERT, CHARLES FREDERICK, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 25th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Feb., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Aprl., 1923; confd., Oct., 1923; offg. senr. supt., Nov., 1932; offg. dep. agent, R.I. rly., March, 1935; confd., Jan., 1936; ret'd., Oct., 1940.

GILBERT, LEONARD BURGES, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 20th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., Aprl., 1933; offg. consulting engr. to the govt. of India (roads), July, 1936; supt. engr., June, 1939; offg. ch. engr., United Provs., March, 1939; confd., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; ret'd., 20th July, 1942.

GILCHRIST, ROBERT NIVEN, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 13th Aprl., 1888).—Apptd. profr., Presy. Coll., Calcutta, 1911; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, 1914; princpl., Krishnagar Coll., 1916; contr., labour bureau, govt. of India, 1921; labour intellige. offcr., Bengal, and dep. sec., commerce dept., 1922; jt. secty. to govt., commerce and marine depts., July, 1930; labour commr., Bengal, March, 1932; C.I.E., June, 1934; reforms commr., Oct., 1934; ret'd., June, 1941; served in Burma Office from Feb., 1940; posted to India Office (pol. dept.); author of 'Indian Nationality,' 'Principles of Political Science,' 'Conciliation and Arbitration.'

GILDEA, ERIC PERCIVAL, Indian State Rlys. (late N.W. Rly.) (b. 9th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Aprl., 1916; temp. asst. loco. supt., Sept., 1916, to Oct., 1919; confd., Nov., 1919; asst. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; offg. variously as divl. rolling stock officer from Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1927; ret'd., 7th May, 1944.

GILL, CLIFFORD ALLCHIN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 12th Aug., 1878).—Ret'd., Aug., 1939.*

GILL, FRANK FREDERICK PERCY, Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 2nd Aprl., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 18th Nov., 1925; confd., Sept., 1929; asst. supt., India home intell., Aprl., 1931, to Feb., 1933; tranf. to Orissa, Oct., 1937; supt. (confd.), March, 1938.

GILL, GURDIAL SINGH, O.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Madras (inspr. genl. of prisons) (b. 21st March, 1896).—Date of first commn., 28th Aug., 1926; offg. supt., jail dept., Nov., 1931; confd., Aprl., 1932; inspr. genl. of prisons and ch. inspr. certified schools, June, 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1942.

GILL, HARRY ANDERSON CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (b. 12th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Bradford and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 10th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1935; under-secty., finance dept., govt. of India, Aprl., 1937; dep. ditto, Aprl., 1938, to Oct., 1939; collr. of cent. excise, Oct., 1942.

GILLAN, SIR GEORGE VAN BAERLE, K.C.I.E.,

Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 3rd Sept., 1890).—First commn. dated 19th Jan., 1912; in milty. employ to 31st Dec., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec. to ditto, Nov., 1924; H.B.M.'s consul-genl., Khasgar, July, 1925; 1st asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Nov., 1928; pol. agent, Gilgit, Sept., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; secty. to A.G.G., Punjab states, Aug., 1935; resdt., W. Rajputana states, Apr., 1937; resdt., Jaipur (addtl. charge), Dec., 1937; resdt., Western Rajputana states, March, 1938; resdt. in Mewar and pol. agent, Southern Rajputana states (addl. charge), Apr., 1938; resdt. for Baroda and Gujarat states, Apr., 1939; resdt. at Gwalior and for the states of Rampur and Benares, Feb., 1940; K.C.I.E., June, 1944; confd. as resdt., cl. I, and resdt. for Rajputana, Jan., 1944.

GILLESPIE, JAMES ROSS, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 5th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Dunfermline High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; a.d.c. to the govr., Aug., 1940; served under cent. govt. from 1942 to 1944.

GILLIAT, ALGERNON EARLE, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 7th Dec., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., rev. sec.'s dept., Apr., 1913; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Apr., 1916; offg. dep. commr., Apr., 1921; offg. sec. to govt., fin. and rev. dep., May, 1923; dep. commr., Sept., 1924; ditto and addl. sec., fin. and rev. dept., Dec., 1924; sec. to govt. fin. dept., May, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1928; offg. commr., May, 1932; confd., Jan., 1936; confd. fin. commr., Feb., 1941; retd., Dec., 1943.

GILLIES, PETER, L.D.S. (b. 22nd May, 1897).—Apptd. dental surg. and supt., Punjab dental hosp., Lahore, on probn., 19th March, 1930; confd., Sept., 1932.

GILLIGAN, WILLIAM BOYD, late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Apr., 1893).—Educ. at High Sch., Dublin, R.M.C., Sandhurst, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 20th Feb., 1922; arrived, 10th Apr., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1926; offg. criminal tribes settlement officer, Bombay Presdy., Nov., 1927; labour officer, Bombay, Oct., 1934; collr. and mag., July, 1935; backward class officer, Nov., 1937; retd., June, 1940.

GILMORE, EDWARD FITZGERALD GELSTON, B.Sc. (Hons.), M.I.Mech.E. (b. 25th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 12th Feb., 1924, as physicist, Govt. Test House, Alipore; supt., Govt. Test House (provl.), Nov., 1934; subst., March, 1938; dep. dir. of scientific and industrial research, June, 1940.

GIMSON, CHRISTOPHER, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 24th Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Oundle and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. commr.; transf. to Assam, Apr., 1912; settlt. offr., July, 1914; mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Feb., 1918, to Jan., 1919; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, Feb., Edg., to Nov., 1921; offg. depy. commr., March,

1923; confd., Apr., 1928; pol. agent, Manipur, Feb., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

GITA RAM, GARG, RAI BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 8th Sept., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apr., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; suptg. engr. (perm.), May, 1943; on spec. duty, Punjab, irrig. sectt., Jan., 1944.

GLADDING, DONALD, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Aug., 1888).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., Southwark, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty, May to June, 1918; under-sec., govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., Oct., 1918; ditto, pol. and appt. dept., Dec., 1918; industrial intelligence officer, Bengal, July, 1920; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1923; labour intelligence officer, Bengal, Jan., 1925; offg. dep. sec., fin. dept., Nov., 1925; confd., Feb., 1927; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1928; mag. and collr., Bengal, July, 1928; dep. secty. to govt. of India, home dept., June, 1931; secty. to govt., finance, commerce and marine depts., Bengal, July, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1936; retd., July, 1939.

GLAISBY, ARTHUR GEOFFREY TINDALL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. contr. stores, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 12th Apr., 1890).—Joined the service, 12th Sept., 1914; asst. engr., G.I.P. rly., Apr., 1917; resdt. engr., Sept., 1921; liaison officer, G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1934; offg. dep. contr. of stores, Nov., 1935; offg. dist. contr. of stores, March, 1936; dep. agent, styled dep. gen. manager (works), May, 1937; dep. contr. stores, G.I.P. Rly., June, 1939.

GLANCY, SIR BERTRAND JAMES, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (govr. of Punjab) (b. 31st Dec., 1882).—Educ. at Monmouth Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and pol. asst.; tempy. under-sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., March to Nov., 1913; asst. resdt., Mewar, May, 1914; 1st asst. to the agent to the govr.-genl. in Rajputana, March, 1915; 1st asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Dec., 1918; dep. sec. to govt. of India, pol. dept. Oct., 1921, and again, Apr., 1927; employed under Kashmir durbar from Nov., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; offg. pol. secty. to govt. of India, June, 1928; offg. agent to govr.-genl., Punjab states, Apr., 1929; in foreign service as president, council of state, Jaipur, Oct., 1929; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Punjab states, Apr., 1932; offg. pol. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., July, 1932, and again, July, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; resdt. and A.G.G. in C.I., June, 1933; member, council of state, variously from July, 1933; secty., Chamber of Princes, Oct., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1935; secty. to H.E. the Crown Representative, Apr., 1937; political adviser to H.E. the Crown Representative (tempy.), Dec., 1938; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1940; govr. of the Punjab, Apr., 1941.

GLASS, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 29th Dec., 1887).—Held non-gazetted appt. from Aug., 1906, to Dec., 1918; offg. asst. accts. officer, posts and tels., Feb., 1919; confd., Dec., 1919; dep. acct. genl., ditto, Aug., 1927; examr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Feb., 1935; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Apr., 1935; attd. officer, office of the dep. acct.-genl., posts and

tels., Madras, Feb., 1937; on for. service as comptr. of posts. and tel. accts., Burma, Apl., 1937; comptr., Assam., Nov., 1939.

GLASS, LESLIE CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 28th May, 1911).—Educ. at Bradfield, Trinity Coll., Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, Burma oilfields, Sept., 1937; asst. settlement offr., Feb., 1939; offg. settlement offr., Sept., 1940.

GLEDHILL, ALAN, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 26th Oct., 1895).—Educ. at Rugby Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; offg. ditto, March, 1928; confd., June, 1928.

GLENN, JOHN ARTHUR GOULD, Indian Police (b. 31st July, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Dec., 1937, confd.; a.d.c. to govr., Apl., 1941.

GLENNIE, EDWARD AUBREY, C.I.E., D.S.O., Brig., R.E., Survey of India (dir.) (b. 18th July, 1889).—First commn., 23rd July, 1910; asst. supt. (proby.), 20th Dec., 1919; confd., Dec., 1921; supt., May, 1928; offg. dir., Nov., 1936; confd., March, 1940; C.I.E., June, 1940.

GLOVER, SIR HAROLD MATTHEW, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 29th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Apl., 1912; offg. consvr., Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1929; served in United Provs., Oct., 1932; posted as consvr., Punjab, June, 1933; Kt., Jan., 1942; retd., 30th Jan., 1944.

GLOVER, JOHN WILLIAM THOMSON, C.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 30th July, 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, 10th Apl., 1914; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir for Leh, July, 1919; pol. agent, Wana, March, 1923; O.B.E., June, 1924; asst. commr. Thal, Apl., 1926; dep. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1926; pol. agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Oct., 1928; C.B.E., June, 1931; pol. agent, Khyber, Aug., 1931; consul-genl. at Kashgar, Nov., 1933; resdt., Kashmir, Oct., 1937; retd., July, 1940.

GOBIND NARAYAN PAREEKH, B.A., Indian Police (b. 10th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 21st Jan., 1929; supt. (confd.), June, 1942.

GODBOLE, YESHWANT ANANT, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (advr. to govr., Bihar) (b. 4th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 24th Nov., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Apl., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; confd., Dec., 1928; regr. of co-op. societies, Bihar and Orissa, March, 1934; offg. dir. of industries in addn., March, 1935; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bihar, Nov., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; commr., Apl., 1941; advr. to govr., March, 1944; C.S.I., June, 1944.

GODFREY, REGINALD WILLIAM, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 10th March, 1899).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 15th March, 1921; confd., March, 1923; offg. supt., Apl., 1930; confd., June, 1930; pol. offcr., Sadiya fron. tract, Apl., 1938; sec. to govr., Oct., 1943.

GODWIN, CHARLES, Office of High Commr.

for India (exec. officer, store dept. (b. 3rd Sept., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, store depot, 2nd May, 1904; transfld. to office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; actg. higher exec. offr., Sept., 1939.

GOFF, GEORGE HERBERT, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 25th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1910; dist. traffic supt., E.B. rly., March, 1931; retd., Apl., 1940.

GOIL, DWARKA PRASAD, M.B., F.R.C.S. (Edin.), K.H.P., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S., retd. (b. 1st Aug., 1879).—Retd., March, 1938.*

GOKHALE, BHALCHANDRA KRISHNA, C.I.E., M.A., India C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 23rd July, 1892).—Educ. at Bombay and Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 15th Dec., 1915; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto and asst. settlmt. officer, Nov., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1927; secty. to govt. educn. and dev. dept., March, 1929; mag. and collr. (provl. sub.), May, 1930; confd., Dec., 1931; secty., fin. dept., Bihar, May, 1939; commr. of excise, etc., Dec., 1942, to Apl., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

GOKHALE, RAO BAHADUR GANESH HARI, B.A., LL.B., late Central Provs. C.S. (b. July, 1881).—Joined the service as munisf, 2nd Apl., 1906; sub-judge, Nov., 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1933; confd., Apl., 1933; retd., Oct., 1936; member, jt. public serv. commn., Bihar, C.P., and Orissa, May, 1937; retd.

GOKHALE, VINAYAK GOPAL, L.Ag. (Bom.), late Indian Agric. Service (b. 10th March, 1883).—Retd., March, 1938.*

GOLDNEY, ALFRED PEEL, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th Jan., 1878).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

GOLDSWORTHY, FRANK TRUSCOTT, India Office (exec. officer, acctnt-genl.'s dept.) (b. 11th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Bristol Gr. Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 25th Feb., 1913; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; lent to Admiralty, June, 1940.

GOLDSWORTHY, LEONARD JAMES, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 12th Dec., 1890).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

GOLLAN, DONALD WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 16th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; suptg. engr., Feb., 1939; retd., Sept., 1941.

GOODALL, J. W. D., Capt., M.D., Ch.B. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (Edin.), I.M.S. (civil surgeon, Bengal) (b. 10th Dec., 1909).—Date of first commn., Feb., 1934; civil surgeon, Sept., 1938.

GOODCHILD, WILLIAM BADEN, late India Office (b. 19th May, 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 22nd March, 1920; higher clerical officer, May, 1928; resdt. clerk, Dec., 1928, to July, 1934; asst. secty., Indian delegations to Assembly of League of Nations, 1928 to 1934; asst. secty., Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; actg. pte. secty. to permit under-secty. of state, July to Oct., 1935; asst. princpl., Nov., 1935; posted to Burma Office, Apl., 1937; pte. secty. to parly. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Jan., 1939; actg. princpl., Jan., 1940; retd., Jan., 1945.

GOODERSON, RICHARD NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 3rd March, 1915).—Educ. at Northampton Town and County Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., B.

7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; a.r.p. offr., Apl., 1942; judge, small cause ct., Simla, Feb., 1944.

GOODMAN, REGINALD TERENCE WYNNE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., N.W.F.P.) (b. 26th July, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 20th Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; dep. transp. contr., Sept., 1943.

GOODWYN, HAROLD PETER, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., home dept., Bengal) (b. 19th July, 1913).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Peterhouse, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1938; special officer, home dept., Aug., 1939; under-secty., home dept., Sept., 1939; confd., May, 1940; dep. sec. to govt., Feb., 1944.

GOPAL, NAND (see Nam Gopal).

GOPAL, RAM (see Ram Gopal).

GOPALASWAMI AYYANGAR, NARASIMHA, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., B.L., Diwan Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 31st March, 1882).—Retd., March, 1937.*

GOPALAKRISHNA RAO, BELTHANGADI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st June, 1909); educ. at Board High Sch., Puttur, University of Madras, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1935.

GOPALAKRISHNAN, PANANGATTI ANANTHANARAYANAIYER, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 20th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at Raja's High Sch., Kollengode, South Malabar, Madras Univ., University Coll., London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1935; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1941; spec. press advr. to Bengal govt., Apl., 1942.

GOPALASWAMI RAMASWAMI AYYANGAR, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sub collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., March, 1930; jt. sec., board of rev., Madras, Feb., 1939; sec. to the National Defence Council, Aug., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

GORDON, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr. and secty. to govt., p.w.d., Sind) (b. 15th June, 1880).—Joined as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; suptg. engr. (subst. temp.), Jan., 1927; ditto (prov. perm.), Dec., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; ch. engr. and secty. to govt., p.w.d., Sind., May, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

GORDON, SIR ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 14th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 5th Dec., 1907; supt., Oct., 1915; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Nov., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta, Apl., 1935; officer, Order of St. John, June, 1941; Kt., Jan., 1943; ret'd., 14th Apl., 1943.

GORDON, CHARLES HOWARD, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Dec., 1916).—Educ. at Oundle and Trinity Hall Coll., Cam-

bridge; apptd. on probn., 11th Oct., 1938; confd., 7th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1941; pte. sec. to govt., Jan., 1941; addl. dist. mag., Apl., 1944; mag. and collr., Aug., 1944.

GORDON, SIR EYRE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Feb., 1884).—Educ. at Rossal and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1912, to July, 1915; settl. offr., Jan., 1916; dep. commr. (prov.), May, 1921; ditto, and rev. sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., June, 1921; commr. of settls., dir. of land records, I.G. of reg., regr.-genl. and sec. to govt., Apl., 1926; offg. commr., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Dec., 1929; confd., Aug., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; temp. member, govt.'s exec. council, Central Provs., May, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1935; chairman, fed. public services commn., Oct., 1936; Kt., Jan., 1939; ret'd., Nov., 1943.

GORDON, GERALD WATSON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Assam) (b. 31st Oct., 1902).—Joined the service, 27th Oct., 1925, as asst. exec. engr.; under-secty., p.w.d., Assam, June, 1937.

GORDON, JAMES, M.B.E., India Office (actg. princpl., E. and O. dept.) (b. 27th Dec., 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Oct., 1921; M.B.E., June, 1934; actg. exec. officer, Nov., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; staff officer for services questions, July, 1939; actg. supt., tel. and mails branch., Aug., 1943; actg. princpl., econ. and overseas dept., July, 1944.

GORDON, JOHN DE LA HAY, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 30th March, 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; in mil. employ till Dec., 1911, when he was apptd. asst. pol. agent (on probn.); asst. resdt., Aden, Apl., 1913; mil. govr., Basra, Sept., 1916, to Sept., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1918; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar states, Jan., 1925; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Dec., 1927; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Nov., 1931; pol. agent, eastern Kathiawar agency, March, 1932; offg. A.G.G., Deccan states, Aug., 1935; offg. A.G.G., states of W.I., June, 1936; dep. A.G.G., states of W.I., Nov., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; offg. resdt., Mysore and chief commr., Coorg, Dec., 1937; confd. as resdt., cl. I, and resdt. in Mysore, Jan., 1939; C.S.I., June, 1941; ret'd., Apl., 1942.

GORDON, NORMAN HORATIUS, B.A., late United Provs. C.S. (b. 27th Dec., 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

GORDON, RONALD EAGLESON, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (govt. inspr. of rlys.) (b. 17th Jan., 1889).—First commn. dated 23rd Dec., 1900; asst. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., June, 1921; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), N.W. rly., Dec., 1932; and again, March, 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), Nov., 1936; provl. perm. dep. ch. engr., May, 1939; transfd. to posts and air dept. as govt. inspr. of rlys., May, 1941.

GORMAN, JAMES HAMILTON, M.B., B.Ch., D.P.H., Maj., I.M.S. (b. 29th Jan., 1904).—First commn. dated 4th Feb., 1930; in mil. employ to 6th Feb., 1939, when he joined the Port Health Dept.; port health officer, Calcutta, March, 1939; in temp. mil. employ from Sept., 1941.

GORRIE, REV. LESLIE MANIFOLD, Indian

Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 21st March, 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 18th Apl., 1919; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1921; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1929.

GORRIE, ROBERT MACLAGAN, D.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 26th June, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Sept., 1930; served under def. dept., Nov., 1939; timber advr. to contr. genl. of inspn.

GORWALA, ASTAD DINSHAW, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., govt. of Bombay) (b. 7th Dec., 1900).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Cambridge Univ. (Non-Coll.); joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 5th Dec., 1924, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1928; offg. asst. rev. officer, Lloyd barr. and C.S., Nov., 1931; dep. secty., govt. of India, communications dept., Nov., 1937, and again, July, 1938; on spec. duty, Sind, May, 1938; offg. secty., govt. of India, communications dept., March, 1939; dep. secty., govt. of India, communications dept., May, 1939; sec. to govt., Bombay (fin.), Oct., 1941; supply commr. in addn., Sept., 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

GOSCHEN, VISCOUNT, Rt. Hon. GEORGE JOACHIM GOSCHEN, OF HAWKHURST, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., C.B.E.—Govt. of Madras, 14th Apl., 1924, to 11th Nov., 1929; actg. govr. genl. of India, June to Oct., 1929; P.C., Feb., 1930.*

GOSS, EDWARD SLADE, M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 25th Sept., 1883).—Ret'd., Sept., 1938.*

GOSSET, HUGH HENRY EYRE, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., ret'd., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 3rd Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1912; asst. engr., July, 1913; on milty. duty from 21st Oct., 1914; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1932; provl. permnt., Apl., 1938; govt. inspr. of rlys., May, 1941; ret'd., 3rd Feb., 1944.

GOSWAMI, UPENDRA LAL, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 5th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; joined the service, 24th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.; depy. regr., genl. dept., high ct., Rangoon, Apl., 1941.

GOULD, ALFRED JOHN, B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Burma) (b. 28th Feb., 1900).—Ret'd., Jan., 1939.*

GOULD, SIR BASIL JOHN, C.M.G., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 29th Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 29th Nov., 1907, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and pol. asst.; asst. to agent to govr.-genl. in Central India, May, 1909; asst. sec. to govt. of India for dept., June, 1910; special duty in for. dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1910; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., Oct., 1910; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse, May, 1912; offg. pol. offr., Sikkim, Oct., 1913; asst. commr., Nowshera, Oct., 1914; ditto, and asst. commr., Charsada, May, 1915; o dist. judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1916; asst. cmmr., Mardan, May, 1917; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, Sept., 1917; on spl. duty under orders of H.B.M.'s Consul, Seistan, from May, 1918; H.B.M.'s Consul for Seistan and Kain, Nov., 1918; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, Apl., 1920; C.I.E., Sept., 1921; first asst. to resident in Kashmir, Dec., 1925; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, June, 1926; and again, June, 1928; chargé d'affaires, Kabul, March, 1927;

C.M.G., June, 1929; pol. agent, Kurram, Nov., 1929; ditto, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Jan., 1931; offg. resdt., Waziristan, Oct., 1931; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1933; and again, Dec., 1934; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, July, 1934; pol. officer, Sikkim, Dec., 1935; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse (in addn.), Aug., 1944; Kt., June, 1941.

GOULD, HERBERT ROSS, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 17th Apl., 1887).—Ret'd., Dec., 1936.*

GOVINDA MENON, A. V., Rao Bahadur, Barr., late Madras C.S. (Judl. Branch) (b. 31st Jan., 1882).—Ret'd., Jan., 1937.*

GOVINDARAJA AYYANGAR, NARASIMHA, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., P.W.D., Madras) (b. 10th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 16th June, 1914; asst. engr., July, 1917; exec. engr., June, 1924; dep. ch. engr., bldgs. and roads, Dec., 1935; suptg. engr., Dec., 1940; ch. engr., March, 1944.

GOVIND DINANATH MADGAONKAR (see Madgaonkar, G. D.)

GOW, PETER FLEMING, Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (b. 28th June, 1885).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1912, apptd. prof. midwifery, Medl. Coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1932; ret'd., June, 1940.

GOYDER, CECIL WILLIAM, B.Sc., A.C.G.I., All-India Radio (chief engr.) (b. 1st Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as chief engr., 20th Aug., 1936.

GRABHAM, REGINALD GEORGE DENIS, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 23rd Dec., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1926; asst. commr., Port Blair, Dec., 1930; supt., May, 1935; addtl. asst. to I.G., Feb., 1937; supt. of dist. pol., Ajmer-Merwara, Aug., 1940.

GRACE, OLIVER GILBERT, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Police (N.W.F.P.) (b. 10th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1920; asst. supt., Feb., 1922; supt., June, 1925; dist. officer, frontier constabulary, N.W.F.P., Jan., 1929; comdt., ditto, Dec., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

GRACIE, CLEMENT FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 29th June, 1907).—Educ. at King Edward VII Sch., Sheffield, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, July, 1936, to Dec., 1936; on milty. service, Nov., 1940.

GRAHAM, CYRIL GEORGE, Indian State Rlys. (ch. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 28th May, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Nov., 1914; exec. engr., May, 1933; offg. dep. agent, G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1935; offg. dep. ch. engr., maintenance, G.I.P. rly., May, 1936; and again, Sept., 1937; provl. permnt., May, 1939; confd., March, 1940; ch. engr., Oct., 1943.

GRAHAM, DAVID LIVINGSTONE, O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 1st Sept., 1882).—Ret'd., Sept., 1937.*

GRAHAM, FREDERICK ERNEST, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 29th Apl., 1892).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th Sept., 1914; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Sept., 1919; transfld. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1926; actg. higher exec. offr., Jan., 1940.

GRAHAM, GEORGE KENNETH, Capt., I.M.S. (b. 30th Apl., 1907).—First commn., 3rd Feb.,

1931; in mil. employ to 4th Nov., 1934; agency surg., N. Waziristan, Nov., 1934; medl. officer, Lady Reading Hosp., Peshawar, March, 1938; in temp. milty. employ, March, 1940.

GRAHAM, HAROLD, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Apl., 1889).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived in Bengal, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as an asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., June, 1915, to Jan., 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; confd., Nov., 1926; pte. sec. to govt. of Bengal, Apl., 1927; offg. commr., Sept., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; secty. to govt., educn. dept., Bengal, March, 1936; ret'd., Oct., 1942.

GRAHAM, HAROLD JOHN, O.B.E., late Indian State Ry's. (G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 1st June, 1886).—Joined the service 30th June, 1905; asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1907; dist. ditto, Sept., 1911; in milty. employ from 4th Oct., 1914, to 14th Feb., 1920; supt. of claims, Nov., 1922; pers. asst. to ch. transportation supt., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. agent, March, 1926; adviser to Indian delegation to the internatl. labour conference, Geneva, May-June, 1928; divl. transportation supt., Nov., 1928; ret'd., March, 1941.

GRAHAM, HUBERT, Indian C.S. (secty., education, forest and excise depts., Bengal) (b. 7th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as an asst. mag. and collr.; offg. addl. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, political dept., Apl., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1923; confd., March, 1931; asst. commr., inc. tax, Bengal, Jan., 1927; commr. of inc. tax, B. & O. (temp.), March, 1929; secty., educn., forest and excise depts., Bengal (confd.), Apl., 1939.

GRAHAM, SIR JAMES DRUMMOND, Kt., C.B., C.I.E., M.B., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 21st Jan., 1875).—Ret'd., Feb., 1934.*

GRAHAM, SIR LANCELOT, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Apl., 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and pol. agent; asst. judge and sess. judge, from Jan., 1909; asst. remembr., legal affairs, and sec. for making laws and regns., March, 1911; judge and pol. agent and judl. asst. in Kathiawar from Aug., 1913; addl. jt. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Nov., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; actg. sec. to govt. of India, legis. dept., Apl., 1923; confd., Dec., 1924; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1930; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1936; govr. of Sind, Apl., 1936 to Apl., 1941.

GRAHAM-SMITH, CYRIL, O.B.E., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 2nd June, 1882).—Ret'd., Apl., 1938.*

GRANT, CHARLES FREDERICK, C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Sept., 1878).—Educ. at Dundee High Sch., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Apl., 1915; on mil. duty from May, 1915, to Oct., 1919; chief sec. to govt. of Burma, Aug., 1922; commr. (s.p.f.), May, 1924; confd., Apl., 1926; offg. finl. commr., Jan., 1928; chairman, dev. trust, Rangoon, Jan., 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; chairman, public services commn., Burma, Apl., 1937; ret'd., Apl., 1939.

GRANT, JOHN LESLIE, C.I.E., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Sind) (b. 12th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; in milty. employ from 1st Aug., 1916, to 18th March, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1942; confd. as jt. ch. engr. and jt. sec., govt. Sind, Dec., 1943.

GRANT, JOHN WILLIAM, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agricl. Service (b. 18th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, on 14th Nov., 1922.

GRANT, LACHLAN COLVILLE PATRICK MAC-ALPINE, O.B.E., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dir. of tels.) (b. 12th Aug., 1902).—Arrived in India, 27th March, 1925; joined the service as asst. div. engr., 30th March, 1925; div. engr., Sept., 1927; p.a. to chief engr., July, 1935; asst. dep. dir.-genl. (engrg.), June, 1938; confd. as dir. tels., May, 1944.

GRANTHAM, STANLEY GEORGE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 26th Aug., 1882).—Ret'd., Feb., 1937.*

GRASSBY, CYRIL GEORGE, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 15th March, 1899).—Joined the service on 29th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Aug., 1931; awarded King's police medal, 1934; ret'd., May, 1940.

GREAVES, SIR WILLIAM EWART, Kt., Barr.—Judge, high ct., Calcutta, 23rd Nov., 1914; ret'd., Dec., 1927.*

GREEN, SIR ALAN MICHAEL, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 11th Apl., 1885).—Ret'd., Nov., 1936.*

GREEN, ARTHUR EATOUGH, Capt., O.B.E., M.C., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Bihar) (b. 16th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-sec., public works dept., Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1926; O.B.E., May, 1937; suptg. engr., confd., Apl., 1938; chief engr., May, 1942.

GREEN, CHARLES WILLIAM TANDY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Bengal) (b. 21st May, 1887).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909, as asst. engr.; I.A.R.O. from Apl., 1915, to Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1926; pers. asst. to ch. engr. and secty., P.W. dept., March, 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1929; confd., Dec., 1931; awarded Empire Gallantry Medal, June, 1934; ch. engr., Oct., 1940.

GREEN, JOHN MOWBRAY, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 23rd Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1921; asst. supt., Sept., 1922; offg. supt., Apl., 1925; confd., Oct., 1930; comdt. of police, Aden, May, 1934; reverted, Oct., 1934; commr. of police, C. and M. Stn., Bangalore, June, 1939.

GREEN, LESLIE BENTON, O.B.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Dept. of Industries, Madras (dir. of industries) (b. 11th June, 1892).—Apptd. offg. asst. dir., 16th Dec., 1915; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; dep. dir., Feb., 1922; dr., June, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1939; served under cent. govt. as tea controller, 1942.

GREEN-ARMYtage, VIVIAN BARTLEY, M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Bengal Civil Medical Dept. (b. 14th Aug., 1882).—Ret'd., July, 1935.*

GREENE, HUBERT EUGENE, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 10th June, 1883).—Ret'd., June, 1938.*

GREENEP, DONALD PRESTWICH, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 20th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma,

on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Nov., 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1941.

GREENFIELD, HARRY, C.I.E., Impl. Customs Service (b. 2nd Oct., 1898).—Joined the Indian Police service as asst. supt. on 27th Dec., 1919; dep. commr., Calcutta, Oct., 1925; asst. collr. impl. customs service, Bombay, 1927; ditto, Calcutta, Feb., 1930; collr. of salt rev., Madras, Nov., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; dir. of inspn., customs and cent. excises, Sept., 1939; member, cent. bd. rev. and jt. sec., fin. dept., May, 1943

GREENFIELD, SIR HENRY CHALLEN, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Central Provs.) (b. 8th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., July to Sept., 1913; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1915; commr. (temp.), ditto, Dec., 1916; to Jan., 1917; 2nd asst. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, July, 1918; dep. commr. (prov.), Feb., 1921; confd., Aug., 1923; rev. sec. to govt., Apl., 1927, and again, July, 1928; secty. in settlts. and land records, etc., depts., Jan., 1929; commr. of settlts. and dir., land records, March, 1933; offg. commr., May, 1933; confd. (prov.), July, 1934; subst., May, 1936; C.I.E., June, 1934; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Central Provs., during March, 1937; advr. to govr., Nov., 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; kt., June, 1944.

GREENING, LAURENCE EDWIN, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs.
