(Ind. trade commr., New York) (b. 28th Jan., 1888).—Educ. at Schs. in India, Bombay Univ., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 15th Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; munpl. commr., Surat, June, 1919 to Jan., 1920; dep. munpl. commr., Bombay, Apl. to Nov., 1921; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, May, 1923; dep. sec. to govt., rev. dept. (temp.), March, 1924; ditto (permanent), Aug., 1925; actg. munpl. commr. for city of Bombay, Apl. to Dec., 1926; on deputation out of India as dep. secty. to Indian Central Comee. to Indian Statutory commn., Apl., 1929; municipal commr. for city of Bombay, July, 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Bombay, genl. and educ. depts., Apl., 1935; ch. secty. to govt., Sind, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; chairman Bombay port trust, Oct., 1938; 2nd grade commr., Nov., 1939; jt. sec. govt. of India (supply), Apl., 1941; on spec. duty, commerce dept., Dec., 1943; Indian trade commr., New York, May, 1944.

KIRPALANI, MOTIRAM KHUSHIRAM, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (dep. secty., to govt. fin. dep. Bengal) (b. 15th Aug., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, New College, Oxford, and School

of Oriental Studies, London ; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926 ; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. ; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1927 ; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1932 ; and again, Feb., 1936 ; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932 ; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1934 ; commr. of salt and exc., Bengal, 1938 ; mag. and collr., June, 1939 ; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Bengal, May, 1940 ; confd. mag. and collr., March, 1941 ; sec. comm. and lab. dept., March, 1943.

KIRPALANI, SANTDAS KHUSHIRAM, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. elec. and ind. depts., Punjab) (b. 7th Apr., 1899).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and New Coll., Oxford ; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922 ; arrived, 20th Dec., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., offg. dep. commr., May, 1926 ; dep. commr. (prov.), Dec., 1929 ; confd., March, 1933 ; junr. secty. to finl. commrs., Dec., 1929 ; offg. rev. secty. to govt., Punjab, Dec., 1931 ; settlement offer., Jan., 1936 ; secty., unempl. cttee., Dec., 1937 ; secty., elec. and ind. depts., Aprl., 1938 ; offr. on special duty, dept. of supply, Aprl., 1941 ; jt. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of supply, May, 1941 ; C.I.E. Jan., 1943.

KIRPA RAM, RAI BAHADUR, B.A. (dep. dir. of admin. and intell.) (b. 20th June, 1890).—Joined the service on 27th Oct., 1913, and held non-gaz. posts up to 9th May, 1930 ; asst. chief contr. of stores, Ind. Stores Dept., May, 1930, and asst. dir. of admin. and intell., June, 1930 ; dep. ditto, June, 1937 ; asst. supt. of clothing, production, directorate-genl. of supply, Nov., 1940 ; dep. dir. directorate-genl. of supply, Aug., 1941, dir., ditto. Aug., 1941.

KIRWAN, ERNEST WILLIAM O'GORMAN, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (b. 18th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1910 ; apptd. profrr. of ophthalm. surg., med. coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1930 ; C.I.E., Jan., 1939 ; retd., Nov., 1944.

KISCH, BARTHOLD SCHLESINGER, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., United Provs. (b. 25th Oct., 1882).—Retd., July, 1935.*

KISCH, SIR CECIL HERMANN, K.C.I.E., C.B., M.A., India Police (actg. dep., under-sec. of state) (b. 31st March, 1884).—Educ. at Clifton and Trinity Coll., Oxford ; served in secty.'s office at post office until 27th July, 1908, when he was apptd. junior clerk, India Office ; resdt. clerk, March to Sept., 1910 ; pte. sec. to under-sec. of state, May, 1911 ; senr. clerk, finl. dept., Aprl., 1914 ; acting as pte. sec. to under-sec. of state, from June, 1915, and to parly. under-sec. of state (in addn.) from Dec., 1915 ; pte. sec. (temp.) to the sec. of state, May, 1917, to June, 1919 ; on deputn. to India in connection with visit of sec. of state, Oct., 1917, to May, 1918 ; C.B., Jan., 1919 ; sec. to commte. on Indian exchange and currency, June to Dec., 1919 ; assist. sec. British Empire Delegation at Peace Conference, Paris, 1918-19 ; dep. sec., finl. dept., Dec., 1919 ; sec. finl. dept., Aprl., 1921 ; acting under-secy. of state, Jan.-Aprl., 1932 ; K.C.I.E., June, 1932 ; member, Ind. staty. rly. bd. cttee., June, 1933 ; ditto, Ind. res. bank cttee., July, 1933 ; adviser to Ind. delegation to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933 ; asst. under-sec. of state, Dec., 1933 ; commr. of inc.-tax, Dec., 1933 ; services loaned to the mines dept., Nov., 1939 to June, 1943 ; actg. dep. under-sec. of state, July, 1943.

KISHAN, RAM (see Ram Kishan).

KISHEN, RADHA (see Radha Kishen).

KISHOR, CHANDRA (see Chandra, K.).

KISHORE, NAND (see Nand Kishor).

KISHORE, THAKUR JUGAL (see Jugal Kishore, T.).

KITCHEN, HUGH MELVILLE, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (dep. chief operating supt.) (b. 13th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 9th Dec., 1919 ; asst. supt., E.I. Rly., Nov., 1926 ; transptn. supt. (traffic comml.), May, 1935 ; dep. ch. operating supt., provisional, Aug., 1941.

KITCHIN, ERIC ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 31st March, 1911).—Educ. at Malvern, Christ's Coll., Cambridge, and New Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1933 ; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. ; apptd. asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Jan., 1938 ; asst. sec. to govr., Bengal, July, 1940 ; addl. sec., home dept., Bengal, Jan., 1942.

KITCHINGMAN, GERALD DAWSON, M.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Punjab) (b. 6th March, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., March, 1921 ; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926 ; offg. consvr., variously, from July, 1934 ; consvr., Aprl., 1940.

KNAPP, SIR ARTHUR ROWLAND, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.B.E., late Indian C.S., Madras.—Retd., Jan., 1927.*

KNASTER, ROLAND MARCUS JULIUS, B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, princpl. exec. grade, education dept.) (b. 19th July, 1890).—Educ. at the Perse School and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge ; apptd. asst. to local adviser to Indian students, London, Sept., 1919 ; warden, 21, Cromwell Road, S.W.7, Feb., 1920 to July, 1923 ; transf. to office of high commr. for India as asst. to joint secs., Indian students' dept., Oct., 1920 ; principal exec. officer, education dept., Aprl., 1925.

KNIGHT, SIR HENRY FOLEY, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (advr. to govr. of Bombay) (b. 19th Jan., 1886).—Educ. at Haileybury Sch., and Caius College, Cambridge ; apptd. after exam. of 1909 ; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1910, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. ; on mil. duty from July, 1916 to March, 1919 ; under-sec. to govt. rev. and fin. depts., Oct., 1919 ; temp. dep. sec., ditto, Jan., 1920 ; dir. of comml. intellige., Bombay, Jan., 1922 ; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1922 ; dir. of industries, May, 1923 ; actg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1924, March, 1926, and June, 1928 ; confd., Jan., 1929 ; secty., home and eccl. depts., Jan., 1929, and again, Aprl., 1935 ; offg. secty., genl. and educ. depts., Bombay, Feb., 1934 ; confd., May, 1934 ; secty., home and eccles. depts., Aprl., 1935 ; C.I.E., June, 1936 ; offg. commr. of excise, Bombay, Sept., 1936 ; commr., Dec., 1936 ; ditto, 1st gr., Dec., 1937 ; advr. to govr. of Bombay, Nov., 1939 ; C.S.I., June, 1941 ; K.C.S.I., June, 1944.

KNOX, KENNETH NEVILLE, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S., United Provs. (b. 21st July, 1878).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

KOHLLI, DALIP RAI, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 14th Dec., 1906).—Joined, 19th Jan., 1931 ; confd. as asst. supt., Madras, Jan., 1933.

KOHLLI, DALIP RAI, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 7th June, 1919).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. ; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1942 ; served in Assam as asst. commr.

KOHLLI, DHARAMNATH PRASAD, M.Sc., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 8th Feb., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 5th Jan., 1931 ; confd., Feb., 1933 ; confd. as supt., July, 1944.

KOLAND VICTOR SAMUEL, B.E., Indian State Ryls. (dist. loco. supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th May, 1894).—Asst. loco. supt. (junior scale), 5th May, 1922; dist. loco. supt. (senr. scale), Nov., 1936.

KOMAN, MALIKAYAL CHANDAPPA BALACHANDRA (see Balachandra Koman, M. C.).

KORDIK, OSCAR, M.M., India Office (higher executive officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 19th May, 1893).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 30th Jan., 1913, and attd. to finl. dept.; on mil. service, from Sept., 1915 to Feb., 1919 (awarded the Military Medal); exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher ditto, Aug., 1931.

KORGAOKAR, HARISH CHANDRA KRISHNARAO, M.A., B.Sc., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ryls. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 16th Sept., 1885).—Asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr. (provl.), July, 1937; retd. Sept., 1940.

KOTHAVALA, SIAVASH RUSTAM, M.A., Khan Bahadur, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 15th May, 1888).—Joined the service as probn. supt., 17th March, 1911; confd., Feb., 1913; Khan Bahadur, Jan, 1935; dep. p.m.g., Oct., 1937; dep. dir.-genl., postal services, July, 1941; senr. ditto, March, 1942; retd., May, 1944.

KOTHAVALA, TEHMAS TEMULJI, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., Bombay C.S. (collr. and dist. mag.) (b. 6th Feb., 1893).—Dep. collr., Jan., 1920; collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1935; under Government of Sind, Sept., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1943; sec., provl. transp. authority, Bombay, May, 1944.

KOTHAWALA, DARASHAH DHANJISHAH, Khan Bahadur, M.B.E. (b. 22nd Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1909; M.B.E., Dec., 1930; promoted to I.P. (Bombay) as supt., May 1936; asst. to dep. inspr.-genl. of police, C.I.D., Poona, Nov., 1938; retd., Feb., 1940.

KRISHEN, PREM, M.A., Indian C.S. (b. 20th Nov., 1911).—Educ. at St. Edward's High Sch., Simla, Delhi Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Sept., 1935, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., July, 1937; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1941; under-secty., educn., health and lands dept., May, 1943.

KRISHEN, SAROOP, M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 18th Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Arya High School, Ludhaina, Punjab Univ., Univ. Coll., London, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. on probn., 23rd Oct., 1939; confd., 24th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

KRISHNA AIYAR, SAMBASIVA, B.A., B.L., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 10th July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

KRISHNA, DAYA KHANNA (see Daya Krishna, K.).

KRISHNA MENON, MANAKAL, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 14th Dec., 1906).—Joined, 19th Jan., 1931; confd. as asst. supt., Madras, Jan., 1933.

KRISHNAMA ACHARYA, SIR VANGAL TIRUVENKATA, K.C.I.E., B.A., B.L., Rao Bahadur, late Madras Civil Service (b. 8th Feb., 1881).—Retd., Feb., 1936.*

KRISHNAMMA, NOBLE RATNAM, Rao Bahadur, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 1st June, 1887).—Joined the service, 2nd Apl., 1910; prin., govt., arts coll., Rajamundry, July, 1921; ditto, Coimbatore, Apl., 1925; offg. dist. educl. offr., Dec., 1925; prin.

ceded districts coll., Anantapur, Apl., 1926; retd. June, 1942.

KRISHNAMOORTHY, ENNAPPADAM SUNDERA, Ayyar, B.A., Imperial Customs Service (dep. secty., Govt. of India, Commerce Dept.) (b. 15th Nov., 1905).—Joined the service, Apl., 1930; asst. collr. of customs, Nov., 1931; on spec. duty fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1938; attaché to the govt. of India, commerce dept., Oct., 1938; under-secty., ditto, Feb., 1939; dep. ditto, Dec., 1942.

KRISHNAMURTHY, PATTADARAL VENKANNA, M.Sc., F.I.A. (b. 2nd Dec., 1907).—Apptd. asst. supt. of insurance, govt. of India, commerce dept., May, 1939.

KRISHNAMURTI, VEPA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 13th Sept., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 30th Apl., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; exec. engr., Apl., 1942; served under Central govt. from March, 1942.

KRISHNA NARAYAN KATHPALIA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 19th Jan., 1902).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 3rd Nov., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1925; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1934.

KRISHNA PRASADA, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (p.m.g.) (b. 4th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived in India, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., jt. mag., Nov., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1926; confd., Aug., 1935; p.m.g., Central circle, Feb., 1935; ditto, Madras, June, 1937; del. to internl. conf., Cairo, 1938; dep. dir.-genl., postal services, Apl., 1938; post-master-genl., Bombay, March, 1939; p.m.g., Punjab and N.W.F. circle, Nov., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; p.m.g., B. and A. circle, July, 1941.

KRISHNA RAO, KARNAD, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 17th Nov., 1899).—Joined the service, 5th May, 1924; dep. dir. of rly. audit, Apl., 1934; dep. acct. genl., Punjab, Apl., 1937; asst. dir. audit (def.), May, 1938; ditto, def. services, southern cmd., Dec., 1939.

KRISHNA RAO, NANDALIKE DEVARAO, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 19th July, 1904).—Educ. at University of Madras, School of Economics, London, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., March, 1930; dist. judge, June, 1939.

KRISHNAN, HARIKESANALUR RAMASASTRI, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 9th Jan., 1910).—Educated at Tirthapathi High Sch., University of Madras, University of Benares and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in B. & O. as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-divl. offr., Feb., 1937; served in fin. and comm. provl. Cent. govt., May, 1942; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1943.

KRISHNAN, ODAYATH, B.A., Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 15th June, 1900).—Joined the service, Apl., 1925; asst. collr. of customs, March, 1927.

KRISHNAN, SIVARAMA (see Sivarama Krishnan).

KRISHNASWAMI AYYANGAR, K. S., Apptd. Judge, High Ct., Madras, July, 1938.

KRISHNASWAMI AYYANGAR, KOMBUR RAMASWAMI, O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., retd. late

I.M.S. (b. 5th July, 1883).—Apptd. dir., Pasteur Institute, Coonoor, 11th Apr., 1928; retd., July, 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1939.

KRISHNASWAMI AYYAR, Diwan Bahadur, SIR ALLADI, Kt., B.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 13th May, 1883).—Apptd. Advocate-genl. for presdy. of Madras, Jan., 1929.

KRISHNASWAMI, V. S., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 12th Aug., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 4th Oct., 1929; dep. consvr., Oct., 1934; dep. commr., fuel, Madras, Feb., 1944.

KRISHNASWAMY, RANGACHARI SRINIVASADESIKA, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd June, 1906).—Educ. at Madras University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May., 1933; addtl. dist. mag., March, 1938 to Jan., 1939; addtl. dist. judge, Aug., 1938; mag. and collr., June, 1940.

KRISHNASWAMY, SUCHEENDRUM YEGNANARAYANA, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 30th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at University of Madras and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Aug., 1932; spcl. sett. offcr., Apr., 1937; spcl. rev. offr., Apr., 1940; sec., bd. of rev., Jan., 1942; spcl. develmt. offr., Dec., 1943.

KUDVA VENKATARAYA NARAYANA, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 13th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr., and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag. Jan., 1925; on for service as estate collr., Sivaganga, Nov., 1930; offg. collr. and mag., July, 1933; supt. of stationery, Madras, Jan., 1936; collr. (confd.), Apr., 1943; sec., local admin. dept., Sept., 1941.

KUKAR, JAGAN NATH (see Jagan Nath, K.).

KULKARNI, LAKSMAN PANDURANG, Diwan Bahadur, M.A., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 12th June, 1880).—Retd., June, 1935.*

KUMAR ASANAND (see Asanand Kumar).

KUMAR, ASHWANI, B.A., Indian Police, Punjab (b. 27th Dec., 1920).—Apptd. asst. supt., on probn., after exam. of 1942.

KUMAR, VIRENDRA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (b. 30th Apr., 1920).—Educ. at Lucknow Univ.; apptd., on probn., after exam. of 1943, and served in Bombay.

KUMTHEKAR, KONDO MAHADEV, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 8th Jan., 1882).—Retd., Jan., 1937.*

KUNDAN LAL, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 12th Apr., 1883).—Retd., Apr., 1938.*

KUNDAN LAL AGGARWAL, M.B.E., M.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 21st Aug., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 8th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928; M.B.E., June, 1941; on depn. to Chambra State, Apr., 1943.

KUNDAN LAL, LALA RAI BAHADUR, B.A., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 21st Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 5th Oct., 1911; asst. engr., Oct., 1912; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; subst. suptg. engr., Nov., 1939; retd., 21st August, 1943.

KUNHIRAMAN, CHURYA, B.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (addtl. judge, Madras high ct.) (b. 1st March, 1888).—Apptd. offg. asst. prof., law

coll., Madras, 10th July, 1911; confd., July, 1916; prof. ditto, July, 1921; 3rd judge, small cause ct., Feb., 1927; 2nd ditto, Feb., 1928; temp. judge, city civil ct., Madras, June, 1932; ch. jurge, small cause ct., July, 1933; addtl. judge, Madras high ct.,

KUNHIRAMAN NAMBIYAR, AMANDA KALOTH, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 15th May, 1911).—Apptd. asst. supt., on probn., 13th Feb., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937.

KUNJ BEHARILAL SETH (see Seth, K. B.). KUPPUSWAMI AYYAR, C. N., Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (high ct. judge, Madras) (b. 1st Apr., 1887).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1908; sub-judge, Apr., 1934; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1936; retd., Apr., 1942; addtl. judge, Madras high ct., June, 1942.

KUPPUSWAMI RAMASWAMI AYYAN-GAR, Income Tax Dept., Madras (asst. commr.) (b. 28th Dec., 1887).—Held non-gaz. appts.; joined the service, Sept., 1918; income-tax officer, Oct., 1923; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, March, 1937.

KUPPUSWAMI SASTRI, S. M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras) (b. 16th Dec., 1880).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

KUPPUSWAMI, VALAVANUR SUBRAHMANYA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 1st July, 1899).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928; served under Central govt. from Oct., 1940.

KUPPUSWAMY, IYENGAR, KRISHNASWAMY, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 5th Apr., 1905).—Joined the service, 12th Apr., 1930; asst. acct. genl., Madras, Apr., 1932; ditto, Bengal, Apr., 1936; dep. govt. exmr. accts., A.B. Rly., Dec., 1939.

KURBAN ALI KHAN, KHAN, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b., 11th May, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 8th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1927; confd., Apr., 1933.

KURESHI, BASHIR AHMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b., 1st Dec., 1914).—Educ. at Sandamen Sch., Quetta, Punjab Univ. and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn. 30th Sept., 1938; confd. 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offr., May, 1942; under-sec., govt., Punjab (elec.) and sec., Punjab and N.W.F.P. jt. publ. serv. commn., Nov., 1942.

KURVA, SHUJAUDDIN ESMAILJI, M.B.E., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 13th July, 1885).—Apptd. judge, small causes ct., Bombay, Oct., 1928; retd., July, 1940; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

KYA, BA, Barr., Judl. dept., Burma (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 14th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service, March, 1921, dist. and sess. judge (confd.) Nov., 1937.

KYAING, MAUNG, offg. D.D.U.S., Burma, from A pl., 1941.

KYAW KHINE, Oo, B.Sc., (see Khine).

KYAW, MAUNG BA, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) (b. 11th June, 1914).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; apptd. on probn., 1st Oct., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in Burma, Oct., 1938, and served as asst. commr.

KYAW MIN, MAUNG (see Min, Maung Kyaw).

KYIN, MAUNG, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 25th Apr., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Rangoon, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-

secty., educn. dept., Aug., 1932; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1934; jt. regr., co-op. soc., Dec., 1935; offg. regr., ditto, March, 1937; dep. commr. (confd.), Jan., 1939; sec. agric. and forests dept., Feb., 1941.

KYIN, U. Po, late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 1st June, 1885).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

LABERN, REGINALD ALBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officr., general dept.) (b. 29th Oct., 1897).—Apptd. temp. clerk, India Office, Oct., 1920; transfed. to H.C.'s Office, Apr., 1922; clerical officr., Jan., 1924; exec. ditto, June, 1927; office secty. to Indian del. to Indo-British trade negotiations, June, 1937 to Sept., 1938.

LABORDA, FELIX EDWARD RICARDO, Indian State Rlys. (C.M.O., E.I. Rly.) (b. 1st Apr., 1908).—Joined the service as dist. medl. offr., 17th Oct., 1919; offg. C.M.O., E.I. rly., variously, from May, 1933; confd., Jan., 1940.

LABOUCARDIERE, BASIL RICHARD EMILE (supt., Bombay) Indian Police.—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 22nd Jan., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937; served in Sind from Feb., 1943; pers. asst. to inspr.-gen., Dec., 1944.

LA BOUCARDIERE, MAURICE PRINCE, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 3rd Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 22nd March, 1920; offg. supt., Apr., 1923; confd., Nov., 1927; additional labour officer, Bombay, March, 1939 to Nov., 1942; King's police medal, June, 1942; confd., supt. (sel. grade), March, 1944.

LACAMP, ADRIAN, Survey of India (asst. supt.) (b. 19th Nov., 1893).—Joined the service, 19th Jan., 1923; asst. supt., mathematical instruments office, March, 1926.

LACEY, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th Dec., 1889).—Retd., July, 1939.*

LACEY, GERALD, C.I.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engineers (United Provs.) (b. 26th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; in mil. employ from 5th Oct., 1917 to 29th Aug., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; profr., T.C.E. Coll., Roorkee, Oct., 1928; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1934; confd., Aug., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; ret'd., 25th July, 1942.

LACEY, ONWARD BAYES, V.D., A.M.I.M.E., M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys., (E.B. Rly.) (b. 10th Feb., 1883).—Retd., Feb., 1937.*

LACEY, WALTER GRAHAM, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Bihar) (b. 17th July, 1894).—Educ. at Norwich and Bedford Gr. Schs., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 1st Dec., 1919; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto and under-sec. to govt., finl. dept., May, 1922; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925, and March, 1926; addl. judl. commr., Feb., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1926; offg. secty., finl. dept., Apr., 1930; offg. supt. of census, Nov., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Nov., 1933; mag. and collr. (prov. subs.), Aug., 1933; confd., Sept., 1935; offg. secty. to govt., finl. dept., May, 1935; secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Oct., 1935; addl. secty. to govt., pol. and appts. dept., Bihar, Apr., 1936; dep. commr., March, 1938; secty. to H.E. the Govr. of Bihar, Apr., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1939.

LACHHMAN PRASAD BHARGAV, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.)—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 15th Oct., 1927; confd., Oct., 1937.

LACCHMAN SITARAM KHER, RAO SAHIB PANDIT, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., United Provs. (b. 1st July, 1881).—Retd., July, 1936.*

LAD, PURUSHOTTAM MANGESH, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 29th March, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1936.

LADLI PERSHAD, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., Commerce Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 12th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service, 26th Aug., 1913; supt., dept. of commerce, June, 1925; asst. secty., ditto, May, 1932; dep. dir., comml. intell., Sept., 1940; dep. ch. contr., imports, May, 1943.

LAHIRI, BISHWA NATH (see Bishwa, N. L.).

LAHIRI, SACHCHIDANANDA, RAI Bahadur, Indian Audit Dept. (b. Oct., 1889).—Joined the service 5th Apr., 1909; asst. acct.-genl., Central revenues, May, 1936; dep. ditto, Bihar, Oct., 1936; Rair Bahadur, June, 1940; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Apr., 1941.

LAINE, SIR ABRAHAM JAMES, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 26th Aug., 1876).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

LAITHWAITE, SIR JOHN GILBERT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., India Office (b. 5th July, 1894).—Educ. at Clongowes and Trinity Coll., Oxford; served in France with 10th Lanc. Fus., May, 1917, to July, 1918; specially employed, War Office, March to Sept., 1919; apptd. junr. clerk, mil. dept., 16th July, 1920; after previous service as temp. junr. clerk since 1st Oct., 1919; asst. principal, Apr., 1921; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, March, 1922; on depn. to India with Lord Winterton, Aug. to Nov., 1922; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1923; principal, services and genl. dept., Sept., 1924; pol. dept., May, 1927; personally attached to prime minister for Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept., Dec., 1931; secty., Indian Franchise cottee., Jan.-June, 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; secty., Ind. delimitn. ctee., Aug., 1935; pte. secty. to Viceroy, Apr., 1936; secty. to govr.-genl. (in addn.), Apr., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1941; acting asst. under-sec. of state, Jan., 1944; loaned to offices of War Cabinet, Jan., 1944.

LAKHPAT RAI, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 8th July, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928.

LAKSHMANAN, CHERUVARI KOTTIETH, L.M. & S., M.R.C.S., D.T.M. & H., D.P.H., Maj., I.M.S. (chief health officr., Delhi) (b. 5th Apr., 1898).—First commn. dated 30th Apr., 1928; in milty. employ prior to 16th Dec., 1935, when he joined the port health dept.; dep. public health commr. with the govt. of India, March, 1939; ch. health officr., Delhi, May, 1941; D.P.H., Bengal, Nov., 1943; addl. dir. of A.I.I. of P.H., Sept., 1944.

LAKSHMAN SARUP GUPTA, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 26th Oct., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

LAKSHMANA RAO, KASARAGOD PATANASHETTI, Diwan Bahadur, Madras C.S. (Judicial Branch) (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 15th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. profr., law coll., Madras, 28th Aug., 1916; profr., ditto, Oct., 1920; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1921; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, variously from Aug.

1930; addl. ditto, Jan., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935.

LAL, BANWARI (see Banwari Lal).

LAL, BASANT (see Basant Lal).

LAL, BEHARI (see Behari Lal).

LAL, BIPEN BEHARI, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag.) (b. 30th Jan., 1917).—Educ. at Delhi and Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in United Provs. as asst. mag.

LAL, BRAJ BHUSAN (see Braj Bhusan Lal).

LAL, CHAMPA (see Champa Lal).

LAL, CHUTTAN (see Chuttan Lal).

LAL, CHUNI, (see Chuni Lal).

LAL, DONALD CHANDU (see Chandu Lal, D.).

LAL, DHAWAN PURUSHOTTAM (see Dhawan Purushottam Lal).

LAL, HARBANS (see Harbans Lal).

LAL, HARIVANSH (see Harivansh Lal).

LAL, JAI (see Jai Lal).

LAL, KRISHNA KANIYA (see Krishna Kaniya Lal).

LAL, KUNDAN (see Kundan Lal).

LAL, KUNK BEHAR MOHAN (see Mohan Lal, K.B.).

LAL, MOHAN, BATRA (see Mohan Lal, Batra).

LAL, MOTI (see Moti Lal).

LAL, MUNNA (see Munna Lal).

LAL, MUTSADDI (see Mutsaddi Lal).

LAL, PANNA (see Panna Lal).

LAL, RAM (see Ram Lal).

LAL, RANG BEHARI (see Rang Behari Lal).

LAL, RANGI (see Rangi Lal).

LAL, SARDARI (see Sardari Lal).

LAL, SHADI (see Shadi Lal).

LAL, SHAM (see Sham Lal).

LAL, SHAVAX ARDESHIR, C.I.E., M.A., LL.B. (b. Nov., 1899).—Joined the service, June, 1930; 2nd class sub. judge, Bombay, June, 1930, to Jan., 1933; legal dept., Bombay, Jan., 1933, to Apl., 1936; dep. secty., legislative dept., Apl., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; jt. sec., legis. dept., Aug., 1943.

LAL SINGH, MUNSHI, B.A., Rai Bahadur, late Punjab C.S. (b. 16th Dec., 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

LAL, SUNDER (see Sunder Lal).

LALKAKA, MINOCHAR DORABJI, B.A., LL.B., Barr., Judicial Dept., Bombay (b. 3rd Aug., 1894).—Apptd. judge, small cause ct., Bombay, 15th Apl., 1929.

LALL (see also under Lal).

LALL, ARTHUR SAMUEL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 14th July, 1911); educ. at Central Model Sch., Lahore, Univ. of the Punjab, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934; and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. officer, May, 1937; under-secty., pol. dept., Punjab, Apl., 1940; officer on spl. duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1941; attache, fin. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1942; dep. secty., fin., Apl., 1944.

LALL, JANARDHAN SHANKAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Central Model Sch., Lahore, Punjab Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

LALL, KRISHEN BEHARI, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Provs.) (b. 30th May, 1915).—Educ. at A.S.V.J. High Sch., Delhi, Delhi Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; dep. sec., food dept., 1944.

LALL, SHAMALDHARI (see Shamaldhari Lall).

LALLUBHAI, MANEKLAL, Rao Bahadur, O.B.E., late Salt Dept., Bombay (b. 9th Nov., 1879).—Retd., June, 1939.*

LALVANI, LALCHAND VARUMAL, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 14th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1921; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., Oct., 1930.

LAMARQUE, WALTER GEOFFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty. to govt. of India, dept. of labour) (b. 12th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at Marlborough and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1940; under-secty., govt. of India, dept. of labour, March, 1941.

LAMB, GEORGE HAMISH, Indian State Rlys. (works manager, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 15th March, 1887).—Joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1921; production engr., G.I.P. rly, Jan., 1929; asst. works manager, G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1931; works manager, Oct., 1932.

LAMB, JOHN CHARLES, Indian State Rlys. (mech. engr. and transportation (power) supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 27th Oct., 1899).—Asst. transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly., Jan., 1926; transportation supt., senior scale, provl., E.I. rly., Nov., 1938; confd., Aug., 1939.

LAMBERT, D. P., Maj., I.M.S. (civ. surg.) (b. May 25, 1902).—Date of first commn., Oct. 15, 1926; civil surg., United Provs., Aug., 1934.

LAMBERT, ERIC THOMAS DRUMMOND, Indian Police (b. 3rd Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Assam, 25th Nov., 1929; asst. pol. officer, from Dec., 1939; pol. officer from Mar. to Aug., 1942; cent. intell. officer, Aug., 1942; King's police and fire serv. Medals, Jan., 1942.

LAMBERT, E. W., M.A., Barr., Burma Judl. Service (govt. advocate) (b. 7th Apl., 1890).—Apptd. offg. asst. govt. advocate, Burma, 25th March, 1926; confd., June, 1934; govt. advocate, May, 1937.

LAMBERT, SIR GEORGE BANCROFT, K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1873).—Retd., Dec., 1932.*

LAMBERT, HENRY, M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educ. Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 22nd Feb., 1881).—Retd., Feb., 1936.*

LAMBERT, OSCAR WILFRED, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 2nd March, 1894).—Apptd. clerical officer, 8th July, 1923, after previous service from 7th Oct., 1919; actg. exec. officer, June, 1931; confd., Apl., 1933.

LAMBRICK, HUGH TREVOR, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 20th Apl., 1904).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. asst. commr. in Sind, Apl., 1931; tempy. dep. secty. to govt., pol. and reforms dept., Bombay, March, 1935; offg. collr. June, 1936; confd., Dec., 1936; supt., census operations, Sind, Oct., 1939; spec. commr. for Sind, Karachi, June, 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

LAMOON, ALBERT JOSEPH, late India Office (b. 4th May, 1881).—Apptd. clerical officer, 29th Aug., 1921; actg. higher clerical officer, Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; ret'd., 7th Dec., 1944.

LANCASHIRE, JOHN KENNETH, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 20th Feb., 1878).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

LANCASTER, HAROLD, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 30th June, 1883).—Apptd. colliery supt., 1st July, 1922; retd., Apl., 1939.*

LANDER, PERCY EDWARD, M.A. (Cantab.), D.Sc. (London), A.I.C., late Dept. of Agric., Punjab (b. 8th May, 1889).—Dept. dir. of agric., 4th Dec., 1920; offg. agricl. chemist to govt., March, 1921; confd., Dec., 1923; retd.,

LANE, A. S., M.B.E., B.Sc., Dept. of Industries and Labour, Govt. of India (b. 22nd May, 1900).—Apptd. aircraft and engine inspr. on the staff of the dir. of civil aviation in India, 29th March, 1929; M.B.E., Jan., 1935; retd.

LANE, BASIL WILLIAM, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 5th Aug., 1905).—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1926; offg. dist. supt., Dec., 1932; and again, March, 1933; confd., July, 1939.

LANE, CHARLES MACDONALD, C.S.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 26th Jan., 1882).—Retd., Jan., 1937.*

LANE, HERBERT ALLARDYCE, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; jt. mag., July, 1916; ditto and asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1916; dep. commr. (provl.) and settlt. offr., Oct., 1918; addl. collr., Nov., 1921; mag. and collr., and settlt. offr., Aug., 1924; rev. and judl. sec. to govt., Oct., 1925; secty. to govt., rev. and p.w.d., May, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; offg. settlt. commr., March, 1929; secty. to govt., rev. and p.w.d. (b. and r.), Feb., 1932; offg. presdt., ct. of wards, Apl., 1934; confd., Nov., 1935; retd., May, 1940.

LANE, HUGH THOMPSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 26th Apl., 1914).—Educ. at Rossall Sch. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

LANE, RICHARD GEORGE McLAREN, late Indian Police (b. 29th June, 1902).—(Bihar and Orissa).—Retd., June, 1938.*

LANG, LIONEL EDWARD, C.I.E., M.C., Lieut.-Col., I.A., late Indian Political Service (b. 1st Nov., 1885).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

LANG, WILLIAM GORDON, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bombay) (b. 23rd Nov., 1916).—Apptd. on probn., 27th Oct., 1936; confd., Sept., 1938.

LANGDON, FREDERICK GUY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ryis. (ch. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th June, 1911; offg. resdt. engr., Nov., 1914; confd., Sept., 1919; divl. engr., Dec., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., contrn., May, 1935; and variously from May, 1936; provl., Nov., 1937; confd., May, 1939; chief ditto, provl., May, 1941.

LANGFORD, JOHN BRIAN, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 21st June, 1907).—Educ. at City and County Sch., Chester, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. secty., rev. dept., United Provs. (tempy.), Apl., 1937; dep. sec., dept. of supply, Jan., 1942; O.B.E., June, 1944.

LANGLEY, GEORGE HARRY, M.A. (Lon.),

late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th July, 1881).—Retd., July, 1936.*

LAO HTIN WAH, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 26th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 30th Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., March, 1932.

LAPSLAY, JAMES BURNE, M.C., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 16th Aug., 1884).—Retd., Apl., 1935.*

LARKIN, ALFRED SLOANE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 11th June, 1894).—Educ. at High Sch., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. under reconstruction scheme; arrived, 2nd May, 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. mag. and collr., March, 1926; offg. ditto, March, 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; offg. commr. of excise and salt, May, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

LARWILL, REV. GUTHRIE JAMES, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senior chaplain) (b. 26th Dec., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 24th Oct., 1926; junior chaplain, Oct., 1928; senior chaplain, Oct., 1938.

LASRADO, EDWARD ALOYSIUS, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 21st June, 1902).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1926 dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; senior timber supply offr., Sept., 1942.

LASRADO, JOSEPH PATRICK, Indian C.S. (name changed to SHENOY, J. P. L. [q.v.]).

LATHAM, HUGH DALLAS, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 5th Feb., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 5th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., Nov., 1931; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1939; confd. as dist. supt., March, 1938; O.B.E., June, 1942.

LATIFI, ALMA, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., LL.M. (Cantab.); LL.D. (Dublin), Barr. (1902), late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 12th Nov., 1879).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

LATIF-UR-RAHMAN, NAWABZADA, A.S.M., M.A. (Cantab.), Bengal Judl. Dept. (chief judge, Calcutta small cause ct.).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1918; judge, Calcutta small cause ct., Oct., 1920; chief judge, ditto, 1937; judge, Calcutta high ct., Apl.-Aug., 1944.

LATIMER, COURTENAY ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 13th July, 1911).—Educ. at Rugby and Christ Church Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; apptd. subs. on probn. to Indian political service, Jan., 1939; on foreign service as guardian to H. H. the Minor Maharaja of Bharatpur, Jan., 1939; vice-consul, Bushire, Dec., 1939; on depn., Aug., 1941; asst. commr., Bannu, Dec., 1941; asst. pol. agt., N. Waziristan, Jan., 1943; asst. commr., Hangu, Apl., 1943.

LA TOUCHE, JAMES FLEMING DIGGES, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 18th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., June, 1930.

LAUGHTON, FREDERICK EASTFIELD, Maj., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 29th Apl., 1900).—First commn., 16th July, 1919; asst. resdt., Aden, Feb., 1929; under-secty. to A.G.G. in the states of W.I., Aug., 1931; asst. commr., Mansehra, Nov., 1931; ditto, Hangu, Nov., 1932; asst. commr., Charsadda, Feb., 1935; asst. commr., Mardan, May, 1936; officr. on spec. duty, Peshawar, Oct., 1936; dep. commr., Bannu, March, 1937; on spec. duty as addl. asst. pol. offer., Sikkim, Oct., 1939;

pol. agt., Kurram, March, 1940; retd., July, 1943.

LAUGHTON, GEORGE CHRISTIAN, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ryis. (dep. ch. eng.) (b. 26th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; divl. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. divl. supt., Nov., 1926; offg. dep. dir., programme; rly. bd., March, 1929; offg. dir., civil engrg., Apl., 1931; offg. secty., rly. bd., June, 1932; offg. dep. dir., ditto, Oct., 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1932; confd., Dec., 1935; on loan to B.B. & C.I. rly., agent and genl. manager, ditto, Apl., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

LAURENCE, REV. GEORGE, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (archdeacon of Lahore) (b. 12th Nov., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 26th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1927; senr. ditto, Nov., 1937; archdeacon of Lahore, May, 1944.

LAURIE, MALCOLM VVYYAN, Indian Forest Service (silviculturist, Dehra Dun) (b. 30th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1925; forest economist, Apl., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; offg. silviculturist, forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1936; dep. dir., directorate-genl. of supply, Nov., 1940.

LAW, HENRY RUSSEL MACKAY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 16th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

LAWIE, WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 22nd Jan., 1895).—Date of first commn., 29th Nov., 1923; civil surgeon, 3rd July, 1933.

LAWLEY, WILFRED, O.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 2nd June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1934; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

LAWRANCE, REV. NEVILE ANTHONY HENRY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 20th July, 1909).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Nagpur Diocese, 1st Sept., 1940.

LAWRENCE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 8th June, 1900).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 27th Oct., 1919; clerical officer, Jan., 1920; transferred to office of high commr., Oct., 1920; actg. exec. offcr., Feb., 1927; confd., June, 1929.

LAWRENCE, SIR HENRY STAVELEY, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Retd., Sept., 1926; member of Royal commn. on agric. in India, 1926-7.*

LAWSON, REGINALD GEORGE BATEMAN, late Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th Dec., 1914; Rangoon, March, 1935, and again, Oct., 1936; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan. to Oct., 1936; asst. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936; commr., Rangoon city police, Jan., 1938; retd., 1st Apl., 1943.

LAWSON, WILFRED CHARLES, Indian Police (b. 7th June, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., Nov., 1940.

LAYARD, AUSTEN HAVELOCK, C.I.E., M.A., Barr.-at-Law, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Rugby and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 29th Dec., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; dep. commr., Delhi, March, 1932; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

LAYTON, GEORGE EDWARD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., store dept.) (b.

12th Oct., 1879).—Apptd., 1st July, 1925, in connection with the G.I.P. Rly. Co.

LAZARUS, MISS THEODORA, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras) (b. 9th Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

LEACH, SIR ALFRED HENRY LIONEL (ch. justice, high ct., Madras).—apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, 15th Feb., 1933; ch. justice, high ct., Madras, July, 1937; kt., Jan., 1938.

LEAKE, JONES INCHIQUIN, late Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 18th March, 1895).—Joined the service, 8th Apl., 1924; asst. supt., Jan., 1925; retd., 6th Nov., 1945.

LE BAILLY, WILLIAM FRANCIS GRAHAME, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Wellington and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1932, and Oct., 1933; ditto (subs. provl.), March, 1935.

LE BROCO, EDWARD HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 25th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 6th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., Apl., 1926; confd., Aug., 1934; princpl., trng. coll., Sardah, Apl., 1939, to Feb., 1943.

LE FEVRE, JOHN REGINALD, Indian Police (b. 15th Aug., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., Nov., 1940.

LECKY-THOMPSON, THOMAS EDWARD, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 7th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, June, 1926; offg. dist. supt., June, 1934.

LECKY - THOMPSON, WILLIAM, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 4th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 10th Sept., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., Feb., 1936; supt. of police supplies, March, 1938; confd., dist. supt. police, May, 1940.

LEDGER, LLOYD KIRKWOOD, O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil and agency surg.) (b. 27th May, 1895).—First commn. dated 15th March, 1920; in milty. employ up to 30th June, 1925; offg. agency surg., Peshawar, July, 1925; offg. medl. offcr., Seistan; agency surg., Bundelkhand, May, 1929; ditto, Gilgit, Oct., 1932; civil surg., Peshawar, Oct., 1936; O.B.E., June, 1938.

LEDWIDGE, WILLIAM BERNARD JOHN, B.A., India Office (asst. principal, political dept.) (b. 9th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at Cardinal Vaughan Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; held Commonwealth Fund Fellowship at Princeton Univ., U.S.A., Sept., 1937, to June, 1939; apptd. asst. principal, 2nd Oct., 1939; served with H.M. Forces from March, 1940.

LEE, REV. ARTHUR OLDFIELD NORRIS, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 6th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 30th Nov., 1921; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1931; retd., June, 1942.

LEE, DIONYS JOHN NORRIS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Oct., 1892).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Apl., 1915; joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1919, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr., from 2nd Dec., 1919; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; dep. commr. (provl.), Dec., 1926; confd., March, 1927; retd., Sept., 1940.

LEE, EDWARD OWEN, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of excise, etc., Bihar) (b. 3rd Nov., 1891).—Educ. at Malvern and King's Coll.,

Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; asst. commr., Assam, Apl., 1922; services replaced at disposal of govt. of Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1925; mag. and collr. (prov. sub.), Jan., 1929; confd., May, 1930; offg. dep. commr., Delhi, Apl., 1929; chairman, Assam labour bd., Apl., 1933; contr. of emigrant lab. in Assam, Oct., 1933; commr. of excise, etc., Bihar, Oct., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1944.

LEE, GEORGE HENRY, M.C., late Indian Police (b. 19th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 18th Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supt., March, 1931; off. dep. supt., watch and ward dept., E.I. rly., Apl., 1933; dist. supt., June, 1939; ret'd., March, 1940.

LEE, JOSEPH STANDISH SEARCHFIELD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 8th Oct., 1883).—Ret'd., Oct., 1938.*

LEE, REV. CANON PERCY ERSKINE, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 9th Feb., 1882).—Ret'd., Aug., 1937.*

LEE, REGINALD HENRY, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 19th May, 1880).—Ret'd., May, 1935.*

LEE, REV. ROBERT EWING, M.A., B.D., M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Bombay) (b. 26th Sept., 1883).—Ret'd., Aug., 1937.*

LEEPER, WILLIAM CLUFF, Maj., O.B.E., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Nov., 1899).—First commn., 15th Apl., 1919; in milty. employ to 21st Aug., 1928; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1929; pers. asst. to ch. commr. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., May, 1930; asst. commr., Peshawar, May, 1931; ditto, Kohat, March, 1933; ditto, Nowshera, June, 1933; ditto, Charsadda, Oct., 1933; ditto, Mardan, Feb., 1935; asst. commr., Manshera, May, 1937; pol. agent, Kurram, Oct., 1937; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., devel. depts., March, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., home dept., Feb., 1943.

LEES, LEONARD MARTYN, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 2nd Dec., 1898).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 21st Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt. (Edn.), June, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1927; dep. commr., p.s., Jan., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; secty., fin. dept., Oct., 1939; secty., L. & R. dept., May, 1940.

LEES, ROGER EDWARD ROWLEY, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 14th Aug., 1914).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 22nd Nov., 1934.

LEIGH, REV. ARTHUR GEORGE DAVIES, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senior chaplain) (b. 3rd Apl., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 26th Dec., 1926; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1928; senior chaplain, Dec., 1938.

LEIGHTON, ERIC, Indian Police (cent. intell. offcr., Orissa) (b. 11th May, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 7th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1928; cent. intell. offcr., Orissa, Apl., 1940.

LE MESURIER, ALGERNON PAUL, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 28th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Rugby, Lausanne, and Ariel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr., June, 1936; confd., June, 1939.

LENNAN, AIDAN WILLIAM, late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 12th Aug., 1913).—Ret'd., Dec., 1937.*

LESLIE, ANDREW SMITH, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 24th June, 1882).—Ret'd., June, 1937.*

LESSLIE, DAVID FINDLAY, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 31st March, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Jan., 1920; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd., Aug., 1929; asst. to dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936, to Dec., 1941.

LESTER-GARLAND, REV. GEORGE HENRY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 9th May, 1895).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., N.W.F.P., 26th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1926; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1936.

L'ESTRANGE, ALBERT PERCIVAL PORTER, Land Records Dept., Burma (asst. dir.) (b. 13th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as probr., 22nd Sept., 1903; supt., Aug., 1907; asst. dir., Aug., 1930.

LETHBRIDGE, MONTAGU HENRY BOWATER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 22nd March, 1889).—Educ. at Westminster, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1915; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Apl., 1918; dist. and sess. judge and commr. for workmen's compensation, Nov., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Assam, legisl. dept., Oct., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; dist. and sess. judge, Bengal, Nov., 1935; confd. in selection grade, Jan., 1939.

LETT, HARRY NELSON, F.D.C., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 21st Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Southampton, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 21st Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925; confd., March, 1935; secty. to finl. commr., Rangoon, Aug., 1933; chief a.r.p. authy. for Burma, June, 1940; civil def. commr. Burma, Jan., 1941.

LETTON, CHARLES THOMAS, C.I.E., M.B.E., late Printing and Stationery Dept. (b. 25th March, 1878).—Ret'd., March, 1935.*

LEVINGE, SIR EDWARD VERE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa).—Ret'd., Aug., 1918.*

LEWIS, SIR CLINTON GRESHAM, Brig., O.B.E., R.E., late Survey of India (b. 25th Nov., 1885).—First commn. dated 21st Dec., 1904; apptd. asst. supt., trigl. survey, 7th Nov., 1907; in charge Miri mission survey detachment, Oct., 1911, to Apl., 1912; tempy. reverted to mily. duty, Oct., 1914, to July, 1918; dep. supt., Aug., 1922; on foreign service with Turco-Iraq frontier delimitation commn., from Feb., 1927; supt., Apl., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1928; offg. dir., Apl., 1933; confd., June, 1934; surveyor-genl., Sept., 1937; kt., Jan., 1941; ret'd., Aug., 1941.

LEWIS, ELWYN BYRON, M.B.E., Cent. Excises and Salt Dept., Northern India (chief mining engr., Khewra) (b. 3rd Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 5th Nov., 1935; chief mining engr., Khewra, Nov., 1935; M.B.E., Jan., 1944.

LEWIS, LUCIEN ALEXANDER, M.I.E.E., Office of High Commr. for India (dep. dir. of inspection, store dept.) (b. 3rd May, 1882).—Educ. at Brighton Gr. Sch., and City and Guilds Inst., Finsbury Coll.; apptd. asst. inspr. of scientific supplies, store depot, 19th Nov., 1906; electrical engr., ditto, Aug., 1917; dep. dir. of

inspection, store dept., July, 1936; actg. dir., ditto, Dec., 1939.

LEWIS, REV. OLIVER GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 11th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 5th Oct., 1929; junior chaplain, Oct., 1931.

LEWIS, SYDNEY ALLAN MAYBURY, M.R.San.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Sind) (b. 23rd Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1933; suptg. engr. (provl. perin.), May, 1936; confd., Dec., 1937; ret'd., 29th Sept., 1941.

LEWIS, WALTER ALAN SCOTT, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st July, 1907).—Educ. at Winchester and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1933; chairman, nat. serv. lab. trib., Bengal, Jan., 1942; contr., purchase, dept. of civ. supplies, Calcutta; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

LEWIS, SIR WILLIAM HAWTHORNE, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (gouv. of Orissa) (b. 29th June, 1888).—Educ. at Oundle, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on censor duty, Bombay, from Apl., 1915, to March, 1916; under-sec. to govt., rev. and judl. depts., Bihar and Orissa, June, 1918; jt. mag., March, 1921; offg. dep. commr., March, 1923; offg. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Oct., 1925; confd. as mag. collr., Oct., 1926; on spl. duty, home dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1927; jt. secty., reforms office, May, 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; jt. secty., reforms office, Jan., 1932; offg. reforms commr., govt. of India, May, 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; addl. secty., reforms, govt. of India, Feb., 1935; reforms commr., July, 1936; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938; K.C.S.I., 24th Dec., 1940; govr. of Orissa, Apl., 1941.

LEWIS, WILLIAM HOWARD, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 7th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 14th Nov., 1927; offg. supt., Feb., 1934; offg. dep. commr., Calcutta, Feb., 1936; on depn. to Orissa state from Feb., 1939; confd., supt., March, 1941.

LEWISOHN, LEON HORNBY, Indian Police (b. 24th Dec., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, Nov., 1932; confd., March, 1935; a.d.c. to govr., Orissa, Feb., 1938; on depn. under Crown repres. police, Necmuch, Dec., 1939, to May, 1942.

LEWYS-LLOYD, IDRIS WYN, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 7th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Colwyn County Sch., Univ. of Wales, and Paris; joined the service, 10th Dec., 1928; arrived, 12th Jan., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1934; settl. offcr., Nov., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; sec. to govr., Sept., 1944.

LIAQAT HAYAT KHAN, SIR, NAWAB, KHAN BAHADUR, K.B.E., late Indian Police Service (Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 22nd May, 1909; asst. supt., March, 1919; supt., Dec., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1923; home sec., Patiala state, Oct., 1923; ret'd., June, 1931; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd

session, Nov., 1932; kt., Jan., 1933; K.B.E., Jan., 1939.

LIDIERTH, JAMES EUGENE, I.S.O., M.B.E. (b. 12th Oct., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st June, 1908, to 15th Oct., 1919; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1921; offg. under-sec. to agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1922, and again, Aug., 1929; awarded K.I.H. medal (2nd class) in 1925; registrar, A.G.G.'s office, Quetta, Apl., 1929; asst. secty. to A.G.G. and C.C. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1929; offg. under-sec., ditto, Dec., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1934; offg. under-sec. to A.G., Baluchistan, July, 1935; I.S.O., May, 1937.

LIGHTFOOT, GORDON SHELLEY, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 18th Aug., 1897).—Served in the Indian Army from 12th May, 1915, to 18th May, 1923; proby. asst. supt., 19th May, 1923; confd., May, 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; supt., May, 1930; pol. officer, Balipara frontier tract, Oct., 1934; ditto Charduar, Oct., 1938, supt., Mar., 1943.

LIGHTOWLER, IVOR HARRISON, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. optg. supt.) (b. 21st Dec., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 20th May, 1912; asst. contr. of stores, Apl., 1919; dist. ditto, March, 1931; dep. ch. optg. supt., Aug., 1941.

LILEY, WALTER HEMMING, A.M.I.M.E., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 7th June, 1882).—Ret'd., Dec., 1935.*

LILLIE, CECIL JAMES WINGATE, Indian C.S. (dep. secty., govt. of India, home dept.) (b. 6th July, 1897).—Educ. at Clifton, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1921; arrived, 1st Dec., 1921; and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925; under-sec. to govt., Dec., 1931; offg. dep. commr., March, 1932; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., May, 1933; offg. secty., rev. dept., Central Provs., Nov., 1936; dep. commr., March, 1937; dep. secty., govt. of India, home dept., Nov., 1938; and again, Oct., 1939; on depn. to H.E.H. the Nizam's govt., June, 1942, to July, 1944.

LILLIE, HORACE, late Indian Police (b. 2nd Apl., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1906; asst. supt., Nov., 1907; supt., Aug., 1914; comdt., frontier constaby., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1933; ret'd., 1940.

LINCKE, SYDNEY HOWARD PHIPPS, Indian State Rlys. (dep. contr. of stores, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 7th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 8th Nov., 1912; dist. contr. of stores, Nov., 1926; dep. ditto, Nov., 1928; offg. contr. of stores, variously from Nov., 1929; confd., June, 1939.

LINCOLN, EDWARD HENNING, V.D., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 16th March, 1889).—Joined the service, 14th Aug., 1905; extra asst. commr., March, 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1933; offg. secty., elec. and indus. depts., Punjab, Apl., to July, 1936.

LINCOLN, HENRY HENNING, M.B.E., late Dept. of Educn., Health and Lands, Govt. of India (b. 25th March, 1882).—Ret'd., Sept., 1937.*

LINDOP, KENNETH JAMES HULME, O.B.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (commr. of settlements and land records, Burma) (b. 13th July, 1898).—Educ. at Denston Coll., Staffs, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma

as asst. commr.; offg. settlt. officer, March, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1928; confd., Jan., 1936; commr. of settlements and land records, July, 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

LINDSAY, SIR HARRY ALEXANDER FANSHAW, K.C.I.E., C.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 11th March, 1881).—Retd., Feb., 1937.*

LINDSAY, ROBERT WILLIAM (b. 20th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th July, 1909; held non-gaz. appt. in mil. dept.; services placed at disp. of govt. of Burma, p.w. dept., from Sept., 1921, as asst. engr.; employed as exec. engr., Port Blair, variously from Apl., 1923, and from Oct., 1929; again asst. engr. (B.E.S.), Burma, Dec., 1928, to Sept., 1929.

LINDSAY, REV. W. T., M.A., Ind. Eccles. Estabt. (b. 2nd Jan., 1897).—Apptd. junior chaplain, Bombay, on probn., 8th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

LINES, ROY NICHOLAS, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 11th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Royal Latin Sch., Buckingham, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

LINGWOOD, FREDERICK, late Office of the High Commr. for India (b. 1st March, 1901).—Retd., Apl., 1944.*

LINLITHGOW, MOST HON. MARQUESS OF, VICTOR ALEXANDER JOHN HOPE, P.C., K.G., K.T., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., O.B.E., D.L., T.D. (b. 24th Sept., 1887).—Educ. at Eton; served European War, 1914-1918 (despatches); civil lord of the admy., 1922-24; presdt., navy league, 1924-31; chairman, departt. ctee. on distribn. and prices of agricl. produce, 1923; chairman, Rl. commn. on Indian agriculture, 1926-28; chairman, jt. select ctee. on Indian constittl. reform, 1933; G.C.S.I., Apl., 1936; vice-roy and govr.-genl. of India, Apl., 1936, to Oct., 1943; Kt. of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, June, 1936.

LISTER, REV. JOHN GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 8th March, 1885).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 2nd Feb., 1914; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1927.

LISTER, TOM, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 10th May, 1887).—Retd., May, 1937.*

LISTER-JACKSON, ARTHUR, late Indian State Rly. (b. 18th Sept., 1891).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

LITTLE, EDWARD PYBUS, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 29th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1935; under govt. of India, def. dept., Oct., 1939.

LITTLEWOOD, GLADYS ELIZABETH, M.B.E., Indian Educl. Service (N.W.F.P.) (b. 1st Nov., 1889).—Joined the service, 16th June, 1918; inspectress of schools, Ambala, Nov., 1919; ditto, N.W.F.P., May, 1920; K.-i.-H. Medal, 1st class, June, 1934; retd.,

LITTLEWOOD, ROWLAND WHITE LAW, Indian Agric. Service (b. 19th June, 1888).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, Jan., 1921.

LITTLEWORTH, MICHAEL CHARLES GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 19th May, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs. on probn., Nov., 1938.

LLEWELLYN, JOHN LISTER, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 10th Apl., 1908).—Educ. at Notting-

ham High Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1944; sec., Bengal admin. enq. ctee., Nov., 1944.

LLOYD, SIR ALAN HUBERT, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Burma, attd. Impl. Customs Service (establishment offer., govt. of India) (b. 30th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. asst. collr. of customs, impl. customs service, Rangoon, March, 1910; confd., Nov., 1911; transfid. to Bengal as asst. collr. of customs, June, 1916; on depn. under commercial intell. dept., India, Dec., 1916, to June, 1917; offg. collr. of customs, June, 1917; confd., Dec., 1919; apptd. first member of central board of rev., govt. of India, Dec., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; tempy. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, June, 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; member, council of state, Apl., 1935; kt., Jan., 1939; tempy. addtl. secty. to govt. of India, commerce dept., May, 1939; sec., ditto, Aug., 1939; retd., Nov., 1942; estabt. offr., govt. of India, Nov., 1942; ditto, and examr. of capital issues, May, 1943.

LLOYD, BARON, RT. HON. GEORGE AMBROSE LLOYD OF DOLOBRAN, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., D.S.O., F.R.G.S.—Govr. of Bombay from 16th Dec., 1918, to 9th Dec., 1923.

LLOYD, SIR IDWAL GEOFFREY, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 13th Jan., 1878).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

LLOYD, REGINALD BROUGHTON, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 31st Aug., 1881).—Retd., Apl., 1936.*

LLOYD, STEPHEN, late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 5th Sept., 1906).—Retd., May, 1935.*

LLOYD-JONES, CYRIL MICHAEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 1st Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Oundle and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 22nd Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

LOBB, JOHN CHALICE, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1897).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1919; supt., Nov., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., variously from Apl., 1935; senr. supt., police, Delhi, Dec. 1942; O.B.E., June, 1943.

LOBO, CHARLES MANUEL, LL.B., Judl. Dept., Bombay (judge, chief ct., Sind) (b. 11th Aug., 1884).—Apptd. govt. pleader and public prosecutor for Sind, 1st Apl., 1929; offg. addl. commr. for Sind, July, 1929; and again, Jan., 1936; confd., March, 1936; addl. judl. commr., Sind, May, 1938; judge, chief ct. of Sind, Apl., 1940.

LOBO-PRABHU, JOSEPH MATHIAS, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 26th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the United Provs. as asst. collr. and mag.; offer. on spec. duty at govt. h.q. (fin.); confd. as mag. and collr., May, 1942; dep. sec., govt. of India (food), July, 1944.

LOCH, PERCY GORDON (name changed to DALYELL of the BINNS), C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—Retd., May, 1938.*

LODGE, RONALD FRANCIS, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 8th Apr., 1889).—Educ. at Liverpool Institute High Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1925; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apr., 1935, and again, Aug., 1936; regr. high ct., 1937; confd. in sel. gr., May, 1939; apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apr., 1941.

LOGAN, FRANCIS Hugh late Indian C.S., (United Provs.) (b. 1st July, 1904).—Retd., June, 1938.*

LOGAN, ROBERT ANGEL, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 13th Nov., 1889).—First commn., 10th Oct., 1919; civil surgeon, Apr., 1932.

LOKUR, NARAYAN SWAMIRAO, B.A., LL.B., Bombay C.S. (judge, Bombay) (b. 21st Apr., 1887).—Held various judl. appts. from 22nd Oct., 1918, to 31st Dec., 1925; judge and sess. judge, Bombay, Jan., 1926; judge, high ct., ditto, Dec., 1942.

LONG, CECIL EUSTACE, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 28th Feb., 1882).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

LONGRIGG, JOHN HEMSLY, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 6th Apr., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1913; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; offg. consvr., Aug., 1928; princpl. for. coll., Coimbatore, Jan., 1937; dep. consvr., July, 1939.

LONSDALE, CHRISTOPHER EDWIN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, 19th Nov., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934.

LORIMER, ALEXANDER PATRICK GORDON, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 9th Dec., 1880).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

LORT-WILLIAMS, SIR JOHN ROLLESTON, Kt., late Judl. Dept., Bengal (b. 14th Sept., 1881).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's Sch. and London Univ.; called to Bar, 1904; member of L.C.C. from 1907 to 1910; M.P. for Rotherhithe, 1918 to 1923; judge, high ct., Calcutta, 28th Dec., 1927 to June, 1941; kt., June, 1936.

LOTHIAN, SIR ARTHUR CUNNINGHAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 27th June, 1887).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. mag. and asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1917; temp. offg. as asst. to pol. agent, Bunkelkhand, from Nov., 1915; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Dec., 1920; sec. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Apr., 1924, and again, Nov., 1927; dep. sec., govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., July, 1926; secty. to resdt., Mysore, May, 1928; resdt., Jaipur, Apr., 1929; on spl. duty, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1931; resdt., Baroda, July, 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, March, 1933; resdt., Jaipur, June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Rajputana, Apr., 1934; addl. secty. to the govt. of India, f. and p. dept., Apr., 1935; employed as spl. representative of the Viceroy, Oct., 1936; addtl. secty. to govt. of India, f. and p. dept., Feb., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; resdt. for Rajputana and C.C. in Ajmer Merwara, Oct., 1937;

K.C.I.E., June, 1941; resdt. at Hyderabad, offg., Oct., 1942; confd., Feb., 1944.

LOTHIAN, MARQUIS OF (cr. 1701), Most Hon. Philip Henry Kerr, C.H.—Parliamentary under-secty. of state for India, Nov., 1931, to Sept., 1932.

LOVATT, ARTHUR WILLIAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 1st Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Dunfermline High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., Vienna Univ., and London Sch. of Economics; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. sec. to govr., Bombay, Jan., 1941; under-sec., Eastern group supply cl., Oct., 1942; under-sec., govt. of India (home), Apr., 1943.

LOVELAND, FRANK, Indian State Ryls. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 14th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 23rd July, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Ryls., Aug., 1932.

LOVETT, SIR HARRINGTON VERNEY, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.).—Retd., Oct., 1919.*

LOW, ALASTAIR PETRIE, O.B.E., Indian C.S. Ind. Political Serv. (b. 30th Jan., 1908).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., pol. and home depts., Punjab, March, 1934; under-secty. to A.G.G., states of W.I., Nov., 1935; secty. to A.G.G., Gujerat states, May, 1936; asst. commr., Charsadda, Apr., 1938; addtl. asst. pol. agt., S. Waziristan, Dec., 1939; asst. commr., Hangu, Oct., 1940; dy. commr., Bannu, Oct., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; finl. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1944.

LOW, PETER DUNSTAN, O.B.E., A.M.I.E.E., Indian State Ryls. (b. 29th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as dist. elec. engr., E.I. Ry., 7th Apr., 1927; dep. ch. mech. engr., elec., B. & A. rly., March, 1940.

LOW, THOMAS RALPH, M.C., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (princl. and prof. of agric.) (b. 21st Dec., 1893).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 4th Dec., 1920; princpl., agric. coll., Cawnpore, May, 1934; offg. dir. of agric., United Provs., Oct., 1935; dir., inst. of plant industry, Indore, July, 1936; confd. in sel. grade, Aug., 1940; dep. dir., Aug., 1941; on depn. to defence dept., Nov., 1941; dir. of agric. and war prodn., Apr., 1943.

LOWE, MISS IRENE H., M.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras) (b. 4th May, 1890).—Apptd. inspectress of schools, 29th March, 1922; dep. directress of public instrn., Aug., 1926; retd., May, 1945.

LOWIS, ROSS HENRY DONALD, M.B.E., Indian Police, Indian Political Service (b. 5th Feb., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1924; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Feb., 1936; under trng., Punjab, Dec., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1938; asst. pol. agt., Chitral, Dec., 1939; confd. in Ind. pol. service, Dec., 1939; asst. commr., Hangu, Oct., 1941; pol. agt., N. Waziristan, Jan., 1943.

LOWNDS, EDWIN ALBERT, late India Office (b. 24th Apr., 1878).—Retd., Apr., 1939.*

LOWTHER, ERNEST HERBERT NEWTON, Indian State Ryls. (divl. supt., E.I. Ry.) (b. 19th May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 10th June, 1911; dist. supt. (s.p.t.), July, 1923; supt. (s.p.t.), Feb., 1925; confd., July, 1925; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., July, 1932;

offg. dep. agent, personnel, E.I. rly, March, 1936; divl. supt., Oct., 1940.

LUBY, THOMAS, M.A., V.D., late Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 12th Nov., 1882).—Educ. at Clifton, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived 28th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1913; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1921; offg. judge, high ct., Patna, July, 1934; and again, July, 1935; retd., 28th Nov., 1942.

LUCAS, E. S. S., Capt., I.M.S. (civ. surg.) (b. 1st Nov., 1897).—Date of first commn., 20th Aug., 1928; civil surgeon, United Provs., Nov., 1934.

LUCAS, HENRY CHARLES EDWIN, Indian Police (b. 27th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 25th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., June, 1934; confd., March, 1940.

LUCAS, LIONEL JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 5th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at St. Xavier's Coll., Calcutta, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Dec., 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1935; dep. secty., education, etc., dept., Bihar, Apl., 1939; dep. commr., Port Blair, March, 1940-July, 1941; dep. sec., educn., etc., dept., Apl., 1944.

LUCE, GORDON HANNINGTON, M.A. (Can-tab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 20th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as profir., Rangoon Coll., 1st Nov., 1912; on mil. service from 24th July, 1918, to 9th Feb., 1919; lecturer in English, May, 1922; lecturer in Burmese and Far Eastern history, Sept., 1929; retd., 1st Nov. 1943.

LUCKMAN, REV. SYDNEY, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 3rd Nov., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 1st May, 1921; junr. chaplain, May, 1923; senr. chaplain, May, 1933; retd., Nov., 1944.

LUMBY, ESMOND WALTER RAWSON, M.A., India Office (princl., pol. dept.) (b. 27th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; asst. princpl., customs and excise dept., 23rd Oct., 1933, to 14th Oct., 1934; asst. princpl., India Office, 15th Oct., 1934; pte. secty. to parly. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937; actg. princpl., Oct., 1938; confd., May, 1939.

LUMLEY, SIR (LAWRENCE) ROGER, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., J.P., D.L. (b. 27th July, 1896).—Govr. of Bombay, Sept., 1937, to March, 1943; G.C.S.I., June, 1943.

LWIN, MAUNG SAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 17th Dec., 1911).—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 18th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; western sub-divl. mag., Rangoon, Dec., 1939; under-secty., educn. dept., Jan., 1941.

LYALL, JOHN HENRY, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Bihar) (b. 16th Aug., 1885).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1910; asst. consvr., Dec., 1910; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; offg. consvr., June, 1929; ditto, United Provs., May, 1930; ditto, Bengal, March, 1931; ditto, Punjab, Oct., 1933; dep. consvr., Bihar, 1939; retd., Aug., 1940.

LYDALL, EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 4th Aug., 1907).—Educ. at Marlborough and St. John's Coll.,

Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. and pers. asst. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Nov., 1935; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1936; offer. on spec. duty, Kalat agency, Aug., 1936; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, March, 1938; asst. commr., Peshawar, Feb., 1938; sec., Kabul legation, Nov., 1939; confd. in Ind. pol. serv., Sept., 1940; asst. commr., Nowshera, Sept., 1942; presdt. Manipur State Durbar, May, 1943.

LYDE, LIONEL GILDEA, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 13th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Rugby, Wadham Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived, 30th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1932.

LYLE, ARTHUR NEVIN (late R.E.), O.B.E., B.A., B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E. (suptg. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; confd., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr. (confd.), May, 1941.

LYLE, THOMAS McELDERRY, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 24th May, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Nov., 1917; C.I.E., Dec., 1928; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1931; confd., Nov., 1933; ch. engr. and jt. secty. to govt., United Provs., irrign. bch. (tempy), Aug., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937; C.S.I., June, 1941; retd., 24th May, 1941.

LYNN, GREGORY, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 13th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service, Nov., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. exec. engr., June, 1928; confd., March, 1938.

LYSTER, ANTHONY ST. GEORGE, C.S.I., B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs (Punjab) (b. 13th March, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Oct., 1911; on mil. duty from 12th March, 1915, to 10th March, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1919; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1933; on depn. to Bihar, Apl., 1936; secty., p.w.d. (irrign.), Apl., 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1943; retd., 13th Apl., 1943.

LYTTON, EARL OF, Rt. HON. VICTOR ALEXANDER GEORGE ROBERT LYTTON, K.G., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., P.C.—Parly. under-sec. of state for India, 1st Oct., 1920; govr. of Bengal from March, 1922, to March, 1927; viceroy and actg. govr.-genl. of India, 10th Apl. to 5th Aug., 1925.

MACALPINE, ROBERT IAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th March, 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928; on mil. serv. May, 1940.

MACARTHUR, DONALD FREDERICK SWAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 7th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st May, 1913; on mil. service from 17th Oct., 1918, to 28th Feb., 1920; asst. engr., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., June, 1921; offg. exec. engr., June, 1921; confd., June, 1928.

MACAULAY, DONALD, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr.) (b. 4th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1920, as asst. loco. supt.; offg. works manager, E.B. rly., Dec., 1930; dist. loco. supt., E.B. rly. (prov.), Aug., 1934; dep. ch. mechl. engr. (elec.), March, 1940.

MCBRIDE, J. B., O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E., late Bengal Factories Service (b. 9th Aug., 1889).—

Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1923; inspr. of factories, Sept., 1924; chief inspr. factories, Bengal, Sept., 1931; O.B.E., June, 1939; retd., Aug., 1944.

MCALL, ANTHONY GILCHRIST, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 7th Jan., 1895).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and R.M.A., Woolwich; joined the service, 5th Aug., 1921; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1930; dep. commr., Oct., 1935; confd., Aug., 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

MCCARTHY, DENIS ARTHUR MACKAURA, Indian Police (b. 11th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Apl., 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; comdt. and dist. supt., Port Blair, Dec., 1936; supt., confd., Apl., 1939.

MC CAUL, REV. MATTHEW WILSON, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 19th Nov., 1882).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

MCLENNAGHAN, HERBERT ERIC ST. GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr.) (b. 5th Aug., 1896).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 14th Nov., 1922; arrived, 8th Jan., 1923, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1926; March, 1931, and again, Apl., 1933; confd., March, 1934; collr., central excise, Bombay, Apl., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

MACCOLL, ALEXANDER MALCOLM, Indian Police (supt., river divn.) (b. 28th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 26th Jan., 1920; dist. supt., Nov., 1924; supt., river divn., Nov., 1936.

MACCOLL, HUGH HERBERT, M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 7th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Nov., 1921; offg. dep. consvr., June, 1923; confd., Apl., 1927.

MC CONAGHEY, RONALD ALLEN, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 21st May, 1912).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., June, 1937; vice-consul, Bunder-Abbas, Apl., 1944; and consul, Kerman (in addn.), Oct., 1944.

MCCORKELL, DAVID ROBERT ANDREW, Indian Police (b. 22nd Apl., 1916).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1936; transf. milty. dept., Nov., 1940.

MCCRACKEN, ANDREW, M.A., and Indian C.S.
