(b. 21st Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Ayr Academy and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1919; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept.; asst. rice commr., Rangoon, Feb., 1920; undersec. to govt., May, 1921; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, reforms office, June, 1930, to Jan., 1932; secty., home dept., govt. of Burma, Feb., 1938; commr., (subst.), Apl., 1942.

MCCREA, KENNETH IAN, M.B.E., Indian Police (b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 16th Nov., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; offg. dep. commr., Kyautpyu, Apl., 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

MACDERMOT, PATRICK JOSEPH, India Office (b. 2nd Feb., 1885).—Apptd. clerical officer, 23rd Aug., 1921; higher clerical officer, Oct., 1931; asst. supt., tel. and mails branch,

March, 1942; actg. asst. supt., record dept., Aug., 1943.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER, M.A. (Glas.), B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 13th Nov., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER MACLEOD, Indian Police (b. 10th Oct., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935; confd., Mar., 1937.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER RANALD HUME, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 3rd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Assam, March, 1934; confd., Aug., 1934; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1934; ditto, S. Waziristan, Nov., 1935; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, Feb., 1937; dep. sec. (home), Nov., 1941; dep. commr. (provl.), Jan., 1942.

MACDONALD, REV. ALISDAIR JOHN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (senr. chaplain) (b. 12th June, 1900).—Apptd. chaplain on probn. Church of Scotland, Oct., 1933; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1935; mil. service, Dec., 1937, to Apl., 1942; presdy. senr. chaplain, July, 1944.

MACDONALD, ANGUS ALEXANDER, O.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Nov., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Edinburgh, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928, and arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1942; confd. as dep. commr., Nov., 1942; home sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1943.

MACDONALD, REV. DONALD, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 22nd Sept., 1891).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, 27th Feb., 1927; apptd. senr. chaplain, Feb., 1937.

MCDONALD, DONALD CHARLES, late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. consvr., 7th May, 1912; confd., Oct., 1916; in milty. employ from 29th March, 1918, to 20th Feb., 1919; apptd. to I.F.S., 25th June, 1929; dep. consvr., June, 1929; employed under Rewa Durbar, Feb., 1933; retd., March, 1944.

MACDONALD, HECTOR IAN, O.B.E., late Military Accounts Dept. (b. 10th Aug., 1885).—Joined service, Jan., 1905; asst. financial adv., milty. fin., Jan., 1921; confd., March, 1921; officer in class II, office of C.M.A., Lahore dist., Apl., 1926; dep. finl. advr., milty. fin., Nov., 1929; officer on specl. duty, Army Dept., Nov., 1929; dir., regulations and forms, Apl., 1930; officer on specl. duty, fin. dept., Jan., 1932; O.B.E., Jan., 1933; dep. secty. (revision), govt. of India, defence dept., Aug., 1937; retd., Feb., 1940.

MACDONALD, IAN HAY, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Orissa) (b. 19th July, 1915).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 16th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Orissa, May, 1939; sub. divl. offr., Apl., 1940; jt. mag. and collr., Oct., 1943.

MCDONALD, THOMAS, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Central Provs., 11th Oct., 1929; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934; served under def. dept., govt. of India,

MACDONELL, OLIVER STEPHEN, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 2nd May, 1878).—Retd., Apl., 1935.*

McDOUGALL, JAMES CURRIE, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 5th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1925; offg. princpl., agric. coll., Nagpur, Nov., 1928; confd., Jan., 1934; dir. of agric. (provl.), Central Provs., Oct., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; retd., 5th Jan., 1945.

MACDOUGALL, RAIBEART MACINTYRE, K.C.M.G., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (counsellor to govr. of Burma) (b. 30th Apl., 1892).—Educ. at Greenock Academy and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1914, and posted to Burma; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; under-sec. to govt., Sept., 1920; regr., high ct., Rangoon (offg.), March, 1923; confd., Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1926; confd., June, 1930; secty., local govt. dept., March, 1931; secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, March, 1934; and again, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1935; secty., home dept., Oct., 1939; commr., confd., Apl., 1941; counsellor to govr., Nov., 1941; K.C.M.G., Jan., 1945.

McDOWALL, ROGER GORDON, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Dumfries Academy, Glasgow, and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1911, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. asst. sec. to govt., Sept., 1916; regr., chief ct., Lower Burma, July, 1920; regr., high ct., Rangoon, Dec., 1922; offg. dep. commr., from Nov., 1923; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), July, 1927; offg. secty., fin. dept., March, 1929; confd., Sept., 1929; secty., reforms office, Nov., 1932; secty. to govt., Burma, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; retd., June, 1941.

MACDOWELL, KENNETH MACLEAN, O.B.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Burma) (b. 16th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr., Jan., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

MACEDWARD, REV. LACHLAN, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 25th Apl., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 15th Feb., 1926; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1928; senr. ditto, Feb., 1938; retd., Aug., 1944.

MCELHINNY, GEOFFREY WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Dec., 1900).—Educ. at Rugby and Corpus Christi, Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 5th Dec., 1924, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1928; under-secty. to govt., home, etc., depts., Sept., 1929; member, legisl. council, Feb., 1932; offg. collr., Sept., 1933; temp. addl. dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1934; collr., Sindh., Nov., 1937; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1939; sec. to govt., Bombay, gen. and educn. dept., June, 1944.

MACEWEN, ALEXANDER ROBERT, C.I.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 8th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1919; arrived 3rd Jan., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1922; offg. under-sec., rev. dept., June, 1924; spcl. asst. settlt. offr., Aug., 1924; spcl. settlt. offr., July, 1925; secty. to bd. of rev., Oct., 1930; collr. (provl. subs.), Jan., 1936; confd., March, 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938; secty. to govt., home dept., Madras,

Dec., 1938; collr., March, 1942; sec. fin. dept., Apl., 1944; sec., p.w.d., Oct., 1944; spcl. offr., rev. dept., Nov., 1944.

MACEWEN, ROBERT BEGG, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 2nd July, 1898).—Retd., Apl., 1937.*

MACFARLANE, DONALD, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Aug., 1882).—Retd.,

MACFARQUHAR, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (rev. sec. to finl. commrs., Punjab) (b. 6th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Royal Academy, Inverness, Univ. of Aberdeen, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Feb., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936; rev. sec. to finl. commrs. and dep. sec. to govt., Punjab, Dec., 1940; dep. sec., dept. of supply, govt. of India, Apl., 1941; dep. dir. ditto, Dec., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

MACGREGOR, ANDREW DAWSON, F.R.C.V.S. Indian Vety. Service (princpl. veterinary coll., Bengal) (b. 5th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1914; I.A.R.O. from 19th Feb., 1916; offg. supt., civil vety. dept., Jan., 1919; confd. July, 1921; princpl., vety. coll., Bengal, Apl., 1922; also veterinary adv. to govt. of Bengal (in addition) from March, 1938.

MACGREGOR, ROBERT ANDERSON, C.I.E., M.I.Mech.E., M.I.S.I., M.I.E.I., F.R.S.A., M.M.G.I. (metallurg. inspr.) (b. 9th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service as officer on special duty, 25th Feb., 1937; offg. metallurgical inspr., Apl., 1937; confd., June, 1937; dir., munitions prodcn., dept. of supply, July, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

MACGREGOR, ROBERT FORRESTER DOUGLAS, C.I.E., M.C., M.B., Lieut.-Col., retd., late I.M.S. (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1910; M.O., Seistan consulate, Dec., 1920; offg. agency surg., Bhopal, Sept., 1925; offg. resdy. surg., Indore, Sept., 1926; resdy. surg., Bushire, May, 1927; agency surg., Gilgit, Oct., 1927; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Sept., 1929; ditto, in C.I., Apl., 1931; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, March, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1939; attd. to office of dir.-genl., I.M.S., Sept., 1939; addl. dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., June, 1940; retd., Jan., 1942.

McGREGOR, WILLIAM MALCOLM, O.B.E., Indian State Ryis. (contr. of stores, N.W. Rly.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1889).—Joined the service, 26th Oct., 1908; dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., Aug., 1919; offg. dep. ditto, Oct., 1928; offg. contr., March, 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; contr. of stores, N.W. rly., Apl., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

McGUIGAN, HUGH SUTHERLAND, Indian Police (b. 28th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt. Bombay, on probn., 23rd Nov., 1936; confd., Sept., 1938.

McGUIRE, ROBERT ELY, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 22nd Aug., 1901).—Educ. at the High School, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dep. Nov., 1930; warden, oil fields, Sept., 1932; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1932; provl., Jan., 1937; confd. Apl., 1937; warden, oil-fields, July, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; addtl. sec., reconstrn. dept., 1944.

McINERNY, EDWARD FOLLET, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1910).—Educ. at Swansea Grammar School and

Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, March, 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1938; addl. mag., Dec., 1941.

MCINTOSH, ROBERT, M.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 23rd May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; M.B.E., Feb., 1937; suptg. engr., May, 1943.

MACINTYRE, JOHN MACLACHLAN, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1928; asst. exec. engr., Baluchistan, Aug., 1935; M.B.E., Jan., 1939.

MCINTYRE, THOMAS, M.B.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ryls. (bridge engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 4th March, 1898).—Asst. exec. engr., bridges, Dec., 1926; bridge engr., E.B. rly., Nov., 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1944.

MCIVER, COLIN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 21st Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1935.*

MACK, EDMUND ELMAR, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 2nd May, 1896).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 5th Jan., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1929; and again, Oct., 1932; confd., Jan., 1933.

MACKARNESSE, CUTHBERT GEORGE MILFORD, C.I.E., Indian Forest Service (senr. consvr., Assam) (b. 15th Apl. 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 7th Dec., 1912; in mil. employ from 23rd Nov., 1915 to 14th March, 1919; dep. consvr., June, 1919; pers. asst. to consvr., Assam, Apl., 1932 senr. consvr., Feb., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

MACKAY, HENRY FISHER, late India Office (b. 18th Oct., 1875).—Retd., Oct., 1936.*

MACKAY, THOMAS IAN SUTHERLAND, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 25th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; suptg. ditto, Aug., 1940; retd., Aug., 1944.

MCKEEVER, RONALD FRASER, Indian Police (b. 20th Aug., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Central Provs., on probn., 14th Nov., 1935; confd., Apl., 1938; supt., Eastern states, jt. police force, Sept., 1943.

MACKEITH, ALEXANDER ADAM, Indian Police (b. 19th March, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Dec., 1937; confd., Dec., 1939; King's Police Medal, Mar., 1940; a.d.c. to govr., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1941 to Apl., 1942.

MCKELVIE, GEORGE MAURICE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Central (p.w.d.)) (b. 21st Feb., 1896).—Joined the service (Central Provs.) as asst. exec. engr., 8th Dec., 1920; offg. exec. engr., and under-secty. to govt., July, 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1928; under-secty., p.w.d., Apl., 1937; on spec. duty with govt. of India, Nov., 1937; exec. engr., cent. p.w.d., Apl., 1938; suptg. engr., July, 1940; consltg. engr., govt. of India (roads), posts and air, Nov., 1942; ditto, war transport, July, 1944.

MCKELVIE, JOHN NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Malvern and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; a.r.p. offr., Sept., 1940; sub-divl. offr., May, 1943.

MCKELVIE, REV. ROBERT FRITZ STANLEY, M.A., D.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 14th Jan., 1882).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

MACKENZIE, REV. DONALD FRANCIS, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 31st March, 1885).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 30th Sept., 1919; junr. chaplain, Church of Scotland, Sept., 1921; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1927; presdty. senr. chaplain, Church of Scotland, Bengal, Aug., 1937; retd., 10th Aug., 1942.

MACKENZIE, REV. DONALD STEWART, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senior chaplain) (b. 2nd March, 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 24th Apl., 1919; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1921; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1930.

MACKENZIE, SIR DUNCAN GEORGE, K.C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 1st July, 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 29th Nov., 1907, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, June, 1912; asst. commr., Ajmer, March, 1913; city mag., Peshawar, Nov., 1914; asst. commr., N.W.F. Prov., Dec., 1915; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, Nov., 1918; first asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1919; sec. to the agent to the govr.-genl., Punjab states, Nov., 1921; pol. agent, southern Rajputana states, Nov., 1926; on foreign service in Bharatpur state, Feb., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1930; pol. agent in Bhopal, Nov., 1930; resdt., Jaipur, Apl., 1931; ditto and in western states of Rajputana, Jan., 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, March, 1933; resdt., Hyderabad, July, 1933; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937; retd., Feb., 1941.

MACKENZIE, JAMES MACAULAY, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 20th June, 1887).—Joined the service, 28th Dec., 1907; asst. supt., Apl., 1910; supt., Nov., 1915; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1929; confd., Aug., 1940; retd., 20th June, 1942.

MACKEOWN, JOHN AINLIE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., govt. of India supply dept.) (b. 27th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Radley and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1925; arrived, 6th Dec., 1925, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; deputed to Bahawalpur state, Nov., 1929; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1932; dep. secty., govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Feb., 1936; offg. jt. secty., ditto, July, 1936, and again Oct., 1936; addl. dep. secty., legisl. dept., govt. of India, March, 1937; dep. secty., dept. of indus. and lab., Apl. to July, 1937; addl. dep. secty., legislative dept., Aug. to Oct., 1937; dep. secty., communs. dept., Nov. to Dec., 1937; addl. dep. secty., legislative dept., Jan. to March, 1938; dep. secty. communs. dept., May, to July 1938; addl. dep. secty. legislative dept., July, 1938; dep. sec., dept. of supply, Sept., 1939; jt. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of supply, Apl., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1942.

MCKERRAL, ANDREW, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agr. Service, Burma (b. 3rd Sept., 1876).—Retd., March, 1936.*

MACKIE, ALFRED WILLIAM WHITE, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 20th Oct., 1877).—Retd., June, 1936.*

MACKIE, JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 10th Feb., 1928; exec. engr., Feb., 1937.

MACKIE, RONALD HASTINGS, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 3rd Dec., 1881).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

MACKINNON, JOHN, C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; on mil. duty from 4th May, 1916 to 17th May, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. dep. agent, N.W. Rly., May, 1926; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1929, and again, Oct., 1933; offg. dep. agent, March, 1931, and again, Oct., 1933; confd., Feb., 1934; offg. ch. engr., N.W. Rly., May, 1935; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., May, 1936; chief engr. (provl.), Nov., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1939; ret'd., Jan., 1941.

MACKINTOSH, CHARLES CALDER, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. engr., March, 1936; addl. ch. engr., p.w.d. (irrign. bch.), Burma, Feb., 1937; ret'd., June, 1941.

MACKINTOSH, REV. KENNETH, M.A., Indian Eccl. Esabt. (b. 10th Jan., 1903) (junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., ch. of Scotland, 27th Apl., 1928; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1930.

McKINTY, FRANCIS PATRICK, late Indian Police (b. 1st Aug., 1889).—Ret'd., Feb., 1936.*

MACKLIN, ALBERT SORTHAIN ROMER, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 4th March, 1890).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd after exam. of 1912; arrived, 1st Dec., 1913, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; judge and asst. sess. judge, Apl., 1922; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, June, 1923; offg. judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; regr. high ct., appellate side, Nov., 1926; judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1929; judl. asst. and addl. sess. judge, Aden, June, 1929; offg. secy. to govt., legal dept., June, 1931; judl. commr. in the states of western India, July, 1932; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1934; confd., June, 1935.

MACKNEY, HERBERT HODDY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st May, 1886).—Educ. at Liverpool Institute High Sch., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from June to July, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; acting judge, high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933; ret'd., 11th June, 1943.

MACLACHLAN, DUNCAN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Bombay) (b. 9th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Mountjoy Sch., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 21st Nov., 1913, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from Aug., 1916 to Nov., 1919; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindhi translator to govt., Apl., 1920; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept. (temp.), May, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Sept., 1928; confd., Jan., 1931; pol. agent, Bhor, Apl., 1932; offg. commr., Apl., 1935; confd., 2nd gr. commr., Nov., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1941; confd., 1st gr. commr., Dec., 1943.

MACLACHLAN, ROBERT BOYD, C.I.E., B.A., B.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th Sept., 1880).—Ret'd., Sept., 1935.*

MACLAGAN, SIR EDWARD DOUGLAS, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Ret'd., Dec., 1924.*

MACLEAN, CHARLES ALLAN, C.B.E., M.C., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Bihar) (b. 22nd May, 1892).—Joined the

service as dep. dir. of agric., 5th Dec., 1920; cane commr., edc. and jt. regr., co-ops., Bihar, Nov., 1939; C.B.E., Jan., 1941; off. dir. agric., Bihar, Apl., 1935; again March, 1943.

MACLEAN, EVAN VICTOR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Apl., 1887).—Ret'd., July, 1938.*

MCLEAN, ANGUS, B.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (Burma) (b. 27th Apl., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir., 16th Dec., 1921; ret'd.,

MCLEAN, ARTHUR ERROL JOHN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (inspr. of schools) (b. 28th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as headmaster govt. high school, Maymyo, 30th Jan., 1922; offg. asst. dir. of public instrcn., Aug., 1933; inspr. of schools, Nov., 1933; offg. asst. dir. of public instrcn., Apl., 1935; inspr. of schools, Rangoon, Jan., 1937; asst. dir. of pub. instrcn., June, 1938; confd., Apl., 1939.

MCLEAN, REV. LAUGHLAN, M.A., B.D., late Indian Eccles, Estabt. (Bombay) (b. 18th Feb., 1886).—Ret'd., March, 1939.*

MCLEAN, SIR ROBERT, Kt., B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P.; Rly.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1884).—Ret'd., July, 1929.*

MCLELLAN, REV. DUNCAN TAIT HUTCHISON, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (presdy., senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Madras) (b. 29th Nov., 1893).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., church of Scotland, 11th Feb., 1922; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1923; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1933; presdy., senr. chaplain, Madras, Apl., 1938; confd., July, 1939.

MACLEOD, ALLAN, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 19th March, 1887).—Ret'd., July, 1937.*

MACLEOD, ARCHIBALD CORRIE, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), (name changed from MacNabb, A. C.), Indian C.S. (commr., Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; city mag., Delhi, Apl., 1914; sec., munpl. cttee., Delhi, Sept., 1915; again, city mag., Delhi, Oct., 1916; spl., duty on staff of lieut.-govr., Punjab, from Oct., 1917 to March, 1918; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1923; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1924; senr., secty. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Nov., 1928; offg. dep.; sec. to govt., Punjab, dev. dept., Apl., 1933; offg. commr., May, 1934; admin. Lahore municipality and chrmm., Lahore impr. trust, Apl., 1937; commr. (perm.), May, 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

MACLEOD, SIR NORMAN CRANSTOUN, Kt., B.A., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept.—Ret'd., June, 1926.*

MACLEOD, RODERICK DONALD WILLIAM DUNNING, B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 24th May, 1886).—Ret'd., Nov., 1935.*

MCLEOD, WILLIAM NORMAN, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs., (Punjab) (b. 15th June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. uptg. engr., Jan., 1926; suptg. engr. (provl., perm.), July, 1935; confd. as suptg. engr., Oct., 1936; ret'd., June, 1940.

McLINTIC, JOHN FRANCIS, Indian Police (b. 29th Aug., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., Nov., 1938.

McMAHON, SIR ARTHUR HENRY, G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Col., Indian Army, ret'd., late foreign dept., govt. of India.—Retd. from Indian Army, March, 1915.*

MACMILLAN, ARCHIBALD MORVEN, C.I.E.,

M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 25th Apr., 1880).—Retd., Feb., 1935.*

MACMILLAN, JOHN NIVEN, Indian State Rlys. (dist. signal engr., E.I., Rly. (b. 20th Oct., 1901).—Asst. signal engr., Oct., 1936; dist. ditto, Nov., 1935.

MACMILLAN, NEIL BOYD (commercial offr., electricity bch., Punjab) (b. 14th Jan., 1880).—Joined the service, 23rd Apr., 1928, as sales manager; asst. comml. manager, Sept., 1931; comml. manager, Apr., 1933; comml. offr., Jan., 1934.

MACNABB, ARCHIBALD CORRIE, C.I.E., C.S. (name changed to Macleod, A. C., [q.v.]).

MCNAIR, SIR GEORGE DOUGLAS, M.B.E., Judl. Dept., Bengal (judge high ct., Calcutta). Apptd. acting judge, high ct., Calcutta, 28th Apr., 1933; confd., Apr., 1934; kt., Jan., 1943.

MCNAIR, SIR ROBERT HILL, Kt., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1877).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

MACNEE, EUSTACE ALBERIC, M.A. (Cantab.) late Indian Educl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 11th Nov., 1885).—Retd., Apr., 1937.*

MCNEIL, JOHN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. dist. judge) (b. 2nd Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ. and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., genl. dept., Rangoon, Apr., 1936 to Apr., 1937; asst. dist. judge, Oct., 1937.

MCNEILL, KENNETH JOHN, O.B.E., V.D., Indian State Rlys (dep. traffic mang., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 13th July, 1890).—Joined the service as traffic probnr, 17th Feb., 1911; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1914; dist. traffic supt., June, 1918; divl. traffic mang., Nov., 1922; offg. supt., watch and ward, June, 1930; offg. dep. traffic manager, variously, from May, 1933; divl. transp'n. supt., Dec., 1934; dep. traffic man., Apr., 1940; regr. contr. rly. priorities under war transpt. dept., July, 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

MACONACHIE, GORDON WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Jail Dept., Madras (b. 13th Nov., 1881).—Retd., July, 1936.*

MACONACHIE, SIR RICHARD ROY, K.B.E., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Sept., 1885).—Retd., July, 1937.*

MACPHERSON, DONALD, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr. Bengal) (b. 22nd March, 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Rl. High Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1918; arrived, 19th Dec., 1917, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., May, 1918 to Jan., 1919; addl. dist. mag., Nov., 1923; and settl. officer, July, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Apr., 1926; confd., March, 1929; offg. secty., bd. of revr., March, 1929; confd., Sept., 1930; offg. commr. of excise and salt, Bengal, June, 1935; confd., June, 1937; confd., mag. and collr., Oct., 1942.

MACPHERSON, SIR HUGH, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa).—Retd., Nov., 1926.*

MACPHERSON, REV. KENNETH CECIL, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (archdeacon of Bombay) (b. 8th July, 1890).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bombay, 10th Oct., 1931; confd., July, 1932; semr. chaplain, July, 1942; archdeacon of Bombay, June, 1943.

MACPHERSON, SIR THOMAS STEWART, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 21st Aug., 1876).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

MACQUEEN, PERCY, late Indian C.S. (Madras)

(b. 13th Nov., 1883).—Educ. at Malvern and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 11th Dec., 1907, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1915; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1919; ditto, Jan., 1920; ditto, March, 1922; confd., Oct., 1927; curator, Madras record office, Apr., 1930 to May, 1935; ret'd., 11th May, 1942.

MCRAE, HENRY WARREN, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 13th July, 1884).—Educ. at City of London Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store dep't, 19th Aug., 1902; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, Apr., 1922; higher exec. offr., March, 1926; actg. princpl. ditto, Sept., 1939; M.B.E., Jan., 1945.

MACRAE, IAN MACPHERSON, C.B., C.I.E., O.B.E., M.B., Major-Genl., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bilhar and Orissa (b. 2nd Oct., 1882).—Joined the service 1st Sept., 1904; in mily. employ. March, 1905, to July, 1908; jail supt., Dec., 1911; on mily. service, Oct., 1914 to Aug., 1920; O.B.E., June, 1919; supt., central prison, Bareilly, Sept., 1920; ditto, Lucknow, March, 1923; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; asst. dir., medl. service, Bombay, Nov., 1933; apptd. King's hon. physician, Jan., 1936; C.B., June 1939.

MACRAE, WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th May, 1880).—Retd., May, 1935.*

MCROBERT, GEORGE REID, Lieut.-Col., C.I.E., I.M.S., Medl. Dept., Madras (supv. govt., genl. hosp., Madras) (b. 21st Jan., 1895).—Date of first commn., 15th March, 1920; apptd. prof'r. of medicine, medl. coll., Madras, Oct., 1934; supt. govt. gen. hosp., March, 1940; surgeon to the govr., Nov., 1941.

MCsWEENY, DENIS LEON, M.C., Office of High Commr. for India (supig. exec. officer, accounts dept.) (b. 26th Jan., 1883).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, store dep't, 14th March, 1904; on mily. service from Aug., 1914 to May, 1919; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. offr., Apr., 1922; suptg. exec. officer., Nov., 1934.

MCturk, JOHN, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) (b. 26th July, 1914).—Educ. at Stowe and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govr. Sept., 1940.

MACWATT, SIR ROBERT CHARLES, Kt., C.I.E., M.B., Major-Gen., late I.M.S.—Retd., Sept., 1926.*

McWATTERS, SIR ARTHUR CECIL, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1880).—Retd., Dec., 1931.*

McWILLIAM, PETER NEISH, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 21st Oct., 1908).—Educ. at Tottenham County School and East London College and Downing College, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931; addtl. dist. mag., March, 1938; mag. and collr., Apr., 1939; dep. commr., Apr., 1943.

MADAN, FRANCIS FALCONER, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 27th Oct., 1886).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

MADAN, FRAMROZ RUSTONJI, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Madras) (b. 3rd March,

1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg. consvr., Apl., 1926; confd., Oct., 1931.

MADAN GOPAL SARDHANA, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 29th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1908; asst. engr., Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. supt. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; retgd., 17th Jan., 1940; afterwards re-empld. as princpl. Thomson Coll., Roorkee.

MADAN, SIR JANARDAN ATMARAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 12th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Elphinstone High Sch., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and Oxford (Non-Collegiate); apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 19th Nov., 1909, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and asst. settlt. offr.; asst. regr., co-op. societies, Oct., 1919; actg. collr. and regr., co-op. societies, Jan., 1920; member of legisl. council, 1925; jt. sec., Royal Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-8; collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1927; C.I.E., March, 1929; offg. dir. of lab. intell. and regr. of trade unions, May, 1930; offg. secty. to govt. rev. dept., Bombay, Jan., 1934; confd., June, 1934; commr., Dec., 1936; C.S.I. Jan., 1939; advr. to govt. of Bombay, Nov., 1939 to Jan., 1942; retgd., Apl., 1942; Kt., June, 1942; chrnmn., Bombay-Sind public serv. commn., Apl., 1942.

MADAN LAL PURI, Lieut.-Col. retgd., late I.M.S. (Punjab) (b. 13th Apl., 1885).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1908; in mily. employ to 11th Dec., 1921; civil surg., Oct., 1922; retgd., Apl., 1940.

MADAN MOHAN SINHA, RAI BAHADUR, M.B.E., (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 26th Apl., 1913, as dep. collr.; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; asst. sec. to govr., March, 1921; sec., U.P. committee for the British Empire Exhibition, Feb., 1923; dep. dir. of industries, Dec., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; confd., May, 1933.

MADANI, GHULAM AHMED (see Ghulam Ahmed Madani).

MADELEY, WILLIAM YORKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, chief ct., Oudh) (b. 26th Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Woodbridge Gr. Sch., and Jesu Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., July, 1917; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918 to Jan., 1919; actg. mag. and collr., May, 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1922; confd., Feb., 1927; actg. judge, chief ct. of Oudh, July, 1941; addtl. judge, Oudh, 11th May, 1943; judge, ditto, Nov., 1944.

MADGAONKAR, SIR GOVIND DINANATH, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 21st May, 1871).—Retgd., Apl., 1931.*

MADHU SUDAN DAYAL, MITHAL, Lala, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 17th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; exec. engr., March, 1935.

MAFFEY, SIR JOHN LOADER, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., K.C.V.O., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (N.W.F.P.)—Retgd., Nov., 1925.*

MAGNIAC, SIR CHARLES LANE, Kt., C.M.G., C.B.E., Brig.-Gen., R.E. (retgd.), late Railway Dept.—Retgd., March, 1922.*

MAGOR, EDWARD WALTER MOYLE, Capt. L.A., Ind. Political Service (b. 1st June, 1911).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probu., Oct., 1937; under-

sec. to resdt., Rajputana, March, 1943; confd., in I.P.S., May, 1944.

MAGRATH, WILLIAM CHRISTOPHER McLEAN, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 25th July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 12th Sept., 1921; offg. supt., Jan., 1924; confd., Apl., 1932; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937.

MAGUB, GEORGE DOUGLAS, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., C.P.) (b. 11th Oct., 1916).—Educ. at Dover County School and Caius Coll., Cambridge; appointed on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; conf., 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in C.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

MAGUIRE, ANGUS DENYS, Indian Police, (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 2nd Oct., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 17th Nov., 1927; offg. supt., June, 1934.

MAHABIR PRASAD, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 7th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 19th Sept., 1911; asst. engr., Sept., 1912; exec. engr., Sept., 1921; offg. dep. dir. of industries, Jan., 1931; prof. of civil engrg., Thomson Coll., Dec., 1934 to Apl., 1937; supt. engr., U.P.; confd., Aug., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1944; retgd., 7th Nov., 1943.

MAHAJAN, MEHR CHAND (judge, high ct., Lahore).—Apptd., June, 1943.

MAHALANABIS, PRASANTA CHANDRA, O.B.E., M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (prof., presy. coll., Calcutta) (b. 29th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1917; prof., Dacca coll., May, 1919; prof. presy. coll., July, 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1942.

MAHAMADI, RASHID AHMED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 24th July, 1909).—Educ. at Madrasra-i-Aliya, Hyderabad, Madras Univ. and School of Oriental Studies, London, joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

MAHANTY, DIRAKAR (see Dibakar, M.).

MAHARAJ NAGENDRASINGH, see Nagendra Singh, Maharaj.

MAHARAJ SINGH, KUNWAR, SIR, C.I.E., M.A., Barr., late United Provs., C.S. (b. 17th May, 1878).—Retgd., May, 1933.*

MAHBUB ALI KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR SHAIKH, O.B.E., Indian Political Service (b. March, 1894).—Joined the service, March, 1915; asst. commr., Peshawar, May, 1930; ditto, Mansehra, March, 1935; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, May, 1936; asst. commr., Thal, March, 1937; dep. commr., Kohat, March, 1938.

MAHBUB, ILAHI CHARAGH, SHAIKH, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 14th March, 1904).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 30th Oct., 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. eng., Oct., 1935; sec. p.w.d. (irrgn.), Oct., 1941; under-sec. (estabt.), ditto, Oct., 1942.

MAHER, JOHN EDWARD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Orissa) (b. 5th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham, and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Apl., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Orissa, Dec., 1939.

MAHIDA, UDAYSINH NARSENHBAYA, B.E., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 20th Apl., 1904).—Apptd. assistant exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1929; dep. secty., govt., Bombay, (p.w.d.), June, 1938.

MAHONY, FRANCIS VINCENT, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd March, 1886).—Held non-

gazetted appts. from 22nd Dec., 1902 to 6th June, 1909; dist. contr. of stores, N.W. rly., May, 1919; offg. dep. contr., May, 1933; and again, Feb., 1934; confd., May, 1934; offg. contr., Aug., 1936; ret'd., March, 1941.

MAIN, THOMAS FORSTER, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 23rd Jan., 1882).—Ret'd., July, 1933.*

MAINPRICE, FREDERICK PAUL, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Aug., 1915).—Educ. at Haileybury and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in the C.P. as asst. commr.; apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Apl., 1942; under-sec. to resdt., Eastern States, Apl., 1942; Brit. trade agt., Gyantse, Oct., 1942; and again, May, 1944; on depn. Aug., 1944.

MAITLAND, VICTOR KENNARD, M.A., M.C., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 12th June, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 16th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1924; ditto, and dir. of forest school, Balaghat, March, 1937; consvr., March, 1940.

MAITRA, SANKAR NATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 7th June, 1912).—Educ. at Mitra Instn., Bhowanipur, Calcutta, Univ. of Calcutta, King's College, London, and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

MAJEED, Abdul, B.Sc., India C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 26th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Govrmt. Intermdt. Coll., Allahabad, Allahabad Univ., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 6th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939; and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

MAJID, A. (see Abdul Majid).

MAJID, HAFIZ ABDUL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offr., Dec., 1933; dep. sec. to govt., Punjab (med. educn. depts.), Feb., 1942; rationing contr., Lahore, Jan., 1944.

MAJID, INAN-UL, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (Indian govt., trade commr., Alexandria) (b. 19th May, 1895).—Educ. at Perse Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Dec., 1921; arrived, 5th Jan., 1922, and served in Assam as asst. commr., under-secty. to govt., Apl., 1929; offg. dir. of indus., etc., Nov., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1935; secty. public dept., office of high commr., Oct., 1937; actg. dep. high commr. for India, March-May, 1938; confd. as dep. commr., Aug., 1938; Indian govt. trade commr., Alexandria, Jan., 1939.

MAJIDULLAH, SARDAR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 14th Oct., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; addtl. dep. transp. contr., June, 1944.

MAJOR, JOHN, India Office (hr. clerical officer) (b. 13th March, 1888).—Apptd. clerical offr., 28th Sept., 1921; actg. higher ditto, Jan., 1935; confd., July, 1935.

MAJUMDAR, DWIJENDRA LAL, M.A. (Cal.), B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Calcutta and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov. 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.

and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1930; offg. addtl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936; commr. of workmen's compensation, Bengal, Apl., 1938; secty., jute, paddy and rice enquiry cttee., Sept., 1938; special offr., fin. dept. (in addn.), June, 1939; secty., Ind. cent. jute cttee., May, 1940.

MAJUMDAR, NRIPENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Feb., 1897).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1922; asst. acct.-genl., Bengal, Sept., 1923; ditto, posts and tels., Nov., 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Jan., 1931; asst. dir., army audit. W. Cmd., Jan., 1932; attd. officer, office of the ch. auditor, E.I. rly., March, 1936; dep. govt. examr., B.N. rly., Apl., 1936; ch. auditor, E.B. rly., Feb., 1938; dep. acct. gen., Burma, March, 1939; comptr., posts and tels. acct., Burma, Apl., 1939; dep. acct.-gen., Burma, June, 1939; contr. of mily. accts., Burma, June, 1939.

MAJUMDAR, SUDHENDU JYOTI, B.A. Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 8th Feb., 1917).—Educ. at Pabna Zilla Sch., Pabna, Calcutta Univ., Univ. Coll., London, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Oct., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr. and under-sec., home and fin. depts., Orissa, Jan., 1944.

MAKAND, SAMUEL NORMAN, M.B., Ch.B., Lieut.-Col. (ret'd.), late I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (b. 10th July, 1886).—Date of first commn., 25th Feb., 1921; in milty. employ until 20th Feb., 1930; medl. offr. of health, Simla, Feb., 1931; offg. dir. of public health, Central Provs., Oct., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937; ret'd., 10th July, 1941.

MAL, DEBI DATTA (see Debi Datta Mal).

MALABARI, PHIROZSHAH BEHRAMJI, KHAN BAHADUR, Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept., (b. 2nd Nov., 1876).—Ret'd., Dec., 1935.*

MALCOLM, AUSTIN KENNETH, Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Dec., 1937; confd., May, 1940.

MALCOLM, HUGH WAUDBY, India Office (exec. offr., acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 21st May, 1900).—Apptd. clerical offr., 23rd Apl., 1920; actg. exec. offr., Feb., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934; actg. hr. exec. offr., July, 1940.

MALCOLM, KENNETH ROBERT, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 17th Dec., 1908).—Educ. at Eton and New College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Apl., 1935; labour commr., Nov., 1944.

MALET, GERALD RION LILLINGTON, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Oct., 1889).—E. B. Rly.; ret'd., Nov., 1939.*

MALHAN, FAQIR CHAND, Rai Bahadur, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 28th May, 1883).—Ret'd., May, 1938.*

MALIK, ABDULLA KHALID, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 7th July, 1910).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Gujrat, Govrmt. Intermdt. Coll., Gujrat, Univ. of the Punjab and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offcr., May, 1937; served with govt. of India from March, 1939, offr. on spel. duty, Indian stores dpt. Apl., 1941; dep. ch. admin. offr. (purchase), dept. of supply, Sept., 1941.

MALIK, BIDHUBHUSAN, apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, Apl., 1944.

MALIK, HARDIT SINGH, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 23rd Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; arrived in India, 6th Jan., 1922; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1926; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1927; dep. trade commr., London, Jan., 1931 to May, 1934; actg. trade commr., May-Sept., 1932; and again, Nov., 1933; dep. secty. to govt. of India, commerce dept., June, 1934; O.B.E., Jan. 1938; trade commr., New York, July, 1938; C.I.E., June, 1941; prime minister, Patiala state, Apl., 1944.

MALIK, JASVANTSINGH, O.B.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 13th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 21st Jan., 1921; offg. exec. engr., July, 1923; confd., Oct., 1929; in foreign service as muncpl. engr., Ahmedabad from Feb., 1930 to May, 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

MALIK, SIR TEJA SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Central P.W.D.) (b. 1st Sept., 1887).—

Joined the service as asst. engr., 4th Nov., 1910; exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; exec. engr., central p.w.d., Apl., 1930; offg. supt. engr., Sept., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; ch. engr., July, 1940; kt., June, 1942.

MALLAM, GEORGE LESLIE, C.I.E., Barr.-at-law, Lieut.-Col. I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—First commn. dated 14th Nov., 1916; joined pol. dept., 14th March, 1921; asst. commr., Bannu, June, 1921; under-sec. to pol. agent, Persian Gulf, Jan., 1923; dist. judge, Abbottabad, Dec., 1926; asst. commr., Tonk, June, 1927; ditto, Mardan, May, 1928; under-secty. to A.G.G. and C.C., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1929; census agent, N.W.F.P., Apl., 1930; counsellor, Brit. legn., Kabul Apl., 1930; dep. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1934; senior sub. judge, Peshawar, Dec., 1938; addl. fin. secy. to govt. N.W.F.P., March, 1939; fin. secy. to govt. N.W.F.P. and jun. advr. to the local admn. N.W.F.P.; Apl., 1939; pol. agent, Dir., Swat and Chitral, Nov., 1939; ch. sec. to govt., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1941.

MALLICK, SUBHOD KUMAR, M.A., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Calcutta Univ.; apptd., on probn. after exam. of 1943, and served in Assam.

MALLIK, BHOLA NATH, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 9th Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 13th Apl., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; transferred to Orissa, May, 1939; confd., supt. Oct., 1939.

MALLIK, HARENDRA KRISTO, M.A., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 16th March, 1886).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1910; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Burma circle, May, 1930; ditto, Bombay circle, Nov., 1931; asst. acct.-genl., United Provs., June, 1932; dep. ditto, Aug., 1934; attd. offcr., office of comp., Orissa, Oct., 1936; dep. acct.-genl., Central revenues, Apl., 1937; exp. outside audit dept., Apl., 1938; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, June, 1938; dep. acct. gen., posts and tels., Nagpur, Feb., 1939; retd., March, 1941.

MAN MOHAN, RAI BAHADUR, M.A., late Punjab Educl. Service (b. 27th July, 1888).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1911; asst. profr. of econ., govt. coll., Lahore, Jan., 1913; lecturer, ditto, Oct., 1921; principal, Khalsa Coll., June, 1924; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1931; asst. dir., pub. instrn., Nov., 1936; C.I.E., June,

1943; commr., post-war planning, Nov., 1944; retd., 28th July, 1943.

MAN SINGH, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 9th Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on probation, 7th Dec., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

MAN SINGH, RAI BAHADUR, C.B.E., B.A., Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 3rd July, 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1937.*

MANAVEDAN RAJA, KISHAKKA COVILA-GATH, Rao Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 1st Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

MANDAL, see MUNDLE.

MANGALMURTI, KESHAO TUKARAM, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 7th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 4th Dec., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr., offg. dep. commr., May, 1927; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1929; and again, June, 1932; confd., Jan., 1936.

MANGAT RAI, EDWARD NIRMAL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 30th March, 1915).—Educ. at R.I.M. Coll., Dehra Dun, Delhi Univ. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 10th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addtl. under-sec. fin. commrs., June, 1941; asst. price contr., etc., June, 1942; asst. dir. civil supplies, etc., June, 1943; dep. ditto, 1944.

MANI, NATESA SUBRAHMANYAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 11th July, 1913).—Educ. at Banadurai High Sch., Kumbakonam, Madras Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1939.

MANI, RANGIAH SUBRA, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., genl. and educn. depts.) (b. 14th August, 1907).—Educ. at Madras University, Imperial College of Science and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; under-secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Aug., 1937; dep. secty. genl. and educn. depts., Bombay, June, 1940; exp. trade contr., Bombay, Apl., 1943.

MANI, TIRUCHENDURAI MUTHUKRISHAN SUBRA, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras), (dep.-sec. govt. of India planning and devel. dept.) (b. 1st Aug., 1908).—Educ. University Coll., London, and Sch of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., sub. collr. and jt. mag., Apr., 1933; under-sec. rev. dept., Madras, Apl., 1936; under-sec., govt. of India, communications dept., Apl., 1940; dept. sec., ditto (planning and devel.), Aug., 1944.

MANICO, EDWARD LESLIE, late Indian State Ryls. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. carriage and wagon supt., 25th Jan., 1911; dist. ditto, Apl., 1918; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1927; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Burma Ryls., Oct., 1931; dist. loco. supt., N.W. rly. Nov., 1932; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. rly., March, 1933; offg. dep. ch. aptg., supt., N.W. rly., Oct., 1933; and again, June, 1934; dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936; dep. ch. operating supt. (power), Jan., 1939; retd., 11th May, 1944.

MANINDRA LAL SEN GUPTA (see Sen Gupta, M. L.).

MANKAR, WAMAN, KESHAVJI, M.A., LL.B. (b. 23rd July, 1884).—Late presdy. mag., Bombay.—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

MANLEY, EDWARD LOVEL, M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th June, 1886).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

MANLEY, FELIX BERNARD, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 23rd June, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

MANMATHA NATH MITRA, L.M.S. (Punjab), L.M. (Dublin), D.P.H. (London), Public Health Dept., United Provs. (b. 23rd May, 1885).—Joined the service as health officer on probn., 1st March, 1914; confd., March, 1915; asst. dir. of public health, 12th March, 1930.

MANNING, DONALD EDMUND BELFORD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 9th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., March, 1928.

MANNOOCH, GEOFFREY HERBERT, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 23rd Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Nov., 1910; supt., Feb., 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., variously, from 1932; confd., dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1941; confd., inspr.-genl., Apl., 1943; King's police medal, 1941; C.I.E., June, 1943.

MANOHAR DAS CHATURVEDI, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Dec., 1923; silviculturist, March, 1927; dep. consvr., Dec., 1931; offr. on spl. duty at govt. h.q., (rural devel.).

MANOHAR, LAL, Barr. (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd., June, 1939.

MANOHAR LAL SOOD, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 28th Jan. 1905).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 19th Nov., 1927; engr. student, Nov., 1928; proby. asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1928; confd., Jan., 1930; exec. engr., Oct., 1937.

MANSER, THOMAS JAMES STEDMAN, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 24th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

MANSFIELD, PHILIP THEODORE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (b. 1st Feb., 1892).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 11th Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mily. duty, Oct., 1918 to Jan., 1919; ditto, and addl. pol. agent, feudatory states, Oct., 1919; jt. mag., Jan., 1923; offg. dir. of land records and surveys, March, 1928; confd., Jan., 1929; mag. and collr. (provl. sub.), Jan., 1929; confd., Nov., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., rev. dept., Apl., 1931; dir. of land records and surveys, Apl., 1933; secty. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, reforms dept., Jan., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; ch. secty. to govt., Orissa, Apl., 1936; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; offg. advt. to govr., Bihar, March, 1944.

MANSON, FREDERICK PERCY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th Dec., 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

MANSON, JOHN ALEXANDER SHAND, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 19th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Oct., 1933.

MARAR, KOTTIL WALAPPIL PARAMESWARA, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 7th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and

School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1933; offg. under secty. to govt., Assam, in depts. under ch. secty., Oct., 1933; awarded K.-I.-H., gold medal, Jan., 1935; apptd. as prov. supt. of census operations, Assam, Dec., 1939; dep. secty., home dept. Sept., 1941; sec. supply dept., Feb. 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

MARCHINTON, WILLIAM FRANKLIN, late Indian Service of Engineers (Bihar) (b. 14th Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

MARJORIBANKS, ALAN GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 28th Oct., 1904) (dist. supt., Central Provs.).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 11th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Dec., 1928, Jan., 1933, and July, 1933; confd., March, 1941.

MARLOW, WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (suptg. exec. officr., general dept.) (b. 8th July, 1889).—Educ. at Brentwood Gr. Sch., Monoux Sch., Walthamstow, and East London Coll.; apptd. sec. div. clerk, board of educ., 29th Apl., 1907; 2nd class clerk, India Office, 29th Oct., 1907; on mil. service from Dec., 1916; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; att. to Indian del. to League of Nations, 1st Assembly, 1920; personal asst. to high commr., Apl., 1921; higher exec. officer, June, 1927; on special duty in connection with the building of India House, March, 1929 to Aug., 1930; services lent to Indian del. Impl. Economic Conf., May to Sept., 1932; asst. sec., ditto, July, 1932; suptg. exec. officr., Oct., 1938.

MARRIOTT, ERIC LLEWELLYN, C.I.E., late Indian Police, Bihar (b. 28th May, 1888).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

MARRIOTT, SIR ROBERT ECKLIN, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (gen. mang., E.I. Rly.) (b. 15th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Dec., 1924; offg. divl. supt., Apl., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1932; ditto (provl. perm.), Apl., 1936; confd., Aug., 1937; chief engr., March, 1938; genl. mang., E.I. Rly., Aug., 1941; kt., Jan., 1943.

MARRIS, SIR WILLIAM SINCLAIR, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 9th Oct., 1873).—Member of council of India, May, 1928—Sept., 1929.*

MARSDEN, PERCY, C.I.E., B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (commr., Punjab) (b. 26th Nov., 1889).—Educ. at Cheltenham and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; personal asst. to pol. agent, Phulkian states, Dec., 1914; sec., municipal cttee., Amritsar, March, 1935 to May, 1917; on mil. duty from July, 1918 to Apl., 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), July, 1925; offg. sec. to govt. trans. depts., Punjab, Dec., 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. commr., Oct., 1935; commr., sub-pemt., Dec., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1942.

MARSH, PERCY WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 14th Oct., 1881).—Educ. at Wellington and Wadhham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 26th Nov., 1905, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and 1st. commr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1914; temply. empd. under War Office from Apl., 1916 to Dec., 1918; mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1929; offg. commr., March, 1931; commr. (provl. subs.), May, 1933; confd., Nov., 1934; memb. bd. of rev., United Provs., Dec., 1938; C.S.I., Jan., 1939; ret. d., 1st Nov., 1942.

MARSH, WILFRED, Indian Police (dep. commr. of police, Rangoon) (b. 20th July, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 25th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1934; dep. commr. of police, Rangoon, March, 1939; confd. June, 1939.

MARSH-SMITH, REGINALD NORMAN, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 24th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1911; asst. dep. inspr.-gen., C.I.D., Sept., 1920; supt., Aug., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1926; inspr.-genl., Gwalior Durbar, May, 1936; confd., as dep.-inspr.-genl., Oct., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

MARSHALL, FREDERICK, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 14th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; on mil. service from 19th Aug., 1916 to 21st Nov., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1931; ditto (provl. subs.), May, 1933; confd., July, 1933; chief engr. (permt.), June, 1940; retd., 14th Nov., 1944.

MARSHALL, SIR JOHN HUBERT, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., Litt.D., F.S.A., Hon. A.R.I.B.A. (b. 19th March, 1876).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (Porson prize), Prendergast and Craven fellowships; student of British Sch. at Athens, 1898-99; apptd. dir.-gen. of archaeology, 22nd Feb., 1902; C.I.E., June, 1910; knighted, Jan., 1915; on spl. duty, Sept., 1928; retd., March, 1931; offcr. on spl. duty, March, 1931 to Sept., 1936.

MARSHALL, REV. NORMAN EDWYN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 31st March, 1887).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, Punjab, 2nd Jan., 1915; senr. chaplain, 6th June, 1927.

MARSLAND, HAROLD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Aug., 1881).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

MARTEN, SIR ALFRED AMBERSON BARRINGTON, Kt., M.A., LL.D. (Barr., 1895) (Bombay Jud. Dept.) (b. 8th Dec., 1879).—Retd., June, 1930.*

MARTEN, ERIC CHARLES, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 14th Feb., 1899).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; joined the service, 17th Sept., 1922; arrived, 21st Nov., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Simla, Apl., 1927; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1930; Oct., 1932; and again, May, 1933; addl. ditto, Sept., 1933; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1935; on spec. duty, legal remembr. office., May, 1938; sec. to govt. legisl. dept., Sept., 1940; addtl. judge, Lahore high ct., Sept., 1943.

MARTIN, ARCHDALE CHERRY, Indian Police (Madras) (b. 1st Apl., 1893).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1923; asst. supt., May, 1915; offg. supt., May, 1921; confd., Sept., 1929.

MARTIN, CHARLES DE CARTERET, M.B., Lieut.-Col., retd., late I.M.S. (b. 3rd Jan., 1888).—First commn., dated 1st Aug., 1914; in mil. employ prior to July, 1925; asst. dir., Pasteur inst., July, 1925; offg. asst. dir., central research institute, Kasauli, Jan., 1929; pathologist, genl. hosp., Rangoon, June, 1930; civil surgeon (temp.), May, 1935; retd., 3rd Jan., 1943.

MARTIN, FREDERICK GEORGE STEPHEN, C.I.E., M.C., Indian State Rlys. (contr. of stores, E.I. Rly.) (b. 26th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. works mang., G.I.P. Rly., 14th Dec., 1923; offg. works mang., May, 1925;

offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1926; on spl. duty under railway bd., 19th March, 1927; dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. Rly., Feb., 1928; offg. contr. of stores, Sept., 1930; confd., Oct., 1931; contr. of supplies, Bengal circle, Calcutta, Nov., 1939; dep. dir.-genl., munitions prodcn., dept. of supply, July, 1940; C.I.E., June, 1941.

MARTIN, THE VEN. FREDERICK WILLIAM, M.A., late Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 16th Jan., 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 10th Apl., 1918; junr. chaplain, May, 1919; senr., chaplain, Apl., 1930; archdeacon, Sept., 1932; retd., May, 1940.

MARTIN, GEOFFREY NOEL, late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 11th Dec., 1893).—Secy. to govt., Burma, forest dept., Apl., 1934; retd., Apl., 1937.*

MARTIN, GEORGE WILLIAM PERCY, late India Office (b. 16th Dec., 1877).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

MARTIN, HARRY WALTER, Office of High Commr. for India (dep. ch. accountg. officer) (b. 26th May, 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 13th Sept., 1902; minor staff clerk, Oct., 1919; staff clerk (actg.), Apl., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from Apl., 1921; higher exec. officr., 1st Apl., 1922; suptg. exec. officr., 21st Apl., 1922; asst. ch. acctg. officr., Nov., 1934; dep. ditto, Nov., 1937.

MARTIN, HUGH BELLASIS, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 6th Oct., 1915).—Educ. at Harrow and Ariel Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 17th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr.; under sec., supply dept., Sept., 1943.

MARTIN, OLAUS MACLEOD, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal) (b. 1st Feb., 1890).—Educ. at Oban High Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913; and served in Bengal as asst. mag., and collr.; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916 to March, 1919; employed in Assam from Aug., 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1920; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1923; offg. labour intell. offr., Bengal, Nov., 1925; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and appt. depts., Nov., 1925; mag. and collr., Aug., 1927; dep. commr., Apl., 1930; secty. to govt., rev. dept., May, 1934; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; commr. (confd.), Oct., 1940; commr., post-war reconstrn., Apl., 1944.

MARTIN, REGINALD VICTOR, C.I.E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (insp.-genl. prisons, Bombay) (b. 20th March, 1889).—First commn. dated 26th July, 1913; in milty. employ to July, 1924; supt. of prison, March, 1927; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Apl., 1931; confd., Sept., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

MARTYN, PHILIP DOCTON, O.B.E., B.A. (Man.), Indian C.S. (dep. secty., home dept., Bengal) (b. 7th Apl., 1904).—Educ. at Stockport Gr. Sch., Victoria Univ., Manchester, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. ch. mang., Dacca Nawab estate, Jan., 1933; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1934; dep. secty., home dept., Bengal, Jan., 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; repres. of govt. of Bengal with h.-q., eastern army, Oct., 1944.

MARVIN, EDITH ANNA, M.B.E., B.A., (London), India Office (staff officer for estb. duties) (b. 20th Apl., 1893).—Educ. at Notting

Hill High Sch. and Bedford Coll.; apptd. clerical officer, 11th July, 1921; higher clerical officer. (acting), Apl., 1922; confd., Dec., 1924; staff officer for estabt. duties (temp.), June, 1933 ditto (perm.), June, 1936; M.B.E., Jan., 1935.

MARWOOD, SIDNEY LIONEL, C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 8th Apl., 1891).—Educ. at St. Pauls' Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 15th Nov., 1915, and posted to Bihar and Orissa; on mil. duty with the I.A.R.O., Nov., 1915, to Oct., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1925; offg. pol. agt., Orissa feudatory states, Oct., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1920; confd. (provl. sub.), Nov., 1929; confd., Feb., 1931; commr. of inc.-tax, Nov., 1931; K.-i.-H. medal, 1st cl., June, 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; rev. commr., Orissa, Apl., 1943.

MASCARENHAS, WILLIAM ZAVIAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 22nd Dec., 1926; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Oct., 1935; dep. secty. to govt., Bombay (roads and bldgs., p.w.d.), Dec., 1935, to Oct., 1937; profir. of engrg., coll. of engrg., Poona, June, 1938.

MASIH, SAIYID MUHAMMAD (see Saiyid Muhammad Masih.).

MASON, LAWRENCE, SIR C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 27th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1910, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; on mil. service, Oct., 1914 to Oct., 1919; ch. forest offr., Andamans, Apl., 1928 to Dec., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. consvr., Central Provs., Apl., 1933; ditto, United Provs., Apl., 1934; confd., Aug., 1934; inspr.-genl. forests and presdt. for research inst., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1938; officer on spec. duty, dept. of supply, Apl., 1940; dir., timber directorate, dept. of supply, July, 1940; dep. dir.-genl., supply, Aug., 1940; ret'd., Aug., 1941; kt., June, 1942.

MASON, NOEL, O.B.E., M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 24th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., Feb., 1913; att'd. for duty, mil. dept., India Office, Dec., 1918 to Aug., 1919; on depn. to India Office, Aug., 1919 to Sept. 1920; confd. as cl. I officer., Oct., 1935 dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1937; revtd. to mil. accts. dept., and promoted cmd., contr., July, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

MASON, PHILIP, M.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 30th Nov., 1882).—Educ. at Norwich Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 9th Dec., 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1914; jt. mag., Oct., 1916; mag. and collr., May, 1923; secty. to govt., local self-govt. and pub. health depts., Nov., 1931; commr. (prov. subs.), Nov., 1936; confd., Dec., 1937; dep. sec. def. co-ord. dept., Sept., 1939; ditto, defence dept., March, 1940; confd. as mag. and collr., Nov., 1941; ret'd., 8th May, 1942.

MASON, PHILIP, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (jt. secty., Govt. of India) (b. 19th March, 1906).—Educ. at Sedburgh and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in the United Provs. as asst. collr. and mag.; under-secy. to govt. of India defence dept., Nov., 1933; offg. dep. secty., defence dept., March, 1936; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1936; confd., Dec., 1937; dep. secty., govt. of India, Sept., 1939; ditto de-

fence/war, March, 1940; confd. as mag. and collr., Nov., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; sec., plans S.E.A.C., Nov., 1943 to July, 1944; jt. sec., govt. of India (war), July, 1944.

MASTER, JAL ARDESHIR, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Madras) (b. 23rd Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. 23rd Nov., 1915; dep. consvr., Nov., 1920; offg. consvr., March, 1936; confd., Sept., 1944.

MASTERMAN, CHRISTOPHER HUGHES, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 7th Oct., 1889).—Educ. at Winchester and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from July, 1917, to Dec., 1919; sub-collr., March, 1922; collr. of salt revenue, Apl., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., July, 1932; ditto (provl. subs.), July, 1933; confd., Feb., 1935; secty. to govt., educn. and public health depts., Madras, Aug., 1936, to March, 1939; C.I.E., June, 1939; collr. from June, 1939, to Feb., 1943; member, bd. of rev., Feb., 1943; C.S.I., Jan., 1944.

MASUD, MOHAMMED, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 25th June, 1916).—Educ. at Central Model High School, Lahore, Punjab Univ. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 21st Oct., 1939; confd., 30th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

MASWOOD, MUHAMMAD ABDUL HAI, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1912).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st March, 1935; confd., 1937.

MATHER, WILLIAM MCKENZIE, O.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (estabt. officer) (b. 12th March, 1888).—Apptd. temp. pte. secty. to High Commr., Oct., 1920; confd., Apl., 1927; O.B.E., Feb., 1937; suptg. exec. offcr., accts dept., Nov., 1937; estabt. offcr., Apl., 1938; on spl. duty, March to July, 1941.

MATHESON, REV. ROBERTUS WILLIAM, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras), Church of Scotland (junn. chaplain) (b. 21st Apl., 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 20th Jan., 1934; junn. chaplain, Feb., 1936; on mil. serv., Sept., 1942, to Sept., 1944.

MATHEW, PUTHENVEETIL CHANDAPILLA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 30th June, 1913).—Educ. at Church Missionary Society High Sch., Mavelikara, Madras Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., March, 1939.

MATHIESON, LEWELLYN CHARLES FLETCHER, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Sept., 1883).—Ret'd., Sept., 1938.*

MATHRA DAS, DIWAN BAHADUR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 9th July, 1910; dist. traffic supt., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. agent, Sept., 1931; dir., rly. bd., Feb., 1933; dep. gen. manager, July, 1939; dep. traffic ditto, Dec., 1939; ret'd., Nov., 1942.

MATHRANI, HARBHAGWANDAS PRIBHDAS, O.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 12th July, 1901).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1927; confd., Oct., 1932; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; dep. sec. to govt., Sind, Aug., 1941-Nov., 1941.

MATHRANI, KEWALRAM PRIBHDAS, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 21st Aug., 1911).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Shikarpur, Edinburgh Univ., and Sch. of

Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; settlt. officr., Kanara, Feb., 1940.

MATHRANI, MITHARAM PRIBHDAS, O.B.E., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Sind) (b. 10th Nov., 1895).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 3rd Aug., 1918; asst. engr. (provl. subst.), Aug., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; offg. exec. engr., 13th Dec., 1923; confd., Aug., 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1933; dep. sec., govt. Sind, Oct., 1939; confd. as suptg. engr., May, 1943.

MATHUR, DHATRI SARAN, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 13th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Government Intermediate Coll., Etawah, Agra University, Thomson Civil Engineering Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

MATHUR, GOBIND SARUP, BABU (see Gobind, S. M.).

MATHUR, JAGDISH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 16th May, 1917).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

MATHUR, KALKA PRASAD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., late Public Health Dept., United Provs. (b. 15th Nov., 1884).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

MATHUR, KRISHNA BEHARI LALL, M.I.E. (India), Indian State Ry. (govt. inspr.) (b. 24th May, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 30th Sept., 1913; asst. exec. engr., July, 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1935; provl. perm., July, 1938; confd., May, 1939; transf. to comm. dept. as govt. inspr. of ry. s., May, 1941.

MATHUR, MATHESANDRA SHANKAR, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 17th Oct., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 5th Jan., 1931; confd., March, 1933; supt. (provl. subs.), July, 1944.

MATHUR, MANMOHAN SARUP, Rai Bahadur, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central p.w.d.) (b. 15th July, 1898).—Joined the service 29th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., March, 1932; transf. to central p.w.d., Aug., 1933; exec. engr., Jan., 1935.

MATHUR, PRAHLAD NARAYAN, P.M.S., late Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 1st Jan., 1886).—Civil surgeon, May, 1927; retd.,

MATTHAL, GEORGE, M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educ. Service (b. 13th Nov., 1887).—Apptd. prof. of zoology, govt. coll., Lahore, 16th Sept., 1918; retd., 13th Nov., 1942.

MATTHAI, JACOB, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd March, 1884).—Retd., March, 1939.*

MATTHAI, JOHN, C.I.E., B.A., B.L., D.Sc., B.Litt., Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Jan., 1886).—Apptd. to the I.E.S. as prof., presdy. coll., Madras, 3rd Apl., 1922; member, Indian tariff board, 22nd June, 1925; offg. president, ditto, Apl., 1931; confd., Dec., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; dir.-genl. of comml. intell. and statistics, Jan., 1935.

MATTHEW, KANIANTHRA THOMAS, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 12th Dec., 1899).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1924; dep. consvr., Sept., 1929.

MATTHEWMAN, THOMAS HENRY, M.I.E.E. (b. 10th Oct., 1883).—Apptd. prof. of elec.,

Maclagan Engrg. Coll., Lahore, 17th Aug., 1924; retd.,

MATTHEWS, HORATIO KEITH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag.) (b. 4th Apl., 1917).—Educ. at Epsom Coll., and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 4th Oct., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

MATTHEWS, VICTOR GEORGE, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (fin. dept., govt. of India) (b. 3rd March, 1907).—Educ. at Latymer Secondary Sch., Edmonton, University Coll., London, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., Central Provs., Aug., 1935; and again, Aug., 1936; pte. secty. to govr., Central Provs., March, 1936; under-secty., dept. of e., h., and l., govt. of India, Oct., 1937; confd., Apl., 1938; asst. fin. advr., mily. fin., Oct., 1938; under-secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1939; addl. dy. secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, June, 1941; offic. on spl. duty, ditto, Oct., 1941; collr., customs, Madras, Apl., 1943; ditto, Bombay, Apl., 1944.

MATTHEWS, WILLIAM, M.B.E., late India Office (b. 23rd Sept., 1878).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

MAUGER, WALTER KYLE, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 1st Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1929, and again, Apl., 1933; confd., Nov., 1934; senr. supt., police, Lahore, June, 1941; Indian police medal, 1943; asst. inspr.-genl., civic guards, Lahore, July, 1944.

MAUNG BA AYE, Burma Police (Cl. I) (asst. supt.).—Apptd. on probn., 10th Jan., 1939.

MAUNG, MAUNG HLA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 11th Feb., 1911).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Akyab, Rangoon Univ., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, oil fields, Feb., 1939.

MAUNG, MAUNG OHN, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 2nd Feb., 1913).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; apptd. on probn., 1st Oct., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in Burma, Oct., 1938, and served as asst. commr.; under-secty., commerce dept., June, 1941.

MAUNG, MAUNG SAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 26th Apl., 1904).—Educ. at University of Rangoon and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936; confd., Oct., 1939.

MAUNG SAN LIN, Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 16th May, 1916).—Educ. at Govt. English High Sch., Maymyo, and Rangoon Univ.; apptd. on probn., 30th Sept., 1938.

MAUNG SEIN TUN, Burma Police (Cl. I) (asst. supt.).—Apptd. on probn., 10th Jan., 1939.

MAUNG SET, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 24th Nov., 1884).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

MAUNG, U. AYE, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Burma) (b. 26th Dec., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1914; asst. exec. engr., June, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937; ch. engr., May, 1939.

MAUNG, U. AYE (2), B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 31st May, 1896).—Joined the service, Oct., 1919; asst. engr.,

Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1928.

MAUNG, U. BA (C.5), B.A., Indian Police (b. 9th Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 27th May, 1929.

MAUNG, U. CHIT (A.2), B.A., Burma Commn. (dep. commr.) (b. 25th March, 1895).—Joined the service, 22nd Jan., 1917; extra asst. commr., June, 1921; dep. commr., March, 1934; secty. to govt., judl. dept., Burma, March, 1936; chairman, Rangoon development trust, March, 1939.

MAUNG, U. KIN, B.A., Indian Police (b. 23rd Feb., 1911).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 7th Feb., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937; transf. to mil. dept., Nov., 1940.

MAUNG, U. MAUNG, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 19th Aug., 1899).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

MAUNG, U. MAUNG (B.7), Barr., late Burma Judl. Dept. (b. 5th Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

MAUNG, ZAW WIN, Burma Police (Cl. I).—Apptd., asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1937.

MAUNSELL, ANTHONY CHARLES KER, Capt., I.A., Indian Pol. Service (b. 23rd Oct., 1907).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1927; pers. asst. to the A.G.G., E. States, Apl., 1934; pol. prob. under trng. in for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1934; ditto, asst. collr., Bareilly, March, 1935; asst. commr., Kohat, June, 1936; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Oct., 1937; confd., March, 1938; asst. commr., Kohat, Dec., 1938; under-secty. to resdt. for Cent. India, Apl., 1940; pol. agt., Malwa, Nov., 1941; sec. to resdt. for Cent. India, July, 1942; ditto, eastern states, March, 1943, and again, Sept., 1943; to the resdt. for Hyderabad, Dec., 1944.

MAXTED, DENIS SYDNEY, late Indian Police (b. 2nd Aug., 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Bengal, Nov., 1932; confd., March, 1935; retd., Sept., 1940.

MAXWELL, SIR REGINALD MAITLAND, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (advr. to sec. of state for India) (b. 24th Aug., 1882).—Educ. at Marlborough and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and mag. and asst. settlt. offr.; compiler of genl. admn. report, 1908-9; on depn. to Madras to study working of salt and excise depts., Aug. to Oct., 1910; offg. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Nov., 1916; dep. commr. of salt and excise, Bombay, Sept., 1917; pte. sec. to H.E. the Govr., Sept., 1920; spl. duty, Bombay sect., Feb., 1921; sec., retrenchment cttee., Sept., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1923; offg. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Jan., 1924; confd., Oct., 1926; offg. secty. to govt., genl., etc., depts., June, 1928; pte. secty. to govt. of Bombay, March, 1929; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, genl. and educ. depts., Oct., 1931; secty. to govt. of Bombay, home and eccles. depts., Apl., 1932; C.S.I., June, 1933; secty., home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1936; member, council of state, Oct., 1936; commr., Dec., 1936; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, June, 1937; secty., home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1937; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Apl., 1938; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1939; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1944; retd., Apl., 1944; advr. to sec. of state for India, June, 1944.

MAXWELL, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., M.V.O.,

late Indian C.S. (Punjab and N.W.F.P.)—Retd., Sept., 1918.*

MAY, PAUL ROBERT BUCHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 19th May, 1905).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Oct. 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; addl. ditto, Oct., 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1936.

MAYA DAS, C. P. (see Das, C. P. M.).

MAYBURY, MAURICE ALFRED, Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 10th Nov., 1914).—Educ. at The Common Weal Secondary Sch., Swindon, and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 30th Sept., 1938.

MAYERS, STANLEY RAY, late Indian Police (b. 27th Oct., 1882).—Retd., June, 1935.*

MAYNARD, REV. BERTRAM MARTIN, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 10th Feb., 1881).—Retd., June, 1935.*

MAYNE, GEN. SIR A. G. O. MOSLEY, K.C.B., C.B.E., D.S.O., A.D.C.—Apptd. princpl. staff offer to the sec. of state for India and for Burma and sec., milty. dept., 1st Jan., 1945.

MAZUMDAR, BIDHAN PRASHAD, L.M.E., D.P.H., D.T.M., Public Health Dept., Bihar (asst. dir.) (b. 22nd Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1915; asst. dir. of public health, May, 1925.

MAZUMDAR, KHAGENDRA NATH, M.A., Barr. (first asst. sec. to govt. of Bengal, legis. dept.).—Joined the service as asst. sec., Bengal legis. council, 1st May, 1919; 2nd asst. sec., legis. dept., May, 1924; offg. 1st ditto, Apl. to Nov., 1930; confd., Sept., 1932; offg. secty., ditto, Nov., 1935.

MAZUMDAR, NALINI NATH, O.B.E., Rai Bahadur, late Indian Police, (b. 1st July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

MAZUMDAR, SUDHENDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian C.S.
