(b. 17th June, 1893).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; on mil. service from Nov., 1915; joined the service, 7th Oct., 1919; served in Assam as asst. commr., from 4th Dec., 1919; presdt., Manipur durbar, June, 1921 to Sept., 1922; offg. under-sec. to govt. of Assam, May, 1924; confd., Jan., 1925; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1929; and again, March, 1933; provincial census supt., Assam, Apl., 1930; commr. of excise, etc., Assam, Nov., 1933; dep. commr., Apl., 1935; reform officer and addl. secty. to govt., Assam, March, 1935; secty. educn. and local self-govt. depts., Apl., 1937; commr. of inc.-tax, Sept., 1939; member centr. bd. rev., Sept., 1941–Oct., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1942.

MULLEN, NORMAN ARTHUR JOHN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Burma) (b. 15th Aug., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 1st Dec., 1930; confd., June, 1933.

MULLICK, SUKUMAR, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 27th Oct., 1916).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. in 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

MULLINGS, SIR CLEMENT TUDWAY, C.S.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 9th Apl., 1874).—Retd. July, 1929.*

MUMIN, MUHAMMAD ABDUL, see Muhammad Abdul Mumin.

MUNDELE, INDU BHUSAN, B.A. late Indian C.S. (b. 12th June, 1881).—Educ. at Bishop's Coll. Sch., Calcutta, and Calcutta Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 29th Dec., 1905, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.

and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1914; sess. and sub-judge, May, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1922; ditto, 1st grade, Nov., 1934; retd., Dec., 1940.

MUNIR, MUHAMMAD (judge, high ct., Lahore).—Apptd., July, 1943.

MUNISHWAR CHAND, LALA, Rai Bahadur, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 28th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1931; under-sec. to govt., irrign. bch., Punjab, Feb., 1935 to March, 1937.

MUNN, JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 27th July, 1920).—Educ. at High Sch. of Stirling and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. on probn. after seln., in June, 1941, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

MUNN, NAPIER (see Napier-Munn).

MUNRO, ALEXANDER SINCLAIR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 4th Sept., 1910).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1937.

MUNRO, CHARLES ALEXANDER, late Public Works Dept., Punjab (b. 14th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as inspr. of draglines, 29th March, 1928; exec. engr., dragline divn., Oct., 1933; retd., 14th Aug., 1944.

MUNRO, DONALD GEORGE, B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 4th Apl., 1895).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, 4th Dec., 1920; retd., Dec., 1938.

MUNRO, JOHN BENNETT LORIMER, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 20th May, 1905).—Retd., June, 1939.*

MUNSEY, SYDNEY THOMAS HENSMAN, C.I.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 12th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 11th Oct., 1911; in mil. employ from 14th Sept., 1915 to 11th Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1935; C.I.E., June, 1944; retd., 11th Oct., 1944.

MURDOCH, JAMES DUNCAN, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), Major, I.M.S. (profr. obst. etc., Patna medl. coll.) (b. 19th Sept., 1900).—Date of first commn., 1st Feb., 1933; granted an ante-date of one year, civil employ in Orissa from 12th May, 1939; profr. of obstetrics, Orissa medl. sch., Bihar, Dec., 1939.

MURISON, TAYLOR DAVID, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 7th Nov., 1882).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

MURPHY, ALFRED, O.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 28th Jan., 1880).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

MURPHY, GERALD PATRICK, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 8th May, 1888).—First commn., 25th Jan., 1908; in mil. employ till Apl., 1913, when he was apptd. asst. pol. agent (on probn.); on mil. service, Dec., 1914 to Dec., 1920; asst. resdt., Aden, Dec., 1920; asst. pol. agent, Apl., 1921; asst. resdt., Aden, Apl., 1923; pol. agent and British consul, Muscat, Sept., 1926; ditto, Banas Kantha, March, 1931; pol. agent, Sahar Kantha, Apl., 1933; ditto, western Kathiawar, May, 1933; secty. to A.G.G., eastern states and pol. agent, Sambalpur, Oct., 1935; A.G.G., eastern states (in addn.), March, 1937; pol. agent, Sambalpur, March, 1937; resdt. for Kolhapur and Deccan states, Oct., 1937; resdt. for Madras states, Jan., 1939; confd. as resdt.

Cl. II, Feb., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1943; retd., Nov., 1943.

MURPHY, PATRICK WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 13th July, 1877).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

MURPHY, RAYMOND CLIVE, Capt. I.A. (b. 4th May, 1912).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Oct., 1938; asst. pol. agt., Orissa states, Aug., 1943, and again, Sept., 1944.

MURPHY, SIR STEPHEN JAMES, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 13th June, 1875).—Retd., June, 1935.*

MURPHY, TERENCE ARTHUR, late Income Tax dept., Bombay (b. 29th Dec., 1888).—Appointed to income tax dept. of central board of revenue, 12th May, 1907; asst. commr. of income tax, Sind, Apl., 1924; ditto, Bombay city, Dec., 1932; retd., 10th Oct., 1943.

MURRAY, SIR ALEXANDER ROBERTSON, K.C.I.E., C.B.E., (b. 29th Nov., 1872), late presdt., spl. tariff bd.*

MURRAY, COLIN ROBERT BAILLIE, Indian Police (inspr.-gen. Orissa) (b. 12th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Nov., 1911; supt., Feb., 1923; cent. intell. offr., B. and O., Feb., 1936; dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1938; inspr.-gen., Orissa, Sept., 1944.

MURRAY, DUNCAN CAMERON, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 26th Apl., 1916).—Educ. at Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 14th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

MURRAY, HERBERT EDWARD, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S. (surg. supt. P.G. hosp., Calcutta) (b. 19th May, 1889).—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1915; apptd. surg. supt., P.G. Hospital, Calcutta, March, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1944.

MURRAY, PETER, B.A., Burma C.S., Cl. I. (asst. commr.) (b. 18th July, 1915).—Educ. at Portsmouth Gr. Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 1st Oct., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1938, and served as asst. commr.; on naval service, Aug., 1940.

MURRAY, WALTER, late Indian Police (b. 21st Oct., 1880).—Retd., Oct., 1935.*

MURRELL, WILLIAM LEE, O.B.E., B.C.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bihar) (b. 26th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr. 1st Oct., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr., March, 1939, O.B.E., Jan. 1941.

MURSHED, KHONDKAR GHOLAM (see Khondkar Gholam Murshed).

MURTI, NORIGOPALAKRISHNA, B.E., Ind. Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 16th Feb., 1910).—Asst. exec. engr., Bombay, Aug., 1932.

MURTI, N. V. SHRINIVAS, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 5th Nov., 1895).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 21st July, 1920; asst. exec. engr., July, 1921; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1925; confd., July, 1930.

MURTRIE, MERVYN DAVID, India Posts and Telegraphs (postmaster-general) (b. 14th Sep., 1893).—Joined the service, Oct., 1914; gazetted postmaster, Oct., 1925; presidency postmaster, Jan., 1938; p.m.g., Feb., 1942.

MUSHARAN, SHAMNATH, Barr., late Legislative Dept., Govt. of India (b. Nov., 1883).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

MUSHTAQ AHMAD, B.Sc., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 14th Feb., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 4th Dec., 1925; transferred to Punjab

from 19th Oct., 1920; dep. consvr., Dec., 1929; soil consvrn. offr., Oct., 1944.

MUSPRATT, SIR SYDNEY FREDERICK, K.C.B., C.S.I., C.I.E., D.S.O., General, I.A., retd. (b. 11th Sept. 1878).—Sec., mil. dept., India Office, Sept., 1931 to Nov., 1933; and again, Oct., 1937 to Dec., 1941.*

MUSTAFA, GHULAM, KHAN SAHIB CHAUDHRI B.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1913; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1929; confd., May, 1936.

MUSTAFA, SAIYID GHULAM, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Sind) (b. 14th July, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; suptg. engr. (confd.), Jan., 1942.

MUSTILL, FREDERICK JOHN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 6th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

MUSTO, SIR ARNOLD ALBERT, Kt., C.I.E., M.Inst.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Aug., 1934.*

MUTKEKAR, SATWAJI GUNDONJI, B.Ag. (Bom.), M.Sc. (Mass.), late Indian Agric. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 22nd March, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1923; retd., March, 1941.

MUTSADDI, LAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 6th Aug., 1913).—Educ. at Shambhoo Dayal High Sch., Ghazia-bad, Agra Univ., Allahabad Univ., London Sch. of Oriental Studies, and Fitzwilliam House, Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

MUZAFFAR HUSSAIN, C.I.E., KHAN BAHADUR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 19th Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., N.W. rly., variously from Apl., 1932; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), Apl., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Jan., 1936; dir., rly. bd., Jan., 1938; member ditto, D3c., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1941; retd., June, 1942.

MUZAFFAR, SHAIKH-UD-DIN, M.A., M.Sc. (Punjab), Ph.D. (Cantab.), Public Works Dept., Punjab (b. 24th Sept., 1896).—Apptd. prof. of science, Maclagan engrg. coll., 22nd June, 1924.

MUZUMDAR, V. D. (asst. commr. of income-tax) (b. 4th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, June, 1923; held non-gaz. posts, Apl., 1918, to May, 1923; income-tax offr., Feb., 1924 to Feb., 1934; asst. commr., Sind, March, 1937.

MYAING, U. THAN, Indian Police. —Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 26th Jan., 1933.

MYERS, MISS KATHLEEN MABEL, K.I.-H. gold medal, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (princpl., Queen Mary's coll., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. addl. prof., Queen Mary's coll., Madras, 26th March, 1921; prof., ditto, Dec., 1925; princpl., ditto, March, 1936, K.I.-H. medal (gold), Jan., 1943.

MYSORE SUBRA, RAO, B.A. (Mysore), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 25th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Bangalore, Mysore Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1930; joined the service, Oct., 1931, and served in B. and O. as asst. mag. and collr.

NADKARNI, LAXMIKANT MADHAVBAO, B.A., M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 3rd March, 1915).—Educ. at Elphinstone High Sch.,

Bombay, Bombay Univ., London Sch. of Economics and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; appointed on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 12th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

NAG, BALWANT SINGH (see Balwant, N. S.).

NAG, KHAGENDRA CHANDRA, M.B.E., B.A. (Cal.), Barr., late Bengal Judicial Service (b. 10th July, 1881).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

NAGARKAR, CHANDRAKANT BALVANTRAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 28th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Wilson Coll., Bombay, and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; judge and sess. judge, May, 1930; regr. high ct., Bombay, June, 1931 to June, 1935.

NAGARKATTI, KRISHNA NARAYANA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 5th March, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Mysore and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 10th Oct., 1929; arrived 15th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1933.

NAGARVALA, JAMSHID DORAB, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt., Bombay) (b. 13th Nov., 1914).—Apptd. on probn., 2nd Feb., 1937; confd., Sept., 1938.

NAGENDRA SINGH, MAHARAJ, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., C.P.) (b. 18th March, 1914).—Educ. at Ajmer Coll., Agra Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 15th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the C.P. as asst. commr.

NAGLE, JAMES NICHOLAS EASTMAN (see Eastman Nagle).

NAIDU, G. NARASIMHULU (see Narasimhulu Naidu G.).

NAIDU, SIRAM LAKSHMINARASINGHA RAO, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. rly.) (b. 7th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service, 3rd July, 1911; dist. traffic supt., March, 1931; statistical offcr., E.B. rly., March, 1931.

NAIFF, JOHN CALVERLEY, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 31st Oct., 1913).—Educ. at Hanley High Sch., Stoke-on-Trent, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

NAIK, ATMARAN SHRIDHAR, B.Sc., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag.) (b. 19th May, 1917).—Educ. at N.M.V. High Sch., Poona, Bombay Univ. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. after seln., in Aug., 1940, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

NAIK, RAMA PRASANNA, M.A., Indian C.S. (b. 20th Dec., 1920).—Educ. at Nagpur Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1943 and served in Madras.

NAIR (see also Nayar).

NAIR, PALAT GOVINDAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-sec., public dept. Madras) (b. 20th May, 1916).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 27th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Jan., 1940; under-sec., public dept., Oct., 1942.

NAIR, THEKATH KUMARAN, B.A. (Mad.), B.Sc. (B'ham), late Indian State Rlys. (b. 16th March, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. chemist and metallurgist, 2nd Aug., 1915; offg.

chemist and metallurgist, July, 1916; confd., July, 1917; ret'd., 23rd Dec., 1942.

NAIR, UNNI KANNAN, Local Fund Audit dept., Burma (asst. examr.) (b. 15th Apr., 1886).—Previous service in non-gazetted appts. from 15th Apr., 1908; divl. auditor, Feb., 1925; offg. asst. examr., May, 1926; confd., Sept., 1926; offg. examr., June, 1931; asst. examr., Jan., 1937.

NAIR, VALLILATH MADHATHIL MADHAVAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 8th Oct., 1919).—Educ. at Christian Coll. Sch., Madras, Madras Univ., Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. after seln. in July, 1942, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

NAMBIYAR, AMANDA KALOTH KUNHIRAMAN (see Kunhiraman Nambiyar, A.K.).

NANAK, SINGH, Sardar Sahib Sardar, late Punjab C.S. (b. 1st March, 1889).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

NARAIN, GOVIND, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 5th May, 1917).—Educ. at A.P. Mission High Sch., Mainpuri, Allahabad Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 21st Oct., 1939; confd., 24th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940 and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

NANA RAO, TANJORE KRISHNARAO, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th March, 1892).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1916; dep. acct. genl., B. and O., Jan., 1934; ditto, Madras, May, 1935; dep. govt. examr. of accts., B.B. and C.I., Rly., July, 1937; chief auditor, Burma Rlys., Oct., 1938; comptr., p. and t. accts., Burma, Nov., 1939; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, May, 1940; dy. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nagpur, Jan., 1942.

NANAVATI, D. H., B.A., LL.B.—Apptd. solicitor to central govt., Bombay, Jan., 1940.

NANAVATI, ERACH MANEKSHAH, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th July, 1877).—Educ. at Surat High Sch., Bombay Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 19th Dec., 1901, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., July, 1911; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1919; offg. judge, chief ct. of Oudh, March, 1928; offg. secty. to govt., legal dept.; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, March, 1930; ret'd., July, 1937.

NANAVATTY, KAIKHUSHRU JEHANGIR, B.Sc. Indian Police (b. 10th March, 1907).—Joined as dep. supt., 2nd Dec. 1930; apptd. to Indian Police as supt., 15th Apr. 1941.

NAND GOPAL, DIWAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., central p.w.d.).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 24th July, 1931; apptd. to I.S.E., as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, July, 1932; transf. to central p.w.d., Apr., 1933; exec. engr., July, 1941.

NAND, GOPAL, LALA, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st March, 1894).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1917; asst. engr., Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1927.

NAND KISHORE, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Nov., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1933.*

NANJAPPA, VENKATASUBRAMANIER, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 26th Jan., 1911).—Educ. at Government Collegiate High Sch., Bangalore, Mysore Univ., University Coll., London, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov.,

1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; on active service from Feb., 1940.

NAPIER, ALEXANDER, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 7th Sept., 1904).—First commn., 26th Aug., 1924; in mil. employ to Nov., 1930; under-sec. to A.G.G. in C.I., Apl., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Dec., 1933; asst. commr., Kohat, Dec., 1936; asst. commr. and city mag., Peshawar, Apl., 1937; under-sec. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1937; v. consul, Khoramshar, Dec., 1938; asst. commr., Tank, May, 1941; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P.; civil defence, May, 1942; home sec. to ditto, June, 1942 (addtl.); sec. and excise asst. to resdt. C.I., March, 1943; 1st sec. to agt.-genl. for India, in China, May, 1944.

NAPIER-MUNN, REV. WILLIAM ERNEST, late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 25th Apl., 1886).—(United Provs.).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 21st Apl., 1929; senior chaplain, Apl., 1939; ret'd., Apl., 1941.

NAQVI, ABU TALIB, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 1st March, 1907).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; on spec. duty at govt. h.q., Nov., 1937; dep. sec., dept. of supply (aircraft), Dec., 1943.

NAQVI, GHAZANFAR ALI (see Ghazanfar, A. N.).

NARA SIMHAM, RAMASWAMY LAKSHMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihur) (b. 27th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1935; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), Oct., 1940.

NARAHARI, RAO, VYAKARANA, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th July, 1893).—Joined the service, 5th March, 1917; dep. acct.-genl. posts and tels., May, 1923, under-sec., fin. dep. govt. of India, Aug., 1924; offg. asst. sec. litto, Nov., 1927; offg. under-sec. govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1929; addl. dep. sec., ditto, March, 1933; temp. jt. sec., ditto, in addn., Aug., 1936; addtl. dep. sec., and Crown fin. offcr., pol. dept., Apl., 1937. C.I.E., May, 1937; offr. on spl. duty pol. dep. and Crown fin. offr., March, 1940; addtl. sec. fin. dept., July, 1944.

NARAIN, GOVIND, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 5th May, 1917).—Educ. at A.P. Mission High Sch., Mainpuri, Allahabad Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 21st Oct. 1939; confd., 24th Aug., 1940; arrived in India Nov., 1940 and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

NARASIMHA AYYANGAR, RAJAGOPALA AYYANGAR, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. Sept., 1881).—Ret'd., Sept., 1936.*

NARASIMHAM PANTULU, MANTHRIFRAGADA, Rao Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1894; dep. collr., March, 1919; offg. collr., May, 1932; member Madras public services commn., Feb., 1935; ret'd., March, 1940.

NARASIMHAN, CHAKRAVARTHI VIJARA-GHAVA, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-sec., devel. dept. Madras) (b. 21st May, 1915).—Educ. at National High Sch., Trichinopoly, Madras Univ., and St. Catherine's Society, Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag.

and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1939; under-sec., devel. dept., Sept., 1942.

NARASIMHULU, NAIDU G., O.B.E., Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.I., late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 9th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 22nd Dec., 1910; confd., 3rd July, 1912; asst. dep. dir.-genl., Sept., 1935; dep. p.m.g., July, 1937; dir. posts and tels., S. and B. circle, June, 1938; dep. dir.-genl., war, Sept., 1939; on depn. to defence dept., March, 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; ret'd., Feb., 1944.

NARAYAN, BRIJ (see Brij Narayan).

NARAYAN, JAGAT (see Jagat N.).

NARAYAN, RAJ (see Raj).

NARAYAN, SHIV, M.A., B.Sc. (Punj.), B.E., M.Sc. (U.S.A.), Mem. A.I.E.E., A.M.I.E.E. (Lond.), A.M.I.E. (India), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th Nov., 1883).—Ret'd., Nov., 1938.*

NARAYAN, S. L. (see Lokur, Narayan Swamirao).

NARAYAN, SRI (see Sri Narayan).

NARAYANA KUDVA, VENKATARAYA (see Kudva, V. N.).

NARAYANA RAI, AJILIDAY, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 28th Jan., 1908).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 7th March, 1932; confd., March, 1934.

NARAYANAN NAIR, KOYAMAN, Diwan, Bahadur, B.A., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 16th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as proby. supt., Nov., 1912; confd., Apl., 1916; depy. postmaster-genl., Apl., 1939; ret'd., July, 1944.

NARAYANASWAMI AYYAR, OMBATHIVELI RAMASWAMI AYYAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1904).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 16th Aug., 1929; exec. engr., Aug., 1938.

NARAYANSWAMI, BRAHMADESAM SUBRAMANYA, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 10th Dec., 1899).—Joined the service as offr., Cl. II, Oct., 1923.

NARAYANASWAMI PILLAI, T. M., Dewan Bahadur.—Apptd. member Madras public serv. commn., Dec., 1944.

NARENDRA NATH SEN, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 1st Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Apl., 1929.

NARGOLWALA, SAKAR DINSHAW, B. Com., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 5th June, 1915).—Educated at Zoroastrian High Sch., Deolali, Bombay Univ., London Sch. of Econ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 8th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

NASH, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 10th Jan., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 15th Oct., 1927; offg. exec. engr., June, 1930; confd., Oct., 1936.

NASH, PHILIP GEOFFREY ELWIN, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (b. 20th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr., under secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Burma, March, 1934 to May, 1936; on spec. duty with the govt. of Burma, May, 1936 to Apl., 1937; secty. to Burma delegate Imperial conf., Apl.-June, 1937; addl. asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Aug., 1937; asst. ditto, Jan., 1938;

dep. ditto, June, 1939, to Aug., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

NASIM ALI, SYED, M.A., B.L., Bengal Judl. Dept. (judge, high ct., Calcutta).—Apptd. addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 13th Nov., 1933; apptd. judge, ditto, Oct., 1936.

NATARAJAN, JAGADISH, B.A., Hons. (Bombay) (b. 17th May, 1902).—Apptd. dep. princpl. information officer, home dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1938; princpl. ditto, dept. of inform. and broadcasting, Nov., 1941.

NATH, AJUDHIA (see Ajudhia Nath).

NATH, AMAR (see Amar Nath).

NATH, GAURI (see Gauri Nath).

NATH, GYAN (see Gyan Nath).

NATH, JAGAN (Juneja see Jagan Nath, J.).

NATH, JAGAN KUKAR (see Jagan, Nath K.).

NATH, JOGENDRA CHANDRA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Jan., 1925; dep. consvr., Jan., 1930.

NATH, PREM (see Prem Nath).

NATH, SURATH (see Surath Nath).

NATHA SINGH, BAWA, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 5th July, 1882).—Retd., July, 1937.*

NAUBAT RAI, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 7th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 1st Apr., 1922; asst. engr. (prov. service), Apr., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; offg. exec. engr., May, 1930; confd., Jan., 1936.

NAWAB SINGH, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 9th Apr., 1907).—Educ. at University of Nagpur and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1935 to Apr., 1937; ditto, sub-permt., Oct., 1938.

NAWABZADA, AMINULLAH KHAN, A.Inst.T., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 16th Aug., 1893).—Asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1915; divl. transpn. officer, May, 1935; retd., 25th Nov., 1944.

NAYAK, PANGAL MANJAPPA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag.) (b. 27th Dec., 1916).—Educ. at Christian High Sch., Udipli, South Kanara, Madras Univ., Imp. Coll. of Science and Technology, London, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd., on probn., 29th Sept. 1939; confd., 24th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

NAYAK, PANGAL RAMANATHA, M.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 29th March, 1911).—Educ. at Christian High Sch., Udipli, Univ. of Madras, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies London; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; dep. sec. (supply), govt. of India, Jan., 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1945.

NAYAR, CHETTUR, Govindan, Diwan Bahadur, C.I.E., B.A., Barr.-at-Law, Judl dept., Madras (law secty. to the govt. of Orissa) (b. 23rd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. district munisf., June, 1910; offg. dist. judge, June, 1933; confd., Aug., 1933; sec., govt. of Orissa, law and comm. dept., Apr., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1939.

NAYAR, SIR CHETTUR MADHAVAN, late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. Jan., 1879).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

NAYAR, KRISHNAPILLAI KARUKAIL KARUNAKARAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 11th Sept., 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and London School of Oriental Studies; joined

the service, 11th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in the United Provs. as tsst. mag. and collr.; judge, small causes ct., Dec., 1936.

NAYAR, MANNATH GOHINDAN, B.A., late Indian Police (b. Aug., 1881).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

NAYAR, MANAKAMPAD KESAVANUNNI, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 25th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

NAYAR, PADUNHARANKUNNATH THAZATHAVIL RAMAN, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1910).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Boys' High Sch., Calicut, Univ. of Madras, and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Jan., 1937; city mag. (1st cl.), Feb., 1938; sub-collr., March, 1939; judl. trng., July, 1942 to Jan., 1944; addtl. dis. and sess. judge, Feb., 1944; again, Nov., 1944.

NAYAR, U. GOVINDAN (see Govindan Nayar, U.).

NAYLOR, LEONARD BROWN, C.B.E., Burma Frontier Service (asst. supt.) (b. 19th March, 1887).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., March, 1914; asst. supt. (B.F.S.), July, 1923; dep. commr. (permt.), July, 1939; C.B.E., June, 1943.

NAYUDU, BANDI VENKATA SRI HARI RAO (see Sri Hari, R. N. B. V.).

NAYUDU, BHASKARA RAO, T. (see Bhaskara Rao Nayudu, T.).

NAYUDU, BUNDY SRIRAMULU (see Sriramulu Nayudu B.).

NAYUDU, PAKALA VENKATARAMANA RAO, Rao Bahadur (see Venkataramana Rao Nayudu, Pakala, Rao Bahadur).

NAZIR, AHMED, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 10th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd., on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 28th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Sind as asst. collr.

NAZIR, ALAM, SHAH, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.) (b. 4th March, 1908).—Joined the service as dep. supt. (prob.), 2nd Jan., 1929; confd., Jan., 1929; apptd. to I.P. as asst. supt., 31st Aug., 1930; offg. supt., Apr., 1931; and again, May, 1933; confd., May, 1943.

NAZIRUDDIN, KAMALUDDIN, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 31st Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 26th June, 1922; asst. exec. engr., June, 1923; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; confd., June, 1932; transf. from Madras to Bihar and Orissa, jt. cadre, Apr., 1936.

NAZR-UL-BAKAR, SHEIKH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 21st Dec., 1913).—Educ. at Denny's High Sch., Rawalpindi, Govt., High Sch., Murree, and Punjab Univ.; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1939; empt. advr. to govt., Aug., 1942; addl. dist. mag., Apr., 1943; dep. dir. civ. supplies, Dec., 1943.

NEAL, REGINALD FRANCIS, Indian Police (b. 8th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Apr., 1933.

NEALE, REGINALD KINGSLEY, Lieut.-Col., Mily. Accts. Dept. (b. 19th July, 1897).—First

commn., Apl., 1916; joined mily. accts. dept. as offcr., Cl. II, July, 1935.

NEALE, SIR WALTER GORDON, Lieut.-Col., K.C.V.O., C.I.E., I.A. (retd.) late India Office (b. 2nd Oct., 1880).—First commn., dated 15th Feb., 1920; in mil. employ until July, 1906, when he was apptd. prob. for pol. dept., N.W.F. Prov.; personal asst. to chief commr., Apl., 1907; dep. commr., temp., D.I., Khan, Dec., 1908; asst. commr., ditto, Jan., 1909; asst. pol. agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Aug., 1909; asst. commr., Hazara, March, 1910; ditto, Oghi and commut., B.M. Police, March, 1910; asst. sec. to chief commr., N.W.F. Prov., and supt. of census, Aug., 1910; asst. to agent to govr.-genl. in Rajputana, Nov., 1911; pol. offcr., Deoli, Apl., 1912; asst. resdnt., western states of Rajputana, Nov., 1912; asst. pol. agent, eastern states of Rajputana, May, 1913; asst. to agent to govr.-genl. in Rajputana, July, 1913; asst. pol. agent, eastern Rajputana states, Nov., 1913; asst. to resdnt. in Persian Gulf, June, 1914 to Apl., 1915; on spl. duty under chief pol. offcr., Basra, 1916; second asst. to agent to govr.-genl. in Rajputana, Oct., 1916; asst. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1917; pol. agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Dec., 1920; pol. agent, eastern Rajputana states, Apl., 1924; first asst. to resdnt. in Kashmir, Nov., 1924; pol. agent, Bhopal, Oct., 1926; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1928; C.I.E., March, 1929; rettd., July, 1930; pol. A.D.C. to secty. of state, Sept., 1933 to Apl., 1945; C.V.O., May, 1937; K.C.V.O., Jan., 1941.

NEEDHAM, SIR RICHARD ARTHUR, Kt., C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., B.Sc., F.R.C.P., D.P.H., Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 31st July, 1877).—Retd., Oct., 1930.*

NEHPAL SINGH, B.A., M.R.A.S., Indian Educl. Service (asst. d.p.s., United Provs.) (b. 14th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as princpl. jubilee inter. coll., Lucknow, 4th July, 1922; hd. master, govt. high schl., Bareilly, Apl., 1924; princpl., govt. inter. coll., Almora, Oct., 1927; offg. inspr. of schools, June, 1928; confd., July, 1932; asst. dir. of public instrn., variously, from Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1937.

NEHRU, BRAJ KUMAR, M.B.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 4th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at the Govt. Intermediate Coll., Allahabad, Univ. of Allahabad, the School of Economics, London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offcr., Apl., 1937; offcr. on spl. duty, fin. dept., Nov., 1939; under-sec., ditto, Sept., 1940; dep. ditto, Apl., 1944; M.B.E., Jan., 1945.

NEHRU, BRIJLAL, M.A., Barr., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 5th May, 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.*

NEHRU, RATAN KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S., Impl. Customs Service (commr. salt rev. (b. 10th Apl., 1902).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1925; arrived, 5th Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1929; Apl., 1932; and again, March, 1933; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, March, 1934; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1935; offg. collr., June, 1936; first secty. to central bd. of rev., etc., Feb., 1937; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1937; first secty., cent. bd. of rev., March, 1938; temp. addl. dep. secty. to govt. of India, commerce dept., Jan., 1939; commr., cent. excises and salt, N.W.

India, from 1st to 10th Dec., 1939; collr., ditto, Dec., 1939; jt. sec., dept. of supply, G./I., July, 1942; ditto, commerce, Oct., 1944.

NEHRU, SHRI SHRIDHAR, M.A., Ph.D., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Agr. Coll. Sch., India, Allahabad; Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, and Heidelberg; apptd. after exam. of 1912, arrived, 1st Dec., 1913, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., March, 1918; under-sec. to govt., Apl., to July, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1923; confd., June, 1930; sec. to govt., industries and educn. dept., June, 1931; dir. of publicity and reforms offr., Jan., 1932; member, Impl. council of agricl. research, June, 1933; mag. and collr. again from Dec., 1933.

NEIL, JOHN FREDERICK, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 12th June, 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, store dept., India Office, Nov., 1919; clerical offcr., Jan., 1920; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. offr., March, 1926.

NELSON, SIR ARTHUR EDWARD, K.C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A. late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 17th Apl., 1875).—Retd., Nov., 1934.*

NELSON, JOHN JOSEPH HARPER, O.B.E., M.C., MD., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (b. 12th Jan., 1882).—Retd., Jan., 1937.*

NELSON, WILLIAM HENRY, C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th June, 1880).—Educ. at Watson's Coll. and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr. and settlt. offr., Sept., 1911; mag. and collr., Sept., 1917; on mil. service, Aug. to Oct., 1918; offg. dir. of land records, Bengal, Feb., 1920; offg. secty. of rev. and irrigation depts., Bengal, Feb., 1928; offg. commr., Dec., 1928; confd., May, 1930; offg. member, board of rev., Bengal, Feb., 1933; and again, Feb., 1936; confd., Feb., 1938; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; retd., Jan., 1940.

NEOGI, PANCHANAN, M.A., Ph.D., F.C.S. (Lond.), late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 4th Oct., 1883).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

NEOGI, PROBHAT CHANDRA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 31st July, 1900).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 22nd Jan., 1926; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Jan., 1935.

NETER PARKASH MOHAM, M.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 12th June, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; M.B.E., Jan., 1942; firewood contr., March, 1944.

NETHERSOLE, MICHAEL HENRY BRADDOX, C.I.E., D.S.O., B.A., Indian Civil Service (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; arrived, 17th Dec., 1919, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1924; asst. mag. and collr., Nov., 1925; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927 to Nov., 1933; sec. inform. dept. and price contr., Nov., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1942.

NEVILLE, COLLIN MAXWELL WRIGHT, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 19th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st May, 1921; supt., Apl., 1929.

NEWCOMB, CLIVE, C.I.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.); late office of high commr. for India (b. 3rd Oct., 1882).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

NEWELL, REV. CHARLES JOHN CUTHBERT, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 29th Jan., 1909).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bombay Diocese, 2nd June, 1944.

NEWMAN, CARL DAMIEN, M.B., B.S., Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as dist. medl. offcr., E.B. Rly., 3rd Dec., 1924; offg. ch. medi. offcr., variously, from June, 1931; confd. B. & A. rly., Feb., 1940.

NEWSAM, WILLIAM OSWALD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 13th March, 1892).—Educ. at Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mily. duty from Nov., 1917 to March, 1919; sub-collr., Oct., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; offg. regr., high court, July, 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Chittoor, and addtl. judge, court of resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1935; ditto, Mysore, Jan., 1936 to July, 1937; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1938; high ct., Madras, July to Oct., 1937, and Feb. to May, 1939; dist. and sess. judge, Chittoor and addtl. judge, court of resdt. and judl. commr. of Coorg, 1939.

NEWTON, BERNARD ST. JOHN, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Apr., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; ditto and under-sec., p.w.d., Nov., 1925; offg. suptg. engr., Apr., 1931; confd., 1937; ch. engr., March, 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

NIAMAT ULLAH, CHAUDHRI, late Judl. dept., United Provs.—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

NIAZ MUHAMMAD KHAN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 12th June, 1907).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1933; under-sec., fin. dept., Apr., 1937; K.-i.-H. Medal, 1938.

NIAZ, MAQBUL MOHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 5th Nov., 1917).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1940, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.

NICHOLL, REV. ESMOND MCKILLOP, M.A., M.C. late Indian Eccles. Estabt., Punjab) (b. 16th March, 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 19th Nov., 1919; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1921; senr., chaplain, June, 1930; retd., Sept., 1941.

NICHOLSON, JOHN PATRICK, O.B.E., United Provs., C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 22nd June, 1907; dep. collr., Apr., 1911; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1931; confd., Sept., 1934; O.B.E., June, 1941.

NICHOLSON, JOHN WILFRED, B.A., C.I.E., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Orissa) (b. 13th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 3rd March, 1916; provl. sylviculturist, Dec., 1921; on depn. to Kenya for 3 years from Sept., 1927; dep. consvr., Feb., 1931; offg. consvr. variously, from Sept., 1931; consvr., Nov., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

NICHOLSON, MARK ALLEYNE, C.I.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (C.M.O., Central India) (b. 10th June, 1885).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; civil surgn., Kurram, Oct., 1922; M.O., S.W. Scouts, Dec., 1923; offg. resdy. surgn. in Persian Gulf, Sept., 1924; agency surgn., Hazara dist., Oct., 1928; ditto, and C.M.O., Rajputana, March, 1931; C.M.O., Central India, and resdy. surgn., Indore, Apr., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

NICHOLSON, PETER FRANCIS COBHAM, Capt. I.A. (b. 11th July, 1914).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Oct., 1939; under-sec. to resdt., Baroda and Gujarat states, May, 1944; under-sec. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1944.

NICOL, CAMERON MACDONALD, C.I.E., M.A., M.D., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Dept. of Public Health, Punjab (dir.) (b. 20th July, 1891).—First commn., 9th Feb., 1923; asst. dir. of public health, Rawalpindi, 18th Feb., 1934; dir. of public health, Aug., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1941.

NICOLSON, JOHN FRANCIS HUNTER, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 28th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Apr., 1920; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925; confd., Oct., 1927; ch. public works offcr., F.S.S., Apr., 1935; O.B.E., June, 1943.

NIGAM, BISHAN NARAIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 4th Apr., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1942.

NIGEL JONES, M. E. (see Jones, M. E. Nigel).

NIGHTINGALE, ARTHUR WINFIELD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908; asst. engr.; on mily. duty from Apr., 1915 to Dec., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; suptg. engr., Sept., 1936; retd., Dec., 1939; and re-empld., Jan., 1941.

NIGHTINGALE, WILFRED ANDREW, B.A. (Mech. Sc.) Cantab., Indian State Rlys. (mech. engr. and transp. (power) offcr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 22nd Feb., 1901).—Asst. works manager, junior scale, E.I. Rly., Feb., 1926, mech. engr. and transp. (power) offcr., senior scale, Nov., 1938.

NILAKANTA AYYAR, MAHADEVA, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Dec., 1878).—Retd., Dec., 1933.*

NILAKANTA MAHADEVA AYYAR, C.I.E., M.A. (Mad.), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 14th May, 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived in India, 2nd Jan., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; and sub-divl. offcr., June, 1924; at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept. (cent. rev.), March, 1925, as offg. asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta; confd., Dec., 1925; asst. collr. of customs, Madras, June, 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Bengal, July, 1928; and again, March, 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1928; offg. ditto, Bengal, Oct., 1930; mag. and collr., July, 1935; addl. dep. secty., dept. of indus. and lab., govt. of India, Apr., 1936; dep. secty., dept. of labour, Nov., 1937; chairman, coal mines stowing bd., Nov., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; confd. mag. and collr., Aug., 1942; dir. and sec., civil supplies, Apr., 1943.

NILKANTH SHIRAM JATAR (see Jatar, N. S.).

NIL RATAN DHAR, M.Sc. (Cal.), D.Sc. (Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S., Indian Educal. Service (b. 2nd Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as profrr., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, 19th July, 1919; with Allahabad Univ. from July, 1922; dep. dir. of public instruction, Apr., 1938; confd., Sept., 1939.

NIND, WILLIAM WALKER, C.I.E., B.A., late

Impl. Customs Service (b. 26th Sept., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1937*; served in India office from Feb., 1940.

NINNIS, ROBERT DOUGLAS KIALLMARK, late Indian Police (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 30th Apr., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th March, 1921; offg. supt., March, 1926; confd., Sept., 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; ret'd., 10th March, 1940.

NIRANJAN DAS, GULHATI LALA, Indian Service of Engrs (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Nov., 1904).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., Oct., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936; under-sec., p.w.d., (irrgn.), Dec., 1941.

NIRINJAN PRASAD, GOYAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Apr., 1903).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 28th Oct., 1905; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

NIVAS, HARI KRISHNA, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th May, 1905) (under secty., public works dept., Bihar).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 14th Nov., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1937; under-secty., p.w.d., Bihar, March, 1939.

NIX-JAMES, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 22nd March, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 28th March, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1927.

NIXON, SIR JOHN CARSON, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 21st Sept., 1887).—Educ. at Technical Institute, Portsmouth, Royal Coll. of Science, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; services placed at disposal of Indian munitions board, March to Dec., 1918; under-sec., govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., Dec., 1918; under-sec., comm. and ind. dept., govt. op. India, July, 1919; under-sec., finance dept., govt. of India, Apr., 1920; dep. acct.-gen., Bengal, Apr., 1921; dep. contr. of currency, Bombay, Oct., 1923; offg. dep. contr., of currency, Madras, Apr., 1925; acct.-genl., Madras, Jan., 1926; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1928; on spl. duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1930; temp. jt. sec., ditto, July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1932; dir., rly. audit, Nov., 1932; acct.-genl., Bengal, Nov., 1933; addl.-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (temp.), March, 1935; member, council of state, Apr., 1935; sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1935; C.S.I., June, 1936; offg. member, govt.-genl.'s exec. council, Apr., 1937; K.C.I.E., June, 1938; ret'd., Feb., 1941.

NIYOGI, SIR MACHRAJ BHAWANI SHANKAR, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., LL.M., Judicial Dept., Central Provs. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 30th Aug., 1886).—Apptd. offg. addtl. judl. commr., June, 1930; confd., Sept., 1930; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936; Kt., June, 1944.

NIZAM-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (inspr.) (b. 3rd July, 1893).—Asst. engr., 17th Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Aug., 1935; supt. of works, Sept., 1938; confd. as inspr., Jan., 1944 (ports and air dept.).

NOBLE, REV. REGINALD HARRY, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Punjab).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 11th Nov., 1928; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1938; ret'd., Oct., 1942.

NOBLE, THOMAS ALEXANDER FRASER, M.A.,

Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 29th Apr., 1918).—Educ. at Rose's Academical Instn., Nairn and Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. on probn. after seln. iq Aug., 1940, and served in N.W.F. Prov. as asst. commr.

NOEL, EDWARD WILLIAM CHARLES, C.I.E., D.S.O., Lieut.-Col., I.A., late Indian Political Service (b. 14th Apr., 1886).—First commn., 21st Dec., 1904; in mil. employ till Dec., 1912, when he was apptd. to pol. dept. on probn.; H.M.'s vice-consul, Ahwaz, Apr., 1915; confd. as pol. offr., Feb., 1916; on special duty in the Caucasus, Jan. to Oct., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; served under high commr. for Iraq, Nov., 1918 to Jan., 1920; pol. agent, Kurram, Apr., 1924; consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, July, 1929; dep. commr., Dehra Ismail Khan, May, 1931; dir. of agric. and allied depts., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1933; dep. commr., Mardan, Apr., 1938; ret'd., Apr., 1941.

NOON, SIR FIROZ KHAN, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E. (member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council).—Educ. Chief's Coll., Lahore, and Wadhham Coll., Oxford; advocate of Lahore high ct., and member Punjab legisl. council from 1921; minister for local self-govt., Punjab, 1927; educn. minister, Punjab 1930; high commr. for India, 1st July, 1930; K.C.I.E., May, 1937; K.C.S.I., Oct., 1941; labour member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Oct., 1941; defence member, ditto, July, 1942.

NORBURY, HARRY CARTER, J.P., M.Inst.T., late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 18th Oct., 1883).—Ret'd., Dec., 1939.*

NORMAN, CHARLES ARCHIBALD KENSIT, O.B.E., B.A., late India Office (b. 12th March, 1884).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; solicitor, supreme court, 1909; served on the legal staff, public trustee's office, and H.M. customs and excise, Jan., 1910 to Feb., 1914; apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 2nd Feb., 1914; asst. solicitor, Aug., 1933; O.B.E., June, 1941; ret'd., Mar., 1944.

NORMAN, DOUGLAS REAY, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Apr., 1890).—Educ. at Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from July, 1916 to Dec., 1918; asst. membr. of legal affairs and sec. to Bombay legis. council, May, 1921; offg. judge and sess. judge, March, 1923; confd., Apr., 1929; judl. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Dec., 1932; ditto, states of western India and Gujerat states, Apr., 1933; and again, Oct., 1935; ret'd., July, 1940.

NORMAN-WALKER, HUGH SELBY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 17th Dec., 1916).—Educ. at Sherborne and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

NORMAN-WALKER J. N. (see Walker, J. N.).

NORMAND, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM BLYTH, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., late Indian Meteorological Dept. (b. 10th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1913, as impl. meteorologist; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Feb., 1916 to Apr., 1919; offg. dir.-gen. of observatories from June to Oct., 1924; and from March, 1927; ret'd., 10th Sept., 1944; Kt., Jan., 1945.

NORONHA, MARK STANISLAUS, B.A., LL.B., Judicial dept., Bombay (b. 19th July, 1889).—Apptd. judge, small cause ct., Bombay, 30th Dec., 1928; ch. judge, ditto, Oct., 1943.

NORONHA, RONALD CARLTON VIVIAN PIEDADE, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.,

C.P.) (b. 14th May, 1916).—Educ. at St. Aloysius European High Sch., Vizagapatam, Madras Univ., University Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 8th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in C.P. as asst. commr.; contr. of food supplies, June, 1943; dep. dir., food (distrbn.), May, 1944.

NOTTAGE, JOHN BOWTELL, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Feb., 1891).—Retd., July, 1939.*

NOYCE, SIR FRANK, K.C.S.I., C.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—(b. 4th June, 1878). Retd., Nov., 1937.*

NUNAN, DR. WILLIAM, B.A., M.D., B.Ch. (Dub.), T.C.D., late Medical dept., Bombay (b. 26th Jan., 1880).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

NUR ILAHI, KHAN BAHADUR, Shaikh, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Punjab) (b. 8th July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

NUR, KHWAJA MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Nur, Khwaja).

NURMUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, Shaikh, O.B.E., M.A., late Punjab C.S. (b. 26th Apr., 1886).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr. on probn., 31st March, 1910; confd., Apr., 1913; waterlogging asst. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Oct., 1928; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1931; confd., July, 1933; O.B.E., June, 1941; retd.,

NUTT, HAROLD ROTHERY, M.D., F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S., retd. (b. 28th Dec., 1876).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

NYUN, MAUNG, B.A., Indian C.S. (addl. secty., com. and indus. dept., govt. of Burma) (b. 30th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at A.V. High Sch., Thaton, Rangoon Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., com. and indus. dept., Burma, March, 1937; asst. collr. of customs, July, 1941; dep. ditto, Aug., 1940; addl. ditto, Jan., 1941.

NYUN, MAUNG SOE, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 6th March, 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., Rangoon Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Apr., 1938; under trng. in office of acctnt.-genl., Burma, Apr., 1941.

OAK, VIDYADHAR GOVIND, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 18th May, 1909).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1934; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1941.

OAKDEN, SIR RALPH, Kt., C.S.I., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 13th July, 1871).—Retd., Dec., 1930.*

OAKLEY, FRANK STUART, Indian Police (asst. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 8th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 22nd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., 1927; confd., July, 1939; cent. intell. offer., Central Provs. and Berar, Apr., 1939, to Jan., 1944.

OBAIDULLAH, ABU ZAFAR, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 28th Feb., 1911).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 1st July, 1931; confd., Sept., 1933; confd., supt., July, 1943.

O'BRIEN, J. A., late Civil Aviation Directorate (b. 1st Feb., 1881).—Joined the service as chief inspr. of aircraft (temp.), Nov., 1936; retd., 12th May, 1944.

O'BRIEN, MICHAEL, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st May, 1883).—Retd., May, 1938.*

O'BRIEN-SMITH, EDWARD QUINTON, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 20th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

O'CALLAGHAN, TIMOTHY PATRICK MORIARTY, C.I.E., late Indian Police, Assam (b. 25th Nov., 1886).—Retd., July, 1937.*

ODDIN-TAYLOR, HARRY WILLOUGHBY, C.B.E., A.C.G.I., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 12th July, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; resigned the service from 26th Sept., 1920; re-apptd. as exec. engr., 7th Dec., 1926; offg. supt. engr., Oct., 1931; O.B.E., Jan., 1933; jt. secty. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, in the p.w.d., Jan., 1936; C.B.E., May, 1937; retd., July, 1943.

ODELL, FRANCIS DAVID, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Agric. Service (b. 20th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. dir. of agric., Feb., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

ODGERS, SIR CHARLES EDWIN, Kt., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 17th Jan., 1870).—Retd., Jan., 1930.*

ODLING, RICHARD ERNEST, O.B.E., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 14th June, 1886).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., July, 1920; dep. finl. advr., R.A.F., May, 1933; D.M.A.G. (senr.), Oct., 1934; cl. I offr., July, 1940; field contr., mil. accts., Dec., 1940; command contr., Nov., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1942; retd., June, 1942.

O'DONNELL, SIR SAMUEL PERRY, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 16th Dec., 1874).—Retd., May, 1930.*

O'DOWD, GERALD RONEY, B.A., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 1st Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., Dec., 1909; confd., cl. I offr., June, 1930; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1930; confd. as cmd. contr., Oct., 1935; retd., June, 1940.

O'FLYNN, ERIC MORTIMER, Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 28th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Apr., 1930; confd., March, 1933; supt., confd., Apr., 1942; p.a. to inspr.-genl., Sind, June, 1944; provl. motor transpt. offr., etc., Nov., 1944.

OGILVIE, SIR CHARLES MACIVOR GRANT, C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. of India, defence dept.) (b. 6th May, 1891).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. administrator, Nabha state, July, 1923; asst. ditto, Sept., 1923; offg. dep. commr., May, 1925; provl., ditto, June, 1926; senr. secty. to finl. commrs., Apr., 1928; C.B.E., June, 1929; home secty. to govt., Punjab, May, 1929; and again, June, 1933; addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, fin. dept., Sept., 1933; offg. secty., ditto, Oct., 1933; confd., Oct., 1934; on depts. for course at Impl. Defence Coll., Jan., 1936; secty. to govt. of India, defence dept., Apr., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1941; kt., Jan., 1944.

OGILVIE, SIR GEORGE DRUMMOND, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 18th Feb., 1882).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

OGLE, JOHN WILFRED, Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt. 25th

March, 1913; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; divl. transportation offcr., July, 1925; publicity offcr., March, 1931; dep. ch. comml. manager, Apl., 1940; divl. supt., senr., Dec., 1940.

O'GORMAN, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bombay) (b. 8th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 2nd July, 1913, as asst. supt.; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; supt. of police, Jan., 1925; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1930; cent. intell. offcr., Bombay Prov., Aug., 1938; confd. as supt. (sel. grade), Oct., 1941; dep. inspr.-genl. (confd.), March, 1944; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1944.

O'GORMAN, MICHAEL THOMAS, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 15th Jan., 1881).—Retd., Jan., 1936.*

O'HAGAN, REV. CHARLES GILBERT, Indian Eccles. Estabt., Punjab (b. 17th Sept., 1901).—Apptd. junr. chaplain on probn., March, 1940.

OHN, MAUNG, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 16th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at Government High Schs., Thaton and Rangoon, Rangoon Univ., and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Jan., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1940; on milty. service, Nov., 1940; dep. commr., Thayetmgo, March, 1941.

OJHA, RADHESHYAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Patna Univ.; apptd. on probn., 4th Oct., 1937; confd., 27th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1940; under-sec., govt. of Orissa, Feb., 1943, to May, 1944.

OKA, DATTATRAYA SITARAM, LL.B., late Bombay C.S. (Judicial Branch) (b. 20th June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1938.*

OLIVER, HENRY ARTHUR, Indian Police (b. 14th Apl., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Dec., 1937; confd. Dec. 1939.

OLIVER, PETER RICHARD, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 3rd June, 1917).—Educ. at Felsted and Trinity Hall Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 7th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

OLIVIER, OF RAMSDEN, 1st Baron, Rt. Hon. SYDNEY HALDANE OLIVIER, P.C., K.C.M.G., C.B., B.A., LL.D.—Secty. of State for India, 23rd Jan. to 6th Nov., 1924.

OLPHERTS-FORRESTER, SHIRLEY WYBRANDTS GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd Sept., 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad, Univ. of Nagpur, University Coll., London, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 15th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; in exec. charge, central jail, Nagpur, Sept., 1942, to Jan., 1943.

OLVER, SIR ARTHUR, C.B., C.M.G., F.R.C.V.S., Col., R.A.V.C., retd., late animal husbandry expert.—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

OLVER, STEPHEN JOHN LINLEY, Indian Police (b. 16th June, 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 1st Nov., 1935; apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Sept., 1944; asst. pol. agt., Quetta-Pishin, Sept., 1944.

OMAR, ZAFAR (see Zafar Omar).

OMMANNEY, FREDERICK ROY, Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 12th Sept., 1898).—Joined the

service as asst. supt., Bombay, 15th Dec., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1926; confd., Jan., 1933.

O'NEILL, Rt. Hon. SIR HUGH, Bart., M.P., partly under-sec. of state for India and for Burma, Sept., 1939, to May, 1940.

O'NEILL, JOHN CONNELL, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 27th May, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1922; services lent to Burma Rlys. from 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. divl. supt., N.W. rly., July, 1934; dep. chief engr. (prov. perm.), Feb., 1938; retd., Jan., 1943.

O'NEILL, NIALL O'HAGAN, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 14th Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Beaumont and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; a.d.c. to the govt., Nov., 1939, to July, 1941; served with East. Group supply cl., Feb., 1942; M.B.E., Jan., 1945.

O'NEIL SHAW, JOHN CHARLES LIDDELL, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 5th March, 1896).—Joined the service, 5th May, 1920; asst. supt., Feb., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd., Aug., 1926; O.B.E., June, 1941.

O'NEILL, REV. WILFRED STEPHEN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Lahore, 24th March, 1927; ditto, N.W.F.P., Apl., 1927; senr. chaplain, March, 1937.

ORAM, ARTHUR, C.I.E., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (N.W.F.P.) (b. 20th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; under-secty. to govt. p.w.d. (irrign.), May, 1926; offg. suptg. engr., Nov., 1927; suptg. engr. (provl. perm.), July, 1935; ch. engr., Apl., 1938; retd., Oct., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

ORANGE, SIR HUGH WILLIAM, K.B.E., C.B., C.I.E.—Retd., 30th Nov., 1910.*

ORDE, SIR PERCY LANCELOT, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 19th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., Apl., 1908; supt., July, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1928; confd. (provl. subst.), May, 1930; confd., July, 1932; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1935; inspr.-genl. and jt. secty. to govt., Punjab, home dept., confd., Sept., 1936; King's police medal, Feb., 1937; k.c. Jan., 1941; retd., 18th Jan., 1943.

ORGILL, TYRREL CHURTON, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.G.S., late Indian Educ. Service (N.W.F.P.) (b. 7th June, 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

O'RIORDAN, KEVIN MORAN, O.S.E., late Indian Police (b. 6th March, 1887).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

ORMEROD, CHARLES MAURICE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 27th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Rugby and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Punjab, l.s.g. dept., Nov., 1930; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936; served under govt. of India, March, 1944.

ORMEROD, RICHARD CATON, B.A., Burma Office (asst. princpl.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., India Office, 11th Oct., 1938; posted to Burma Office, Jan., 1939; resdt. clerk, June, 1940, to Nov., 1941; on mil. service, Nov., 1941.

ORR, ARCHIBALD, Indian State Ry. (supt. of collieries) (b. 27th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. mining engr., 28th Apl., 1920; asst. colliery supt., June, 1929; offg. ch. mining engr., July, 1931; offg. manager, Jerangdih colliery, March, 1933; confd. as supt. collieries, June, 1935.

ORR, CECIL BRUCE, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 2nd March, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 30th Nov., 1921; offg. dist. supt., May, 1929; confd., May, 1933.

ORR, JOHN WARD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 1st Apl., 1916).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., St. Andrew's Univ., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 29th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., serving in Orissa.

ORTCHESON, JOHN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Perth Academy, Edinburgh Univ., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., March, 1936; spcl. commr., and engrg. offr., July, 1939; dist. and sess. judge (sub permit.), Aug., 1939; spcl. enq. commr., Nov. to Dec., 1941.

OSBORN, JACK DASHWOOD BRETON, Indian State Ry. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Ry.) (b. 16th June, 1902).—Asst. traffic supt., E.B. ry., Jan., 1926; dist. traffic supt., provl. permit., July, 1938.

OSMAN ALI, SHUJAT, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 1st July, 1912).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Coll., Coonoor, Madras, Univ. of Madras, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. collr., and mag.; sub. collr., Feb., 1938; empld. in ch. sec. Oct., 1939, to March, 1940; city mag. (1st cl.), Madwa, Apl., 1942; ch. presdy. mag., Sept., 1944.

OSMASTON, ARTHUR EDWARD, late Indian Forest Service (b. 4th March, 1885).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

OSMASTON, BERTRAM HUTCHINSON, Indian Forest Service (consvr., United Provs.) (b. 30th March, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., 9th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; offg. asst. forest economist, Dehra Dun, May, 1928; pers. asst. to forest economist, Apl., 1931; confd. commr., July, 1941.

OSMASTON, FITZ WALTER COMPLYON, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Orissa) (b. 3rd July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Feb., 1923; instr., forest research institute, Apl., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; served in Orissa; under govt. of India, def., Oct., 1940.

OSMASTON, GEORGE HUTCHINSON, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1898).—First commn. dated 26th May, 1916; in milty. employ to 30th June, 1921; proby. asst. supt., July, 1921; confd., Oct., 1925; supt., Aug., 1929.

OSS, HENRY WILLIAM, late Indian State Ry. (b. 16th Nov., 1879).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

O'SULLIVAN, DENNIS REIL (judge, chief ct. of Sind).Apptd., Jan., 1943.

O'SULLIVAN, HUGH PATRICK, late Indian Police (b. 26th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay 7th June, 1923; offg.

supt., March, 1926; confd., Dec., 1933; retd., Oct., 1940.

OULSNAM, SAMUEL HARRISON YARDLEY, C.I.E., M.C., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dep. secty., dept. of educn., health and lands, govt. of India) (b. 17th Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Newcastle-under-Lyme High Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1921 arrived, 2nd Dec., 1921, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1926; offg. dep. commr., March, 1928; Sept., 1932; and again, Apl., 1933; offg. reforms offcr., Jan., 1935; dep. commr., Oct., 1937; C.I.E., May, 1937; addtl. dep. sec., dept. of educn., health and lands, May, to Nov., 1938; jt. sec., supply dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1940; dep. secty., dept. of educn., health and lands, Apl., 1940; jt. sec., ditto, Apl., 1943.

OUNG, TUN HLA, Indian Police.—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 23rd May, 1927; confd., dist. supt., May, 1941.

OWDEN, JOHN SCAMBLER, late Indian Forest Service (b. 28th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 18th Dec., 1909; in mil. employ from 12th Nov., 1915, to 8th March, 1919; dep. consvr., March, 1919; offg. consvr., United Provs., June, 1929; ditto, Assam, Apl., 1930; and again, June, 1932; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1935; confd., July, 1934; retd., Jan., 1941.

OWEN, GODFREY ELWIN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 2nd Feb., 1890).—Retd., Sept., 1935.*

OWEN, LEONARD, M.A., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Bangor; apptd. after exam. of 1914; on mil. duty to March, 1919; arrived, 27th March, 1919, and posted to the United Provs.; jt. mag., Nov., 1921; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1924; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. settlement offcr., Nov., 1926; repres. of United Provs. govt. in central legisl. assembly, Aug. to Sept., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; retd., July, 1941.

OWEN, LLEWELLYN GORDON, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service, Burma (b. 5th March, 1885).—Joined the service as profr. of mathematics, 12th Feb., 1915; on mil. service from 29th Aug., 1916, to 26th Oct., 1919; retd., March, 1940.

OWEN, MORRIS, M.Sc., F.Inst.P.H., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 16th Feb., 1885).—Joined the service as profr., Victoria Coll. of Science, Nagpur, 3rd Feb., 1912; princpl., Morris coll., Nagpur, Apl., 1923, and again, Jan., 1926; princpl., Victoria Coll. of Science, Nov., 1924; offg. princpl., coll. of science, Nagpur, July, 1926; confd., May, 1928; offg. dir. of public instrn., Central Provs., Dec., 1934; confd., Apl., 1937; retd., Feb., 1940.

OWENS, JOSEPH HUBERT, C.I.E., M.B.E., late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 18th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 12th Jan., 1909; confd., Dec., 1910; on depn. to I.E.F. from Oct., 1914, to Apl., 1922; dep. p.m.g., June, 1935; p.m.g., Sept., 1938; dep. dir.-genl., postal services, Oct., 1939; senr. dep. dir.-genl., May, 1940; p.m.g., United Provs. circle, Aug., 1940; retd., Feb., 1942.

OXBURY, HAROLD FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S., Impl. Customs Service (dep. commr.) (b. 11th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at King Edward VI Sch., Norwich, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1931

ditto, local govt. dept., March, 1932; ditto, home and pol. dept., March, 1933; dep. secty., fin. dept., Burma, Dec., 1934; asst. collr., impl. customs service, May, 1935; asst. commr., Feb., 1938; secty. to finl. commr., Nov., 1938; dep. commr. (provl.), March, 1938; confd., July, 1938; collr. of customs, Rangoon, Feb., 1940.

OXLEY, HUMPHREY LESLIE MALCOLM, India Office (asst. solr., legal adviser's dept.) (b. 9th Oct., 1909).—Apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 23rd Sept., 1933; asst. solr., March, 1944.

OZANNE, ROBERT DE LA CONDOMINE, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 26th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 13th Oct., 1923; offg. dis. supt., Apl., 1926; confd., Aug., 1937.

PACKMAN, KENNETH CHALMERS, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th May, 1899).—First commn. dated 15th Apl., 1919; in milty. employ to March, 1924; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, May, 1925; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Sept., 1928; 2nd adil. ditto, June, 1930; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Oct., 1930; ditto, N. Waziristan, Apl., 1931; secty. to A.G.G., Deccan states, March, 1935; A.G.G., Deccan states, and resdt. at Kolhapur (in addn.), May, 1936; consul-genl., Kashgar, Oct., 1936; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, March, 1939; resdt. in Waziristan, May, 1941; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Nov., 1941; on depn., Nov., 1944.

PADDON, ERROL GRAHAM EVANS, B.Sc., A.C.G.I., Indian State Rlys. (dist. elec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 21st June, 1898).—Asst. elec. engr., Aug., 1921; dist. ditto, May, 1938.

PADDON, SIR STANLEY SOMERSET WREFORD, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (retd.) late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 28th March, 1881).—First commn., 15th Aug., 1900; apptd. to I.A., March, 1905; retd., Feb., 1914; re-employed as temp. staff offr., directorate of mil. operations, war office, Nov., 1914; ditto, mil. dept., India office, July, 1917; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; supt., India store depot, March, 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; dir.-genl., store dept., 3rd Oct., 1923; kt., Jan., 1932; retd., May, 1940.

PADDONROW, LINDSAY ARTHUR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Sind) (b. 18th Feb., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; confd. as supt., Feb., 1943; dep. commr., Bombay, Feb., 1943.

PADHI, RAMA KRISHNA, Indian Police, Bihar and Orissa (b. 25th Jan., 1919).—Apptd. sub. dep. collr. on probn., 11th Dec., 1939; apptd. asst. supt., police, on probn., 28th Apl., 1941; confd., Apl., 1943.

PADMANABHA AYYAR, NARAYANA, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 10th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 11th June, 1923; asst. exec. engr., July, 1924; offg. exec. engr., May, 1928; confd., July, 1933.

PADMANABHAN, DORAI SWAMIER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 15th March, 1909).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Apl., 1934; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1941.

PAGE, SIR ARTHUR, Kt. K.C., Barr. (1901),

late Judicial dept., Bengal (b. 9th March, 1876).—Retd., Apl., 1936.*

PAGE, ARTHUR JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 2nd Nov., 1882).—Retd., March, 1935.*

PAHLUMAL, KALUMAL (see Kalumal Pahlumal).

PAI, AMMEMBAL VITTAL, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (jt. sec. commonwealth relns. dept.) (b. 11th Oct., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Wadhham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1928; under-secty. to govt., p.w. and labour dept., Feb., 1931; agent of the govt. of India in Ceylon, Apl., 1936; O.B.E., June, 1939; addl. dep. secty., educn., health and lands dept., govt. of India, Feb., 1941; dep. secty., dept. of Indians overseas, Oct., 1941; contr.-genl. of emigrn. and jt. secty. commonwealth relations dept., Sept., 1944.

PAI, K. RAMA (see Rama Pai, K.).

PAI, MANGALORE PURUSHOTHAM, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 1st Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, King's Coll., London, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1919; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1930; diwan of Sivaganga, July, 1938; contr. of coffee, Dec., 1940.

PAINE, CRISPIN BERNARD CHITTY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 2nd March, 1906).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Assam, Apl., 1933; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1935; presdt. Manipur State Durbar, Mar., 1936; dep. sec. (home), Oct., 1939; sec. (educn. etc.), Feb., 1941; dep. commr. (provl.), Mar., 1942.

PAING, MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 7th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Jan., 1932, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, June, 1936; dep. commr., July, 1938; under trng., income-tax dept., June, 1940; asst. commr. of income tax, May, 1941.

PAISLEY, JOHN FREDERICK, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 18th June, 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, Oct., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1937.

PAKENHAM-WALSH, ERNEST, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 19th June, 1875).—Retd., June, 1935*

PAKENHAM-WALSH, WILFRED PAKENHAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 2nd June, 1898).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., Rangoon, March, 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1929; regr., high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; transfed. to govt. of Punjab, 1938; commr. under publ. servants enq. act, Feb., 1940; legal remembrancer to govt., Punjab, June, 1940; regr., high ct., Apl., 1941.

PAL, NIRANJAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 26th Oct., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

PALIT, ANATH NATH, O.B.E., F.R.C.S.E., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (b. 14th Oct., 1883)—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

PALMER, CADWALLADER EDWARDS, M.A., M.B., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (Assam) (b. 24th Dec., 1880)—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

PALMER, FRANCIS EDWARD CHETWYND, late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 6th May, 1887)—Joined the service, 1st July, 1911; extra asst. consvr., June, 1914; promoted to I.F.S. as dep. consvr., United Provs., Nov., 1931; ret'd., 6th May, 1942.

PALMER, REGINALD WILLIAM VAUGHAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 2nd May, 1894)—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., July, 1923.

PALMER, WILLIAM JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S., (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Apr. 1909)—Educ. at Cheltenham Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1940; dep. sec., home dept., Apr., 1940; addl. dist. mag., May, 1941; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1944.

PAMPHILON, ALFRED HART, India Office (exec. officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1890)—Served as asst. clerk, customs house, from June, 1910, to July, 1912, and as sec. div. clerk, foreign office, from July to Nov., 1912; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 11th Nov., 1912; and attd. to registry and record dept.; transf. to mil. dept., Jan., 1917; on service with Royal Navy from Nov., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. offcr., Apr., 1922; transf. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept., Feb., 1923.

PANANDIKAR, DR. SATTYASHRAYA GOPAL, M.A. (Born.), D.Sc. (Lon.), Indian Educl. Service (b. 18th July, 1893)—Joined the service as prof. of history and pol. economy, Elph. Coll., Bombay, 24th Oct., 1924; at Gujarat Coll., Ahmedabad, Nov., 1938, to June, 1939; Karnatak Coll., Dharwar, June, 1939.

PANCHAPAGESAN, TIRUVALUR RAMASWAMI SASTRIAR, M.A., Finance Dept., govt. of India (b. 4th Nov., 1887)—Joined the service as asst. prof., presdy. coll., Madras, 31st July, 1911; probnr., finance dept., Apr., 1915; asst. acctnt.-genl., Calcutta, July, 1916; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1916; ditto, Bombay, May, 1921; ditto, central revs., Oct., 1923; dep. auditor, N.W. rly., Apr., 1924; divl. audit offcr., N.W. rly., Nov., 1924; asst. dir. army audit, southern comd., March, 1925; dep. acctnt.-genl., Madras, Apr., 1927; ditto, Bombay, Feb., 1928; ditto, Delhi, Nov., 1929; ditto, central revs., July, 1930; acctnt.-genl., ditto, May, 1931; dep. acctnt.-genl. (works), Punjab, May, 1932; ditto (civil), Nov., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Nov., 1933; offg. acctnt.-genl., Burma, May, 1935; offg. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Nov., 1933; offg. acctnt.-genl., Burma, May, 1935; offg. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., June, 1935; comptr., Orissa, Apr., 1936; dir., rly. cig. accts., May, 1938; ch. audr., G.I.P. rly., and govt. exr. accts., B.B. & C.I. rly., Sept., 1939.

PANCHAPAKESA AYYAR, AIYLAM SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 26th Jan., 1899)—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Oxford Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 5th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug.,

1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; confd., March, 1935.

PANDALAI, KRISHNAN GOPINATH, M.B., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (Madras) (b. 20th May, 1885)—Retd., May, 1939.*

PANDE, BHAIRAB DATT, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 17th March, 1917)—Educ. at Government Intermediate Coll., Allahabad, Allahabad Univ., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr.; regional grain offcr., Oct., 1943.

PANDE, BHUVANESHWAR PRASHAD, RAI BAHADUR, late Bihar C.S. (Judl. Branch) (b. 1st March, 1887)—Apptd. dist. and sess. judge, Bihar, on 9th Sept., 1938; labour commr., Oct., 1939; ret'd., March, 1942.

PANDE, GAURI DATT, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 10th Aug., 1892)—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1928; suptg. engr., May, 1941; ret'd., July, 1944.

PANDE, JIVA NAND (see Jiva Nand Pande).

PANDE, PITAMBAR DUTT, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Dec., 1896)—Joined the service, 22nd March, 1921; services placed at the disposal of Finl. Commr., Rlys., Dec., 1935; on for. service under govt. of Burma, Oct., 1937; dep. ch. accts. offr., G.I.P. rly., Oct., 1938; dir., R.C.A. office (confd.), Sept., 1944.

PANDIT, ANAND DATTARAYA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 1st Apr., 1909)—Educ. at Patwardhan High Sch., Nagpur, Univs. of Nagpur and Allahabad, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provs. as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., March, 1936.

PANDIT, GOVIND NARAYAN, B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 28th March, 1906)—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 11th Aug., 1931.

PANDRANG ROW, VOMBATHKERE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 11th June, 1881)—Educ. at Govt. Coll., Mangalore, Madras Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 19th Dec., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1924; dep. sec., local self-govt. dept., Oct., 1924; collr. and dist. mag. and sec. to govt., devl. dept., Dec., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Jan., 1934; confd., July, 1934; ret'd., June, 1941.

PANDYA, ISHWARRAI NARBHERAM, late Income Tax Dept., Bombay (b. 21st Sept., 1883)—Retd., Sept., 1938.*
