PANDYA, DEBI-DAT (see Debidat, P.).

PANIKAR, ITEKOTE ACHUTA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th July, 1900)—Joined the service, 21st Feb., 1923; dep. acctnt.-genl., Burma, Sept., 1935; on for. service under the govt. of Burma, Apr., 1937; dep. acctnt.-genl., Bombay, May, 1940.

PANJABI, KEWALRAM LALCHAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Bombay) (b. 29th Dec., 1898)—Educ. at St. Patrick's Sch., Karachi, and Bombay and London Univs.; apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Surat, March, 1927; offg. regr., co-op. societies, Poona, Nov., 1931; collr. and magte., Oct.,

1936; sec. govt., Bombay (gen. and educ. dept.), Jan., 1941; jt. sec., govt. of India (food), Dec., 1943.

PANNA LAL, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab), B.Sc., LL.B., Barr., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1883).—Educ. at Agra Collegiate Sch., Allahabad, and Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 31st Dec., 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Jan., 1916; under-secty. to govt., Apl., 1917, to Aug., 1918; forest settlt. offr., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1918; magte. and collr., Oct., 1923; spl. offer. in addn., July, 1924; dep. secty. to govt., industries dept., Apl., 1927; secty. to govt., industries and educn. dept., July, 1927, and again, Feb., 1935; offg. commr., July, 1931; and again, Feb., 1933; confd., Feb., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; chief secty. to govt., United Provs., July, 1939; confd., member, bd. of rev., Nov., 1941; actg. advr. to the govt., Nov., 1939; author of reports on "Jaunsar-Bawar Forest Settlement," "Forest Settlement, Etawah District," and "Kumaum Local Customs"; C.S.I., June, 1942.

PANT, PANDIT CHINTAMANI, Rai Sahib, late Central Public Works Dept. (b. 11th May, 1885).—Joined the service, 17th July, 1908; asst. engr., Feb., 1920; transfed. to central p.w.d., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Apl., 1930; retd., May, 1940.

PANT, PARASHURAM MADHAVRAO, B.Sc., Indian Police, Bombay (b. 27th Apl., 1920).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn. after exam. of 1941.

PANT, PITAMBAR, Rai Bahadur, P.M.S., late Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 1st Oct., 1883).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

PAPWORTH, HAROLD CHARLES, O.B.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 16th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as profir. of English, presdy. coll., Madras, on 29th Jan., 1916; offg. princpl., govt. arts coll., June, 1926; offg. princpl., Victoria Coll., Palghat, Jan., 1928; ditto, Govt. Muhammad Coll., Madras, Jan., 1933; princpl., presdy. coll., Madras, Feb., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; retd., Dec., 1943.

PARANJOTI, ROBERT CHARLES, B.E., B.Sc. (Eng.), London, A.M.I.L.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th Oct., 1894).—Asst. loco. supt., Jan., 1926; dist. loco. supt., Feb., 1938.

PARANJPE, DHUNDIRAJ VISHWANATH, Rao Suhadur, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 31st Dec., 1884).—Rao Suhadur, July, 1940; retd., Dec., 1939.*

PARANJPE, GOPAL RAMCHANDRA, M.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (b. 30th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. profir. of physics, Royal Inst. of Science, Bombay, 26th Oct., 1920; offg. princpl., ditto, June, 1933; confd., May, 1939.

PARANJPYE, SIR RAGHUNATH PURUSHOTTAM, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Bom.), D.Sc. (Cal.) (b. 16th Feb., 1876).—Educ. Maratha H.S., Bombay; Fergusson Coll., Poona; St. John's Coll., Cambridge (Fell); Indian govt. scholar; princpl. and profir. of maths., Fergusson Coll., Poona, 1902-26; Fellow of the Univ. of Bombay, 1904-27; sometime vice-chancellor of the Indian Women's Univ., Poona; member, Bombay legis. council, 1913; awarded K.-i.-H. medal, 1st class, 1916; minister, Bombay govt., 1921-23, 1927; member of council of India, Apl., 1927, to Apl., 1932; member, Indian taxation inquiry ctee., 1924-25; member, reforms inquiry ctee., 1924; delegate of the govt. of India to internatl. labour conferences, Geneva, 1927-

1930; member, int. conf. on treatment of foreigners, 1929; int. conf. for formation of int. relief union, 1927; int. conf. on limitation of manufacture of narcotic drugs, 1931; kt., Jan., 1942; high commr. for India in Australia, Oct., 1944; author of lives of Gopal Krishna Gokhale and Dhondo Keshar Karve, and "The Crux of the Indian Problem."

PARASHURAM JOG, DATTATRAYA, Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 9th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1931; confd., June, 1933; exec. engr., Oct., 1940.

PARE, JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 22nd July, 1914).—Educ. at County Sec. Sch., West Bridgeford, Nottingham Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

PAREEKH, GOBIND NARAYAN (see Gobind N.P.).

PARIJA, PRANKRISHNA, O.B.E., B.Sc., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 1st Apl., 1891).—Apptd. profir. of botany, Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack, 4th Aug., 1921; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

PARIKH, HIMATLAL BAPALAL, M.I.E. (Ind.), L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Sind) (b. 24th May, 1888).—Joined the provl. service as asst. engr., 29th March, 1911; exec. engr., March, 1921; spl. road engr., Bombay, Nov., 1935; ditto, Sind, Apl., 1936; retd., 24th May, 1943.

PARISH, FREDERICK FRANK, Indian Railway Accts. Service (dep. ch. accts. offr.) (b. 5th May, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acctnt., G.I.P. Rly., 1st May, 1919; services permanently placed at the disposal of finl. commr., rlys., Oct., 1929; confd. as accts. offcr., gr. I, May, 1935; confd. as dep. ch. accts. offcr., Jan., 1938.

PARISH, JAMES EDWARD, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. engr., mechanical transport, store dept.) (b. 21st March, 1887).—Apptd. to Office of H.C. for I., July, 1921; asst. inspr. of stores, Sept., 1922; asst. engr., mechanical transport, March, 1926.

PARK, REV. WILLIAM ROBERT, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Burma) (b. 12th Jan., 1880).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

PARKER, HARDING NEVILLE, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (dep. traffic manager, E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 2nd Dec., 1912; in milty. employ. from 12th May, 1919, to 14th Aug., 1920; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1921; offg. asst. agent, E.B. rly., Jan., 1928; dep. traffic supt., Oct., 1930; offg. dep. traffic manager, comml., E.B. rly., March, 1933; ditto, transpn., July, 1935; offg. dep. agent personnel, E.B. rly., July, 1936; dep. traffic manager (provl.), Dec., 1939; ch. transpn. manager, Feb., 1942.

PARKER, HERBERT, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 17th Apl., 1889).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

PARKER, RICHARD HENRY, late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 21st July, 1898).—Retd., March, 1939.*

PARKER, RICHARD NEVILLE, F.C.H., late Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 4th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1905, as asst. consvr., dcp. consvr., Dec., 1910; offg. consvr., Dec., 1922; confd., Nov., 1926; on depn. to forest research inst., Dehra Dun,

Sept., 1924; offg. ch. consvr., May, 1933; confd., Aug., 1934; retd., Dec., 1939.

PARKES, RODERICK WALLIS, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 2nd Apl., 1909).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1935; asst. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1935; secty., British legation, Kabul, Aug., 1936; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Oct., 1938; asst. pol. agent, Chhattisgarh states, Nov., 1939; under-secty. to resdt. for states of W. India, Sept., 1940; and again, Apl., 1942; pol. agt., W. Kathiawar agency, March, 1942; pol. agent to E. Kathiawar agency, Nov., 1942; natl. war front officer for W. region, Dec., 1942; secty. to resdt., Rajputana, Nov., 1943; sec. to resdt., Baroda, Feb., 1944; secty. to resdt., Kolhapur and Deccan states agency, Apl., 1944.

PARKIN, GEORGE DEVEREUX, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 30th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; supt. of police, Benares State, Apl., 1931; supt., Nov., 1932; on depn. as dep. inspr.-genl., Rampur state, Dec., 1934, to Nov., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

PARKINSON, JOSEPH ERNEST, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 13th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service as vice-princl., C.T. Coll., Lahore, 10th March, 1913; inspr. of schools, Sept., 1922; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Apl., 1925; inspr. of training institutions, Oct., 1925; princpl., C.T. Coll., Lahore, Oct., 1926; offg. dir., public instrn., and under-secty. to govt., Punjab, July, 1933; confd., Oct., 1935; educl. commr., with the govt. of India, Nov., 1936; retd., Apl., 1938; re-empld. as educl. commr. with govt. of India, Apl., 1938, to Jan., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1938.

PARLBV, JOSHUA, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.Sc., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 3rd March, 1889).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., Nov., 1913; finl. asst., I.E.F., Apl., 1917, to Dec., 1918; O.B.E., 1919; dep. secty., high commrs. sect., Iraq, Dec., 1918; advr. and secty. to Iraqi acct.-genl., Jan., 1921; fin. secty. to high commr., Iraq, Apl., 1922; spec. duty for closing of "Accts. of Occ. Territories," July, 1922, to May, 1923; aud.-genl. and inspr.-genl. of accts., Iraq, March, 1924, to May, 1925; on dep. to London and Geneva as jt. rep. of Iraq govt., arbitration proceedings, League of Nations, Jan., 1925; secty. to finl. commn., Iraq, Apl., and May, 1925; compt. and aud.-genl., Iraq, May, 1925, to May, 1935; awarded the "Order of the Two Rivers" by H.M. the King of Iraq, 1935; confd. as cl. I officer., Apl., 1937; spec. duty in mil. acctnt.-genl.'s office, Nov., 1937; offg. mil. acctnt.-genl., Apl., 1938; dep. mil. acctnt.-genl. (senior), Sept., 1938; offg. mil. acctnt.-genl. again, Oct., 1939; promoted command contr., July, 1940; mil. acctnt.-genl., Aug., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1942.

PARPIA, YUSUF RAHIM, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; administ., Kurla municipality, Feb., 1936; services placed at disposal of govt. of Sind, March, 1939; confd. as collr. Nov., 1939; controller of import trade, Bombay,

May, 1940, to Feb., 1943; served in Sind from March, 1944.

PARR, CLIFFORD HEATLEY, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (b. 15th July, 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., United Provs., 1st Jan., 1920; apptd. impl. agriculturist, impl. agric. research inst., Delhi, Aug., 1940.

PARRY, CLAUDE FREDERICK, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 9th March, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1919; dist. supt., Oct., 1925; princpl., pol. training sch., Saugor, Feb., 1933; on depn., crown representative police, Neemuch, June, 1941; O.B.E., June, 1943.

PARSONS, SIR ALFRED ALAN LETHBRIDGE, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Oct., 1882).—Retd. from I.C.S., Nov., 1934; member of council of India, Nov., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1935; advr. to the sec. of state for India, Apl., to Nov., 1939.*

PARSONS, SIR ARTHUR EDWARD BROADBENT, K.C.I.E., C.B.E., D.S.O., Lieut.-Col. I.A. retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 5th Aug., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

PARSONS, CYRIL GORDON, M.C., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 7th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., March, 1923; confd., Apl., 1927; confd. in seln. grade, Sept., 1944.

PARSONS, FRANK THOMAS, Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1942; aide-de-camp to H.E. the govr. of Orissa.

PARSONS, RUDOLPH ELLIOT REBSCH, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 17th Jan., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Assam, 14th Oct., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; asst. to pol. officer., Sadiya frontier tract, May, 1933, sec. provl. trpt. authy., etc., Oct., 1941.

PARTAB SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1896).—Educ. at Khalsa Coll., Amritsar, and Punjab Univ.; arrived, 11th Jan., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (provl.), August, 1928; ditto (perm.), Apl., 1932.

PARTAP SINGH, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th Jan., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, on 9th Nov., 1923; transf., N.W.F.P., March, 1932.

PARTHASARATHI AYYANGAR, SRINIVASA AYYANGAR, B.A., Indian Police (b. 1st May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 1st May, 1927; offg. supt., Apl., 1931; confd., July, 1940; inspr.-genl., police, Travancore, Jan., 1944.

PARTRIDGE, EDWARD HENRY WILLIAM, late Office of the High Commr. for India (b. 22nd Apl., 1879).—Apptd. asst. engr., mechanical transport, Oct., 1924; engr., ditto, March, 1926; retd., Apl., 1944.

PASCOE, SIR EDWIN HALL, Kt., M.A., D.Sc., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 17th Feb., 1878).—Retd., June, 1932.*

PASRICHA, MUKUND LAL, C.I.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 25th July, 1879).—Retd., May, 1935.*

PATANJALI SASTRI, MANDUKULATHUR,—Apptd. judge, high court, Madras, March, 1939.

PATCH, CHARLES JAMES LODGE, M.C., Lieut.-Col., retd., late I.M.S. (b. 31st Oct., 1887).—First commn. dated 15th Dec., 1919; in milty. employ. to 5th Nov., 1922; supt., Punjab lunatic asylum, Nov., 1922; supt., Punjab mental hosp., July, 1924; retd.,

PATEL, BHAGVATPRASAD RAOJIBHAI, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 24th Aug.,

1912).—Educ. at Proprietary High Sch., Ahmedabad, Bombay Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Sind as asst. collr.; dir. of civ. supplies and ch. contr. prices, Nov., 1944.

PATEL, BHAILAL KHUSHALDAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Sind) (b. 12th Apl., 1906).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; confd. as collr., Sept., 1939; dep. sec., supply dept., May, 1943.

PATEL, BHAILAL SHANKARBHAI, B.Ag., N.D.A., N.D.D., C.D.A.D., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 29th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., June, 1921; profr. of agric., Apl., 1924; dep. dir. of agric., Jan., 1929; offg. dir., Feb., 1935; livestock expert up to Dec., 1937; princpl. and profr. of agric. coll., Poona, Nov., 1938; retd., 29th Sept., 1942.

PATEL, BHOGILAL PRABHUDAS, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Oct., 1912).—Educ. at Shree-Sayaji High Sch., Baroda, and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PATEL, HIRALAL MULJIBHAI, Indian C.S. (b. 27th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. collr., Jan. and Dec., 1932; attd. central bd. of rev., June, 1934; offg. dep. sec. to govt., Bombay, fin. dept., March, 1936; Indian trade commr., Hamburg, July, 1937; dep. Indian trade commr., Jan., 1940; dep. sec., govt. of India, dept. of supply, Sept., 1940.

PATEL, RANCHHODBHAI BHAI BABHAI, C.I.E., M.A., LL.B., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 18th Dec., 1880).—Joined the dept., 3rd Feb., 1905; asst. prothonotary, Sept., 1921; offg. prothonotary, June, 1926; confd., Oct., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; retd., Apl., 1941.

PATERSON, GEORGE FREDERICK JOSEPH, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., Lieut.-Col. (local Col.), I.A. dir., military lands and cantonments, def. dept.; (b. 26th March, 1885).—First commn., Jan., 1904; in milty. employ until Sept., 1919; joined the late cantonments mag.'s dept. (now contonments dept.), Sept., 1919; apptd. dir., military lands and cantonments, defence dept., July, 1935.

PATERSON, HUGH JAMES, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central p.w.d.) (b. 18th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., 18th Dec., 1928; confd., June, 1930; transf. to central p.w.d., Jan., 1934; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1935; confd., Dec., 1939.

PATERSON, NOEL KENNEDY, O.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Provs.) (b. 25th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, Univ. of Edinburgh, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, 25th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Central Provs., March, 1934; under-sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, May, 1937; dep. commr., Port Blair, Apl., 1938, to March, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

PATERSON, ROBERT HAND, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 21st June, 1912; asst. contr. of stores, N.W. rly., Apl., 1917; dist. contr. of stores, March, 1931; offg.

dep. contr. of stores, Aug., 1936; confd., March, 1941.

PATERSON MORGAN, JOHN ANTHONY, see Morgan, J. A. P.

PATHAK, CHAUBE SALIG RAM (see Salig Ram, P. C.).

PATHAK, VINAYANAND (see Vinayanand, P.).

PATIL, RAMRAO KRISHNA RAO, B.Sc., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 13th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Benares Univ., Nagpur Univ., and Cambridge (non-collegiate); joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; retd., Apl., 1942.

PATKAR, SIR SITARAM SUNDERRAO, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., Bombay (b. 16th May, 1873).—Retd., May, 1933.*

PATON, WILLIAM CALDER, M.C., M.B., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals and prisons, N.W.F.P.) (b. 27th Jan., 1886).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1912; resdt. medl. offcr., gen. hosp., Madras, Jan., 1924; offg. civil surg., Coonoor, July, 1924; offg. D.M.O., Sept., 1927; confd., June, 1929; profr., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1932; chief medl. offcr. and civil surg., Delhi, Jan., 1934; specialist in obstetrics with gynaecology, Madras, Oct., 1938; inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals and prisons and dir. of public health, N.W.F.P., Sept., 1939.

PATRICK, PAUL JOSEPH, C.S.I., M.A., India Office (actg. asst. under-sec. of state) (b. 6th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Rugby and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. to sec.'s office, G.P.O., Jan. to Sept., 1913; apptd. jurr. clerk, India Office, 1st Oct., 1913, and attd. to mil. dept.; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., March, 1916; tempy. asst. sec., army dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1917, to May, 1918; served with 4/11th Gurkha Rifles in Palestine, May, 1918, to Jan., 1919; resumed duty at India Office, Feb., 1919; private sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, July, 1920, to Sept., 1920; addl. pte. sec. to govr. of Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1920, to Apl., 1921; senior clerk, March, 1921; princpl., Apl., 1921; establishment offcr., Feb., 1922; delegate of govt. of India to permit. cttee. of intl. bureau of public health, Paris, 1922; admin. advr. to Indian delegation, internl. sanitary conf., Paris, 1926; sec. to Indian delegns. to 5th, 6th and 7th assemblies of League of Nations, 1924, 1925 and 1926; actg. secty., pol. (internl.) dept., Apl., 1930; jt. sec. to British delegn., Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept., Dec., 1931, 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; jt. sec., Indian states enquiry cttee., Jan.-June, 1932; asst. sec., pol. (internal) dept., Dec., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1934; actg. asst. under sec. of state, July, 1941.

PATRO, ANNEPU VASUDEVA, M.B.E., B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, 11th June, 1930; confd., June, 1932; M.B.E., June, 1944.

PATTENSON, ARTHUR ERIC TYLDEN, C.S.I., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 15th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1908; asst. traffic supt., May, 1910; dist. ditto, June, 1916; asst. transpt. supt., Nov., 1922; asst. traffic manager, Dec., 1922; dep. ditto, Oct., 1924; offg. ch. traffic manager, Dec., 1923, Oct., 1924, and again, Nov., 1926; offg. ch. publicity offcr., March, 1927; confd., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. transpn. supt., Apl., 1930; agent, July, 1932; offg. member, rly. bd., Nov., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935; C.S.I., June.

1939; manager, London rly. bureau, Dec., 1939; ret'd., June, 1941.

PATTERSON, BRIAN WALTER, Indian State Ry's. (dep. ch. contr., stores, E.B. Ry.) (b. 24th Jan., 1899).—Asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1918; dist. ditto (prov'l.), Aug., 1936; confd., July, 1938; dep. ch. contr., stores, March, 1941.

PATTERSON, DAVID CLARKE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 5th May, 1879).—Ret'd., May, 1939.*

PATTIPATE HANIMIREDDIGARI RAMA REDDI (see Rama Reddi, P. H.).

PATTE, ERNEST GODFREY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's Sch., London; open scholar, St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settl. officer; regr., ch. ct., Lower Burma, Jan., 1917; warden of the Yenang-Yaung, Minbu, and Singu oil-fields, Dec., 1917, to March, 1919; contr. of rents, city of Rangoon, Apl., 1920; dep. commr., Oct., 1923; services placed at disposal of central board of revenue as commr. of income tax, Apl., 1925; offg. sec. to govt., educn., local govt. and public health dept., Burma, Dec., 1925; regr., co-op. societies, Nov., 1926; senr. warden, oil-fields, June, 1927; offg. commr., Sept., 1933; commr. (prov'l.), Jan., 1936; ditto (perm.), Jan., 1937; finl. commr., March, 1938; ret'd., Apl., 1941.

PATTON, ARNOLD GORDON, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec., fin. and rev. dept., Assam) (b. 2nd Dec., 1892).—Educ. at City of London Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1916, and served in Assam as asst. commr. on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1925; and again, Feb., 1932; confd., Feb., 1934; sec., fin. and rev. dept., Apl., 1937; and again, Jan., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1943.

PATWARDHAN, RAMKRISHNA PURUSHOTAM, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof., Elph. coll., Bombay) (b. 8th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1921; prof. of Indian history and economics, Deccan Coll., Poona, Feb., 1921; prof., Elph. Coll., Bombay, July, 1926, Oct., 1937, and June, 1940; prof. of history, Deccan coll., Poona, June, 1932.

PAUL, CHAVUR JOHN, Rao Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 6th July, 1887).—Supt., rev. secty., Jan., 1919; asst. secty., dev. dept., July, 1921; dep. collr., Aug., 1931; sub-collr., Apl., 1936; secty. to govt., dev. dept., Madras, Apl., 1936; collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1939; ret'd., 1943, and re-empld. as dep. sec., govt. of India (home), July, 1944.

PAUL, MARCUS JAYARAJAN ROLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 17th Oct., 1910).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1936; under secty., home dept., Apl., 1940.

PAVRI, KEKI SHIAVAX, Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 4th June, 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., March, 1940.

PAW, MAUNG SAN, Indian State Ry's. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 6th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 6th March, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Ry's., Dec., 1932.

PAWSEY, CHARLES RIDLEY, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.C., B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 14th July, 1894).—Educ. at Berk-

hamsted Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; served in Assam as asst. commr. from 4th Dec., 1919; offg. dir. of land records, inspr.-genl. of registrn., etc., Assam, Sept., 1932; and again, March, 1934; dep. commr. (prov. subs.), Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; C.I.E., Dec., 1943; C.S.I., Feb., 1945.

PAYMASTER, BARJOR BEHRAMJI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 10th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Parsi High Sch., Panchgani, Univ. of Bombay, Univ. Coll., London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Sind as asst. collr.; from Dec., 1935, to Sept., 1940; served in Bombay as asst. judge, Oct., 1940, to June, 1942; served in Sind as asst. judge, July, 1942.

PAYTON, WILFRED HUGH, C.M.G., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. sec. govt. of Burma) (b. 7th Apl., 1892).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1919; asst. supt., S. Shan states, Apl., 1921; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1925; confd., June, 1930; offg. secty. to govt., educn. dept., Apl., 1928; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept.; addl. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1930; secty., ditto, March, 1932; lab. commr., Burma, Apl., 1935; secty., home dept., Apl., 1937; commr., confd., June, 1941; on spec. duty, ch. sec.'s office, 1944; C.M.G., Jan., 1945.

PE, MAUNG WIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 5th May, 1912).—Educ. at New College, Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty., fin. dept., Sept., 1940.

PEACE, GORDON, O.B.E., F.I.C., F.R.A.S., Dept. of Explosives (ch. inspr.) (b. 4th Aug., 1890).—Apptd. ch. inspr. of explosives in India, 10th Oct., 1934; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

PEACOCK, DAVID HENRY, M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., F.C.S., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 9th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as lecturer in chemistry, univ. coll., Rangoon, 24th Oct., 1922; prof. of chemistry, Rangoon Univ., Nov., 1935; ret'd., Dec., 1942.

PEARCE, CHARLES FREDERICK BYRDE, C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govr., Burma) (b. 13th June, 1892).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Aug., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under sec. to govt., June, 1923; offg. dep. sec., ditto, Dec., 1924; asst. settl. officer, March, 1925; settl. officer, July, 1927; dep. commr., Oct., 1930; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Burma, June, 1934; secty., ditto, Nov., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; apptd. representative of the govt. of Burma, applicn. cttee. for the Indo-Burma finl. award, Jan., 1936; secty. to govr. of Burma, Feb., 1939; C.B.E., June, 1941.

PEARCE, CYRIL, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Ry's. (transpn. supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1923, as asst. dist. loco. supt., E.I. rly.; asst. supt., Dec., 1929; transpn. supt. (power), Aug., 1934.

PEARCE, GEORGE ALFRED, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt.,

18th Oct., 1920; offg. supt., May, 1923; addl. supt., Dec., 1926; supt., Aug., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1938; confd. in sel. grade, July, 1944.

PEARCE, ROGER RAMSDEN, B.A., Indian, C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 28th Oct. 1914).—Educ. at Bishop's Stortford Coll., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; served in Sind from Nov., 1938.

PEARMAN, JAMES EDGAR, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 18th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 10th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., Jan., 1924; confd., June, 1926; awarded King's police medal; O.B.E., June, 1935; asst. to I.G. and supt., C.I.D., May, 1937.

PEARSON, REV. CANON CYRIL GREENWOOD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 3rd Nov., 1881).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

PEARSON, FRANCIS FENWICK, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 13th June, 1911).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1931; apptd. to pol. dept., Oct., 1935; subst. on probn. and seconded as a.d.c. to Viceroy; under trng. in United Provs., Sept., 1936; under-sec., resd. for Western India states, Sept., 1938; confd. in Indian political service, June, 1939; asst. pol. agent, Punjab hill states, Sept., 1940; pol. agent, ditto, Oct., 1941 (addnl. charge); on depn. under civil defence dept., Feb., 1942; under sec., pol. dept., Feb., 1943.

PEARSON, SIR HERBERT GRAYHURST, Barr. (b. 5th Aug., 1878).—Late legal adviser and solicitor to the sec. of state, June, 1933; retd., Nov., 1938.*

PEARSON, H. J., Madras C.S. (b. 28th June, 1892).—Held non-gazetted appointments from Dec., 1910; apptd. extra asst. commr., Feb., 1922; addl. dep. sec., fin. dept., Oct., 1935; dep. sec., fin. dept., Feb., 1937.

PEARSON, SIR RALPH SNEYD, C.I.E. F.L.S., late Indian Forest Service, Dehra Dun. —Retd., Aug., 1927.*

PECK, LEONARD JASPER, M.A., M.C., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 2nd May, 1884).—Retd., July, 1937.*

PEDDIE, KENNETH, M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 20th July, 1907; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; exec. engr., July, 1925; offg. supt., way and works, Nov., 1925; offg. dep. ch. engr., variously from Sept., 1930; confd., Aug., 1935; retd., Nov., 1940.

PEDLEY, JOHN EDWARD, C.I.E., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 4th Dec., 1891).—Educ. at Repton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. regr., high ct., Allahabad, March, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1930; confd., Jan., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1939; chairman, sugar commn., United Provs.

PEEBLES, ROBERT WILLIAM HORSBURGH, Burma C.S. (cl. 1) (asst. commr.) (b. 14th Dec., 1916).—Educ. at Glasgow Academy and St. Andrew's Univ.; apptd. on probn., 5th Oct., 1939; joined service, 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1940; served as asst. commr.

PEEK, THOMAS ARTHUR, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, general dept.) (b. 14th Aug., 1884).—Apptd. to India Office, 27th June, 1919; transfd. to office of

high commr. for India, Apl., 1922; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1924.

PEEL, EARDLEY GARFORTH BRYAN, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 12th March, 1888).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

PEEL, ROLAND TENNYSON, C.B.E., M.C., B.A., India Office (asst. sec., external dept.) (b. 9th Oct., 1892).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, correspondence dept., 28th July, 1919; war service—served in France with 6th Bn. Seaforth Highls. (T.F.), May, 1915, to Sept., 1917 (M.C., Jan., 1918); asst. princpl., Apl., 1921; resident clerk, Apl., 1921, to March, 1922; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, March, 1922; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Oct., 1923; princpl., Aug., 1924; actg. asst. sec., public and judl. dept., Nov., 1930, to Apl., 1931; and again, Sept., 1931; confd., Oct., 1937; asst. sec., political (ext.) dept. (now external dept.), Apl., 1938; C.B.E., June, 1939.

PEERS, ELWIN VICTOR AUSTIN, Barr., Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 17th March, 1921; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., June, 1928; sec., provl., transpt. authy., Nov., 1940; jt. sec. to govt., March, 1944.

PEJAVAR MOHAN RAO (see Mohan Rao, Pejavar).

PELLY, CORNELIUS JAMES, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 8th Apl., 1908).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Coll., Co. Kildare, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on for. serv. as colon. officer, Bahawalpur govt., Nov., 1937; asst. pol. agent, W. Kathiawar agency, May, 1940; pol. officer, Trucial coast, Sharjah, Apl., 1941; pol. agt and consul, Muscat, May, 1942; pol. officer, Kuwait, Oct., 1943; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

PELLY, HUTCHISON RAYMOND, Lieut.-Col., retd., late I.A. (Burma) (b. 22nd Sept., 1887).—First commn., Aug., 1908; apptd. to I.A., Nov., 1909; seconded as asst. commr., Burma, Jan., 1914; reverted to mil. employment, Dec., 1914, to Jan., 1920; asst. commr., Namtu, Feb., 1920; offg. dep. commr., March, 1923; transfd. to supply list, Jan., 1924; offg. dep. commr., July, 1926; confd., Apl., 1930; commr., confd., Oct., 1940; retd., Sept., 1942.

PEMBERTON, FREDERICK, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 7th Sept., 1890).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., 18th Dec., 1929; offg. exec. engr., March, 1931; confd., Feb., 1938.

PENMAN, DAVID, C.I.E., D.Sc., late Dept. of Mines in India (b. 9th Apl., 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

PENNELL, KENNETH EUSTACE LEE, C.I.E., B.A., M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Assam) (b. 26th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1912; in mil. employ. from 6th Dec., 1915, to 16th July, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1922; exec. engr., Dec., 1925; offg. under-secty., p.w.d., Assam, Jan., 1928; asst. ch. engr. and under-secty. to govt. (p.w.d.), Assam, Nov., 1934; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; chief engr. and secty. (p.w.d.), June, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

PENNELLS, SIDNEY GILBERT, India Office (actg. princpl., Burma Office) (b. 4th May, 1896).—Apptd. clerical officer, 20th Oct., 1921; exec. officer, May, 1927; actg. hr. ditto, May,

1938; conf., June, 1939; actg. asst. admin. officr., Apl., 1940; pte. sec., to under sec. of state, June, 1942, to Jan., 1944; actg. princpl., Jan., 1944.

PENNEY, THOMAS MAXWELL MONCRIEFF, Maj., R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 28th March, 1893).—First commn. dated 19th July, 1912; asst. supt. (proby.), 13th Oct., 1920; confd., Oct., 1922; supt., Dec., 1928; confd. as director, Aug., 1941.

PENNY, CYRIL CUNNINGHAM, Indian State Rlys. (asst. transpn. (power). G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 19th March, 1891).—Asst. transpn. supt., junr. scale, Sept., 1924; senr. scale (provl.), June, 1936; confd., Aug., 1940.

PENNY, SIR JAMES DOWNING, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (finl. commr. to govt. of Punjab) (b. 25th May, 1886).—Educ. at Marlborough Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. regr., co-operative credit societies, Punjab, March, 1914; pol. asst. and commdnt., B.M. police, July, 1916; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Punjab, Oct., 1917; on mil. duty, June, 1918, to March, 1919; supt., Simla hill states, Apl. to May, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1923; confd., Oct., 1933; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, commerce dept., June, 1926; ditto, fin. dept., Dec., 1926; confd., March, 1927; sec., fin. dept., Punjab, Nov., 1927; offg. commr., variously from June, 1932; on special duty, Punjab civil sec., Aug., 1933; chief sec. to govt., and C.I.E., May, 1937; C.S.I., June, 1939; finl. commr. (rev.), Nov., 1941; finl. commr. (dev. and sec., dev. dept., Nov., 1941; finl. commr. (confd.), Dec., 1941; K.C.I.E., June, 1943.

PEREIRA, LEO VINCENT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 28th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, Feb., 1920; asst. engr., Feb., 1922; exec. engr., Jan., 1936.

PERKIN, EMIL ATHOL OWEN, C.I.E., late Indian Police (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 25th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1909; supt., July, 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1934; deputed as inspr.-genl., Orissa, Apl., 1936; inspr.-genl., July, 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; retd., 30th Sept., 1944.

PERROTT, ARTHUR FINCH, C.I.E., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 13th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1912; asst. supt., May, 1914; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1919; supt., March, 1924; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1925; dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Dec., 1925; offg. commdnt. frontier constab., Apl., 1926; offg. dep.-inspr.-genl., Feb., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; provl. motor transp. contr., etc., May, 1943.

PERRY, EDWARD WILLIAM, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec., rev. dept., Bombay) (b. 12th June, 1891).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 9th Dec., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., genl., etc., dept. (tempy.), Oct., 1922; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept. (tempy.), June, 1923; offg. collr., Bombay, sub. dist., and Salsette dev. officr. (in addn.), May, 1924; offg. collr. and supt. of stamps, Apl., 1926; member, legisl. council, Feb., 1927; pte. sec. to govr., Apl., 1927; asst. sec., Indian statutory commission, Nov., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1930; secty., re-organisation cttee., collr., Jan., 1933;

offg. dir. of information and commr. of labour, Bombay, May, 1933; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Apl., 1934; sec. govt., Bombay, Dec., 1936; prime minister, Kolhapur state, from Oct., 1941; reconstrn. commr. and sec. reconstrn. dept., Bombay, and sec. to govt., Bombay (revenue), Nov., 1944.

PERRY, THOMAS VICTOR, B.A., Indian Eccles. Dept. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 11th Nov., 1935.

PERRY, WILLIAM ERIC, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Survey of India (b. 2nd Jan., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1935.*

PERSHAD, LADLI (see Ladli, P.).

PETCH, LADLAL BURMAN, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 16th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. commr. and settlt. officr., July, 1924; under-sec. to govt., educn., local govt. and public health dept., Nov., 1925; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; ditto, (provl. subs.), Dec., 1934; sec. to fin. commr., Burma, Nov., 1934; addl. sec., rev. dept., Burma, July, 1936; sec., com. and indus. dept., May, 1937, again, Oct., 1939; dep. commr., Nov., 1938; sec., govt. of Burma, 1944.

PETERS, CHARLES ROBERT, late Indian Police (b. 26th Sept., 1886).—Retd., May, 1939.*

PETERS, FELIX THEODORE, late India Posts and Tels. (Madras) (b. 12th Aug., 1882).—Retd., Aug., 1937.*

PETERS, GERALD HENRY, Burma C.S. (cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1917).—Educ. at Diocesan Boys' High Sch., Rangoon, and Rangoon Univ.; apptd. on probn., 26th Sept., 1939; joined service, 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1940, and served as asst. commr.

PETERSON, IVAN EMILLE KERR, late Central Excises and Salt Dept., Northern India (b. 13th Sept., 1885).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

PETRIE, SIR DAVID, Kt., C.I.E., C.V.O., C.B.E., M.A., late Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1879).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. asst. dist. supt. of police, Dec., 1900; adjutant, Samana Rifles, Oct., 1907; supt. of police and ditto, Aug., 1908; asst. to dep. inspr.-genl., C.I. dept. and rly., Apl., 1909; asst. dir., criminal intelligence, India, March, 1911; special duty, Delhi, Dec., 1912; dep. inspr.-genl. of police, May, 1915; C.I.E., June, 1915; spl. duty under dir., central intelligence, from March, 1916; O.B.E., Jan., 1918; C.B.E., Jan., 1919; M.V.O., Feb., 1921; dep. inspr.-genl., intelligence bureau, March, 1921; C.V.O., June, 1922; member of royal commn. on the superior civil services in India, 1923-24; dir., intelligence bureau, Oct., 1924; kt., June, 1929; member, public services commn., Oct., 1931; chairman, ditto, Aug., 1932.

PETRIE, HARRY, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officr., store dept.) (b. 4th Apl., 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, store dept., India Office, March, 1920; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officr., Jan., 1926; actg. higher exec. officr., Apl., 1941.

PETTIGREW, GUY IRVINE, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 2nd Nov., 1909).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1929; on special duty, Western India states agency, Oct., 1935; under trng. in Punjab, Apl., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Punjab hill states, Oct., 1937; asst. commr.,

Hazara, Dec., 1938; confd. in Indian political service, June, 1939; asst. commr., Kohat, March, 1940; addtl. asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1940; city magte., Peshawar, June, 1941; asst. commr., Charsadda, Nov., 1941; secty., Brit. legation, Kabul, May, 1943.

PETTY, JOHN, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Sind) (b. 24th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1923; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

PHARASKHANEWALA, BALKRISHNA SHAMRAO, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 10th July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

PHATAK, SHANKAR DIWAKAR, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., Central Provs. (b. 14th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936; retd., 14th Nov., 1942.

PHELIPS, DENIS CHARLES PRESCOT, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Dec., 1899).—Educ. at Gresham's Sch., Holt, Norfolk, and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. settlt. officr., Dec., 1924; warden, oilfields, May, 1926, and again, March, 1932; off. dep. commr., variously from June, 1929; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1936; confd., Jan., 1937; warden, oilfields, Jan., 1939; addl. secty., home dept., Feb., 1939; secty., defence dept., March, 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

PHELPS, JOHN MURRAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 7th Nov., 1916).—Educ. at Winchester and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PHEROZE MERWAN KHAREGAT, SIR, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (addl. sec., educn., h. and l.) (b. 29th May, 1890).—Educ. at Bombay and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 9th Dec., 1914, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1922; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, June to Nov., 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1923; confd., Apl., 1931; offg. regr., co-op. societies, Dec., 1926; secty. to govt., United Provs. dept. of industries and educn., March, 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935; labour commr., July, 1938; vice-chairman, impl. council of agric. research, Apl., 1939; kt., June, 1943; addl. sec., dept. of educn., h. and l., May, 1944.

PHILIP, CHARLES LYALL, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1881).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

PHILIP, GEORGE MORRISON, D.S.O., M.C., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th March, 1892).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

PHILIP, MODEIL MANI (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 13th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Sept., 1928; arrived, 20th Oct., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1935; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936; dep. sec., educ., etc., dept., Oct., 1938; regr., co-op. societies, Dec., 1940.

PHILIPP, CHARLES HENRY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 12th Oct., 1881).—Retd., Oct., 1936.*

PHILLIPPE, WILLIAM AUGUSTINE, Income Tax Dept., Bengal (asst. commr.) (b. 17th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 11th July, 1904; asst. commr., Aug., 1934.

PHILLIPS, ALAN ANDREW, B.A., late Indian State Ryls. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 18th Nov., 1912; dist. traffic supt., 2nd July, 1924; divl. comml. officr., N.W. Rly., Feb., 1927; offg. comml. instructor, rly. staff coll., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1929; divl. supt. (junr.), Oct., 1939; ch. comml. mgr. (prov.), Oct., 1940; retd., Aug., 1944.

PHILLIPS, ARTHUR GORDON, C.I.E., Barr., late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 20th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1906, as asst. supt.; supt., Aug., 1918; offg. dep. in spr.-genl., Sept., 1930; confd., Sept., 1933; in spr.-genl., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; retd., Oct., 1940.

PHILLIPS, JAMES ROLAND, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 1st Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., March, 1927; confd., Sept., 1933; dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, p.w.d. (irrign. and rlys.), Nov., 1933.

PHILLIPS, JOHN, India Office (exec. officr., acctnl.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 8th Feb., 1896).—Apptd. clerical officr., 18th July, 1921; exec. officr., May, 1927; actg. hr. ditto, Apl., 1940; lent to min. of shipping, June, 1940.

PHILLIPS, JOHN ALFRED STEELE, C.I.E., M.B., C.H.B., D.P.H., Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 6th Oct., 1882).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

PHILLIPS, KEITH DAVID, Indian Police (b. 5th Aug., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Dec., 1937.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM HENRY, Factory Dept., Bombay (inspr.) (b. 22nd May, 1892).—Apptd. inspr. of factories, Bombay, 15th Oct., 1923, after non-gazetted service from 12th May, 1922; offg. ch. inspr., May, 1930.

PHILPOT, HAMLET CUNNINGHAM VACHELL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 21st Oct., 1887).—Retd., Apl., 1936.*

PHIPPS, ALBERT HENRY, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 22nd Feb., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 14th Jan., 1922; offg. dist. supt., March, 1928; confd., July, 1932.

PHIPSON, EDWARD SELBY, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., B.S., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Col., late I.M.S. (b. 10th March, 1884).—First commn., dated 1st Aug., 1908; in mill. employ prior to March, 1914; reverted to mill. duty, Oct., 1914, to Apl., 1917; on depn., 1st Jan. to 28th Feb., 1922, in connection with H.R.H. Prince of Wales' visit; health officr., Simla, Oct., 1915; offg. health officr., port of Aden, July, 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; in spr.-genl., civil hosp. and prisons, Assam, Nov., 1937; retd. and re-empld.; health officr., Simla, Apl., 1941.

PHOOLKA, PURSHINDER SINGH, B.A., Indian Police, Punjab (b. 15th Apl., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn. after exam. of 1940.

PICKETT, WILLIAM DAVID, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 15th Oct., 1880).—Retd., Oct., 1935.*

PILDITCH, SIR DENYS, C.I.E., Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1912; supt., July, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; addl. dep. dir., intell. bureau, Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; dir., intell. bureau, Dec., 1939; confd. as dep. in spr.-gen., Dec., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; kt., Jan., 1944.

PILLAI, GOBIND KRISHNA, Public Works Dept., United Provs. (b. 10th Oct., 1887).—

Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. engr., July, 1922; on depn. to Allahabad municipal bd., July, 1925.

PILLAI, NARAYANA RAGHAVAN, C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A. (Mad.), M.A., L.L.B., Indian C.S. (secty., commerce dept., govt. of India) (b. 24th July, 1898).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Nov., 1922; arrived, 16th Jan., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; asst. collr. customs, Madras, Dec., 1927; dep. dir. of comml. intell., Calcutta, May, 1929; dep. sec. to govt. of India, commerce dept., March, 1932; tempy. jt. secty., ditto, June, 1934; offg. ditto, Feb., 1936; collr., Karachi, Apl., 1936; C.B.E., Feb., 1937; offcr. on special duty, govt. of India, commerce dept., Apl., 1937; dep. commr., confd., July, 1937; joint. secty., govt. of India, commerce dept., Apl., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1939; addtl. sec., commerce dept., Feb., 1941; sec., ditto, Oct., 1942; on depn. to U.K., Oct., 1944.

PILLAI, T. G. P. S. (see Sanjivi Pillai, T. G. P.).

PILLAI, VELLAYAMBALAM KRISHNA BALA-KRISHNA (see Balakrishna Pillai, V. K.).

PILLAY, THILLAINAYAKAM-SIVARAMA SETU, O.B.E., M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Apl., 1899).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1923; attd. offcr., office of auditor-genl., Feb., 1936; asst. auditor-genl. (personnel), March, 1936; 2nd secty., cent. bd. of rev., May, 1938; 1st secty. to the cent. bd. of revenue and ex-officio dep. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (cent. revenues), Oct., 1938; dep. secty., govt. of India (commerce), and secty. econ. resources bd., Oct., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1941; jt. secty., dept. of comm., govt. of India, March, 1943.

PIM, SIR ALAN WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 17th May, 1871).—Retd., July, 1930.*

PIMPUTKAR, MORESHWAG GAJANAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1919).—Educ. at Sarvajanik High Sch., Surat, Bombay Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. on probn. after seln. in Aug., 1940, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PINHEY, LOUIS ALEXANDER GORDON, O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Oct., 1901).—First commn., 14th July, 1921; in milty. employ to Nov., 1925; under-secty. to A.G.G.; and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Apl., 1929; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Aug., 1929; ditto, Sibi, Dec., 1929; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, July, 1930; vice-consul, Zahidan, Nov., 1931; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Feb., 1933; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Oct., 1934; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1934; addl. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1936; offg. pol. agent, ditto, in addn., Nov., 1936; secty. to A.G.G., resdt. and C.C., Baluchistan, Sept., 1937; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta-Pishin, June, 1938; again, Sept., 1940; sec. to A.G.G., resdt., and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Nov., 1938; wazir-i-azam, Kalat state, Oct., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

PINNELL, LEONARD GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 10th June, 1896).—Educ. at City of London Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; manager, Dacca Nawab estate, Nov., 1925; addl. dep. secty., fin., commerce and marine depts., Feb., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1930; confd., July, 1933; offg. lab.

commr., Bengal, Apl., 1934; pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, Aug., 1935; secty. to the govr., Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; supervisor, I.C.S. probrs., Nov., 1940; pte. sec. to viceroy, Feb., 1942; dir., civ. supplies, Aug., 1942; princpl., officers trng. centre, Darjeeling, Oct., 1944.

PINTO, LIGUORI SIMON, late Indus. Dept., Madras (b. 19th Nov., 1890).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

PIPE, THOMAS SYLVANUS, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th May, 1883).—Retd., Aug., 1937.*

PIR, AHSAN-UD-DIN, B.A., Indian C.S.—Name changed to AHSAN-UD-DIN (q.v.).

PITAMBAR PANT (see Pant Pitambar).

PITAMBERDAS LAVJI THACKER (see Thacker, P. L.).

PITAM CHAND AGRAWAL, RAI BAHADUR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (supg. engr., United Provs.) (b. Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1929; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1931; sec., irrign. cttee., Jan., 1939; confd. as suptg. engr., Aug., 1943; p.a. to ch. engr., Nov., 1944.

PITKEATHLY, SIR JAMES STOTT, K.C.I.E., C.M.G., C.V.O., C.B.E., D.S.O., Indian Stores Dept. (chief contr. of stores) (b. 10th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909, as electrical inspr.; electrical engr., Apl., 1911; C.V.O., Dec., 1911; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1918; asst. dir. of works, electrical and mechanical sections, Mesopotamia, from Aug., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; chief contr. of stores, Indian Stores Dept., 1st Jan., 1922; on foreign service under Ceylon govt., June, 1928; C.M.G., Jan., 1930; kt., June, 1933; on spl. duty under govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Nov., 1936, to Jan., 1937; offcr. on special duty, defence co-ordn. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1939; K.C.I.E., June, 1939; dep. dir.-genl. of supply, govt. of India, Aug., 1939.

PITT, CHARLES HARRY, M.C., M.Sc., late Northern India Salt Revenue Dept. (b. 11th March, 1893).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

PLANT, JOHN FREDERICK, LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 30th May, 1918).—Educ. at Hanley High Sch., and Birmingham Univ.; apptd. on probn. after seln. in June, 1941, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PLATT, ARTHUR JAMES, Indian C.S. (pte. sec. to govr., Madras) (b. 13th Aug., 1908).—Educ. at Liverpool Collegiate Sch., and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., March, 1934; under sec., rev. dept., March, 1940; dep. ditto, May, 1942; pte. sec. to govr., Dec., 1942.

PLATT, SYDNEY ERNEST, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Public Health Circle (engrg. bch.), Bihar (b. 17th Aug., 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.*

PLATTS, MATHEW GEORGE, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., B.Sc., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 17th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. eng., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; suptg. engr. in electricity dept., Oct., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; suptg. engr., May, 1939; ch. engr., elect. dept., Nov., 1939; retd., Dec., 1941.

PLAYNE, HAROLD FRANCIS, B.A., V.D., late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 14th Jan., 1885).—Retd., 18th Dec., 1939.*

PLEW, SIDNEY FERDINAND, Indian Police (b. 8th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United

Prov., on probn., Nov., 1936, confd., Apl., 1939.

PLOWDEN, CHARLES TERENCE CHICHELE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Political Service (b. 6th Feb., 1883).—Ret'd., Feb., 1938.*

PLOWDEN, PHILIP PETER MEREDYTH CHICHELE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Eltham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 13th Dec., 1913, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Aug., 1919, to Nov., 1919; joint mag., Apl., 1921; addl. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1922; sess. and sub-judge, Feb., 1927; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1927; confd., June, 1929; offg. judl. secty. to govt., United Provs., May, 1933, to Oct., 1934.

PLOWMAN, ALBERT CHARLES, M.B.E., India Office (b. 16th June, 1883).—Educ. at Bedford Modern Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 12th June, 1901; transfid. to finl. dept., Jan., 1917; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1919; higher clerical offcr., Apl., 1922; supt., tel. and mails bch., July, 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1944; ret'd. and re-employed.

PLUMB, WALTER CYRIL, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., N.W.F.P.) (b. 14th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 26th Dec., 1915; asst. supt., June, 1919; asst. dist. offcr., Oct., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; supt., Oct., 1924; dist. offcr., frontier constabulary, Sept., 1925, to March, 1937; confd. as dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1942.

PLUMPTRE, CYRIL MACDONALD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 27th Dec., 1884).—Ret'd., March, 1939.*

POLLARD, RICHARD CHARLES, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1908).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 26th Nov., 1929; confd., Jan., 1933; on spec. duty for a.r.p., Sept., 1939; confd. supt., Nov., 1943.

POLLOCK, JOHN DOUGLAS ALEXANDER, Indian Police (b. 28th June, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provs., Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

POLLOCK, RONALD EVELYN, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 17th Apl., 1891).—Educ. at Harrow and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 11th Dec., 1915, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; ditto, and under-sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1920; sub-judge, Nov., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1924; confd., Dec., 1928; secty., legal dept., Central Provs., March, 1930; offg. addl. judl. commr., June, 1932, and again, May, 1933; conjd., Jan., 1935; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

PONT, LEON VICTOR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 7th Dec., 1883).—Ret'd., Nov., 1938.*

POPE, ALFRED HENRY GEORGE, M.B.E., India Office (actg. supt., tel. and mails bch.) (b. 24th March, 1904).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 14th Oct., 1921; actd. higher ditto, Nov., 1932; confd., Dec., 1934; M.B.E., May, 1937; actg. asst. supt., tel. and mails bch., Aug., 1943; actg. supt., ditto, and clerk of codes, July, 1944.

PORTEOUS, JOHN BRIAN, India Office (exec. offcr., acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 29th Aug., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 13th Oct., 1919; clerical offcr., Jan., 1920; exec. offcr., Dec., 1926; actg. higher ditto, March, 1940.

PORTER, ALFRED ERNEST, C.I.E., Indian

C.S. (addl. secty., govt. home dept., Bengal) (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. dir. of comml. intell. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1925; offg. under-secy., govt. of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept.; confd., March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1927; supt., census operations, Bengal, Apl., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1933; confd., June, 1933; jt. secty., agric. and indus. dept., Bengal, July, 1935; addtl. secty. to govt., home dept., Bengal, Oct., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1942; secty. to govt., Feb., 1944.

PORTER, ARTHUR WILLIAM, late Burma Frontier Service (b. 23rd Dec., 1887).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Jan., 1911; asst. supt. (B.F.S.), Dec., 1922; dep. commr., March, 1938; ret'd., May, 1943.

PORTER, REV. JOHN, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senr. chaplain) (b. 7th Apl., 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 2nd Oct., 1921; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1923; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1931.

POTTER, ARTHUR KINGSNOTE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 7th Apl., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secy., home and pol. dept., Aug., 1931; dep. secty., fin. dept., Burma, March, 1934; offg. dep. commr., June, 1934; contr. of fin. (defence), Apl., 1937; dep. commr. (confd.), March, 1938.

POULTON, HENRY MORTIMER, C.I.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Apl., 1898).—First commn., dated 18th June, 1917; joined the pol. dept., 16th May, 1922; supy. asst. commr., Bannu, March, 1924; asst. commr. ditto, Apl., 1944; asst. commr., Kohat, March, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, Dec., 1927; pol. agent, Chagai, and dep. commr., Shorarnd, Aug., 1928; pol. agent, Zhob, Nov., 1928; ditto, Kalat, June, 1929; ditto, Chagai, Aug., 1929; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Aug., 1930; pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1931; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, May, 1932; ditto, Haroati and Tonk, Oct., 1932; ditto, E. Rajputana States, Sept., 1934; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar agency, Aug., 1935; pol. agent, Bunkelkhand, Feb., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; pol. agent, Jaipur, Apl., 1942; pol. agent, E. Rajputana States (in addtn.), Aug., 1942; resdt., Gwallor and States of Rampur and Benares, Apl., 1944.

POUNDE, HERBERT RICHARD GILDER, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 31st Jan., 1905).—Apptd. exec. offcr., 10th Dec., 1935, after previous unstabd. service from 8th Aug., 1927.

POWELL, ARTHUR BARRINGTON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 24th Apl., 1918).—Educ. at Cowbridge Gr. Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 26th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

POWELL, EDWIN VALENTINE MURRAY, Indian State Rlys. (transp. offcr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 14th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 23rd July, 1920; offg. variously as dist. loco. supt., from March, 1921; transp. offcr., Apl., 1929; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Oct., 1936.

POWELL, JOHN GLYNN, late Indian Service

of Engrs. (b. 27th Sept., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

POWELL, REV. LLEWELYN MONTAGUE SAXON, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 8th Feb., 1906).—Joined the service, Feb., 1936; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1938.

POWELL, RICHARD, late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 4th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1908; in mil. employ from 19th Apl., 1915, to 4th June, 1916; asst. to inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Aug., 1918; supt., May, 1922; princpl., P.T. sch., Moradabad, Dec., 1925; asst. to inspr.-genl., Jan., 1937; dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1939; retd., 4th Apl., 1944, and re-empld. in dept. of war transport, Apl., 1944.

POWELL, WILLIAM JACKSON, C.I.E., B.A., M.D., B.Ch., Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Central Provs. (b. 17th Dec., 1881).—In mil. employ to Aug., 1920; apptd. supt., central jail, Jubbulpore, 18th Aug., 1920; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Cent. Provs., Jan., 1921; confd., Feb., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. inspr.-genl., civil hosp., Central Provs., March, 1933; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, Army Dept., Dec., 1934; hon. surg. to the King, Feb., 1937.

POWELL-PRICE, JOHN CADWAGAN, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 11th June, 1888).—Joined the service, 9th July, 1920; head master, govt. high sch., Allahabad, Dec., 1920; princpl., govt. inter. coll., Almora, Apl., 1921; offg. inspr. of schs., Apl., 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; asst. dir. of public instrn., United Provs., March, 1934; offg. dep. dir., ditto, March, 1936, and again, Oct., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937; dir. of public instruction, Apl., 1939; confd., Sept., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1941; retd., Sept., 1943.

POWER, DAVID LLOYD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 20th March, 1919).—Educ. at University Coll. Sch., Hampstead, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. after seln. in May, 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

POWER, GERVASE BUSHE, C.I.E., M.C., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 17th Aug., 1883).—Retd., Oct., 1936.*

POWER, ROBIN TURENNE, late Indian State Rlys., Burma (b. 9th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th July, 1908; dist. traffic supt., July, 1924; offg. dep. traffic manager, June, 1932; confd., Aug., 1932; offg. traffic manager, March, 1933; retd., June, 1940.

POWIS, HARRY BADAN, late Indian Police (b. 4th Nov., 1880).—Retd., Sept., 1935.*

POWIS, LEONARD JOHN PEARSON, M.I.St.E., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 9th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st June, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1922; exec. engr., June, 1924; retd., Dec., 1943.

PRABH SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, Sardar, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 6th July, 1883).—Retd., May, 1937.*

PRABHU DATTA SHASTRI, M.A., B.Sc., Ph.D. (Kiel), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th March, 1885).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 14th Oct., 1905, to 30th Nov., 1911; prof., presy. coll., Aug., 1912; vice-princl., Hooghly Coll., July, 1926; offg. prof., presy. coll., Calcutta, July, 1928; vice-princl., Rajshahi coll., Jan., 1929; offg. princpl., ditto, May, 1933; posted from Bombay presy.; retd., March, 1940.

PRADHAN, DWARKANATH RAMCHANDRA,

B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 8th June, 1906).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and Fitzwilliam House, Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Dec., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PRADHAN, SIR GOVIND BALVANT, Kt., B.A., LL.B., late member, govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, Sept., 1928.*

PRAGER, MISS MILDRED F., B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th July, 1882).—Retd., July, 1937.*

PRALL, SAMUEL REGINALD, M.D., B.C., M.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Bombay (supt., St. George's hosp.) (b. 18th Feb., 1891).—Date of first commn., 25th Feb., 1921; in milty. employ until 23rd June, 1930; civil surgn., June, 1932; offg. supt., St. George's hosp., March, 1934; confd., July, 1934.

PRANCE, BASIL CAMDEN, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Retd., May, 1935.*

PRANCE, GEOFFREY BERNARD SANDEMAN, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 28th July, 1896).—Joined the service, 29th Apl., 1919; asst. supt., May, 1921; addl. supt., Apl., 1925; supt., Aug., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1928; dist. officer, frontier constaby., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1944.

PRASAD, JAGAT (see Jagat Prasad).

PRASAD, KUNWAR JAGDISH (see Jagdish Prasad, Kunwar).

PRASAD, KANCHI (see Kanchi Prasad).

PRASAD, MAHABIR (see Mahabir Prasad).

PRASAD, NIRINJAN, GOYAL, LALA (see Nirinjan, P. G. L.).

PRASAD, RAM (see Ram Prasad).

PRASAD, RAMESHWARA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 6th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, Colombo Univ., King's Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 8th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

PRASAD, SHYAM SUNDER (see Shyam Sunder Prasad).

PRASADA, KRISHNA (see Krishna Prasada).

PRASADA, SHANKAR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 11th March, 1905).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Apl., 1935.

PRASAD, SHANTI (see Shanti Prasad).

PRASAD, SITAL (see Sital Prasad).

PRASANNA KUMAR ACHARYA, M.A., D.Litt., Ph.D., Indian Educl. Service (prof., Muir central coll., Allahabad) (b. 21st Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as prof., Muir central coll., Allahabad, 1st Dec., 1920; with Allahabad Univ. from 1st July, 1922.

PRASHAD, BAINI (see Baini Prashad).

PRASHAD, BALESHWAR, Indian Police, Bihar and Orissa (b. 1st July, 1902).—Apptd. dep. supt., Nov., 1923; prom. to I.P. as supt., July, 1939.

PRASHAD, DURGA (see Durga Prashad).

PRASHAD, KAMTA (see Kamta Prashad).

PRATAP, RAM (see Ram Pratap).

PRATT, EDWARD BROUGHTON, Indian Police (b. 22nd Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1926; offg. comdt. of police, Aden, Apl., 1933; a.d.c. to the govr., Apl., to Oct., 1936; supt.,

customs intell., W. India, Feb., 1941, to March, 1942; confd. as dist. supt., Sept., 1944.

PRATT, SIR HENRY SHELDON, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 26th Jan., 1873).—Retd., July, 1930.*

PRATT, WALTER PHILIP R., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th May, 1879).—Retd., Feb., 1932.*

PREM CHAND, PURI, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

PREM NATH, LALA late Indian Forest Service (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Apptd. asst. consvr., Punjab, 19th July, 1922; on depn. to Chambha state from 19th Apl., 1926, to Jan., 1931; dep. consvr., Jan., 1931; retd.,

PREM SWARUP, Indian Police (b. 16th Aug., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Feb., 1938.

PRESCOTT, RICHARD GORDON BATHGATE, C.M.G., O.B.E., Indian Police (dir.-inspr.-genl. of police, Burma) (b. 30th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 21st Dec., 1915; dist. supt., Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, June, 1932; dep. commr., Rangoon police, May, 1937; commr., March, 1939; confd. dir.-inspr.-genl. of police, May, 1941; O.B.E., June, 1941; C.M.G., Jan., 1943.

PRESSWELL, HENRY EDWARD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Jan., 1879).—Retd., Nov., 1935.*

PRIA DAS, TANDAN, Rai Sahib, jala., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 6th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

PRICE, ARCHIBALD MILNER, Dept. of Indus. and Labour, Govt. of India (b. 18th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1906; supt., dept. of indus. and lab., July, 1927; asst. secty., Oct., 1934; ditto, lab. dept., Nov., 1937; admin. officer., cent. p.w.d., July, 1940.

PRICE, EARDLEY EDWARD CARNAC, C.I.E., A.C.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th March 1892).—Joined the service, 11th June, 1919; asst. acctnt.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1920; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Aug., 1925; asst. acctnt.-genl., Bombay, Nov., 1928; asst. dir., comml. audit, Bombay, Nov., 1928; dep. acctnt.-genl., Bombay, May, 1932; services lent to Jodhpur Durbar, Jan., 1933; services lent to the govt. of N.W.F.P., Apl., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1942; confd. acctnt.-genl. (cl. II), Oct., 1943.

PRICE, ERNEST, M.B.E., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 14th Dec., 1879).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

PRICE, GLADYS MAUD, India Audit Office, (hr. exec. officer.) (b. 22nd May, 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer., 20th Jan., 1922; exec. officer., Oct., 1926; hr. ditto, Sept., 1938.

PRICE, MURRAY ROMEY, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 29th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 23rd May, 1924; offg. supt., May, 1929; confd., Feb., 1934; princpl., police trng. sch., Nasik, Apl., 1943, to Sept., 1944; served in Sind from Oct., 1944.

PRICE, WILLIAM ALAN BOURNE, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 28th Apl., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., Apl., 1925; confd., May, 1929.

PRICHARD, HENRY MONTAGUE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 29th May, 1887).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

PRIDEAUX, EDWIN TERREY, O.B.E., B.A.

(Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 16th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Latymer Upper Sch., Hammersmith, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1935; O.B.E., June, 1942; sec., local self govt. dept., Oct., 1943.

PRIDMORE, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Aug., 1910).—Educ. at Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1936.

PRIESTLEY, GERALD WILLIAM, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (advr. to the govr., Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at West Monmouthshire Sch., Pontypool, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; supt., Pudukkottai state, Aug. to Sept., 1915; spl. asst. settlt. officer., Nov., 1916; sub-collr. and jt. mag. and ditto, Oct., 1917; special settlt. officer., July, 1919, to March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1927; confd., Aug., 1930; commr. of Coorg, Nov., 1937; commr. of labour, Nov., 1936, to Oct., 1937; member, bd. of rev., Aug., 1939; chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1942, to Dec., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1944; advr. to the govr., July, 1944.

PRING, NOEL GRATTAN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 30th Dec., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 6th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Dec., 1926; served under def. dept., Sept., 1941.

PRINGLE, IVAN TERENCE ST. CLAIR, M.A., late Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th Dec., 1911; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1920; offg. asst. agent, E.B. rly., May, 1926; offg. dep. secty., rly. bd., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. agent, E.B. rly., Dec., 1929; offg. dep. agent (personnel), E.B. rly., Jan., 1933; confd., Apl., 1934; dep. traffic manager, July, 1939; retd., Feb., 1942.

PRINGLE, REGINALD JAMES, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 23rd March, 1910).—Educ. at Felsted Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. pte. secty. to govr., Bengal, Feb., 1936; apptd. to fin. and comm., provl., March, 1940; under-secty., dept. of commerce, govt. of India, Sept., 1940; M.B.E., Jan., 1944; sec., cent. bd. of rev., Oct., 1944.

PRIOR, SIR CHARLES GEOFFREY, K.C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Dec., 1896).—First commn. dated 14th July, 1915; joined the pol. dept., 24th Aug., 1923; under-secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Oct., 1924; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Aug., 1926; sec. to ditto, Apl., 1927; under-secty. to A.G.G., Punjab states, March, 1929; pol. agent, Bahrein, Apl., 1929; offg. dep. secty., for. and pol. dept., June, 1933; confd., May, 1934; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1934; and again, Apl., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1936; prime minister, Alwar state, Nov., 1936; pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Sept., 1939; member, Middle East war council, as repr. of govt. of India, Nov., 1941; K.C.I.E., June, 1943.

PRIOR, HENRY CARLOS, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. of India, dept. of lab.)

Bihar (b. 6th Jan., 1890).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 30th Nov., 1914; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. to army dept., Oct., 1915 (commtd., 3rd Naga Hills) Bn., Assam Rifles, to Oct., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1921; confd., Jan., 1929; manager, Bettiah wards estate, June, 1926; addl. secty. to govt., rev. dept., July, 1929; secty., ditto, Oct., 1931; secty., fin. dept., B. and O., Apl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1936; temp. addtl. secty. to govt. of India, commerce dept., Apl., 1940; secty., dept. of lab., Feb., 1941; C.S.I., June, 1943; sec., dept. of labour, June, 1944.

PRITAM SINGH, BRAR, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 17th Nov., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 7th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1928; confd., Nov., 1933.

PRITCHARD, JOSEPH WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 5th Aug., 1903).—Educ. at Newport (Salop) Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927; and served in Madras as asst. magte. and collr.; sub-collr., July, 1932; offg. mag. and collr., variously from May, 1936; commr. of Coorg, Nov., 1939; ch. commr., July, 1940.

PROCTOR, ALFRED HENRY, D.S.O., M.D. Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 21st Jan., 1879).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

PROCTOR, ERNEST, M.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 3rd July, 1894).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1920, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Burma rlys., July, 1933.

PROMODE KUMAR DEY (see Dey, P. K.).
PROPHULLA CHANDRA KANJILAL, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 2nd March, 1886).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1911; dep. consvr., Oct., 1923; with govt. of Assam from Sept., 1929-Oct., 1931; ret'd., 2nd March, 1941.

PROTHERO, EDWIN LYNDON, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; addl. secty. to govt., rev. dept., July, 1929; under-secty. to govt. (estab.), Punjab, Feb., 1935; on for serv., Bawalpur state, Aug., 1938; suptg. engr., perm't., July, 1941.

PROUDLOCK, ROBIN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. comm'l. offcr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th June, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 8th Nov., 1914; asst. dist. ditto, Jan., 1922; offg. dist. ditto, Oct., 1923 (lent to Q.M.Q.'s branch, Jan., 1927); divl. transpn. offcr., July, 1927; divl. comm'l. offcr., Oct., 1930; dep. ch. comm'l. manager, provl., Dec., 1940; divl. supt. (jr.), May, 1942.

PRU, U SAW HLA, B.A., B.L., Burma C.S. (excise commr., Burma) (b. 24th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., 22nd Feb., 1915; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; secty. to govt., judl. dept., Burma, Oct., 1933; excise commr., Burma, March, 1936.

PRVDE, ARTHUR WALTER, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 3rd Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, 26th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., Oct., 1932; lab. offcr., Bombay, Nov., 1935; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1939.

PUCKLE, FREDERICK HALE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (dir. genl. of information) (b. 8th June, 1889).—Educ. at Uppingham, and

King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from May, 1915, to Apl., 1919; dep. commr. (prov'l.), Oct., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1930; fin. secty. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1933; confd., Feb., 1935; employed on spl. duty in the home dept., govt. of India, May, 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; sec., home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1938; again, Oct., 1939; dir. genl. of information, Dec., 1939; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

PUDDEN, HEDLEY HERBERT CRAWFORD, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 1st Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Oct., 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

PUGH, MAURICE DOUGLAS, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 9th Nov., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 12th Dec., 1923; asst. dist. offcr., frontier constab., Nov., 1929; offg. supt., Sept., 1933.

PULLA REDDI, OBULREDDIGARI, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., excise, Madras) (b. 11th July, 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., July, 1930; under-secty. to govt., dev. dept., March, 1931; Diwan of Jeyapore estate, Jan., 1932, to Dec., 1935; dep. commr., excise, Aug., 1937; commr., corp'n. of Madras, Jan., 1940.

PULLEYNE, GEOFFREY BERNARD, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 12th May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1910; dist. supt., March, 1924; supt., rly. police, Hyderabad state, Oct., 1929.

PURANIK, WASUDEO RAMCHANDRA, B.A., LL.B. (advocate-genl., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1886).—Govt. advocate, Jan., 1936; advocate-genl., Apl., 1937; judge, Nagpur high ct., 22nd June, 1942; puisne judge, Oct., 1943.

PURDY, JOSEPH WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 29th Nov., 1912).—Joined the service as proby asst. supt. of police, Bengal, Nov., 1933.

PURI, AMAR NATH, Rai Sahib, B.A., LL.B., Commerce Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 3rd May, 1897).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1918; supt., dept. of comm., Jan., 1929; asst. secty., govt. of India, dept. of comm., Apl., 1935; offg. asst. collr. customs, Sept., 1939; confd., Jan., 1940; dep. ch. contr., imports, June, 1944.

PURI, BALDEO DAS, M.A. (b. 26th March, 1876).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1922, as prof'r. of maths., Thomson Civ. Engrg. Coll., Roorkee; confd., May, 1926.

PURI, B. D., RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 23rd June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1938.*

PURI, BALWANT SINGH, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., cent. p.w.d.) (b. 16th July, 1894).—Joined the service in Burma, 7th Oct., 1918; asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1929; services placed at disposal of central p.w.d., March, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1944.

PURI, INDRA SINGH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 17th May, 1895).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1919; dep. dir., rly. audit, Apl., 1929; offg. contr., rly. accts., March, 1931; accts. offcr., rly. clearing accts., Feb., 1933; offg. dep. dir., ditto, March, 1933; accts. offcr., N.W. rly., Nov., 1933; offg. dep. ch. accts.

offcr., N.W. rly., July, 1934, and again, June, 1937; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accounts office, Oct., 1935; dep. ch. accts. offcr., N.W. rly., June, 1937; fin. offcr. and ch. accts. offcr., E.B. rly., Nov., 1940; offg. dir. of fin., rly. bd., May, 1941; dir., ditto, May, 1943; confd. as acctnt.-genl., Aug., 1944.

PURI, NEHCHAL DAS, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Deptt., Punjab (inspr.-genl. of prisons) (b. 5th Dec., 1888).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; supt., central jail, Jan., 1924; dep. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Feb., 1927; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, March, 1932; June, 1933; and again, Feb., 1935; confd., May, 1939.

PURI, PREM CHAND (see Prem Chand Puri).

PURI, TARA CHAND, M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Apl., 1914).—Educ. at D.A.V. High Sch., Lahore, Punjab and Nagpur Univs., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., under sec., educn., etc., dept., Aug., 1942; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1944.

PURI, YOGENDRA KRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 25th July, 1916).—Educ. at D.A.V. High Sch., Lahore, Punjab Univ., Univ. Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 7th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Assam as asst. commr. under sec. (home), June, 1943.

PURSELL, RICHARD STANLEY, C.I.E., O.B.E., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 5th July, 1882).—Retd., May, 1938.*

PUTTICK, HAROLD WALTER, M.I.M.E., Indian State Ryis. (ch. elect. engr., N.W. Ry.) (b. 17th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. elect. engr., 4th Oct., 1921; offg. dist. elect. engr., Oct., 1928; confd., Nov., 1936; ch. elect. engr., N.W. ry., June, 1940.

PYNE, WILLIAM HENRY JOHN, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 30th June, 1882).—Retd., May, 1939.*
