PYPER, JOHN CLARK, O.B.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (b. 18th March, 1889).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1921; in milty. employ. to Oct., 1928; agency surg., Gilgit, 19th Oct., 1928; resdy. surg., Kashmir, May, 1933; medi. offcr., Zabul, Nov., 1934; civil surg., Quetta, Nov., 1937; retd. and re-empld.

PYU, MAUNG KHIN MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 19th March, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Rangoon and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1938; provl. dep. commr., Apl., 1941; confd., June, 1941.

QADIR, ABDUL (see Abdul Qadir).

QAIYUM, A. (see Abdul Qaiyum).

QAMAR UL ISLAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 18th Jan., 1919).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1942; served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.

QARNI, ABSAR HUSAIN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 31st March, 1917).—Educ. at Aligarh Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

QUAYLE, THOMAS, C.I.E., D.Litt., M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (sec., educn. dept.) (b. 13th Sept., 1884).—Apptd. jt. sec. for Indian students, July, 1921; joint sec., educn.

dept., Apl., 1925; asst. ditto, Aug., 1926; designation changed to secty., ditto, Oct., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1936.

QUILTER, CHARLES FREDERICK GRANT, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 13th Dec., 1883).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

QUIN, THOMAS MICHAEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Sept., 1911).—Educ. at Stonyhurst, New Coll., Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

QUINLAN, PATRICK B. GOT, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (chairman, public service commission, Burma) (b. 12th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as princpl. of govt. high and normal sch., Moulmein, 16th Feb., 1915; on mil. service from 1st Sept., 1916, to 2nd March, 1919; inspr. of schs., Aug., 1919; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; dir., ditto, Feb., 1935; chairman, public service commission, Apl., 1939.

QUINN-YOUNG, FREDERICK CHARLES STEPHEN (dist. supt.), Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Central Provs., 27th Nov., 1922; offg. dist. supt., June, 1925, Feb., 1926, and again, Oct., 1932; confd., Aug., 1937.

QUINTON, HAROLD, late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 10th Nov., 1890).—Retd., July, 1935.*

QURAISHI, ABUL HASAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st July, 1908).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Ballia, Queen's Intermediate Coll., Benares, Allahabad Univ., and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Oct., in Bengal, as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and sub-divl. offcr., July, 1935; spec. offcr., fin. dep., Nov., 1937; mag. and collr., Jan., 1942; dep. sec., commerce, etc., dept., central govt., Oct., 1944.

QURAISHI, MANZOOR ALAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag.).—Apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in United Provs. as asst. mag.

QURBAN ALI KHAN, Mir, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 8th Nov., 1921; asst. supt., Nov., 1923; supt., July, 1932; dep. inspr.-genl., in addn., May, 1935; served under govt. of India, def. dept., as d.i.g. spec. staff, June, 1941.

QURESHI, SALAH-UD-DIN, Indian Police (North West Frontier Prov.) (b. 15th Oct., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Feb., 1940.

RAB, SAIYID MUJIBUR (see Saiyid Mujibur, R.).

RACHHPAL SINGH, Rai Bahadur THAKUR, late United Provs. C.S. (b. 20th Sept., 1880).—Joined the service as offg. munisf., 1st Dec., 1905; confd., Nov., 1907; sub-judge, Jan., 1917; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1930; confd., Apl., 1931; acting judge, high ct., Allahabad, Nov., 1932; actg. addl. ditto, Feb., 1933; and again, July, 1933; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, Jan., 1934; judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1934; retd., Sept., 1940.

RACHPAL SINGH, late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 9th June, 1887).—Apptd. asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 26th July, 1922; offg. dist. supt., May, 1924; confd., Jan., 1936; retd., June, 1942.

RADFORD, JOHN EDWARD DICEY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 12th Feb., 1916).—Educ. at Winchester and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1928; confd., 12th Sept., 1939; arrived

in India, Oct., 1939, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

RADFORD, RONALD WALTER, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 28th Feb., 1916).—Educ. at High Sch. for Boys, Southend-on-Sea, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., under sec., fin. dept., Oct., 1943.

RADHA KISHEN, RAI BAHADUR LALA, M.A., late Punjab C.S. (b. July, 1878).—Retd., July, 1933.*

RADHA KRISHNA AIYAR, TARURAI SESHUIYAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th Oct., 1907; asst. acntnt.-genl., posts and tels., Aug., 1925; ditto, United Provs., Oct., 1925; currency offer., Cawnpore, June, 1926; dep. acntnt.-genl., Madras, Feb., 1930; ditto, posts and tels., Madras, May, 1930; govt. examr. of accts., M. and S.M. and S.I. rlys., May, 1936; dep. ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Jan., 1937; dep. acntnt.-genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, Jan., 1937; Rao Bahadur, Jan., 1938; retd., Feb., 1940.

RADHA KRISHANA GUPTA, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 18th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as apprenteice engr., Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933; under-sec. (genl.), p.w.d., irrig., Aug., 1944.

RADHAKRISHNAN, KOMARALINGAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at the P.S. High Sch., Mylapore, Madras, the Madras Univ., the Sch. of Economics, London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Central Provs., Jan., 1937; under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1940, to July, 1941; admin., Baster state, Jan., 1942, to Sept., 1942; sub-judge under trng., Dec., 1943.

RADICE, PETER WOLFE, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1908).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1933; dep. commr., Port Blair, Sept., 1941.

RAE, BERTRAM LANGFORD DENIS, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 28th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 12th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., May, 1933; confd., Feb., 1939.

RAE, CECIL JAMES, M.Eng. B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Bombay, Feb., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931; retd., Dec., 1940.

RAFI, MIAN MUHAMMAD, B.A. (Oxon.), Barr. (secty., legislative assembly dept.) (b. 1st Sept., 1889).—Apptd. dep. secty., legis. assembly dept., 1st Aug., 1931; secty., ditto, Sept., 1933.

RAGHAVACHARI, NEMAN ECHAMBADI SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-secy., p.w.d., Madras) (b. 10th June, 1912).—Educ. at Town High Sch., Guntur, Andhra Univ., Madras Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 17th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr. sub. collr.

and jt. mag., July, 1938; addtl. under-sec., public dept., Jan., 1942; under-sec., ditto, May, 1942; under-sec., p.w.d., Oct., 1942.

RAGHAVAN, TANDALAM NARASIMHACHARI SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 1st Oct., 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Nov., 1925, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Aug., 1927; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, July, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; secty. to commr. of excise, Madras, July, 1936; dep. secty. to govt. fin. dept., Madras, May, 1938; agent to the govt. of India in Ceylon, Sept., 1940, to June, 1943; sec., bd. of rev., Dec., 1943.

RAGHAVENDRA RAO, PATTADAKAL VENKANNA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 19th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Maharaja's High Sch., Mysore, Univs. of Mysore and Calcutta, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

RAGHUNATH RAI, HANDA, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 9th Nov., 1899).—Joined the service, 11th Oct., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. under-sec., local self govt. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1933; under-sec (constrn. and gen.) and dir., central designs, Punjab, May, 1943.

RAGHUNATHA RAO, S., B.A., Barr., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 10th May, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928.

RAHIM, JALLALUDDIN ABDUR, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. of Customs, Calcutta) (b. 27th July, 1906).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge, and Munich Univ.; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., March, 1935; asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Nov., 1936; dep. sec. (supply), govt. of India, Apl., 1944.

RAHIM, KHWAJA ABDUR, B.A., Indian C.S.; name changed to Abdur Rahim (q.v.).

RAHIM, MUHAMMAD INAMUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Morris Coll., Nagpur, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. by nomination in India; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., variously from March, 1927; under-sec., govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., June, 1927, to Oct., 1930; dep. commr. (confd.), March, 1937; sec. to govt. (comm. and indus.), Apl., 1941; commr. of settlts., May, 1942.

RAHIMTULLA, SIR IBRAHIM, G.B.E., K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Member of govt.'s exec. council, Bombay, from 1st March, 1918, to 17th March, 1923.*

RAHIMUDDIN AHMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Police (Assam) (b. 22nd Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

RAHMAN, SIR Q. F., B.A. (Oxon.), LL.D. (member, federal pub. services commission).—Profr. of history, M.A.O. Coll., Aligarh, and later Muslim Univ., Aligarh, 1914-1921; Provost, Muslim Hall, Univ. of Dacca, 1921-27; secty., Aligarh Univ. enquiry cttee., 1927; member, Punjab Univ. enquiry cttee., 1932-1933; vice-chancellor, Univ. of Dacca, and chairman, bd. of inter. and secondary educn.,

Dacca, 1934-1936; member, federal public service commission, 1937; kt., Jan., 1942.

RAHMAN, ABDUR (see Abdur Rahman).

RAHMAN, ABUL FAEZ MUJIBOR (see Abul Faez Mujibor Rahman).

RAHMAN, AZIZUR (see Azizur Rahman).

RAHMAN, MOWDUDUR (see Mowdudur Rahman).

RAHMAN, MUHAMMAD ABDUR, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 22nd Oct., 1881).—Ret'd., Oct., 1936.*

RAHMAN, MUHAMMAD EHSANUR (see Muhammad E. R.).

RAHMAN, SHAIKH ABDUR, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 4th June, 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Apl., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935; ditto, sub perm., Dec., 1937.

RAHMATULLAH, SHAHABUDDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 9th June, 1913).—Educ. at Arrah Zillah Sch., Arrah, and Patna Univ.; joined the service, 1st Dec., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1939; addl. dist. mag., June, 1943; mag. and collr., Apl., 1944.

RAI, AJILIDAY NARAYANA (see Narayana Rai, A.).

RAI, C. B. (see Roy, C. B.).

RAI, GANPAT, LALA (see Ganpat Rai, L.).

RAI, HAKIM (see Hakim Rai).

RAI, HAKUMAT (see Hakumat R.).

RAI, INDARJIT (see Indarjit Rai).

RAI, LAKHPAT (see Lakhpai Rai).

RAI, MANGAT (see Mangat Rai).

RAI, NAUBAT (see Naubat Rai).

RAINY, SIR GEORGE, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 11th Feb., 1875).—Ret'd., Feb., 1935.*

RAISMAN, SIR ABRAHAM JEREMY, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 19th March, 1892).—Educ. at Leeds Central High Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 5th Dec., 1916, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., finl. dept., Oct., 1920; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, Oct., 1922; ditto, Calcutta, June, 1928; commr. of inc. tax, Punjab and N.W.F.P., Feb., 1928, and again, Sept., 1929; on spl. duty, central bd. of rev., Aug., 1929; tempy. dep. secty., govt. of India, commerce dept., Feb., 1931; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1931; confd., March, 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. member, central bd. of rev., June, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; addl. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (tempy.), June, 1936; offg. secty., ditto, Apl., 1937; director, Reserve Bank of India, Oct., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Apl., 1939; to Apl., 1945; kt., June, 1939, K.C.S.I., June, 1941.

RAJAMANNAR, P. V., Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 10th May, 1901).—Apptd. advocate-genl., 10th July, 1944.

RAJ NARAYAN BRAHMAWAR, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; divl. forest offcr., Apl., 1926; dep. consvr., Oct., 1931.

RAJ NARAYAN SINGH, M.B.E., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Jan., 1899).—Joined the

service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., May, 1926; M.B.E., Jan., 1941.

RAJARAM, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E. (b. 28th June, 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1919; tempy. asst. engr., P.H. dept., 1st Apl., 1919, to Oct., 1922; apptd. profcr. of civ. engrg., Thomason C.E. Coll., Roorkee, Oct., 1922; confd., May, 1926; on for. serv. under Ind. research fund assen., as malaria engr., from July, 1934, to July, 1937; ret'd., May, 1939.

RAJADHAKSHA, ANANT GANESH, Indian Police (b. 2nd Feb., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Feb., 1939.

RAJADHYAKSHA, GANPAT SAKHARAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 9th Sept., 1896).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1920; arrived, 18th Jan., 1921, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bombay, Aug., 1921; doing duty as asst. judge, Apl., 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1926; offg. dep. secty. to govt., legal dept., Jan., 1928; confd., May, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., legal dept., Bombay, July, 1935, and again, Apl., 1936; confd. in seln. grade, Dec., 1942; addtl. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1944; judge, high ct., Aug., 1944.

RAJAGOPAL, POORAEFTI NADAMUNI, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 19th Apl., 1896).—Joined the service, 2nd March, 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Madras, Jan., 1922; currency officer, Madras, May, 1924; examr., local fund accts., Punjab, June, 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Apl., 1931; currency officer, Bombay, Sept., 1931; asst. dir., army audit, northern cmd., Jan., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nagpur, Apl., 1935, and again, March, 1937; offg. dep. dir.-genl., fin., posts and tels., June, 1936; attd. offcr. of course, Orissa, Nov., 1937; contr., Orissa, Jan., 1938; govt. examr. accts., M. & S.M. and S.I. rly., Apl., 1940; ch. auditor, G.I.P. rly., and govt. examr., B.B. & C.I. rly., Apl., 1941; govt. examr., M. & S.M. rly., Apl., 1944.

RAJAGOPAL AIYAR, CHIDAMBARA AIYAR, O.B.E., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 11th July, 1888).—Held non-gaz. and sub-gaz. appts., Aug., 1908, to July, 1920; M.B.E., 1919; apptd. as mil. acctnt., 2nd cl., July, 1920; A.M.A.G., July, 1922; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Apl., 1925; revtd. to mil. accts. dept., Aug., 1928; promoted cl. I, Aug., 1941; O.B.E., June, 1943.

RAJAGOPALA RAO, KARADIBAVI LAKSHMAN, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Oct., 1897).—Joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; curr. offcr., Madras, Aug., 1933; serv. placed at disposal of Res. Bank of India, Apl., 1935; examr., loc. fund accts., Central Provs., Apl., 1938; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, July, 1938.

RAJAGOPALA THAMBAN, VENGANAD KOVILAGAM, Diwan Bahadur, late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 10th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 10th Oct., 1918; offg. supt., Feb., 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1936; Diwan Bahadur, Jan., 1938; ret'd.

RAJAGOPALAN, KUMMAYA MANAKADAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 26th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Christian College Sch., Georgetown, Madras, and Madras Univ.; joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

RAJAGOPALAN, PUNDI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 29th Apl., 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 15th Nov., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1933; confd., July, 1936.

RAJAGOPALAM, TANJORE KRISHNA, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 10th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1906, as prob., finance dept.; asst. contr.-genl., Delhi, Oct., 1916; dep. acct.-genl., Madras, Ap., 1920; ditto, Bombay, March, 1922; on special duty in finl. dept., govt. of India, from Nov., 1923; offg. ch. auditor, G.I.P. rly., May, 1930; ditto, and govt. examr. of accts., B.B. and C.I. rly., May, 1931; ditto, G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1932; offg. ch. accts. offcr., G.I.P. rly., Sept., 1933; dir. of rly. audit, Apl., 1934; acct.-genl., Madras, Oct., 1934; acct.-genl., Central revs., Apl., 1936; dir. of rly. audit, Apl., 1938; dep. auditor-genl. of India, Apl., 1940; retd., Oct., 1940.

RAJAMANNAR, P. V., Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 10th May, 1901).—Apptd. advocate-genl., 10th July, 1944.

RAJAN, ARUNACHALA TYAGA, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 6th Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Almalapuram High Sch., Madras Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 1st Jan., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dist. judge, July, 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922.

RAJAN, SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Jan., 1911).—Educ. at American Coll., Madura, Univ. of Madras, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1939, to Aug. 1940.

RAJAN, VISWANATHA NATA, M.Sc., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Oct., 1907).—Educ. at the Sch. of Economics and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1937; contr. of distrn., civil supplies, June, 1944.

RAJESHWAR DAYAL, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 12th Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Kayastha Pathahala Intermediate Coll., Allahabad, Allahabad Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 7th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1936.

RAJU, MANTHENA TIMMA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 5th Oct., 1911).—Educ. at P.S. High Sch., Madras, Govt. Model Secondary Sch., Benares Hindu Univ., University Coll., London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., Apl., 1938.

RAJU, VADREVVU BHADIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 28th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge (under

trng.), Nov., 1937; dep. sec., govt. of Sind, Apl., 1944.

RAJWADE, LAKSHMANRAO GANPATRAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 14th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at V.C. High Sch., Gwalior, and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; dep. sec., govt. of Sind, Apl., 1944.

RAKHA, RAM (see Ram Rakha).

RAKSHIT, JITENDRA NATH, RAI SAHIB, F.I.C., F.C.S. (b. 15th June, 1887).—Joined the service, May, 1912; held non-gaz. appts., May, 1912, to Apl., 1924; opium chemist, govt. of India, Oct., 1929; retd., 8th March, 1940.

RAM, AMOLAK (see Amolak Ram).

RAM, BASHI (see Bashi Ram).

RAM BATALIA (see Batalia, R.).

RAM CHANDRA, B.A., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th June, 1890).—Retd., Apl., 1935.*

RAM CHANDRA, C.I.E., M.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (ch. contr. of imports, Simla) (b. 1st March, 1889).—Educ. at Central Model Sch., Lahore, Punjab, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 17th Nov. 1913, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; coln. offcr., Upper Chenab canal, Nov., 1915, to Jan., 1917; M.B.E., June, 1919; temp. under-sec. to govt., Punjab, June, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), Dec., 1925; sec. to govt., transferred depts., Punjab, Jan., 1926; dep. secty. to govt. of India, educn. dept., May, 1928; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Aug., 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; offg. secty. to the govt. of India, educn. dept., Sept., 1935; secty., fin. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1936; ch. contr., of imports, Nov., 1942.

RAM CHANDRA SAHAI, KUNWAR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 19th March, 1900).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1923; apprentice engr., May, 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., July, 1927; confd., Oct., 1933.

RAM, GITA (see Gita, R.).

RAM GOPAL, C.I.E., M.Sc., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1921; dep. acct.-genl., United Provs., Oct., 1936; ch. auditor, E.B. rly., June, 1938; audit offcr., Indian Stores Dept., Oct., 1938; ch. audr., ditto, Oct., 1939; contr. of supply accts., Sept., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1944; on spec. duty, rail-road co-ordn., Oct., 1944.

RAM JAI (see Jai Ram).

RAM, KESHO, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 17th Dec., 1914).—Educ. at Ramjas High Sch., Daryaganj, Delhi, Delhi Univ., King's Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1940; dep. contr., rationing, May, 1944.

RAM KISHAN, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st May, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; divl. engr., O. and R. rly., Apl., 1925; on loan to Baroda durbar; dep. ch. engr., July, 1934; retd., May, 1939.

RAM LAL, DIWAN, B.A., Judl. Dept., Punjab (high ct. judge).—Apptd. offg. asst. legal remembrancer, 17th May, 1925; asst. legal remembrancer (conveyancing), Feb., 1930; admr. genl. and off. trustee, June, 1931; govt. advocate, Oct., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., Punjab, legisl. dept., Oct., 1936; advocate-

genl., Apl., 1937; judge, high ct., Lahore, Feb., 1938.

RAM LAL, KHANNA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 16th Jan., 1929; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; on spec. duty, Irrig. bch., Apl., 1944.

RAM LAL, LALA RAI BAHADUR, M.B.E., Dept. of Industries, Punjab (dir. of industries) (b. 18th Aug., 1888).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 2nd May, 1912; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1918; M.B.E., Jan., 1921; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1927; dir. of industries, Aug., 1930.

RAM LAL SETHI (see Sethi, R. L.).

RAM LAL, SONDHI, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. to consulting engr., p.w. bch., govt. of India) (b. 17th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Burma, 10th Feb., 1921; transferred to Punjab, July, 1929; exec. engr., Feb., 1930; asst. to consulting engr., dept. of indus. and lab., govt. of India (p.w. bch.), Jan., 1935.

RAM, MOTI (see Moti Ram).

RAM PRASAD, BURMAN, LALA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 18th Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., May, 1925; exec. engr., Apl., 1935.

RAM PRATAP, VASISHTH Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 21st Oct., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 25th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

RAM RAKHA, RAI BAHADUR LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1938.*

RAM, SADHU (see Sadhu Ram).

RAM, SALIG (see Salig Ram).

RAM, SANT (see Sant Ram).

RAM SAHGAL, BASHI (see Bashi Ram, S.).

RAM SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, late Indian State Ry. (b. 17th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th Sept., 1910; asst. engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., Apl., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr. variously from Sept., 1929; retd., Dec., 1941.

RAMA AYYAR, KOLLINGODE, RANGA IYER, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 28th Feb., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Nov., 1921; asst. dir. of audit, United Provs., Apl., 1926; dep. dir., ditto, Sept., 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, June, 1929; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1932; ditto, posts and tels., Madras, May, 1935; ditto, Bombay, Apl., 1937; dep. ch. accts. offcr., N.W. rly., June, 1938; ditto, E.B. rly., Apl., 1939; dep. ch. accts. offcr., E.I. rly., Dec., 1940, again, March, 1943.

RAMA IYER, NARAYANSWAMI IYER, Indian Police (b. 20th Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay on probn., Feb., 1939.

RAMA PAI, KRISHNA, M.A., Patents and Designs (contr.) (b. 15th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as proby. asst. examr., 3rd Sept., 1920; confd., Sept., 1922; contr., Feb., 1926.

RAMA RAU, SIR BENEGAL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 10th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 17th Nov., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Nov., 1917; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1918; offg. under-sec. to govt. development dept., July, 1920; ditto, local self-govt. dept., Sept., 1920; confd., July, 1921; temp. dep. sec. to govt., local self-govt., Oct., 1922; offg. dep. sec. to govt., finl. dept., Apl., 1926;

offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., June, 1926; confd., March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1930; on depn. to Indian Statutory Comm., 1929-30; offg. jt. secty., dept. of indus. and labour, govt. of India, Oct., 1930; jt. secty., British Indian delegation to Indian round table conf., 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; collr., Sept., 1932; secty. to British Indian delegation to jt. ctee. on Indian Constitutional Reform, May-Nov., 1933; dep. high commr. for India, Dec., 1934, to March, 1938; agent-genl. for India, S. Africa, May, 1938, to May, 1941; kt., June, 1939; chairman, Bombay port trust, Sept., 1941.

RAMA REDDI, PATTIPATE HANIMIREDDIGARI, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (Madras) (b. 15th Feb., 1888).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, 17th Jan., 1919; vice-princl., agricl. coll., Coimbatore, June, 1928; offg. princpl., ditto, Sept., 1929; secty., Indian central cotton ctee., Dec., 1932; dep. dir. agric., Madras, Sept., 1937; dir. agric., Dec., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; retd., July, 1944.

RAMABHADRAN, TIRUYALYANGUDI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 25th Aug., 1910).—Educ. at Hindu High Sch., Triplicane, Delhi Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1936.

RAMACHANDRA AYYAR, VENKATARAMA, Rao Bahadur, M.A., late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 13th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

RAMACHANDRAN, COMAL SUNDARAM, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 13th Dec., 1916).—Educ. at Madras Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1940, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.

RAMACHANDRAN, THIAGARAJA SADASIVA, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 12th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, London Sch. of Economics, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1928; offg. collr. and dist. mag., June, 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936, to Aug., 1941; on special duty, E. States Agency, Aug., 1941.

RAMADHYANI, RANGASWAMY KALKUNTE, B.S., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Mysore Univ., University Coll., London, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1934; employed under cent. govt. as provi. supt. of census ops., Dec., 1939.

RAMAKRISHNA AYYAR, PERUMALAYYAR, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 5th Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. magte., Feb., 1936, again, Aug., 1940; under-sec. to govt. development dept., Madras, July, 1939; judl. trng., June, 1941, to Dec., 1942.

RAMAKRISHNAN, CHITTUR ANANTA-KRISHNA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 9th Nov., 1910).—Educ. at P.S. High Sch., Mylapore, Univ. of Madras, University Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1934;

arrived in India, Nov., 1934; and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1937.

RAMAKRISHNA, VELGAPUDI, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 4th Apl., 1896).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 10th Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1923; offg. under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and sec. to govt., rev. dept., June, 1925; dewan to Maharaja of Jeypore, Nov., 1925; dir. of indus., Madras, Nov., 1931; offg. dep. sec., dept. of indus. and lab., govt. of India, June, 1935; commr. of labour, Madras, May, 1939.

RAMALINGAM, ALAMPADI SUBBARAYA, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 26th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; retd., March, 1944.

RAMALINGAM, PONNAMBALAM, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 1st July, 1897).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1921; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1931, and again, March, 1933; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), July, 1934; served temp. with govt. of Orissa, Apl., 1936, to Dec., 1936.

RAMAMURTI, SONTI VENKATA, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, bd. of rev., Madras) (b. 1st Aug., 1888).—Educ. at London Mission High Sch., Vizagapatam, Madras Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Apl., 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. sec. to govt. dev. dept., July, 1929; dir. of agric., Madras, Feb., 1932, to June, 1935; commr. of lab., July, 1938; member, bd. of rev., May, 1939; ch. sec., Nov., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; advr. to govr., var. from Aug., 1942, to July, 1944; served with central govt. from July, 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

RAMAN, AGNESWARA VENKATA, late Public Works Dept., Madras (b. 12th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as offg. asst. sany. engr., 22nd Jan., 1921; confd., March, 1922; dep. sany. engr., Apl., 1927; retd., March, 1944.

RAMAN, KODAGANALLUR SRINIVASA VENKATA, India C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 7th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., govt. of Orissa, home and rev. dep., March, 1937; dep. dir. genl., finance, posts and tels., and ex-officio asst. finance offcr., communications dept., Apl., 1939; offcr. on spl. duty, fin. dept., Aug., 1940; asst. finl. a lvr., mily. fin., Oct., 1940; dep., ditto, Apl., 1941.

RAMAN, KRISHNASWAMI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 28th Oct., 1911).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., govt. of Orissa, home and fin. depts., Apl., 1940, to May, 1942.

RAMANAN, COIMBATORE SUBRAHMANYA

VENKATA, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Madras (sup.).—First commn., 11th Jan., 1921; in milty. employ to 22nd June, 1931; jail supt., Madras, July, 1933.

RAMANATHAN, KALPATHI RAMA KRISHNA, Dewan Bahadur, M.A., D.Sc., Meteorological Dept. (suptg. meteorologist) (b. 28th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as temp. meteorologist, 2nd Oct., 1925; meteorologist on probn., Apl., 1926; confd., Apl., 1927; dir., Kodaikanal observatory, March, 1932; suptg. meteorologist, Oct., 1938.

RAMANATHAN, VENKATARAMA, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (dep. sec., educ. dept., Orissa) (b. 4th Sept., 1911).—Educ. at Board High Sch., Namakal, Univ. of Madras, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935; and served in B. and O., as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., finl. dept., Bihar, March, 1938; to May, 1940; dep. sec., Orissa, home, fin. dept., Apl., 1943.

RAMAPPA, UCHIL, B.A., B.L., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 6th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 24th July, 1912; sub. judge, Apl., 1929; offg. judge, city civil ct., Madras, July, 1933; confd., Oct., 1933; retd., 1940.

RAMASUNDARAM, UNJALUR SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 28th Dec., 1901).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 2nd July, 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. exec. engr., May, 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., Oct., 1934; served under central p.w.d. from June, 1941.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, RANGASWAMI IYER, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 24th Aug., 1920; dep. govt. examr. of accts., S.I. rly., Apl., 1936; dep. acctnt. genl., P. and T., Nagpur, Oct., 1937; ditto, Madras, March, 1939; dep. ch. accts. offcr., G.I.P. rly., June, 1940.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, BATLAGUNDU SITARAMA, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 18th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 26th June, 1911; asst. engr., June, 1912; exec. engr., June, 1921; suptg. engr., Jan., 1940; retd., Apl., 1944.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, SIR CHETPAT PATTABHERAMA, K.C.I.E.—Apptd. advocate-genl., Madras, Jan., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; member of govr.'s exec. council, Madras, Feb., 1923, to Apl., 1928; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; on depn. to League of Nations from 8th Aug. to 9th Oct., 1926; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; temp. member of govr.-genl.'s council, Aug., 1931; and again, June, 1933; delegate of India to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; member, govt. of India cttee. on secretariat procedure, 1935-1936; member of govr.-genl.'s exec. council, July, 1942, to Aug., 1942.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, GOPALA AYYAR, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Feb., 1881).—Retd., Feb., 1936.*

RAMASWAMI, PALAMANERI NARAYANASWAMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 17th May, 1900).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; confd., Sept., 1937.

RAMASWAMI MUDALIAR, ARCOT (see Mudaliar, Sir Ramaswami).

RAMASWAMI, VAIDYANATHIER, M.Sc. (Allbd.) Indian C.S. (dist. and sess., judge, Bihar) (b. 30th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, Univ. of Allahabad, Imperial Coll., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 29th Nov., 1929, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; actg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936; confd., Oct., 1939.

RAMCHANDANI, RANJIT SINGH V., Indian Ryis. Accts. Service (depy. chief accts. offcr.) (b. 19th June, 1888).—Held non-gaz. appts. from June, 1911; confd. as asst. contr. of stores, E.I. rly., June, 1926; confd. as asst. audit offcr., May, 1927; asst. ditto, A.G. rlys., Aug., 1927; transf. to Dehra Dun Coll., Nov., 1929; accts. offcr., gr. I (prov.), July, 1933; on special duty, rly. bd., Nov., 1937; depy. contr., rly. accts., March, 1938; depy. chief accts. offcr., E.I. rly., July, 1938; dep. contr. of rly. accts., Feb., 1939; confd. as dep. ch. accts. offr., Sept., 1939.

RAMESAM, SIR VEPA, Kt., B.A., B.L., late Madras Judicial Service (b. 27th July, 1875).—Retd., July, 1935.*

RAMESHWAR, SAHAI, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., U.P.) (b. 13th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 11th Nov., 1929; offg. dist. forest offcr., Aug., 1933; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934; transf. to Orissa, Apl., 1936; ditto, Bihar, Jan., 1939; ditto, U.P., Jan., 1940.

RAMPLING, HAROLD, late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 18th Apl., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 26th Jan., 1920; dist. supt., June, 1927; ret. d., March, 1940.

RAMSAY, NORMAN, M.B.E., Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 11th Nov., 1907).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1927; in milty. employ prior to 20th Nov., 1933; 2nd-in-command and adjt., Kolhapur Inf., June, 1933; p.a. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1933; under training in the Punjab, July, 1934; under-secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Apl., 1935; again under training in the Punjab, Jan., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Punjab hill states, Oct., 1936; under secty. to the resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; asst. pol. agent, Punjab hill states, Nov., 1938; under-secty., pol. dept., Sept., 1940; M.B.E., Jan., 1942; dep. secty. (war) pol. dept., Feb., 1943; on foreign service as Wazer-i-Azam, Kalat states, Nov., 1943.

RAMSBOTHAM, RICHARD BURY, M.B.E., M.A. (Oxon.), B.Litt. (Oxon.), F.R.Hist.S., late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 27th Apl., 1880).—Retd., Sept., 1935.*

RAMUNNI MENON, P. K., B.A., B.L., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 27th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

RAMSDEN, GEOFFREY CHARLES FRESCHVILLE, C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Apl., 1893).—Educ. at Haileybury and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Sept., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; confd., July, 1927; excise commr., and supt. of stamps, Jan., 1929; offg. commr., Feb., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1942.

RAMZAN, ALI, Khan Bahadur, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 14th Aug., 1883).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

RANA LOKE SHAMSHER JANG, late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 4th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 11th July, 1910; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1920; confd., Jan., 1930; ret. d., July, 1940.

RANA, UDAYASINHJI HARMIRSINHJI, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 3rd Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 20th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1932; confd., July, 1936; exec. offcr., R.T.A., Sept., 1944.

RANADE, DATTATRAYA DAMODAR, Rai Bahadur, late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Retd., March, 1938.*

RANCE, HUGH DUNSTAN HOLWELL, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 19th May, 1912).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., May, 1944; asst. for Mekran to pol. agt. in Kalat, Sept., 1944.

RANDHAWA, MOHINDAR SINGH, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educated at Khalsa High Sch., Huktsar, Univ. of the Punjab and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1936.

RANDLE, HERBERT NIEL, M.A. (Oxon.), D.Phil., India Office (librarian) late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 31st July, 1905, and officiated as prof. of philosophy, Queen's Coll., Benares; inspr. of schools, Apl., 1906; prof., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, Nov., 1906; ditto, Queen's Coll., Benares, Apl., 1909; ditto, Muir Central Coll., Sept., 1918; actg. prinpl., Queen's Coll., Apl., 1919; Allahabad Univ., from July, 1925; asst. librarian, India Office, Sept., 1927; librarian, Sept., 1933.

RANDOLPH, REV. ROY BEVERLEY, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 5th Jan., 1909).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 11th Jan., 1944.

RANDOLPH, REV. THOMAS BERKELEY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 15th March, 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bengal; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1934.

RANG BEHARI LAL, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 19th March, 1889).—Joined the service, 5th March, 1914; probn., fin. dept., Feb., 1915; asst. acct. genl., U.P., May, 1916; ditto, posts and tels., Aug., 195; ditto, Punjab, May, 1917; asst. acct. genl. posts and tels. (tel. branch), Apl., 1919; asst. auditor, E.B. rly., Sept., 1920; ditto, O. and R. rly., Nov., 1921; and again, Jan., 1924; asst. acct. genl., posts and tels. (postal branch), Sept., 1923; asst. auditor, N.W. rly., Oct., 1924; divl. audit offcr., N.W. rly., Nov., 1924; examr. of accts., C.I.S. coalfields rly. construction, Feb., 1926; audit offcr., Indian stores dept., Aug., 1926; dep. acct. genl., posts and tels., Nov., 1926; govt. examr., M. and S.M. rly., March, 1928; dep. auditor, G.I.P. rly., Oct., 1929; offg. ch. pay and accts. offcr., U.P., Feb., 1930; offg. dep. ch. acctg. offcr., U.P., Apl., 1931; dep. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Apl., 1932; dep. acct. genl., Burma, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Jan., 1933; offg. audit offcr., Lloyd barr. and canals constrn., Feb., 1933; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Nov., 1933; offg. dep. contr. of rly. accts., Jan., 1935, and again, Dec., 1935; ch. auditor G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1938; dep. contr. of rly. accts., July, 1938; acct. gen., Bihar, Dec., 1938; fin. adviser and ch. accts. offr. N.W. rly., Apl., 1939; acct. genl., United Provs., Aug., 1941.

RANGANATHAN, CONDRAMANICKAM RAGHUNATHA, M.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 15th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; dir. forest coll., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1938.

RANGANATHAN, MAYAVARAM KRISHNASWAMI, B.A., B.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 16th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service as appr. engr., on 10th June, 1916; asst. engr., June, 1917; asst. exec. engr., July, 1922; exec. engr., June, 1926.

RANGANATHAN, SIR SAMUEL E. (see Runganathan, Sir S. E.).

RANGANATHAN, SAURIRAJAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 12th Nov., 1908).—Educated at Madras Univ., Sch. of Oriental Studies, London, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Feb., 1935; asst. settlement offcr., Nov., 1936; collr. and dep. mag., Apl., 1939; apptd. to fin. and comm., provl., Oct., 1939; under-secty., fin. dep., Nov., 1939; dep. ditto, March, 1942; dep. dir. of inspn. customs, Nov., 1942; sec., cent. bd. of rev., Dec., 1943.

RANGANATHAN, SUBRAHMANYA, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, 1st Jan., 1918, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1919; sub-collr., Dec., 1922; emigration agent, Ceylon, Sept., 1923; offg. dep. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Apl., 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1930; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1930; ditto (provl. subs.), Dec., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; inspr., mun. councils etc., Madras, Oct., 1938; collr., Apl., 1941.

RANGASWAMI AYYANGAR, GIYARPURAM NADATHUR late Indian Agricultural Service (Madras) (b. 19th May, 1887).—Apptd. asst. economic botanist, June, 1920; millet specialist, Oct., 1924; retd., May, 1942.

RANGASWAMI AYYANGAR, S., B.A., B.L., Judl. dept., Madras (b. 21st July, 1889).—Apptd. administrator-genl. and off. trustee, 16th July, 1929.

RANGASWAMI IYER, ALAGIAMANAVARAM, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 4th March, 1881).—Retd., March, 1936.*

RANGI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 1st March, 1882).—Retd., March, 1937.*

RANGNEKAR, SIR SAJBA SHANKAR, B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 20th Dec., 1878).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

RANKIN, SIR GEORGE CLAUS, P.C., Kt., Barr., late Bengal Judicial Dept. (b. 12th Aug. 1877).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 14th Nov., 1918; Kt., Jan., 1925; chief justice, high ct., Nov., 1926; retd., Nov., 1934; P.C., 1935; member of judcl. cttee. of P.C., Oct., 1935 to 6th Oct., 1944.

RANSFORD, ALISTAIR JOHN, Col., C.I.E., late R.E. (mint master, Bombay) (b. 5th Jan., 1895).—In military employ up to Jan., 1924; dep. mint master, Feb., 1926; offg. mint master, May, 1927; again, May, 1929; confd., Sept., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1936.

RAO, BELTHANGADI GOPALAKRISHNA (see Gopalakrishna, R. B.).

RAO, CHIKBALLAPUR VENKATA SRINIVASA (see Srinivasa Rao, C.V.).

RAO, CHIRRAVOORI BALKRISHNA (see Chirra-voori, B. Rao).

RAO, DHANVADA ANANDA (see Ananda Rao D.).

RAO, EKANATHA RAJARAM (see Ekanatha Rajaram Rao).

RAO, GHANTA VENKATA (see Venkata Rao, G. RAO, KABAD APPAJI (see Appaji Rao, K.).

RAO, KASARAGOD PATANASHETTI LAKSHMANA (see Lakshmana Rao, K.P.).

RAO, KAIWAR SETHU (see Kaiwar, S.R.).

RAO, KOTY VENKATA RAMANA (see Ramana Rao, K.V.).

RAO, KRISHNASWAMI ANANDA (see Ananda Rao K.).

RAO, KRISHNA KARNAD (see Krishna Rao).

RAO, MUTTA VENKATASUBBA (see Venkata-subba Rao).

RAO, MYSORE SUBBA (see Mysore Subba Rao).

RAO, NANDALIKE DEVARAO KRISHNA (see Krishna Rao N. D.).

RAO, NEELAMANGALA KESHAVA (see Keshava Rao, N.).

RAO, PEJAVAR MOHAN (see Mohan Rao, P.).

RAO, BENEGAL SANJIVA (see Sanjiva Rao, B.).

RAO, P. H. SESHAGIRI (see Seshagiri Rao.).

RAO, S. RAGHUNATHA (see Raghunatha Rao, S.).

RAO, UDYAVAR RAMA (see Rama Rao, U.).

RAO, VALLURI KAMESWARA (see Valluri K. Rao.).

RAO, VYAKARANA NARAHARI (see Narahar Rao, V.).

RAO, V. SURYAPRAKASA (see Suryaprakasa Rao, V.).

RAPER, SIR JOHN HUGH FRANCIS, Indian State Rlys. (member, rly. bd.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 28th June, 1912; asst. traffic supt., June, 1915; dist. ditto, March, 1920; asst. transpn. supt., Nov., 1922; asst. traffic mang., Nov., 1926; offg. dep. ditto, Nov., 1926; confd., July, 1929; offg. ch. traffic mang., June, 1931; and again, Jan., 1933; confd., March, 1933; offg. agent, G.I.P., rly. May, 1936; memb., rly. bd., May, 1939; Kt. June, 1942.

RASHEED KHAN, Abdul, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 28th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Lucknow Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

RASHID, ABDUL (see Abdul, R.).

RASHID, MIAN ABDUL, Khan Bahadur, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. traffic commc. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 3rd May, 1894).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1919; provl. in senr. scale, Aug., 1937; on depn. to Gaekwar, Baroda state rly., from Apl., 1935; confd. in senior scale, Dec., 1937.

RASHID-UZ-ZAMAN, SAIYID, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 28th March, 1895).—Educ. at Patna Coll., Presidency Coll., Calcutta, and London Univ.; joined the service, 14th Dec., 1921; arrived, 19th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1925; confd., Jan., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1929; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Apl., 1933; secty., public dept., office of high commr. for India, March, 1934; actg. dep. trade commr., June, 1934; adviser to govt. of India delegation to internatl. labour conf., Geneva, June, 1935; reverted to Bihar as mag. and collr., Nov., 1937; dep. sec. to govt. of India (supplies), July, 1940; jt. sec., com-

merce dept., govt. of India, March, 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

RASTOGI, PIARE LAL, Babu (see Piare Lal Rastogi).

RATCLIFF, GEORGE MASSEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 8th Aug., 1906).—Educ. at King Edward VII Sch., Lytham, Lancs., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1933; addtl. dist. mag., Oct., 1939; addtl. dist. judge, Feb., 1940; sec. legisl. dept., Bengal, Jan., 1940.

RATHBONE, PATRICK WARRE, Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 17th Dec., 1917).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 5th Oct., 1939; joined service, 23rd Aug., 1940; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1940, and served as asst. commr.

RATNAGAR, SHAPURI NASARVANJI JAMSWEDJI, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 28th Sept., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

RATNASWAMI, M., C.I.E., Barr. (member, Madras services commn.) (b. 15th Aug., 1885).—Apptd. offg. princpl., law coll., Madras, 3rd Jan., 1928; confd., June, 1928; member, Madras services commn., Jan., 1930; offg. chairman, ditto, June, 1935.

RAU, BANTWAL GOPALAKRISHNA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 10th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Carana High Sch., Mangalore, Madras Univ., City and Guilds (Engineering) Coll., London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

RAU, SIR BENEGAL NARSINGA, C.I.E., B.A. (Camb.), late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 26th Feb., 1887).—Educ. at Canara High Sch., Mangalore, Madras and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1916; ditto, Assam, May, 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1922; offg. sec., legisl. dept., supt. and remembr., legal affairs, March, 1925; confd., Dec., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; addl. jt. secy., legisl. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1934; offg. jt. secy., ditto, Jan., 1935; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, May, 1935; on spec. duty, reforms office, govt. of India, Sept., 1935; on depn. to India Office, Oct.-Dec., 1936; knighted, June, 1938; judge, high ct., Bengal, Dec., 1938; confd., 1938; judge, high ct., Bengal, Dec., 1938; confd., Jan., 1939; retd., Feb., 1944.

RAU, B. RAMA (see Rama Rau, Bengal).

RAU, HATTIANGADI SHANKAR, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 29th Sept., 1887).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

RAU, KALLE RAMA RAU SADASHIVA (see Sadashiva Rau, K. R. R.).

RAU, P. RAGHAVENDRA (see Raghavendra, R. P.).

RAU, PENDYALA SATIANARAINA, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; served in Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 27th Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1924; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., C.P., Feb., 1936; offg. finl. secy. to govt., C.P., March, 1936 to March, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; on depn. to govt. of India (food), Dec.,

1942 to May, 1943; chairman, indus. cttee., 1944.

RAW, WILLIAM GEOFFREY, Capt. I. A., Ind. Polt. Service (b. 6th Oct., 1910).—Apptd. to I.P.S. on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., Jan., 1942; consul Bushire, Oct., 1944.

RAWLINSON, HUGH GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A., (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th May, 1880).—Retd., May, 1935.*

RAWLEY, RATAN CHAND, M.A., M.Sc., D.Sc. (Lond.), late Dept. of Industries, Punjab.—Apptd. dir. of industries, 7th Nov., 1927; adviser and secy. to the Indian delegation to the internatl. labour conf., Geneva, May-June, 1928.

RAY (see also Roy).

RAY, ANNADA SANKAR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 14th March, 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Patna and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Aug. 1936; addl. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1939.

RAY, SIR ASOKE KUMAR (see Roy, Sir A.K.).

RAY, BIMALENDU, B.A., Indian Police (supt.) Bihar, (b. 11th Oct., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt. Bihar and Orissa, 15th Apl., 1926; offg. supt., Oct., 1936; confd., Aug., 1938.

RAY, GERALD GEORGE, O.B.E., Indian Police (inspr. genl., Sind) (b. 14th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1915, as asst. supt.; on mil. duty from 20th June, 1917 to 17th Feb., 1919; offg. supt., Apl., 1922, Bombay; confd., June, 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; confd., supt. selection grade, Nov., 1942 O.B.E., June, 1943; inspr. gen. (confd.), Sind, Dec., 1943.

RAY, GORDON MEREDITH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 2nd Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Caterham Sch. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dpy. collr., Dec., 1937; supt., Bhagalpur gaol, Oct., 1942.

RAY, JAMINI PRASANNA, Rai Bahadur, M.A., Bengal C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1892).—Joined the service, Dec., 1916; dep. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1918; mag. and collr., Feb., 1937; jt. sec. agric. dept.,

RAY, JITENDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Aug., 1892).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1917; dep. supt., Apl., 1928; apptd. to I.P. as supt., June, 1934; Rai Bahadur, June, 1939.

RAY, KARUNAMAY, B.A., late Indian Police (b. 10th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1910; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Bengal, Dec., 1935; retd., Nov., 1940.

RAY, KIRAN CHANDRA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 27th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 2nd Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

RAY, KUMUD BHUSON, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 30th Jan., 1891).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 15th Jan., 1915; asst. engr., Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1925; exec. engr., July, 1937; dep. ch. engr., Jan., 1942.

RAY, LALIT KUMAR, L.M.S. (Punjab) D.P.H. (Cantab.) Public Health Dept., United Provs. (asst. dir. of public health) (b. Feb., 1887).—

Joined the service as health offcr., 1st March, 1914; asst. dir. of public health, Nov. 15th, 1934.

RAY, NAGENDRA NATH, late Bihar C.S. (b. 1st July, 1885).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1905; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1907; mag. and collr., May, 1937; retd., June, 1940.

RAY, NIKUNJA KISHORE, M.Sc., Indian Police, Bihar (b. 10th Feb., 1920).—Appptd. asst. supt., on probn., after exam. of 1941.

RAY, NIKUNJA KISHORE, Indian Police, Bihar and Orissa (b. 10th Feb., 1920); Asst. supt., on probn., 24th Feb., 1942; confd., Feb. 1944.

RAY, PHILIP BICKNELL, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 10th July, 1917).—Appptd. asst. supt., on probn., Oct., 1939.

RAY, PRABHAT CHANDRA, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs (Bengal) (b. 23rd Feb., 1888).—Joined the service, 15th Feb., 1913; asst. engr., Feb., 1914; exec. engr., June, 1926; retd., Feb., 1945.

RAY, RAJESHVAR PRASHAD, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 15th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 20th June, 1920; transferred to I.P. as supt., May, 1934.

RAY, RANAJI KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1944; addl. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1939; mag. and collr., Apl., 1941; dep. dir., distrbn., spec. offcr., rice scheme.

RAY, REGINALD EDWARD ANTHONY, C.I.E., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 15th May, 1891).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 16th Dec., 1910; confd., June, 1912; addl. supt., Nov., 1924; supt., Aug., 1925; awarded Indian police medal, 1933; dep. inspr. genl., var. from 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; commr. of police, Calcutta, Oct., 1943.

RAY, RICHARD LIONEL, V.D., M.I.M.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Feb., 1880).—Retd., Jan., 1937.

RAY, SAILENDRA NATH GUHA (see Guha Ray, S. N.).

RAY, SANAT KUMAR, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Orissa) (b. 6th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1910; asst. engr., Feb., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; suptg. engr., March, 1934; ch. engr., Orissa, March, 1940; retd., 1941.

RAY, SATISH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th May, 1888).—Appptd. inspr. of schools, Assam, 12th Nov., 1923; offg. princpl. Cotton Coll., Ganhati, variously from July, 1933; confd., May, 1939; princpl., Murarichand Coll., Sylhet, June, 1940.

RAY, SATYENDRA NATH, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 25th June, 1902).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Apl., 1925; arrived, 4th May, 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; confd., March, 1937; dep. secty., fin. dept., Bengal, Oct., 1937; mag. and collr. (confd.), Oct., 1938; import trade contr., Bengal and Orissa, May, 1940; jt. sec., commerce dept. govt. of India, March, 1943; finl. advr., civil supplies dept., Dec., 1943.

RAY, TARAK CHANDRA, late Bengal C.S. (b. 1880).—Retd., July, 1935.*

RAY CHAUDHURI, NIRMALKANTI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dy. collr., Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931; addtl. dist. judge, from June, 1937.

RAYNEAU, CYRIL ALEXANDER, Indian State Rlys. (coal supt.) (b. 8th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. coal supt., 1st May, 1916; offg. coal supt., Apl., 1928; confd., Apl., 1930.

RAYNER, ADRIEN BOOKER, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service, Oct., 1919, as asst. engr.; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr. (perm.), May, 1940.

RAYNER, WILLIAM RUFFELL, India Office (admin. offcr. for statistical questions, economic and overseas dept.) (b. 16th March, 1890).—Educ. at King Charles' Sch., Tunbridge Wells; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 25th Jan., 1909, and attached to acctnt. genl's dept.; transf. to pol. dept., March, 1912; services lent to Colonial Office from March, 1921 to Jan., 1922; transf. to judl. and public dept., Jan., 1922; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1922; dep. reading clerk to Council, Feb., 1923; asst. princpl., Aug., 1924; jt. editor, India Office list, Oct., 1932 to May, 1934; admin. officer for statistical questions, economic and overseas dept., May, 1934.

RAYNES, CHARLES HENRY, M.C., Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 10th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 19th Feb., 1921; offg. dist. supt., May, 1927; confd., May, 1933; crimes asst. to D.I.G. Rlys., and C.I.D., June, 1937.

RAYNOR, ERIC WALTER, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 20th Sept., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 12th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Sept., 1925.

RAYNOR, FRANK HENRY CHARLES, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 18th Jan., 1902).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1921; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Oct., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1941.

RAZA, AGHA GHULAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 4th July, 1917); apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Punjab as asst. commr.

RAZA ALI, SYED, SIR, Kr., C.B.E., B.A., LL.B. (b. 29th Apl., 1882).—Member, public services commn., Oct., 1926, to Nov., 1931; agent-genl. for govt. of India in S. Africa, Jan., 1935.*

RAZA, KAZIM (see Kazim Raza).

RAZA, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Raza).

RAZA, SAIYID ALAY (see Alay Raza Saiyid).

RAZA, SAIYID HASHIM (see Hashim Raza, S.).

READ, JAMES late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Winchester and Wellington Mil. Coll., India; on mil. service, May, 1916 to Apl., 1921; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1923; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Simla, March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1930; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1931; confd., Oct., 1936; retd., 10th Feb., 1940.

REAY, ROBERT WILLIAM, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 14th June, 1875).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

REBELLO, ALBIN RICHARD, O.B.E., F.S.S., F.R.E.S., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 3rd Apl., 1886).—Joined the service, 25th March, 1908;

supt., fin. dept., March, 1922; asst. secty., taxation enquiry cttee., Nov., 1925; asst. acct. genl., Punjab, March, 1927; asst. dir., army audit, May, 1927; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, March, 1933; ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Nov., 1935; on foreign service under Govt. of Burma, Apl., 1937; re-transferred to India, Oct., 1938; under Jodhpur Durbar, Nov., 1938; retd. Nov., 1941.

REDCLIFT, STANLEY WHITLOCK, M.B.E., (b. 9th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. electric adviser and electric inspr., Bengal and Assam (on probn.), 1st July, 1926; confd., July, 1928; electrical advr. and ch. electrical inspr., Bengal, Dec., 1928; M.B.E., Jan., 1941; retd., Oct., 1943.

REDDI, OBULREDDIGARI PULLA (see Pulla Reddi, O.).

REDDI, P. H. RAMA (see Rama Reddi, P. H.).

REDHA, KANTA SARAN, Income-tax Dept., Bihar and Orissa (asst. commr.) (b. 29th June, 1886).—Asst. commr., Sept., 1921.

REDMOND, LIONEL, M.I.C.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th Dec., 1878).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

REDPATH, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 4th Sept., 1909).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1929; pers. asst. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1934; under trng. in Punjab, July, 1935; under-secty. to the resdt. for C. India and dep. opium agent, Malwa Apl., 1937; confd., March, 1938; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1939; sec. to resdt., E. States, Apl., 1944.

REES, OWEN MEREDITH, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma and Bengal).—Retd. July, 1927; apptd. second addl. mag., 24 Parganas, Jan., 1933 to March, 1935.*

REES, STANLEY VERNON LLOYD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 1st May, 1907).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1926; in training to May, 1928; proby. asst. supt., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1928; offg. supt., rly. police, March, 1935; spec. duty, Shillong, Nov., 1939.

REGE, DATTATRAYA VAMAN, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 18th Sept., 1897).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and Cambridge (non-collegiate); joined the service, 14th Nov., 1921; arrived, 7th Jan., 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1925; and again, May, 1931; confd. (provl.), July, 1934; confd., May, 1936; regr. co-operative societies and dir. of industries Apl., 1939; chrmn., lab. investgn. cttee., labour dept., govt. of Ind., Jan., 1944.

REGE, SHANTARAM BALKRISHNA, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 24th Oct., 1890).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 30th March, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., March, 1925.

REHMAN, MUHAMMAD BAZLUR, M.A., Ph.D., Bombay Educl. Service (princl., Ismail Coll.) (b. 11th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as princpl., Ismail Coll., Andhari, on probn., 7th Apl., 1930; confd., Apl., 1932.

REID, ARTHUR BEATSON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 16th March, 1888).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

REID, DOUGLAS FERNIE PATON, Indian Police (b. 4th March, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 15th Oct., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1930; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., W.I. States Agency, March, 1932; supt., Sept., 1935; comdt., Aden, July, 1936; under Col. govt., Aden, Apl., 1937; principal, police training school, Nasik, June, 1939 to Apl., 1943; Indian

police medal, Jan., 1942; served in Sind from June, 1943

REID, JAMES EDWARD, Indian Police (dir. civ. def., Assam) (b. 5th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1927; confd., June, 1928; personal asst. to inspr.-genl., Sept., 1929 to Nov., 1933; dep. inspr. genl., Nov., 1930; dir. civil def. Assam, March, 1942.

REID, REV. JAMES POTTER, M.A., K.-i.H., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 8th July, 1885).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, 1st Jan., 1923; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1935; K.-i.H. medal; retd., Dec., 1940.

REID, JOHN STUART, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 30th Aug., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1927; offg. exec. engr., May, 1931; confd., Oct., 1936; dep. dir., cent. irrign. research station, Dacca, Dec., 1941 to Jan., 1944.

REID, SIR ROBERT NEIL, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th July, 1883).—Educ. at Malvern and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., rev. and gen. depts., Jan., 1912; ditto, and jt. mag. and dep. collr., and on special duty as sec., Calcutta port facilities cttee., Nov., 1913; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916 to Feb., 1919; mag. and collr., Dec., 1919; K.-i.H. medal, Jan., 1924; offg. secty., govt. of Bengal, agric, etc., dept., Apl., 1927; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. commr., March, 1930; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, Sept., 1930; confd., June, 1932; temp. member, exec., council, Bengal, June, 1932; and again, Dec., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1937; govr. of Assam, March, 1937; actg. govr. of Bengal, June to Oct., 1938; and Feb., 1939 to June, 1939; kt. of the Order of St. John, Dec., 1940; retd., 1st Nov., 1943.

REILLY, SIR BERNARD RAWDON, K.C.M.G., C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 25th March, 1882).—Retd., March, 1937.*

REILLY, SIR HENRY D'ARCY CORNELIUS, late Indian C.S. (b. 15th Jan., 1876).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. ditto, Aug., 1909; regr., Madras High Court, July, 1910 to July, 1913; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1916; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1924; ditto, July and Aug., 1925; ditto, June, 1926; actg. addl. judge, ditto, May, 1927; judge, ditto, Dec., 1928; retd., July, 1934; chief justice in Mysore State; knighted, Jan., 1938.

REINHOLD, CARL HENRY, M.C., F.R.C.S.E., Col., late I.M.S. (b. 4th Dec., 1880).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

REMPRY, CHARLES OLIVER, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., Bengal (b. 19th Sept., 1877).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

REMINGTON, JAMES BOILEAU, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (ch. transportation supt.) (b. 12th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1910; asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1913; dist. ditto, June, 1917; asst. transpn. supt., Nov., 1922; offg. divl. trans. supt., Jan., 1925; confd., Apl., 1926; offg. ch. transpn. supt., May, 1933; confd., May, 1942.

RENNICK, DENIS KINGSTON, M.B.E., late Survey of India (b. 25th Apl., 1884).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

RENNIE, REV. JOHN YULE, M.A., B.D., B.Litt., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (presdy., senr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 10th Dec., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 1st Dec., 1920; junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Dec., 1922; senr. ditto, Dec., 1932; presdy. senr. ditto, Oct., 1937; confd., March, 1939.

RENNISON, REV. ERIC DAVID ROBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 7th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 14th Oct., 1922; junior chaplain, March, 1923; senr., ditto, March, 1929.

RENWICK, HENRY PERCIVAL, Indian State Rlys (ch. mech. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 6th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., Nizam's G.S. Rly., 3rd Aug., 1912; dist. loco. and carr. supt., June, 1918; dist. loco. supt., G.I.P. Rly., Aug., 1920; asst. trans. supt., Dec., 1922; supt., Feb., 1929; offg. divl. transpn. supt., March, 1933; offg. dep. transpn. supt., Oct., 1933; confd., Oct., 1936; ch. mech. engr., provl., Aug., 1940.

REUBEN, DAVID EZRA, Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Patna) (b. 3rd Sept., 1893).—Educ. at Bombay and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; arrived in India, 23rd Jan., 1919, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1925; offg. settlement officer., Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932; sec. to govt., judl. dept., Apl., 1938; addtl. judge, Patna ct., Aug., 1943; judge, ditto, March, 1944; chrmn., mica enquiry cttee., Nov., 1944.

REYNELL, DOUGLAS, C.I.E., M.A., B.C.L. (Oxon.) (late Indian Educl. Service (Punjab) (b. 23rd Nov., 1877).—Retd., May, 1937.*

REYNOLDS, FRANK ARMSTRONG ABBOTT, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 30th May, 1913).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Burma, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; supt., Jan., 1936.

REYNOLDS, HENRY FORSYTH, late Indian Police (b. 11th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 4th Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; offg. commr. of police, Rangoon city, March, 1932, and again, Dec., 1935; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Dec., 1932; commr. of police, Rangoon, Dec., 1936; retd., 11th May, 1941.

REYNOLDS, HENRY OSBORNE, C.M.G., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 11th Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Sedbergh Gr. Sch., Owen's Coll., Manchester, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Nov., 1911; asst. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1911 to Nov., 1912; under sec., chief, sec.'s dept., Feb., 1913 to Nov., 1914; dep. commr., March, 1918; offg. secy. to Govt., reforms office, Jan., 1922; secy. to govt. and dep. commr., Jan., 1923; confd., Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to govt., agric., excise and forest dept., Nov., 1925; confd., March, 1926; commr. of settlements and land records, Nov., 1928; offg. addl. secty. home and pol., dept., Oct., 1931; offg. commr., Apl., 1932; confd., Aug., 1935; finl. commr., Burma, Apl., 1935; C.M.G., Jan., 1939; retd., Nov., 1942.

REYNOLDS, SIR LEONARD WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.C., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th Feb., 1874).—Retd., Dec., 1933.*

RHIND, DONALD, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (economic botanist, Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service as mycologist,

Burma, 21st March, 1923; economic botanist, Dec., 1927.

RHODES, SIR CAMPBELL WARD, K.C.I.E., C.B.E. (b. 17th Jan., 1874).—C.B.E., Dec., 1919; prest., Bengal ch. of com. and prest., assoc. ch. of commn. of Indian and Ceylon, 1922; member of Indian fiscal commn.; kt., Jan., 1923; member of cl. of India, 12th May, 1925 to May, 1935; vice-chairman, Ind. staty. rly. bd. cttee., June, 1933; member, Ind. res. bank cttee., July, 1933; K.C.I.E., June, 1935.

RIAZ AHMAD, MIAM, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (b. 24th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 22nd Jan., 1929; p.a. to ch. commr., Oct., 1941.

RICH, WILLIAM HAROLD ADRIAN, Indian Police. —Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab 20th Nov., 1928; asst. dir. intell., govt. of India, Oct., 1939; on depn. with def. dept., June, 1940; supt., Oct., 1941; asst. D.I.G., Punjab, 1944.

RICHARDS, CECIL JOHN, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 8th March, 1894).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Aug., 1920; arrived, 5th June, 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. settl. officer, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1927; confd., Apl., 1931.

RICHARDS, REV. GEORGE HENRY, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 24th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 26th March, 1930.

RICHARDS, PHILIP BERNARD, A.R.C.S., F.E.S., Indian Agric. Service (entomologist to govt., United Provs.) (b. 31st May, 1886).—Joined the service as entomologist to govt. on 15th March, 1921; offg. dir. of agric., United Provs., May, 1934, and again, May, 1936.

RICHARDSON, ALAN, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 9th Nov., 1882).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

RICHARDSON, CHARLES RICHARD SUGDEN, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Oct., 1889).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

RICHARDSON, FRANCIS ALBERT ILFORD, Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 26th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service, 17th March, 1921; asst. collr., March, 1923.

RICHARDSON, HUGH EDWARD, O.B.E. B.A., Indian C.S. Indian Pol. Service (b. 22nd Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Glenalmond and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1932; offg. asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1934; British trade agent, Gyantse, July, 1936; pol. offic., Sikkim, in addn., from May, 1937 to Nov., 1937; asst. commr., Charsadda, June, 1940; confd. in Ind. Pol. Serv., Sept., 1940; dy. commr., Hazara, Nov., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944; jt. sec. to govt. of India, external dept., Dec., 1944.

RICHARDSON, MAURICE ROBERT, C.I.B., B.A., B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

RICHARDSON, SIR THOMAS WILLIAM, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Retd., Nov., 1924.*

RICHMOND, SIR ROBERT DANIEL, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 29th Oct., 1878).—Retd., Nov., 1932*; member, Madras public services commn., Nov., 1932; chairman, Jan., 1935 to March, 1940; knighted, June, 1936; retd. March, 1940.

RICKERBY, LEONARD WILLIAM ROBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offic.;

accts. dept.) (b. 1st March, 1901).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 1st March, 1920, after previous service from 23rd Apl., 1919; exec. offcr., Nov., 1934.

RICKETTS, ALFRED ARTHUR HICKMAN, M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 6th Oct., 1906; asst. engr., Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. supt. engr., May, 1930; confd., Sept., 1934; retd., Jan., 1940.

RICKETTS, THOMAS CHARLES DONALD, O.B.E., M.C. (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 3rd July, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Dec., 1934; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

RICKIE, JAMES HEPBURN, Indian State Rlys. (b. 25th July, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 1st Nov., 1906; ditto (perm.), July, 1908; dist. engr., Sept., 1920; offg. dep. ch. engr., variously, from May, 1926; confd., March, 1935; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1932; ch. engr., Oct., 1935.

RIDLEY, SIDNEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. sec., govt. of Sind) (b. 26th March, 1902).—Educ. at Royal Gr. Sch., Lancaster, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug. 1929; offg. dep. commr., U.S. frontier, Oct., 1931; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, fin. dept., Jan., 1935; secty. to govt., fin. dept., Sind, Apl., 1936; secty. to agt.-genl. for India in South Africa, Oct., 1936 to Oct., 1940; collr. and mag. variously, from Jan., 1941; ch. sec., govt. of Sind, June, 1944.

RIEU, SIR JEAN LOUIS, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 23rd Nov., 1872).—Retd., Oct., 1929.*

RIGG, REV. ARTHUR CECIL PIETRONI, M.A., Ecces. Dept., United Provs. (senr. chaplain) (b. 27th Feb., 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th Apl., 1917; junr. chaplain, Apl. 1919; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1929.

RIGG-STANSFIELD, REV. HUBERT, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 22nd Sept., 1897).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 25th Dec., 1931.

RIJHWANI, JETHANAND MANDHANDAS, M.B.E., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 4th June, 1903).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 21st Apl., 1928; offg. exec. engr., March, 1933; confd., Apl., 1937; dep. sec. govt. Sind., Nov., 1941, to July, 1943; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

RILEY, PERCY BROWN, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Veterinary Service (b. 13th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as 2nd supt., civil vety. dept., Bihar and Orissa, 27th March, 1920; dep. dir., Dec., 1925; offg. dir., Apl., 1926, and again, Feb., 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; dir. vety. services, Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1932; retd. June, 1940.

RIPDAMAN, SINGH, Ind. Service of Engrs., (b. 6th Oct., 1908).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st May, 1933; confd. as exec. engr., Sind, May, 1942.

RIPPON, STEWART ROSS, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (dir., Burma) (b. 29th July, 1895).—Joined the service as supt., Burma, 18th Feb., 1924; dep. dir., Dec., 1929; offg. dir., July, 1932; confd., March, 1940.

RISHWORTH, HENRY RICHARD, O.B.E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Rlys. (ch. medl. offcr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 8th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as dist. surg., G.I.P. Rly., 22nd June, 1923; dist. medl. offcr., Apl., 1929;

O.B.E., June, 1939; ch. medl. offcr. (confd.), Apl., 1942.

RITCHIE, ADAM ALEXANDER, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service, Punjab (b. 5th Jan., 1890).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

RITCHIE, JOHN HALL, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1891).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

RIVETT-CARNAC, HERBERT GORDON, Lieut.-Col. (retd.), late Indian Pol. Service (b. 13th Feb., 1892).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

RIZAWI, SAIYID MUHAMMAD SHUAIB, Khan Bahadur (see Saiyid, M. S. R.).

ROBERT, ARTHUR WILLIAM, late Indian Service of Engrs., Madras (b. 28th March, 1880).—Retd., March, 1935.*

ROBERTS, DAVID EDWARD, M.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Assam) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—Retd., May, 1939.*

ROBERTS, SIR ERNEST HANDFORTH GOODMAN, Kt., Barr., Burma, Judl. Dept. (ch. justice, high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. ch. justice, high ct., Rangoon, 28th Apl., 1936; knighted, June, 1936.

ROBERTSON, ALAN CRAIGIE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th Jan., 1883).—Retd., July, 1936.*

ROBERTSON, ANTHONY WILLIAM, O.B.E., D.F.C., late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 1st Dec., 1896).—Joined the service, 17th Apl., 1919; offg. supt., Sept., 1923; confd., Nov., 1932; asst. commr. in the Andamans from 16th Oct., 1923 to 7th Feb., 1927; Diwan, Bundi State, Sept., 1936; retd., March, 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

ROBERTSON, SIR BENJAMIN, K.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., C.I.E., LL.D., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.).—Retd., Feb., 1920*; member of the Council of India, Nov., 1922 to Nov., 1927.

ROBERTSON, CHARLES ROBERTSON OVENS, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 22nd Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Apl., 1920; confd., June, 1934.

ROBERTSON, DONALD MURRAY SLADEN, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Oct., 1882).—Retd., May, 1939.*

ROBERTSON, EDWARD GEORGE DONALD, Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1916).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1936.

ROBERTSON, FRANCIS CALDER FORD, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 19th March, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., April, 1929.

ROBERTSON, FREDERICK WYNNE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Feb., 1885).—Retd., March, 1937*; chairman, pub. serv., commn., Bengal, Apl., 1937; C.S.I., June, 1941; ditto, fed. pub. serv. comm., March, 1942.

ROBERTSON, GEORGE STRAFFORD, B.A.I., B.A., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th May, 1886).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

ROBERTSON, HENRY FERGUSON, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Burma) (b. 31st May, 1895).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, 16th Dec., 1921; profg. of agric. and mycologist, July, 1927; offg. princpl., agricl. coll., Mandalay, Apl., 1932; dep. dir. of agric., Burma, March, 1933.
