(jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 18th May, 1914); educ. at Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 8th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., asst. cane commr., Apl., 1942; addtl. dist. mag., Sept., 1943.

SOHONI, VISHVANATH VISHNU, B.A., M.Sc. (Bombay), Meteorological Dept. (b. 6th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as meteorologist, 12th March, 1922; suptg. ditto, Aug., 1942.

SOKHEY, SAHIBSINGH, M.A., B.Sc., M.D. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 15th Dec., 1887).—Joined, 26th July, 1913; in mil. employ to 7th Aug., 1925; apptd. supernumerary offr., Haffkine Inst., Bombay, Aug., 1925; offg. asst. dir., ditto, Feb., 1926; offg. dir., ditto, March, 1928; dir., ditto, Oct., 1932.

SOLOMAN, SAMUEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 20th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1933; offg. publicity offr., Bihar and Orissa, March, 1935; dir. of devel., Orissa, Apl., 1940; mag. and collr., July, 1941.

SOLOMON, WILLIAM EWART GLADSTONE, late Indian Educl. Service (Bombay) (b. 24th March, 1880).—Ret'd., March, 1937.*

SOM NATH, KAPUR LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1908).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 17th July, 1931; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., July, 1932; exec. engr., July, 1941.

SOMAYRA, SRI BODUGURU.—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, Feb., 1939.

SONDHI, GURU DATT, B.A. (Cantab), M.A. (Pb.), Barr., Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Dec., 1890).—Apptd. prof. of economics, govt. coll., Jahore, 17th Sept., 1917; princpl., ditto, Sept., 1940.

SONDHI, RAM LAL (see Ram Lal, S.).

SONDHI, SUNDAR DAS, M.C., M.B., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S., Jail Dept., Punjab (supt.) (b. 25th Apl., 1890).—First commn., dated 9th

Nov., 1917; in mil. employ prior to 25th March, 1927; supt., Apl., 1927; revtd. to mil. employ, June, 1940; ret'd., July, 1942.

SOOD, MANOHAR LAL (see Manohar Lal Sood).

SOOFI, MAHMOOD ALI, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 20th March, 1901).—Educ. at Punjab Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, variously, from May, 1932; confd., Nov., 1936; addtl. judge, ct. of judl. commr., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1940 to June, 1941; dist. and sess. judge, Punjab, Feb., 1942.

SORBY, WILLIAM JOHN WOODCRAFT, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Apl., 1893).—Asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 2nd Oct., 1920; divl. mech. engr., senr. scale, Oct., 1936.

SORLEY, HERBERT TOWER, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (offg. ch. sec. to govt. of Bombay) (b. 12th Apl., 1892).—Educ. at Gr. Sch., Aberdeen, Aberdeen Univ., Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 9th Dec., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dep. commr. of salt and excise, June, 1923; criminal tribes settl. offr., Bombay Presy. (offg.), March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1926; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; offg. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Feb., 1929; offg. prov. supt. of census, Bombay presdy., Apl., 1933; sec. to govt. gen. and educ. depts., Bombay, March, 1936; C.I.E., June, 1939; offg. ch. sec., Nov., 1939.

SOTHERS, DONALD BEVAN, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Sind) (b. 11th March, 1889).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1912, as asst. consvr., Bombay; on duty as asst. censor from 2nd Dec., 1914 to 11th Jan., 1919; I.A.R.O., from 23rd Apl., 1915; asst. contr., famine fodder, Jan. to Apl., 1919; dep. consvr., Apl., 1919; offg. consvr., May, 1926; and again, March, 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; offg. ch. consvr., Bombay, June, 1935; transf. to Sind, Nov., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

SOUTER, SIR CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 13th June, 1877).—Ret'd., Apl., 1937.*

SOUTHERN, REV. GERALD HOLTE BRACEBRIDGE, M.A., late Indian Ecclesial Estab. (United Provs.) (b. 1st Nov., 1889).—Ret'd., Feb., 1938.*

SOUTHORN, ANDREW HERBERT, M.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. sec., public dept., Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at St. Edwards Sch., Oxford, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Sept., 1934; under-sec., public dept., Oct., 1940; dep. ditto, May, 1942; M.B.E., June, 1943.

SPACKMAN, WILLIAM COLLIS, M.B., Col., I.M.S. (b. 23rd Sept., 1889).—First commn., 26th July, 1913; offg. civil surg., March, 1924; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. prof., G.M. Coll., and supt., Bai Motilal and Sir D. M. Petit hospitals, Bombay, July, 1927, March, 1930, and again, Nov., 1932; inspr.-gen., civ. hosp., June, 1941.

SOUTHERN, PETER STERLING, Indian Police (Bihar) (b. 28th May, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Jan., 1941.

SPARGO, FREDERICK WILSON, B.A., B.Sc.,

Barr, late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 30th Oct., 1888).—Retd., Sept., 1940.*

SPARKE, WALTER, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Aug., 1878).—Retd., Nov., 1934.*

SPEECHLY, ALBERT GEORGE, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 10th Jan., 1881).—Held non-gaz. posts from 1st Feb., 1898; on depn. with E. Persian Cordon F.F., Sept., 1918, to May, 1920; ch. supt., Calcutta tel. office, Oct., 1932; ret. Jan., 1936.

SPEECHLEY, EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th July, 1880).—Retd., Apr., 1936.*

SPENCE, SIR GEORGE HEMMING, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec., govt. of India, legis. dept.) (b. 6th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1917; addl. dist. mag., Delhi, Jan. to Nov., 1918; temp. under-sec. to govt., May, 1919; spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, from June, 1919; under-sec. to govt. of India, reforms office, Dec., 1919; addl. dep. sec. to govt. of India, legislative dept., Dec., 1920; offg. dep. sec., ditto, Apr., 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; sec., council of state, Nov., 1933; and Jan., 1935; offg. sec. to the govt. of India, legis. dept., June, 1934, and again, Nov., 1935; confd., Apr., 1936; sec. to the govr.-gen.'s exec. council, in addn., Aug., 1936; C.S.I., Feb., 1937; kt., June, 1939; K.C.I.E., June, 1943.

SPENCER, FRANK, M.B.E. (revision offr., govt. of India, def. dept.) (b. 26th Sept., 1888).—Served with reg. army from 18th Nov., 1908, to 11th March, 1924; held minis. appts. at A.H.Q. up to Dec., 1930; offr. supervisor, general staff branch, A.H.Q. (offg.), Jan., 1931; confd., March, 1931; M.B.E., Jan., 1932; apptd. revision offr., def. dept., Apr., 1938.

SPENCER, JOHN EDWARD, late Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1892).—Retd., Aug., 1934.*

SPENS, SIR WILLIAM PATRICK, O.B.E., K.C. (ch. justice of India).—Apptd., Apr., 1943.

SPILLER, THOMAS RUEBEN, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 9th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; princpl., New Coll., Patna, Oct., 1923; headmaster, Zila Sch., June, 1927; offg. princpl., Patna training coll., variously from May, 1933; inspr. of schs., Bihar and Orissa, variously from Apr., 1933; headmaster, Ranchi Zila Sch., again, Oct., 1933; princpl., Patna trng. coll., Apr., 1937; ret. d., 9th Dec., 1944.

SPITTELER, ARTHUR ERNEST, Indian Police (b. 29th Oct., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 24th Jan., 1936.

SPRATT, NOEL NEWTON, India Office (higher clerical offr., mil. dept.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1891).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 9th March, 1914; on mil. service from Nov., 1916, to July, 1919; higher clerical offr., Apr., 1922; actg. hr. exec. offr., Apr., 1940.

SPRAWSON, SIR CUTHBERT ALLEN, Kt., C.I.E., M.D., M.R.C.P., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S., ret. d. (b. 1st March, 1877).—Retd., March, 1937.*

SPRAY, WILLIAM ALFRED, Indian State Rlys. (supt. of collieries) (b. 27th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 13th Aug., 1919; offg. variously as colliery manager from Oct., 1919; confd., Sept., 1926; supt. of collieries, July, 1938.

SPRINGFIELD, ERNEST, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 3rd Nov., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 7th Dec., 1922;

offg. supt., May, 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; supt., July, 1934.

SQUIRE, GILES FREDERICK, C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 12th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Marlborough and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, on probn., as first asst. to resdt. of Baroda, Apr., 1924; confd., March, 1925; sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Apr., 1926; under-sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Nov., 1926; sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Punjab States, Dec., 1927; ditto, W.I. States, Oct., 1929; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Nov., 1933; pol. agent, Kalat, Apr., 1934; ditto, Sibi, Dec., 1935; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, March, 1936; consul-gen. for Khorasan, Nov., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1941; on depn., 1941; minister, Kabul, July, 1943.

SQUIRES, HOWARD JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 3rd Sept., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 21st Oct., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1937; dep. sec., reconstr. dept., 1944.

SREEKANTESWARAN, KOLAR VENKATSWAMIER, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th March, 1903).—Joined the service, 8th March, 1926; asst. acct.-gen., Burma, Apr., 1929; curr. offr., Bombay, Dec., 1933; dep. acct.-gen., Punjab, June, 1935; accts. offi., E.I. rly., Apr., 1936; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. rly., Nov., 1938; examiner, outside audit dept., Bombay, March, 1939; dep. fin. advr., supply fin., Apr., 1941.

SRIHARI RAO NAYUDU, BANDI VENKATA, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.C., late Registration Dept., Madras (b. 19th Feb., 1887).—Held non-gaz. appt., offg. dep. collr., Feb., 1920; confd., May, 1923; offg. 3rd presdy. mag., Apr., 1928; offg. 4th ditto, July, 1928; offg. asst. sec., bd. of rev., May, 1929; 3rd presdy. mag., March, 1930; 2nd ditto, Oct., 1930; inspr.-gen. of registration, Feb., 1931; ret. d., Feb., 1940.

SRINIVASA RAO, CHIKBALLAPUR VENKATA, C.I.E., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service, 17th March, 1914; asst. acct.-gen., Burma, Nov., 1915; asst. comptr., Assam, Dec., 1915; asst. comptr., India treasuries, Nov., 1916; currency offr., Rangoon, May, 1919; asst. acct.-gen., Burma, July, 1919; dep. acct.-gen., posts and tels., Madras, Jan., 1924; on spl. duty in fin. dept., March, 1925; offg. dep. contr. of rly. accts., March, 1933; addl. dep. dir. (fin.) rly. bd., Jan., 1935; offg. finl. advr., posts and tels., May, 1935; offg. dep. ch. accts. offr., Burma rlys., March, 1936; offg. ch. ditto, Apr., 1936; on for. service as acct.-gen., Burma, Apr., 1937; auditor-gen., Burma (subst.), Feb., 1940; acct.-gen., Burma, May, 1940; dir. audit, def. serv., June, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

SRINAVASAN, KALYANA CHENNAKESAVA, M.A., F.C.S., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 26th Apr., 1896).—Joined the service, 24th March, 1921; asst. auditor, E.B. rly., Aug., 1922; on depn. with govt. of India in connection with accounts for dairy farms, Feb., 1923; sec., rly. rates advisory cttee., May, 1926; divl. audit offr., Karachi, June, 1928; govt. exr. of accts., B.N. rly., March, 1929; dy. ch. auditor, G.I.P. rly., May, 1930; dep. govt. exr., B.B. and C.I. rly., Apr., 1931; on spl. duty under contr. of rly. accts., 1936; dep. ch. accts. offr., Jan., 1939;

dep. dir. of fin., Apl., 1939; offr. on spl. duty, rly. bd., June, 1939.

SRINIVASAN, KUPPUWAMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 1st May, 1907).—Educ. at University Coll., London, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Jan., 1934.

SRINAVASAN, MANDAYAM MUDEMBAL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 15th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 10th Nov., 1931; dep. consvr. Nov., 1936.

SRINIVASAN, SUBRAMANIA IYER, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th June, 1903).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1926; currency offr., Karachi, Sept., 1932; asst. acct.-gen., Madras, May, 1935; examiner, local fund accts., Central Provs., March, 1937; dept. acct.-gen., Central Provs., July, 1938; accts. offr., G.I.P. rly., March, 1938; ditto, E.I. rly., May, 1940; ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Dec., 1941.

SRIRAMULU NAYUDU, BUNDY, late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 4th July, 1885).—Retd., July, 1940.*

SRIVASTAV, JASODA NAND, Rai Sahib, P.M.S., late Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. Dec., 1884).—Civil surg., Apl., 1928; retd.

SRIVASTAVA, SIR JWALA PRASAD, K.B.E. (member, gov.-gen.'s exec. council, food) (b. 16th Aug., 1889).—Member, United Provs. legisl. cl., 1926-36; legisl. assembly, 1936-42; min. of educn., United Provs., 1931-36; min., fin. and indus., 1937; ch., United Provs. Simon cttee., 1928; ch., Cawnpore improve. trust, 1928-31; member, nat. def. cl., 1940-42; member, gov.-gen.'s exec. council (food), July, 1942.

SRIVASTAVA, RAM CHANDRA, O.B.E., B.Sc., Industries Dept., United Provs. (dep. dir.) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Apptd. dep. dir. of industries, United Provs., 1st Sept., 1928; services placed at disposal of Impl. Council of Agricultural Research, for employment as sugar tech., July, 1930; dir., impl. inst. of sugar tech., Cawnpore, Oct., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1938.

SRIVASTAVA, SHYAM MOHAN, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (b. 13th July, 1910).—Educ. at K.P. Intermediate Coll., Allahabad, Univ. of Allahabad, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., jt. mag., Nov., 1936; sec., Impl. council of agric. res., Feb., 1944.

STAFFORD, PAUL, Indian Police (b. 12th Dec., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd., Sept., 1940; served in Sind from Aug., 1944.

STAGG, MONTAGU, Col., R.E., O.B.E., late Mint Dept., Calcutta (b. 4th Nov., 1885).—Retd., July, 1940.*

STAGGS, MALCOLM JAMES ALLAN, M.B.E. (asst. sec. to govt. of India, defence dept.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1900).—Joined the service, June, 1919; apptd. asst. sec. to the govt. of India, defence dept. (offg.), March, 1939; confd., July, 1939; M.B.E., Jan., 1942.

STAIG, SIR BERTIE MUNRO, C.S.I., Indian C.S., attd. Indian Finance Dept. (b. 14th Aug., 1892).—Educ. at St. Andrews, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1918; arrived, 18th Dec., 1917, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., May, 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. under-sec., govt. of Bengal, pol. and

appts. dept., March, 1921; ditto, fin. dept., Nov., 1921; transf. to Indian audit and accts. list, Nov., 1922; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, May, 1924; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1924; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1925; finl. advr., P.W.D., and jt. sec. to govt. of Punjab, fin. dept., March, 1926; sec., hydro-electric branch enquiry cttee., Oct., 1929; sec. to govt., P.W.D. (elec.), Punjab, Apl., 1933; addl. finl. advr., mil. fin., July, 1934; finl. advr., mil. fin., March, 1935; also relief commr., Baluchistan, and jt. sec., govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1935; C.S.I., June, 1936; on spl. duty in for. and pol. dept., Sept., 1936; member, frontier watch and ward cttee., Nov., 1936; finl. commr., rlys., Apl., 1937; dep. auditor gen. in India, Aug., 1940; finl. advr., Iraq commd., Nov., 1941; kt., Jan., 1942; addtl. sec., finl. dept., June, 1942; on spl. duty, war transp., Sept., 1942; auditor-gen. of India, Oct., 1944.

STAINTON, VERNON BRUCE, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. sec., home dept., govt. of Punjab) (b. 31st March, 1904).—Educ. at Harrow, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1932, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., Nov., 1936; dep. sec. to govt., home dept., Nov., 1937; dep. commr. (sub. provl.), June, 1939; dep. sec., govt. Punjab (home), Aug., 1940; dep. commr. (sub. perm.), Dec., 1941; O.B.E., June, 1943.

STALEY, HENRY SAMBLE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 20th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 27th March, 1915; inspr. of schs., Apl., 1916; offg. dep. dir. of public instrn., Apl., 1926; offg. princpl., Spence trng. coll., Jubbulpore, July, 1934; retd., May, 1941.

STALLARD, WILLIAM LEONARD O'BRIAN, Indian Police (suft., Bihar) (b. 29th Aug., 1908).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 28th Dec., 1928; confd., Apl., 1930; A.D.C. to govt., Orissa, Apl., 1936; supt., July, 1940.

STAMPE, SIR WILLIAM LEONARD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 31st Oct., 1882).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

STAMPER, THOMAS HENRY GILBORN, C.I.E., F.S.I., M.C. (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Late surveyor to govt., Bombay; retd., Oct., 1939.*

STANDEN, SIR BERTRAM PRIOR, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.).—Retd., June, 1926.*

STANFORD, JOHN KEITH, O.B.E., M.A., M.C., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 29th Apl., 1892).—Retd., March, 1938.*

STANGER-LEATHES, HUGH ELLIS, Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 1st Nov., 1878).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

STANIFORTH, WALTER HARLAND, India Office (suftg. exec. offr., acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 21st June, 1886).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 15th Aug., 1907; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; actg. staff clerk, Apl., 1921; acting higher exec. offr., Apl., 1922; confd., Oct., 1925; also assessor of income tax; suptg. exec. offr., Oct., 1936; assessor of inc. tax, May, 1942.

STANSFIELD, REV. HERBERT RIGO, Indian Ecclesi. Establ. (b. 22nd Sept., 1897).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 25th Dec., 1931; name changed by deed poll to RIGG-STANSFIELD (q.v.).

STANLEY, RAYMOND MORTON, Indian

Police (United Provs.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1916).—
Appdt. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

STANSGATE, VISCOUNT (formerly BENN,
THE Rt. HON. W. WEDGWOOD), D.S.O., D.F.C.—
Secty. of State for India, June, 1929, to
Aug., 1931. Created Viscount, Jan., 1942.

STANTON, WILLIAM THOMAS, Indian State
Rlys. (colliery manager) (b. 16th May, 1887).—
Joined the service as asst. colliery manager,
15th Aug., 1919; colliery manager, Sept., 1931.

STAPLES, FRANCIS HAMMOND, M.A. (Oxon),
Barr., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b.
1st July, 1884).—Retd., July, 1936.*

STAPYLTON, JOHN MILES, late Indian C.S.
(Central Provs.) (b. 13th Nov., 1896).—Retd.,
Sept., 1938.*

STARTE, HORACE WILDERSPIN, B.A., late
Indian Forest Service (Bombay) (b. 13th March,
1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr.,
1st Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg.
consvr., Feb., 1929, and again, Sept., 1932;
confd., May, 1935; retd., May, 1942.

STARTE, OLIVER HAROLD BAPTIST, C.B.E.,
B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 23rd Jan.,
1882).—Retd., Apl., 1936.*

STEAD, SIR CHARLES, Kt., C.B.E., M.V.O.,
late Indian Police Service (Punjab) (b. 30th Aug.,
1877).—Retd., Aug., 1932.*

STEADMAN, JOHN WILLIAM, Office of High
Commr. for India (asst. chief acctg. offr.) (b.
3rd July, 1885).—Clerk to Rl. commn. on Indian
decentralisation, June, 1908, to March, 1909;
apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 28th March,
1909, and attached to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.;
clerk to royal commn. on public services, May,
1913, to Sept., 1915; asst. sec. to Jails Cttee.
from July, 1919, to Sept., 1920; minor staff
clerk, Nov., 1920; services lent to high commr.
for India from Apl., 1921; higher exec. offr.,
Apl., 1922; suptg. exec. offr., June, 1927;
asst. ch. acctg. offr., Nov., 1937.

STEAVERSON, FREDERICK HENRY, late
Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 19th Dec., 1885).—
Retd., Nov., 1936.*

STEEN, HUGH BARKLEY, M.D., Lieut.-Col.,
I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1895).—Apptd.
examr. of surgical instruments, India Store
Dept., and addl. member, medl. bd., India
Office, May, 1934, to May, 1941.*

STEIGER-HAYLEY, THOMAS THEODORE
(see Hayley, T. T. S.).

STEIN, ARCHIBALD HYNDMAN, O.B.E.,
B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.,
Central Provs.) (b. 11th Jan., 1903).—Joined the
service as asst. consvr., 21st Feb., 1924; dep.
consvr., Feb., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1942;
utilization offr., Apl., 1943.

STEIN, GEORGE LESLIE HAMILTON, late
Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 4th March, 1890).—
Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov.,
1909; supt., Oct., 1920; retd., March, 1941.

STEIN, JOHN ALAN, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.A.,
late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b.
31st Oct., 1888).—Joined the service as asst.
engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921;
offg. suptg. engr., March, 1931; and again, Apl.,
1933; confd., March, 1934; spec. offr., road
fund wks., Apl., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1943;
retd., 1st May, 1944.

STENT, PERCY JOHN HODSOLI, C.I.E., B.A.,
late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 24th Nov.,
1888).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

STEPHEN, SIR HARRY LUSHINGTON, Bt.,
Barr. (1885).—Late judge, high ct., Calcutta;
retd., Nov., 1914.*

STEPHENS, CHARLES VIVIAN, A.M.I.C.E.,

A.M.I.M.E., late Indian Service of Engrs.
(Madras) (b. 28th Apl., 1887).—Joined the
service as temp. engr., 25th June, 1910; perm.
in provl. service, 1st Feb., 1920; asst. exec.
engr. in I.S.E., 1st Oct., 1925; offg. exec. engr.,
May, 1926; confd., Nov., 1931; retd., Apl.,
1942.

STEPHENS, JAMES, B.A., Indian Forest
Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 6th Sept.,
1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr.,
30th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

STEPHENS, JOHN, Indian State Rlys.
(transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 22nd July,
1887).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and
wagon supt., 3rd Apl., 1920; asst. transpn.
supt., Apl., 1923; transpn. supt., July, 1930.

STEPHENS, STANLEY DONALD, Indian
Police (asst. supt.) (b. 31st Aug., 1910).—Apptd.
proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1931;
confd., Jan., 1934.

STEPHENSON, HUGH SOUTHERN, O.B.E.,
Indian C.S. (sec. to govr., United Provs.) (b.
29th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Winchester and
Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of
1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and
served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and
collr.; addl. under-sec., fin. dept., govt. of
India (temp.), Jan., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935;
asst. sec., Niemeyer finl. enquiry, Jan., 1936;
addl. under-sec., home dept., govt. of India,
Nov., 1936; offr. on spec. duty, ditto, Dec.,
1936; under-sec. ditto, Apl., 1937; sec.
to govr., United Provs., March, 1940; O.B.E.,
June, 1944.

STEPHENSON, REV. WILLIAM, B.A., Indian
Ecclesl. Establ. (b. 4th March, 1898) (junr.
chaplain, Punjab).—Apptd. chaplain on probn.,
15th Oct., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929.

STEVENS, HAROLD SAMUEL EATON, C.S.I.,
C.I.E., M.C., M.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt.,
agric., etc., depts., Bengal) (b. 29th Nov., 1892).—
Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, and
Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 23rd Aug.,
1920; arrived, 7th Dec., 1920, and served in
Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. mag.,
Nov., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1926;
dep. sec. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and apptt.
depts., March, 1930; mag. and collr., June,
1931; offg. sec. to govt., Bengal, agric. and
indus., p.w. and irrign. depts., July, 1935;
confd., Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1938; spec.
offr. (jute), agric. and indus. depts., May, 1940;
dir., civil def., Dec., 1941; sec., home dept.
and jt. ch. sec. and ch. civ. def. commr., Apl.,
1942; ch. sec. and ditto, Oct., 1944; C.S.I.,
June, 1944.

STEVENSON, ARCHIBALD MCCORKELL, Mil.
Accts. Dept. (b. 11th Aug., 1897).—Joined the
service as offr., cl. II, Feb., 1921; A.M.A.G.,
July, 1929; dep. fin. advr., sec. fin., Dec., 1940;
revtd. to mil. accts. dept., Apl., 1941; promoted
cl. I, Sept., 1941; comd. contr., June, 1942.

STEVENSON, FREDERICK, M.B., Lieut.-Col.,
I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 1st June, 1884).—Retd., Oct.,
1938.*

STEVENSON, REV. GEORGE, M.A., late
Indian Ecclesl. Establ.—Apptd. junr. chaplain,
Burma, Dec., 1932; retd., July, 1940.

STEVENSON-MOORE, SIR CHARLES JAMES,
K.C.I.E., C.V.O., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—
Retd., Sept., 1921.*

STEWART, ALEXANDER DRON, C.I.E.,
M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Lieut.-Col. (ret'd.), late I.M.S.
(b. 22nd June, 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

STEWART, CLYNE GARDEN, O.B.E., M.A.,
Indian Police (dep. inspr.-gen., Burma) (b.

6th July, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 4th Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1932; O.B.E., June, 1932; offg. asst. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1933, to March, 1936; dir., defence bureau, govt. of Burma, Apr., 1937; dep. inspr.-gen. (confd.), Nov., 1937.

STEWART, DONALD, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 14th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Apr., 1925; O.B.E., May, 1937; jt. timber advr. offr., rly. and def. depts., Sept., 1939, to May, 1940; dep. dir. (timber directorate), supply dept., May, 1940; dir., ditto, Aug., 1940; consvr. (confd.), July, 1942; dir.-gen. (supply), Apr., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

STEWART, SIR DOUGLAS LAW, Bart., late Indian Police Service (b. 1st June, 1878).—Retd., Nov., 1931.*

STEWART, ERNEST HENRY JAMES M.C., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 5th June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; offg. exec. engr., March, 1925; confd., Oct., 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1940.

STEWART, FRANCIS WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.C., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 28th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 24th Nov., 1909, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1916; on mil. duty from Jan., 1916, to Nov., 1919; sub-collr. and asst. commr., Oriya agency, May, 1922; offg. variously as collr. and dist. mag., Apr., 1924, to Apr., 1928; collr. and dist. mag. (provl.), Oct., 1928; confd., Aug., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; 3rd member, bd. of rev., Jan., 1938; confd., Apr., 1940; ret'd., 23rd Aug., 1944.

STEWART, GERALD PAKENHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 5th March, 1906).—Educ. at Portora Royal Sch., Enniskillen, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; president, Manipur State Durbar, Feb., 1933, to Feb., 1936; pol., agt., Manipur, Mar.-Nov., 1938; p.o.w. in Japan, Sept., 1941.

STEWART, HERBERT RAY, C.I.E., F.R.C.Sc.I., D.I.C., N.D.A., Dept. of Agric. (b. 10th July, 1890).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; profr. of agric., Punjab Agric. Coll., Dec., 1923; actg. princpl., ditto, March, 1927; offg. dir. of agric., May, 1927; on spl. duty, dept. of agric., Dec., 1927; asst. dir. of agric., Nov., 1928; offg. dir. of agric., Apr., 1931; confd., Dec., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1939.

STEWART, JOHN ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.C., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 19th Jan., 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

STEWART, PETER MCARTHUR, Barr., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 21st Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1907, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, Oct., 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1933; confd., supt., sel. grade, July, 1933; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; princpl., police trng. sch., July, 1938, to June, 1938; ditto, pre-cadet sch., Poona, Feb., 1942.

STEWART, SIR SAMUEL FINDLATER, G.C.B., G.C.I.E., C.S.I., LL.D., India Office and Burma Office (perm. under-sec. of state for India and for Burma) (b. 22nd Dec., 1879).—Educ. at

Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. junior clerk, 19th Oct., 1903, and served in mil. dept. to Jan., 1910; reading clerk to Council, May, 1906; pte. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, Jan., 1910; senr. clerk, fin. dept., May, 1911; on duty in mil. dept. from Nov., 1911, to Feb., 1912, and from Nov., 1914; asst. sec., mil. dept., June, 1918; C.I.E., June, 1919; dep. sec., mil. dept., Apr., 1919; joint sec., mil. dept., July, 1920; sec. to royal commn. on the public services in India, May, 1923, to March, 1924; C.S.I., June, 1924; asst. under-sec. of state and clerk of the council, Sept., 1924; sec., Indian Statutory Commission, Nov., 1927; K.C.I.E., June, 1930; perm. under-sec. of state for India, 11th July, 1930; K.C.B., Jan., 1932; G.C.I.E., June, 1935; perm. under-sec. of state for Burma, Apr., 1937; G.C.B., June, 1939; dir.-gen. of the Ministry of Information, Sept., 1939, to Nov., 1939; sec. for spl. duties, June, 1940.

STEWART, SIR THOMAS ALEXANDER, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Feb., 1888).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; on mil. service from July, 1918; actg. asst. collr., Imperial customs service, Jan., 1919; dep. commr. of rice, Rangoon, Jan., 1920; rice commr., July, 1921; offg. collr. of customs, Rangoon, Dec., 1923; collr. of customs, Madras, Oct., 1925; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1928; collr. of salt revs., May, 1932; offg. addl. sec., commerce dept., Oct., 1932; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, March, 1933; confd., March, 1934; C.S.I., June, 1935; member, council of state, Apr., 1936; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937; member, govr. gen.'s exec. council, Apr., 1937; chairman, cent. adv. cl. for rlys., Nov., 1937; actg. govr., Bihar, May to Sept., 1938; K.C.S.I., June, 1939; govr., Bihar, Aug., 1939; kt. of the Order of St. John, Dec., 1940; ret'd., 9th June, 1945.

STEWART-WILSON, SIR CHARLES, K.C.I.E., B.A., Barr. (1886), late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Retd., Aug., 1913.*

STIBBS, JAMES CHARLES, O.B.E., late India Office (b. 5th Sept., 1878).—Educ. at Plymouth Corp. Gr. Sch., and Penzance Coll. Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 5th Nov., 1897; staff clerk, March, 1919; junior clerk, Apr., 1921; suptg. exec. offr. (acting), Apr., 1922; confd., Nov., 1922; also assessor of inc. tax; asst. acctnt.-gen., March, 1927; O.B.E., Jan., 1933; dep. acctnt.-gen. and clk. to the commrs. of inc. tax, Dec., 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1941; re-employed as temp. suptg. exec. offr., Jan., 1941, to Apr., 1942.

STIRLING, ALEXANDER GILCHRIST, India Office (exec. offr., mil. dept.) (b. 19th May, 1916).—Apptd. exec. offr., 10th Feb., 1936; on mil. service from May, 1940.

STIRLING, REV. JAMES CLERK PAUL, M.A., B.D., Indian Ecclesi. Establ.—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Church of Scotland, May, 1931; Madras diocese, senr. chaplain, May, 1941; on mil. duty, Nov., 1940, to Sept., 1943; presdy. senr. chaplain, Nov., 1943.

STOCK, THOMAS DAVID, D.I.C., A.R.C.S., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 19th June, 1891).—Retd., Jan., 1936.*

STOCKWELL, FREDERICK RANDOLPH, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.).—

Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 1st Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

STODART, JAMES CARLYLE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Feb., 1880).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1912; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1919; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1924; addl. judge, ct. of resdt. of Hyderabad, Nov., 1932; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1935; confd., July, 1936; ret'd., Feb., 1940.

STOKES, SIR HOPETOUN GABRIEL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 1st Aug., 1873).—Ret'd., March, 1935.*

STOKES, RICHARD CROSBY, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 20th Apl., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 26th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. supt., Sept., 1933.

STONE, BERNARD SIDNEY, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offr., store dept.) (b. 23rd Jan., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 30th July, 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; second class clerk, 29th Apl., 1920; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. offr., Apl., 1922; actg. higher exec. offr., Oct., 1939.

STONE, SIR GILBERT, Barr. (b. 6th June, 1886).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, 30th Nov., 1930; ch. justice, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; kt., Jan., 1936; ret'd., 1943.

STONE, JULAND CHETWYN TALBOT WATKINS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Central Provs.) (b. 17th June, 1886).—Joined the service, 5th June, 1911; asst. engr., Nov., 1915; exec. engr., July, 1924; ret'd., Feb., 1941.

STONE, SIR JOHN LEONARD, O.B.E. (chief justice, high ct., Bombay).—Apptd. 1st Oct., 1943; presdt., Bombay disaster enq. commn., May to Sept., 1944.

STONE, WILFRED JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (bridge engr., Burma rlys.) (b. 5th May, 1884).—Joined the service, 28th Aug., 1925; bridge engr., Jan., 1928.

STORK, HERBERT CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 28th June, 1890).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; regr., high ct., Calcutta, appellate side, Jan., 1925; offg. first land acquisn. offr., Calcutta, Nov., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, Assam Valleys Districts, March, 1937; remember. of legal affairs, etc., Assam, Apl., 1939.

STORR, NORMAN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Holgate Gr. Sch., Barnsley, and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; regr., high ct., Allahabad, Feb., 1939; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1940.

STORRS-FOX, REV. EDWIN AUBREY, M.A., late Indian Ecclesi. Establ. (Punjab) (b. 27th Jan., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 18th March, 1916; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1919; senr. ditto, Oct., 1920; ret'd.

STOTT, HUGH, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.D., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S. (surgn.-genl., Madras) (b. 18th July, 1884).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1908; apptd. surg. to H.E. the Govt. of Madras,

5th Feb., 1913; on mil. duty from Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1918; profir., King George's med. coll., Lucknow, Nov., 1922; offg. princpl., ditto, Aug., 1925, and again, Aug., 1927; employed under Lucknow Univ. from Nov., 1927; inspr.-gen., civil hosp., Bihar, Nov., 1937; comdr. of the Order of St. John, Dec., 1940; surgn.-gen., Madras, Feb., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1941.

STOTT, RAYMOND, Home Dept., Govt. of India (govt. examr. of questioned documents) (b. 26th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 31st May, 1915; on mil. duty up to 17th Sept., 1920; asst. examr. of questioned documents, Sept., 1920; offg. govt. examr. of questioned documents, March, 1923; confd., June, 1926.

STOW, VINCENT AUBREY STEWART, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., Mayo Coll.) (b. 27th July, 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; asst. master, Marlborough Coll., 1906; asst. master, Daly Coll., Indore, Feb., 1907; services placed at the disposal of the ch. commr., Cent. Provs., from 24th Aug., 1912; princpl., Rajkumar Coll., Raipur, Aug., 1912; on mil. service, 1918-19; princpl., Mayo Coll., Ajmer, July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934.

STRACEY, ERIC LEWIS, Indian Police (Madras) (b. 28th Nov., 1920).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn. after exam. of 1942.

STRACEY, HUGH AUBREY ST. CLAIR, Indian Police (asst. supt.).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., 27th Jan., 1934; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938; asst. supt., Crown representative's police, Neemuch.

STRACEY, PATRICK DONALD, Indian Forest Service (b. 31st Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 9th Nov., 1930; dep. consvr., June, 1937.

STRACEY, RALPH AUGUSTINE ST. CLAIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 30th Aug., 1909).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Coll., Bangalore, Madras Univ., and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. pte. sec. to govr., Bengal, Feb., 1936; mag. and collr., May, 1942; dep. sec., public health, etc., dept., Dec., 1943.

STRANGE, FRANK HORACE LUMSDEN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th Aug., 1884).—Ret'd., Aug., 1939.*

STRANGMAN, SIR THOMAS JOSEPH, Kt., B.A., LL.B., Barr.—Late advocate-genl., Bombay; ret'd., May, 1922.*

STRATFORD, MISS LENNA, B.A., M.B.E., late Indian Educl. Service (Punjab) (b. 9th Aug., 1877).—Ret'd., Aug., 1933.*

STRATHIE, SIR DAVID NORMAN, K.C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (advr. to govr., Madras) (b. 31st Oct., 1886).—Educ. at Glasgow Academy, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 22nd Jan., 1911, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., July, 1917; under-sec. to govt. of India, comm. and ind. dept., 1918; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras, May, 1919; commr. of inc. tax, Madras, June, 1923; offg. collr. and sec. to govt., rev. dept., June, 1928; offg. regr., co-operative societies, Dec., 1929; collr., March, 1930; confd., Oct., 1931; inspr., mun. cls. and local boards, May, 1937; commr. of labour, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; member, bd. of rev., July, 1938; ch. sec., var. from Feb., 1940; for. service under Palestine govt., Jan., 1941, to Apl., 1942; liaison offr. with Southern

Army, Aug., 1942, to Jan., 1943; advr. to govt., Madras, Jan., 1943; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

STRATTON, REV. BASIL, M.A., Indian Ecclesl. Establ. (United Provs.) (junr. chaplain) (b. 27th Apl., 1906).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 15th Sept., 1935; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1937.

STREATFEILD, REV. SIDNEY FREMLYN, B.A., Ecclesl. Dept., Central Provs. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. garrison chaplain on probn., 25th June, 1930; junr. chaplain, June, 1932.

STREATHFIELD, TERENCE ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 18th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Sutton Valence Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Bombay, Jan., 1934; confd., June, 1934; offg. collr., June, 1936; confd., July, 1939; backward cl. offr., Sept., 1943, to Sept., 1944.

STRELLEY, RICHARD COLLIN, late Indian Police (b. 30th June, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 16th Nov., 1925; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1932, and again, May, 1933; retd., 1st Sept., 1941.

STUART, SIR LOUIS, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 12th March, 1870).—Retd., March, 1930.*

STUART, MALCOLM MONCRIEFF, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 21st May, 1903).—Educ. at Sedbergh, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Jan., 1927; arrived, Feb., 1927, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., variously from Apl., 1932; on spec. duty, rev. dept., Bengal, Jan., 1938; addtl. dep. sec., home dept., ditto, Sept., 1939, to March, 1940; mag. and collr. (confd.), Apl., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

STUART, SIR ROBERT CHARLES OCHILTREE, K.C.S.I., Maj.-Gen., R.A. (retd.).—Retd., Aug., 1919.*

STUBBS, JOHN LAWRENCE COMMERELL, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 23rd May, 1908).—Educ. at Radley and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

STUBBS, LAWRENCE MORLEY, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provs.) (b. 23rd March, 1874).—Retd., Dec., 1933.*

STUBBS, STANLEY SEVERS, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th Oct., 1891).—Asst. traffic supt., Dec., 1919; divl. transpn. offr., May, 1935; on loan to I.R.C.A. as dep. gen. sec. from Nov., 1936; retd., Oct., 1941.

STUBBS, SHIRLEY GRAHAM, O.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

STUDDERT-KENNEDY, REV. HENRY GERALD, M.A., Indian Ecclesl. Establ. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Punjab, 7th March, 1932; junr. chaplain, March, 1934.

STUDDY, HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 20th Apl., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 22nd Dec., 1914; supt., Oct., 1923; persl. asst. to inspr.-gen., March, 1925, to Oct., 1926.

STURGIS, GERALD CLARE, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 4th Sept., 1892).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

SUBBA RAO, MYSORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 25th Oct., 1907).—

Educ. at Sch. of Economics, London, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; mag. and collr., Orissa, March, 1937; dir. of devel., etc., May, 1939; provl. press advr., Sept., 1939.

SUBBARAYAN, VIRARACHAVA, Indian Police (b. 28th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 12th Apl., 1926; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1931; to Dec., 1943.

SUBBAYANAYUDU, RAMISETTI, Diwan Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. March, 1885).—Joined the service, 30th Apl., 1905; dep. collr., Oct., 1918; sub-collr., Aug., 1932; offg. collr., June, 1931; joint secty., local self-govt. dept., Madras, Sept., 1934; commr., corporation of Madras, Jan., 1937; retd., March, 1940.

SUBERWAL, BANKE LAL, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 14th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Dec., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1933; confd., Jan., 1936; retd., Aug., 1944; re-apptd. temp. ch. engr., Aug., 1944.

SUBHEDAR, GANPAT LAXMAN, B.A., late Judicial Dept., Central Provs. (b. 26th Apl., 1876).—Retd., Apl., 1936.*

SUBHEDAR, LAKSHMIKANT SADASHKO, B.A., LL.B., late Income Tax Dept., Central Provs. (b. 2nd Dec., 1889).—Retd., July, 1938.*

SUBHEDAR, TRIMBAK RAGHUNATH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 22nd Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 29th Jan., 1931; confd., Apl., 1933.

SUBRAMANYA AYYAR, TRIPUNITTURA SUBRAMANI, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th June, 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1922; asst. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Oct., 1926; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, March, 1927; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. rly., June, 1930; asst. ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Nov., 1930; dep. govt. exr., B. and N.W. and R.K. rlys., July, 1933; ditto, S.I. rly., Oct., 1937; dep. acct.-gen., Madras, Apl., 1936; dep. govt. examiner of accts., S.I. rly., Nov., 1937; govt. examiner of accts., M. & S.M. rly., Feb., 1938; dep. acct.-gen., Madras, Sept., 1938; dep. ch. accts. offr., N.W. rly., Nov., 1940.

SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, CHANDRASEKHARA, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 8th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. examr., 10th Aug., 1907; govt. examr. of accts., June, 1918; dept. auditor, E.B. rly., July, 1921; dep. acct.-gen., Madras, Oct., 1921; seer. ditto, Madras, June, 1923; dep. auditor, N.W. rly., March, 1925; ditto, E.B. rly., July, 1926; ditto, E.I. rly., May, 1928; offg. dep. dir. of rly., audit (insp.), Apl., 1929; offg. govt. examr. of accts., M. & S.M. rly., Apl., 1930; ch. auditor, E.B. and Burma rlys., and govt. exr. of accts., Assam Bengal rly., Feb., 1932; ch. auditor, G.I.P. rly., and govt. examr. of accts., B.B. and C.I. rly., Jan., 1935; retd., Oct., 1940.

SUBRAHMANYAM, ATHAMANAKOTTAI, M.B.E., M.A. (b. 26th June, 1901) (military accounts dept.).—Joined service as an offr. in cl. II, Apl., 1926; M.B.E., June, 1941; dep. fnl. advr., mil. fn., Oct., 1942.

SUBRAHMANYAM, NARAYANASWAMY, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 20th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Municipal High Sch., Mayavaram, and Madras Univ.; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937;

arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., March, 1939.

SUBRAHMANYAN, MANGUDY, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 5th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1910; asst. acctnt.-genl., May, 1911; dep. ditto, July, 1924; offg. dep. auditor-gen., March, 1929; acctnt.-gen., March, 1929; acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Aprl., 1932; ditto, Bengal, March, 1933; ditto, Cent. Provs., Aprl., 1938; acctnt.-gen., Cent. revs., Aprl., 1938; acctnt.-gen., Posts and Tels., Oct., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1943.

SUBRAHMANYAN, NATESA, B.A., Indian C.S. (name changed to MANI, N. S. (q.v.)).

SUBRAMANIAN, KAVASSERI NARAYANA IYER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 4th June, 1909).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec., July, 1938; dep. sec. to govt., Aug. 1, 1944.

SUBRAMANYAN, MINAKSHISUNDARAM VENKATA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sec., educn. and public health dept., Madras) (b. 8th July, 1900).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., Aug., 1926; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1931; sec., educn. and public health dept., Aprl., 1942; again, Aprl., 1943.

SUBRAMANIAN, VAIDYANATHIER VENKATA, Indian C.S. (dep. sec., fin. dept., Madras) (b. 17th May, 1908).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., July, 1932; addtl. under-sec., public dept., Sept., 1939; dep. sec., educn. dept., Feb., 1941; dep. sec., fin. dept., Aprl., 1944.

SUD, AMAR NATH, Rai Bahadur, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th July, 1890).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 9th Sept., 1913; asst. engr., March, 1915; exec. engr., 9th March, 1924; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), July, 1940.

SUDHANSU KUMAR HALDAR (see Haldar, S. K.).

SUDHINDRA KUMAR HALDAR (see Haldar, S. K.).

SUFTI, MOHAMMED HUSAIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 13th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Pindi Bhatian, Punjab Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 6th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

SUHRAWARDY, SIR HASSAN, O.B.E., M.D. (b. 4th Jan., 1884).—Served for 7 years 5½ months as gaz. medl. offcr. under govt. of Bengal; apptd. as dist. medl. offcr., E.I. rly., Aprl., 1916; O.B.E., June, 1927; ch. medl. and health offcr., Indian State Rlys., 1928-1937; K.-i.-H. Medal, 1st class, June, 1930; vice-chancellor, Calcutta Univ., 1930-1934; kt., Feb., 1932; LL.D. (Hon. Lond.), 1933; D.Sc. (Hon. Cal.), 1934; member, public services commn., Bengal, Aprl., 1937; advr. to the secy. of state for India, July, 1939, to July, 1944.

SUHRAWARDY, SIR ZAHNADUR RAHIM ZAHID, Kt., Barr.-at-law, late Judicial Dept., Bengal (b. 27th Nov., 1870).—Retd., Nov., 1931.*

SUKH, DAYAL, late Punjab Med. Service (b. 16th Oct., 1882).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

SUKHDEO PRASAD, TANDON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1914; asst. engr., Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1923; confd., Sept., 1924.

SUKTHANKAR, YESHWANT NARAYAN, C.I.E., B.A., LL.D., Barr., Indian C.S. (jt. secy., commerce dept., govt. of India) (b. 24th Aug., 1897).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at Cambridge Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 16th Dec., 1922, and served in Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aprl., 1927; under-secy. to govt., Cent. Provs., Oct., 1932; offg. rev. secy. to govt., Cent. Provs., June, 1933; dep. India trade commr., July, 1934; actg. Indian trade commr., Oct., 1934; and again, Sept., 1935; dep. secy., commerce dept., govt. of India, July, 1937; dep. commr. (confd.), Aug., 1937; tea contr. for India, Nov., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1941; jt. sec., commerce dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1943.

SULAIMAN, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Sulaiman).

SULE, RAMCHANDRA GOVIND, C.I.E., L.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., Bombay) (b. 17th May, 1888).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 1st Aprl., 1912; asst. engr., Aprl., 1913; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., Aprl., 1922; supt. engr., Aprl., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1943.

SULLIVAN, BASIL MARTIN, C.I.E., O.B.E., F.R.I.B.A., A.M.T.P.I. (b. 30th Aug., 1882).—Late architect to the Punjab govt.; retd., 1st Sept., 1938.*

SULLIVAN, EDMOND HERVEY, late Indian Police (b. 21st Nov., 1886).—Retd., July, 1937.*

SULLIVAN, MICHAEL JOHN KENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Provs.) (b. 22nd March, 1913).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Sept., 1941.

SULTAN ALI, NOON, MALIK, Indian Agric. Service (dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. 23rd March, 1895).—Joined the service as supy. dep. dir. of agric., 2nd Sept., 1918; dep. dir. of agric., June, 1924; dir., ditto, May, 1943.

SUMER CHAND, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 14th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as tempy. engr., 9th Sept., 1918; asst. engr. (provl. service), Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1923; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., Oct., 1930.

SUNDAR DAS, KHANGAR, Lala, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 24th Nov., 1897).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930.

SUNDAR SINGH, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 7th March, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 8th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Aprl., 1932.

SUNDARA RAJ, B., M.A., Ph.D. (b. 25th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1912, as piscicultural asst., ditto expert 12th Aprl., 1917; asst. dir. of fisheries (sub. pro. tem.), 7th Feb., 1918; asst. dir. of fisheries, inland, 1st Jan., 1919; actg. dir. of fisheries, 7th Aug., 1923; dir. of fisheries (perm.), 1st Nov., 1924.

SUNDARACHARI, RANGASWAMI, Dewan Bahadur, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 7th Apr., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

SUNDARAM CHETTI, KRISHNANAM CHETTIYAR, DIWAN BAHADUR, B.A., B.L., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 18th Nov., 1875).—Retd., Sept., 1935.*

SUNDARAM, RAO BAHADUR GANGADARA, M.A., A.M.I.E.E. (suptg. engr., elec. dept., Madras) (b. 14th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1926, as elec. asst.; confd. as exec. engr., Apr., 1933; suptg. engr. (confd.), Feb., 1940.

SUNDARAM, KUTHER VAIDYANATHA KALYAN, B.A. (Hons.) (Mad.), late Indian C.S. Cent. Provs. (b. 1st Jan., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, 15th Dec., 1927, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr., sub judge, Dec., 1931; regr. judl. commr.'s court, June, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1934; ditto (subs. provl.), Jan., 1936; offg. regr., high ct., Jan., 1936; attd. legis. dept., govt. of India, May, 1936; on spec. duty, reforms office, govt. of India, Sept., 1936, to Oct., 1938; addl. dep. secty., legis. dept., Oct., 1938; dep. ditto, Oct., 1939; addtl. ditto, Jan., 1940; retd., Nov., 1940; dep. sec., govt. of India (legis.), Aug., 1943.

SUNDARAM, RAMASWAMY MEENAKSHI, Indian C.S. (addtl. sec., fin. dept., Madras) (b. 22nd May, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1931; Diwan of Sivaganga, July, 1934 to Nov., 1938; addtl. sec., devel. dept., June, 1943.

SUNDARAM, VADUVUR SHRINIVAS, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 24th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as probnr., 21st Nov., 1908; asst. acct.-genl., May, 1909; asst. compr., India Treasuries, Apr., 1915; dep. ditto, March, 1916; examr., local fund accts., Nov., 1917; dep. acct.-genl., Apr., 1922; sec., central board of revenue, and under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., March, 1924; offg. commr. of inc. tax, Bihar and Orissa, Apr., 1930; dep. contr. of rly. accts., Nov., 1930; offg. ch. accts. offcr., N.W. rly., Nov., 1932; offg. dir. of finance, rly. bd., Jan., 1933; offg. ch. accts. offcr., E.I. rly., Sept., 1933; contr. of rly. accts., Jan., 1935; jt. secty., Niemeyer finl. enquiry, Oct., 1935; acct.-genl., Madras, May, 1939; retd., Nov., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

SUNDARARAMAN, SUBRAHMANYA, M.A., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 7th Nov., 1881).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

SUNDAR LAL, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Nov., 1882).—Retd., Nov., 1937.*

SUR, HRISHIKESH, O.B.E., B.A., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 2nd Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as apprentice asst. supt., 4th Oct., 1906; asst. supt., Dec., 1907; supt., Apr., 1917; asst. p.m.g., July, 1918; dep. ditto, Apr., 1920; divl. engr., Oct., 1921; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan, March, 1931; offg. dep. dir.-genl., tels., June, 1933; offg. dir., tel. engrg., Bengal and Assam, March, 1934; dir., tel. engrg., Dec., 1934; elec. engr. in ch., Dec., 1935; O.B.E., May, 1937; dep. dir.-genl., tels., Apr., 1938; offcr. on spl. duty, Oct., 1939.

SUR, SACHINDRA NATH, M.B., D.P.H., Dept. of Public Health, Bengal (asst. dir. of public health) (b. 4th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service

as offg. dep. sany. commr., Dec., 1916; asst. dir. of public health, Dec., 1918.

SURATH NATH, CHANDHURI, RAI BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Orissa) (b. 30th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1910, and held non-gazetted appt. prior to 12th Apr., 1924; asst. engr., Apr., 1924; apptd. to I.S.E., 1st Jan., 1930, as asst. exec. engr.; offg. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1930; confd., Apr., 1935; under-secty., govt. of Orissa, p.w.d., Sept., 1939.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, M.A., Ph.D., B.Litt. (Oxon.), F.R.Hist.S. (dir. of archives, govt. of India) (b. 29th July, 1890).—Educ. at Dacca Coll., Calcutta and Oxford Univs.; profr. of history and English literature, Robertson Coll., Jubbulpore, 1916-17; lecturer in Maratha history and Marathi literature, Calcutta Univ., 1917-1931; Ashutosh profr. of mediaeval and modern Indian history, Calcutta Univ., 1931; honorary advr. on records, govt. of Bengal, 1931; keeper of the records of the govt. of India, June, 1939; dir. of archives, ditto, March, 1944.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, M.A., LL.D., Judicial Dept., United Provs. (judge, high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. offg. judge, high ct., June, 1927; addl. judge, ditto, March, 1928; confd., Oct., 1930.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, Barr., late Judicial Dept., Burma.—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

SURYAPRAKASA RAO, V., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th March, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 8th Nov., 1930.

SUTCLIFFE, FREDERICK COWAN, Indian Police (b. 7th June, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Nov., 1938.

SUTHERLAND, JOHN WILLIAM ROSS, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 11th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1922; lecturer in forestry, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, Nov., 1924, to Apr., 1926; dep. consvr., Apr., 1926.

SUTTON, NOEL DUDLEY TAYLER, Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 20th Dec., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Punjab, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

SUTTON, STANLEY CECIL, M.Sc. (Econ.), India Office (b. 23rd Sept., 1907).—Educ. at Tottenham County Sch., London Sch. of Econ. (Graham Wallas prizeman), and Univ. Coll., London (Sir John Macalister Medallist); senr. asst., Brit. lib. of pol. and econ. science, 1931-35; asst. keeper, library, India Office, 17th June, 1935; on loan to E. and O. dept., from 1939.

SUTTON, WARWICK EDWARD, late Indian Police (b. 21st March, 1882).—Retd., March, 1937.*

SWAIN, LEO HARRY, Indian State Rlys. (dir., rly. bd.) (b. 31st Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as bridge engr., 28th March, 1920; asst. contr. of standardisation, July, 1932; offg. dep. ch. contr., central standards office, Apr., 1934; confd., Apr., 1935; dep. ch. engr. (bdgs.), Aug., 1935; dir., rly. bd. (civ. engrg.), Nov., 1938.

SWAMINATHAN, THINNIAM SUBRAHMANYA, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 23rd Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1931.

SWAMINATHAN, THIRUMALRAYA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1912).—Educ. at Pachaiyappa's High Sch., Chidambaran, Madras Univ., Univ.

Coll., London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

SWANN, ROBERT SWINNEY, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 17th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh Univ., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 16th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939; and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Orissa, Nov., 1939; jt. mag. and dep. collr.; dep. sec., govt. of Orissa, home dept.

SWANZY, ROBERT EDWARD, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (commr., Bihar) (b. 17th July, 1888).—Educ. at Rugby, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service, Oct., 1915, to Apl., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1924; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. commr., March, 1936; confd., Oct., 1940; spec. offcr., pol. dept., Apl., 1941; offg. member, bd. of rev., Apl., 1944.

SWEENEY, JAMES AUGUSTINE, C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 25th May, 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1934.*

SWEET, JACK McMURDO, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Madras) (b. 20th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1923; confd. as consvr., March, 1943.

SWIFT, GERALD ARTHUR, Indian Police (b. 13th July, 1895) (supt., United Provs.).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Nov., 1920; offg. supt., Sept., 1923; confd., June, 1930.

SWINNERTON, ALBERT RICHARD, India Office (actg. princpl., milly. dept.) (b. 6th Apl., 1896).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 3rd Aug., 1921; exec. offcr., Jan., 1929; actg. hr. ditto, Aug. to Oct., 1938; confd., July, 1939; actg. asst. admin. offcr., Apl., 1940; actg. princpl., 1942.

SWINSTEAD, NORMAN HILLYARD, M.A. (Cantab.), M.B.E. (Mil. div.), India Posts and Tels. (dir. of tels.) (b. 21st March, 1891).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1920; divl. engr., wireless, 3rd Oct., 1921; asst. dir.-genl., wireless, May, 1930; offg. dir., Aug., 1932; asst. dir.-genl., Oct., 1935; div. engr., Feb., 1936; dir. of tels., Aug., 1940; confd., dep. dir.-genl., tels., May, 1944.

SWITHINBANK, BERNARD WINTHROP, C.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Oct., 1884).—Educ. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. offcr.; dep. commr. (prov. subs.), March, 1924; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. secty., local govt. dept., Aug., 1929; offg. commr., Apl., 1933; perm., Jan., 1937; C.B.E., Jan., 1938; chairman, bribery and corruption enq. cttee., Burma, Dec., 1939, to May, 1940; spec. duty educn. dept., and dept. of health and public wks., June, 1940; commr., Nov., 1940; ret'd., Aug., 1942; apptd. advr. to sec. of state for Burma, Aug., 1942.

SYED ABDUL VAHID, B.A., B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 20th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr. and silviculturist, Jan., 1927; ret'd., 4th Aug., 1942.

SYED FIDA HASSAN (see Hassan, Syed Fida).

SYED ISKANDER ALI MIRZA, SAHIBZADA,

C.I.E., O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 13th Nov., 1899).—First commn., 16th July, 1920; asst. mag. and collr., Aligarh, Aug., 1926; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, March, 1927; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Apl., 1928; ditto, Tonk, May, 1928; ditto, Bannu, Apl., 1930; ditto, Nowshera, Apl., 1931; dep. commr., Hazara, May, 1933; asst. commr., Marden, Oct., 1936; dep. commr., ditto, Jan., 1937; pol. agent, Khyber, Apl., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1939; dep. commr., Peshawar, March, 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

SYKES, THE RT. HON. SIR FREDERICK HUGH, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., G.B.E., K.C.B., C.M.G., Maj.-Gen. (ret'd.).—Govr. of Bombay from 8th Dec., 1928, to Dec., 1933.

SYKES, JOHN FRANCIS GASKELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. asst. and comdt., Border Mily. Police, Apl., 1936; sub. div. offcr., June, 1937; asst. colonization offcr., Oct., 1938; famine offcr., March, 1940; on for service, Bahawalpur State, Jan., 1941, to 1944.

SYLVESTER, HAROLD FRASER, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 15th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 12th Jan., 1909; asst. engr., Jan., 1923; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1928; exec. engr., Jan., 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1940.

SYMINGTON, DAVID, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govr., Bombay) (b. 4th July, 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and supt. of stamps, May, 1929; offg. collr., Jan., 1933; backward class offcr., Bombay presdy., Nov., 1937; confd. as collr., Dec., 1936; jt. sec. and sec. to govt., Bombay (home) and a.r.p. contr., var. from Apl., 1942, to Aug., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1943; sec. to govr., Sept., 1943.

SYMNS, JOHN MONTFORT, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Jan., 1879).—Ret'd., Feb., 1935.*

SYMON, ALEXANDER COLIN BURLINGTON, O.B.E., India Office (asst. princpl.) (b. 13th May, 1902).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 22nd Apl., 1920; asst. secty., Indian deleg. to the disarmament conf., 1932-33; exec. offcr., March, 1933; asst. princpl., Nov., 1934; secty., Indian deleg. to London naval conf., 1935-36; pte. secty. to permnt. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1938; actg. princpl., Feb., 1939; services loaned to the Ministry of Information, Sept., 1939, to Nov., 1939; on loan to govt. of India, Aug., 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

SYMONS, NOEL VICTOR HOUSMAN, C.I.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (secty., rev., etc., dept., Bengal) (b. 27th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at King Edward VI Sch., Bath; joined the service, 26th Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. mag., March, 1926; offg. magte. and collr., Apl., 1927; offg. dep. commr., March, 1929; offg. secty. to bd. of rev., Bengal; confd., May, 1932; offg. pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, Sept., 1934; mag. and collr., July, 1935; secty., rev., etc. dept., Bengal; confd., Aug., 1938; a.r.p. contr., Dec., 1940; confd. mag. and collr., Aug., 1941; addl. sec., p.h. dept., Oct., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

SYMONS, RONALD STUART, Indian C.S. (dep. secty., fin. dept., govt. of India) (b. 24th

July, 1904).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept., Dec., 1937; dept. secty., fin. dept., Apr., 1938; offcr. on spl. duty, dept. of supply, Aug., 1940; jt. finl. advr. (m.p.), Oct., 1940; addtl. finl. advr., mil. fin. and ex-officio joint sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, March, 1943.

SYMONS, SIR THOMAS HENRY, K.B.E., C.S.I., K.H.S., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 17th May, 1872).—Retd., Sept., 1930.*

SYNGE, GEORGE BERNARD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 11th Feb., 1903).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and apptt. depts., Jan., 1930; confd., March, 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1933; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), Apr., 1939.

SYNNOTTI, NEVILLE WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. contr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 31st July, 1869).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 14th July, 1909; supt., July, 1925; offg. dep. ch. operg. supt. (movement), March, 1935; dep. ch. contr., movements, Apr., 1941.

TAELA, PADAM CHAND, T.C.E., Coll., Roorkee, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr. (prov.), March, 1938.

TAIT, DOUGLAS WALTER, India Office (suptg. exec. offcr., acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 12th Aug., 1889).—Served as 2nd divn. clerk (customs), from Nov., 1907; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 6th July, 1909, and attached to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; transf. to library, March, 1913; on milty. service from March, 1917, to Nov., 1919; higher clerical offr., Apr., 1922; higher exec. offcr., Apr., 1926; assessor of inc. tax, Oct., 1936; actg. suptg. exec. offcr., Nov., 1937; confd., Jan., 1938.

TAJAL ISLAM MUHAMMAD NURANABI CHAUDHURI, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 8th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., Calcutta; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1927; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933; dir., rural reconstrn., Bengal, March, 1938; sec., public health, etc., dept., Oct., 1940; to Dec., 1943.

TAKAKHAV, NILKANTH SADASHIV, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 13th Aug., 1881).—Retd., Aug., 1936.*

TAKLE, GANESH GOVIND, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 14th Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 4th Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Feb., 1930.

TALBOT, REV. ARNOLD DIXON, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 28th Dec., 1881).—Retd., Apr., 1935.*

TALEYARKHAN, KAIKHUSRO MANEKSHAH, Barr., late Judl. Dept., Bombay.—Retd., Apr., 1938.*

TALIA MUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, RANA, B.A., late Indian Police (b. 14th Dec., 1884).—Retd., 1937.*

TALLENTS, PHILLIP CUBIT, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 13th Apr., 1886).—Retd., Aug., 1939.*

TALUKDAR, JATINDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd July, 1901).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Wadhams Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 1st Dec., 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1932; addl. secty. to govt., pol. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1935; dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India, 1938 to July, 1940; mag. and collr. (confd.), Apr., 1939; contr. of emigrant labour, Nov., 1940; spec. offcr., home dept., Feb., 1945.

TAMBLING, REV. FRANCIS GORDON HILL, late Indian Ecclesl. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—Retd., July, 1939.*

TAMHANE, VINAYAK ATMARAM, M.Sc., M.Ag., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 4th May, 1884).—Retd., May, 1939.*

TAMPOE, ALFRED McGOWAN COOMARASWAMI, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 25th June, 1881).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

TANDAN, BALWANT RAI, Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 8th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1933; and again, Feb., 1936; ditto (prov. subs.), Dec., 1936; dep. secty., rev. dept., govt. of Punjab, Oct., 1939; dep. commr. (confd.), May, 1941.

TANDON, SUKHDEO PRASAD (see Sukhdeo, P. T.).

TANDY, SIR EDWARD ALDBOROUGH, Kt., Brigadier, R.E. (ret'd.), late Survey of India Dept.—Retd., Dec., 1928.*

TANDY, MAURICE PATRICK O'CONNOR, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th May, 1912).—First commn., 28th Jan., 1932; under training in Rajputana, 8th Nov., 1936; ditto, pol. dept. sect., May, 1937; under-sec. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, June, 1937; under trng. Punjab, Nov., 1937; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Zabul, June, 1939; confd. in Indian Pol. Serv., Dec., 1939; asst. pol. agt., Bahrein, Nov., 1944.

TANNAN, MOHAN LAL, M.Com. (Bir.), Barr., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 2nd May, 1885).—Held various appts. in provl. service from 2nd Jan., 1915, to 31st Aug., 1920; apptd. princpl. and profr. of banking, Sydenham Coll. of Com. and Economics, Bombay, Nov., 1920; secty., Indian accountancy bd., March, 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1940.

TANNER, JOCELYN THEODORE, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bombay) (b. 6th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1910, as asst. supt.; on milty. duty from July, 1917, to Apr., 1919; supt. of police, Jan., 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1932; confd., supt., sel. grade, Dec., 1940; confd. dep. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1943.

TANNER, MAURICE OSBORNE, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 3rd Dec., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 29th Nov., 1913; dist. supt., May, 1918; awarded King's police medal, March, 1929; O.B.E., Jan., 1935.

TANNER, RICHARD LIONEL, Indian State Rlys. (production engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 22nd Oct., 1888).—Apptd. production engr., E.I. rly., 1st Nov., 1927.

TAPENDRA KUMAR GHOSH CHAUDHURI (see Ghosh Chaudhuri, T. K.).

TAQI, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Taqi).

TARAK, NATH GHOSH, Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Jan., 1937.

TARAKAD GOPALAKRISHNA NARAYANA AYYAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 15th Feb., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 18th Oct., 1926; arrived, 7th Dec., 1926, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936.

TARA SINGH, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st June, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

TARGETT, SIR ROBERT WILLIAM, C.I.E. (dep. dir. genl. of supply) (b. 23rd Feb., 1891).—Joined the service, 22nd May, 1918; asst. contr. clothing, Indian Munitions Bd. (tempy.); employed in I.M.B. and surplus stores organsn., up to Dec., 1921; contr. of leather and textile purchase, Indian stores dept. (which post was subsequently converted to dep. dir. of purchase), Jan., 1922; ch. contr. of stores, Apl., 1939; dep. dir.-genl. of supply, March, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; addtl. dir.-genl., supply, July, 1942; dir. gen. supply and disp., Oct., 1943; kt., Jan., 1945.

TARLOK, SINGH, B.A., B.Sc. (Econ.), Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 26th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at St. Vincent's High Sch., Poona; Bombay Univ., and London Sch. of Economics; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. finl. advr., supply fin., Dec., 1943; under-sec., fin. dept., Jan., 1944.

TASKER, SIR THEODORE JAMES, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 20th Jan., 1884).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., Apl., 1913, to March, 1915; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1916; dist. mag. and collr., Bangalore, Nov., 1917; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; commr., Coorg, Dec., 1923; on for. service under Nizam's govt., Jan., 1927; collr., March, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; kt., May, 1937; third member, board of rev., Madras, March, 1938; on for. service under Nizam's govt., March, 1939; supervisor, I.C.S. prob., July, 1942; retd., Nov., 1944.

TATE, JOHN CREEVY, Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 14th Aug., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1937.*

TATE, THOMAS BAILEY, C.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 11th Dec., 1882).—Retd., Dec., 1938.*

TAUNTON, IVON HOPE, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (advr. to govr., Bombay) (b. 19th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Sind as asst. mag. and collr.; on mill. service, Nov., 1917, to Apl., 1919; offg. collr. and dis. mag., May, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1925; chairman, cattle theft commn., Dec., 1925; offg. collr. and supt. of stamps, Jan., 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt., home and eccl. depts., Apl., 1926; offg. dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., June, 1927; in foreign service as finl. and rev. member,

Khairpur State Exec. council, Oct., 1927; offg. collr., Sholapur, and pol. agent, Akalkot, Nov., 1932; collr., Dec., 1932; municip. commr. for city of Bombay, July, 1934; ch. secty. to govt. of Sind, home, legal, etc., depts.; member, Sind rev. tribunal, Jan., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; advr. to govt., Bombay, Dec., 1942.

TAVENER, EDWARD SYDNEY BEAUMONT, M.A., Dip. of Forestry, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 29th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1923, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Jan., 1924.

TAYLOR, ALFRED GEORGE TUTTON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

TAYLOR, BASIL CHURTON, late Indian Police (Central Provs.) (b. 19th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1910; dist. supt. and princpl., police trng. sch., Saugor, Jan., 1923; on depn. to Indore as inspr.-genl. from Apl., 1926, to Oct., 1930; and again, March, 1932; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1936; on depn. to Jaipur State as inspr.-genl. of police, June, 1939; retd., 19th Aug., 1944.

TAYLOR, BERTRAM FENWICK, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Assam) (b. 10th March, 1881).—Retd., March, 1936.*

TAYLOR, BRUCE HENRY, Indian Police, Bombay (supt.) (b. 18th Feb., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 19th Nov., 1931; confd., Feb., 1934; confd. as supt., Aug., 1942; dep. commr., C.I.D., Aug., 1942, to June, 1943; asst. comdt., Sind police rifles, Jan., 1945.

TAYLOR, CECIL GEORGE, late Impl. Customs Service (b. 11th Nov., 1881).—Retd., Nov., 1936.*

TAYLOR, ERNEST BERTRAM NEAVE, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 2nd Oct., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 20th Oct., 1910; exec. engr., May, 1919; offg. supt. of works, Dec., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1930, and again, Dec., 1932; offg. senr. govt. inspr., Oct., 1934; dep. ch. engr. (prov.), Nov., 1935; confd., Apl., 1938; ch. engr., provl., Oct., 1941; confd., June, 1941; retd., Oct., 1943.

TAYLOR, E. MCKENZIE, M.B.E., Ph.D. (Cantab.), D.Sc. (Durham), F.I.C., late Public Works Dept., Punjab—Apptd. dir., irrign. research, Punjab, 17th Nov., 1930; retd., Nov. 13th, 1944.

TAYLOR, FRANCIS ERNEST ALFRED, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Orissa.) (b. 16th Sept., 1903).—Educ. at Glenalmond, Univ. of St. Andrews, and Merton Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927, arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1929; sec. to govt., Orissa, July, 1940; sec., rev. and dev. depts., March, 1942; mag. and collr., July, 1942.

TAYLOR, FRANK HERBERT GRAHAM, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Central Provs.) (b. 17th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., March, 1910; dist. supt., Nov., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1936; dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1941; on depn. to H.E.H. the Nizam's govt., Jan., 1942.

TAYLOR, HENRY JOHN BOSANQUET, B.Sc.,

Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 23rd Apl., 1909).—Educ. at the Northern Secondary Sch., Portsmouth, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub divl. offcr., Apl., 1934; adminstr., Lahore municipal cttee., etc., Aug., 1940.

TAYLOR, SIR JOHN, C.I.E., D.S.O., V.H.S., M.D., D.P.H., Col. (actg. Maj.-Gen., I.M.S.) (retd.) (dir., central research inst., Kasauli) (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1908; arrived in India, 28th Feb., 1907; in milty. employ from 26th Feb., 1907, to 31st Aug., 1909; in civil employ in Bombay from 1st Sept., 1909, to 15th Oct., 1914; reverted to milty. duty from 16th Oct., 1914, to 5th Aug., 1920; offg. sanitary commr., Assam, Aug., 1920; offg. asst. dir., Pasteur Inst., Rangoon, March, 1923; offg. dir., ditto, March, 1925; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. dir., Haffkine inst., Feb., 1932; dir., central research inst., Kasauli, Oct., 1932; C.I.E., May, 1937; kt., June, 1942.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WILLIAM, Indian State Ryis. (ch. engr., E.B. Ry.) (b. 16th March, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., March, 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Oct., 1935; confd., Nov., 1937; ch. engr., Dec., 1940.

TAYLOR, SAMUEL, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 23rd Feb., 1883).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

TAYLOR, STANLEY GRISEWOOD, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 19th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 2nd Dec., 1913; addl. supt., March, 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Aug., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; princpl. police training coll., Sardah, Jan., 1936; confd. in sel. gr., Apl., 1941.

TAYLOR, WALTER, India Office (actg. princpl., econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 29th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 5th Aug., 1914, and attached to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; on mil. service from July, 1915, to Jan., 1919; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; jt. editor, India Office List, Nov., 1934; actg. hr. exec. offcr., Sept., 1935; confd., Oct., 1936; attd. finl. dept., Dec., 1935; editor, India Office and Burma Office List, Nov., 1937, to March, 1940; attd. Oct., 1938, to expert cttee. on the defence of India, 1938-39; asst. administrative offcr., June, 1939; lent to Air Ministry, March, 1940; actg. princpl., Air Min., May, 1940; actg. princpl., India Office (in absentia), Nov., 1943; returned to India Office, Jan., 1945.

TAYLOR, WALTER, D.V.H., M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Veterinary Service (Punjab) (b. 25th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as princpl. and profr. of med., vety. coll., Punjab, 21st March, 1913; offg. dir., civil vety. dept., Punjab, March, 1929; offg. dir., vety. services, Punjab, Apl., 1932; offg. dir., impl. inst. of vety. research, Nov., 1933; retd., Aug., 1940.

TAYLOR, WILFRED REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., late Indian State Ryis. (Burma) (b. 18th March, 1881).—Retd., March, 1938.*

TEGART, SIR CHARLES AUGUSTUS, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.V.O., late Indian Police Service (Bengal) (b. 5th Oct., 1881).—Retd., Dec., 1931*; member of council of India, Dec., 1931, to Dec., 1936.

TEJ NARAYAN MULLA, M.A., LL.B. (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. July, 1887).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1921, as addl.

dist. and sess. judge; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1923; offg. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936; judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1940.

TEJA SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, Sardar, B.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S., Judcl. Bch. (judge, Lahore high ct.) (b. 1st Jan., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th July, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; addtl. judge, Sept., 1943, to beginning of summer vacation, 1944; judge, high ct., Lahore, Apl., 1944.

TEJASWI PRASAD BHALLA, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 11th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt. United Provs., 24th May, 1923; offg. supt., July, 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; supt., June, 1935; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D. (Gen.), Apl., 1936; O.S.D., at govt. hdqtrs., Nov., 1937; to Jan., 1940.

TEK CHAND, SIR BAKHSHI, M.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Punjab.—Apptd. judge, high ct., Lahore, 27th Jan., 1927; retd., 20th Aug., 1943.

TELFER, GEORGE HENRY, late Indian State Ryis., Burma (b. 11th May, 1883).—Retd., 11th Nov., 1940.*

TEMPLE, JOHN, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 12th May, 1882).—Retd., March, 1938.*
