TEN-BROEKE, BERTRAM ST. LEGER, M.C., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bihar) (b. 8th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th March, 1921; supt., July, 1925; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937; dep. inspr.-genl., July, 1940.

TENNANT, WILLIAM ROBERT, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (b. 26th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ.; arrived, 30th Nov., 1919, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mgr. and collr.; dep. supt. of census, March, 1921; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1923; dep. acctnt.-genl., Bombay, Nov., 1924; ditto, United Provs., Nov., 1925; addl. dep. sec., govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1927; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Dec., 1929; confd., March, 1930; offg. finl. advr., milty. fin., Apl., 1933; acctnt.-genl., Bombay, Oct., 1934; dir. of audit, def. services, Oct., 1937; dep. aud.-genl., India, provl., Aug., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; aud.-genl. of India (substantive), Aug., 1942; dep. ditto, Oct., 1942; dir. of rly. audit, Dec., 1944.

TERRY, DOUGLAS EDGAR, India Office (hr. clerical offcr.) (b. 15th May, 1899).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 1st June, 1920, after previous service from 2nd May, 1917; hr. clerical offcr., Nov., 1935; actg. staff offcr. for services questions, Aug., 1943.

TEULON, OLIVER HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 29th June, 1883).—Retd., June, 1938.*

THA, MAUNG CHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (off. dep. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Minbu, Rangoon Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., dept. of comm. and indus., June, 1940; offg. dep. commr., July, 1941.

THACKER, PITAMBERDAS LAVJI, B.A., LL.B., Bombay, late Judicial Dept. (b. 31st July, 1881).—Retd., July, 1936.*

THACKWELL, ALBERT HENRY, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 19th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 19th June, 1920; asst. works manager, July, 1920; works

manager, June, 1925; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., July, 1932, May, 1933, and again, Apl., 1935; confd., Jan., 1937; retd., Apl., 1941.

THADANI, NENUMAL POHOOMAL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Educ. in India and at Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to I.C.S. by nomination; arrived, 4th Jan., 1917, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. offcr.; jt. mag., July, 1922; addl. dep. commr., June, 1925; magte. and collr. (prov. sub.), Dec., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933; retd., March, 1943.

THADANI, THAKURDAS VASANMAL (judge, ch. ct., Sind).—Apptd. Aug., 1944.

THAKUR, KESHAR SADASHIV, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Bengal) (b. 2nd Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 31st July, 1909; in milty. employ up to 22nd Feb., 1921; offg. civil surgeon, Bengal, Sept., 1921; ditto, 24th Parganas and protector of emigrants, Calcutta, Feb., 1934.

THAMBAN, VENGANAD KOVILAGAM RAJAGOPALA (see Rajagopala Thamban, V. K.).

THAPAR, PREM NATH, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 13th Apl., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1933; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), July, 1935; ditto, perm., May, 1940; dep. sec., govt. of India (infn. and broadcasting), Nov., 1941; jt. sec., ditto, Sept., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1944.

THAW, ALFRED BA, M.B., Maj., I.M.S., Burma Medl. Dept. (civil surg.) (b. 13th March, 1892).—First commn. dated 15th Jan., 1918; in milty. employ prior to 29th May, 1924; asst. medl. supt., Rangoon genl. hosp., June, 1924; civil surg., March, 1926.

THEIN, MAUNG KYAW, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., customs) (b. 10th June, 1910).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Akyab, Rangoon Univ., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. collr., customs, Nov., 1937.

THEIN, U BA HLA, late Burma Judl. Service (b. 24th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 11th Jan., 1909; asst. judge, July, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; confd., Nov., 1937; retd., Dec., 1940.

THIRUMALAI IYENGAR, MANDAYAM ANNADHORAI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 6th Nov., 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934; dist. judge, Aug., 1938; mag. and collr., July, 1943.

THIRUVEN KATA CHARI, R., Indian C.S., name changed to R. T. CHARI (q.v.)

THOM, HERBERT JAMES, C.I.E., M.C., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 24th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., May, 1923; confd., May, 1929; services lent to H.H. the Maharaja Bahadur of Benares from Feb., 1927; supt., Dec., 1931; princpl., police trng. sch., Apl., 1936, to Dec., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; asst. inspr.-gen., July, 1940.

THOMAS, ALAN ROBERT, late Indian Forest Service (Assam) (b. 26th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 22nd Dec., 1911; in mil. employ from 24th Sept., 1915, to 11th Oct., 1919; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg.

consvr., Jan., 1933, to Feb., 1933; retd., Jan., 1943.

THOMAS, ANTHONY RYLANDS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 8th Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1938; dep. dir., cent. irrig. and hydro. research stn., Poona, May, 1940.

THOMAS, ARNOLD NEWALL, D.S.O., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 7th Aug., 1880).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

THOMAS, BERNARD GORDON PROTHERO, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1906; supt., Apl., 1920; asst. inspr.-genl., rly. police, March, 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1933; O.B.E., Jan., 1934; dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936.

THOMAS, EDWARD FRANCIS, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 30th June, 1880).—Retd., Feb., 1938.*

THOMAS, GEOFFREY BENION, M.B., Ch.B., M.Sc. (Sheffield), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), M.R.C.O.G. (Eng.), Capt., I.M.S., Madras Medical Dept. (b. 28th Jan., 1905).—First commn. dated Feb., 1933; in milty. employ until June, 1938; specialist in obstetrics with gynaecology, June, 1938; supt., govt. hosp. for women and children, Aug., 1942.

THOMAS, GEOFFREY DAVID IVOR, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 21st Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; settlt. offcr., Nov., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1927.

THOMAS, GEORGE ARTHUR, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 4th May, 1877).—Retd., Feb., 1935.*

THOMAS, SIR GEORGE HECTOR, Barr.-at-law, Judicial Dept., United Provs. (chief judge, ch. ct., Oudh) (b. 23rd June, 1884).—Apptd. govt. advocate, ch. court, Lucknow, Nov., 1925; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, May, 1934; ch. judge, ditto, July, 1938; kt., Jan., 1941.

THOMAS, JOHN HERBERT, C.I.E., F.I.A., govt. of India, Commerce Dept. (supt. of insur.) (b. 21st Feb., 1895).—Apptd. to home civil service, 1914; services loaned to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, as supt. of insurance, June, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

THOMAS, VINCENT EUSTACE, A.M.I.Struct.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 16th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as temp. engr., G.I.P. rly., 17th Aug., 1916; resdt. engr., May, 1922; exec. engr., G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1935; retd., Aug., 1942.

THOMPSON, Miss Amy, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 5th Nov., 1883).—Retd., Nov. 1938.*

THOMPSON, BRIAN BELL, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 16th June, 1898).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; supt. of police, Central India agency, Apl., 1927; supt., Nov., 1932; awarded Indian police medal, July, 1936.

THOMPSON, CLARENCE EDWARD, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Aug., 1878).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

THOMPSON, CLAUDE MATTHEW, Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret.), late Survey of India (b. 21st Sept., 1883).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

THOMPSON, COLIN HUSTWICK, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 20th March, 1901).—Retd., March, 1935.*

THOMPSON, EDWARD HENRY, M.C., Office of High Commr. for India (hr. exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 30th Oct., 1889).—Apptd. second class clerk, 30th June, 1908; on mil. service from Jan., 1916; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; attached Indian del., Imp. econ. conf., July to Sept., 1932; hr. exec. offcr., Dec., 1935; actg. princpl., ditto, Apl., 1940; progress offcr. for medl. stores, Apl., 1941.

THOMPSON, ERIC BERTRAM, M.B.E., late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 21st July, 1887).—Joined as proxy. supt., 25th Apl., 1908; confd., Dec., 1909; on depn. to I.E.F., Aug., 1914, to Apl., 1919; dep. p.m.g., July, 1935; p.m.g., Madras circle, Dec., 1940; retd., March, 1943.

THOMPSON, REV. FREDERICK ARTHUR, B.A., Indian Ecclesi. Estabt., Bombay (b. 11th Nov., 1911).—Apptd. junr. chaplain on probn., 18th Dec., 1939.

THOMPSON, HENRY WALTER, Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 10th Dec., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939; a.d.c. to govt., Supt., Dec., 1942, to Dec., 1943.

THOMPSON, JOSEPH HERBERT, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 9th March, 1898).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1923; arrived, 12th Dec., 1923, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1924; offg. asst. commr., D.I. Khan, July, 1926; city mag., Peshawar, Dec., 1926; dist. mag., ditto, Jan., 1928; asst. commr., Nowshera, Sept., 1929; divl. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Aug., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar and Hazara, Aug., 1931; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1932; secty. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1934; resdt., Jaipur, March, 1938; pol. agent, E. Rajputana States, Dec., 1938; resdt., Jaipur (in addn.), May, 1939, to Oct., 1939; addtl. dep. secty., pol. dept., March, 1941; dep. secty., ditto, Sept., 1941; resdt., Kolhapur and Deccan States, Sept., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

THOMPSON, MALCOLM STUART HAUGHTON, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras) (b. 20th March, 1885).—Joined the service, 6th Jan., 1910; asst. inspr. of schs., Dec., 1911; inspr., Sept., 1922; dist. educl. offcr., Feb., 1923; offg. princpl., govt. trng. coll., Rajahmundry, Nov., 1927; offg. inspr., schools, Coorg and Bangalore, Feb., 1936; princpl., govt. Muhammadan Coll., Madras, Feb., 1937; retd., March, 1940.

THOMPSON, MICHAEL JOSEPH, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 11th Oct., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

THOMPSON, SAMUEL PERCY, O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 16th Dec., 1902).—Educ. at St. Olave's and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub. collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1928; asst. secty., Indian franchise cttee., Jan., 1932; dist. and sess. judge (confd.), Feb., 1940; regr., high ct., Dec., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

THOMPSON, WILLIAM PERCY, M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Jan., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

THOMSON, ALEXANDER WELSH, O.B.E., India Office (ch. exec. offcr., mil. dept.) (b. 26th Dec., 1878).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 12th Nov., 1900; temp. attached to mil. dept. from Dec.,

1915; minor staff clerk, Apl., 1918; staff clerk (actg.), Nov., 1919; dep. reading clerk to council, Feb., 1920, to Feb., 1923; higher exec. offcr., June, 1922; supt. exec. offcr., June, 1924; asst. acct.-genl., Dec., 1934; assessor of inc. tax, from Dec., 1934, to Dec., 1937; actg. ch. exec. offcr., mil. dept., Nov., 1937; confd., Jan., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1938; actg. princpl., milty. dept., June, 1940.

THOMSON, ERIC SCOTT, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Apl., 1926; confd., July, 1933.

THOMSON, GEORGE, late Indian State Rys. (b. 3rd July, 1882).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

THOMSON, JAMES, India Office (actg. princpl. econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 1st Apl., 1895).—Apptd. exec. offcr., 16th Aug., 1927; actg. hr. exec. offcr., March, 1935; confd., Feb., 1936; actg. asst. information offcr., Oct., 1936, and again, Dec., 1937, to Feb., 1938; actg. informm. offcr., Oct., 1937, to Dec., 1937; sec. admin. side, mil. dept., Feb. to Oct., 1938; attached to Burma Office, Jan., 1939; actg. princpl., Feb., 1940.

THOMSON, JOHN STUART, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W.F.P.) (b. 4th Aug., 1888).—Retd., July, 1937.*

THOMSON, RONALD ORD CAMPBELL, O.B.E., A.M.I.C.E., M.C., Indian State Rys. (exec. engr., N.W. Ry.) (b. 11th Dec., 1892).—Asst. exec. engr., 27th Nov., 1919; exec. engr., Apl., 1936; O.B.E., Jan., 1941.

THORNBURGH, DAVID GILL, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 7th Feb., 1911).—First commn., 27th Aug., 1931; under trng. in Rajputana, Oct., 1935; pers. asst. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1935; under trng. in Punjab, Jan., 1936; under trng. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1936; under trng. in Punjab, March, 1937; asst. pol. agent, Mekram, Sept., 1937; 2nd asst. pol. agent, Mekram (in addn.), June, 1938; under sec. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Oct., 1938; confd. in Indian Political Service, Feb., 1939; asst. commr., Peshawar, Oct., 1939; Brit. trade agt. at Gyantze and asst. to pol. offcr., Sikkim, Feb., 1940; asst. commr., Bannu, Aug., 1940; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Apl., 1941; ditto, Chitral, Oct., 1941; vice-consul, Zabul, Dec., 1944.

THORNE, HAROLD LIONEL, late Indian State Rys. (N.W. Ry.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, Sept., 1905; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1919; divl. transpn. offcr., May, 1935; retd., 6th July, 1944.

THORNE, SIR JOHN ANDERSON, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Indian C.S. (secty. to govr.-genl., public dept.) (b. 17th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov. 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; sec. to board of revenue, land revenue, March, 1920; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1922; ditto, May, 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1931; secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, Nov., 1931; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, home dept., June, 1935; addl. ditto, Oct., 1935; jt. secty., ditto, Apl., 1936; again, Nov., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; secty. to govr.-genl. (public), Apl., 1938; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, June to Oct., 1939; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1942; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. cl., Apl. to July, 1944.

THORNTON, FRANCIS RUTHVEN, M.C.,

M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 5th Aug., 1884).—Ret'd., Aug., 1939.*

THORNTON, NORMAN, M.I.E.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (dep. ch. engr.) (b. 5th March, 1894).—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1934; exec. engr., March, 1934; offg. dep. ch. engr., March, 1935; confd., Oct., 1940.

THOULESS, KENNETH JAMES, M.A. (Mech. Sc. Hons., Cantab.), Indian Posts and Tels. (dir. of tels.) (b. 13th May, 1900).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1923, as asst. div. engr., tels.; div. engr., Oct., 1932; dir., S. and B. circle, Aug., 1935; temp. transf. to def. dept., June, 1940; divl. engr., June, 1938; dir. of tels., Sept., 1940; on mil. service, June, 1940, to Aug., 1942.

THRUPP, WILFRED GRAHAM, late Indian Police (b. 26th Oct., 1892).—Ret'd., Nov., 1937.*

THURSFIELD, REV. GERALD ARTHUR RICHARD, M.A., late Indian Ecclesl. Estabt., Burma (b. 13th Apr., 1886).—Ret'd., Feb., 1938.*

TIKA RAM MISRA, RAI BAHADUR, PANDIT, M.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., United Provs. (b. 1st Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1906; sub-judge, Oct., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1934; ret'd., Feb., 1940.

TILNEY-BASSETT, REV. HUGH FRANCIS EMRA, M.A. (Oxon), Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 16th Jan., 1901).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bengal, 12th Aug., 1929; confd., Nov., 1931.

TIMS, BASIL DUNCAN, M.B.E., India Office (actg. princpl., finl. dept.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1898).—War service: R.N. Air Service and R. Navy, 1916-1919; apptd. clerical offcr., 19th Sept., 1921; on depn. to India in connection with Rl. commn. on agric. in India, Oct., 1927, to May, 1928; hr. clerical offcr., Apr., 1930; staff offcr. for currency duties, finl. dept., Dec., 1937; actg. admin. offcr. for currency duties and asst. govt. dir., rly. coys., July, 1943; M.B.E. June, 1943; actg. princpl., Feb., 1944.

TIN, MAUNG (A), Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 5th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., Burma, educn. dept., Dec., 1934; dep. secty., agric. and for. dept., Nov., 1937; jt. regr., co-ops., Sept., 1938; offg. regr., ditto, Feb., 1941.

TIN, MAUNG SEIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 15th June, 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Rangoon and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., March, 1939; dep. sec., home, def., and forest depts., Dec., 1940; provl. dep. commr., Feb., 1941; confd., Apr., 1941.

TIN, MAUNG THET, Burma Police.—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., from 4th Apr., 1941.

TIN TOON, MAUNG A. (see Toon, Maung Tin).

TIN TUT, MAUNG (see Tut, Maung Tin).

TIN, U PE MAUNG, M.A., B.Litt., Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th Apr., 1888).—Apptd. to the I.E.S. as lecturer in Oriental Studies, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, from 1st Sept., 1924.

TINSLEY, WILLIAM STEWART, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th Aug., 1890).—Ret'd., Apr., 1939.*

TINT, MAUNG BA, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., fin. dept., Burma) (b. 6th Nov., 1908).—Educ. at St. Peter's High Sch., and Kelly High Sch., Mandalay, Rangoon Univ., and the Sch.

of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., fin. dept., Nov., 1937; asst. import trade contr., Burma, June, 1940; asst. collr., customs, Aug., 1940.

TINT, SOE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 5th Nov., 1910).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Rangoon, Rangoon Univ., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr. on mil. service, Nov., 1940; hdqtrs. asst., Insein, March, 1941.

TIPPER, GEORGE HOWLETT, M.A. (Cantab.), F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 26th Feb., 1881).—Apptd. asst. supt., geological survey, 6th Dec., 1903; curator, geological museum, Calcutta, Aug., 1910; apptd. to I.A.R.O., May, 1915, to Dec., 1919; supt., Jan., 1920; ret'd., Sept., 1929; mineral advr. to High Commr. for India, Nov., 1928; loaned for service under Min. of Economic Warfare, Sept., 1939, to July, 1940; on spl. duty, Apr., 1941.

TIPSON, KENNETH GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 29th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 15th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., March, 1933; services placed at disposal of Benares State, Apr., 1937; supt. (confd.), Nov., 1939.

TIRUMALAI AYYANGAR, MALUR SRINIVASA AYYANGAR, R.B., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 1st Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 1st June, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1921; exec. engr., Jan., 1936.

TODD, HERBERT JOHN, C.I.E., Indian Political Service (b. 15th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, 4th Dec., 1913; confd., Nov., 1914; in milty. employ from 1st March, 1917, to 8th Jan., 1919; under civil administration, Bagdad, from Jan., 1919, to July, 1921; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Oct., 1921; ditto, Kalat, 5th July, 1922; pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1927; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1932; pol. agent. E. Rajputana states, Oct., 1935; presdt., Council of State, Bharatpur (in addn.), from May, 1936, to Aug., 1936; prime minister and vice-presdt., council of state, Jaipur, Apr., 1939; rest. in Mewar, and pol. agent, S. Rajputana states, Dec., 1940; secty. to agent to govr.-genl., resdt. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; resdt., Eastern states, Dec., 1944.

TODD, JOHN, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 3rd May, 1894).—Ret'd., Apr., 1937.*

TODHUNTER, SIR CHARLES GEORGE, K.C.S.I., F.R.Hist.S., F.S.S., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Ret'd., May, 1925.*

TOLLEMACHE, HAROLD VINCENT, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 14th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Feb., 1927; ret'd., 7th Sept., 1944.

TOLLER, GEORGE GORDON TAYLOR, O.B.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd Feb., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.) 6th Jan., 1910; ditto (perm.), July, 1911; on milty. duty from 18th Dec., 1914, to 18th Dec., 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; dist. engr., Apr., 1921; offg. supt. engr., Dec., 1927; ditto, Irrawaddy bridge constrn., Nov., 1932; exec. engr., ditto, July, 1933; offg. dep. ch. engr., March, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. ch. engr., Apr., 1936; ch. engr., July, 1937; presdt., rly. bd., Burma, March, 1938; ret'd., Dec., 1941.

TOLLINTON, HUGH PHILLIPS, Indian C.S.,

Indian Political Service (b. 19th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Radley, Balliol Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932, and again, Apr., 1933; asst. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1934; ditto, Tank, Feb., 1935; ditto, Charsadda, Nov., 1936; home secty., govt., N.W.F.P., June, 1938; secty., ditto devel. depts., July, 1938; confid., Sept., 1938; senr. sub-judge, Peshawar, March, 1940; civ. a.r.p. offcr., N.W.F.P., July, 1940; settlt. offcr., Hazara, Apr., 1941.

TOLSON, KENNETH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 11th Nov., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1931; confid., Jan., 1934; awarded Indian police medal, 1935; a.d.c. to govr. of Bengal, May, 1938.

TOMKINS, THOMAS LESLIE, India Audit Office (exec. offcr.) (b. 4th Dec., 1889).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 29th March, 1922; exec. offcr., Oct., 1926; actg. hr. exec. offcr., Apr., 1941.

TOMKINS, JAMES, D.S.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 25th Jan., 1918).—Educ. at Cardiff High Sch., and Univ. of Wales; apptd. on probn. after seln. in Aug., 1940, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

TOMKINS, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, C.I.E., O.B.E., India Office (princpl., economic and overseas dept.) (b. 29th July, 1882).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 24th Jan., 1902; transfid. to rev. and stat. dept., March, 1906; minor staff clerk, May, 1914; staff clerk, June, 1919; sec., Indian delegations at Intl. Labour Conf., Genoa, 1920, and Intl. Conf. on Transit and Communications, Barcelona, 1921; adminis. offcr., Apr., 1922; pte. sec. to delegate for India at Intl. Economic Conf., Genoa, 1922, and Hague Conf., 1922; delegate for India at Intl. Conf. on Customs Formalities, Geneva, Oct., 1923; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Aug., 1924; actg. princpl., Dec., 1927; confid., July, 1929; secty. to Indian delegations to assembly of League of Nations, 1934 to 1936; O.B.E., May, 1937; actg. asst. secty., econ. and overseas dept., Jan., 1940; lent to min. of supply, Aug., 1940-Aug., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1941.

TOMS, FRAZER WOODLAND, late Indian Police, Punjab (b. 22nd March, 1888).—Retd., July, 1938.*

TOOGOOD, GEORGE WATTS, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt.) (b. 20th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1920, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., E.R. rly. (prov.), Aug., 1934.

TOOKE, COLIN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Burma) (b. 30th Sept., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, Nov., 1933; confid., Nov., 1935.

TOON, J. CHAN (see Chan Toon, J.).

TOON, MAUNG TIN, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1896).—Educ. at Norris Coll., Rangoon, and Cambridge Univ.; joined the service, 1st July, 1922; arrived, 7th Sept., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1925; confid., May, 1932.

TOOP, REV. WILLIAM JOHN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 20th June, 1907).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Lahore Diocese, 11th May, 1944.

TOPLIS, ARTHUR ROBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 13th Nov., 1885).—Retd., Jan., 1936.*

TORICK, SIR AMEER ALI (see Ameer Ali, Sir T.).

TOSTEVIN, FRED PERCY, O.B.E., M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 29th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Jan., 1912; prof. of English, Morris Coll., Nagpur, Feb., 1912; dep. dir. of public instrn., Central Provs., July, 1921; princpl., Morris Coll., Nagpur, Apr., 1922; princpl., King Edward Coll., Amraoti, Apr., 1923; O.B.E., June, 1935; retd., Apr., 1942.

TOTTENHAM, SIR ALEXANDER ROBERT LOFTUS, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 31st July, 1873).—Retd., June, 1933.*

TOTTENHAM, SIR GEORGE RICHARD FREDERICK, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 18th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Harrow and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1914, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. to resdt., Travancore and Cochin, Sept., 1917; sub. collr. and jt. mag., June, 1919; under-sec. to govt., Feb., 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, army dept., 1924; addl. ditto, Aug., 1926; offg. ditto, Sept., 1926; dep. sec., ditto, Nov., 1926; C.I.E., June, 1930; addl. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Madras; secty. to govt. of India, army dept., Apr., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; member, council of state, Oct., 1936; kt., May, 1937; presdt. of council, Bharatpur, March, 1938; divan, Bharatpur State, Oct., 1939; addl. secty., home dept., govt. of India, June, 1940.

TOWNEND, HERBERT PATRICK VICTOR, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. March, 1887).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., finl. dept., Aug., 1917; jt. mag. and dep. collr. and dir. of civil supplies, Oct., 1918; offg. dir.-genl. of comml. intelligence, Jan., 1920; dep. sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin., comm. and marine depts., Apr., 1923; sec., Indian coal cttee., Oct., 1924, to Apr., 1925; mag. and collr., May, 1925; dep. commr., Apr., 1926; offg. secty., agric. and indus., and p.w. depts., Bengal, Nov., 1930; offg. sec., local self-govt. dept., Apr., 1932; confid., Sept., 1932; offg. rural dev. commr., Feb., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; spec. offcr., home dept., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1937; confid. as commr., govt. of March, 1938; retd., Dec., 1944.

TOWNSEND, REGINALD STEPHEN, M.C., M.D., Col., I.M.S., ret. (b. 10th Nov., 1882).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

TOWNSHEND, EDMUND GEORGE, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 5th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., N.W. rly., 24th Jan., 1914; asst. rolling stock offcr., Nov., 1926; divl. ditto, Sept., 1927; transfid. to Burma Rlys., 29th Jan., 1930; dist. loco. supt., Feb., 1930; retd., Dec., 1942.

TOYNE, WILLIAM, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th July, 1882).—Retd., July, 1937.*

TREANOR, JAMES STANLEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 5th Feb., 1916).—Educ. at Methodist Coll., Belfast, Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confid., 31st Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

TREASURE, EDWARD SHEPPARD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 4th July, 1914).—

Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Madras, 22nd Nov., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

TREASURE, PATRICK MOORE, Indian Police (b. 21st Aug., 1916).—Asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, apptd. on probn., Dec., 1936; confd., Apl., 1938.

TREGENZA, EDWARD LESLIE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 15th May, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 11th March, 1922; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., March, 1928; princpl., police trng. coll., March, 1936, to June, 1941; comdt., Crown Representative police force, March, 1943.

TRENCH, REV. ALBERT CHARLES, M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 25th Jan., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1938.*

TRENCH, WILLIAM LAUNCELOT CROSBIE, C.I.E., B.A.I., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Sind) (b. 22nd July, 1881).—Retd., July, 1936.*

TRENCH, CHENEVIX (see Chenevix-Trench).

TRESHAM, CECIL HENRY, V.D., late Survey of India (b. 3rd Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1903; extra asst. supt., June, 1911; supt., Oct., 1932; retd., Apl., 1939.

TRESTON, MAURICE LAWRENCE, C.B.E., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Burma Medl. Dept. (civil surg.) (b. 9th Feb., 1891).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1914; civil surg., June, 1923; asst. medl. supt. and police surg., Rangoon genl. hosp., March, 1926; supt., Dufferin hosp., Rangoon, Feb., 1928; gynaecologist, genl. hosp., Rangoon, June, 1934; inspr.-genl., civil hosp., Burma, June, 1941; C.B.E., Jan., 1944.

TREVELYAN, HUMPHREY, O.B.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Lancing Coll. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served as asst. collr. and mag., Madras; sub. collr. and jt. mag., March, 1931; under-secty. to A.G.G. in Central India, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. opium agent in Malwa, Apl., 1933; under-secty., pol. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1935; guardian to Maharajah of Gwalior, Nov., 1935; secty. to Resident and C.C., Coorg, March, 1937; dep. commr., Bahawalpur state, May, 1940; O.B.E., June, 1941; pol. agent in Mewar and Southern Rajputana states, Oct., 1941; secty. to agt.-genl. for India in U.S.A., Apl., 1944.

TREVOR, SIR CHARLES GERALD, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 28th Dec., 1882).—Retd., Apl., 1938.*

TRIBHUBAN PRASAD, SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 13th May, 1913).—Educ. at the Banka H.E. Sch., Bhagalpur, Patna Univ., and London Sch. of Economics; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and aerved in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

TRIGG, CHARLES THOMAS, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 15th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; on depn. to Forest research institute, Dehra Dun, from 14th July, 1922; dep. consvr., Jan., 1932; on depn. to civ. pioneer force as spec. officr.

TRILOK NATH, Indian Police, Bihar and Orissa (b. 1st Dec., 1916).—Asst. supt. on probn., 10th Feb., 1939; confd., Feb., 1941.

TRIMMING, GEORGE AUGUSTUS RODERICK, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 24th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 8th Oct., 1913; works manager, Apl., 1923;

offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Sept., 1928; confd., March, 1934; offg. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. rly., March, 1935; confd., July, 1935; ch. contr. of standardisation, Apl., 1940; dep. dir.-genl. munitions prod. dept., dept. of supply, Jan., 1941; retd., Sept., 1944.

TRIMURTI, NARAYANASWAMI, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 15th July, 1900).—Arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1925; transf. from Central Provs., 27th June, 1926; asst. consvr., Madras, July, 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1929.

TRIPATHI, SHYAMA CHANDRA, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Ravenshaw Coll.) (b. 1st March, 1893).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1920; profr. of physics, Feb., 1921; offg. dep. dir. of public instn., Oct., 1930; confd., Aug., 1933; offg. princpl., Ravenshaw Coll., Feb., 1936; confd., Aug., 1936.

TRIVEDI, CHANDULAL MADHAVLAL, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian C.S. (sec., govt. of India) (war) (b. 2nd July, 1893).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, Dec., 1917, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1924; offg. regr., co-op. societies and dir. of industries, Central Provs., June, 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; dep. commr., March, 1927; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; dep. sec., home dept., govt. of India, May, 1932; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1935; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Cent. Provs. Oct., 1937; C.S.I., June, 1941; addtl. sec., govt. of India (commns.), March, 1942; sec., ditto (war), July, 1942.

TRIVEDI, NARAYAN VISHWANATH, Rao Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 5th Sept., 1886).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 18th Jan., 1909, to 26th Aug., 1914; dep. supt., Aug., 1914; employed under dir., intell. bureau, from Oct., 1923, to Jan., 1929; promoted to I.P. as supt. from 24th Jan., 1929; dep. commr. of police, C.I.D., Bombay, March, 1939.

TRIVEDI, RAMANLAL SHANKERLAL, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 20th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag. jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1932; addtl. dist. mag., June to Nov., 1937; addtl. dist. judge from Apl., 1942.

TRIVEDI, RATILAL VITHAL DAS, M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit dept. (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 11th Apl., 1921; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Aug., 1922; asst. dir. of audit, United Provs., Sept., 1928; currency officr., Cawnpore, Dec., 1928; dep. acctn.-genl., Bombay, Sept., 1931, and again, June, 1937; examr., outside audit dept., Bombay, March, 1935; examr., local audit dept., Bombay, Feb., 1936; dep. acct.-genl., Central rev., Oct., 1937; ditto, Punjab, March, 1941.

TROTMAN, EWART WASHINGTON, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 10th July, 1895).—Educ. at the Rich Sch., Gloucester; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1923; arrived, 28th Oct., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dep. secty. to govt., home and eccl. depts., June, 1927; offg. under-secty. to ditto, Aug., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; offg. secty. to ditto, June, 1929; offg. collr. and mag., Apl., 1931; confd., Nov., 1935.

TOTMAN, REV. LIONEL WILLIAM, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b.

12th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probn., Bengal, 19th June, 1930; junr. chaplain, June, 1932.

TROTTER, HUGH, C.I.E., Indian Forest Service (asst. forest economist, forest research institute and coll., Dehra Dun) (b. 16th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 30th Dec., 1913; on mil. service from 28th Nov., 1914, to 9th March, 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; asst. forest economist, Sept., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1943.

TROUSDELL, HENRY FRANK, Printing and Stationery Dept. (manager, govt. of India press) (b. 14th July, 1891).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Nov., 1916; asst. manager, govt. of India press, Jan., 1926; offg. dep. contr., forms, Nov., 1927; confid., Dec., 1930; offg. manager, govt. of India press, Nov., 1931; confid., Oct., 1932.

TRUSCOTT, ROBERT RODGER BATE, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 14th Nov., 1935; confid., July, 1938.

TRUTWEIN, PERCY WALLACE, B.A., late Burma Commission (b. 30th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

TUCK, HOWELL ARTHUR, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 26th Feb., 1888).—Asst. signal engr., 1st Nov., 1917; dist. ditto, 1st July, 1937; dep. ch. ditto, March, 1941; retd., Oct., 1943.

TUCKER, REV. GORDON ELIJAH, B.Sc., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (archdeacon of Calcutta) (b. 8th Oct., 1896).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probn., 22nd Feb., 1929; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1931; senr. ditto, Feb., 1941; archdeacon of Calcutta, Oct., 1944.

TUCKER, OWEN RUFUS, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt.) (b. 27th March, 1897).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1923; asst. supt., E.I. rly., July, 1925; transpn. supt. (power), June, 1935.

TUCKETT, ROBERT HENRY, India Office (hr. exec. officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 17th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 1st July, 1913; on mil. service from Dec., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; actg. hr. ditto, Sept., 1938; confid., June, 1939; assessor of income tax, March, 1939.

TUCKEY, ARTHUR DAVYS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 12th Oct., 1886).—Retd., March, 1936.*

TUCKWELL, CHARLES ADOLPHUS, O.B.E., late India posts and tels. (b. 20th Jan., 1884).—Retd., May, 1938.*

TUFFIELD, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt. [senr. scale], G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 19th March, 1889).—Asst. transpn. supt., (junr. scale), July, 1926; ditto (senr. scale), June, 1937.

TUFNELL-BARRETT, HUGH, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 13th Jan., 1900).—Educ. at St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Wellington Cadet Coll., India; joined the service, 10th Sept., 1923; arrived, 16th Nov., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1924; offg. under-secty., pol. and appt. dept., March, 1927; confid., July, 1927; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. dept., June, 1930; again, Apl., 1931; addtl. mag. and collr., Nov., 1931; offg. addl. dep. secty., pol. dept., Bengal, Apl., 1931; again, Nov., 1933; K.-I.-H. gold medal, June, 1938; mag. and collr. (confid.), Jan., 1939; dep. sec. govt. of Bengal home, Apl.,

1939; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dep. of lab., Nov., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; jt. sec. to govt. of India (lab.) Apl., 1943; member cl. of state, July, 1943; civ. rep., Bengal govt., with H.Q., E. Cmd., 2nd addtl. sec., Bengal (home) and provl. press advr., Dec., 1943; dir.-genl., food, Calcutta, Jan., 1945.

TULL, THLMAS STUART, Indian C.S. (ass. commr., Punjab) (b. 11th Oct., 1914).—Educ. at Rossall and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confid., 20th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

TULLIS, J., B.Sc., Dept. of Industries, Bihar (b. 20th May, 1888).—Joined the service as prof., Bihar coll. of engrg., 4th April, 1924; offg. princpl., Apl., 1925; princpl., Apl., 1937.

TUN, MAUNG SEIN NYO, Indian C.S. (ass. commr., Burma) (b. 24th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. secty., fin. dept., Feb., 1939; offcr. under trng. acct.-genl.'s office, Burma, March, 1940; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, March, 1941.

TURNBULL, FRANCIS FEARON, B.A., India Office (pte. sec. to sec. of state for India and for Burma) (b. 30th Apl., 1905).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge (Scholar); asst. clerk, House of Commons, 1928-30; apptd. asst. princpl., 12th Aug., 1930; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state, Jan., 1932; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, Sept., 1934; actg. princpl., July, 1935; confid., Apl., 1937; pte. secty. to secty. of state for India and for Burma, Nov., 1941.

TURNER, ALEXANDER BERTRAM, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.), A.M.I.M.E. (b. 15th May, 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1937.*

TURNER, ALFRED CHARLES, C.I.E., M.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty., fin. dept.) (b. 12th March, 1892).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 11th May, 1920, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; M.B.E., June, 1919; jt. mag., Sept., 1921; offg. mag. and collr. from Feb. to Oct., 1924; and from March, 1925; confid., Feb., 1932; provl. supt. of census opers., United Provs., Apl., 1930; offg. dep. secty., United Provs., fin. dept., Nov., 1933; offg. secty. to govt. United Provs., rev. and p.w. depts., Feb., 1935; ditto, fin. dept., Apl., 1936; commr. of inc. tax, Bengal, Nov., 1938; ditto, Bombay Presidency, Sind and Baluchistan, Apl., 1939; jt. secty., fin. dept., May, 1941; C.I.E., June, 1941; addtl. sec., Apl., 1944.

TURNER, ALFRED ELLIS, late Indian Police (Cent. Provs.) (b. 6th Aug., 1893).—Retd., July, 1938.*

TURNER, ERNEST JAMES, C.B., C.B.E., late India Office (b. 26th Feb., 1877).—Retd., Oct., 1937*; re-empld. temp. as asst. secty., Aug., 1940, to July, 1941.

TURNER, JOHN TRENCH, M.A. (Cantab.) (b. 24th Dec., 1879).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1905, as headmaster, High Sch., Poona; vice-princpl., Rajkumar Coll., Rajkot, Aug., 1906; actg. princpl., ditto, Nov., 1912, to Nov., 1913; actg. princpl., Elphinstone High Sch., Bombay, Dec., 1914; on mil. service, Apl., 1915, to Jan., 1919; princpl., Rajkumar Coll., Rajkot, Jan., 1923.

TURNER, JOSHUA EDGAR CARRINGTON, late Indian Forest Service (b. 15th Sept., 1883).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

TURNER, LEONARD GEORGE HENRY, late Indian State Ryis. (G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 15th Oct., 1884).—Retd., Oct., 1939.*

TURNER, SIR SIDNEY, C.B.E., B.A., F.I.A., India Office (acctnt.-genl.) (b. 18th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge (10th Wrangler); apptd. junr. clerk, acct.-genl.'s dept., 6th Oct., 1905; sec. to Lord Welby's commn. on Indian Mil. Service Family Pension Regns., 1913; senr. clerk, July, 1915; acct.-genl., Dec., 1921; also contr. of pension funds; C.B.E., June, 1927; commr. for inc. tax, Nov., 1930; apptd. chairman, applicn. cttee. for the Indo-Burma finl. award, Jan., 1936; apptd. commr. for the I.C.S. Family Pension Fund, Indian Military Service Family Pension Fund, Indian Military Widows' and Orphans' Fund, Superior Services (India) Family Pension Fund from Oct., 1937; kt., Jan., 1942.

TURNER, WILLIAM HENRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 2nd Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., May, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1925; confd., Oct., 1927.

TURNER, WILLIAM HOVELL, M.A., M.C., India Office (auditor) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Perse Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to March, 1919; apptd. junr. clerk, p.w. dept., 29th Aug., 1919; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Oct., 1920; actg. princpl., acct.-genl.'s dept., March, 1922; transf. to finl. dept., June, 1923; confd. as princpl., Jan., 1924; posted to econ. and overseas dept., Apl., 1932; actg. dep. clk. of council, Jan.-July, 1932; secty., Indian del. to Assembly of League of Nations, 1932 and 1933; asst. to auditor, March, 1934; sec. to Burma Office as asst. secty. Aug., 1940; auditor, Oct., 1944.

TUT, MAUNG TIN, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (b. 1st Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Dulwich, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 16th Jan., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt. agric. dept., July, 1924; jt. regr., co-op. socs., June, 1927; under-secty., govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Apl., 1928; dep.-secty., ditto, June, 1929; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1933; confd., Dec., 1935; dep. secty., fin. dept., govt. of Burma, May, 1935; secty., forest dept., Burma, Dec., 1935; ditto, fin. dept., June, 1938; commr. on spl. duty, premier's office, Jan., 1941; advr. and secty., reconstr. dept., 1944; author of essays on 'The Kingdom of Pagan in Legend and History,' 'Burma's Part in the War,' 'Tales of Burma Labour Corps,' etc.

TWELLS, Miss HAZEL VIOLET, M.B.E., B.A. (Lond.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 7th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as inspectress of girls's schs. in Bombay, 1st Oct., 1922; M.B.E., June, 1942.

TWINBERROW, JOHN DENIS, M.C., Indian Police (supl., Punjab) (b. 14th Feb., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on probn., N.W.F.P., 22nd Feb., 1924; confd., Apl., 1927; supt., Jan., 1941.

TYWNAM, SIR HENRY JOSEPH, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. (Man.), Indian C.S. (govr. of Central Provs. and Berar) (b. 24th Apl., 1887).—Educ. at Pensionat de la Salle, Rouen, Manchester, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Bengal, Apl.,

1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1916; I.A.R.O., June, 1915, to Jan., 1919; sec., Bengal, employment and labour board, Jan., 1919; vice-presdnt., Cooch Behar state, Feb., 1920; to Apl., 1921; mag. and collr., June, 1924; secty. to govt. of Bengal, rev. and irrig. depts., March, 1929; offg. ch. sec. to govt. of Bengal, July, 1932; offg. commr., Oct., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; temp. addl. member, bd. of rev., Bengal, June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, variously from Apl., 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937; actg. govr. of Assam, Feb., 1939, to Oct., 1939; actg. govr., Central Provs. and Berar, June, 1940, to Sept., 1940; govr., Central Provs. and Berar, Oct., 1940; K.C.S.I., Oct., 1940.

TYABJI, BADRUDDIN FAIZ HASAN BADRUDDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 12th Nov., 1907).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., under-secty., home and pol. depts., Oct., 1936; secty., Punjab and N.W.F.P., jt. pub. serv. commn., May, 1937; under-secty. to govt. of India, defence dept. (offg.), Dec., 1938; attache, defence dept., March, 1939; under-secty. to govt. of India, defence dept., Apl., 1939; under-secty., govt. of Punjab, Sept., 1939; dep. commr., Apl., 1940, to Nov., 1941; contr. of supply, Karachi, Sept., 1942; ditto, Bombay, Sept., 1943; dep. secty., govt. of India (planning and devel.), Nov., 1944.

TYABJI, HATIM BADRUDDIN, Judl. Dept., Sind, apptd. judge, court of judl. commr., Sind, Feb., 1940; judge, ch. ct., Sind, Apl., 1940.

TYABJI, SALMAN BADR-U-DIN, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 6th Nov., 1883).—Retd., Nov., 1938.*

TYAGARAJA, AYYAR, P.C., B.A., B.L., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. Jan., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1939.*

TYDD, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian Police (asst. collr. of customs) (b. 12th Nov., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 3rd Dec., 1929; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. supt., Dec., 1935; asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, May, 1938; transf. to customs dept. as asst. collr. (preventive), Feb., 1940.

TYLER, SIR HENRY HEWEY FRANCIS MACDONALD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 13th Feb., 1877).—Retd., May, 1931*.

TYMMS, SIR FREDERICK, C.I.E., M.C., Civil Aviation Directorate (b. 4th Aug., 1889).—Apptd. dir. of civil aviation in India, 23rd Feb., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; kt., June, 1941.

TYNDALE, ARTHUR CARRINGTON, O.B.E., M.A., late Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (b. 20th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1909, and employed in milty. accts. dept.; offg. dep. finl. advr., milty. fin., March, 1927; confd., May, 1927; confd. as cl. I offcr., Aug., 1929; confd. as cmd. contr., Feb., 1934; O.B.E., June, 1938; retd., Nov., 1941.

TYSON, JOHN DAWSON, C.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. dept. of e., h. and l.) (b. 14th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914; joined the service, 14th Feb., 1920; arrived, 11th May, 1920, and posted to Bengal; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin. and commerce dept., Nov., 1922, to March, 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; offg. ch. presdy. mag.,

June, 1926; secty. to agent of govt. of India in South Africa, May, 1927; pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, June, 1930; C.B.E., Jan., 1933; mag. and collr., Sept., 1935; advr. to H.H. the Maharajah of Couch Behar, March, 1936; commr., Dacca divn., March, 1938; secty. to govr., Bengal, May, 1938; jt. secty., dept. of e., h. and l., govt. of India, Nov., 1939; secty., ditto, Apl., 1940; confd., July, 1940.

U (see also Maung).

U BA AYE, B.A., Burma Police (asst. supt.).—Apptd. on probn., Jan., 1939; transf. to milty. dept., Oct., 1940.

U BA U, M.A., Barr., Burma Commn. (judge, high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. govt. prosecutor, Apl., 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; confd., Jan., 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, Feb., 1930; confd., Apl., 1932.

U BA ZAN, A.T.M., B.A., late Burma C.S. (b. 25th Feb. 1892).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., May, 1921; dep. commr., Aug., 1935; ret'd., Dec., 1942.

U HLA (1), B.A., Burma C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 14th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., June, 1922; dep. commr., Apl., 1938.

U KYAW (3), B.A., Burma C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 12th July, 1896).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., May, 1921; dep. commr., June, 1934; secty. to govt. educn. dept., Oct., 1937.

U KYAW DIN, A.T.M., B.A., Burma C.S. (secty., educn. dept.) (b. 7th March, 1894).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Apl., 1922, under secty. to govt., judicial dept., Jan., 1930; ditto, local govt. dept., March, 1934; dep., commr., Apl., 1938; addl. secty. to govt. educn. dept., Apl., 1938; secty., educn. dept., Aug., 1940.

U KYAW U., Barr., Burma Judicial Dept. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 9th Nov., 1890).—Dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1934.

U MYA (3), A.T.M., Burma Judicial Dept. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 11th March, 1886).—Joined the service, Dec., 1909; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1936.

U PO HAN, Barr.-at-law, Member, Public Service Commn., Feb., 1938.

UP PO SA, B.A., A.T.M., F.R.E.S., Burma C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 13th Aug., 1890).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Dec., 1922; dep. commr., June, 1938.

U THA TUN KHINE, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 22nd Apl., 1913).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Feb., 1937.

U THA ZAN U, B.A., B.L., late Burma Commn. (b. 17th May, 1881).—Ret'd., May, 1936.*

U THAN TIN, B.A., Burma C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 1st March, 1893).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Jan., 1922; dep. commr., June, 1935; dep. secty., agric. and forests dept., Aug., 1937; secty., agric. and forests dept., Sept., 1937; ditto, fin. dept., Feb., 1941.

U TIN MRA, U, A.T.M., Burma Police (cl. I) (supt.) (b. 8th June, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1914; promoted to Burma Police (cl. I), June, 1937.

UBAIDULLAH, KHAWAJAH, KHAN BAHADUR, O.B.E., Indian Railway Accts. Service (asst. dir. of fin. in the rly. bd.) (b. 30th Oct., 1900).—Joined the service, Feb., 1923; accts. offcr. Aug., 1935; asst. dir. of fin. in the rly. bd., Oct., 1936; under-secty. to govt. of India, dept. of supply, June, 1941; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

ULLAH, CHAUDHRI NIAMAT (see Niamat Ullah, Chaudhri).

ULLAH, H. M. SALAMAT (see Salamat Ullah, H. M.).

ULLAH, MUHAMMAD SANA ULLAH (see Muhammad, S. U.).

UMAR HAYAT KHAN, Hon. Maj.-Gen. NAWAB MALIK, Sir, Tiwana, of Kalra, G.B.E., K.C.I.E., M.V.O. (b. 1874).—Educ. at Aitchison Chief's Coll., Lahore; served in Somaliland; joined Tibet expedition; was attached to late Amir of Afghanistan; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; served in France and Mesopotamia in Great War; K.C.I.E., June, 1916; represented Punjab, Delhi War Conference, 1918; served in 3rd Kabul war; C.B.E., June, 1919; member, Esher cottee., 1920; member, council of State, 1926; member of Council of India, June, 1929, to June, 1934; Hon. A.D.C. to King, Nov., 1930; G.B.E., June, 1934; hon.-col., 19th (K.G.O.) Lancers, Oct., 1935.

UNDERWOOD, FREDERICK LEWIS, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 18th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; cent. intell. offcr., Madras presdy., Feb., 1936, to May, 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

UNGER, OSCAR RAMSEY, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bihar (inspr.-genl. of prisons) (b. 12th Sept., 1889).—First commn. dated 16th July, 1915; in milty. employ to 13th Apl., 1924; supt., central jail, Gaya, Jan., 1925; ditto, Hazaribagh, Feb., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933.

UNITT, ALFRED, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 22nd June, 1902).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1921; comdt., Baluchistan levy, May, 1924; offg. supt., March, 1928; confd., Aug., 1932; asst. inspr.-genl., civic guards, Apl., 1942; senr. supt., Quetta, Oct., 1944.

UNNITHAN, KRISHNAN MADHAVAN, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 13th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 17th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. magte. and collr.; sub.-collr. and jt. magte., Aug., 1930; dep. commr. of excise, Jan., 1940; ditto, commercial taxes, Jan., 1944; inspr. of municipal cts., etc., Apl., 1944.

UNWIN, RICHARD, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 6th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 16th Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; lecturer in forestry, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, from 1st March, 1923, to 10th Sept., 1924, and again from 26th Oct., 1925; offg. consvr., Apl., 1934; confd., Oct., 1936; ret'd., Dec., 1942.

UPTON, SIR THOMAS EVERARD TICHEBORNE, Kt., late Legislative Dept., Govt. of India.—Ret'd., Apl., 1932.*

U'REN, CECIL WILLIAM EDWARD, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 9th Nov., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 4th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Feb., 1932, Jan., and Sept., 1933; offg. dep. commr., Bombay, Feb., 1936; confd. as supt., Oct., 1939; Indian police medal, 1941; dep. commr. (special bch.), June, 1943, to May, 1944.

URQUHART, DONALD WALLACE JOHNSTONE, Barr., late Burma Commn. (b. 5th Oct., 1879).—Ret'd., Oct., 1935.

USMAN, SIR MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Usman).

USMANI, ISHRAT HUSAIN, M.Sc., Indian C.S.

'asst. collr. and mag.' (b. 15th Aprl., 1917).—Apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

UTTAM SINGH, SARDAR SAHIB (exec. engr., central p.w.d.) (b. 24th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Aprl., 1922; exec. engr., 10th Jan., 1934.

UZIELLI, HERBERT REX, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st Aug., 1890).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1918; temp. under-sec. to govt., rev. spl. dept., Nov., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., Dec., 1925; offg. sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Aprl., 1926; dep. sec. to ditto, May, 1926; offg. collr. and dist. magte., June, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; collr. (prov. subs.), Aug., 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; secty. to govt., Madras, rev. dept., Nov., 1934, to May, 1937; ditto, home dept., Aug., 1938; ret'd., July, 1940.

VACHHA, JAMSHEDI BEJANJI, Khan Bahadur, C.I.E., B.A., B.Sc., late Bombay S.C. (b. 26th May, 1879).—Ret'd., May, 1939.*

VAGHAIWALLA, RUSTOM BEHERAMSHAH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 24th Jan., 1919).—Educ. at Bombay Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1942; served in Assam as asst. commr.

VAHID, SYED ABDUL (see Syed Abdul Vahid).

VAIDYA, JAGANNATH BALKRISHNA, M.R.C.S., Maj., I.M.S., Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—First commn., Jan., 1914; civil surg., United Provs., Aprl., 1930.

VAIDYANATHAN, PERUVEMBA PARASURAMAYEER, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. judge, Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Madras Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Sept., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., Dec., 1939; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1943; dist. judge, Dec., 1944.

VAISH, RADHA KRISHNA, M.A., Imperial Customs Service (b. 28th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service, Aprl., 1924; asst. collr. of customs, Aprl., 1926; confd. as asst. commr., July, 1943.

VAKIL, KRISHNALAL SURAJRAM, B.A., M.Ed. (Leeds), late Indian Educl. Service (Bombay) (b. 13th Oct., 1878).—Ret'd., Oct., 1933.*

VALLURI KAMESWARA RAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 15th July, 1914).—Educ. at the Board High Sch., Razole, Madras Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., May, 1941; under-sec., fin. dept., Aug., 1941; dep. contr., procurement and purchase, May, 1944.

VAN WYCK, WILLIAM, B.A., Burma C.S. (cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 12th March, 1913).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1938, and served as asst. commr.

VAN DER BEEK, AUBREY GUILDFORD, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 22nd Aprl., 1890).—Joined the service, 4th March, 1914, as asst. engr. (temp.); ditto (perm.), March, 1915; exec. engr., Burma rlys., Sept., 1932.

VAN SOMERAN, LEONARD WILLIAM, V.D., late Indian State Railways (E.B. Rly.) (b. 16th Dec., 1884).—Ret'd., Dec., 1939.*

VANCE, ROBERT LANCELOT, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (legation surg.).—First commn., 30th May, 1916; in milty. employ to 29th Aprl., 1928; offg. C.M.O., W.I. States Agency and resdy., surg., Rajkot, May, 1928, and again, March, 1933; offg. civil surg., Quetta, Aug., 1928; confd., Aprl., 1931; resdy. surg., Kashmir, May, 1935; legation surg., Nepal, March, 1937.

VARADACHARI, RAGHVA DIKSHIDAR, Rao Bahadur, Income Tax Dept. (commr. of inc. tax) (b. 23rd Aprl., 1890).—Joined the service, Aprl., 1923; inc. tax offer., Aprl., 1923, to Dec., 1936; asst. commr. of inc. tax, Dec., 1936; offer. on spec. duty, Feb., 1939, to Aprl., 1940; commr. of inc. tax, Punjab, 1941; confd. as commr., June, 1943.

VARADACHARIAR, SIR SRINIVASA, B.A., LL.B. (judge federal ct. of India) (b. 20th June, 1881).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, 3rd March, 1934; judge, federal ct. of India, Feb., 1939; kt., Aprl., 1939.

VARGHESE, THOPPI ANTHONY, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 17th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Madras Univ., University Coll., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Aprl., 1932; sec., central road traffic bd., May, 1942; dep. sec. fin. dept., June, 1943.

VARMA, BADRI NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 27th Aug., 1917).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Central Prov. as asst. commr.

VARMA, BRIJ BIHARI, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 30th Nov., 1897).—Joined service, Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., O. & R. rly., Nov., 1921; exec. engr. provl. perm., E.I. rly., July, 1938; M.B.E., Jan., 1942.

VARMA, BRIJ BHUSHAN SARAN (see Brij, B. S. V.).

VARMA, B. P. (see Bhagwati Prasad Varma).

VARMA, RAGHUNANDAN PRASAD, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., N.W. rly.) (b. 5th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; dep. ch. eng. (provl.-perm.), Oct., 1939; divl. supt. (snr.), Jan., 1940; dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1941.

VARMA, RUP LALL, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th Dec., 1904).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Assam, 2nd Jan., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1937; under sec., p.w.d., Oct., 1942.

VARMA, SARADA PRASHAD, Babu Indian Police (asst. supt., Orissa) (b. 15th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 2nd Jan., 1931; confd., Dec., 1931.

VARMA, S. P. (see Sidheshwari Prasad).

VARMA, SUKDEV PRASAD, Barr., Judl. Dept., Bihar (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, 23rd Jan., 1934.

VAS, EDWIN THOMAS CHRISTIAN, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 25th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service, 20th July, 1921; asst. consvr., Aug., 1921; dep. consvr., Feb., 1927.

VASIST, SHYAM SUNDER, B.A. (Hons.), M.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (asst. transpn. supt., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 1st Aug., 1902).—Joined service, Feb., 1926; asst. transportation supt., Feb., 1929; asst. transpn. (traffic and commercial) supt., senior scale, provl., July, 1938; confd., Oct., 1939.

VASUDEEV, KESHAVRAO BALKRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay C.S. (b. 14th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, Oct., 1907; dep. collr., Aug., 1909; actg. asst. judge and sess. judge,

Aprl., 1913; asst. judge and jt. judge and addl. sess. judge, Oct., 1921; judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1936; confd., Nov., 1937; retd., Jan., 1943.

VASUDEVA, BHANDARI, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 8th Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Sept., 1933.

VAZIFDAR, SOHRAB SHAPOORJI, L.M. & S. (Bom.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), Lieut.-Col. (retd.), late I.M.S. (b. 1st Aug., 1883).—Retd., Aug., 1938.*

VELLODI, MULLATH VIKKARA, Rao Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 23rd Dec., 1884).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

VELLODI, MULLATH KADINGI, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., govt. of Orissa, rev. and developments, depts.) (b. 14th Jan., 1896).—Educ. at Presidency Coll, Madras; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and mag., Jan., 1924; spl. asst. settlt. offcr., Jan., 1926; spl. settlt. offcr., Sept., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1936; offg. dir. of land records, Madras, Aprl., 1933; jt. secty., bd. of rev., Madras, Nov., 1933; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from March, 1937; sec., govt. of Orissa, educ., etc., depts., Dec., 1937; secty. govt. of Orissa; revenue and developm. depts., Nov., 1938; price adv. offcr., govt. of India, Feb., 1940; export trade contr., Bombay, Nov., 1942; C.I.E., June, 1944.

VENABLES, ARTHUR VINCENT, M.C., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. rly.) (b. 16th Oct., 1880).—Retd., July, 1937.*

VENGANAD KOVILAGAM RAJAGOPALA AHTMBAN (see Rajagopala Thamban, V. K.).

VENKATA ACHARYA, ATUR RANGASWAMI, Rao Sahib, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr.) (b. 21st June, 1895).—Joined the service as appr. engr., Madras, 30th June, 1917; asst. engr., June, 1918; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; dep. chief exec. engr., July, 1927; suptg. engr., March, 1944.

VENKATA RAO, GHANTA (sany. engr., Madras) (b. 18th Feb., 1886).—Apptd. dep. sany. engr., 3rd March, 1923; offg. sany. engr., Sept., 1931; confd., Feb., 1935.

VENKATA REDDI NAYUDU, RAI BAHADUR, SIR Kurma, K.C.I.E.—Member of govr.'s exec. council, Madras, 26th Aprl., 1934 to March, 1937; actg. govr. of Madras, June, 1936; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

VENKATA, SUBRAMANYAN MINAKSHISUNDARAM (see Subramanyan Minakshisundaram, V.).

VENKATACHAR, CADAMBI SHESHACHAR, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 11th July, 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ., University Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 15th Jan., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., 1925; offg. magte. and collr., March, 1927; under-sec. to the agent to the govr.-genl. in States of W.I., Oct., 1927; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, Nov., 1927; under-secy. to A.G.G. in States of W.I., Dec., 1928; census supt. C.I., Aprl., 1930; offg. dep. commr., United Provs., Aprl., 1933; agent to Govt. of India in British Malaya, Sept., 1937; O.B.E., June, 1941.

VENKATAKRISHNA AYYAR, LAKSHMINARAYANA AYYAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b.

7th May, 1888).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 18th July, 1912; asst. engr., July, 1913; exec. engr., July, 1922; suptg. engr., Dec., 1939; ch. engr., Madras, Aug., 1940; retd., May, 1943.

VENKATAPPIAH, BURRA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; confd. as dep. sec. (fin.), June, 1940; jt. sec. (fin.), temp., Aug., 1942.

VENKATARAMA AYYAR, K.C., B.A., B.L., Income Tax Dept., Madras (asst. commr. of inc.-tax) (b. 1st Aprl., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Aprl., 1922; asst. commr., Jan., 1931; retd., Aprl., 1937.

VENKATARAMA AYYAR, THIRUVADAMARUDUR SUBRAHMANYA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 10th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as appr. engr., June, 1921; asst. exec. engr., June, 1922; offg. profir., coll. of engrg., Guindy, Aug., 1927; exec. engr., Nov., 1931.

VENKATARAMA AYYAR, TIRUKKARUGAVUR NANA AYYAR, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 19th July, 1883).—Retd., July, 1938.*

VENKATARAMA RAMACHANDRA AYYAR (see Ramachandra Ayyar, V.).

VENKATARAMAN, KOMARATCHI SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 25th Jan., 1913).—Educ. at Pachayappa's Coll., Madras, Madras Univ., University Coll., London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1937; city, 1st cl., mag., Aprl., 1940; judl. trng., June, 1942; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1944.

VENKATARAMAN, SWAMINATHA ADUTHURAI, C.I.E., M.A., Ind. C.S. (dep. dir.-genl. of supply) (b. 22nd June, 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922; and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aprl., 1931; dep. secty., fin. dept., Aprl., 1932; regr. of co-operative societies, Madras, March, 1939; confd., collr., prov. subst., June, 1940; dep. dir.-genl. of supply, dept. of supply, Jan., 1942; jt. sec., branch secretariat, Calcutta, dept. of supply, July, 1942; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

VENKATARAMAN, ANAIKUPPAM RAMASWAMI, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 1st May, 1933; confd., Sept., 1933.

VENKATARAMAN, SIR TIRUVADI SAMBASIVAIYER, C.I.E., B.A., Rao Bahadur, late Indian Agricultural Service (Madras) (b. 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

VENKATARAMANA AYYAR, KODUMUDI RAMASWAMI Rao Bahadur, late Indian Forest Service (b. Jan., 1880).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

VENKATARAMANA RAO NAYUDU, PAKALA, Rao Bahadur.—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1935; retd., 1942.

VENKATARAMANA RAJU, PATTAYYA RAJU, B.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 14th June, 1918; asst. engr., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr.,

Feb., 1922; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925; confd., June, 1928; suptg. engr., March, 1944.

VENKATARAMANI, P., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 28th March, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Nov., 1931; dep. consvr., Nov., 1936.

VENKATASUBBA RAO, SIR MUTTA, Kt., B.A., B.L., late Madras Judicial Dept., (b. 18th July, 1878).—Retd., July, 1938.*

VENKATASUBRAMANIAN, VAITHINATHIER (see Subramanian, V. V.).

VENKATESWARA, AYYAR, Gopala, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 19th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct. 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. May, 1932; ch. presdy. mag., Nov., 1941; sec., bd. of rev., Oct., 1944.

VENKATESWARAN, SUNDARAM, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Jan., 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 30th Nov., 1925, and served as asst. collr. and mag. in Madras; sub-collr., Aug., 1927; sec. to board of rev., Madras, Nov., 1938; M.B.E., June, 1943.

VENUGOPAL, GEORGE, late Indian Forest Service, Madras (b. 14th July, 1882).—Retd., July, 1937.*

VERDON, PHILIP, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., (dist. medl. offcr., Madras) (b. 22nd Feb., 1886).—First commn., dated 8th Aug., 1917; in milty. employ until 30th March, 1925; dist. medl. offcr., Feb., 1925; offg. surg., 4th dist., Nov., 1928; confd., June, 1929; supt., govt. ophthalmic hospital, etc., Madras, Apl., 1937.

VERGHESE, GEORGE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S. (Orissa) (b. 9th June, 1888).—First commn., 27th Aug., 1917; in milty. employ to March, 1933; supt., central jail, Orissa, July, 1933; dir. of health and inspr.-genl. of prisons, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1941; retd., June, 1943.

VERMA, CHHOTELAI, I.S.O., Rai Bahadur, late Central Provs. C.S. (b. 6th Jan., 1883).—Retd., Jan., 1938.*

VERMA, HIRALAL, Rai Sahib, M.B.E., B.A., late Central Provs. C.S. (b. 25th Apl., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1937.*

VERMA, KAMALAKANTA (judge, high ct., Allahabad)—Apptd., Nov., 1937.

VERMA, KRISHNA PRASAD, Rai Sahib, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., Central Provs. (b. 1st June, 1882).—Retd., June, 1938.*

VERNEDE, RAYMOND VEVEYSAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Bradford and Hertford Coll., Oxford, joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928; and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and coll.; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935; confd., Nov., 1942.

VESUGAR, JAMSHED BURJORJI, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 26th May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 22nd Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; under-secty., p.w.d., b. and r. branch, Apl., 1937; consltg. engr. to govt. of India (roads), May, 1942; suptg. engr. (subst.), Aug., 1943; industl. advr. to govt. of India, Apl., 1944.

VIBART, FRANK, Indian State Rlys. (supt., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 17th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1911; asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1915; supt., Sept., 1929; senr. sc., May, 1932.

VICK, HENRY JOHN HOWARD, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 28th Sept., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

VICKERS, CHARLES ALFRED, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 31st Oct., 1899).—Educ. at King Edward's High Sch., Birmingham, Quetta Cadet Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1924, and served in Burma as an asst. commr.; sub. divl. offcr., June, 1925; offg. under-sec., home and pol. dep., Nov., 1926; judge, sub-dvnl. courts, Aug., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1934.

VICKERS, HAROLD JAMES, C.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl.) (b. 1st June, 1895).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1914; on milty. duty from 16th Sept., 1916 to 17th Feb., 1920; asst. supt., May, 1920; asst. dist. offcr., frontier constabulary, June, 1920; dist. offcr., ditto, Dec., 1922; supt., Oct., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1926; dep. dir., intelligence bureau, Nov., 1933; C.B.E., June, 1936; dep. inspr.-genl., Dec., 1940; Indian police medal, 1944.

VICKERY, PHILIP CRAWFORD, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 23rd Feb., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1909; asst. supt., Apl., 1911; on milty. duty, 1st Nov., 1915 to 24th March, 1921; supt., Aug., 1921; O.B.E., June, 1923; on depn. outside India, from Oct., 1926; C.I.E., June, 1939.

VIDYARTHI, PUTTAN LAL, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 1st July, 1886).—Joined the service, June, 1903; accts. offcr., Aug., 1935; retd., July, 1941.

VIJAYARAGHAVA ACHARYA, SIR TIRUVALYANGUDI, K.B.E., M.A., Diwan Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 27th Aug., 1875).—Retd., Oct., 1935.*

VIJAYARAGHAVAN, CHERUVYAR KOTTIETH, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Madras Christian Coll. and Law Coll.; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; tempy. dep. commr. of excise, May, 1930; offg. collr., Sept., 1934; offg. dep. secty., finance dept., Madras, Apl., 1935; confd. collr., Dec., 1940; sec. p.w.d., Apl., 1943; commr. of labour, June, 1944.

VIJAYARAGHAVAN, NARASINHA (see Raghavan, N. V.).

VIJAYASINHJI, KUMAR SHRI, Indian Police Bombay (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Joined 17th May, 1930; prom. to Indian Police as supt., 15th Apl., 1944.

VILLAR, ARTHUR REGINALD, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 28th June, 1888).—Arrived 28th Dec., 1910, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Jan., 1916; offg. consvr., Aug., 1928; and again, Apl., 1931; confd., Feb., 1935; retd., Apl., 1942.

VINAYANAND PATHAK, PANDIT, late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 17th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 1st Nov., 1912; asst. supt., Feb., 1920; offg. supt., Nov., 1921; confd., Feb., 1927; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Aug., 1933; asst. I.G. rly. police, Aug., 1938; retd., 17th Feb., 1944.

VINING, ARTHUR CYRIL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. optg. supt., N.W. rly.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 22nd Nov., 1909; on milty. duty, 1st Apl., 1916; dist. traffic supt., Aug., 1922; divl. transp. supt., Oct., 1924; dep. ch. opertg. supt. (provl.), Dec., 1937; confd., Dec., 1940.

VINING, LEONARD EDWARD, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1908; dist. ditto, May, 1917; on spl. duty with Midland Rly., June, 1921; supt., July, 1925; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., June, 1929; and again, Dec., 1932; offg. divl. supt., May, 1932; dep. ch. operating supt., E.I. rly. (prov.), June, 1934; confd., Jan., 1936; retd., Apl., 1941.

VIPAN, ALFRED, C.I.E., M.I.C.E. late Indian Service of Engrs. (Orissa) (b. 9th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; ch. engr. to Travancore durbar, Apl., 1927; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1933; confd., Oct., 1935; secty. to govt., p.w.d. Orissa, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; retd., March, 1940.

VIRA, DHARMA, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 20th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., Lucknow Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

VISHNU SAHAY (see Sahay, V.).

VISVANATHA AYYAR, PATHIYAVAD SESHACHALA, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 10th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Madras, Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., May, 1933; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Apl., 1936.

VISVANATHA RAO, VELLIYUR NOTT, Diwan Bahadur, M.A., B.L., late Madras C.S. (b. 16th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th Feb., 1913; dep. collr., March, 1916; offg. collr., March, 1930; offg. secty. to govt., Madras law and educn. dept., Sept., 1931; sub.-collr., June, 1931; actg. secty. to govt., Madras, law and educn. dept., Sept., 1931; secty. to govt. of Madras development dept., Feb., 1939; collr., Jan., 1940; retd., 16th Oct., 1944, and re-empld. as collr.

VISWANATHAN, VENKATA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 25th Jan., 1909).—Educ. at University Coll., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936; asst. settlmt. offcr., Oct., 1936; settlmt. offcr., Oct., 1937; attaché, dept. of e.h. and l., govt. of India, Nov., 1940; under-sec., ditto, Apl., 1941; under-secy., dept. of Indians overseas, govt. of India, Oct., 1941; sec. to repr. of govt. of India in Ceylon, Aug., 1943; ditto, Burma and addtl. dep. sec., commun. and reltns. dept., Sept., 1944.

VIVIAN, GRAHAM LINSSELL, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 1st Aug., 1887).—Educ. at Epsom Coll. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto, and asst. settlt. offcr., Oct., 1916; jt. mag., June, 1917; on mily. duty, Sept., 1917, to Jan., 1919; special duty at Lucknow, Oct., 1922 to Feb., 1923; settlt. offcr., Oct., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; confd., July, 1928; offg. presdnt., ct. of wards, Feb., 1935, to Nov., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1944.

VOHRA, SAHDEV, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 5th July, 1917).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1940, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

VOMBATHKERE PANDRANG ROW (see Pandrang Row, V.).

VORLEY, JOHN STUART, C.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 19th Dec., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1924; C.B.E., Jan., 1942.

VYALL, FREDERICK STANLEY, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 8th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; on mil. service from 22nd Aug., 1916 to 25th Nov., 1919; exec. engr., Aug., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1930; confd., Dec., 1935; retd., Jan., 1942.

VYAS, BHANUSHANKAR SUNDARJI, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bombay) (b. 19th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; in Bhavnagar State as state engr., from May, 1924 to Jan., 1930; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; confd. as suptg. engr., Apl., 1944.

VYAS, DEVSHANKAR VAJESHANKAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 14th Aug., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. in 1923; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; offg. judge and sess. judge, March, 1931; and again, Feb., 1933; confd., Nov., 1934; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1942.

WACE, ERNEST WILLIAM CORNISH, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1914; on mily. duty from 5th March, 1915 to 17th Dec., 1917; asst. supt., Apl., 1918; supt., Sept., 1924; asst. to dep. inspr. genl., C.I.D., Dec., 1926; dep. inspr. genl. (confd.), Dec., 1939; addtl. ditto, C.I.D., March, 1940 to July, 1940; C.I.E., June, 1941.

WACE, FERDINAND BLYTH, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (commissioner, Punjab) (b. 21st Feb., 1891).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 14th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from Sept., 1916, to Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1923; colonization officer, Nili Bar, Nov., 1924; dep. commr. (prov.), Oct., 1926; confd., Oct., 1928; regr. co-op. societies, Punjab, Apl., 1933, and again, Jan., 1937; addtl. secty. to govt., Punjab, July, 1935; offg. commr., Oct., 1936; commr., June, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1939.

WADDELL, GILBERT, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 24th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1914; offg. supt., Apl., 1920; confd., Feb., 1926; confd. in sel. grade, Oct., 1940.

WADDY, REV. RICHARD PATTERSON STACY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 31st May, 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., March, 1935; junr. chaplain, March, 1936.

WADE, HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. offcr., accis. dept.) (b. 7th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at Preston Gr. Sch.; served as second class clerk, India store depot, from 27th Jan., 1913; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 28th Feb., 1913, and attd. to acctng. genl.'s dept.; on mily. service from Aug., 1914 to April, 1919; services lent to high commr. for India from Apl., 1921; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; higher exec. offcr., Apl., 1925.

WADHAMS, JOHN OSWALD, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 13th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst.

consvr., 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Aug., 1929.

WADIA, BOMANJI JAMSHEDJI, late Judicial dept., Bombay.—Apptd. offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, 17th June, 1930; confd., June, 1931; retd., June, 1941.

WADIA, DARASHAW NOSHERWAN, M.A., B.Sc., F.G.S., F.R.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 23rd Oct., 1883).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

WADIA, HIRJIBHOY HORMUSJI, M.A., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 24th March, 1875).—Retd., March, 1935.*

WADIA, MANECK DHUNJISHAW, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col. (retd.), late I.M.S. (Punjab) (b. 5th July, 1883).—Retd., July, 1938.*

WADIA, SIR NAVROJI JEHANGIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 27th Aug., 1884).—Educ. at St. Vincent's High Sch., Poona; Bombay Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 19th Nov., 1909, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; actg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1923; dep. sec., govt. of Bombay, legal dept., and sec. to the leg. council (in addn.) Aug., 1925; reforms secy. (temp.), Dec., 1927; judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, Jan., and again June, 1933; confd., Dec., 1933; chairman, Bombay provl. delimitn. cttee., June, 1935; kt., Jan., 1944.

WADLEY, KEITH LLOYD HAMILTON, M.B.E., B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., cent. p.w.d. (b. 5th Sept., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1930; transf. to Central P.W.D., Oct., 1935; exec. engr., Apl., 1940; M.B.E., June, 1943.

WADSWORTH, SIDNEY, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 21st Dec., 1888).—Educ. at Loughborough Gr.
