Sch. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 8th Dec., 1913; and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. censor, Madras, Oct., 1914; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; temp. under sec. to govt., rev. (spl.) dept., June, 1918; sec., board of revenue, land revenue, Aug., 1922; actg. registrar, high ct., July, 1925; confd., Sept., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1926; confd., July, 1927; judge, high ct., Madras, June, 1935.

WADSWORTH, SYDNEY HERBERT, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 25th Dec., 1881).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 26th June, 1905; minor staff clerk, Sept., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from Oct., 1920; higher exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; suptg. exec. offcr., Oct., 1930; retd., Dec., 1941.

WAGH, MAHARUDRA GANAPAT, Indian Police (Bombay) (b. 8th Nov., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Feb., 1940.

WAGLE, NITYANAND MANGESH, M.A., M.Sc. (Econ.), Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Theosophical National Sch., Benares, Benares and Bombay Univ. and London Sch. of Economics; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 20th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

WAGSTAFF, HENRY WYNTER, M.C., (member R.E., Indian State Rlys. (member, rly. bd.) (b. 19th July, 1890).—First commn. dated 23rd Dec., 1910; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., June, 1922; offg. supervisor of rly. lab., rly. bd., Apl., 1931; offcr. on spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., Aug., 1936; conciliation offcr. (rlys.), Dec., 1937; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), Oct., 1939; dir., rly. bd., Dec., 1939;

col., 1940; dep. ch. engr. (perm.), Jan., 1942 member, rly. bd., May, 1942.

WAGSTAFFE, KEITH DESMOND, Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 15th June, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 20th Nov., 1927; confd., Nov., 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1932; confd., Mar. 1942; King's police and fire service medal, June, 1942.

WAH, LAO HTIN (see Lao Htin Wah).

WAIGHT, HARRY GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 16th March, 1892).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1928; sec., judl. dept., Bengal, Jan., 1944.

WAITE, HERBERT WILLIAM, C.I.E., late Indian Police (N.W.F.P.) (b. 30th June, 1887).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., Apl., 1909; supt., Sept., 1917; prinpl. police training sch., Phillour, Feb., 1926; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1928; and again, June, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1938; retd., 1942.

WAITE, REV. FRANCIS EDWARD WESTON, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estb. (junr. chaplain) (b. 15th Apl., 1905).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras diocese, July, 1936; junr. chaplain, July, 1938.

WAJAHAT HUSAIN, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 23rd Aug., 1894).—Educ. at presidency coll., Calcutta, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 9th Jan., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1925; confd., June, 1932; del. to Indian round table conf., London, 3rd session, Nov., 1932; minister, Kashmir Darbar, July, 1932; secty., p.w.d., b. and r., Jan., 1938; retd., Aug., 1943.

WAKEFIELD, EDWARD BIRKBECK, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 24th July, 1903).—Educ. at Haileybury and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec., self-govt., dep., Punjab, Sept., 1930; asst. commr., Ajmer Merwara, Dec., 1930; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, March, 1933; on foreign service in Kalat state, Oct., 1933; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Feb., 1937; sec. to resdt. for Punjab States, Oct., 1937; presdt., council of regency, Nabha State, Nov., 1939; on deptn., 1941; on for. serv., Rewa States, Dec., 1943.

WAKELY, SIR LEONARD DAY, K.C.I.E., C.B., B.A., B.Sc., late India Office (b. 29th March, 1880).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junior clerk, judl. and public dept., 15th Oct., 1902; resdt. clerk, Nov., 1902; private sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Apl., 1904; private sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, March, 1906 to Dec., 1909; senr. clerk, rev. and stat. dept., Oct., 1909; asst. sec., political dept., March, 1917, but temply. retained in rev. and stat. dept., until Apl., 1919; acting sec., pol. dept., Feb., 1921; confd. as sec., pol. dept., Jan., 1924; C.B., June, 1924; actg. asst. under-secy. of state, Jan., 1929; confd., July, 1930; commr. of inc.-tax, Nov., 1930; actg. dep. under-secy. of state, Sept., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934; K.C.I.E., June, 1935; compiled decennial report on moral and material progress of India, 1913; retd., Oct.

1941; re-employed as asst. under-secty. of state, Oct., 1941 to Oct., 1943.

WAKELY, LEONARD JOHN DEAN, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep.-sec. to the govt. of India, defence dept.) (b. 18th June, 1909).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., Christ Church, Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., pol. dept., Nov., 1937; under-secty. defence dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1938; under-secty. to the Govt.-genl. (defence co-ordn.), Dec., 1938; under-sect. to the Govt. of India, defence co-ordn. dept. in addn., Feb., 1939; M.B.E., Jan., 1942; dep. sec., govt. of India (defence), Aug., 1942; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

WALI MUHAMMAD, M.A., Ph.D., late Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 18th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1919; asst. sec. to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., June, 1920; with Lucknow Univ. as prof. of physics from 31st Aug., 1921; retd., Aug., 1941.

WALI-ULLAH, MOHAMMED (judge, high ct., Allahabad).—Apptd., Apl., 1944.

WALKER, FREDERIC PERCY, late Indian Police (b. 17th Nov., 1882).—Retd., July, 1937.*

WALKER, G. E. D., Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., 27th Nov., 1934; asst. to pol. offcr., Sadiya, May, 1937; a.d.c. to the govt. of Assam, Aug., 1940; asst. pol. offcr., Mar., 1942; pol. offcr., Aug., 1942.

WALKER, GAVIN, late Indian Police (b. 29th Nov., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Bengal, Nov., 1933; confd., June, 1936; retd.

WALKER, GEORGE DAVID, M.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 23rd Sept., 1884).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edin., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 4th Dec., 1908, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, Apl., 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1916 to Apl., 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; dep. commr., Nov., 1924; offg. commr., Nov., 1932; commr., Oct., 1935; Assam repres. to Indian legis. assembly (nom.), Jan., 1938; civ. liaison offr., Mar., 1942, to Sept., 1943; retd., Dec., 1943.

WALKER, SIR GILBERT THOMAS, Kt., C.S.I., M.A., D.Sc., Ph.D., F.R.S., late Meteorological Dept.—Dir.-genl. of observatories, Sept., 1906*.

WALKER, HAROLD MACKENZIE, Indian State Ry's. (divl. rolling stock offcr., N.W. rly.) (b. 26th July, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 17th Nov., 1915; divl. rolling stock offcr., Nov., 1928; offg. divl. supt. (junr.), N.W. rly., Jan., 1936; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Sept., 1936 to Sept., 1937; dep. ch. mechl. engr. (prov.), May, 1944.

WALKER, JAMES, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 4th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1925; dep. dir., dir.-genl. of supply, June, 1940.

WALKER, RONALD LESLIE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty. fin. dept., Bengal (b. 9th Apl., 1896).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-sect. to govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., March, 1924; under-sect., finl., commerce and marine depts., Nov., 1924; offg. dep. sec. to ditto, March, 1926;

offg. secty., Indian Tariff Board, Apl., 1928; confd., Feb., 1929; offg. dir.-genl., comml. intell., Nov., 1928; offg. magte. and collr., July, 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. dep. secty., fin., commerce and marine depts., Bengal, March, 1934; confd., Sept., 1934; offg. secty., ditto, Oct., 1934; secty., fin. dept. (confd.), Aug., 1939; C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

WALKER, THOMAS JACK, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 4th July, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 15th Dec., 1923; offg. addl. supt., March, 1932; supt., July, 1936; O.B.E., Jan., 1945.

WALKER, ULRIC WILLIAM FERRIER, M.C., M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (dir., vety. serv., Punjab) (b. 3rd Dec., 1891).—Joined the service, Aug., 1920; profcr. of surgry., Punjab vety. coll., Oct., 1920; offg. princpl., ditto, March, 1929; Sept., 1930, Apl., 1932 and Nov., 1933; offg. dir. vety. service, Punjab, Apl., 1935; dir., ditto, Jan., 1940.

WALKER, WILLIAM FRANK, O.B.E., M.C., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., United Provs.) (b. 11th July, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; suptg. engr., Nov., 1940; O.B.E., Jan., 1943; ch. engr. (confd.), Nov., 1943.

WALL, WILLIAM GEORGE POYNER, M.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., trng. coll., Allahabad) (b. 7th March, 1889).—Joined the service as prof., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, 30th Nov., 1916; vice-princpl., training coll., Allahabad, Aug., 1923; offg. dir., technological laboratory, Bombay, Apl., 1926; princpl., govt. inter. coll., Allahabad, Dec., 1927; on spl. duty, office of dir. of public instrn., Apl., 1929; offg. inspr. of schools, Oct., 1930; confd., Nov., 1931; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., United Provs., Apl., 1932; princpl. trng. coll., Allahabad, and inspr. of schools, Oct., 1933; dir. of public instrn., May, 1943.

WALL, ROY MANSFIELD, Indian Police (Punjab) (b. 30th Jan., 1920).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Oct., 1939.

WALL, WILLIAM RICHARD, O.B.E. (commr. of inc.-tax, Central and United Provs.) (b. 9th Nov., 1893).—Apptd. asst. commr. of inc.-tax, B. & O., 1st Dec., 1923; offg. commr. of inc.-tax, Apl., 1930, variously, to March, 1937; commr. of inc.-tax, Central and United Provs., March, 1937; commr., 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

WALLACE, CHARLES LINDSAY, M.C., B.A., late Indian C.S., United Provs. (b. 22nd Nov., 1887).—Retd., July, 1938.*

WALLACE, HILL CECIL, B.A., B.E., V.D., late Indian State Ry's. (b. 18th Nov., 1884).—Retd., Jan., 1937.*

WALLACE, REV. JOHN MURDOCK, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 3rd Oct., 1907).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras Diocese, 29th Apl., 1943.

WALLACE, WALTER IAN JAMES, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 18th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Bedford Modern and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1933; provl., Dec., 1937; confd., March, 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1943.

WALLER, SIR DAVID GRIERSON, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 4th July, 1872).—Retd., July, 1932.*

WALLER, RIGHT REV. EDWARD HARRY MANSFIELD, D.D., M.A. (Cantab.) late Madras

Ecclesi. Dept. (b. 8th Dec., 1871).—Bishop of Madras from 1st Jan., 1923 to Nov., 1941.*

WALLER, FREDERICK JOHN, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Jan., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1938.*

WALLEY, ARTHUR FRANKS, Indian State Ryrs. (supt. of tels., Burma Ryrs.) (b. 14th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. tel. supt., 16th Nov., 1914; tel. supt., Nov., 1925.

WALLEY, DAVID, Indian C.S. (dep. sec., rev., United Provs.) (b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Gr. Sch., Swansea, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dep. secty., rev. dept., United Provs., Apl., 1939; dep. ditto, Nov., 1939.

WALLIS, SIR JOHN EDWARD POWER, Kt., M.A., late Judl. Serv., Madras; resigned, July, 1921*

WALLIS, L. G. C., (see Coke-Wallis, L. G.).

WALLIS, WILLIAM CLIFFORD, India Office (b. 21st Apl., 1900).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 11th Aug., 1919, after previous service from 19th Oct., 1917; second class clerk, 29th Apl., 1920; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; actg. higher ditto, Sept., 1936; confd., Jan., 1938; asst. admin. offcr., June, 1939; resdt. clerk, Apl., 1940 to Feb., 1942; pte. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state, Aug., 1941 to June, 1942; on loan to min. of prodn., June, 1942; ditto, govt. of India, July, 1944.

WALMSLEY, SIR HUGH, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal)—Retd., Nov., 1926.*

WALMSLEY, JAMES ANDREW, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bihar) (b. 4th Oct., 1912).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937; and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec., govt. of Bihar, fin. dept., May, 1940; sec. to govt., Oct., 1942; asst. fin. advr., supply, govt. of India, fin. and com. pool, Oct., 1943.

WALSH, SIR CECIL HENRY, Kt., K.C., M.A. (Oxon.), Barr., late Judl. dept., United Provs.—Retd., Nov., 1928.

WALSH, ERIC, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th June, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 20th Nov., 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. supt., May, 1927; confd., May, 1937.

WALSH, WILFRED CULLEN DE CLANCY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 5th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922.

WALSH-ATKINS, LEONARD BRIAN, B.A., India Office (asst. principal, econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 15th March, 1915).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., 11th Oct., 1937; resident clerk, May, 1939, to Apl., 1940; on mily. serv. from Nov., 1940.

WALTERS, REV. THOMAS, Indian Ecclesi. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 27th March, 1900).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras Diocese, Oct., 1938; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1940; on mily. duty from Sept., 1939.

WALTON, SIR CUSACK, Kt., D.S.O., Col., R.E., retd., late Indian State Railways (N.W. rly.) (b. 26th Feb., 1878).—Retd., Feb., 1933.*

WALTON, SIR JOHN CHARLES, K.C.I.E., C.B., M.C., M.A., India Office (actg. under-sec. of state for Burma) (b. 14th March, 1885).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (Arnold Prize, 1909); secty.'s dept., Admiralty,

Dec., 1908; junior clerk, India Office (mil. dept.), 29th Oct., 1909; resdt. clerk, Sept., 1910; transf. to judl. and public dept., Dec., 1911; reading clerk to council, Dec., 1911; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, July, 1913; on mily. serv. from May, 1916, to Dec., 1918; senr. clerk, March, 1917; asst. sec., March, 1921; sec. to Indian delgns. to Assembly of League of Nations, 1920 and 1923; actg. sec., industries and overseas dept., Jan. to March, 1924; a delg. for India, intl. opium conf., 1924-25; adv. intl. labour conf., 1927; advr. and sub. delg., ditto, 1928; actg. sec., pol. dept., Jan., 1929; C.B., Jan., 1930; sec., pol. (external) dept., Apl., 1930; asst. under-sec. of state, July, 1936; commr. of inc.-tax, June, 1940; actg. under-sec. of state for Burma, May, 1941; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1942.

WANCHOO, KAILAS NATH, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 25th Feb., 1903).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 1st Dec., 1926, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Oct., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge Oct., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936.

WANCHOO, NIRANJAN NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. sec., govt. of India (food)) (b. 1st May, 1910).—Educ. at Rangmahal Mission High Sch., Lahore, Univ. of the Punjab and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934 and served in Bihar, and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec., govt. of Orissa, h. and r. depts., Aug., 1938; on spec. duty, Madras, to Oct., 1940; under-sec., central govt., commerce dept., Nov., 1940; ditto, food, Dec., 1942; dep. sec., food dept., Feb., 1944; offcr. on spec. duty, Persian Gulf war trade bureau, March, 1942.

WARD, HUMPHREY PLOWDEN, B.A. (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 20th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1925; forest utilisn. offcr., and in charge, timber supply circle, Nov., 1939.

WARD, LANCELOT BANGOR, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 14th Sept., 1883).—Retd., Dec., 1935.*

WARD, RICHARD PERCYVALE, C.I.E., M.C., D.F.C., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Orissa) (b. 28th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 7th March, 1921; arrived 1st May, 1921, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1927; confd., Dec., 1935; sec. to govt. of Orissa, rev. and devel. depts., March, 1940; polt. and appts. sec., July, 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1943.

WARD, WILLIAM JOSEPH, B.A., late Imperial Customs Service (b. 11th March, 1887).—Joined the service as probnr., 6th Oct., 1911; asst. collr., Oct., 1913; offg. collr., Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1936; retd., Feb., 1941.

WARE, FRANK, C.I.E., F.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (dir. of animal husbandry) (b. 22nd Feb., 1886).—Apptd. supt., civil vety. dept., Madras, Nov., 1912; ch. supt., civil vety. dept., Madras, Oct., 1925; offg. dir., Imperial Inst., vety. research, Muktesar, March, 1926, and again, June, 1929; prin., vety. coll., Madras, Nov., 1926; offg. vety. adviser to govt. of Madras, Nov., 1927; confd., May, 1928; dir. of vety. services, Madras, Sept., 1928; offg. dir., Imperial Inst. of vety. research, June, 1929; confd., Sept., 1930; offg. animal husbandry commr., Imperial council of agric. research, var., from Aug., 1935; confd., Jan., 1939;

C.I.E., May, 1937; dir. of animal husbandry, Jan., 1944.

WARMINGTON, REV. GUY WILSON, M.A., late Eccles. Dept. (b. 22nd May, 1886).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

WARNER, HERBERT WALTER, Indian Police (asst. commr.) (b. 21st Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 19th May, 1920; offg. dist. supt., June, 1926; confd., Sept., 1928; asst. commr., Rangoon, Jan., 1936.

WARREN, AUBREY GRANVILLE, Lieut.-Col. (elec. inspr.) (b. 17th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. elec. inspr., United Provs., 1st Oct., 1914; offg. ditto, Sept., 1918; confd., Nov., 1934.

WARREN, DOUGLAS DAINTRY, C.I.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 17th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., June, 1922; offg. under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1926; pte. secty. to govt., June, 1929; dir. of land records, Madras, Apl., 1930; on for serv. as commr., corp. of Madras, June, 1931; collr., July, 1940; sec., p.w.d., Madras, Dec., 1940; jt. sec., govt. of India (war transp.), Apl., 1943; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

WARREN, WILLIAM DOUGLAS MARDEN, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bihar) (b. 26th July, 1895).—Joined the service as dep. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, 19th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Bihar, 1924.

WARTH, FREDERICK JOHN, B.Sc., M.Sc., (Bir.), late Indian Agrl. Service (Burma) (b. 23rd March, 1879).—Retd., Nov., 1935.*

WARWICK, GEORGE DUNGATE, B.A., S.Bc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 25th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Jan., 1927; dir., forest sch., Dec., 1936.

WASSOODREW, K. B. (see Vasudev, K. B.).

WATAL, ANAND PRAKASH (see Anand Prakash, W.).

WATERBURY, REV. FRANK GARFIELD, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 8th Nov., 1885).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 25th Jan., 1922; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1924; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1934; retd., Nov., 1940.

WATERFALL, CHARLES FRANCIS, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief commr., A. & N. Islands) (b. 24th Feb., 1888).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 26th Nov. 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr., dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1921; confd., Oct., 1924; dir. of land records, Central Provs., March, 1930; secty. in settlements and land records, etc., depts., March, 1930; offg. finance sec. to govt., Central Provs., March, 1933; offg. commr., July, 1933; offg. ch. sec. to govt., Central Provs., June, 1934; C.I.E., June, 1935; ch. sec. to govt., Central Provs., Oct., 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937; chief commr., Andamans, Feb., 1938.

WATERFIELD, THOMAS EDWARD, Indian C.S. (regr., high ct., appellate side, Bombay) (b. 8th Oct., 1905).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; regr., high ct., appellate side, Bombay, Dec., 1938.

WATKINS-MENCE, CHARLES ROWLATT, C.I.E., B.A., late Impl. Customs Service (b. 17th Sept., 1882).—Retd., July, 1936.*

WATNEY, NORMAN CHARLES, Indian State Rlys. (div. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1901).—Asst. rolling stock offr., Jan., 1926; div. mech. engr., provl., June, 1939; confd., Aug., 1940.

WATSON, ANDREW HUGH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 24th July, 1911).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 4th Dec., 1930; confd., Dec., 1932.

WATSON, CHARLES MARCUS GUY, Indian Police (b. 5th Nov., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1938.

WATSON, DENNIS GEORGE, Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Central Provs.) (b. 23rd Apl., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Feb., 1912; dist. supt., Apl., 1924; inspr.-genl., police, Indore, Oct., 1929, and again, May, 1937; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1936; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937; on for service as I.G. of police, Indore State, March, 1937 to Feb., 1941; confd. as dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1941; confd. as inspr.-genl., Aug., 1944.

WATSON, ERSKINE PHILLIP, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st Oct., 1886).—Retd., Dec., 1937.*

WATSON, GEORGE LEO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at the County Sch., Whitby, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

WATSON, JOHN DOUGLAS, Indian State Rlys. (bridge engr.) (b. 17th March, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. exec. bridge engr., 26th March, 1926; exec. engr., bridges, N.W. rly., March, 1933.

WATSON, JOHN DYKES, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 4th Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933; services perm. transf. to M.S. and M. rly., Oct., 1937.

WATSON, ROBERT, O.B.E., N.D.A., Indian Agric. Service (b. 9th June, 1894).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, 28th Dec., 1921; O.B.E., Jan., 1944.

WATT, WILLIAM HUTCHEON, O.B.E., R.N.R., (ret'd.), Lieut.-Commr., Civil Aviation Direct. (chief aerodrome offr.) (b. 8th March, 1892).—Joined the service as chief aerodrome offr., on 1st March, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1939.

WATTAL, PYARE KISHAN, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 10th May, 1890).—Joined the service, 9th May, 1912; asst. acctnt. genl., Nov., 1912; dep. ditto, Apl., 1924; examr., outside audit, Bombay, Nov., 1924; services placed at disposal of Kashmir durbar, 11th June, 1926; dep. acctnt. genl. (civil), Punjab, Nov., 1932; ditto (works), Oct., 1933; offg. acct. genl., Central Provs., Apl., 1935; ditto, Madras, March, 1936; confd. as acct. genl., Punjab, Sept., 1939; confd., acct. genl. (cl. I), Nov., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1944.

WATTERS, WILLIAM JAMES, late Indian State Rlys., E.I. rly. (b. 26th Sept., 1882).—Retd., Nov., 1939.*

WATTS, HAROLD CHRISTOPHER, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1895).—Retd., July, 1939.*

WAUGH, ARTHUR ALLEN, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (sec., supply, govt. of India) (b. 25th July, 1891).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1914, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Apl., 1918; offg. excise commr., March, 1924; mag. and

collr. (provl. sub.), July, 1930; confd., Apl., 1931; sec. to govt., U.P., rev. and p.w.d. (b. and r.), Apl., 1934, and again, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., May, 1937; settlement commr., United Provs., Apl., 1939; war prodn. commr., and sec., indus. dept., United Provs., Feb., 1942; C.S.I., Jan., 1943; sec., govt. of India, supply dept., May, 1943.

WAVELL, FIELD MARSHAL THE RT. HON. ARCHIBALD PERCIVAL, of Cyrenaica and of Winchester, P.C., G.C.B., G.M.S.I., G.M.I.E., C.M.G. M.C. (b. May, 1883)—Apptd. member of govr.-genl.'s exec. cl. and C.-in-C., July, 1941; again, March, 1942; Viceroy and govr.-genl., Oct., 1943.

WAZIR CHAND, L. MEHRA, O.B.E., Indian Police (b. 11th Sept., 1907).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Punjab, 6th Jan., 1930; confd., Feb., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1941.

WAZIR HASAN, SIR SAYID, Kt., B.A., LL.B. (late chief judge, Oudh) (b. 14th May, 1874)—Retd., May, 1934.*

WEAKFORD, CHARLES FREDERICK, M.B.E., late Printing and Stationery Dept. (b. 29th Sept., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st June, 1915; on deptn. to pol. resdy. in Persian Gulf, March, 1916; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; mang., form printing press, Caccutta, March, 1924; overseer, G.I. Press, Calcutta, June, 1924; offg. dep. contr., Jan., 1925; confd., Oct., 1926; offg. contr., May, 1932; and again, Jan., 1935; confd., March, 1935; retd., Sept., 1941.

WEALE, CYRIL, O.B.E., Indian Police (asst. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 12th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 29th Nov., 1921; addl. supt., March, 1926; supt., March, 1932; prin., police training coll., Sardah, June, 1933; and again, Jan., 1935; asst. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1942.

WEBB, SIR CHARLES MORGAN, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (ret'd.) (Burma).—Retd., Feb., 1926.*

WEBB, HORACE OSCAR, Engr.-comdr., R.I.M. Marine Dept., Bombay (b. 17th Nov., 1881).—Apptd. 2nd engr. and ship surveyor, March, 1927; princpl. engr. and ship surveyor, Bombay, Feb., 1929; engr. and ship surveyor, Karachi, Aug., 1931.

WEBB, JOHN ROBERT DOUGLAS, O.B.E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 4th Jan., 1888).—Retd., July, 1936.*

WEBB, WILFRED FRANCIS, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Feb., 1897).—Joined the dept. as asst. commr., Ajmer Merwara, 6th May, 1921; on duty under High Commr., Bagdad, from Dec. 1918; on for service in Tonk State from Oct., 1924; under-sec. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1929; sec. to ditto, March, 1930; on for service in Rewa; diwan, Bundi State, Feb., 1932; pol. agent, Orissa States, Sambalpur, Oct., 1937; ditto, Chhalthesgarh, Raipur, May, 1938; pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Oct., 1939; diwan Cutch State, Oct., 1941; C.I.E., Jan., 1944; pol. agt., Malwa, May, 1944.

WEBB-JOHNSON, STANLEY C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian Legislv. Dept. (2nd solicitor) (b. 1st March, 1888).—Apptd. asst. solcr. to govt. of India, Feb., 1920; 2nd solcr., Nov., 1927; O.B.E., Dec., 1930; offg. solcr., variously, from Apl., 1933, C.I.E., June, 1941.

WEBSTER, ANDREW McMILLAN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 12th March, 1915).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th

Sept., 1938; confd., 18th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

WEBSTER, GEOFFREY ALAN, Indian Police (b. 3rd March, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., Nov., 1938; confd. Nov., 1940; addl. supt., Oct., 1943.

WEBSTER, GEORGE EVELYN EDEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 21st Apl., 1905).—Educ. at Calabar High Sch., Kingston, Jamaica, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1929; arrived Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1932, July, 1934, and again, Sept., 1935; ditto home and pol. dept., Feb., 1935; offg. dep. commr., March, 1938; commr., provl., Aug., 1939; confd., Oct., 1940.

WEIGHTMAN, HUGH, C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 29th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Hymer's Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Jan., 1922; arrived, 17th Feb., 1922, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, June, 1925 to Feb., 1926; offg. under-sec. to govt., Assam, Oct., 1927; confd., March, 1928; under-sec. to A.G.G. in C.I., Apl., 1929; asst. pol. agt. and asst. commr. in Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1930; sec. to A.G.G. and ch. commr., Baluchistan, Apl., 1932; pol. agt., Kalat, June, 1935; ditto, Bahrain, Oct., 1937; again, May and Oct., 1938; dep. sec., ext. affairs dept., Oct., 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941; jt. sec., ext. affairs dept., Sept., 1941; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, May, 1944.

WEIR, JAMES LESLIE ROSE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Police Service (b. 29th Jan., 1883).—Retd., March, 1938.*

WEIR, RALPH SOMERVILLE, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service, U.P. (b. 10th Sept., 1884).—Retd., Sept., 1939.*

WELLBORNE, CYRIL DE MONTFORT, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., late Indian Police (Burma) (b. 2nd July, 1884).—Retd., June, 1939.*

WELLS, ARTHUR GEOFFREY, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 10th Apl., 1903).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr.; asst. judge and sess. judge, Feb., 1933; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Oct., 1935; ditto, Bombay, from Feb., 1936.

WELLS, HARRY LESLIE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 25th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

WELSH, JOHN ALBERT VICTOR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 24th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as forest engr., Burma, 30th Nov., 1921; trans. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

WEMYSS, FREDERICK FLEMING, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 7th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 6th Sept., 1921; asst. commr. of police, Ran- goon, Feb., 1927; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., Dec., 1930.

WESSON, R. A., Major, I.M.S. (civ. surg.) (b. 7th Feb., 1895).—Date of first commn., 2nd Feb., 1932 civil surg., United Provs., Dec., 1934.

WEST, ALGERNON SYDNEY, Indian State Ry.ys., Coal Dept. (mang., coke ovens) (b. 9th June, 1897).—Asst. mang., coke ovens, June, 1921; mang., coke ovens, Nov., 1936.

WEST, EDWARD JOHN WRIGHT, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 4th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

WEST, STEWART ELLES LAWRENCE, C.I.E., O.B.E., V.D., Indian State Ry.ys. (dir., rly. bd.) (b. 2nd March, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 1st March, 1909; on mil. duty, 1st May, 1916; O.B.E., Feb., 1919; dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. ch. comml. mang., Nov., 1925; transpn. supt., Nov., 1930; offg. dep. ch. comml. mang., Feb., 1932; and again, Jan., 1934; offg. dep. ch. oper. supt., N.W. rly., May, 1933; offg. divl. supt., junior, N.W. rly., March, 1934; confd., Sept., 1934; ditto (senior), May, 1937; dep. ch. oper. supt. (provl.), Dec., 1937; dir., rly. bd., July, 1940; ch. comml. mang., N.W. rly., Oct., 1940; C.I.E., June, 1943.

WEST, WILLIAM FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 13th June, 1882).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 22nd Nov., 1900; jun. clerk, Apl., 1909; senr. clerk, Nov., 1915; transfid. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; contr., tpl., 1922; actg. dir. of purchase, May, 1940; actg. dir.-genl., store dept., July, 1941.

WESTCOTT, THE MOST REV. FOSS, M.A., D.D., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Bishop of Calcutta and Metropolitan of India and Ceylon) (b. 23rd Oct., 1863).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Peterhouse, Cambridge; Bishop in Chota Nagpore, 1905-1919; Bishop of Calcutta, Aug., 1919.

WESTERDALE, JOSEPH STANLEY, B.Sc., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 10th Apl., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Apl., 1921; offg. sanyt. engr., Apl., 1922; confd., Oct., 1925; on foreign service under Travancore durbar, Aug., 1930 to Aug., 1933; suptg. engr., May, 1938; ch. engr., July, 1940; retd., Apl., 1941.

WESTLAKE, ALAN ROBERT CECIL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 18th July, 1894).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Frognal, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 7th Aug., 1921; arrived, 7th Oct., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; subcollr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. collr., Feb., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1931; ditto (provl. subs.), Sept., 1935; sec., bd. of rev., Madras, Aug., 1933; collr., and dist. mag. (subst.), Sept., 1936; dir. of agric., June, to Dec., 1940; sec., educ. and pub. health dept., June, 1941; ditto, rev. dept., July, 1941; ditto, devel. dept., Jan., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1943; memb., bd. of rev., Apl., 1944.

WESTOBY, EDWARD HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. offcr.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1900).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 6th Feb., 1920; actg. exec. offcr., June, 1927; confd., Nov., 1929.

WESTON, A. T., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Dept. of Industries, Bengal (dir. of industries), Bengal (b. 10th May, 1882).—Joined the service, 4th March, 1909; temp. works mang., Cossipore, Dec., 1915; dep. dir. of industries, Bengal, Nov., 1920; offg. dir., ditto, Apl., 1925; confd., May, 1927.

WESTON, ERIC B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 8th Dec., 1892).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1916, and

served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge, Sept., 1921; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Jan., 1924; judge and sess. judge, Apl., 1929; judl. asst., and addl. sess. judge, Aden; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Oct., 1933; judl. commr., states of W.I., Sept., 1934; judl. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, June, 1937; judge, ct. of judl. commr., Sind, Nov., 1938; judge, chief ct. of Sind, Apl., 1940; ditto, Bombay high ct., Jan., 1943.

WESTON WAITE, FRANCIS EDWARD, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Dept. (junior chaplain).—Apptd., 2nd July, 1936.

WEYMOUTH, JAMES CHARLES, late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 5th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Dec., 1907; supt., Aug., 1921; asst. inspr.-genl., rly. police, June, 1933; dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1937; retd., March, 1942.

WHEATLEY, WILLIAM GEOFFREY, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr.) (b. 6th June, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 26th Sept., 1924; exec. engr., Apl., 1934; suptg. engr., under govt. of India, ordnance factory, Dec., 1942.

WHEELER, REV. CHARLES ERNEST RUAPRU, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 13th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 20th Dec., 1920; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1924; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1930; retd., Oct., 1942.

WHEELER, SIR EDWARD OLIVER, M.C., Brig., late R.E., Survey of India (b. 18th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd June, 1910; in mil. employ to 8th Dec., 1919; asst. supt., 9th Dec., 1919; offg. dy. supt., Oct., 1920; on depon. with expdn. for ascent of Mount Everest from 15th Apl. to 20th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1926; confd., Oct., 1927; dir., March, 1939; surv.-genl., Apl., 1941; kt., Jan., 1943.

WHEELER, SIR HENRY, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 2nd June, 1870).—Retd., Oct., 1927*

WHELAN, ERNEST CARNEGIE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ry.ys. (Burma) (b. 2nd Nov., 1889).—Retd., May, 1939.*

WHELAN, JOHN PRATT, Indian Police (dist. supt. Central Provs.) (b. 18th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 22nd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Nov., 1927; confd., Nov., 1937; confd., March, 1940; asst. to D.I.G., Oct., 1940.

WHIPP, H., M.B.E., B.Sc., A.M.Inst.C.E. (Executive Engr., Public Health Circle, Bihar).—Joined service, 29th Dec., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936; special offer, in charge of public health circle, March, 1939; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

WHITAKER, CECIL CHARLES, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 6th Nov., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

WHITBY, ROBERT BERTRAM HINDHAUGH, Indian State Ry.ys. (supt., E.I. Ry.) (b. 21st Aug., 1897).—Joined the service, Nov., 1919; asst. traffic supt., Dec., 1920; supt., senr. scale, Apl., 1936.

WHITCOMBE, CHARLES EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 30th Dec., 1887).—Retd., Jan., 1935.*

WHITE, ARTHUR JOHN STANLEY, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 28th Aug., 1896).—Retd., March, 1938.*

WHITE, CHARLES FITZWILLIAM, late Indian State Ry.ys. (b. 3rd Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. works mangr., 7th Jan., 1925; works mangr., Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl.

engr., G.I.P. Rly., Nov., 1933; provl. confd., Sept., 1937; ret'd., Dec., 1940.

WHITE, DESMOND ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 31st May, 1918).—Educ. at Agra Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1941; served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.

WHITE, GERALD SEBASTIAN, O.B.E., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 12th Feb., 1878).—Ret'd., March, 1938.*

WHITE, REV. JACK, Indian Eccles. Estabt., Madras (senr. chaplain) (b. 12th March, 1903).—Apptd. 1st Apl., 1930; junr. chaplain, March, 1932; senr. ditto, March, 1942; actg. Archdeacon, Apl., 1944.

WHITE, JOHN, Capt., M.D., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), I.M.S. (civil surg., Bengal) (b. 14th Feb., 1901).—Date of first commn., Apl., 1933; civil surg., Jan., 1939.

WHITE, JOHN GROVE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 30th Aug., 1882).—Ret'd., Sept., 1935.*

WHITE, LESLIE STUART, late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 30th March, 1888).—Ret'd., March, 1939.*

WHITE, SYDNEY WILFRED, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 22nd June, 1884).—Ret'd., July, 1939*; dep. dir., muntns. prodn., dept. of supply, Nov., 1940.

WHITEHEAD, GEORGE SYDNEY, B.Sc. (Econ.), India Office (exec. offcr., mil. dept.) (b. 15th Nov., 1915).—Apptd. exec. offcr., 12th Dec., 1934; on mil. serv., from June, 1940; actg. higher exec. offcr (in absentia), Jan., 1941.

WHITEHEAD, GEORGE UNSWORTH, late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 5th Nov., 1903).—Ret'd., Apl., 1937.*

WHITEHEAD, THOMAS ALEC, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 8th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1907, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1912; offg. consvr., June, 1923; confd., Feb., 1926; chief consvr., June, 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938; ret'd., July, 1940.

WHITEHOUSE, GEORGE EDWARD, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 22nd July, 1911).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1930; asst. supt., Nov., 1932; offg. A.D.C. to govr., U.P., Dec., 1933; offg. supt., June, 1936; confd., May, 1943.

WHITEHOUSE, VICTOR, Indian Police (b. 31st May, 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 1st Nov., 1935.

WHITFIELD, WILLIAM HARVEY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 18th March, 1917).—Educ. at Harris Acad., Dundee, St. Andrews' Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

WHITLOCK, PERCY ODDIE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 15th March, 1884).—Ret'd., Apl., 1935.*

WHITNEY, ALBERT, late Indian State Ryls. (G.I.P. rly.) (b. 9th March, 1886).—Joined the service, 16th Jan., 1915; dist. signal engr., G.I.P. rly., March, 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1936; ret'd., Feb., 1943.

WHITNEY, WILLIAM WELCH, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 11th May, 1888).—Joined the service, 25th Feb., 1921, as asst. dist. loco. supt.; asst. supt., E.I. rly., Feb., 1925; transpn. supt., E.I. rly., May, 1934; ret'd., March, 1940.

WHITTAKER, ARNOLD, C.I.E., B.Sc., late

Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 27th July, 1900).—Ret'd., Oct., 1939.*

WHITTLE, GEORGE WILLIAM MURDOCH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 7th Apl., 1907).—Educ. at Secondary Sch., Washington Village, Durham, Durham Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Dec., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; sec., steel control bd., July, 1941; dep. sec., supply, govt. of India, Jan., 1942.

WHITTY, SIR JOHN TARLTON, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 1st Feb., 1876).—Ret'd., Feb., 1935.*

WHITWORTH, CHARLES STANLEY, C.I.E., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 14th June, 1880).—Ret'd., June, 1935.*

WHITWORTH, HUGH HOPE ASTON, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 21st May, 1914).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; M.B.E., Jan., 1945.

WHITWORTH, RUDGE, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 13th Oct., 1886).—Apptd. asst. signal engr., Apl., 1920; dist. signal engr., Aug., 1934; signal engr., Nov., 1938; ret'd., Oct., 1941.

WHYTE, SIR ALEXANDER FREDERICK, K.C.S.I., M.A.—Presdt., Indian legisl. assembly., 1920 to 1925.*

WHYTE, FREDERICK HAMILTON, M.B., B.Ch., M.Ch., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg.) (b. 1st Feb., 1893).—First commn. dated 22nd Feb., 1924, in mil. employ prior to 21st Jan., 1932, when he was transferred to civil employ, civil surg., Apl., 1939.

WHYTE, QUINTIN ALEXANDER, M.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 7th March, 1909).—Ret'd., Oct., 1935.*

WICKENDEN, THOMAS DOUGLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 2nd March, 1903).—Educ. at St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 5th Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1929; sub-judge, Oct., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1932, May, 1933, and again, May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1936; under legal trng. from May, 1941; legal sec. to govt., Dec., 1943.

WICKHAM, EDWARD THOMAS RUSCOMBE, M.V.O., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Dept. (b. 4th May, 1890).—Ret'd., Jan., 1935.*

WICKHAM, HARRY TEMPLE, late Indian Police (N.W.F.P.).—Ret'd., June, 1925.*

WILBERFORCE-BELL, SIR HAROLD, K.C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 17th Nov., 1885).—First commn., 29th Nov., 1905; asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, Jan., 1910; on special duty in connection with the Delhi Durbar, 1911; pol. agent, Sorath (Kathiewar), Nov., 1912; tutor and guardian to H.H. Minor Nawab of Junagadh, Apl. to Dec., 1914; on mil. duty 26th Dec., 1914, to 9th Dec., 1919; asst. mil. sec. to H.E. the C.-in-C. in India, May, 1918, to March, 1919; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Feb., 1920; dep. sec., pol. dept., Bombay, 1920; asst. resdt., Aden, March, 1922; pol. agent, Kathiawar, Jan., 1924; advr. to H.H. the Raja of Chamba, 1925; sec. to agent to govr.-genl. in Cent. India, Nov., 1926; pol. agent in Bhopal, May, 1927; ditto in Southern States of Cent. India and in

Malwa, July, 1927; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1928; confd., May, 1929; offg. pol. sec., ditto, July, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; pol. agent, Southern Maratha country states, Apr., 1932; on special duty under for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1932; A.G.G. Deccan states and resdt., Kolhapur, Apr., 1933; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Punjab states, Oct., 1934; resdt., 1st cl., confd., March, 1937; resdt. for Punjab States, Apr., 1937; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938; ret'd., Nov., 1940; author of "The History of Kathiawar," and other works.

WILBY, FREDERICK WILLIAM, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 22nd May, 1882).—Ret'd., May, 1937.*

WILCOCK, JOHN STEWART, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab), Indian C.S. (collr., Orissa) (b. 6th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., pol. and appts. dept., March, 1932; offg. sec. to A.G.G., Eastern States, March, 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1935; pte. sec. to govr., Orissa, Apr., 1936; sec., ditto, Apr., 1937, and 1939; O.B.E., Jan., 1939; agent to the govr. and collr., Feb., 1940; sec., price control and ch. contr. prices and supplies, Apr., 1944.

WILES, SIR GILBERT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (advr. to sec. of state for India) (b. 25th March, 1880).—Educ. at Perse Gr. Sch., and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and regn., Nov., 1910; supt. of land records, Aug., 1915, to May, 1916; temp. dep. dir. of civil supplies, June, 1918; dep. sec. to govt., home dept., Nov., 1921; asst. collr. and sec. to govt., fin. dept., Dec., 1923; collr. and ditto, Oct., 1925; member, legisl. council, 1924, 1927, 1930, 1931, and 1932; also finl. advr. to govt., dev. and p.q. depts., April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; member, tariff bd., Sept., 1933; presdrt., ditto, Oct., 1934; chairman, Bombay Port Trust, June, 1935; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938; ch. sec. to govt. (pol. and serv. dept.), Bombay, Jan., 1938; advr. to govt. of Bombay, Nov., 1939; ret'd., March, 1941; advr. to sec. of state for India, May, 1941.

WILKES, JOHN SEBASTIAN, late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 31st Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1908; asst. supt., May, 1910; supt., Nov., 1922; dep. commr., Apr., 1929; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1934; confd., June, 1936; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938; ret'd., Jan., 1943.

WILKIE, HUGH GRAHAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr.) (b. 13th March, 1893).—Educ. at Uppingham and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to March, 1919; apptd., 7th Oct., 1919; arrived, 9th Dec., 1919, and served in Burma; colonization offcr., Jan., 1922; headqrs. asst., May, 1923; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; confd., May, 1931; administrator of govt. estates, Nov., 1926; land offcr., Rangoon dev. trust, March, 1928; offg. secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, Oct., 1930; secty., lands and reve. dept., May, 1937; commr., Apr., 1939; ch. secty. to govt. of Burma, 1944.

WILKIE, RALPH SCUDAMORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 13th March, 1896).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921;

arrived, 20th Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., June, 1924; asst. settlt. offcr., Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1928; settlt. offcr., Nov., 1930; dep. commr., confd., Oct., 1934.

WILKINS, PAUL BURGMAN, O.B.E., M.C., D.F.C., Indian Police (supdt., Bombay) (b. 11th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1933; supt., Jan., 1936; O.B.E., June, 1942; dep. commr., Bombay, March, 1943.

WILKINSON, REV. ERNEST ROLAND, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 24th July, 1882).—Ret'd., March, 1936.*

WILKINSON, HECTOR RUSSELL, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 11th March, 1888).—Ret'd. Apr., 1939.*

WILLAN, EDWARD GERVASE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 17th May, 1917).—Educ. at Radley and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 28th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Central Prov. as asst. commr.

WILLFORD, GEORGE MARK, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service as extra asst. consvr., July, 1915; dep. consvr., July, 1930.

WILLIAMS, ALFRED ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th May, 1910).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's Sch., Crosby, and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub divl. offcr., Oct., 1937.

WILLIAMS, ARTHUR DE COETLOGAN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (food and supplies commr., Bengal) (b. 27th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Marlborough and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; on mil. duty in England, Aug. to Dec., 1914; arrived, 7th May, 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty in Rawalpindi, Quetta and E. Persia, Dec., 1916, to March, 1919; on mil. duty in Quetta, May to Sept., 1919; jt. mag., 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, 1921; attache, legal. dept., govt. of India, Apr., 1924; services lent as offg. dist. and sess. judge to govt. of Assam, Apr., 1925; offg. secty., legl. dept., govt. of Bengal and secty., legl. council, Feb., 1926; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, May, 1926; addl. dep. secty., legl. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1927; offg. dep. sec., ditto, Apr., 1929; on spl. duty, home dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1931; secty., legl. dept., govt. of Bengal, Feb., 1934; supt. and membr. of legal affairs, Bengal, July, 1934; offg. jt. sec. and draftsman, legl. dept., govt. of India, March, 1935; offg. sec., ditto, Oct., 1935; secty., council of state, Dec., 1935; dep. secty., legl. dept., govt. of India, Apr., 1936; addl. jt. secty., ditto, Nov., 1936; offg. secty. to govr.-genl.'s exec. cl. in addn., Nov., 1936; confd., March, 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938; addl. secty., def. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1938; secty., govr.-genl. def. co-ordn., Dec., 1938; secty., def. co-ordn. dept., govt. of India, in addn., Feb., 1939; sec. to govr., Bengal, Sept., 1942; ch. sec., govt. of Bengal, Sept., 1943, to Oct., 1944; food and civil supplies commr., Oct., 1944.

WILLIAMS, REV. BERNARD RHYS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (chaplain)

(b. 11th July, 1908).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Nov., 1938; apptd. chaplain Oct., 1944.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES FREDERICK VICTOR, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 21st May, 1898).—Educ. at West Ham and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1923, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Feb., 1928; under-secty. to govt., ch. secretariat, Madras, July, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round-table conf., London, Nov.-Dec., 1932; under-secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1933; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1934; secty. to agent of the govt. of India in South Africa, Sept., 1934; collr. and dep. magte., Aug., 1937; collr., civil and mil. station, Bangalore, Aug., 1937; collr., I gr. (provl. subst.), March, 1939; confd. as collr., Nov., 1943; C.I.E., June, 1944.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES ROBERT FARMER, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922; princpl., Madras forest coll., Coimbatore, July, 1924, to July, 1931; consvr., Nov., 1942.

WILLIAMS, CUTHBERT ARTHUR, late Indian Stores Dept. (b. 20th Jan., 1887).—Retd., July, 1935.*

WILLIAMS, DAVID, V.D., Indian State Ryis. (divl. transp. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 12th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as dist. loco. supt., 25th March, 1920; asst. trans. supt., 1st Nov., 1922; supt., May, 1926; offg. divl. trans. supt., variously from Aug., 1926; divl. transp. supt., May, 1942.

WILLIAMS, EDWIN OWEN, Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as forest engr., Punjab, 11th Nov., 1921; lecturer, forest coll., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1938; timber supply circle, March, 1940.

WILLIAMS, EMILY MATILDA, M.B.E., India Office (higher clerical offcr., acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 21st Feb., 1894).—Apptd. clerical offcr., 5th Sept., 1921; higher clerical offcr., Sept., 1932; welfare offcr., 29th Dec., 1941; M.B.E., June, 1944.

WILLIAMS, GEOFFREY BAYNTON, C.I.E., M.C., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 19th Aug., 1896).—First commn. dated 13th Oct., 1914; in mil. employ prior to 27th July, 1925; asst. collr. and magte., Bareilly, July, 1925; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., March, 1926; ditto, in States of W.I., Dec., 1926; guardian and tutor to heir-apparent of Nawab of Junagadh, Dec., 1928; secty. to A.G.G., in the states of W.I., Nov., 1932; under-secty., ditto, Oct., 1933; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha, Dec. 1933; secty. to A.G.G., Eastern states, Dec., 1935; pol. agent, Bhopal, March, 1936; ditto, W. Kathiawar agency, Apl., 1940; ditto, E. Kathiawar agency, Sept., 1941; pol. agt., W. Rajputana States, Dec., 1943; pol. agt., Jaipur, in addn., Apl., 1944; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

WILLIAMS, HAROLD VINITH, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th June, 1898).—Retd., Feb., 1935.*

WILLIAMS, REV. HENRY FRANK FULFORD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 11th Nov., 1885).—Retd., Dec., 1936.*

WILLIAMS, HERBERT BRIAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 8th July, 1918).—Educ. at Newton-in-Makerfield Gr. Sch., Lancs. and

Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn. after seln., in May, 1940; and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

WILLIAMS, JOHN HOWARD FRY, Indian Police (b. 6th Oct., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., 10th Nov., 1935; asst. to pol. offcr., Sadiya, May, 1937; confd. as asst. supt. of police, July, 1937; asst. pol. offcr., Passighat, Jan., 1939; spec. duty, Naga Hills, Apl. to Aug., 1944.

WILLIAMS, RICHARD AELWYN ELLIS, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 5th Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Wales and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sect. to govt., pol. dept., March, 1930; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa feudatory states, March, 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; sec. to govt., rev. and comm. depts., Bihar, July, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

WILLIAMS, RUSHBROOK (see Rushbrook Williams).

WILLIAMS, SEVERN, O.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

WILLIAMS, REV. WILLIAM PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 10th March, 1929; junr. chaplain, March, 1931; offg. archdeacon, Nagpur, Feb., 1936; archdeacon, Nagpur, Oct., 1938; senr. chaplain, March, 1941.

WILLIAMSON, ALEC, C.M.G., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 7th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Kirkcudbright Academy, Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. offcr.; on mil. service, Jan., 1918, to Apl., 1919; dep. chairman, bd. of trustees for development of Rangoon, March, 1925; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), Feb., 1926; offg. land offcr., Rangoon Development Trust, March, 1927; offcr. i/c., Mandalay municipal cttee., Oct., 1929; offg. chairman, Rangoon dev. trust, July, 1933; excise commr., March, 1934; commr. of settlt. and land records, Burma, March, 1936; offg. commr., Feb., 1938; confd., May, 1939; offg. finl. commr., Nov., 1940; C.M.G., Jan., 1944.

WILLIAMSON, HAROLD, O.B.E., M.D., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., ret'd., late I.M.S. (b. 26th Feb., 1891).—First commn., 3rd Dec., 1921; offg. agency surg., Quetta, Apl., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, Apl., 1935; civil surg., Sibi and Loralai, Oct., 1935; offg. resdy. surg., Mysore, March, 1937.

WILLIAMSON, SIR HORACE KT., C.I.E., M.B.E., late Indian Police (United Provs.) (b. 16th July, 1880).—Retd., Sept., 1936*; member, council of India, Dec., 1936; advisor to sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937, to March, 1942.

WILLMOTT, HENRY CLAUDE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 18th May, 1914).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; served with govt. of Assam from Mar., 1942; asst. dir. civ. def., Apl., 1942; dep., dir., ditto, July, 1942; addl. dep. commr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1943.

WILLMOTT, HERBERT, India Audit Office (higher exec. offcr.) (b. 9th June, 1887).—Apptd. clerical offr., 9th Nov., 1921; exec. offr., Apl., 1922; hr. exec. offr., March, 1936.

WILLOUGHBY, VERE WILLIAM DIGBY, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Aug., 1901).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; in mil. employ to 20th June, 1927; vice-consul, Ahwaz, Dec., 1928; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Nov., 1931; under-secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, March, 1932; secty. to ditto (temp.), July, 1934; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Aug., 1935; ditto, Zohb, Oct., 1935; vice-consul, Zabul, June, 1938; secty. to resdt. for Kolhapur and the Deccan States, Aprl., 1939; asst. pol. agent, Orissa States, Dec., 1941; consul, Kerman, Nov., 1944.

WILSON, ANDREW, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 27th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. economic botanist, United Provs., 27th Jan., 1912; dep. dir. of agric., I.A.S., Madras, on probn., June, 1916; confd., March, 1920; spec. offcr., Impl. Coll. of Agric. Research, Oct., 1937; ret'd., Aprl., 1940.

WILSON, SIR ARNOLD TALBOT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.M.G., D.S.O., M.P., Lt. Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.).—Ret'd., Aug., 1921.*

WILSON, ARTHUR HENRY, O.B.E., B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 12th July, 1893).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., July, 1920; D.M.A.G. (junr.), Dec., 1931; ditto (senr.), Nov. 1932; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., March, 1934; revtd. to mil. accts. dept., July, 1937; snr. dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Jan., 1940; cl. I offcr., July, 1940; command contr., Aug., 1940; O.B.E., June, 1942.

WILSON, ARTHUR NORMAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 4th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1935; vice-princl., govt. sch. of engrg., Rasul, Sept., 1936, to Nov., 1937; on mil. serv., May, 1942.

WILSON, CECIL CLAUDE, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 11th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr., dep. consvr., 1914; on mil. service, Oct., 1916, to March, 1919; actg. consvr., Sept., 1924; confd., March, 1926; forest economist, Dehra Dun, Nov., 1925; ret'd., July, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1941.

WILSON, DENIS GREY, Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. Jan., 1880).—Ret'd., Jan., 1935.*

WILSON, REV. GEORGE ALBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 24th Aprl., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras, 29th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, March, 1928; senr. ditto, March, 1938.

WILSON, RT. HON. SIR GUY DOUGLAS ARTHUR FLEETWOOD, P.C., G.C.I.E., K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—Member of govr.-genl.'s council, fin. dept., 9th Nov., 1908, to June, 1913; discharged functions on behalf of the govr.-genl. after the attempt on the latter's life in Dec., 1912; K.C.M.G., 1909; G.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; P.C., 1914.

WILSON, JAMES LEITCH, M.A., Edin., Punjab Educl. Service (b. 31st March, 1892).—Joined the service as offg. inspr. of schs., 27th March, 1925; confd., March, 1930; vice-princl. March, 1925; confd., March, 1930; vice-princl., Central Trng. Coll., Lahore, Nov., 1935; supt. of educn., Baluchistan, May, 1937; to Aprl., 1943; princpl., govt. coll., Lyallpur, Aprl., 1943; senr., sel. grade (cl. I), July, 1943.

WILSON, JOHN CYRIL, Indian Police (supl., Bombay) (b. 24th May, 1902).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., Nov., 1930; asst. to inspr.-genl.,

dep. commr., police, Bombay, Aprl., 1938; Indian police medal, July, 1940; dep. commr., Bombay, Aug., 1941, to Nov., 1942; confd. as supt. (sel. grade), March, 1944; King's police medal, Jan., 1943.

WILSON, JOSEPH ARNOLD, B.A., Indian Forest Services (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 12th Dec., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

WILSON, SIR LEONARD, B.E., K.C.I.E., Indian State Ryis. (ch. commr., ryis.) (b. 12th March, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 24th Aprl., 1911; confd., June, 1915; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; offg. divl. trans. supt., Sept., 1925; confd., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. trans. supt., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. agent, Dec., 1929; offg. ch. engr., Aprl., 1930; confd., March, 1933; offg. agent, G.I.P. rly., May, 1934; provl., confd., Feb., 1935; ch. commr. of ryis., Aprl., 1941; kt., June, 1941; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1945.

WILSON, RT. HON. SIR LESLIE ORME, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., C.M.G., D.S.O., Lieut.-Col., R.M.L.I. (ret'd.).—Govr. of Bombay from 10th Dec., 1923, to 8th Dec., 1928.*

WILSON, LESLIE TALBOT, Indian Police (asst. inspr.-genl., Burma) (b. 11th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 5th Aprl., 1921; offg. dist. supt. and asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, March, 1926; confd., March, 1932; asst. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1936.

WILSON, NORMAN METHUEN, C.I.E., O.B.E., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1881).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1905; in mil. employ till June, 1912; plague medl. offr., Punjab, Nov., 1912; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to Sept., 1921; O.B.E., Aug., 1920; civil surg., Punjab, Sept., 1921; war services, Waziristan operations, 1919-21 (mentioned in despatches); civil surg. and C.M.O., Delhi, Aprl., 1932; inspr. genl. of civil hosps., Central Provs., Jan., 1934; offg. dir. of public health, Central Provs., March, 1936; surg.-genl. with govt of Madras, Sept., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; comdr. of the Order of St. John, Dec., 1940; ret'd., Feb., 1941.

WILSON, PERCY, A.R.I.B.A., late Indian State Ryis. (G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 4th Nov., 1891).—Ret'd., Sept., 1938.*

WILSON, SIR ROGER COCHRANE, K.C.B., D.S.O., M.C., Lieut.-Gen., I.A. (b. 26th Dec., 1882).—Mil. sec., India Office, May, 1936.*

WILSON, RONALD PATERSON, late Indian Police (b. 31st Oct., 1897).—Ret'd., Aprl., 1935.*

WILTON DAVIES, PERCY WILLIAM, V.D., Indian State Ryis. (dep. ch. engr., N.W. Ry.) (b. 14th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1912; asst. engr., Dec., 1913; exec. engr., March, 1931; dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1943.

WIMBERLEY, MAJ. EDMUND ARABIN, O.B.E., R.E. (dep. mint master) (b. 16th Sept., 1906).—Under mil. dept. up to 14th Aprl., 1937; dep. mint master (on probn.), Aprl., 1937; off-mint master var. from Sept., 1938; confd. dep. ditto, Aug., 1940; offg. mint master, May, 1941; O.B.E., June, 1941; offcr. i/c. new mint, Lahore, May, 1942.

WIMBUSH, ANTHONY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 19th May, 1886).—Ret'd., June, 1937.*

WIN, U. ZAW, Burma Police (cl. I).—Apptd. asst. supt. of police, on probn., Dec., 1937; transf. to mil. dept., Nov., 1940.

WINDLE, OLIVER ETHE LBERT, O.B.E., late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 20th July, 1884).—Ret'd., July, 1939.*

WINGATE, RONALD EVELYN LESLIE, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. and Indian Political Service (b. 30th Sept., 1889).—Retd., Apl., 1939.*

WINGFIELD, DESMOND ERNEST, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 17th Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 26th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. supt., Nov., 1935; confd., Apl., 1944.

WINTERTON, EARL, RIGHT HON. EDWARD TURNOUR, P.C., M.P., J.P., D.L.—Parliamentary under-sec. of state for India, March, 1922, to Jan., 1924, and Nov., 1924, to June, 1929; delegate to Burma round-table conf., London, Dec., 1931; delegate to Indian round-table conf., London, Nov.-Dec., 1932.

WISE, SIR JOHN HUMPHREY, K.C.M.G., C.B.E., Indian C.S. (counsellor to the govr. of Burma) (b. 11th March, 1890).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Oxford Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1913; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1914, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from Apl., 1915, to July, 1919; under-sec. to govt., Feb., 1921, to Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to finl. commr., Aug., 1923; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, health dept., from Nov., 1923, to Feb., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1924; secy., public service commn., Oct., 1926; dep. commr., Feb., 1930; secty., rev. dept., Burma, Nov., 1932, and again, Apl., 1934; secty., commerce and indus. dept., March, 1937; C.B.E., June, 1939; contr. of supplies, Burma, Oct., 1939; counsellor to the govt. of Burma, June, 1942; K.C.M.G., Jan., 1943.

WITHERS, FREDERICK WALTER, late Indian Forest Service (b. 16th June, 1896).—Retd., Jan., 1933.

WITHINSHAW, LESLIE, late Indian Police (Madras) (b. 27th Feb., 1886).—Retd., Aug., 1935.*

WOLFENDEN, RALPH, M.B.E., M.Sc., M.I.M.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Barr., late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 18th Feb., 1884).—Retd., Feb., 1939.*

WOOD, ANTHONY ST. JOHN, Indian Police, (asst. supt.) (b. 19th Jan., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

WOOD, ARTHUR EDWARD FRANCIS, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 18th June, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Dec., 1910; supt., Nov., 1925; retd., 16th Nov., 1943.

WOOD, ARTHUR WESTMORELAND, Barr., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 1st Aug., 1883).—Retd., March, 1935.*

WOOD, CHRISTOPHER FRANCIS, late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 26th Aug., 1884).—Educ. at Grocers' Company's Sch., London, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and asst. commr.; on mil. duty, I.A.R.O., Sept., 1916, to Feb., 1919; jt. mag., March, 1918; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1920; jt. sec. to board of rev., Feb., 1921; sec., ditto, Apl., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1924; confd., July, 1925; offg. commr., Dec., 1925; confd., Apl., 1939; retd., 2nd Dec., 1944.

WOOD, ERIC RAWLINSON, C.I.E., M.C., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Orissa) (b. 24th March, 1893).—Educ. at Denstone Coll., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1919; arrived, 1st Dec., 1919, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and

jt. mag., Jan., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; ditto (provl. subs.), March, 1935; collr., March, 1936; served tempy. with govt. of Orissa from Apl., 1936; secty. to govt. of Orissa, Educ. Dept., March, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; retd., July, 1944.

WOOD, ERNEST CLEMENT, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 12th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Cheltenham and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mily. duty from Oct., 1916, to May, 1919; asst. to resdt., Travancore and Cochin, June, 1919; sub-collr., March, 1922; offg. commr. of Coorg, May, 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1926; confd., March, 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1930; collr. (provl. subs.), May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; secty. to govt., educn. and p.h. dept., July, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1943; member, bd. of rev., Apl., 1943.

WOOD, GEORGE HERBERT ANDERSON, Indian Fin. Dept. (b. 31st Aug., 1894).—Joined the service, 14th June, 1919; asst. acctnt.-genl., Nov., 1920; currency offer., Madras, Oct., 1922; ditto, Lahore, May, 1925; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1926; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. Rly., June, 1932; services placed at disposal of finl. commr. of rlys., Feb., 1933; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts., June, 1935; dep. ch. accts. offer., E.R. rly., May, 1936; on for. service under Jodhpur State, Jan., 1937; dir., rly. clg. accts., Delhi, Oct., 1939; fin. advr. and ch. accts. offer., N.W. rly., May, 1941.

WOOD, JOHN LUTHER, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 25th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at Burnley Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., March, 1934.

WOOD, LESLIE DERING, Indian Police (b. 5th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th Nov., 1928; offg. supt., Apl., 1935; confd., Dec., 1940.

WOOD, PERCY, Indian Railway Accts. Service (ch. accts. offer.) (b. 17th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acctnt., G.I.P. rly., 7th Nov., 1913; confd. as asst. auditor, G.I.P. rly., Dec., 1920; on depts. to Agency Dept. as supt. of statistics, G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1924, to March, 1929; served under finl. commr., rlys., Oct., 1929, and again, Dec., 1935; dep. ch. accts. offer., G.I.P. rly., Oct., 1929; served under agent, G.I.P. rly., July, 1932; confd. as dep. agent, Jan., 1933; dep. ch. accts. offer., Nov., 1936; ch. accts. offer., G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1938.

WOOD, WILLIAM GEORGE, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. offer., store dept.) (b. 27th July, 1897).—Apptd. clerical offer., 1st Apl., 1927, after previous service from Apl., 1921; exec. offer., 1st Nov., 1932.

WOOD, WILLIAM OUTERSON, O.B.E., Indian Police (b. 14th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 20th Nov., 1923; awarded King's police medal, March, 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; O.B.E., May, 1936; senr. asst. dir., intell., govt. of India, Nov., 1940.

WOODFORD, GEORGE PEARCE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 21st Dec., 1915).—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 23rd Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

WOODHEAD, SIR JOHN ACKROYD, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th June, 1881).—Retd., Dec., 1939*; advr. to sec. of state for India, Dec., 1939; advr. to sec. of state for Burma, Sept., 1941, to Aug., 1942; ditto for India, Aug., 1942, to Dec., 1944.

WOODHOUSE, ALBERT CYRIL, C.I.E., B.A., Madras C.S. (sub. collr.) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 30th Nov., 1912; offg. collr. and magte., July, 1926; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; collr. (provl. perm.), Apl., 1939.

WOODHOUSE, HAROLD LISTER, M.C., Col., R.E., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 30th Jan., 1887).—First commn. dated 25th July, 1906; asst. engr., 23rd March, 1911; on mil. service from 20th Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Jan., 1917; divl. supt., Sept., 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1933; confd., May, 1935; offg. dir., rly. bd., July, 1934; confd., June, 1935; ch. engr., Nov., 1937; retd., Jan., 1942.

WOODHOUSE, SIDNEY, late Survey of India (b. 9th Nov., 1882).—Retd., Aug., 1937.*

WOOD ROBINSON, HAROLD, Indian State Ryls. (dep. ch. contr. of standardisation, rly. bd.) (b. 7th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. exec. bridge engr., 2nd Feb., 1923; bridge engr., N.W. rly., March, 1931; services loaned to M. and S.M. rly. from Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), N.W. rly., March, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; dep. ch. contr. of standardisation, rly. bd., Apl., 1940.

WOODS, GEORGE WILLIAM MURRAY, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 3rd June, 1899).—Retd., Sept., 1935.*

WOODS-BALLARD, BASIL, C.I.E., M.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Sept., 1900).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1920; in milty. employ prior to 1st Jan., 1925; probnr. in pol. dept., May, 1925; asst. commr., D.I. Khan, Dec., 1926; city magte., Peshawar, Jan., 1928; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, June, 1929; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, July, 1919; under-secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Apl., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Quetta, Pishin, Oct., 1934; M.B.E., Nov., 1935; pol. agent, Loralai, Feb., 1936; sec. to the resdt. for the Punjab States, Nov., 1939; pol. agent and dep. commr. in Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1941; C.I.E., June, 1942.

WOOLDRIDGE, LOVELL WILLIAM, O.B.E., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Dec., 1901).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to May, 1927; asst. pol. agent at Chilas, Nov., 1930; under-secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Dec., 1934; asst. commr., Tank, Feb., 1937; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1937; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Oct., 1938; secty. and excise asst. to resdt., Central India, Nov., 1941; O.B.E., June, 1944; secty. to resdt., E. Rajputana, Oct., 1944.

WOOLLER, ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd May, 1912).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and sub divl. offcr., Apl., 1937; apptd. to Indian Pol. Service (subst.), and posted as asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1939; vice-consul, Zabul, Jan., 1941; ditto, Ahwar, Oct., 1941; on depn. to N.W.F.P., March, 1944.

WORMALD, REV. ROBERT LEONARD, M.B.E., M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 7th Apl., 1882).—Retd., Sept., 1935.*

WORT, SIR ALFRED WILLIAM EWART, late Judl. Dept., Bihar (b. 17th March, 1883).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1927; offg. ch. justice, July, 1933; Apl., 1936; and July, 1938; kt., June, 1939; retd., Sept., 1940.

WORTH, MEREDITH, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Repton, Grenoble, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse, and asst. to pol. offcr. in Sikkim, Apl., 1933; offg. Brit. trade agent, Yatung, Apl., 1933; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain (temp.), May, 1934; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Oct., 1935; offg. pol. agent in addn., March, 1936; senior sub-judge, Hazara, Dec., 1936; asst. commr., Hazara, Jan., 1937; ditto, in addn., Manshera, Apl., 1937; confd., Apl., 1937; counsellor, Brit. Legation, Kabul, July, 1937; asst. commr., Hazara, Dec., 1937; secty. to resdt. for Baroda and Gujarat States, March, 1938, to Baroda, 1939; on special duty as boundary settl. offr., Baroda, Oct., 1939; sec. to resdt. for the States of Western India, Nov., 1939; secty. to resdt. for Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Sept., 1941; pol. agt., Orissa States, Sept., 1944.

WORTHY, GEORGE TOYNBEE, late India Office (b. 9th Apl., 1882).—Educ. at Royal Gr. Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; apptd. 2nd div. clerk, War Office, 14th March, 1901; apptd. 2nd class clerk, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 13th Nov., 1905; transf. to registry and record dept., Apl., 1906, and to milty. dept., Jan., 1917; on milty. service, July, 1918, to Feb., 1919; exec. offcr., Apl., 1922; actg. higher clerical offcr., Apl., 1922, to Sept., 1923; retd. and re-empld. (temp.), Apl., 1942.

WRENCH, JOHN CECIL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 31st Jan., 1922; dep. consvr., Jan., 1925; offg. princpl., Madras forest coll., Jan., 1930, to Apl., 1934; dep. consvr., Feb., 1940; pers. asst. to ch. consvr., March, 1944.

WRENCH, JOHN MERVYN DALLAS, C.I.E., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 15th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th Aug., 1906; dist. loco. supt., Apl., 1913; supt. of state workshops, Gwalior, Dec., 1914, to Apl., 1915; spl. duty with contr. of stores, mill. ryls., from Nov., 1916 (mentioned in despatches); ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. rly.; offg. dir. mechl. engr., rly. dept., March, 1925; on spl. duty, rly. bd., Oct., 1926; ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. rly. bd., 1928; C.I.E., March, 1929; on spl. duty with rly. bd., Feb., 1929; offg. ch. contr. of standards, rly. bd., March, 1930; dir., mechl. engr. in addn., Nov., 1932; ch. contr. of standardisation, Apl., 1935; retd., Apl., 1942.

WRIGHT, ALBERT ERNEST, B.A. (Oxon.), Imperial Customs Service (b. 30th June, 1902).—Joined the service, Nov., 1926; asst. collr. of customs, Nov., 1926; dep. commr., cent. excises and salt dept., N. India, Feb., 1938; collr. of central excise, Calcutta, Dec., 1939.

WRIGHT, FRANCIS GERALD GRIBBLE, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Prov.) (b. 2nd June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 4th Oct., 1921; offg. dist. supt., March, 1925; confd., Jan., 1929; O.B.E., June, 1943; asst. to inspr.-genl., May, 1942, to Feb., 1944.

WRIGHT, HERBERT LAWRENCE, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 24th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service, 24th Dec., 1909;

served in the Punjab as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; on depn. to Kashmir as consvr. of forests from Jan., 1923; ch. consvr., Kashmir, Aug., 1924; consvr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932; apptd. offic. on special duty in Gilgit Agency, Aug. to Oct., 1937; ret'd., Dec., 1941.

WRIGHT, PHILIP VICTOR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Sept., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 16th Dec., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

WRIGHT, ROBERT ERNEST, C.I.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 2nd Oct., 1884).—Ret'd., Nov., 1938.*

WRIGHT, ROBERT MARTIN, late Indian Police (Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Nov., 1908; supt., March, 1917; awarded King's police medal, 1918; princpl. police trng. coll., Bengal, Feb., 1928; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1929; confd., Feb., 1932; ret'd., May, 1944.

WRIGHT, ROBERT WALLACE, M.B.E., India Office (supt. of records) (b. 20th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. second-class clerk, India Office, 2nd July, 1912, and att'd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; transf'd. to registry and record dept., July, 1912, and to store dept., Jan., 1917; on mill. service from Nov., 1915; retransferred to registry and record dept., June, 1919; transf'd. to comm. and rev. dept., Feb., 1922; higher clerical offic., Apl., 1922; resident clerk, March, 1923; parly. clerk, Apl., 1926; asst. sec., Indian delegation to 7th and 8th assemblies of League of Nations, 1926 and 1927; M.B.E., June, 1932; asst. supt. of records, Nov., 1937; supt. of records, Apl., 1939.

WRIGHT, SYDNEY WILLIAM WALTER, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 29th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 20th Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1931; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1933; confd. as dist. supt., July, 1937; awarded King's police and fire services medals, Jan., 1944.

WRIGHT, WALTER HENRY, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 13th Sept., 1891).—Joined 22nd Nov., 1911; asst. supt., Nov., 1914; offg. supt., July, 1919, to May, 1921, and Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; supt. (provl. perm.), Jan., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; confd. dist. supt. (seln. grade), Oct., 1939; dep. inspr.-genl. (perm.), July, 1940.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM ALAN, A.F.C., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 27th Nov., 1895).—Educ. at Oundle, and Calus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1921; arrived, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1924; confd., March, 1929.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM MCKENDREE, M.B.E., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th June, 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., United Provs., Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; asst. dir., intell. bureau, govt. of India, Oct., 1940; M.B.E., June, 1944.

WRINCH, ROBERT CORNICK, India Posts and Tels. (p.m.g.) (b. 26th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. divl. engr., March, 1921; divl. engr., tels., Aug., 1928; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan, March, 1934; offg. dir., tels., Apl., 1936; confd., Dec., 1936; confd. as p.m.g., Feb., 1943.

WUNNAM, ESWARA VENKATA SITAPATI RAO, LL.B. (London Univ.), Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical offic., accts. dept.) (b. 20th June, 1893).—Apptd. to India Office,

acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 19th Apl., 1920; transf'd. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st. Oct., 1920; clerical offic., Oct., 1921; exec. offic., 1st Apl., 1923; higher clerical offic. (actg.), Apl., 1927; confd., June, 1927; actg. hr. exec. offic., Oct., 1940, to March, 1941.

WYLIE, SIR FRANCIS VERNER, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (pol. advr. to C.R.)
