(b. 9th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on milty. duty from July, 1916, to March, 1919; temp. offg. as asst. commr., Charsadda, from May, 1919; pol. agent, Tochi, May, 1921; settlt. offic., Peshawar, Sept., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1929; offg. dep. sec. for. and pol. dept., Dec., 1929, to June, 1930; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1930; dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1930; prime minister, Alwar, March, 1933; dep. agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1935; jt. secty. to the govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1936; resdt., Jaipur, Aug., 1936; on spl. duty in for. and pol. dept., Sept., 1936; resdt., Jaipur, Feb. to Dec., 1937; govr., Central Provs., and Berar, May, 1938; K.C.S.I., May, 1938; pol. advr. to H.E. the Crown Representative, Dec., 1940; H.B.M.'s Minister at Kabul, Aug., 1941; pol. advr. to Crown Representative, Aug., 1943.

WYNDHAM, GEORGE COLVILLE, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1916).—Educ. at Stowe, Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Dec., 1939, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1941, to Oct., 1943; under-secty. to govr.-genl. (public), Nov., 1943.

WYNNE, MARTIN GOYMOUR, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 26th May, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 29th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; asst. commr., Port Blair, Nov., 1934, to Feb., 1938.

YADAVA MOHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as engr. student, 10th Oct., 1928; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1938.

YAHYA ALI SAHIB, KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 23rd Aug., 1893).—Joined the service, June, 1936; apptd. dist. and sess. judge, on probn., June, 1936; confd., June, 1937.

YAKUB SHAH, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 2nd Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Jan., 1929; asst. contr., rly. accts., March, 1930; offg. dep. ch. accts. offic., N.W. rly., Sept., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., Nov., 1933; exr., local fund accts., Punjab, May, 1935; offg. asst. secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1935; dep. dir., rly. bd. (temp.), May, 1936; offg. dep. dir., fin., rly. bd., Oct., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; dir., ditto, May, 1938; ch. auditor, N.W. rly., Dec., 1940; fin. advr. and ch. accts. offic., E.B. rly., May, 1941; confd., ditto, B. & A. rly., Oct., 1942.

YARDI, MADHUKAR RAMRAO, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1916).—Educ. at Bombay Univ.; apptd. on probn. after exam. of 1940 and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

YARNOLD, FREDERICK HERBERT, M.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b.

18th Nov., 1911).—Educ. at the King's Sch., Worcester, and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., home dept., Nov., 1937; ditto, def. dept., Sept., 1938; headqts. asst., Mergin, March, 1941; asst. commr., March, 1941; M.B.E., Jan., 1943.

YATES, CLEMENT MERVYN SCOTT, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 7th June, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 7th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., June, 1932, and again, Feb., 1933; a.d.c. to govt., Bombay, Feb., 1936, to Sept., 1937; confd. as supt., Nov., 1939; Indian police medal, 1941; princpl., trng. sch., Nasik, Aug., 1943.

YEATTS, WILLIAM WALTER MURRAY, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. secty., dept. of supply) (b. 30th Sept., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 28th Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; under-secty. to govt., ch. sectt., Apl., 1926; census supt., Madras, Apl., 1930; dep. secty., reforms office, June, 1932; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Apl., 1935; offg. dep. secty., ditto, June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. jt. secty., ditto, variously from Sept., 1935; and again, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; census commr. for India, March, 1939; confd. as dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1939; jt. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of supply, July, 1941.

YELD, PERCIVAL HARLEY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Retd., July, 1939.*

YENNEMADI, DATTATRAYA VENKATRAO, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay C.S., Judicial Branch (b. 23rd Apl., 1883).—Retd., May, 1938.*

YIN, KHIN MAUNG, B.Sc., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 19th Feb., 1897).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., educn. dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1931; confd., Jan., 1937.

YORKE, ROBERT LANGDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Feb., 1887).—Educ. at Haileybury, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1917; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., Oct., 1917; revtd. to civil duty, Jan., 1919; actg. dist. and sess. judge from July, 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt., judl. branch, March, 1926; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1926; judl. sec. to govt., May, 1927; apptd. judge, Oudh ch. ct., Jan., 1938; judge, Allahabad high ct., 6th Jan., 1943.

YOUNG, REV. ERNEST JOSEPH, B.A., late Indian Ecclesi. Estabt. (Bengal) (b. 26th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 6th Apl., 1918; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1918; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1928; offg. archdeacon of Calcutta, Mar., 1935; confd., Sept., 1936; retd., Oct., 1943.

YOUNG, FRANCIS WILLIAM WALLACE (asst. collr. of customs) (b. 26th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1906; held non-gaz. posts, June, 1906, to Jan., 1926; pay and accts. offcr., Jan., 1926, to July, 1931; ch. accts. offcr., Apl., 1931; asst. collr. of customs, March, 1938.

YOUNG, FREDERICK, C.I.E., C.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 16th May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 16th Dec., 1909; supt., June, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1924; inspr.-genl. of police, Jaipur

State, May, 1931; dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1939; C.B.E., Jan., 1944.

YOUNG, GERARD MACKWORTH, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.G.S., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 7th Apl., 1884).—Retd., Aug., 1934*; re-empld. July, 1941.

YOUNG, HAROLD NORMAN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Sept., 1890).—Retd., Sept., 1940.*

YOUNG, JAMES, Maj., M.B.E., Indian Stores Dept. (dep. dir. of purchase) (b. 4th July, 1885).—In milty. employ from 20th May, 1915, to 15th Sept., 1924; M.B.E., Jan., 1920; asst. dir. of purchase, Sept., 1924; offg. dep. dir., ditto, May, 1925; confd., Feb., 1926.

YOUNG, JAMES WOLSTON, O.B.E., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 18th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1909; asst. acctnt.-genl., Sept., 1909; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; employed by Jodhpur Durbar from 10th March, 1923; delegate to Indian round-table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931.

YOUNG, SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, late Judl. Dept., United Provs. (ch. justice, high ct., Lahore) (b. 7th Apl., 1883).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, 15th Jan., 1929; ch. justice, high ct., Lahore, May, 1934; kt., Jan., 1935; retd., 19th Jan., 1943.

YOUNG, REV. PHILIP NORTON FRUSHARD, M.A., Indian Ecclesi. Estabt. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Punjab, 2nd Nov., 1930; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1932.

YOUNG, WILLIAM HOARE HATCHELL, M.C., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service, 28th Apl., 1904; asst. traffic supt., N.W. rly., Jan., 1909; I.A.R.O., March, 1915; dist. traffic supt., Aug., 1917; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., July, 1925; offg. divl. supt., Nov., 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. operating supt., E.I. rly., Aug., 1935; ch. comml. manager, E.I. rly., Nov., 1935; ch. operating supt., E.I. rly., Dec., 1937; retd., Aug., 1941.

YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 20th May, 1911).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 23rd Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

YULE, HARRY HOWARD, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 30th Nov., 1880).—Retd., July, 1937.*

YUSAF, SALAHUDDIN AHMED (see Salahuddin, A. Y.).

YUSUF ALI KHAN, AGHA, late Indian Agricl. Service (b. Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1907, and held non-gazetted appts. to 31st March, 1919; extra asst. dir. of agric., Apl., 1919; offg. dep. dir. of agric., March, 1928; retd.,

YUSUF, SHEIKH MOHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 13th Nov., 1913).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 19th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

ZACHARIAH, KURUVILA, M.A. (Oxon), Indian Educl. Service (dir., public instrn., Bengal) (b. 24th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as prof'r., presdy. coll., 10th Jan., 1916; princpl., Hooghly Coll., July, 1930; dir., public instrn., Bengal, Aug., 1944.

ZAFAR, MUHAMMAD ABU, Indian Police.—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 11th Feb., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937.

ZAFAR HASAN, KHAN BAHADUR, MAULVI, O.B.E., B.A., late Archaeological Survey Dept.

(b. 17th July, 1886).—Joined the service, 17th June, 1913; supt., Feb., 1925; offg. dep. dir.-genl., Feb., 1931; confd., Apl., 1932; supt., northern circle, Oct., 1935; O.B.E., June, 1941; retd., Jan., 1941.

ZAFAR HUSSAIN KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., LL.B., A.M.I.T., Indian State Ryls. (divl. transportation offcr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1894).—Joined service, Feb., 1917; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1919; junr. scale, Aug., 1919; divl. transportation (traffic-commercial) offcr., senr. scale, Dec., 1937.

ZAFAR OMAR, B.A., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 27th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 7th Jan., 1908; supt., Sept., 1924; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1938.

ZAFRULLAH KHAN, CHAUDHRI, SIR MUHAMMAD, K.C.S.I. (judge, Federal Court of India).—Apptd. temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Apl., 1935; confd., May, 1935; kt., June, 1935; chairman, central advisory council of rlys., July, 1935, to Nov., 1937; K.C.S.I., May, 1937; on spec. duty., Apl. to Aug., 1938; and again, Oct. to Dec., 1939; judge, federal ct. of India, Oct., 1941.

ZAHEER, SAIVID HASAN (see Hasan Zaheer Saiyid).

ZAHADUR, RAHIM ZAHID SUHRAWARDY (see Suhrawardi, Sir Z. R. Z.).

ZAHID HASAN KHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as appr. engr., Nov., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1927; exec. engr., confd., Feb., 1940.

ZAHID HUSAIN, M.A., C.I.E., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, 14th March, 1918; asst. auditor, N.W. rly., Apl., 1921; ditto, E.B. rly., Nov., 1923; ditto, E.I. rly., Dec., 1925; dir. of army audit, westn. circle, Feb., 1929; dep. contr., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1931; asst. auditor-genl., Apl., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, rly, clearing accts. office, March, 1933; dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels. (fin.), Jan., 1934; offg. compt., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1935; fin. advr. to C.C., Delhi, Feb., 1937; dir. of fin., rly. bd., Apl., 1939; jt. finl. advr., mil. fin., March, 1940; addl. ditto (supply dept.), July, 1940; C.I.E., Jan., 1942; finl. commr., rlys., Oct., 1943; confd. acct.-genl. (cl. II), Dec., 1943.

ZAHIRUDDIN, AHMAD FARUQI, MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th Nov., 1890).—Retd., Dec., 1939.*

ZAKIR HUSAIN, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 2nd Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 26th Sept., 1923; offg. supt., Sept., 1930; confd., Aug., 1936.

ZAMAN, AKHTARUZ, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr.) (b. 29th Jan., 1917).—Educ. at Hoogly Collegiate Sch., Chinsura, Kandi Raj

Sch., Murshidabad, Calcutta Univ., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1939; confd., 27th Aug., 1940; arrived in India, Nov., 1940, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1941.

ZAMAN, S. R. (see Rashid-Uz-Zaman).

ZAW PE, U. (see Pe, U Zaw).

ZETLAND (MARQUESS OF), MOST HON. LAWRENCE JOHN LUMLEY DUNDAS, K.G., P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—Member of the Royal commn. on the public services in India, 1912-15; G.C.I.E., Feb., 1917; govr. of Bengal from 26th March, 1917, to 20th March, 1922; P.C., 1922; delegate to Indian round-table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; sec. of state for India, June, 1935, to May, 1940; sec. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937, to May, 1940; K.G., June, 1942; author of 'Sport and Politics under an Eastern Sky,' 'On the Outskirts of Empire In Asia,' 'A Wandering Student in the Far East,' 'An Eastern Miscellany.'

ZIA-UL HASAN, KHAN BAHADUR, MUNSHI, B.A., late Judl. Dept., United Provs. (b. Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 26th June, 1907; sub. judge, Jan., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1932; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, July, 1934; retd., Dec., 1940.

ZIAUL HAQ, MIAN MUHAMMAD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 10th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 20th July, 1915; extra asst. consvr., July, 1917; promoted to I.F.S., United Provs., Apl., 1931; dep. consvr., Oct., 1931.

ZIAULLAH KHAN, MUHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 4th Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Lucknow Univ. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1937.

ZINKIN, MAURICE, LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th May, 1915).—Educ. at Haberdashers' Aske's Hampstead Sch., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 21st Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

ZUBERI, MASARRAT HUSAIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 25th June, 1911).—Educ. at St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offcr., Apl., 1939; pol. asst. and commdt., Border mil. police, March, 1940; sec., Punjab and N.W.F.P. jt. publ. serv. commn., Oct., 1940; under-sec., govt. of India, Sept., 1942.

LONDON

PUBLISHED BY HIS MAJESTY'S STATIONERY OFFICE

To be purchased directly from H.M. STATIONERY OFFICE at the following addresses:
York House, Kingsway, London, W.C.2 : 134 Castle Street, Edinburgh 2;
39-41 King Street, Manchester 2; 1 St. Andrew's Crescent, Cardiff;
80 Chichester Street, Belfast;
or through any bookseller

1945

Price £1 5s. 0d. net.

LONDON : HIS MAJESTY'S STATIONERY OFFICE
1946
SIXPENCE NET

S.O. Code No. 70-366-I-45

GOVERNMENT OF INDIA

Headquarters of Government—New Delhi

VICEROY AND GOVERNOR-GENERAL OF INDIA.

His Excellency Field Marshal The Right Honourable VISCOUNT WAVELL OF CYRENAICA AND WINCHESTER, P.C., G.C.B., G.M.S.I., G.M.I.E., C.M.G., M.C.

Assumed charge of office 20 October, 1943.

PERSONAL STAFF OF H.E. THE VICEROY.

Private Secretary—G. E. B. Abell, C.I.E., O.B.E., I.C.S.

Deputy Private Secretary—I. D. Scott, I.C.S.

Military Secretary—Col. D. H. Currie, C.B.E., M.C., D.C.M., I.A.

Assistant Private Secretary—Major C. W. B. Rankin, M.B.E.

Comptroller—Major W. B. Henderson, Royal Marines.

Surgeon—Lieut.-Col. H. Williamson, O.B.E., I.M.S. (rettd.).

Aides-de-Camp—Capt. P. S. Montgomery, Intell. Corps; Capt. F. H. M. FitzRoy, Coldstream Guards; Capt. J. W. Brooke, 10th Hussars; Capt. J. W. Scott, Grenadier Guards.

Indian Aides-de-Camp—Risaldar-Major and Hony. Capt. Raghbir Singh, Sardar Bahadur, M.B.E., O.B.I., I.D.S.M., I.A.C.; Risaldar-Major and Hony. Lieut. Muzaffar Khan, Sardar Bahadur, O.B.I., I.A.C.

Comdt. of the Body Guard—Major P. Massey, Ind. Armoured Corps.

Adjutant of the Body Guard—Capt. H. C. Badhwar, M.B.E., Ind. Armoured Corps.

GOVERNOR-GENERAL'S SECRETARIAT.

(PERSONAL.)

Secretary—G. E. B. Abell, C.I.E., O.B.E., I.C.S.

Deputy Sec.—I. D. Scott, I.C.S.

Asst. Sec.—W. L. Harrison, M.B.E.

(PUBLIC.)

Secretary—Rao Bahadur V. P. Menon, C.S.I., C.I.E.

Under-Secretary—G. C. Wyndham, I.C.S.

(REFORMS.)

Reforms Commissioner—Rao Bahadur V. P. Menon, C.S.I., C.I.E.

Deputy Sec.—E. C. Gaynor, M.B.E.

Officer on Special Duty—Sir B. N. Rau, C.I.E.

COMMANDER-IN-CHIEF.

Field Marshal Sir Claude Auchinleck, G.C.B., G.C.I.E., C.S.I., D.S.O., O.B.E., A.D.C.

COUNCIL OF THE GOVERNOR-GENERAL.

Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru—External Affairs and Commonwealth Relations.

Sardar Baldev Singh—Defence.

Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel—Home, including Information and Broadcasting.

Dr. John Matthai—Finance.

Mr. M. Asaf Ali—Communications (War Transport and Railways).

Dr. Rajendra Prasad—Agriculture and Food.

Mr. Jagjivan Ram—Labour.

Sir Shafaat Ahmad Khan—Health, Education and Arts.

Syed Ali Zaheer—Legislative Posts and Air.

Mr. C. Rajagopalachari—Industries and Supplies.

Mr. Sarat Chandra Bose—Works, Mines and Power.

Mr. C. H. Bhabha—Commerce.

SECRETARIAT.

Secretary—Sir Eric Coates, C.S.I., C.I.E., I.C.S.

Joint Sec.—H. M. Patel, C.I.E., I.C.S.

Estabt. Officer—Sir Alan Lloyd, C.S.I., C.I.E.

Dep. Secs.—Brig. H. L. Wyndham, D.S.O., S. Jagannathan, I.C.S.

Under Secs.—Wazir Ali, Maj. G. G. Bewoor, Maj. L. B. O. Tugwell.

Asst. Sec.—E. C. Donoghue.

INDIA

THE COUNCIL OF STATE.

(58 Members, 32 elected, 26 nominated, including 13 officials.)

President—Sir Maneckji Byamji Dadabhoj, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E.

THE LEGISLATIVE ASSEMBLY.

(142 Members, 102 elected, 40 nominated, including 22 officials.)

President—Ganesh Vasudeo Mavalankar.
